This paper provides a retrospective review of such systems, with an attempt to develop a rational categorization for the spectrum of approaches that have been explored.

title: Feed-In Tariff (FIT) System to Green Energy Auction Program (GEAP): The Philippines’ Strategic Move Toward Competitive Renewable Energy Market
authors: Jayson O. Ranara, Rogelyn T. Ronquillo, Dinna O. Dizon, Eunice F. Bernabe
publish: 2025 25th Conference of the Electric Power Supply Industry (CEPSI)
abstract: This paper examines the Philippines' strategic move towards achieving its energy transition goal and fostering a competitive renewable energy market. With the enactment of the Renewable Energy Act of 2008 (RE Law), the Feed-in Tariff (FIT) System was established to encourages the development of emerging renewable energy sources like wind, solar, biomass, and run-of-river hydro power plants by permitting priority dispatch and giving qualified plants a fixed rate known as the Feed-in Tariff (FIT). After several rounds of the FIT, the Department of Energy (DOE) introduced the Green Energy Auction Program (GEAP) in 2021. GEAP operates like the FIT System but with a key difference in rate-setting. The GET (GEA REs rate) will be paid for 20 years, similar to the FIT System. The key contribution of this paper is to provide a comparative analysis between FIT and GEAP mechanisms in terms of rate-setting, cost-effectiveness, and RE capacity development. Data shows that GEAP's auction-based model has delivered lower tariffs—up to 46% less than FIT rates—while attracting substantial new capacity commitments. The paper also outlines the results of succeeding GEAP rounds and presents the additional capacities toward achieving the national RE targets of 35% by 2040 and 50% by 2050. In addition, the paper highlights the implementation challenges and the inclusion of energy storage in GEAP to enhance grid reliability. Overall, the study underscores how auction-based mechanisms can drive cost-effective and sustainable RE growth in emerging economies like the Philippines.

title: Ganoderma Detection on Oil Palm Stem using YOLO Approach
authors: Anindita Septiarini, Hamdani Hamdani, Encik Akhmad Syaifudin, Fazri Rahmad Nor Gading, Novianti Puspita, Masna Wati
publish: 2025 2nd Beyond Technology Summit on Informatics International Conference (BTS-I2C)
abstract: Ganoderma is the primary pathogenic fungus infecting oil palm stems and poses a serious threat to the palm oil industry, as it has the potential to cause significant economic losses. Accurate and timely detection is crucial for effectively controlling its spread. This study investigates the application of deep learning-based object detection to enable automated, precise, and rapid identification of Ganoderma infections. Specifically, it evaluates and compares the detection performance of medium variants of three models: YOLOv8, YOLOv9, and YOLOv10, each fine-tuned using pre-trained weights on a custom dataset of 354 annotated images containing instances of Ganoderma and Primordium (the early fruiting body). Through image augmentation, the dataset was expanded to 798 training images, 70 validation images, and 18 test images. Experimental results on the test set show that each model achieved mAP50 scores of 95.41%, 94.54%, and 95.10%, mAP50:95 scores of 74.40%, 69.74%, and 67.71%, and Mean Recall values of 93.93%, 92.91%, and 91.21%, respectively. These findings demonstrate that YOLOv8m strikes the optimal balance between detection accuracy and robustness, making it a strong candidate for deployment in early detection systems for oil palm plantations.

title: Discovering Communities in Continuous-Time Temporal Networks by Optimizing L-Modularity
authors: Victor Brabant, Angela Bonifati, Rémy Cazabet
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Data Mining (ICDM)
abstract: Community detection is a fundamental problem in network analysis, with many applications in various fields. Extending community detection to the temporal setting with exact temporal accuracy, as required by real-world dynamic data, necessitates methods specifically adapted to the temporal nature of interactions. We introduce LAGO, a novel method for uncovering dynamic communities by greedy optimization of Longitudinal Modularity, a specific adaptation of Modularity for continuous-time networks. Unlike prior approaches that rely on time discretization or assume rigid community evolution, LAGO captures the precise moments when nodes enter and exit communities. We evaluate LAGO on synthetic benchmarks and real-world datasets, demonstrating its ability to efficiently uncover temporally and topologically coherent communities.

title: A Hybrid FEM-NN with Embedded Boundary Conditions for Solving Second and Fourth Order ODE Boundary Value Problems
authors: Allen Mire, Lisa M. Kuhn
publish: 2026 IEEE 16th Annual Computing and Communication Workshop and Conference (CCWC)
abstract: Numerical methods for solving differential equations in structural simulations face a critical trade-off: traditional methods like the Finite Element Method (FEM) are accurate but computationally intensive, while data-driven Artificial Neural Networks (ANNs) often ignore mathematical theory, transforming well-posed problems into ill-posed ones that violate physical laws. Existing hybrid approaches using FEM-enhanced neural networks fail to converge on higher-order problems. In this work, we introduce custom boundary layers that embed known boundary conditions directly into the network architecture, rather than relying solely on modified loss functions. This informed machine learning approach leverages FEM's stiffness matrix K and force vector f to guarantee physical validity while exploiting ANNs' capacity as universal function approximators. Our model successfully solves a fourth-order differential equation where the previous hybrid FEM-NN failed, achieving convergence in 1,191 epochs. By enforcing boundary constraints at the architectural level, we demonstrate that informed neural networks can solve complex structural problems with guaranteed physical compliance, though at the cost of reduced generalization to problems with different boundary conditions. This work establishes a path toward neural network-based simulators that combine the speed of machine learning with the mathematical rigor of traditional numerical methods.

title: A Comparative Analysis of Evaluation Metrics for Large Language Models in the Medical Question-Answering Domain
authors: Anant Kekre, Eeshaan Garkhedkar, Radha Mahajan, Svara Dadhe, Ruchi Sharma
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Artificial Intelligence for Innovations in Healthcare Industries (ICAIIHI)
abstract: Large language models (LLMs) have become the new trend that is changing healthcare practices through automated medical question answering, aiding diagnosis and improving communication with patients. These systems are tricky to evaluate, however, since traditional NLP measures frequently do not adequately measure clinical accuracy and depth of reasoning. The current paper investigates BLEU, ROUGE-L, METEOR, Precision, Recall, and F-score measures in transformer-based systems (BioBERT, DistilBERT, and BERT Large). Based on PubMedQA and MedQuAD datasets, we evaluate how each measure shows the factual and reasoning abilities of a model. Results show that conventional measures poorly address semantic accuracy and clinical validity and instead domain-specific models, such as BioBERT, exhibit better contextual understanding. Our work highlights that dynamism in appraising hybrid systems with regards to automated metrics and expert evaluation is necessary to maintain trust and safety in applying LLMs in medical care.

title: Reinforcement Learning for Tailored Vocabulary Growth in Second Language Education
authors: Karrar M. Khudhair, P. Balamurugan, Uktam Abdinazarov, Karrar Abbas Yousif, Raami Riadhusin
publish: 2025 International Conference on AI-Driven STEM Education and Learning Technologies (AISTEMEDU)
abstract: Vocabulary training is an important aspect of language learning, particularly in ESL (English as a Second Language) learners, whose vocabulary training should be personalized. As a method that learns through interaction and feedback, Reinforcement Learning (RL) has the potential to be a successful way of dynamically personalizing vocabulary teaching. Current vocabulary learning systems tend to be based on the static information presentation and do not provide real-time adjustment to the specific learner improvement, motivation and cognitive requirements. To resolve them, this paper presents one framework Personalized Vocabulary Training based on Reinforcement Learning (PVT-RL) which can be used to adapt vocabulary drills based on the performance of learners, their engagement patterns, and the accuracy of their responses over time. The suggested approach employs an RL agent to suggest suitable vocabulary tasks, maximizing learning sequences in the course of the continuous feedback loop. The students are taken through a sequence of customized challenges in which the level of difficulty in the tasks grows relative to the changing mastery of the student. The experimental findings with the ESL learners of university level indicate that PVT-RL results in considerably higher vocabulary memory, enhanced engagement among the learners, and increased long-term memory as compared to non-adaptive learning systems. This framework enhances a more effective and interesting learning process by smartly matching learning instructions with specific learner profiles.

title: AI-Augmented Software Engineering for Rapid Feature Delivery and Operations Automation
authors: Sureshkumar Karuppuchamy
publish: 2026 IEEE 16th Annual Computing and Communication Workshop and Conference (CCWC)
abstract: Modern software organizations need to deliver new features rapidly without sacrificing reliability. We present a metrics-driven, governance-first blueprint for integrating generative AI assistants across the enterprise software development lifecycle (SDLC). This approach embeds large language model (lLM)-based assistants into every phase-coding, testing, code review, CI/CD, and operations-with multi-layered governance guardrails to ensure safe and effective use. We deployed this blueprint at a global e-commerce company (”RetailCo”) and achieved a <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$20 \times$</tex> increase in deployment frequency without any loss of reliability. Our primary contributions are: (1) a reusable governance template for safe AI adoption (combining usage policies, content filtering, security scanning, and human oversight), (2) a holistic integration pattern for AI across the SDLC (demonstrated at enterprise scale with phased rollout guidance), and (3) a quantitative evaluation framework grounded in DORA metrics to measure impact on velocity and quality. Results from production show development cycles accelerated (lead times <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\approx 40 \%$</tex> shorter) and throughput increased <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(\approx 15 \%$</tex> more work items completed), while defect rates and incident resolution times improved. These findings offer a practical roadmap for safely boosting developer productivity and operational efficiency with AI, demonstrating that with appropriate controls, generative AI tools can dramatically speed up delivery without compromising software quality.

title: Predicting Academic Success of College Students Using MLP Technique
authors: Vidhya, Praveen Kumar Kaithal
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Internet of Things: Smart Innovation and Usages (IoT-SIU)
abstract: Prediction of the academic performance of the group of college students has become one of the urgent issues of the educational establishment to enhance the results and reduce the process of dropouts. In the next paper, it is seen how a model of students academic performance based on demographic information, previous academic performance and other behavioral indicators is made and predicted using the Multilayer Perceptron (MLP) neural network. The new proposed MLP algorithm can be trained and tested on top of actual world data and the proposed algorithm is more predictive than the classical machine learning classifiers. The findings offer potential solutions on how learning algorithms of deep learning can be used in educational data mining offering viable recommendations to academic temptors and policy makers, on how to take people meat timely interventions and actions to help students in need. The outcomes of simulations, in its turn, show that the MLP is miles ahead of the traditional machine learning approaches.

title: Engineering Student Success: The Interplay Between Academic Buoyancy and Personal Best Goals in Higher Education
authors: Xiao Zhou, Sidhu Gurnam Kaur
publish: 2025 IEEE 14th International Conference on Engineering Education (ICEED)
abstract: Tertiary students often face significant academic challenges that require both buoyancy and sustained motivation to achieve success. This study examines the interplay between academic buoyancy and personal best goals among engineering undergraduates. Adopting a quantitative approach, data were collected through a structured questionnaire and analyzed employing descriptive statistics and structural equation modeling (SEM). The findings revealed a statistically significant, moderate positive relationship between academic buoyancy and personal best goals, indicating that students with higher buoyancy were more likely to adopt personal best goals. The model demonstrated moderate explanatory power, with academic buoyancy accounting for 44.5% of the variance in PB goal orientation, while also showing large effect size and moderate predictive relevance. These findings imply that academic buoyancy is a key psychological factor that supports engineering students in setting and pursuing challenging self-improvement goals. This study adds to existing knowledge on student motivation within STEM fields by emphasizing how resilience-related factors facilitate academic achievement.

title: Coordinated Optimal Scheduling Method for Distribution Systems Based on the Analytic Target Cascading Method
authors: Nan Ning, Min Xie, Guohong Wang, Yun Rao, Keyang Chen
publish: 2025 25th Conference of the Electric Power Supply Industry (CEPSI)
abstract: With the development of distributed energy technology and the in-depth promotion of power system reform, the distribution network presents the characteristics of multiple stakeholders. Distribution network operators, distributed energy providers, combined cooling, heating and power (CCHP) load integrators and other distribution network entities have different interest needs. In order to describe the model of these stakeholders, realize the energy balance of cooling, heating and power in the distribution network, and maintain the safe and stable operation of the power system, a collaborative optimization scheduling method of distribution system based on analytic target cascading (ATC) is proposed. The distribution network operators, distributed energy providers and CCHP load integrators under the distribution network environment are modeled and the distribution system collaborative optimal scheduling is carried out. Each subject coordinates by exchanging contact variable information, and finally obtains the optimal collaborative optimal scheduling strategy. Finally, the proposed model and algorithm are simulated and verified based on an example of ieee33 distribution network system. It is shown that the coordinated optimal scheduling of the distribution system based on the ATC algorithm decouples and parallelly solves the liaison variables between each stakeholder and the distribution network operator, which greatly improves the calculation efficiency.

title: Eye Disease Classification based on Levenberg-Marquardt and One-Step Secant Models
authors: Yessi Jusman, Naelil Muna Aya Sofiya, Indah Arisanti, Julnila Husna Lubis
publish: 2025 2nd Beyond Technology Summit on Informatics International Conference (BTS-I2C)
abstract: Vision is essential for human health and daily functioning, yet untreated eye diseases can lead to severe visual impairment or blindness. This study developed an automated system for accurate eye disease classification, supporting early diagnosis. Images were pre-processed using Contrast Limited Adaptive Histogram Equalization (CLAHE), and 391 texture-based features were extracted using Histogram of Oriented Gradients (HOG). Classification was performed using two multilayer perceptron (MLP) models: Levenberg-Marquardt (MLP-LM) and One-Step Secant (MLP-OSS), with hidden layer configurations of 10 and 20 nodes. Evaluation using accuracy, confusion matrices, and ROC curves demonstrated that MLP-OSS with 20 hidden nodes achieved the highest testing accuracy of 78.9% ± 3.03%, outperforming other configurations. These results indicated that MLP-OSS has provided robust generalization and reliable performance, offering a promising tool for supporting clinical decision-making in the early diagnosis of eye diseases.

title: Dynamic Brightest Intensity ROI for Smartphone-Based PPG: A Comparative Analysis of Frame Selection Methods for Hemoglobin Estimation and Anemia Classification
authors: Nipuni Vithana, Rasika Rajapaksha
publish: 2025 International Conference on Advances in Technology and Computing (ICATC)
abstract: Smartphone photoplethysmography (sPPG) has become one of the promising non-invasive methods of estimating hemoglobin and screening for anemia, especially where resources are limited. However, extracting PPG signals from smartphone fingertip videos is extremely sensitive to frame quality, changes in illumination and motion artifacts. This study fills a literature gap by introducing a frame selection algorithm, Dynamic Brightest Intensity Region of Interest (ROI) and compares its performance with five existing algorithms namely Fixed Center ROI, Right to Left ROI, Temporal Difference ROI, Template Matching ROI and Frame Adaptive ROI. The latter methodology uses a set of data augmentation and preprocessing techniques to improve the quality of PPG signals obtained based on fingertip videos. The signals are then processed by physiologically-informed feature extraction and then refined by systematic feature selection to accurately estimate hemoglobin and classify anemia severity by using XGBoost and Artificial Neural Networks.In this study, Frame Adaptive ROI method gave the best predictive performance; however, it involves intensive pixel-level operations and dynamic masking, which makes the method time-consuming and more computationally intensive. Conversely, the suggested Dynamic Brightest Intensity ROI algorithm showed a desirable trade-off between accuracy and efficiency with a high level of hemoglobin regression (RMSE = 0.5043) and anemia severity classification (accuracy = 0.9783). It has a reduced computational complexity, and thus is better applicable in real-time or mobile deployment environments where processing time is a key factor. These results indicate that the dynamic selection of the frame is significant in enhancing the quality of the signal.

title: Sentiment-enhanced multi-class Transformer model for efficient suicide detection in Reddit texts
authors: Peizhi Yan, Tala Talaei Khoei, Aditi Singh
publish: 2025 International Conference on Electrical and Computer Engineering Researches (ICECER)
abstract: This study presents a transformer-based framework for suicide risk stratification using Reddit data, addressing limitations in existing binary classification approaches and emotional nuance modeling. Leveraging 70,000 posts from mental health subreddits as weakly supervised risk indicators, we integrate VADER sentiment tokens directly into model inputs and implement hybrid text normalization to preserve platform-specific semantics. Six transformer architectures were evaluated, with BERT, RoBERTa, and ELECTRA achieving 94.27% accuracy in multi-class risk categorization. Key innovations include explicit emotion-content associations through prepended sentiment labels ([VERY NEG] to [VERY POS]) and efficient processing of noisy user-generated content. DistilBERT demonstrated optimal efficiency-accuracy balance with 94.16% accuracy score. The framework enables granular detection of emotional escalation patterns, offering computational feasibility for real-world deployment while maintaining clinical relevance through alignment with suicidality continua. Error analysis highlights persistent challenges in distinguishing semantically adjacent risk categories, informing future directions for context-aware mental health monitoring systems.

title: Layer-2 Optimized NFT Lending with Risk-Aware Smart Contracts and Interoperable Blockchain Protocols
authors: Palaniappan Sellappan, Kavitha Shanmugam, Vishnu Periyannan Palaniappan
publish: 2025 World Conference on Cutting-Edge Science and Technology (WCCEST)
abstract: The rise of non-fungible tokens (NFTs) has moved beyond digital art into decentralized finance (DeFi) for loans. NFTs are now commonly used as collateral in many decentralized lending platforms in DeFi. The paper evaluates three lending protocols: NFTfi, Arcade, and BendDAO in detail. These platforms show different methods for risk mitigation, liquidation, and borrower-lender interactions. This paper suggests a detailed risk assessment framework for NFT collateral in DeFi lending platforms. It highlights NFT illiquidity, high volatility, valuation uncertainty, and protocol design problems. The use of Layer -2 blockchain based lending model results in reduced gas fees and supports scalability. It is specifically optimized for networks like Polygon that offer higher throughput and lower operational costs. Layer-2 deployment facilitates faster processing speeds and significantly reduces transaction fees for users. The framework integrates interoperable blockchain protocols that support seamless NFT collateral migration across chains. This cross-chain operability increases platform liquidity and lending flexibility for decentralized finance participants. Dynamic, risk-aware smart contracts modify lending terms in real time using asset volatility indicators. They assess borrower risk profiles with on-chain data to maintain adaptive, secure lending conditions. Monte Carlo simulation is used to estimate default risks and delays in NFT liquidation. This simulation helps understand results when the market conditions keep changing. Findings say lending platforms need dynamic risk settings and strong pricing oracles for safety. Smart, borrower behavior-based lending strategies are also needed for steady growth in NFT backed DeFi lending. This paper also provides insights into use of advance techniques beyond smart contracts to enhance the system. This research gives useful ideas to DeFi developers, investors, and regulators handling NFT collateral. It helps people understand NFT lending risks better and enable us to design stronger lending systems.

title: Wind Power Forecasting Through Machine Learning and Deep Learning: A Hybrid Model Approach
authors: Pradeep Kumar Mehta, Ranjeet Kumar Osari
publish: 2025 World Conference on Cutting-Edge Science and Technology (WCCEST)
abstract: Wind energy forecasting plays a vital role in optimizing energy management and ensuring grid stability. This study presents a comprehensive evaluation of Machine Learning (ML) and Deep Learning (DL) models for wind power prediction using SCADA data from a wind turbine in Turkey. We assess multiple ML models, including Linear Regression, Decision Tree, and Random Forest, alongside advanced DL architectures such as a Hybrid Convolutional Neural Network and Bidirectional Long Short-Term Memory (Hybrid CNN-BiLSTM). The performance of these models is rigorously evaluated using key metrics: Mean Squared Error (MSE), Root Mean Squared Error (RMSE), Mean Absolute Error (MAE), R-squared (R<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup>), and Mean Absolute Percentage Error (MAPE). Our findings demonstrate that hybrid deep learning models significantly outperform traditional ML approaches, highlighting their superior capability in wind energy forecasting. The proposed model was evaluated on different parameters, on which it performs approximately 96% accuracy over traditional. model. This research contributes to more accurate and efficient renewable energy utilization, supporting better integration into power systems.

title: A Machine Learning Approach for Classifying Broadleaf and Coniferous Forests in Meghalaya Using Multispectral Data
authors: Khrawkupar Rani, Ngamklet Nonglamin, Khwairakpam Amitab
publish: 2025 World Conference on Cutting-Edge Science and Technology (WCCEST)
abstract: Accurate classification of forest types is critical for effective resource management and conservation. This study focuses on the multi-class classification of broadleaf and coniferous forests in Meghalaya, India, using Sentinel-2 multispectral imagery and a range of machine learning models. Sentinel-2's spectral bands, including visible, near-infrared, and shortwave infrared, were used as input features to distinguish between the forest types. This study evaluates traditional machine learning models, including Random Forest, Support Vector Machine, k-Nearest Neighbors, XGBoost, and Artificial Neural Networks, alongside advanced deep learning models such as FT-Transformer and TabNet. We have also employed ensemble methods, voting, and stacking ensembles with XGB as a meta-learner. Among all models, FT-Transformer achieved the highest accuracy of 97%, followed by stacking ensembles and voting ensembles with 96% accuracy. A forest cover map of Meghalaya was generated using the FT-Transformer model. Analysis of the map reveals that broadleaf forest covers 61.96%, coniferous forest covers 13.83%, and non-forest areas make up the remaining 24.21% of the surface area.

title: Autonomous Regulation of Energy Storage Units: An Analysis of Systemic Control in Electric Mobility
authors: Abhishek Baer
publish: 2025 World Conference on Cutting-Edge Science and Technology (WCCEST)
abstract: This document investigates the intelligent regulation of energy storage units within electric vehicles, focusing on the systemic control mechanisms that govern battery operation. It elucidates the methodologies for assessing charge status, health parameters, and longevity predictions, and it also explores the critical role of thermal equilibrium maintenance. The study further examines the integration of computational algorithms in optimizing energy utilization and ensuring operational safety, addressing the challenges of battery degradation and the pursuit of enhanced vehicle autonomy.

title: Resilient Pattern Mining
authors: Pengxin Bian, Panagiotis Charalampopoulos, Lorraine A. K. Ayad, Manal Mohamed, Solon P. Pissis, Grigorios Loukides
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Data Mining (ICDM)
abstract: Frequent pattern mining is a flagship problem in data mining. In its most basic form, it asks for the set of substrings of a given string <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$S$</tex> of length <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$n$</tex> that occur at least <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\tau$</tex> times in <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$S$</tex>, for some integer <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\tau\epsilon[1,n]$</tex>. We introduce a resilient version of this classic problem, which we term the <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(\tau,\ k)$</tex>-Resilient Pattern Mining (rpm) problem. Given a string <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$S$</tex> of length <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$n$</tex> and two integers <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\tau, k\in[1, n\vert$</tex>, RPM asks for the set of substrings of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$S$</tex> that occur at least <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\tau$</tex> times in <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$S$</tex>, even when the letters at any <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$k$</tex> positions of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$S$</tex> are substituted by other letters. Unlike frequent substrings, resilient ones account for the fact that changes to string <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$S$</tex> are often expensive to handle or are unknown. We make the following contributions. First, we present RPM-DP, a simple exact <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathrm{O}(n^{{3}}k\log n)$</tex>-time and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathrm{O}(n^{2})$</tex>-space algorithm for RPM that is based on an existing dynamic programming algorithm. Second, we propose RPM-ESA, an exact <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathrm{O}(n\log n)$</tex> -time and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathrm{O}(n)$</tex> -space algorithm for RPM, which employs advanced data structures and combinatorial insights. Third, we conduct experiments on real large-scale datasets from different domains demonstrating that: (I) The notion of resilient substrings is useful in analyzing genomic data and fundamentally different from that of frequent substrings, as frequent substrings are often not resilient and thus do not remain frequent for long in versioned datasets; (II) RPM-ESA is several orders of magnitude faster and more space-efficient than RPM-DP; and (III) Clustering based on resilient substrings is effective.

title: Learn while Unlearn: An Iterative Unlearning Framework for Generative Language Models
authors: Haoyu Tang, Ye Liu, Xi Zhao, Xukai Liu, Yanghai Zhang, Kai Zhang, Xiaofang Zhou, Enhong Chen
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Data Mining (ICDM)
abstract: Recent advances in machine learning, particularly in Natural Language Processing (NLP), have produced powerful models trained on vast datasets. However, these models risk leaking sensitive information, raising privacy concerns. In response, regulatory measures such as the European Union's General Data Protection Regulation (GDPR) have driven increasing interest in Machine Unlearning techniques, which enable models to selectively forget specific data entries. Early unlearning approaches primarily relied on pre-processing methods, while more recent research has shifted towards training-based solutions. Despite their effectiveness, a key limitation persists: most methods require access to original training data, which is often unavailable. Additionally, directly applying unlearning techniques bears the cost of undermining the model's expressive capabilities. To address these challenges, we introduce the Iterative Contrastive Unlearning (ICU) framework, which consists of three core components: A Knowledge Unlearning Induction module designed to target specific knowledge for removal using an unlearning loss; A Contrastive Learning Enhancement module to preserve the model's expressive capabilities against the pure unlearning goal; And an Iterative Unlearning Refinement module that dynamically adjusts the unlearning process through ongoing evaluation and updates. Experimental results demonstrate the efficacy of our ICU method in unlearning sensitive information while maintaining the model's overall performance, offering a promising solution for privacy-conscious machine learning applications.

title: Semantic Textual Similarity and Unsupervised Text Segmentation: A Hybrid Approach
authors: Rama Krishna Vankamamidi, P.M.S.S. Chandu, M. Giri, K. Ammulu, M. Srinivasa Rao, M.L.M. Prasad
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent and Innovative Technologies in Computing, Electrical and Electronics (IITCEE)
abstract: The day-by-day large collection of is generated by many social media tools that need proper technique to process gathered information, analyzing information, and provide information as and when required. In this paper we developed hybrid approach that combines by calculating similarity of textual information with NLP technique to detect semantics of required text parts and also maintaining accuracy while discovering information. We adopted statistical measures with NLP to overcome the problems that are faced by the similar approaches like topic modelling and search using keywords. Experiments are conducted and our proposed approach outperform than other models. The model is recommended for many social media or online application domain for discovering needed information by maintaining accuracy.

title: Rule-Based Reasoning for Personalized Learning in a Web-Based Programming Environment
authors: Hui Kit Yuen, Hang Man, Ronnie Cheung
publish: 2025 IEEE 14th International Conference on Engineering Education (ICEED)
abstract: This paper presents PleJ, a personalized e-learning framework designed to deliver a Java programming course that adapts to individual student learning paces and preferences. The framework features a three-level Java course, enabling course promotion and demotion based on student performance, thereby facilitating diverse learning pathways. PleJ leverages ontologies for the user model, domain model, and adaptation model, integrated within a rule-based expert system shell. This integration combines ontology reasoning with rule-based inference to deliver a personalized adaptive learning environment. The system incorporates web-based programming facilities, where student interactions within a programming environment are logged and synchronized with cloud storage for performance assessment. To evaluate learners for course progression, PleJ analyzes successive program compilation and debugging behaviors using a sophisticated scoring approach. Our implementation demonstrates the effectiveness of integrating and reusing information for adaptive learning within a web-based programming environment.

title: Comparative Forecasting of Electric Vehicle Charging Demand Using Regression Models
authors: Parth Goyal, Ria Chadha, Anuradha Tomar
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Internet of Things: Smart Innovation and Usages (IoT-SIU)
abstract: This study examined the charging demands of electric vehicles (EV) over the past 10 years in a city and its effect on overall load patterns. The aim is to compare forecasting models and see which one offers the most reliable long term prediction of EV charging demand. With more people using EVs, it is important to estimate future charging behavior so that the grid can be managed properly. This creates a need to compare different forecasting methods in a structured way. In this paper a detailed comparative analysis of three regressionbased forecasting models was conducted. Model performance was evaluated using standard metrics like the root mean squared error (RMSE). Our evaluation shows that bagged trees achieved the best performance (RMSE $=0.035$), with the next best performance by fine trees $(\mathrm{RMSE}=0.894)$ and robust linear ($\mathbf{R M S E} \boldsymbol{=} \mathbf{1. 1 1 6}$). The forecasts point toward increasing charging demand up to 2035 stressing the need for effective grid planning, infrastructure planning and sustainable EV ecosystem development.

title: Blockchain-Enabled and Paperless Identity Systems for Environmental Sustainability
authors: Deniz Salucu, Tunga Sayıcı
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Metaverse, and Cybersecurity (ICAMAC)
abstract: The rapid rise of adoption of digital identity presents a transformative opportunity to eliminate resource-intensive physical identity systems, no longer requiring printed cards, plastic credentials, or in-person office visits, thereby substantially reducing carbon footprints. This paper introduces a comprehensive Self-Sovereign Identity (SSI) model built upon Hyperledger Indy, enabling end users to securely store and manage their identities directly on personal devices. We explore two different enrollment methods: QR code-based digital credential issuance and NFC-powered chip-based identity verification, further enhanced through zero-knowledge proofs and verifiable credential protocols. The ecological advantages are emphasized through the eradication of physical identity artifacts and associated administrative processes, advancing the field of green digital technologies in support of ecological preservation and sustainable identity infrastructures.

title: Extracting Actionable Insights from Building Energy Data using Vision LLMs on Wavelet and 3D Recurrence Representations
authors: Amine Bechar, Adel Oulefki, Abbes Amira, Fatih Kurogollu, Yassine Himeur
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Data Mining (ICDM)
abstract: The analysis of complex building time-series for actionable insights and recommendations remains challenging due to the nonlinear and multi-scale characteristics of energy data. To address this, we propose a framework that fine-tunes visual language large models (VLLMs) on 3D graphical representations of the data. The approach converts 1D time-series into 3D representations using continuous wavelet transforms (CWTs) and recurrence plots (RPs), which capture temporal dynamics and localize frequency anomalies. These 3D encodings enable VLLMs to visually interpret energy-consumption patterns, detect anomalies, and provide recommendations for energy efficiency. We demonstrate the framework on real-world building-energy datasets, where fine-tuned VLLMs successfully monitor building states, identify recurring anomalies, and generate optimization recommendations. Quantitatively, the Idefics-7B VLLM achieves validation losses of 0.0952 with CWTs and 0.1064 with RPs on the University of Sharjah energy dataset, outperforming direct fine-tuning on raw time-series data (0.1176) for anomaly detection. This work bridges time-series analysis and visualization, providing a scalable and interpretable framework for energy analytics.

title: SheSafe: Empowering Women's Safety Through Intelligent Technology
authors: Vishnu Priya K R, Sanjana Murthy, Chaitra M
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent and Innovative Technologies in Computing, Electrical and Electronics (IITCEE)
abstract: Women's safety has become a major concern in modern society, with existing safety applications often being limited to manual activation. This paper presents an Enhanced Hybrid Women Safety System which combines both Artificial Intelligence (AI) and Internet of Things (IoT) to enable an autonomous and silent distress detection module. The AI module uses an voice emotion recognition using a Convolutional Neural Network-Long Short-Term Memory (CNN-LSTM) architecture to perform emotional analysis in speech and recognize patterns. A user-defined keyword acts as a trigger to activate the distress detection module, upon which the system classifies the emotional state as distress or neutral. Simultaneously, the IoT component uses a Bluetooth Low Energy (BLE) button embedded in a wearable device that allows users to initiate emergency alerts in quiet places. The system integrates GPS-based location tracking, SMS notifications, and audio recording to ensure timely assistance. Experimental results show an accuracy of approximately 90 % for distress detection and classification, with an alert dispatch of average 2-3 seconds. The proposed model guarantees reliability, responsiveness, and accessibility in emergency situations, offering a significant improvement over traditional mobile-based safety system.

title: AI-Powered Noise-Specific Hearing Protection
authors: Prajkta P. Dandavate, Atharva S. Gilbile, Tejas N. Bapat, Atharva R. Kadu, Rohit P. Bansod, Om S. Bathe, Suraj B. Bhanagre
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Artificial Intelligence for Innovations in Healthcare Industries (ICAIIHI)
abstract: The Noise-Specific Hearing Protection project aims to provide a new, nature-inspired manner to enhance hearing protection with the smart filtering of unwanted noise. To do this, they adapt artificial intelligence, machine learning, and real-time digital signal processing (DSP) to selectively filter out damaging noise frequencies while preserving the contribution of meaningful signals like human voice and ambient context. Novelty is created from small and easily integrated microphone arrays and AI on wearable technology that is expected to disrupt how hearing protection is enhanced in industry, military, air travel, building, and everyday consumer domains. Our intended outcome is to enhance access to hearing protection by negotiating safety and situational awareness. Despite hurdles with power source, time delay, and miniaturization, this is a significant advancement and disruptive technology to break down communication barriers in high decibel environments and more importantly, protect against hearing loss. The research shows that on-device AI implemented on tiny microcontrollers can offer a practical, low-cost alternative to complex industrial hearing protection systems.

title: A Deep Reinforcement Learning Approach for Optimizing the Placement of 5G-Enabled UAVs in Disaster Scenarios
authors: Mohamed Karam Behaz, Fatima Zahra Rabahi
publish: 2026 IEEE 16th Annual Computing and Communication Workshop and Conference (CCWC)
abstract: In disaster situations, maintaining reliable communication is critical to support effective search and rescue operations. This paper presents the development and implementation of a Deep Q-Network (DQN) and Proximal Policy Optimization (PPO) methods for managing a fleet of unmanned aerial vehicles (UAVs) in a search and rescue (SAR) mission. The objective is to maximize the coverage of individuals in a designated area to restore communication using a 5 G network in a disaster scenario where traditional communication infrastructures have failed. The proposed methods are evaluated in a simulated 3D environment, demonstrating their effectiveness in optimizing coverage and enhancing the overall SAR mission performance.

title: Federated Explainable AI for Fair and Inclusive Credit Scoring: A Comprehensive Review
authors: Veeramani Sampathkumar, Ishan Prashant Pathak, Dinesh Kumar Ramaraj, Rajesh Kotha, Darshan Pankajkumar Patel
publish: 2026 IEEE 16th Annual Computing and Communication Workshop and Conference (CCWC)
abstract: Over 150 artificial intelligence (AI) applications in financial services caught on within 2020–2025 due to digital transformation during and after the pandemic. Credit scoring systems that use machine learning (ML) to predict credit scores have enhanced predictive accuracy, which can be over 85–90 percent AUC on standard benchmark dataset, but are associated with significant issues of privacy, lack of transparency, and bias. Federated learning (FL) became a privacy-preserving framework, which allows training a model in a decentralized manner that does not require the sharing of raw data. Simultaneously, explainable AI (XAI) methods, including SHAP, LIME, and counterfactual reasoning, became popular in explaining complex models and making them regulator friendly. As legal systems, such as the EU AI Act and fair lending legislation in the United States, have established a set of legal standards to guarantee fairness, the necessity to measure these trade-offs between privacy, interpretability, and equity has become more pronounced. Even with the swift progress, studies in FL, XAI, and fairness were not connected to one another, but did not have unified benchmarks and cross-domain solutions. The paper includes a systematic review of 25 peer-reviewed studies which include 20 peer-reviewed articles and 5 contextual sources articles that have been published in 2020–25 and converge at the intersection of credit scoring. It determines that there are long-standing gaps, such as fairness-conscious aggregation in FL, explainability in privacy limits and bias detection across institutions. To overcome them, we introduce Federated Explainable AI (FedXAI), a conceptual framework that integrates the metrics of fairness (e.g. equal opportunity), explainability instruments (e.g. SHAP fidelity scores), and differential privacy into the federated learning cycle. FedXAI promotes inclusive, auditable, and human-centered credit scoring, which forms the basis of benchmarking in the future, regulatory alignment, and practical implementation.

title: Revolutionizing Diabetic Retinopathy Screening for Early Vision Preservation by Integrating Convolution Neural Network
authors: Pooja A, Dhanajaya V
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent and Innovative Technologies in Computing, Electrical and Electronics (IITCEE)
abstract: Diabetes is one of the increasing serious health issues, affecting millions of people. Consequently, the greater portion of individual is affected by diabetes, some of the related issues such as cardiovascular disease, kidney failure, nerve damage, foot problems and vision problems are growing rapidly. Diabetic retinopathy (DR) is a severe complication of diabetes and a leading cause of preventable blindness worldwide. Early detection requires expert evaluation of retinal fundus images, but limited access to specialists delays diagnosis. This work presents an automated DR screening pipeline using pertained Convolutional Neural Networks (CNNs), including VGG16, VGG19, and ResNet50, for five-class DR severity classification. Using 3,662 Kaggle fundus images with systematic preprocessing, augmentation, and class balancing, the proposed approach achieves a highest accuracy of 96.3 % and F1-score of 0.898. The model supports improved early diagnosis by enabling scalable, automated retinal image screeningto enhance primary diagnosis and minimizes blindness risk.

title: Cloud-Based Learning Management Systems for Scalable Education
authors: T. Raghu, S.M. Srilanga Meenatshi, Kahmeera Shaik, Dipanjana Biswas, Naresh Goke, Sumit Surve S
publish: 2025 World Conference on Cutting-Edge Science and Technology (WCCEST)
abstract: This empirical research paper explores the scalability of and performance of cloud-based Learning Management Systems (LMS) in the educational context based on the secondary data and quantitative research. The results indicate that the cloud-based platforms are more effective than the traditional systems in terms of scalability (0.72-0.94), 3,500-7,500 concurrent users and system uptime (99.2-99.9%). The study determines the best performance levels and major aspects of scalability. Moreover, cloud-based LMSs provide 20-35% annual cost savings but high performance and user satisfaction rates above 4.2/5.0, which provides efficient, large-scale digital learning solutions to education institutes.

title: Sentiment and Social Network Analysis on Public Perception of the Access by KAI Application
authors: Thesion Marta Sianipar, Amalia Nur Alifah, Rifdatun Ni'Mah, Ananda Taqhsya Dwiyana, Aulia Wulandari
publish: 2025 2nd Beyond Technology Summit on Informatics International Conference (BTS-I2C)
abstract: Digitalization in public transportation is accelerating in Indonesia, yet there is a lack of comprehensive studies examining how public sentiment and social dynamics influence perceptions of these digital services. One prominent initiative is the Access by KAI application, developed by PT Kereta Api Indonesia to facilitate ticket booking and enhance travel experiences This study uses a dual approach, combining sentiment analysis and social network analysis (SNA), to assess user perception of the app. Sentiment analysis, conducted on 9,835 entries from Twitter and Google Play Store collected between July 16, 2014 and April 4, 2025 (Google Play Store) and between January and March 2025 (Twitter) using a BERT-based classifier, reveals that 61.7% of public responses are negative, often linked to login failures, payment issues, and app instability. Meanwhile, 20% are positive, highlighting usability and convenience, and 18.3% are neutral. SNA shows that while @KAI121 serves as the central information node, verified institutional accounts and individual influencers significantly contribute to opinion formation. These findings demonstrate that user discourse is shaped not only by official communication but also by decentralized community interactions. The study addresses a research gap by combining sentiment and network analysis in the Indonesian transport context and offers actionable recommendations for PT KAI to enhance user satisfaction through inclusive, responsive, and community-aware digital strategies.

title: Real-Time Fraud Detection in Digital Banking using Explainable Machine Learning and Data Engineering Pipeline
authors: Afam Okonkwo
publish: 2025 International Conference on Electrical and Computer Engineering Researches (ICECER)
abstract: As digital solutions grow and become indispensable to our everyday lives, personal and business information has also become omnipresent. Some of this information includes payment and financial data, which is increasingly vulnerable to exposure to fraudsters. However, many financial institutions, practitioners, and researchers have, over the years, tried to tighten their cybersecurity measures to mitigate and potentially reduce the number of fraudulent transactions. Indeed, they have adopted various approaches, including rule-based, human-in-the-loop, and more recently, machine-learning and deep-learning-based approaches. Nevertheless, financial analysts have seen many of these models as 'black-box' models whose inner workings are not transparent; thus, there is still some gap regarding exploring more explainable, interpretable, resilient, distributed, and real-time fraud-detection systems to promptly report any suspicious transaction in order to enable the financial analysts to act quickly. In this work, a combination of explainable and hybrid machine-learning approaches, along with a data-engineering pipeline, was adopted to immediately report any fraudulent transaction in real-time (in less than a second) before the transaction is committed. With an average F1-score of 98% and an AUC of 96%, the adopted explainable, interpretable, and real-time approach can inspire microfinance and other financial institutions to tighten their defenses.

title: Energy Consumption Minimization in 5G Cellular Networks via Multi-Objective Machine Learning for Dynamic Resource Allocation
authors: Tiyas Sarkar, Raghu Raj Sharma, Ravi Shanker, Pallavi Koundal, Kapil Jairath, Sunil Thakur
publish: 2025 World Conference on Cutting-Edge Science and Technology (WCCEST)
abstract: Energy consumption Minimization is becoming one of the most important parameters in network design as the 5G wireless communication systems require unprecedented levels of data throughput rates, ultra-low latency performance, and the ability to connect numerous devices, simultaneously. This research builds hybrid machine learning architectures specially in the area of intelligent radio resource management in lieu of both channel assignment and transmit power optimization with a combination of twin goals, such as decrease of network energy use and ensuring terrific quality-of-service guarantees. The 5G network infrastructures possessed today initially have a troublesome intricacy of heterogeneous user equipment requirements, fluctuated time-traffic designation, and variably dispersed propagation circumstances. The traditional optimization strategies have been found lacking in flexibility to such changing operational circumstances. This study suggests a promising multi-paradigm machine learning system which embraces the supervised classification approach, unsupervised clustering algorithm and deep reinforcement learning agents to develop self-sustaining and high-capacity resource allocation behaviours. The specified hybrid architecture starts resource provisioning with the assistance of supervised learning modules that are implemented with historical datasets of network performances to determine the most basic policies of the channel and power assignment. Thereafter, deep Q-network reinforcement learning agents carry out the task of updating continuous policy starting with the real-time input of the network state data (user mobility patterns, variations in traffic load, channel quality indicators). Simultaneously, unguided k-means clustering algorithms help to generate the pattern of user distribution by space and give out hotspots of the traffic to bring improved granularity of the resources in the ultra-dense deployment. Such built-in machine learning allows predictive network optimization functionality with endogenous adjustment to the environmental noise and excellent computational performance. It is revealed after testing comprehensive system-level simulations that aggregate network energy consumption is reduced by 30 percent as compared to that of conventional greedy allocation algorithms but without a detrimental impact on the throughput performance of users or latency requirements. The developed framework shows a technologically viable solution to fundamental trade-off between performance requirements and energy sustainability goals in next generation wireless systems that are required to work under increasingly higher capacity demands and worst under increasingly strict environmental rules.

title: DynaCLIP: A Novel Framework for Video-Text Retrieval Via Dynamic Curriculum Learning and Adaptive Prompt Mixture-of-Experts
authors: Ning Chen, Xingming Liao, Chengzhong Lin, Zhuowei Wang, Peijian Zeng
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Data Mining (ICDM)
abstract: Understanding and modeling time remains a key challenge in today's video understanding systems. With language playing a central role in driving powerful generalization, foundational video-language models must inherently capture a sense of temporality. However, Video-text retrieval faces three challenges: Parameter efficiency bottlenecks (traditional prompt learning requires adjusting over 10 % of parameters, leading to a sharp increase in computational costs), limitations of static course strategies (manually defined difficulty thresholds cause pseudo-label biases in highly abstract actions), and insufficient adaptability to heterogeneous architectures (cross-modal hybrid expert frameworks rely on shared weights, restricting the flexible combination of heterogeneous models such as CLIP and ViT). To address these challenges, this paper proposes the Dynamic Curriculum Learning with Adaptive Prompt Mixture of Experts (DynaCLIP) framework. Through the collaborative optimization of the Adaptive Prompt Mixture of Experts (APMoE) module and dynamic curriculum learning, the parameter efficiency and dynamic adaptability are significantly improved. APMoE adopts orthogonal initialization expert pools and cross-modal routing networks. It freezes the pre-trained backbone networks (such as CLIP/BERT) on the premise of fine-tuning only less than 1 % of parameters (static expert pool + dynamic prompt generator), and generates instance-aware text encoder prompts by dynamically selecting Top-k experts and weighted fusion. Here, these instanceaware prompts are implemented as continuous soft prompts in the embedding space, rather than discrete natural language text. Meanwhile, a two-dimensional difficulty quantification system is constructed based on the verb abstraction level (VerbNet three-level classification) and BERT semantic similarity. Combined with the improved binary search strategy to balance high-confidence samples and diversity exploration, a cognitiveinspired course learning mechanism is formed. Experiments show that DynaCLIP achieves 7.4 % R@1 on the TEMPO dataset, 67.5 % accuracy in temporal inference. The code is available at: https://github.com/18162195164/DynaCLIP.

title: A Data-Free Analytical Quantization Scheme for Deep Learning Models
authors: Ahmed Luqman, Khuzemah Hassan Qazi, Murray Patterson, Malik Jahan Khan, Imdadullah Khan
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Data Mining (ICDM)
abstract: Despite the success of CNN models on a variety of image classification and segmentation tasks, their extensive computational and storage demands pose considerable challenges for real-world deployment on resource-constrained devices. Quantization is one technique that aims to alleviate these large storage requirements and speed up the inference process by reducing the precision of model parameters to lower-bit representations. In this paper, we introduce a novel post-training quantization method for model weights. Our method finds optimal clipping thresholds and scaling factors along with mathematical guarantees and minimizes quantization noise. Empirical results on real-world datasets demonstrate that our quantization scheme significantly reduces model size and computational requirements while preserving model accuracy.

title: STRIDE: Spectral-Temporal Representation and Instance-Weighted Domain Expansion
authors: Doan Ngoc Chi Nam, Tran Van Tung, Karkulali Pugalenthi
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Data Mining (ICDM)
abstract: Industrial time series applications such as fault diagnosis and equipment monitoring often suffer from severe performance degradation when machine learning models are transferred across varying operating conditions, sensor setups, or machinery. This challenge stems from domain shifts and the scarcity of labeled data in the target environment. To address this, we introduce STRIDE: Spectral-Temporal Representation and Instance-weighted Domain Expansion—a novel framework designed for few-shot unsupervised domain adaptation (FSUDA). STRIDE employs a temporal encoder to capture sequential de-pendencies, constructs source-domain prototypes to guide feature alignment, and integrates entropy-based instance reweighting to select reliable pseudo-labeled target examples. It further enhances generalization by combining spectral and temporal MixUp strate-gies, improving robustness without the need for target labels. We evaluate STRIDE on two industrial benchmark datasets—CWRU and Paderborn University—which represent realistic domain shifts in sensor type, load, and rotational speed. Results demonstrate that STRIDE consistently outperforms competitive baselines in accuracy and stability, even with as few as 5–10 unlabeled target samples per class. Its efficiency and modularity make it highly applicable for real-world deployment in data-scarce, domain-variant industrial environments.

title: An artificial intelligence model for software project team formation considering skill assessments
authors: Tiago da Rosa Santos, Avanilde Kemczinski, Rafael Stubs Parpinelli
publish: 2025 International Conference on Electrical and Computer Engineering Researches (ICECER)
abstract: Team formation is one of the main challenges in software project management, especially when it comes to allocating professionals with specific skills in complex and dynamic contexts. This work presents TeamFit, an artificial intelligence-based solution that uses the Random Forest algorithm to recommend team formations aligned with the skills required for projects. The model is fed with data from skills assessments conducted within the PSAS (Peer Skills Assessment System) framework, which allows considering both the historical performance and the temporal evolution of individuals. The proposed solution aims to mitigate limitations of traditional methods, such as selection bias and lack of knowledge of the profile of individuals, by integrating predictive and visualization mechanisms to support the project manager’s decision-making. The evaluation of the solution with simulated data demonstrates high generalization capacity and accuracy in team formation recommendations, indicating its potential for application in organizational and educational contexts.

title: Behavioural Biometrics: A Gait Recognition Approach
authors: Kuntal Gaur, Pradeep Kumar, Shivansh Tripathi, Aryan Tyagi, Khushi Sharma
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Metaverse, and Cybersecurity (ICAMAC)
abstract: In this research the authors presented a new frame-work for gait recognition which leverages the WHU Gait Dataset of gait with clothing, carrying conditions, and viewpoints to illustrate our multi-stream method, which contains silhouette information using appearance-based models and motion dynamics, together with optical flow motion dynamics in the multi-stream and fusing out the models at the end to bolster gait recognition robustness in difficult conditions. We utilize a data set appropriate data augmentation strategy on the WHU dataset to establish the efficacy of gait recognition models. Our experimental conclusion demonstrate that our framework is computationally efficient and competitively accurate given testing across settings compared to Deep Learning methods. The applicability of the framework to biomechanics, while being interpretable, and robust to real-world gait variation further suggests future applicability in biometrics systems comfortable in both surveillance and authentication.

title: User Perceptions of Neuromorphic and Flat User Interface Designs: A Comparative Study
authors: Mia Rovis, Tihomir Orehovački
publish: 2025 International Conference on Electrical and Computer Engineering Researches (ICECER)
abstract: This study examines differences in user perceptions of neuromorphic and flat user interface (UI) styles within an e-commerce context. A within-subjects experiment involving 103 participants compared two prototypes across seven constructs: aesthetic appeal, intuitiveness, clarity, innovativeness, enjoyment, ease of use, and behavioural intention, using a structured questionnaire. Non-parametric Wilcoxon signed-rank tests showed that the neuromorphic variant was consistently perceived as more innovative, whereas the difference in clarity did not remain significant after correction for multiple comparisons. The flat prototype, however, elicited stronger behavioural intention, although this effect was robust only under false discovery rate correction. This outcome may reflect participants’ greater familiarity with established flat conventions, as most reported limited prior exposure to the neuromorphic style. No significant differences were observed for aesthetic appeal, intuitiveness, enjoyment, or ease of use. The findings indicate that neuromorphic elements can enhance perceptions of innovativeness, but their influence on behavioural intention remains limited, underscoring the need for context-sensitive integration. Such insights provide a basis for guiding the application of neuromorphic design in practice.

title: Economic Time Series Decomposition Using Seasonal ARIMA in Macro-Level Planning Models
authors: Gayrat Bekbergenov, K.Bhargava Triveni Nandana, Mamasidikova Naima Tokhirjon Kizi, Sureka M, Mariyam Ahmed
publish: 2025 International Conference on AI-Driven STEM Education and Learning Technologies (AISTEMEDU)
abstract: The macro-level indicators, including Gross Domestic Product (GDP), inflation, and employment, cannot be explained without economic time series decomposition. SARIMA models provide an efficient statistical methodology that can be used to trace such conditional seasonal economic trends. Nevertheless, the current procedures tend to ignore the complex seasonal correlations and cannot differentiate between transient and enduring economic shifts, which result in the inadvisory policy decisions. The current paper suggests a model of GDP growth pattern decomposition by the use of SARIMA to improve the accuracy of macroeconomic planning. The suggested approach is able to provide more accurate trend, seasonal, and residual separation, which is a major weakness of the previous models. The SARIMA-based decomposition can help the policymakers to develop data-based strategies of fiscal and monetary policies by exposing the economic dynamic that may have been supported by seasonal changes under the surface. SARIMA in the experimental results has a forecast accuracy of 6.5, an error rate of 11.5, the data processing time is 95 seconds and moderate computational complexity (5/10). These values prove that SARIMA is better than the available models in terms of accuracy and the efficiency of operation.

title: A Deep Learning Approach for Prediction of Neurodegenerative Disease Progression
authors: Vinay Kumar Singh, Manjushree Nayak, Veena Hada, Roshan Lal, Shalini Bahuguna, Sanjay Singh Rawat
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Artificial Intelligence for Innovations in Healthcare Industries (ICAIIHI)
abstract: Many Neurodegenerative disease like Alzheimer’s disease, Parkinson’s disease, amd amyotrophic lateral sclerosis (ALS) are generally quite difficult to provide solution as they have a very complex nature of progress. Disease progression is quite important to predict to that timely treatment and personalized planning for treatment can be done in time to get the desired outcomes. The traditional treatment methods are sometime not sufficient to handle the large amount of data that is generated from neuroimaging, genomics and clinical records. This paper proposes a framework based on deep learning to predict the neurodegenerative disease progression using multimodal data integration. The proposed method uses the convolutional neural networks (CNNs) for imaging data and recurrent neural networks (RNNs) for longitudinal clinical data. The proposed models give more accurate predictions based on deep learning algorithms and gives a valuable insight into the disease progression. This paper uses the data from open source data sets including the Alzheimer’s disease neuroimaging initiative (ADNI), for evaluating the performance of the algorithms. The results thus obtained show a significant improvement in the accuracy obtained in prediction of the disease progression as compared to the traditional approaches. Presented research gives an insight into the use of artificial intelligence (AI) for improving the early detection, monitoring and controlling of neurodegenerative diseases, and showing the way for more precise machine learning and an improvement in the human lisestyle for the affected patients.

title: Smart Olfactory Monitoring System for Early Hazardous Odor Detection in Elderly and Alzheimer’s Care Homes
authors: Gurusigaamani Ayyanar Muthulingam, Velmurugan Subbiah Parvathy, Jeya Mithra K, Rajesh Kannan L, Meenakshi B, Karthik Reddy U V S
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Artificial Intelligence for Innovations in Healthcare Industries (ICAIIHI)
abstract: The increasing prevalence of older adults and Alzheimer’s patients in residential care programs necessitates smart technology to monitor their environment and identify potentially hazardous situations in real-time. Most existing safety systems consist of single-parameter detection systems that are limited to detecting specific odorants and/or complex smell patterns that could indicate serious risks to the patients. This study describes a Smart Olfactory Monitoring System (SOMS) that leverages the MQ135 gas sensor, MQ02 gas sensor, DHT11 temperature-humidity sensor, flame detector as peripheral sensors to extract sensor data to the ESP32 microcontroller. SOMS pre-processes the data obtained from the various sensors, and an ultra-lightweight ML classifier is used to detect undesirable odor patterns and provide alerts via voice warnings and notifications through a mobile device. Controlled experimental assessment of the SOMS in simulated residential care environments demonstrated >92% detection accuracy; reduction in false alarms from 35% compared to existing single-parameter systems; and average alert time of 1.3 s. The findings demonstrate that SOMS is a low-cost, proactive, and effective approach for improving safety and early detection of hazards for older adults with Alzheimer’s.

title: From Noisy Labels to Reliable Predictions: A Hybrid Learning Approach for ICD-Based Disease Classification
authors: Velavolu Sravanthi, Dasari Venkata Lakshmi
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Artificial Intelligence for Innovations in Healthcare Industries (ICAIIHI)
abstract: Automating the categorization of diseases from descriptive medical titles supports clinical decision-making, epidemiological analysis, and digital health workflows. To explore this capability, we propose a comparative framework that combines traditional machine learning with modern deep learning techniques. The workflow starts with text preprocessing (normalization, tokeniza- tion, stopword removal, and lemmatization), followed by label encoding and the exclusion of underrepresented classes to achieve balanced training data. Baseline classifiers, including Linear Support Vector Classifier and Multinomial Naïve Bayes, are assessed with TF–IDF features. In parallel, neural architectures such as Convolutional Neural Networks (CNN), Long Short- Term Memory networks (LSTM), Bidirectional LSTM, and Transformer-based models are trained on sequences of padded tokens. All deep learning models are optimized with the Adam optimizer at a learning rate of 0.001. Evaluation uses accuracy, precision, recall, and F1-score to provide a thorough comparison. Findings indicate that deep learning methods generally outper- form traditional approaches, with the LSTM variant yielding the top accuracy and highlighting the value of sequential neural models for mapping textual disease descriptions to category labels.

title: Comparison of Different Database Management Systems in Terms of Suitability for Storing Iot Data on-Premise
authors: Anoop Kiran Angadi Manjunatha, Sarala T, Yasha Jyothi M Shirur
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent and Innovative Technologies in Computing, Electrical and Electronics (IITCEE)
abstract: The Internet of Things (IoT) is the latest technology. With the rapid development of this technology, IoT applications along with the end devices which are called sensors will generate massive amounts of data soon. But the solutions for managing, storing and using the gigantic amounts of data generated by these IoT devices are still immature and need to be researched upon. There are many types of databases like relational database management systems (RDBMS) which is termed as a traditional one these days. RDBMS has supported all the user requirements together with simplicity, robustness, flexibility, scalability, and performance. But the most limitation they're facing is their static schema which is making RDBMS not suitable for IoT applications. Likewise, there are NoSQL databases emerging in market and they have claimed to perform better than SQL or RDBMS databases. NoSQL databases are non-relational databases that schema free without the need to join multiple tables, easy replication support, and very easy to scale horizontally, etc. And the third kind of database system is the time-series databases that are specifically developed to handle the time series data which the IoT sensors produce. With the rise of IoT, time-series databases (TSDBs) are growing in popularity. Time series databases are developed to manage and analyze large amounts of time series data. However, it's not easy to choose the best database system and the best provider that is suitable for a specific IoT application.

title: Modern XGBoost-CNN Hybrid Stacking for Diabetes Risk Prediction Using BRFSS 2021
authors: A. Prathiba, V. Venkataraman, C. Bharathy, S. Raghavendran
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent and Innovative Technologies in Computing, Electrical and Electronics (IITCEE)
abstract: Diabetes, being an inveterate chronic condition, creates a serious worldwide health challenge. Therefore, its reliable and accurate forecast is essential for significant disease management and timely intervention. The publicly available 2021 BRFSS (Behavioral Risk Factor Surveillance System) dataset from Kaggle is used to mitigate the constraint of conventional models such as XGBoost, LightGBM, and Logistic Regression, which often fail to catch non-linear feature interactions and interdependencies among base learners. To address these difficulties, a strong stacking structure is put forward that combines modern XGBoost with histogram-based trees, LightGBM, and convolutional neural network (CNN)derived meta-features. To overcome data imbalance, multiple resampling methods were tested, including Random Oversampling, ADASYN, SMOTE, and the hybrid SMOTEENN approach, among which SMOTEENN exhibited outstanding performance in balancing class distribution while holding back data integrity. The outputs from base learners were subsequently combined using a Multi-Layer Perceptron (MLP), serving as a non-linear meta-learner, thereby increasing the model's generalization ability. Moreover, SHAP (SHapley Additive exPlanations) analysis was implemented to combine model behavior and identify the most significant features presenting to diabetes risk prediction. The outputs disclosed that features such as age, blood pressure, and body mass index (BMI) played a prominent role in classification results, improving the model's transparency and interpretability. The proposed hybrid structure attained exemplary metrics, including an accuracy of 0.951, an AUC of 0.907, a precision of 0.965, a recall of 0.983, and an F1-score of 0.974. These outputs displayed the advantages of integrating advanced gradient boosting algorithms, CNN-based meta-features, and non-linear ensemble integration for increased diabetes detection. In short, this research not only prominently enhances predictive performance but also provides a encouraging path for individualized healthcare guidance and early clinical decision support in diabetes management through the approach of modern machine learning and model fusing strategies.

title: Towards Carbon-Aware DevOps: A Framework for Cloud Footprint Tracking and Optimization
authors: Mansi H L, Rakshitha M, Karthik S
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent and Innovative Technologies in Computing, Electrical and Electronics (IITCEE)
abstract: Digital transformation has been accelerated by the widespread adoption of cloud computing and DevOps practices. However, the rapid growth of energy-intensive data centers has raised critical concerns regarding their carbon footprint and long-term sustainability. Prior research in cloud computing, supply chains, agriculture, and healthcare has demonstrated significant progress in reducing emissions through renewable energy integration, carbon-aware scheduling, and green process optimization. Despite these advances, the sustainability dimension of DevOps pipelines remains largely unexplored, with current CI/CD workflows optimized primarily for speed and reliability rather than environmental impact. This paper introduces a conceptual framework for a Cloud Carbon Footprint Tracker and Optimizer that directly integrates with CI/CD tools such as GitHub Actions and Jenkins. The framework estimates emissions using provider-specific factors and delivers actionable recommendations, including greener region selection, off-peak scheduling, and energy-efficient resource allocation. A DevOps Carbon Dashboard is also designed to provide developers with real-time feedback, historical trends, and sustainability scores alongside traditional performance metrics. By embedding carbon awareness into software delivery workflows, the proposed approach aligns operational efficiency with sustainability goals. This work establishes a foundation for carbon-aware DevOps, advancing the fields of green computing and sustainable software engineering while contributing to global climate objectives.

title: Review of a Specialised Civil Engineering Technology (Construction) Course: Outcome-Based Learning Approach
authors: Chee-Ming Chan, Alina Shamsuddin, Azeanita Suratkon
publish: 2025 IEEE 14th International Conference on Engineering Education (ICEED)
abstract: Shifting the focus from traditional content delivery to student-centered learning, Outcome-Based Education (OBE) is widely adopted in technology-based programmes, where it prioritizes what students are expected to achieve by the end of a learning experience as measurable outcomes. The teaching methodologies, assessment strategies and learning activities are deliberately aligned to ensure that every component of the curriculum supports the attainment of these predetermined outcomes. Specifically in the context of engineering technology, this encompasses designing curricula that equip students with not only theoretical understanding but also practical, hands-on skills relevant to real-world engineering challenges. With the iterative progression of ‘learning’ steadily reinforcing their comprehension and mastery of the subject matter, students are progressively nurtured to transition seamlessly into successive phases of learning. This ongoing refinement of knowledge functions as a launchpad, allowing learners to build upon the solid ground of newly constructed ‘learned’ knowledge and skill. The efficiency of this process lies in the student's ability to internalize and apply previously acquired concepts, effectively leveraging their cumulative knowledge base. This outcomedriven approach fosters a culture of continuous improvement, in turn creating a dynamic educational environment that empowers students to meet the demands of a complex, innovation-driven market with confidence and capability via reflective practices. The paper reviews a core discipline Civil Engineering Technology course, through the course structure and contents, learning outcomes, delivery and activities, and finally the evaluation of learning outcomes.

title: Design and Development of AI-ML Based Next Generation Communication Networks for Improving the Capacity, Efficiency with Mathematical Models
authors: Sarala T, T.C. Manjunath
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent and Innovative Technologies in Computing, Electrical and Electronics (IITCEE)
abstract: The work presented in this article gives the design and development in AI & ML based next generation communication networks for improving the capacity and efficiency - an overview is presented along with some results.
