Because of the improvement in advancements, the next-generation wireless network will be extremely mixed, convoluted and furthermore as per the changed requests of the network users. The current network architecture strategies and approaches are conventional and cannot help the following stage network to use their resources most properly. The restricted ability of the network users' smart devices will not permit the network suppliers to satisfy the requests of the network's support in the future. Therefore, this paper will present upon “AI - Artificial Intelligence”, “ML - Machine Learnings” & the “Big Data Analytic” for working on the limit and viability of the next-gen WSNs. The paper would examine the mission of these new advancements in working on the network resources and execution of the network. This article will outline that ML, large information investigation, and AI help in making the “cutting edge of the next generation wireless network” as adaptable, authoritative and dynamic. At the end of the paper, it will be given that future wireless communication networks cannot work without moving their functional system to computer-based ML and AI innovations. Next generation wireless network is very active and composite network and include the 5G and beyond networks. It is service ambitious and massive need a solo infrastructure distribute multiple services to improve the efficiency of the network system. To advance manageability, smart creation requires worldwide points of view of perception creation application innovation. Brief survey of the AI & ML based 6G Next-Gen nets survey could be seen in this article which provides a base for the future researchers to work upon with. Also, the mathematical model of the 6G system is developed which is used for simulating the output response. Some simulations are also shown.

title: CrimeScanAI - Cybercrime Reporting Assistant
authors: Ananya Shenoy, Shreedevi S Ronihal
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent and Innovative Technologies in Computing, Electrical and Electronics (IITCEE)
abstract: With the rapid increase in cybercrime cases such as UPI frauds, sextortion, OTP scams, and fake social media accounts, many victims remain hesitant to report incidents due to privacy concerns, low digital literacy, language barriers, and fear of retaliation. Traditional cybercrime reporting portals are often form-heavy, text-based, and available in limited languages, which further discourages reporting. CrimeScanAI is a user-centric, AI-powered cybercrime reporting assistant designed to simplify this process. The system accepts complaints via text, voice, or image input, supports multiple Indian languages, and uses Optical Character Recognition (OCR) and Natural Language Processing (NLP) to extract key details like scam type, date, amount, and contact information. CrimeScanAI automatically generates a structured FIR draft and provides a dashboard for law enforcement to review, categorize, and respond to complaints efficiently. By combining accessibility, multilingual support, and AI-driven automation, CrimeScanAI aims to empower citizens, improve reporting rates, and assist authorities in faster and more accurate cybercrime investigations.

title: LLM Cheat Prevention via Adversarial Question Paraphrasing
authors: Boya Balaji, P. Pavankumar, Shaik Riyaz, M Dhanunjaya Reddy, A Munna, B Ranga Sai
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent and Innovative Technologies in Computing, Electrical and Electronics (IITCEE)
abstract: As Large Language Model (LLM) chatbots have become increasingly accessible and their misuse for academic dishonesty has raised growing concern. Current methods that attempt to detect LLM-generated text are unreliable and risk producing false positives, which can unfairly harm genuine students. This paper offers an alternative by developing an “inoculation” process that generated paraphrased questions to find semantically similar ones that LLMs answer incorrectly. Using Llama 3.2 3B, create and evaluate paraphrases for MMLU questions, then test GPT-4o mini on them to identify effective inoculated questions. The approach successfully finds inoculations for 26.7% of correctly answered questions, requiring review of on more than 20 paraphrases per question. Detects weakness in instructor LLM responses with low cost.

title: Comparative Analysis of Deep Learning Approaches for Stock Chart Pattern Detection in Technical Analysis
authors: Rowen Nicholas, Enrico Deccen Aristan, Angeline Rachel, Bakti Amirul Jabar
publish: 2025 2nd Beyond Technology Summit on Informatics International Conference (BTS-I2C)
abstract: Technical chart patterns are widely used in trading decisions, yet manual identification is subjective and inconsistent. This is evident in market such as the Indonesia Stock Exchange (IDX) where automated pattern recognition research studies are still limited. Therefore, deep learning offers strong capabilities for capturing complex temporal structures in price data, making it suitable for automated chart pattern classification. This study develops deep learning models to classify four continuation patterns using IDX stock price data. Five architectures (ANN, RNN, LSTM, GRU, CNN) are benchmarked and their pairwise ensembles are stacked with XGBoost. Models are trained on normalized price sequences, evaluated through stratified 5-fold cross-validation and trading backtests. Results show that CNN with GRU and GRU with ANN outperform standalone models with F1 scores above 0.99 while backtesting confirms higher risk-adjusted returns and reduced drawdowns when paired with an ExRem signal filter. These findings show that ensemble deep learning improves objective chart pattern detection.

title: Collaborative Filtering Using Random Split and Cold-Start Scenarios
authors: Rahmawati Febrifyaning Tias, Triyanna Widiyaningtyas, Wahyu Sakti Gunawan Irianto, Mas Nurul Hamidah, Salahudin Robo
publish: 2025 2nd Beyond Technology Summit on Informatics International Conference (BTS-I2C)
abstract: Recommendation systems are essential in guiding users to relevant items in large-scale platforms. One of the simplest yet widely adopted approaches is K-Nearest Neighbor (KNN), in both user-based and item-based forms. Although considered as a baseline, the performance of KNN remains highly dependent on similarity measures and data conditions such as scarcity and cold-start. This study presents a comprehensive evaluation with the Collaborative Filtering approach on the MovieLens-100K dataset, using three similarity functions namely Cosine, Pearson Correlation Coefficient (PCC), and Jaccard, and two evaluation scenarios, namely Random Split (5-Fold Cross Validation), Cold-Start (User-Item) The results show that in the Random Split scenario, both User-KNN and Item-KNN produce competitive performance on Cosine and Jaccard with MAE ranging from 0.82-0.84 while RMSE ranges from 1.04-1.07. In contrast, PCC produces higher errors, especially on User-KNN. Under cold-start conditions, Item-KNN is superior in handling new users and User-KNN shows better accuracy for new items. And in terms of computational efficiency, Item-KNN proved to be computationally faster. These findings confirm the limitations of traditional baselines and emphasize the importance of scenario-dependent evaluation. Furthermore, this study shows that a hybrid strategy combining User-KNN and Item-KNN can provide a more robust solution for recommendation systems. Overall, this study contributes to a systematic baseline analysis that not only measures accuracy but also takes into account computational efficiency, while offering further development of recommendation research.

title: Improving Radiographic Tumor Detection with CNN Ensembles
authors: Advait Kankati, Tiasa Jana, Shashi Bhushan
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Internet of Things: Smart Innovation and Usages (IoT-SIU)
abstract: Bone tumors are a major problem that must be identified early and precisely for proper treatment in orthopedic medicine. Conventional approaches rely on manual identification of bone tumors in radiological images by radiologists. This approach is error-prone and is dependent on the availability of resources at the health center and the expertise of the radiologist. In order to classify the X-ray images as healthy or tumorous, our research presents a unique ensemble-based approach. Our method uses complementary feature extraction capabilities by creating an ensemble architecture with three different neural networks: a simple CNN, a medium complexity CNN and a transfer learning model based on MobileNetV2. We demonstrated a good categorization performance with our architecture achieving an accuracy of $76.36 \%$. The model performs well across different metrics with a precision of $78.16 \%$, recall of $73.51 \%$ and an F1 score of $75.77 \%$. This work provides an efficient classification network that might improve clinical workflow and improve the treatment pipelines by early diagnosis.

title: Labrador Underground Substation Power Cable Circuit Cooling Design
authors: Wen Kwang Chern, Joo Meng Phung, Yong Khang Lee
publish: 2025 25th Conference of the Electric Power Supply Industry (CEPSI)
abstract: This paper presents the power-circuit cooling system (PCCS) design for direct-buried 230 kV cable circuits supporting SP PowerAsset’s first Labrador Underground Substation (LBUGSS) in Singapore—the largest in Southeast Asia. The study compares installation schemes (direct-buried vs. cable tunnel), determines optimal cooling-water setpoints and pipe configurations, and verifies ampacity using IEC 60287, an IEC- modified proprietary 2.5D model, and 3D CFD/FEA. Chilled-water plant design and space integration are addressed to ensure conductor temperatures remain within limits under target loading.

title: How to Extend the Life Cycle of Ageing Cables in MV/HV Grids
authors: Klaus Winter, Christian Bernauer, Wolfgang Wiebel, Andreas Winter, Thilo Ihle, Johan Hollander
publish: 2025 25th Conference of the Electric Power Supply Industry (CEPSI)
abstract: Many of the XLPE cables in MV/HV grids have already passed their forty-year design life cycle. Authorities now advise utilities to replace these cables. A strait forward replacement of underground cables having passed their calculated life cycle will turn the streets of many cities into permanent workplaces. A more systematic approach is necessary to avoid this situation.By means of voltage injection into the neutral, the Ground Fault Neutralizer (GFN) can control phase-to-ground voltages. Full control of phase-to-ground voltages admits on-line PD testing at levels above normal operating voltages, enabling systematic forechecking strategies for the early detection of defective system components. This is done online, without at all affecting the power supply to end-users.

title: Real-Time Fish Detection with YOLOv8 on a Semi-Autonomous Underwater Vehicle for Aquaculture Monitoring
authors: Achmad Riezqi Murtadlo, Erwin Adriono, Yudi Eko Windarto
publish: 2025 2nd Beyond Technology Summit on Informatics International Conference (BTS-I2C)
abstract: Aquaculture is a vital sector for supporting food security, yet fish monitoring in floating net cages is still dominated by manual inspection, which is inefficient and prone to error. Advances in computer vision have enabled more reliable automated underwater observation, particularly with modern deep learning detectors. This paper presents a real-time fish detection system that integrates the YOLOv8s model with a semi-autonomous underwater vehicle (AUV) for monitoring aquaculture environments. The AUV captures underwater video using a Logitech C525 camera, while all inference is computed on a local GPU laptop, achieving an effective processing rate of 10–15 FPS. The model was trained on a custom dataset of 3,477 annotated underwater images containing Nile tilapia (Oreochromis niloticus), and evaluated using precision, recall, and mean Average Precision (mAP). The trained YOLOv8s model achieved a mAP@0.5 of 0.900, precision of 0.869, and recall of 0.804, outperforming a YOLOv5 baseline by 2.38%. Results show that detection performance remains stable under challenging conditions such as turbidity, occlusion, and low illumination. These findings demonstrate the feasibility of combining YOLO-based perception with AUV platforms to enhance the efficiency of aquaculture monitoring and support future extensions toward fish length estimation and large-scale deployment.

title: AutoSOC Cyber Analyst (ASOC-CA): Using AI to Automate SOC Tier 1 & 2 Activities
authors: Iason Mihalopoulos, Jen-Ryann Ngo-Antonio, Matthew Pugh, Damon Neal, Yinzhi Cao, Lanier Watkins
publish: 2026 IEEE 16th Annual Computing and Communication Workshop and Conference (CCWC)
abstract: Hackers are now beginning to use artificial intelligence (AI) to expand the capabilities and sophistication of their attacks, creating challenges for network owners. As state-sponsored adversaries and advanced persistent threats (APTs) utilize AI to their advantage, network owners must evolve concurrently to address the emergent threat landscape shaped by advanced AI systems. Thus, in this paper, we propose an end-to-end Machine Learning Framework to automate and strengthen cybersecurity operations. To demonstrate the feasibility of our approach, in this work-in-progress paper, we built and evaluated, a framework that consists of a machine learning (ML) model that automates the classifications of alerts generated by the Splunk Security Information and Event Management system (SIEM) to map adversary behaviors to MITRE ATT&CK stages. Our automated detection efforts are then fed into an LLM to generate an incident response report for cyber threat intelligence (CTI) and incident response teams. Our experimental results are intended to highlight the feasibility of combining adversary emulation with early real-time AI-assisted detection to strengthen organizational resilience (i.e. in Critical Infrastructure) against sophisticated campaigns.

title: A Comparative Study of BiLSTM and LSTM Architectures for Sentiment Analysis on a Mobile Banking App Review Dataset
authors: Nasywa Alif Widyasari, Hasmawati, Bunyamin
publish: 2025 2nd Beyond Technology Summit on Informatics International Conference (BTS-I2C)
abstract: With the growing use of digital banking applications, it is crucial for financial institutions to understand user responses to improve their services. One of the mobile banking apps in Indonesia is Wondr by BNI. Previous studies on Wondr user sentiment analysis have utilized classic machine learning methods such as Support Vector Machine (SVM), Naive Bayes, and K-Nearest Neighbor (KNN) to analyze user sentiment, but have not discussed a comparison with neural network architectures. To provide a broader methodological perspective, we implement and compare multiple sentiment analysis models, including Bidirectional Long Short-Term Memory (BiLSTM), unidirectional LSTM, and an SVM baseline. We collected 10,000 Wondr by BNI reviews from the Google Play Store, with a class distribution of 41.11% positive and 58.89% negative. This paper provides a comprehensive benchmark of sentiment analysis models on a dataset of Wondr app reviews, demonstrating that a BiLSTM model outperforms both a unidirectional LSTM and a classic machine learning method. The proposed BiLSTM model (Accuracy: 94.93%, F1: 94.92%) significantly outperformed unidirectional LSTM (Accuracy: 86.65%, F1: 86.22%), and the best classic ML model from previous study, SVM (Accuracy: 94.26%, F1: 94.24%).

title: Transforming Pdfs Into Assessments: Nlp-Based Question Generation
authors: Harwin C. Mendoza, Ronielle B. Antonio, Edward Allen B. Manaloto, Mark P. Lagman, Rafaela Mae M. Landayan, Aldrin T. Balilo, Richard John W. Hernandez
publish: 2025 IEEE 14th International Conference on Engineering Education (ICEED)
abstract: This study focuses on the development of an NLP-based system designed to generate test questions from PDF documents, facilitating assessment creation for teachers. The research employed the Research and Development methodology, encompassing the design, development, testing, and validation of the software. This approach was chosen to address the need for an innovative and modern solution for transforming existing educational materials into structured assessments. Two sets of evaluation instruments were utilized: one for teachers, assessing the system's Functionality, Reliability, Usability, and Efficiency, and another for IT experts, evaluating additional aspects such as Maintainability and Portability. Feedback from IT experts highlighted the system's strong Functionality, Usability, and Maintainability. Overall, the system was rated as Excellent, demonstrating reliability and effectiveness in automating the creation of test questions and printable assessment materials.

title: Federal Funding of Public Key Cryptography
authors: Martin Hellman
abstract: None

title: Title Page
authors: N/A
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Metaverse, and Cybersecurity (ICAMAC)

title: GTR: Global Token Retrieval for Window-Based Vision Transformer
authors: Xin Huang, Long Chen, Qian Yu, Lei Qi
publish: IEEE Transactions on Cognitive and Developmental Systems
abstract: Vision Transformers have achieved remarkable success in visual recognition tasks due to their strong capability to model long-range dependencies through global self-attention. However, the inherent quadratic complexity of global attention severely limits their scalability when processing high-resolution inputs. To address this issue, window-based attention mechanisms have been proposed to reduce computational overhead, but they often struggle with limited receptive fields and insufficient global context modeling. In this work, we propose GTR-ViT, a novel and efficient Vision Transformer framework that integrates a Global Token Retrieval (GTR) mechanism with an enhanced feedforward network in a synergistic manner. The GTR mechanism dynamically retrieves globally relevant tokens for each token, enriching the local features with comprehensive global context. These globally enhanced features are then fed into a gated feedforward network equipped with depthwise convolution, which further refines local representations through adaptive feature modulation. In turn, the improved local features serve as more representative queries for the GTR module, enabling more accurate global token retrieval in subsequent layers. This mutual reinforcement forms a virtuous cycle of local-global interaction, significantly boosting the models capability in both context understanding and feature discrimination. Extensive experiments on image classification and downstream tasks across multiple datasets demonstrate that GTR-ViT consistently surpasses prior state-of-the-art methods, highlighting the effectiveness of this collaborative design.

title: Pose-Guided Multi-Cue Explicit Query Construction for Disambiguating Human-Object Interactions_supp1-3667102.pdf
authors: Qingtian Zeng
abstract: Human-Object Interaction (HOI) detection remains challenging due to the semantic ambiguity of interaction categories and the limited discriminability of their feature representations. Existing approaches often improve recognition by employing sophisticated models or auxiliary textual annotations. While effective in certain gains, these solutions incur additional computational or annotation costs and struggle to capture intrinsic interaction regularities. To address these issues, we propose Pose-Guided Multi-Cue Explicit Query Construction (PM-EQC), a unified Transformer-based framework that builds upon collaborative modeling of appearance, spatial, and pose cues for discriminative interaction reasoning. At its core, the Collaborative Multi-Cue Query Constructor (CM-CQC) jointly models dependencies among visual cues to generate explicit query embeddings. CM-CQC further incorporates a hierarchical pose contextualization mechanism: global body configurations adaptively guide attention to local critical joints, yielding fine-grained pose embeddings and more precise interaction disambiguation. Owing to its modular design, PM-EQC integrates seamlessly with diverse backbones and benefits from their advances. Extensive experiments on PhysLab, HICO-DET, and V-COCO datasets demonstrate that PM-EQC achieves state-of-the-art performance, and the code is publicly available at https://github.com/ZMHSDUST/ PM-EQC.

title: Generative AI-Enabled Edge-Cloud Middleware for Deterministic Industrial Communications
authors: Meghna K. Bhatt, Mohammad Shabaz
abstract: The use of generative AI (GenAI) has become one of the most facilitating technologies of advanced data creation, intelligent decision-making, and automated processing of the content in the current edge-cloud computing environments. This survey explains recent studies that indicate the future promise of GenAI algorithms combined with edge-cloud middleware architectures, specifically in application areas, including cybersecurity, environmental monitoring, innovative media, and intelligent industry systems. These works use a number of generative models, such as large language models, diffusion models, and agent-based generative models, to assist in synthetic data generation, behavior simulation, data fusion, and intelligent content generation. The survey classifies the existing works based on the generative algorithms on which they are based, and evaluates their contribution to better middleware-assisted interoperability, scalability, and deterministic service support in heterogeneous systems. The findings show that GenAI-based middleware leads to a major enhancement of flexibility, scalability, and contextual understanding in complex and distributed environments. Nevertheless, the analyzed literature demonstrates the difficulties associated with the domain dependency, the reliability of the data, the verification of the produced results, and the explainability, which are significant to take into consideration when standardizing GenAI-assisted edge-cloud middleware in Industry 4.0 and deterministic communications.

title: RV-WINO: A RISC-V Neural Network Accelerator Based on Winograd Algorithm Fabricated in 55-nm CMOS Process
authors: Xingbo Wang, Yucong Huang, Qu Lu, Xinyu Kang, Yuru Li, Yu Wei, Qi Wang, Terry Tao Ye
publish: IEEE Transactions on Very Large Scale Integration (VLSI) Systems
abstract: The rapid evolution of artificial intelligence (AI) in IoT applications necessitates the execution of inference tasks on edge devices. However, the deployment of computation-intensive neural networks on resource-constrained edge systems presents a significant challenge. This brief presents the RV-WINO processor, the first silicon implementation of a RISC-V processor based on the Winograd algorithm for convolution and general matrix multiplication (GEMM) acceleration. The processor incorporates a Winograd module, which significantly reduces multiplication operations during convolutions, leading to a substantial decrease in energy consumption. In addition, the processor includes a matrix multiplication module that reuses the multipliers of the Winograd module, accelerating fully connected and dot product operations in neural networks. The RV-WINO processor fabricated in a 55-nm CMOS process achieves the peak computational performance of 0.95 and 2.39 GOPS in INT32 and INT8 modes, with its peak energy efficiency reaching 112 and 237 GOPS/W. In convolutional neural network (CNN) inference tasks, the execution time is reduced by over 80% compared with the baseline processor.

title: Hierarchical Information Embeddings with Neural ODEs for Personalized Federated Learning_supp1-3662990.pdf
authors: Sijie Wang
abstract: Personalized federated learning (PFL) plays a pivotal role in ensuring efficient privacy preservation and secure collaborative learning. However, PFL faces significant challenges due to data heterogeneity and device diversity. To enhance personalization and robustness in PFL, we propose a novel model called FedNODE, which leverages hierarchical embeddings. FedNODE incorporates personalized, pseudo-generic, and fusion embeddings to facilitate hierarchical information representation. We utilize a hypernetwork based on neural ordinary differential equations (ODEs) within the server to generate backbone parameters for different clients, enabling the creation of personalized embeddings. Additionally, we introduce a pseudo-generic embedding based on a learnable vector to balance personalized and generic information. A neural ODE-based network follows the backbone module for each client, integrating personalized and pseudo-generic embeddings. To validate the efficacy of FedNODE, we conduct extensive evaluations across various classification datasets, encompassing diverse statistically heterogeneous settings and noisy scenarios. The results demonstrate that FedNODE achieves state-of-the-art performance.

title: VIRGi: View-dependent Instant Recoloring of 3D Gaussians Splats_supp1-3665650.pdf
authors: Alessio Mazzucchelli
abstract: 3D Gaussian Splatting (3DGS) has recently transformed the fields of novel view synthesis and 3D reconstruction due to its ability to accurately model complex 3D scenes and its unprecedented rendering performance. However, a significant challenge persists: the absence of an efficient and photorealistic method for editing the appearance of the scene's content. In this paper we introduce VIRGi, a novel approach for rapidly editing the color of scenes modeled by 3DGS while preserving viewdependent effects such as specular highlights. Key to our method are a novel architecture that separates color into diffuse and viewdependent components, and a multi-view training strategy that integrates image patches from multiple viewpoints. Improving over the conventional single-view batch training, our 3DGS representation provides more accurate reconstruction and serves as a solid representation for the recoloring task. For 3DGS recoloring, we then introduce a rapid scheme requiring only one manually edited image of the scene from the end-user. By fine-tuning the weights of a single MLP, alongside a module for single-shot segmentation of the editable area, the color edits are seamlessly propagated to the entire scene in just two seconds, facilitating real-time interaction and providing control over the strength of the view-dependent effects. An exhaustive validation on diverse datasets demonstrates significant quantitative and qualitative advancements over competitors based on Neural Radiance Fields representations.

title: Multivariable Decoupling Current Control for Open-End Winding Permanent Magnet Motor Drives
authors: Gyu Cheol Lim, Junhyuk Yang, Cheol-Min Hwang, Kahyun Lee, Jung-Ik Ha
abstract: This article presents a current controller design for an open-end winding permanent magnet synchronous motor (OEW-PMSM) supplied by a common DC source. Conventional control approaches for OEW-PMSMs have typically treated the zero-sequence (n-axis) component as an independent variable, ignoring its dynamic influence on the dq components. In this work, a unified dqn motor model is first established by incorporating mutual inductances and rotor position-dependent spatial harmonics across all axes. Then, based on the proposed model, a multivariable controller is analytically derived to achieve fully decoupled current dynamics while ensuring stable operation under load variations. The proposed controller accounts for interaxis coupling in its structure, enabling active regulation of the zero-sequence current. This leads to improved steady-state and transient performance. Experimental validation is conducted on a motor developed for electric vehicle applications. The results confirm the effectiveness of the proposed method over conventional control, demonstrating improved steady-state waveform quality, and a reduction in total harmonic distortion (THD) of the current from 6.61% to 4.86%. In addition, the transient response is significantly enhanced. Even during abrupt torque changes, the zero-sequence current remains stable, unaffected by disturbances in the d–q currents.

title: UniqueRank: Identifying Important and Difficult-to-Replace Nodes in Attributed Graphs
authors: Erica Cai, Benjamin A. Miller, Olga Simek, Christopher L. Smith
publish: IEEE Transactions on Network Science and Engineering
abstract: Node-ranking methods that focus on structural importance are widely used in a variety of applications, from ranking webpages in search engines to identifying key molecules in biomolecular networks. In real social, supply chain, and terrorist networks, one definition of importance considers the impact on information flow or network productivity when a given node is removed. In practice, however, a nearby node may be able to replace another node upon removal, allowing the network to continue functioning as before. This replaceability is an aspect that existing ranking methods do not consider. To address this, we introduce UniqueRank, a Markov-Chain-based approach that captures attribute uniqueness in addition to structural importance, making top-ranked nodes harder to replace. We find that UniqueRank identifies important nodes with dissimilar attributes from its neighbors in simple symmetric networks with known ground truth. Further, on real terrorist, social, and supply chain networks, we demonstrate that removing and attempting to replace top UniqueRank nodes often yields larger efficiency reductions than removing and attempting to replace top nodes ranked by competing methods. Finally, we show UniqueRank's versatility by demonstrating its potential to identify structurally critical atoms with unique chemical environments in biomolecular structures.

title: Structure-Informed Hex-dominant Mesh Simplification_supp1-3667544.pdf
authors: Guoning Chen
abstract: Hex-dominant mesh generation has recently received increasing attention from researchers and the simulation community due to its robustness compared to pure hex-mesh generation techniques. In this work, we present a first structure-informed simplification framework, aiming to reduce the number of non-hex cells from the hex-dominant meshes. Our framework extracts individual sub-structures via parallel relations from the input hex-dominant mesh, decomposes self-tangent and self-intersecting sub-structures for structure complexity control, and collapses certain sets of edges that are adjacent to particular non-hex cells to remove them. We design a filtering and ranking strategy to select edges and sub-structures for collapsing. To better understand the complexity of sub-structure configurations, we introduced a novel relation graph that captures the connections between edges and between edges and sub-structures. Additionally, we designed a smoothing algorithm for hex-dominant meshes that enhances hex cell quality, even in meshes containing various cell types. We evaluate the effectiveness of our framework by applying it to many hex-dominant meshes produced by three state-of-the-art hex-dominant meshing techniques. Our results achieve various levels of reduction in the number of non-hex cells of the input meshes, affirming the applicability of our framework for improving the hex-dominant meshes.

title: Mish-BiFPN: Temperature-Controlled Mish Activation with Bidirectional Feature Pyramid Networks for Thermal Hotspot Detection and Energy Conservation in Photovoltaic Panels
authors: Rhaditia Kurnia Asyuri, Arun Kumar Sangaiah, Wei Tong Chen
abstract: The current level of adoption of solar photovoltaic systems requires better methods of thermal fault monitoring, which will support sustainable energy production systems. The application of traditional methods in detecting hotspots is highly significant in analysing the thermal anomaly pattern, but the procedure is ineffective since the process of inspection is manual, time-consuming, and labour-intensive. However, thermal imaging devices offer scalable solutions that are limited in terms of managing complex thermal patterns and combining temperature data at various scales. This paper proposes a Mish-BiFPN Framework to detect thermal hotspots in solar PV systems. Unlike conventional BiFPN or Mish-based models, the proposed framework integrates a temperature-controlled Mish activation with CBAM-guided fusion at every pyramid level, combined with a RegNet backbone and thermal-preserving preprocessing to enhance sensitivity to subtle heat gradients. The offered architecture uses multi-scale feature extraction and attention layers in a way that the model can focus on temperature-sensitive and spatial features at different pyramid layers. It ensures thermal characteristics are retained through preprocessing and data augmentation. The framework further provides a basis to combine the real-time thermal performance monitoring with long-term energy planning that allows dynamically calibrating estimates of solar PV energy generation. This model is trained and validated on thermal hotspot datasets, achieving 12 percentage-point improvement in precision, a 6% improvement in recall, 9% in F1-score, and 21% in Intersection over Union in comparison to the YOLOv10 model. The model shows its potential to address complicated thermal patterns. Energy loss modeling demonstrates that single hotspot detection can prevent approximately 9 kWh per year of power losses per 450 W module. Therefore, this model is very appropriate for large-scale solar PV systems, with both accurate detection and energy conservation optimization.

title: Solder Joint Inspection Using Digital Model Priors for Industrial Applications
authors: Chunyuan Li, Peng Zhang, Shuangming Wang, Lie Liu, Mingquan Shi
abstract: With the deepening exploration and application of machine vision technology, researchers are actively introducing advanced object detection and recognition methods in the industrial domain, particularly in the critical manufacturing process of welding quality inspection. To address the limitations of traditional solder joint inspection methods,-such as low efficiency, high false alarm rates, and poor adaptability to complex environments,-this study innovatively proposes an industrial visual solder joint inspection algorithm that integrates digital model optimization with deep learning. The core contributions of the algorithm are twofold: first, it refines the conventional image processing pipeline by incorporating prior information from known digital models, thereby enhancing the efficiency and quality of image preprocessing; second, it introduces a multi-scale and multi-template matching strategy coupled with a Faster RCNN three-stage cascade detection framework guided by digital model coordinate Regions of Interest (ROIs), enabling efficient and accurate recognition of solder joints in images. In this process, digital model information is utilized to optimize and improve the underlying detectors. This design not only strengthens the algorithm’s robustness in complex industrial settings but also significantly improves the accuracy and robustness of solder joint detection. Experimental validation on a solder joint dataset constructed in a real manufacturing environment demonstrates the promising detection performance of the proposed approach, offering reliable technical support for real-time monitoring and quality enhancement in industrial automation. This research not only advances the practical application of machine vision in industrial inspection but also lays a solid foundation for the future development of intelligent and precise manufacturing.

title: IBNS: Optimizing Intent-Based 6G Network Slicing for Conflict Detection and Mitigation
authors: Behnam Ojaghi, Ricard Vilalta, Raül Muñoz
publish: IEEE Transactions on Network and Service Management
abstract: The Sixth-Generation (6G) mobile networks aim to automate network resource allocation and support both new and existing vertical services, each with diverse Quality of Service (QoS) intent requirements. Digital Service Providers (DSPs) must consider specific intent expectations, and targets set by different services, and re-configure and prioritize the most critical intents when resources are insufficient. This paper presents an optimization model for a flexible network paradigm using an Intent-Based Network Slicing (IBNS) framework that can manage the complexity of QoS intents and identify slice intent conflicts through closed-loop evaluation and monitoring. It dynamically adjusts the slice configurations to handle and mitigate detected conflicts by executing the agreed-upon Service Level Agreement (SLA) objectives (%) for higher-priority slices to ensure that critical intents are addressed. According to the results, this approach successfully meets the SLA target we set for slice but it negatively impacts the performance of other slices and degrades their slice capacity.

title: Zero forgetting lifelong dictionary learning based on low-rank decomposition for multimode process monitoring
authors: Keke Huang, Zhongyu Zhang, Zixuan Chen, Dehao Wu, Chunhua Yang, Weihua Gui
abstract: Modern industrial systems typically operate in a multimode environment due to continuous changes in raw materials and production schedules. Traditional static monitoring methods often struggle to accurately capture the intricate relationships between different modes, leading to model mismatches and a significant decline in monitoring performance. On the other hand, emergence of a new mode often prompts model updates that focus on the new data, potentially causing the model to forget historical knowledge, which is a phenomenon known as catastrophic forgetting. To address these issues, we propose a zero forgetting lifelong dictionary learning (ZFLDL) method for multimode process monitoring. ZFLDL integrates a low-rank basis matrix growth mechanism with an adaptive basis matrix selector, enabling effective representation of knowledge across both new and historical modes. Specifically, a novel low-rank decomposition method is first proposed to address feature redundancy in data representation with an overcomplete dictionary. This method utilizes a low-rank matrix, i.e. a “basis matrix”, as the fundamental representation unit, which can accurately capture the core data features and enhance the dictionary's representation capability. Then, to address the emergence of new modes, a zero forgetting continual learning framework based on low-rank basis matrix growth is proposed. By adaptively adding basis matrices for each new mode while freezing those associated with historical modes, the dictionary efficiently assimilates new knowledge while ensuring zero forgetting of historical knowledge. Additionally, since only the basis matrices related to the new mode are updated, this approach significantly reduces the number of parameters requiring training, thus enhancing the model's learning efficiency. Finally, an adaptive basis matrix selector is proposed. By evaluating the contribution of each basis matrix to different modes, it dynamically selects the basis matrices that best match the specific mode, ensuring accurate mode recognition and reliable process monitoring. Extensive experiments have demonstrated that the proposed method outperforms several state-of-the-art methods, exhibiting superior continual learning capability and enhanced process monitoring performance.

title: Optimized Feature Extraction and Selection-Integrated Machine Learning Framework for Accurate Brain Tumor Severity Classification and Diagnostic Tool Development Using MRI
authors: D. Sandhya Rani, Anjaiah Adepu
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Artificial Intelligence for Innovations in Healthcare Industries (ICAIIHI)
abstract: The early and accurate assessment of the severity of intracranial tumors is an essential step for the prognosis of brains recovery of patients and for the timely treatment. In this study, we propose an improved machine-learning framework, which combines advanced feature extraction and selection techniques to improve the classification of brain tumor severity of magnetic resonance (MR) images. The framework needs to solve two challenges: how to select the most discriminative features from the high-dimensional imaging data and how to make the diagnostic solution of the model interpretable and useful for clinic diagnosis. First, a range of predefined and learned features (handcrafted and based on deep learning) were computed from preprocessed MRI scans capturing textural information of tumor regions, morphological and spatial features. The chosen features were used as an input to several machine learning classifiers, such as Random Forest, Support Vector Machines, and Gradient Boosting, after hyperparameters were calibrated using Grid Search as well as Bayesian Optimization to make the best contribution from the model. Benchmarking was benchmarked on public benchmarks of brain tumor datasets and showed significant increase in classification accuracy, sensitivity and specificity compare both with the baseline models. Moreover, the optimal model was incorporated in a user-friendly diagnosis tool which can automatically analyze MRI as well as support real-time severity prediction. The application interface shows a radiologist or clinician how to upload MRI images, obtain classification outputs, and interpret relevant feature insights as phenotype-matcher is being used, enabling model transparency and trust for the AI-assisted decisions. In conclusion, this work provides a new, scalable, and clinically relevant machine learning solution for brain tumor severity stratification. Combining strong feature engineering with model optimization and tool development, the work bridges the divide between theoretical innovation and practical diagnostic utility, and lays the groundwork for more accurate, interpretable, and accessible neuro-oncological assessments.

title: Adopting sustainability management in asset end of life & asset disposal process using digital systems
authors: Lim Jun Nian, Nurulhuda Binti Abdullah, Mohd Shafiz Bin Mustapah
publish: 2025 25th Conference of the Electric Power Supply Industry (CEPSI)
abstract: A paper that describes the process of asset disposal in a Grid utility company accompanied using digital platforms to facilitate sustainable management of asset end of life & asset disposal process. This paper discusses highlights on the investment of digital platforms that helps to streamline the asset end of life process to provide clear line of sight in making the right decision on whether to repurpose the asset to continues its operation or to proceed with asset scraping following the sustainable goals set. The discussed digital platforms – EAMF, E-Decom and E-Disposal have crossed into a stable stage after 3 years in operations and the several indicators were taken sample as the evidence of overall improvement in the operation, in managing asset end of life and disposal process.

title: AI-Based Predictive Models for Effective Diabetes Management
authors: S.Girija, S.Thangamayan, Rini Adiyattil, M.Sivadharshini, Anurag Shrivastava
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Artificial Intelligence for Innovations in Healthcare Industries (ICAIIHI)
abstract: AI and ML have emerged as transformative tools in the management of diabetes, enabling early detection, risk prediction, personalized therapy, and improved patient adherence. Recent studies prove that AI can analyze huge datasets, including electronic health records, continuous glucose monitoring, lifestyle input, and biomarker profiles, for the identification of patients who have a high risk of developing type 2 diabetes and its complications. Various predictive models have been proposed with supervised learning, deep learning, ensemble methods, and time-series analysis, which have performed well in predicting glycemic events, treatment response, and comorbidity risk. Moreover, AI-driven systems enable personalized interventions, optimize insulin dosing, dietary planning, and continuous monitoring for precision care in diabetes. However, there are considerable challenges regarding model generalizability, integration into clinical workflows, data privacy, and real-world implementation. Systematic reviews present the potential of AI to enhance decision-making while highlighting robust validation, standardized datasets, and interdisciplinary collaboration. As the use of AI-based solutions gains momentum in healthcare systems, these technologies will improve outcomes and decrease complications while enabling proactive management of diabetic populations worldwide. Further research efforts should be directed toward scalable, ethically responsible frameworks to guarantee equity in access and seamless integration into routine clinical practice.

title: AI-Driven Literary Analysis for High School Education Using Transformer Architecture
authors: Khaled Shaalan, Raavi Deepthi, Fatima S. Hussian, Ali Abbas Hadi, Chesha P. Chellappan, Nasiba Eshqulova
publish: 2025 International Conference on AI-Driven STEM Education and Learning Technologies (AISTEMEDU)
abstract: Even though the process of integrating AI into the educational domain continues to increase, the literary analysis remains a difficult problem to automate because of the intricate and subtle character of literary works. The classic approaches do not necessarily represent the complicated literary devices, themes, and character development. In order to address the concerns, the paper uses AI based Automated Literary Analysis (AI-ALA) model which uses Generative Pretrained Transformer (GPT-4), a state of the art generative language model in order to automate literary analysis in high school literature courses. The fact that this method is interactive at the time of lesson and that the teacher works with pupils to assess literary tools such as metaphors and sarcasm as well as discuss the character and theme in a setting is revolutionary. The successful adjustment of the algorithm to a set of books with high school students is the guarantee of their adequacy and applicability in the field of education. The experiments indicate that GPT-4 is more precise and context-responsive as compared to rule-based. It is an in-depth examination of characters, themes, and literary strategies. The strategy, according to the statistics, can present teachers with an effective and scalable tool to enhance student learning. The concept simplifies the process of literary analysis through interactive interaction combined with questions and answers sessions. Literary analysis automation and personalization will break the existing boundaries and enhance literature classes teaching.

title: ClusterSSFDA: Clustered Semi-Supervised Federated Domain Adaptation
authors: Michele Craighero, Taguhi Mesropyan, Diego Carrera, Beatrice Rossi, Diego Stucchi, Pasqualina Fragneto, Giacomo Boracchi
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Data Mining (ICDM)
abstract: Most Federated Learning (FL) approaches assume a single global model is updated locally by clients and aggregated at the server. However, the single-model assumption is often too restrictive, especially in scenarios involving a large amount of different users, where adaptation to different domains and personalization are necessary to improve model performance. In this paper, we propose ClusterSSFDA, the first FL framework to leverage clustering for addressing domain shifts in Semi-Supervised Federated Learning (SSFL), where clients collect data without supervision. In particular, ClusterSSFDA clusters clients based on their models' agreement, and updates multiple models at the server, each tailored to a cluster of similar clients. ClusterSSFDA significantly improves adaptation in SSFL, striking a balance between a single global model, which may be suboptimal in the presence of domain shift among the clients, and fully personalized models, which would be trained on too small datasets. Our experiments on real-world scenarios with multiple levels of domain shift demonstrate that ClusterSSFDA outperforms existing methods, achieving superior performance in challenging SSFL settings.

title: Leveraging Artificial Intelligence and Cloud Computing for Secure Early Diagnosis and Treatment of Medical Disease
authors: Shikhar Sharma
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Artificial Intelligence for Innovations in Healthcare Industries (ICAIIHI)
abstract: AI coupled with cloud computing will revolutionize healthcare through smart, scalable, and secure disease detection and management systems. Recent research works have explored how cloud-based AI frameworks improve medical data processing, predictive analytics, and real-time diagnosis based on modern machine learning, deep learning, and hybrid optimization algorithm. These systems enable precision medicine, early disease prediction, and personalized therapy to deal with large-scale health data emanating from IoT devices, biomedical sensors, and electronic health records. In addition, AI-driven cloud infrastructures enhance data accessibility and interoperability and improve cybersecurity while preserving patient privacy and regulatory compliance. Eventually, edge AI and multi-cloud strategies will improve the reliability and speed of healthcare delivery, particularly in resource-constrained, remote, and disaster-prone areas. Furthermore, AI-cloud synergy is unfolding new applications in biomedical engineering, genomics, and medical imaging that assure remarkable enhancement of diagnostic accuracy, better patient outcomes, and healthcare resilience. In summary, converged AI-cloud technologies will bring about smarter, datadriven, and patient-centered healthcare ecosystems.

title: AI-Driven Mortality Risk Classification Using HMIS Data: A Case Study from Nagaon District, Assam, India
authors: Nur Alom Talukdar, Abida Ahmed, Jaganathan D, Rubul Kr. Bania, Deepthi Chamkur V, Chayapathi A R
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Artificial Intelligence for Innovations in Healthcare Industries (ICAIIHI)
abstract: Maternal, infant, and adult mortality is one of the vital public health concerns in India. In the present study machine learning models were implemented on Health Management Information System (HMIS) dataset, considering Nagaon district of Assam, India for the classification of district blocks to low and high-mortality risk groups. Various service indicators and Mortality were accumulated at the block level. Different classifiers like, Random Forest, Logistic Regression, Linear and RBF SVM, KNN, and Gaussian Naive Bayes were evaluated. Repeated 5×5 Cross-validation technique is used for the analysis and evaluated purpose. Random Forest (RF) produce an accuracy of 86.7%. Out of the total classifiers applied Random Forest turned out to be the best-balanced technique. While in terms of ROC-AUC, RBF SVM generated highest 96%, which signifying robust discriminative capacity. Logistic Regression and SVM remain stable and explainable baselines. KNN and Naive Bayes yield modest performance. Analysis confirmed that antenatal care and child immunization as the vital predictors of mortality consequences. Moreover, these outcomes highlight the necessity of robust justification in AI-driven system for health analytics along with validate the potential of ensemble method and SVM technique to support HMIS-based mortality investigation and guide aimed interventions. From a public health viewpoint, these outcomes advocate that AI-driven analytics can improve routine HMIS data use for evidence-based planning. Recognizing the high-risk mortality blocks, respective department of health can line up interventions viz., antenatal care, immunization, and maternal health services.

title: Design and Simulation of a Stair-Climbing Robot with a Stabilized Payload Platform
authors: Anu Karthick N, Gandham Swetha, Deepesh K, Mohammed Said U Y, Ramya S Moorthy, Hari Krishnan R
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent and Innovative Technologies in Computing, Electrical and Electronics (IITCEE)
abstract: This paper presents the design and simulation of a stair-climbing robot equipped with a stabilized payload platform. The CAD model of the robot has been developed using SolidWorks, and its operation has been simulated in CoppeliaSim. Forward Kinematics and Dynamic analysis were performed to examine the motion characteristics of the link and joints, and to maintain the center of gravity within a safe range for stable operations. Stability analysis has been performed for each phase of stair climbing, and the corresponding graphs have been plotted. Simulation results confirm that the robot effectively climbs stairs while maintaining the payload platform in a stable horizontal orientation.

title: Beyond Accuracy: Fidelity Evaluation Framework for Synthetic Data in Network Intrusion Detection
authors: Daniel Perez Fiadzeawu, Luis Vincent Jabla, Jielun Zhang, Fuhao Li
publish: 2026 IEEE 16th Annual Computing and Communication Workshop and Conference (CCWC)
abstract: In network intrusion detection tasks, real-world datasets often suffer from severe class imbalance, and synthetic data augmentation is commonly used to improve minority-class learning. However, most existing work evaluates only classification metrics and lacks a systematic assessment of synthetic data quality, leaving it unclear whether generated samples are reliable enough to enhance model performance. To address this gap, this study introduces a fidelity evaluation framework tailored for synthetic data in intrusion detection. The framework quantifies differences among generation methods in terms of statistical distribution, feature importance, and decision-level consistency. Using the UNSW-NB15 dataset, we generate minority class samples via SMOTE, CTGAN, and CVAE, and train four independent MLP classifiers on real and synthetic datasets. Beyond standard classification metrics, we propose three complementary fidelity indicators, i.e., Statistical Fidelity (SFI), Explainability Fidelity (EFI), and Robustness Fidelity (RFI), which are integrated into a unified Fidelity Score (FS) for comprehensive quality assessment. Experimental results indicate that the three generation methods exhibit different advantages: CVAE achieves the highest accuracy, CTGAN improves minorityclass detection, and SMOTE attains the highest overall fidelity score in our framework. These findings show that our fidelity evaluation provides complementary insight beyond traditional metrics and offers a more reliable basis for selecting synthetic data strategies in intrusion detection.

title: Burger: Robust Graph Denoising-Augmentation Fusion and Multi-Semantic Modeling for Social Recommendation
authors: Yuqin Lan, Laurence T. Yang
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Data Mining (ICDM)
abstract: In the era of rapid development of social media, social recommendation systems as hybrid recommendation systems have been widely applied. Existing methods capture interest similarity between users to filter out interestirrelevant relations in social networks that inevitably decrease recommendation accuracy, however, limited research has a focus on the mutual influence of semantic information between the social network and the user-item interaction network for further improving social recommendation. To address these issues, we introduce a social recommendation model with robust graph denoising-augmentation fusion and multi-semantic Modeling(Burger). Specifically, we firstly propose to construct a social tensor in order to smooth the training process of the model. Then, a graph convolutional network and a tensor convolutional network are employed to capture user's item preference and social preference, respectively. Considering the different semantic information in the user-item interaction network and the social network, a bi-semantic coordination loss is proposed to model the mutual influence of semantic information. To alleviate the interference of interest-irrelevant relations on multi-semantic modeling, we further use Bayesian posterior probability to mine potential social relations to replace social noise. Finally, the sliding window mechanism is utilized to update the social tensor as the input for the next iteration. Extensive experiments on three real datasets show Burger has a superior performance compared with the state-of-the-art models.

title: Augmenting Managerial Decision-Making with AI and Machine Learning
authors: A. Shameem, M. Vanisree, Muralidhar L. B, Kawerinder Singh Sidhu, Patel Yogeshkumar Jethabhai, Lakshmikanth R
publish: 2025 World Conference on Cutting-Edge Science and Technology (WCCEST)
abstract: The way businesses function, plan, and compete is changing as a result of the incorporation of AI and ML into managerial decision-making processes. This study examines how AI and ML techniques improve data-driven decision-making, lessen cognitive biases, and enable predictive and prescriptive analytics to enhance managerial capacities. AI systems may find patterns, produce insights, and assist in making decisions that would be challenging or impossible for human managers to do on their own if they had access to large datasets and complex algorithms. In particular, machine learning enables systems to consistently acquire from fresh data, thus enhancing their functionality and applicability. AI helps managers by automating repetitive work and providing real-time dashboards, risk assessments, scenario planning, and tailored suggestions. But there are drawbacks to adopting AI as well, such as moral dilemmas, problems with data quality, model interpretability, and the requirement to upskill the workforce. This essay makes the case that a hybrid strategy that capitalises on the advantages of both human judgement and machine intelligence is necessary for the effective integration of AI into managerial decision-making. By doing this, businesses can create decision-making structures that are more resilient, flexible, and informed—which promotes innovation and long-term competitive advantage in a business climate that is becoming more complex by the day.

title: Immersive 3D and Augmented Reality Learning for Electrical Substations and Induction Motors
authors: Sanjay Rao Puthli, Kishore Bingi, Ramani Kannan
publish: 2025 IEEE 14th International Conference on Engineering Education (ICEED)
abstract: As the field of electrical engineering continues to evolve, enhancing educational methods becomes increasingly important to keep pace with technological advancements. Thus, this paper presents two immersive 3D applications that aim to improve the quality of electrical engineering education through accessible augmented reality technologies. It first presents an Android application that converts 2D induction motor diagrams to interactive 3D models. It also presents a Windows application that provides a third-person walkthrough of a 3D substation model. Both programs are implemented using Unity, with optimisations of the 3D models and the development of simple and effective interfaces to ensure smooth operation on commonly available hardware. They overcome the drawbacks of conventional teaching using interactive and realistic learning experiences. The results demonstrate improved engagement, understanding, and accessibility, and the advantages of utilising immersive technologies in engineering education are presented.

title: Weakly Supervised Masked Siamese Networks (MSN) for Image-Based Cell Phenotype Prediction
authors: Tiba H. Shamman, Sura Z. Alrashid
publish: 2025 2nd Beyond Technology Summit on Informatics International Conference (BTS-I2C)
abstract: A framework was developed to extract meaningful phenotypic representations from high-content fluorescence microscopy images, integrating Masked Siamese Networks (MSN) with weak supervision. This approach incorporates a Vision Transformer backbone and employs MSN to match representations across many masked views of the same image, encouraging the model to focus on essential visual structures without requiring significant annotations. This strategy improves representation quality and outperforms previously published approaches on the BBBC021 benchmark Dataset by integrating weak labels, such as compound information, during fine-tuning. The model attains 99% accuracy in mechanism of action (MOA) predictions in the not-same-compound context and 96.89% accuracy in the not-same-compound-and-batch situation. The results indicate that the proposed framework successfully acquires robust and biologically relevant phenotypic representations directly from microscope pictures, providing a scalable alternative to conventional single-cell analysis methods.

title: Intelligent Prosthetic Limb Actuation Using EMG Sensors for Neuromuscular Rehabilitation in Amputees
authors: Serena Eugene Francis, Mohamed Faisal Hussain, Chaitra M
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent and Innovative Technologies in Computing, Electrical and Electronics (IITCEE)
abstract: The research project titled “Intelligent Prosthetic Limb Actuation Using EMG Sensors for Neuromuscular Rehabilitation in Amputees” proposes the design of an intelligent prosthetic limb that allows real-time control of a robotic arm with the help of bioelectrical muscle signals. The device aims to benefit amputees and neuromuscular disabled individuals by providing them with functional hand motion through actuation based on electromyography (EMG). The surface EMG sensors with gel electrodes sense muscle signals from the forearm, processed by an ESP32 microcontroller to create related control signals. These are fed to a PCA9685 servo controller, operating six MG90S servo motors-five for finger movements and one for rotation of the thumb-all embedded in a 3D-printed low-weight prosthetic apparatus. For stable and safe operation, the circuit is powered by a regulated 5V DC supply. A Unity 3D simulation system is also included, which verifies the motion's synchronicity, accuracy, and smoothness prior to hardware implementation. For effective neuromuscular rehabilitation, the experimental validation is carried out to guarantee responsive, accurate, and proportionate control of the prosthetic hand on an inexpensive, ergonomic, and intelligent assist system.

title: Blockchain-Enabled Voting with Multi-Factor Authentication: Face Id, Aadhar & OTP
authors: P. Udhaya Kumar, L. A JoelJashwa, B. Uma Maheswari
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent and Innovative Technologies in Computing, Electrical and Electronics (IITCEE)
abstract: The initial concept behind the system is to create a secure and powerful smart voting mechanism that incorporates the use of face recognition, the algorithm used being Local Binary Pattern Histogram (LBPH) and Aadhar identification, and use of OTPs to authenticate voters. The main idea behind this is to modernize the voting process removing the errors in it that were made manually, stop fraud in identity identification and allow participating in the process regardless of location. The LBPH which has a reputation of being simple and efficient in real-time face recognition transforms the facial images into local binary patterns subsequently lending them to the creation of histograms which depict features of distinct faces. The training phase involves taking several faces of registered voters in case of different lighting conditions and pose conditions. These pictures are scanned to black and white, they are pre-processed and marked with respective voter identities. The voting is then done in the trained LBPH model to authenticate the voter upon real-time webcam feedback where the system detects the face process it and match it with the trained database. Upon successful verification of the face, the system further cross verifies the Aadhar details of the voter and sends him a one-time password (OTP) to his/her recorded phone number. The voter is then allowed into the candidate panel after the successful multi-factor authentication, where he or she votes. Votes are hash stored in a blockchain where each confirmed vote is hashed with the secure hash, SHA-256, providing the requirement of vote recording that is hashing, traceable, and immutable. Such a multi-tiered solution does not only provide a better class of security and transparency, but also fosters accessibility in long-distance voters, thus the proposed system is a strong and highly scalable answer to the current democratic procedures.

title: CivicEye: AI and Blockchain-Powered Platform for Transparent Civic Issue Reporting and Resolution
authors: Prathyush K S, S Vishakh, Vanishree B R, S. Srividhya
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent and Innovative Technologies in Computing, Electrical and Electronics (IITCEE)
abstract: Urban infrastructure networks increasingly depend on smart technologies to solve chronic civic issues like potholes, garbage pileup, water leaks, and malfunctioning streetlights. Conventional grievance reporting systems are plagued by inefficiency, poor traceability, and public distrust. A convergence of Artificial Intelligence (AI), Blockchain, and Internet of Things (IoT) presents a revolutionary solution for streamlining issue identification, reporting, and fixing. AI-based models automatically classify urban issues from images and video feeds, while blockchain guarantees tamper-proof complaint tracking through smart contracts and decentralized storage. IoT-sensing devices and smartphones facilitate real-time monitoring and proactive detection of infrastructure anomalies. Crowdsourcing tools and tokenized incentives are used to further encourage citizen participation and enhance validation accuracy. Combined, these technologies make available a transparent, scalable, and participatory civic management ecosystem as per the vision of future-proofed smart cities.

title: Game Theory–Driven AI for Zero Trust Security
authors: Al Amin Hossain, Darryl Ahner
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Metaverse, and Cybersecurity (ICAMAC)
abstract: The rapid expansion of networked cyber-physical systems safeguarding critical infrastructure demands rigorous methods for evaluating and fortifying their resilience. Existing re-search is often fragmented, emphasizing single-technique models that are of limited relevance to operational environments. This study presents a comprehensive game-theoretic AI framework that unifies game-theoretic reasoning, probabilistic modeling, and mathematical optimization to assess and enhance cyber defense capabilities. The framework models interactions between attackers and defenders using a discrete-time Markov chain, enabling the quantitative estimation of expected network damage and the conditional analysis of system vulnerabilities. To further strengthen security, the approach incorporates zero-trust archi-tecture principles, embedding continuous authentication, least-privilege policies, and dynamic access controls into the state-space formulation. By focusing on these elements, the methodology provides a data-driven foundation for cyber defense analysts to evaluate risk, optimize resource allocation, and bolster network resilience against sophisticated adversarial threats.

title: Slow Electronics: Tunable Long-Timescale Neuromorphic Devices Enabled by Interfacial Control for Low-Power Edge AI
authors: Yuki Koh, Hisashi Inoue, Ai Kitoh, Masafumi Tamura, Isao H. Inoue
publish: 2025 International Conference on Electrical and Computer Engineering Researches (ICECER)
abstract: We introduce a hysteretic SrTiO<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">3</inf> ionic FET whose gate-controlled oxygen-vacancy drift-diffusion endows the device with a 0.1 – 1 s leaky-integration time constant, while dissipating only 50 pJ per spike. By inserting, removing, or substituting a nanometre-scale interfacial layer (Parylene-C, HfO<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</inf>, or Al<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</inf>O<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">3</inf>) for the gate insulator, we tune the leaky-integration timescale drastically without altering the channel geometry. A slow-neuron model derived from this transistor, when mapped onto an FPGA reservoir, learns the fine-grained stroke dynamics of a person drawing a triangle and subsequently performs user verification: a triangle redrawn by the same person is accepted, whereas an ostensibly identical triangle drawn by an impostor is rejected. Reservoirs built from fast-neurons (10 μs) fail at the same task, underscoring the need for biologically matched timescales. Compared with state-of-the-art artificial neurons, our SrTiO<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">3</inf>-based neuron offers (i) an intrinsic time constant among the longest reported for long-timescale devices, (ii) energy consumption of only ca. ten picowatts per spike, and (iii) excellent endurance exceeding 10<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">8</sup> switching cycles. These results establish Slow Electronics—solid-state circuits that compute on human timescales—as a promising route to privacy-preserving, battery-friendly edge AI.

title: Enhanced Approaches for Plant Disease Recognition Across Varied Conditions: Controlled Environment and Field Settings Using Deep Learning
authors: Tamilarasi M, Gowtham M, Yogeshwaran C
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent and Innovative Technologies in Computing, Electrical and Electronics (IITCEE)
abstract: Plant diseases are a major danger to global food security resulting in large crop losses and economic consequences. Disease detection must be done early and accurately in order to treat and mitigate diseases effectively. Convolutional Neural Networks (CNNs) have recently exhibited extraordinary effectiveness in image recognition tasks, making them a promising tool for detecting plant diseases based on visual symptoms. This study introduces a new CNN algorithm intended exclusively for plant disease detection. The suggested CNN architecture is designed to detect complicated patterns and variations in plant illnesses in photos, improving disease classification accuracy and resilience. An extensive dataset covering a wide variety of plant species and various disease signs is collected and pre-processed help improve the model's capacity for generalization and standardize image quality. The weights of the CNN are optimized throughout the training process using a stochastic gradient descent approach on the pre-processed dataset. The model's hyper parameters are fine-tuned to avoid overfitting and improve performance on a different validation dataset. The trained CNN is extensively tested on an independent test dataset, assessing accuracy, precision, recall, F1 score, and generating a confusion matrix.

title: Quantum Computing: Challenges, Innovations, and Future Directions
authors: Emily Clifton, Chuck Easttom
publish: 2026 IEEE 16th Annual Computing and Communication Workshop and Conference (CCWC)
abstract: Current Quantum computing involves the convergence of quantum computing and high-performance computing. The near intermediate scale quantum computing (NISQ) promises to solve problems intractable for classical systems. The current paper explores the theoretical foundations, advancements in hardware and software, challenges such as scalability and error correction, and future directions for quantum computing. Updated details on IBM's Osprey and Condor processors highlight recent hardware innovations. Additionally, the mechanisms of superconducting quantum computing, including Josephson junctions and cryogenic systems, are elaborated. Through an analysis of major quantum processors and hybrid architectures, this work highlights the transformative potential of quantum computing in optimization, cryptography, and scientific simulations.

title: A Framework for Autonomous Podcast Generation
authors: Ashwin N Mysore, Shreesha M Rao, M Pramath, Laxmi V
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent and Innovative Technologies in Computing, Electrical and Electronics (IITCEE)
abstract: The fast and massive growth of digital audio content, especially podcasts, has created a significant demand for scalable and efficient content creation tools. While production has traditionally been a manual, resource-intensive process involving extensive scripting, recording, and post-production, recent advancements in Artificial Intelligence (AI) present a huge potential for streamlining and automating content ideation and creation. This paper reviews the key technologies forming the basis of automated audio content creation—ranging from generative audio models to advanced prompting techniques—and proposes using an agentic AI system designed to pioneer fully autonomous podcast generation. By leveraging Large Language Models (LLMs) in a structured orchestration framework, our framework allows the system to generate an entire podcast episode from a single user-provided topic. We make use of the potential of prompt-based models to generate dialogues, to make sure the result is natural, coherent, and lifelike, ultimately resulting access to high-quality audio content creation.

title: From Fuzzy Insights to Strategic Impact: Integrating AURA with q-Rung Orthopair Fuzzy Sets in Multi-Criteria Decision-Making
authors: Muhammad Mukhlis Kamarul Zaman, Zahari Md Rodzi, Yusrina Andu, Ahmad Bazli Khairuddin, Nur Fathiah Fatin Mohamad Fauzi, Wan Syaidatul Izzati Wan Abdul Rahman
publish: 2025 IEEE 14th International Conference on Engineering Education (ICEED)
abstract: In this work, the q-Rung Orthopair Fuzzy Sets (q-ROFS) and Adaptive Utility Ranking Algorithm (AURA) are integrated to tackle uncertain and hesitant situations in multicriteria decision-making (MCDM). The result of fuzzy q-ROFS is determined using a new defuzzification formula so that it fits comfortably within AURA. Through the more expressive characteristics of q-ROFS, this integration makes it easier to form a flexible model of membership and non-membership and also utilized the advance properties of AURA with respect to normalization and ranking. The proposed q-ROFS-AURA method is used to assess five widely used educational technology platforms against measures such as usability, money, engagement, and technology performance. The results show that the framework manages uncertain judgments from experts, helping to reliably rank the strategies. This type of analysis provides decision-makers with a powerful method for deciding on the best options whenever the environment is unclear, especially important for education technology assessment. This study links q-ROFS theory to existing fuzzy ranking algorithms which helps improve the accuracy and insight gained from their use.

title: Efficient prediction of Ocular disease using deep learning model
authors: A. Sahaya Anselin Nisha, K. Rambabu, Ramavath Bikku, V. Priya, Nettem Adithya Valli, B. Venkataramanaiah, K. B. Anusha
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Artificial Intelligence for Innovations in Healthcare Industries (ICAIIHI)
abstract: Eyes are more sensitive and vital organs, and diseases like Glaucoma, Cataract and Diabetic Retinopathy can lead to vision problems or blindness if not detected early. People, especially in villagers and low-resource areas, don’t have easy facility to ophthalmologists or advanced treatment tools. Early disease prediction is difficult but often limited by the availability of expert doctors and manual treatment complexities. To address this issues, The proposed work process automated eye disease detection system using the deep learning models. Fundus images are preprocessed first to enhance the features and then given into a convolutional neural network(CNN) based detection model to identify ocular conditions. The proposed model is trained and tested publicly available dataset(ODIR-5K). Enabling multi-class classification of ocular diseases. The proposed system produces high accuracy in disease prediction , decrease diagnostic time in early screening. This Proposed automated research can support large -scale eye screening tool for opthalmologists and contributing to timely treatment and prevention vision loss

title: Huddle.ai: An AI-Driven Platform for Real-Time Collaboration and Mentorship in Virtual Environments
authors: Geetha M P, Barath Raj P, Karthikeyan M, Logabaalan R S
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent and Innovative Technologies in Computing, Electrical and Electronics (IITCEE)
abstract: Communication platforms play vital roles in the virtual workplace of the twenty first century in encouraging professional growth, learning and business development. Nevertheless, lack of confidence, scarcity of engagement and access to quality mentorship continue to be a problem to many users. In one of these attempts, we present Huddle.ai, an AI-based platform that reinvents virtual interviews, mentoring, and skill development by providing an interactive and video-based experience. This system includes two applications that are strictly connected with each other, Huddle Pro is a set of AI-based assessment tools that are offered to organizations, and Huddle.ai (User) is a personalized coaching space offered to individuals. Huddle Pro is an intelligent, automated scheduling, live testing, automatic marking, data analytics, integrity testing, and session summaries of video, transcripts, and summaries. Huddle.ai (User) also allows its users to develop their own AI mentors, have personal training, and be supported by realistic digital avatars, meaning that engagement and confidence are improved. Huddle.ai's design capabilities also include a number of features that are likely to be useful for understanding natural language, visual analysis, as well as for interactive AI experiences; these all have implications for how virtual communication will occur and support the creation of successful organizational systems as well as quality guidance experiences. This research focuses on the unique technological framework, special advantages and leads to characteristics of Huddle.ai in areas such as corporate learning, employee development, and professional development in a work environment.

title: Modeling Housewives’ Stress Levels Using IndoBERT with the Support of Indonesian Linguistic Features
authors: Indrawan Wijaya, Maria Susan Anggreainy
publish: 2025 2nd Beyond Technology Summit on Informatics International Conference (BTS-I2C)
abstract: Housework is a huge load that most housewives must carry out daily. Housewives’ daily life can be stressful because of their heavy and repetitive workload, especially when they lack moral support from those closest to them while doing all the labor. In this paper, a system for classifying a person’s mental stress condition using IndoBERT will be built and compared between a pure fine-tuned model and a model enhanced with linguistic features to help in early detection and intervention for stress condition management among housewives in the household.
