The model’s training dataset was obtained by scraping an Indonesian housewife’s forum on social media, generating roughly three thousand forum posts in total. The collected dataset will then be labeled for stress levels using a lexicon-based approach before proceeding with the overall training and evaluation process. The results reveal that the pure fine-tuned end to end IndoBERT slightly outperforms the IndoBERT model enriched with linguistic features, with an accuracy value of 0.9078 and weighted F1-Score of 0.9071.

title: YOLO-Based Enhancement Method for Accurate Chili Leaf Disease Detection
authors: Miwan Kurniawan Hidayat, Jufriadif Na'Am, Ferda Ernawan
publish: 2025 2nd Beyond Technology Summit on Informatics International Conference (BTS-I2C)
abstract: Chili is one of the strategic horticultural commodities with high economic value and extensive cultivation rates globally. This plant is very susceptible to various leaf diseases such as cercospora leaf spot, powdery mildew, nutritional deficiencies, as well as pest attacks mites and thrips. Early detection of leaf diseases is an important aspect of modern agricultural management, but manual visual identification methods are still limited in terms of speed, range, and accuracy. This study aims to improve the accuracy and reliability of chili leaf disease detection through the application of four You Only Look Once (YOLO) architectures, namely YOLOv8s, YOLOv9s, YOLOv10s, and YOLOv11s. The testing was conducted using a COLD dataset covering five classes of diseases with structured data sharing for training, validation, and testing. The evaluation results showed that YOLOv11s achieved the most balanced performance with an F1-Score of 0.836, a mAP50-95 of 0.807 and a total operating time of 9.3 ms. The combination of several excellent components in the YOLOv11s architecture is able to maintain visual information from finely textured areas and small patches. Overall, the YOLO model proved to be effective in adaptively recognizing leaf patterns and textures, surpassing the performance of previous Xception models.

title: CrackSense: High-Accuracy Solar Panel Inspection via Ensemble Learning
authors: Tiasa Jana, Shashi Bhushan, I Malaserene, Uddipan Sarkar, Advait Kankati, Tanupriya Choudhury
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Internet of Things: Smart Innovation and Usages (IoT-SIU)
abstract: Solar panel micro-cracks pose a serious threat in photovoltaic (PV) system maintenance due to their ability to reduce power output while accelerating degradation. The small fissures and cracks in the photovoltaic cells might be invisible to naked eyes but causes a great damage. Traditional inspection techniques are labour-intensive and expensive, thereby generating the need for automated detection systems. This work provides a new method based on an upgraded ensemble architecture with three different models to automatically detect micro-cracks in solar panels. The three different deep leaning networks used are - Deep Regularized Network, Simple Network and Medium Network. The 764 solar panel picture dataset comprising 506 functional, 258 non-functional photos were pre-processed, normalized, and then applied to the ensemble model. Experimentally, we have achieved 0.965 accuracy utilizing our method with optimal threshold settings, showcasing robust performance. With precision of 0.97, recall of 0.965, and F1-score of 0.9675 (weighted average), the model exhibits strong performance through different evaluation metrics. The strong ROC AUC of 0.9744 further supports the strength of the discriminating capacity of the model. This work presents a major contribution to automated solar panel inspection systems by providing a dependable and effective method for crack detection that can improve panel lifetime and hence maintenance efficiency

title: Palm Vein Biometrics under Different External Conditions: Data Collection & Assessment
authors: Suhas Chate, Vijay Patil, Yuvraj Parkale, Shailendrakumar Mukane
publish: 2025 World Conference on Cutting-Edge Science and Technology (WCCEST)
abstract: Palm vein biometrics is a non-contact modality that has unique vascular patterns, although its resistance to real-life conditions, including dust, lotion, and temperature changes, is poorly studied. This paper presents a novel dataset of 500 palm vein region of interest images of 100 subjects, which were collected under five external conditions: normal, dust, lotion, hot and cold. The image robustness is measured against six complementary quality measures, specifically Global Contrast Factor, blind Signal-to-Noise Ratio, entropy, Tenengrad sharpness, edge density and noise estimation by median absolute deviation. Systematic comparison between preprocessing methods, between baseline, Histogram Equalization, and CLAHE using uniform and Rayleigh distribution is statistically validated using one-way ANOVA. Findings indicate that the worst and most critical quality degradation is due to lotion and dust, Histogram Equalization gives the best contrast but increases noise, and CLAHE with uniform distribution gives the best trade-off between entropy, edge density and clarity although with high running time. The work is the first multi-metric set of data and assessment of palm vein biometrics in adverse external conditions, providing useful information to consider when developing adaptive and deployable recognition systems.

title: Performance Analysis of Serial and Parallel Computing Techniques for Natural Language Processing Tasks
authors: Mallegowda M, Vishal Chaurasia, Vansh Nahar, Uditanshu Kamal, Vijender, Susheel Seervi H
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent and Innovative Technologies in Computing, Electrical and Electronics (IITCEE)
abstract: This paper explores the efficiency and scalability of serial and parallel computing techniques applied to common Natural Language Processing (NLP) tasks, including keyword extraction, sentiment analysis, named entity recognition, and text summarization. Using a combination of Python libraries such as spaCy, transformers, Dask, and multiprocessing, we evaluate the execution time and memory usage for these tasks on varying dataset sizes and numbers of processing threads. The experiments are conducted on a system with an integrated AMD Radeon GPU, where all computations run on the CPU due to the lack of CUDA support. The challenges and limitations of utilizing GPU acceleration for NLP tasks on such hardware are also discussed. Our findings reveal that while serial execution is straightforward, parallel computing can significantly reduce execution time for large datasets when appropriately configured. However, increasing the number of threads beyond the system's core count can lead to performance degradation due to overhead from context switching, memory contention, and the Global Interpreter Lock (GIL). Additionally, the limitations of integrated GPUs in supporting popular NLP frameworks are analyzed. Recommendations for optimal thread usage, multiprocessing strategies, and profiling techniques are provided to improve the performance of NLP workloads. The study highlights the importance of understanding system architecture and task characteristics when designing efficient NLP pipelines. These insights can benefit researchers and practitioners aiming to optimize NLP workflows in resource-constrained environments.

title: Innovative Enterprise Think Tank Enhancing Value Creation Mechanisms
authors: Yaqiong Tan
publish: 2025 25th Conference of the Electric Power Supply Industry (CEPSI)
abstract: This study takes China Shenhua Energy Company Limited (China Shenhua) as a case to explore the innovative practices of the new enterprise think tank system in the energy industry. By constructing a "four-dimensional, one-core" governance framework (coordination layer, supervision layer, connectivity layer, and activated scenario layer) and integrating intelligent tagging technology, Robotic process automation(RPA), a Text Convolutional Neural Network(TextCNN) error detection model, and the Apriori association rule algorithm, the research achieves intelligent control over the entire report compilation process, highlighting the key value of report cycle management in corporate knowledge. Practical results demonstrate that this system improves report compilation efficiency by 69%±3 %, reduces error rates by 80%±3 %, cuts labor costs by 50%±3 %, and significantly saves management expenses. Additionally, it has generated core technological achievements, including three nationally authorized invention patents and one national software copyright. The study presents a replicable paradigm of human-machine collaboration combined with knowledge chain reconstruction for the digital transformation of the energy industry, which facilitates the modernization of corporate governance and sustainable development.

title: Open Source Autonomous Vehicle as a Safety and Transportation Research Platform
authors: Hyun Sik Oh, Hwi Chan Eun, Zillur Rahman, Paul Oh, Brendan Morris
publish: 2026 IEEE 16th Annual Computing and Communication Workshop and Conference (CCWC)
abstract: This paper presents the implementation of SAE Level-4 autonomous driving using UNLV's autonomous research vehicle and demonstrates how an open-source architecture can serve as a flexible platform for safety and transportation research. Using Ubuntu, ROS 2 Humble, the Autoware Universe stack, the LIO-SAM mapping algorithm, and open-source sensor drivers, we achieved Level-4 autonomous driving along a <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\approx 0.8 ~\text{km}$</tex> campus route between Parking Lot <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{R}$</tex> and Parking Lot <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{P}$</tex>. On the same vehicle and software stack, we then implemented a Time-toCollision (TTC) calculator based on LiDAR range and CANbased speed in approximately four hours and used it to collect and analyze low-speed approach data toward a fixed obstacle. Together with prior work on pedestrian emergency braking, lane detection, and LiDAR-camera fusion on this platform, these experiments show that while open-source research vehicles are not yet practical for consumer deployment, they are ideal for reproducible, customizable studies of safety metrics, human-vehicle interaction, and the relationship between transportation systems and infrastructure.

title: PULSAR: Advancing Interval-Based Time Series Classification to State-of-the-Art Performance
authors: Nestor Cabello, Lars Kulik
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Data Mining (ICDM)
abstract: State-of-the-art Time Series Classification (TSC) models such as HC2 and MultiRocket-Hydra reach high accuracy but rely on representations that are inherently difficult to interpret. Interval-based classifiers summarize local segments and thus provide more intuitive features. However, they are usually behind the most accurate methods. Our approach PULSAR—Pooled mUlti-scaLe Summaries from rAndomized inteRvals significantly enhances the accuracy of interval-based approaches and is close to MultiRocket-Hydra. PULSAR first converts every series into several representations (raw, derivative, periodogram, and others). It then uses sub-series of varied length and dilation and computes simple local statistics. To build higher-order features, it partitions each sequence of local statistics at multiple depths, applies a randomly chosen set of pooling operators to every partition, and keeps all aggregates from the coarsest level. For finer partitions we apply a supervised feature selection strategy to retain only the most discriminative features. Finally, we concatenate the selected aggregates with the global ones and train an ensemble classifier. We test PULSAR on 142 UCR datasets. It outperforms all interval-based approaches by a statistically significant margin and matches the predictive performance of HC2 and MultiRocket-Hydra. PULSAR sets a new benchmark for interval-based TSC while preserving a feature structure that we can still interpret.

title: A Novel Hybrid Deep Learning Approach for Brain Tumor Classification from MRI Images with Grad-CAM Interpretability
authors: Ahmad Abumihsan, Majdi Owda, Amani Yousef Owda, Fathi Gasir, Mobarak Abumohsen
publish: 2025 2nd Beyond Technology Summit on Informatics International Conference (BTS-I2C)
abstract: Early and precise diagnosis of brain tumors is essential for successful treatment planning and improved patient outcomes. This paper introduces a novel hybrid deep model that incorporates DenseNet121, a convolutional neural network (CNN), and the Swin Transformer, a vision transformer model, by feature-level fusion to classify brain tumors from magnetic resonance imaging (MRI) scans. The suggested method provides a more discriminative and better representation by uniting the global context capability of the Transformer model with the local feature extraction capability of the CNN model. The suggested method was trained and assessed on a publicly available brain MRI dataset of four classes: glioma, meningioma, pituitary tumor, and no tumor. Experimental results indicate that the proposed approach outperforms many baseline models including VGG16, MobileNetV2, and AlexNet with an accuracy of 99.39%, precision of 99.36%, recall of 99.34%, and F1-score of 99.35%. Grad-CAM was utilized to visualize class-discriminative regions in the MRI scans to enhance interpretability, hence validating the model’s emphasis on tumor-relevant regions. These outcomes prove the efficacy of coupling Transformer and CNN architectures in obtaining accurate and interpretable brain tumor classification from MRI scans.

title: Adaptive Motion Prediction for Precision Interaction with Dynamic Platforms
authors: Ebenezer Afrifa, Mohammadreza Mehrabian
publish: 2026 IEEE 16th Annual Computing and Communication Workshop and Conference (CCWC)
abstract: Autonomous precision interactions with dynamic platforms by heterogeneous robotic systems remain challenging due to the need for accurate real-time motion prediction under regime changes and strict computational constraints. Building on prior LSTM-plus-Kalman filtering approaches, we identify three limitations for safety-critical hardware deployment: fixed-sequence prediction requiring horizon indexing, reliance on a single offline-trained predictor with no online adaptation, and limited robustness under curved and accelerating target motion. We present a hybrid framework that couples a high-level setpoint controller with multi-method, ML-based target-motion prediction. The prediction stack fuses a horizon-aware LSTM (conditioned directly on the query horizon), a Gaussian Process with uncertainty, and a linear velocity model via confidence-weighted selection and plausibility guards. An asynchronous online-learning mechanism updates the LSTM during flight without disrupting the 20 Hz control loop. Hardware experiments on a Crazyflie 2.0 demonstrate substantially lower landing error and higher success rate than a representative LSTM +KF baseline across a repeatable scenario with straight and circular segments. Bootstrap confidence intervals confirm the reliability of the improvements. These results show that a high-level setpoint controller, augmented with learned, horizon-aware prediction and online adaptation, offers a practical path to robust, precise engagement with moving platforms in safety-critical heterogeneous robotic applications.

title: Blockchain-Secured Tracking System
authors: Bhavya V, Vinutha B R, Avani Ivy, G. Sai Chaitra, Ashmita Chavan
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent and Innovative Technologies in Computing, Electrical and Electronics (IITCEE)
abstract: Supply chain management in the automotive spare parts industry faces persistent issues, such as counterfeit products, inefficient verification processes, and limited traceability of spare parts during their lifecycle in the supply chain. To address these issues, we propose a Blockchain Secured Tracking System partially integrated with an AI Chatbot that provides dedicated consumer support. Each spare part issued in the supply chain is assigned a unique digital identity (QR codes), with all events in a part's lifecycle, from manufacturing through distribution, to purchase, recorded immutably on a decentralized blockchain ledger. The AI Chatbot is a consumer-support assistant that answers question and provides assistance on spare part specifications, warranty information, and historical data, as well as supporting users in their system interactions. Smart contracts are leveraged to automate essential engagements such as ownership transfer, warranty transfer, and certification verification for parts-automating will reduce manual intervention and increase operational efficiency. Simulation results demonstrate the proposed system enhances traceability, reduces the circulation of counterfeits in the supply chain industry, and ultimately will increase confidence due to verified, transparent records. Overall, the solution provides a secure, scalable, hybrid blockchain-AI framework providing a usercentred experience ensuring certainty, accountability, and consumer engagement throughout the automotive spare parts ecosystem.

title: Machine-Readable Ads: Accessibility and Trust Patterns for AI Web Agents interacting with Online Advertisements
authors: Joel Nitu, Heidrun Mühle, Andreas Stöckl
publish: 2025 International Conference on Electrical and Computer Engineering Researches (ICECER)
abstract: Autonomous multimodal language models are rapidly evolving into web agents that can browse, click, and purchase items on behalf of users, posing a threat to display advertising designed for human eyes. Yet little is known about how these agents interact with ads or which design principles ensure reliable engagement. To address this, we ran a controlled experiment using a faithful clone of the news site of the Tiroler Tageszeitung, developed through a multi-stage pipeline using Figma, Bolt.new, and Cursor, seeded with diverse ads like static banners, GIFs, carousels, videos, cookie dialogues, and paywalls. We ran 300 initial trials plus follow-ups using the Document Object Model (DOM)-centric Browser Use framework with GPT-4o, Claude 3.7 Sonnet, Gemini 2.0 Flash, and the pixel-based OpenAI Operator, across 10 realistic user tasks regarding e.g. subscription flows, promotional searches and article summarization. Our results show these agents display severe satisficing: they never scroll beyond two viewports and ignore purely visual calls to action, clicking banners only when semantic button overlays or off-screen text labels are present. Critically, when sweepstake participation required a purchase, GPT-4o and Claude 3.7 Sonnet subscribed in 100% of trials, and Gemini 2.0 Flash in 70%, revealing gaps in cost-benefit analysis. We identified five actionable design principles, semantic overlays, hidden labels, top-left placement, static frames, and di-alogue replacement, that make human-centric creatives machine-detectable without harming user experience. We also evaluated agent trustworthiness through "behavior patterns" such as cookie consent handling and subscription choices, highlighting model-specific risk boundaries and the urgent need for robust trust evaluation frameworks in real-world advertising.

title: Enhancing Heart Disease Prediction with Reliable Machine Learning Models
authors: Manita, Ginika Mahajan, Nirmal Kumar Gupta
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Artificial Intelligence for Innovations in Healthcare Industries (ICAIIHI)
abstract: Millions of fatalities worldwide are caused by cardiovascular disorders. They are the leading cause of death globally. Detecting these diseases early can help reduce the death rate and the overall impact of heart disease. However, traditional diagnostic methods have several challenges, including misdiagnoses and delays in treatment. These issues can complicate cases and raise medical costs. A promising way to tackle these problems is by using machine learning algorithms and artificial intelligence methods. This research focuses on accurate cardiovascular illness prediction and highlights the essential role of machine learning in heart health. This research article primarily focuses on identifying patients who, given a variety of medical characteristics, are more likely to suffer heart disease. Different types of machine learning (ML) algorithms like logistic regression, support vector machines (SVM), and decision tree, to predict and classify patients with heart disease is used. The suggested model demonstrated a satisfactory level of strength and demonstrated the ability to precisely predict the existence of heart disease in an individual based on KNN and Logistic Regression. The highest accuracy of 96.05% is achieved using the Decision Tree and UCI repository open access dataset is used. This accuracy was better than that of earlier state-of-the-art models.

title: Enhanced Visibility Wavelet Dehazing using Integrated CLAHE for Improved Image Clarity
authors: Akhil Sehgal, Aarti Gautam Dinker
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Internet of Things: Smart Innovation and Usages (IoT-SIU)
abstract: This paper solves the issue of haze in images due to atmospheric conditions which may be indoor or outdoor. The paper proposes an enhanced dehazing technique called Enhanced Visibility Wavelet Dehazing, which combines Contrast Limited Adaptive Histogram Equalization in a wavelet transform framework with Dark Channel Prior and guided filtering. Wavelet-based decomposition supports the retention of edge details while maintaining the effectiveness of dark channel for haze estimation. Integration of adaptive histogram equalization enhances the contrast and visibility in hazy images. Implementation and comparative evaluation are done on MATLAB using datasets of hazy images. Experimental results have shown that the proposed EVWD approach achieved perceived significant improvement in image quality with high contrast and entropy values compared to the conventional approaches. Improved visibility and recovery of detail in the processed images are further validated quantitatively by means of fog density, entropy, and contrast metrics.

title: Virtual Energy Manager: Enabling Smart Energy Ecosystems
authors: Muhammad Ibrahim Rozman, Ir. Ts. Mahesvaran Sibeperegasam, Nur Shuhada Abdullah, Ir. Ts. Siti Ruzainida Ibrahim, Muhamad Firdaus Nazren
publish: 2025 25th Conference of the Electric Power Supply Industry (CEPSI)
abstract: The transition towards a more intelligent and efficient energy ecosystem demands solutions that go beyond traditional monitoring methods. Initially designed as a data visualization and analytics tool, the Virtual Energy Manager (VEM) provided businesses with real-time insights into their energy consumption. By interfacing with digital power meters and customer meter pulse signals, VEM enabled users to monitor energy usage, identify inefficiencies, and improve consumption patterns. As industries strive for enhanced energy optimization and market engagement, VEM is evolving into a comprehensive energy aggregation platform. This transformation is driven by advanced analytics, predictive capabilities, and demand-side management strategies. A pivotal advancement in this evolution is the Maximum Demand (MD) Forecast, which allows consumers to predict and manage their peak demand, thereby reducing costs and boosting operational efficiency. Additionally, automated reporting and data-driven recommendations support better energy decision-making and regulatory compliance. By leveraging machine learning and predictive analytics, VEM is expanding its capabilities to consolidate and optimize energy usage across multiple consumers, facilitating demand response participation and grid stabilization. This development positions VEM as a catalyst for a decentralized and intelligent energy management ecosystem, where consumers can proactively manage, exchange, and optimize their energy portfolios. This study explores the technological advancements, strategic planning, and practical applications driving VEM's transformation. Examples from early adopters will demonstrate how businesses and industries benefit from this evolving platform, underscoring its potential to revolutionize energy management and aggregation.

title: Alpha-SFC: Transformer-Guided Monte Carlo Tree Search with Multi-Path Routing for Online Service Function Chain Embedding
authors: Stephen Reilly, Yang Wang, Maryam Jalalitabar
publish: 2026 IEEE 16th Annual Computing and Communication Workshop and Conference (CCWC)
abstract: Network Function Virtualization (NFV) enables service providers to deploy network functions as software on general-purpose infrastructure, increasing flexibility and resource efficiency. Many of these services require an ordered sequence of virtual functions, known as a Service Function Chain (SFC), to be embedded and routed across the substrate under tight resource constraints. The Service Function Chain Embedding (SFC-E) involves mapping virtual sequences of network services and their virtual links onto a physical substrate network. Many solutions have been developed for this problem, ranging from exact optimization methods to heuristic and evolutionary algorithms. More recent work has focused on deep reinforcement learning (DRL) to model the problem as a sequential decision process. However, standard DRL requires extensive sampling and faces sparse, delayed rewards, obscuring which decisions caused acceptance versus rejection. In this work, we propose Alpha-SFC, an AlphaZero-style framework that combines a graph-based policy-value transformer with a Monte Carlo Tree Search (MCTS) planner. Our contributions are twofold. First, the neural network guides the search, enabling the system to focus on promising placements and prune early with a clearer evaluation than pure MCTS alone. Second, we integrate in-tree multi-path routing during learning: at each node placement, the planner enumerates up to k loopless shortest candidates for each newly realized virtual edge and commits during expansion to the first bandwidth-feasible path, allowing search and reservation to co-evolve inside MCTS. This coupling—rather than performing post-hoc routing outside the planner—consistently improves acceptance and overall embedding efficiency across a range of routing budgets.

title: AI-Based Symptom Checker Tools: Towards Accessible and Reliable Primary Healthcare Support
authors: Gaurav Sharma, Manoj kumar Ojha, Dr Prinima Gupta, Anjum Rouf, Yogesh Kumar Rathore, Shivam kumar rao
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Artificial Intelligence for Innovations in Healthcare Industries (ICAIIHI)
abstract: The swift development of artificial intelligence (AI) is revolutionizing primary care through the introduction of new diagnostic, triage, and decision support tools. Current research demonstrates the adoption of AI-powered symptom checkers, chatbots, and clinical decision support systems to increase accessibility, efficiency, and diagnostic precision in various settings, ranging from rural areas to low-resource environments. Evidence indicates that AI-powered tools have the capacity to automate patient-provider interactions, enhance condition detection early on for diseases like Alzheimer's disease and skin conditions, and minimize waiting times via automated self- triage. Systematic reviews and scoping reviews provide encouraging evidence on accuracy, cost, and patient empowerment but also indicate problems with data quality, physician uptake, clinical validation, and ethics. In low- and middle-income nations, AI has tremendous potential to improve workforce scarcity and enhance delivery of care. But issues regarding algorithmic bias, small datasets, and regulatory blocks highlight the necessity for thorough scrutiny and proper implementation. What is emerging from case series and randomized trials is that whereas AI cannot substitute doctors, it can assist in decision-making, improve patient engagement, and facilitate sustainable health models. In general, AI-based solutions have the potential to be central to reframing primary care towards more individualized, fair, and technology- facilitated health systems.

title: CARE-PCM: Classification and Analysis of Reliability with Efficient Models for PCM
authors: Yanilette Montano, Marjan Asadinia
publish: 2026 IEEE 16th Annual Computing and Communication Workshop and Conference (CCWC)
abstract: Phase Change Memory (PCM) is an emerging nonvolatile memory technology known for its scalability, high read speed, and potential to replace conventional DRAM. However, endurance limitations pose challenges to long-term reliability, making the prediction of the health status critical for system stability. This study investigates lightweight and interpretable machine learning models such as Logistic Regression and Decision Trees to classify PCM cells into healthy, degraded, and near failure categories using operational indicators such as write count, error frequency, and temperature. The primary focus was on simulations using a standard memory size, with additional experiments conducted on larger memory configurations to assess the model's scalability. Among the models tested, the balanced decision tree achieved the highest accuracy of 98.4%, while logistic regression reached 92%. In imbalanced data, the Decision Tree maintained robust performance with 99.8% accuracy, whereas Logistic Regression dropped 81%, struggling especially with minority class detection. Feature importance analysis revealed that write count and error frequency were the most predictive indicators, particularly for identifying Near-Failure cells. These results highlight the potential of lightweight classifiers as a practical and cost-effective solution for real-time PCM health monitoring.

title: A Dual-Phase Attention-Guided Deep Learning Framework for Imbalanced Diabetic Retinopathy Detection and Feature Localization
authors: Sudha. K, Priya. R
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent and Innovative Technologies in Computing, Electrical and Electronics (IITCEE)
abstract: Diabetic Retinopathy (DR) is currently among the major causes of visual disability among diabetic patients and the right diagnosis at an early stage using automated image based systems requires. Even though deep learning models have been successful in healthcare in medical imaging, they still decline when class imbalance and lesion heterogeneity occur. As a work, this paper presents a DualPhase Attention-Guided Deep Learning (DP-AGDL) architecture that can be used to detect instances of binary DR and lesion-level informative deep reading on retinal fundus images. The proposed model combines two synergistic steps: Lesion-Aware Feature Encoding (LAFE) with a adapted residual backbone and Spatial-Channel Squeeze-and-Excitation (SCSE) attention to highlight clinically useful areas, and Flection Fusion (ARF) with focal loss and dynamically changing importance to address imbalance in datasets. Transfer learning using EfficientNet increases generalization and gradient centralization and adaptive learning rate timetable increase convergence capacity. Benchmark analysis of eyePACS and Messidor-2 illustrates that DP-AGWL outperforms current CNN and transformer-based baselines in terms of sensitivity (94.7 %), and AUC (0.982). Besides, Grad-CAM visualization affirms that the model has the interpretability necessary to be used in clinical decision-making during early DR screening.

title: Energy-Aware Speed Regulation of Electric Vehicles Using Type-2 Interval Fuzzy Logic Control
authors: Parwinder Kaur Dhillon, Rajinder Vir, Parvinder Singh, Amandeep Kaur, Zeenat Zahra, Raghu Raja Sharma
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent and Innovative Technologies in Computing, Electrical and Electronics (IITCEE)
abstract: One of the most crucial challenges for electric vehicles (EVs) is dependable and efficient control of speed regulation. Control strategies must consider tracking performance, comfort and energy trade-offs under varying operational conditions. This research presents the Energy-Aware Speed Regulation (EASR) approach using Type-2 Interval Fuzzy Logic Controller (IT2-FLC) primarily to optimize the performance and energy use of EVs. The designed controller employs fuzzy rules to adapt motor torque commands in real time using the speed error, error change, and state-of-charge (SOC) variables. Unlike standard Type-1 fuzzy or PID controllers, the IT2-FLC takes advantage of the Footprint of Uncertainty (FOU) to handle variable road grade, load changes, and road grade, load changes, and sensor perturbations. An additional control layer aimed at energy supervision targets SOC levels at the expense of electrical power loss to control oscillations. Control law, fuzzy rules and membership function activation levels are fine-tuned using PSO. Simulation results showed the effectiveness of the IT2-FLC in energy savings of 7-10% and improved tracking performance over PID controllers and Type-1 fuzzy controllers, across simulated driving cycles (WLTC, FTP-75, and US06) in MATLAB/Simulink. The findings validate that the amalgamation of interval Type-2 fuzzy reasoning with energy-aware supervision yields a resilient and computationally viable solution for real-time electric vehicle speed control applications.

title: Aggregated and DLL-Integrated PSCAD Modeling of Multipulse Rectifiers for Hydrogen Electrolyzers
authors: Sumit Prakash Patil, Ibrahim Makandar, Rahul Halappagol
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent and Innovative Technologies in Computing, Electrical and Electronics (IITCEE)
abstract: Green hydrogen is crucial for achieving carbon emission targets in most major economies, and electrolysis is currently the most optimal method to produce scales of green hydrogen required. The AC-DC rectifier is a key component in such hydrogen production plants - typically operating at a multi-megawatt scale. Robust and reliable grid connectivity of such hydrogen plant depends largely on the rectifier performance. Detailed simulation models of rectifier systems are needed at various design and commissioning stages to ensure grid code compliance. These models are commonly developed using tools like PSCAD and Power Factory and can be broadly classified as open-source (aggregated) or encrypted (detailed) models. This paper presents both an aggregated modelling approach for thyristor rectifiers (6-pulse, 12-pulse, and 18-pulse) and an external code software-based modelling approach for a 6-pulse thyristor rectifier. The proposed methodology uses dynamically linked libraries (DLL) and Fortran wrapper to enhance PSCAD/EMTDC simulation capabilities, enabling high-fidelity modelling of complex power conversion systems. Results demonstrate the effectiveness of DLL-enhanced PSCAD modelling in power electronic interfaces for electrolysis applications using a 6-pulse thyristor rectifier.

title: Dynamic $\boldsymbol{K}$-Means Clustering for Sybil Attack Detection in VANETs
authors: Gideon Adele, Anirudh Paranjothi, Mohammad S. Khan
publish: 2026 IEEE 16th Annual Computing and Communication Workshop and Conference (CCWC)
abstract: Detecting Sybil attacks in Vehicular Ad hoc Networks (VANETs) presents the challenge of validating proposed detection methods across varying scenarios because many proposed detection methods are not validated across diverse scenarios. Clustering also poses difficulties, as determining the optimal number of clusters <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(k)$</tex> is a complex problem. In traditional clustering algorithms, the number of clusters (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\boldsymbol{k}$</tex>) can vary depending on the type of VANET scenario considered in determining the appropriate value of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\boldsymbol{k}$</tex>. To solve this problem of optimal selection of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$k$</tex>, a dynamic <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$k$</tex>-means clustering framework, which adaptively determines the number of clusters required to group nodes and detect Sybil nodes with high accuracy, is developed. This method effectively clusters nodes in VANETs while identifying Sybil attacks. OMNET++ and SUMO simulators are used for simulation experiments. The proposed approach achieves an average accuracy of 99%, an average adjusted rand index of 0.91, and an average completeness of 0.76 across different scenarios and varying percentages of attackers on the network.

title: Revealing the Path to Future Business Decision-Making: A Survey of AI Awareness Among Management Students
authors: Shagun Tyagi, Sidhi Verma, Ashulekha Gupta, Veethika Gupta
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Internet of Things: Smart Innovation and Usages (IoT-SIU)
abstract: The current study investigates the relationship between management students’ corporate decisionmaking, education, and awareness related to the usage of artificial intelligence (AI). Further current study focused on the perception of management students in utilising AI tools in both their academic and professional work. The result of the study reveals a positive relationship between dependent and independent variables. As the perception of students is found to be positive towards AI utilisation, the education industry should train and aware them. Government policies and support are needed in this process.

title: Towards Transparent Planetary Defense: Explainability-Driven AI Approaches for Near-Earth Object Hazard Prediction
authors: Apurba Nandi, Tista Mukherjee, Preeti Dey, Rajiv Ganguly, Avik Kumar Das, Sandip Mandal
publish: 2025 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence for Computing, Astronomy and Renewable Energy (AICARE)
abstract: Near-Earth objects (NEOs) are a significant threat to our planet with their effects ranging from minor atmospheric explosions to catastrophic impacts. Accurate early identification of hazardous asteroids is crucial for timely mitigation. In this study a deep learning approach using a Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM) neural network is presented for predicting potentially dangerous asteroids based on NASA’s extensive dataset. By leveraging 19 carefully selected features using exploratory data analysis, encompassing physical, orbital, and temporal parameters, our model captures complex nonlinear and sequential patterns in the data. The proposed LSTM model achieved remarkable accuracy of 98.93%, with strong precision, recall, and an AUC of 0.9983, outperforming traditional machine learning techniques. Additionally, explainability through LIME reveals key features influencing predictions, enhancing transparency and trust in the models’s decisions. This work demonstrates the effectiveness of sequential modeling in asteroid hazard prediction, contributing to improved planetary defense strategies and advancing real-time risk assessment frameworks.

title: Research on Multi-type Resource Coupling Operation and Scheduling Strategy for Distributed Photovoltaic Intelligent Distribution Network
authors: Ge Wang, Lei Ding, Jingjing Zhao, Xiaolei Cui, Han Liu, Baoquan Liu, Yufei Ji, Yang Bai, Li Tian
publish: 2025 25th Conference of the Electric Power Supply Industry (CEPSI)
abstract: In recent years, a massive amount of distributed photovoltaics have been connected to the distribution network, which can easily cause problems such as reverse heavy overload of lines and difficulties in photovoltaic consumption. With the improvement of functions such as observability, measurability, adjustability, and controllability of distributed photovoltaics, it has become possible to coordinate the operation of various regulating resources in distributed new energy and distribution networks, and research on optimizing scheduling is urgently needed. This article first sorts out various resources of the intelligent distribution network, and adopts an improved K-means method for data clustering analysis of random resources such as photovoltaics and loads. Mechanism modeling analysis is carried out for controllable resources such as distributed photovoltaics, energy storage, and charging piles. On this basis, a mathematical optimization model based on multi-scenario stochastic programming is constructed, which fully considers the economic and environmental aspects in the model objectives, and the constraints comprehensively cover the technical characteristics and balance mechanism of the source, load, and storage links. Then, the model is transformed into Mixed Integer Nonlinear Programming (MILP) and accurately calculated using CPLEX solver. Finally, a simulation was conducted using a distributed photovoltaic intelligent distribution network as an example, and the results showed that the scheduling strategy proposed in this paper can effectively reduce the photovoltaic power abandonment rate and distribution network operating costs.

title: Blockchain Technology in E Commerce: Enhancing Security and Transparency in Digital Transaction
authors: Srinivas D, Preeti C M, P. Tirumala, Nitesh Ghodichor, Padmalosani Dayalan, Mukesh Soni
publish: 2025 World Conference on Cutting-Edge Science and Technology (WCCEST)
abstract: It is anticipated that blockchain-based technologies will significantly alter a wide range of corporate processes and applications, which will have a significant impact on ecommerce. This study introduces a blockchain-based ecommerce transaction model, emphasising how it might improve efficiency, security, and transparency. By merging the state-of-the-art Blockchain Consent Algorithm (BCA) with Distributed Ledger Technology (DLT), particularly Decentralised Identifiable Distributed Ledger Technology (DIDLT), this study investigates how to improve the security and privacy of ecommerce transactions. This study examines the ways in which BCA, a state-of-the-art consensus algorithm created for blockchain environments, and DIDLT, which integrates decentralised identity management with blockchain technology, cooperate to safeguard e-commerce transactions against data manipulation, payment fraud, and identity theft. By offering a thorough examination of their application, this study elucidates the potential of BCA and DIDLT to revolutionise the e-commerce security and safety sector. The network's safety efficacy is greatly increased by using the integrated DIDLT-BCA model, which leads to 99.1% security, up to 150 milliseconds in performance times, and times of mining of up to 0.99 seconds.

title: Comparative Evaluation and Overhead Analysis of a Low-Intrusive Tool for Monitoring Energy-Constrained Nodes in WSN Deployments
authors: Igor Koropiecki, Krzysztof Piotrowski
publish: 2026 IEEE 16th Annual Computing and Communication Workshop and Conference (CCWC)
abstract: One of major challenges in deploying IoT systems to energy-constrained environments is ensuring reliable monitoring and debugging. Addressing this challenge requires tools that impose minimal overhead while still providing meaningful insights. This paper presents a comparative evaluation and overhead analysis of a low-intrusive tool designed for debugging custom low-power nodes during different lifecycle stages. The evaluated tool prioritizes minimal interruption and negligible performance degradation, in attempt to ensure the same runtime conditions for both debugging and final deployments. The tool reduces overhead by 72% in clock cycles and lowers memory usage, when compared to direct UART logging. This reduction translates to extended battery life and reduced environmental impact from battery replacements in long-term deployments.

title: Comparative Evaluation of IndoBERT-base and IndoBERT-lite for Detecting Indonesian-Language Spam Comments on Instagram
authors: Rheno Febrian Cholistyo, Kemas Muslim Lhaksmana
publish: 2025 2nd Beyond Technology Summit on Informatics International Conference (BTS-I2C)
abstract: The rapid growth of social media platforms has increased the prevalence of spam comments, disrupting user engagement and reducing platform credibility. This study compares two Indonesian transformer-based language models, IndoBERT-base and IndoBERT-lite, for detecting spam content on Instagram. Using the SPAMID-PAIR dataset containing over seventy-two thousand post–comment pairs, a comprehensive preprocessing framework was applied, including text normalization, emoji translation, slang correction, and stopword removal. The dataset was balanced using undersampling and split into training, validation, and testing sets with a 70:15:15 ratio. Both models were fine-tuned with optimized parameters and evaluated using accuracy, precision, recall, and F1-score metrics. The results show that IndoBERT-base achieves slightly higher performance and contextual understanding, while IndoBERT-lite provides better computational efficiency. These findings highlight the trade-off between accuracy and efficiency, offering insights for developing lightweight NLP models for Indonesian spam detection.

title: Innovative-Ability-Cultivation Effectiveness Evaluation of College Students Based on Improved Random Forest Algorithm: A Competition Perspective
authors: Jin-Gang Jiang, Tian-Yi Feng, Chun-Yuan Shi, Kai-Rui Wang, Lei Wang, Shu-Qin Luo
publish: 2025 IEEE 14th International Conference on Engineering Education (ICEED)
abstract: With the new quality productivity being elevated to the height of national strategy, science and technology innovation has become the core engine for promoting China's economic transformation and upgrading. Meanwhile, artificial intelligence technology is developing rapidly, and AI-enabled evaluation is becoming a key breakthrough from experience-led to data-driven education model. This paper focuses on the deep integration of TRIZ innovation method and CDIO engineering practice, and proposes the “TRIZ CDIO integration model”. By collecting data from 221 university competitions from 2012 to 2024, we designed a quantitative index system of five innovation ability dimensions, used Analytic Hierarchy Process (AHP) to assign weights, and introduced the improved Random Forest algorithm to construct an innovation ability assessment model. The results show that TRIZ CDIO integration depth, iteration ability and solution generation ability are the core variables predicting innovation ability, and students in the high integration group significantly outperform those in the low integration group in terms of comprehensive scores <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(p<0.01)$</tex>. The study effectively verified the promotion effect of TRIZ CDIO fusion parenting model on college students' innovation ability, and realized the paradigm shift from subjective experience to AI-assisted and data-driven parenting evaluation.

title: Flying Connection Detection and Recovery
authors: Clairol B. Sagaysay, Ralph M. Menchavez, Adrian Daniel D. Camacho, Dan Paulo B. Flores
publish: 2025 25th Conference of the Electric Power Supply Industry (CEPSI)
abstract: The impact of the COVID-19 pandemic, coupled with rising inflation, severely affected the economic capacity of Filipino citizens to meet financial obligations including essential utilities. Approximately 269,000 services were terminated due to unpaid bills, with outstanding balances accumulating to Php 5 billion as of June 2023. As electricity is a fundamental necessity, some of these terminated services were found to continue consuming electricity by sourcing energy from neighboring active services, a practice also known as flying connection. This practice violates legal mandates that prohibit the redistribution of electricity between service accounts. Moreover, the practice creates significant safety risks, including the potential for fires or electrical hazards due to the unauthorized and unregulated nature of the connection. This paper discusses a methodology devised to recover outstanding balances and re-contract terminated services. First, a proximity-threshold model was developed to identify the terminated services with the most propensity to engage in flying connections. The model was designed to detect the co-occurrence of anomalous spike in energy consumption of neighboring active services providing evidence of the potential transfer of energy usage from the terminated service. Implemented on a big data platform, the mapping of terminated services with the nearby active services resulted to 3.5 terabytes worth of network data. Through this multi-step and targeted approach, the model narrowed down addressable leads down to 130,000 terminated services with Php 3.3 billion in potential arrears. As of June 2025, site verification had been completed for 62,000 of these terminated services, which represented 48% of the leads identified through the model. The verification process led to the 16% confirmed involved in flying connections, the recovery of Php 189 million in unpaid arrears and the re-contracting of 7,300 customers.

title: Context Aware Authentication with Embedded Malware Scanning for IoT Security
authors: Keerthana. R, Barakkath Nisha. U, Yasir Abdullah. R
publish: 2025 World Conference on Cutting-Edge Science and Technology (WCCEST)
abstract: Most IoT devices still rely on simple, reusable passwords, making them easy prey for brute-force dictionary attacks. We address this gap with a holistic defence that fuses adaptive authentication and on-device malware analysis. Our authentication engine tracks each node's trust score-derived from recent login history, geographic anomalies, and behavioural red flags-and automatically tightens login requirements whenever risk rises, adding stronger passwords or multi-factor checks on the fly. In parallel, a lightweight malware scanner watches system calls and quick signature hashes to catch malicious code before it can steal credentials. Because both engines exchange risk signals in real time, the framework detects dictionary attacks sooner and either escalates challenges or quarantines the endpoint altogether. All computation occurs locally, so bandwidth overhead remains negligible even on low-power boards. In a controlled testbed the solution halved successful brute-force break-ins and spotted malware with better than 93% accuracy, while keeping CPU and battery impact within acceptable limits. The approach scales across heterogeneous IoT environments and could later adopt federated learning to share threat intelligence without compromising privacy.

title: Evolution and Trends in Research on Intelligent Educational Technologies Applied to Vocational Retraining: A Bibliometric Analysis (2004 to 2024)
authors: Gerlix Bienvenu Adankon, Pélagie Houngue, Michel Comlan Hountondji
publish: 2025 IEEE 14th International Conference on Engineering Education (ICEED)
abstract: This bibliometric study analyses the evolution of scientific research on intelligent educational technologies in the specific context of vocational retraining. The study covers a period of 20 years (2004-2024). After filtering, the work was carried out based on 164 publications, all indexed in Web of Science. Through this analysis, we identify the main trends around our research axes, the most influential groups of authors, the topicality of the subject, as well as emerging themes in this rapidly expanding field. Our study has considered the COVID period, with its e-learning challenges, which had a major impact on publications on this specific subject. These results underline the growing importance of this field of research. Our findings include a significant increase in the number of publications, with an annual growth rate of 13.23 %, a lack of longitudinal studies, and the fact that most studies fail to take account of the profession-al and cultural reconversion contexts of underdeveloped countries.

title: GeoAI - Automated detection of solar panels using Deep Learning for detecting unregistered on-grid solar panels
authors: Thitiporn Intarabamroong
publish: 2025 25th Conference of the Electric Power Supply Industry (CEPSI)
abstract: Thailand’s solar market is rapidly growing, driven by a favorable geographical location, abundant sunlight, supportive governmental policies, decreasing installation costs, and reduced consumer electricity bills. Metropolitan Electricity Authority (MEA) is an electrical provider in the metropolitan region. MEA actively supports government policies regarding renewable energy to support electricity users installing solar systems. Solar panel installation needs to comply with MEA regulations and safety standards. However, the issue arises from unregistered on-grid solar systems connected to electricity systems. These unauthorized connections pose a danger to MEA’s operating officers and can cause damage to electrical equipment from excess electrical power.To solve this issue, MEA has initiated a project to investigate unregistered on-grid solar installations. As solar installations continue to increase, traditional field inspections and manual image reviews have become increasingly time-consuming, requiring 40 personnel and approximately 30 days per cycle. Despite these efforts, installation targets remain unmet. This study introduces a GeoAI workflow that integrates deep learning-based solar panel detection with GIS-enabled spatial analysis. The framework leverages high-resolution drone imagery (5 cm GSD) and combines transfer learning with custom model development. A fine-tuned Mask R-CNN model achieved an F1-score of 0.864, demonstrating the effectiveness of leveraging existing domain knowledge for rapid deployment in utility operations. Compared to traditional methods, this approach resulted in a 100% reduction in workforce requirements due to the fully automated workflow.Moreover, the deep learning approach reduces processing time by approximately 85% and eliminates related labor costs, enabling repeatable and large-scale monitoring. These results show that deep learning and GeoAI technologies can enhance operational efficiency, reduce costs, and support electricity providers in managing the increasing adoption of solar panel systems.

title: Breast Ultrasound Images Enhancement Using Speckle Reducing Anisotropic Diffusion Filter
authors: Audrey Nabilla Shafa, Risanuri Hidayat, Teguh Bharata Adji
publish: 2025 2nd Beyond Technology Summit on Informatics International Conference (BTS-I2C)
abstract: Diagnostic ultrasound imaging is an essential tool in modern medical diagnostics due to its non-invasive nature, real-time imaging capability, and absence of ionizing radiation. However, the presence of speckle noise inherently degrades image quality, reducing contrast and spatial resolution, and potentially compromising diagnostic accuracy. This paper applies the Speckle Reducing Anisotropic Diffusion (SRAD) algorithm to suppress speckle noise while preserving crucial structural and textural details within the image. The performance of the SRAD method is quantitatively evaluated using Peak Signal-to-Noise Ratio (PSNR) and Structural Similarity Index Measure (SSIM). Experimental results based on clinical ultrasound images demonstrate that the SRAD algorithm effectively reduces speckle noise and enhances overall visual quality. Compared with conventional filtering methods, SRAD achieves higher PSNR and SSIM values, indicating improved edge preservation and image fidelity. Furthermore, the analysis reveals that the intensity of speckle noise significantly influences filtering performance, with optimal outcomes observed at lower noise levels. Overall, the proposed approach demonstrates robust noise suppression while maintaining fine anatomical structures, thereby improving diagnostic reliability in clinical breast ultrasound imaging. These results suggest that SRAD has strong potential for broader application in medical imaging domains where speckle noise remains a critical challenge.

title: A UML-Guided Software Architecture for Real-Time Railway Condition Monitoring
authors: Bhavana Bhardwaj, Kendall Nygard, Raj Bridgelall, Neeraj Dhingra
publish: 2025 International Conference on Electrical and Computer Engineering Researches (ICECER)
abstract: Railroad condition monitoring is essential for a train's safe and efficient passage. However, the high cost and slow speed of the current methods hinder the ability of railroads to monitor the tracks more frequently and network-wide. The need for alternative solutions that are more cost-effective and scalable motivated the design of a real-time information system that agencies can use to make better decisions. This paper introduces the Railway Autonomous Inspection Localization System (RAILS), which will enable safe, reliable, cost-effective, and proactive maintenance. The authors introduce the system architecture and apply Unified Modeling Language (UML) to explain the software design. An experiment using the data collected from a regional railroad validated the system's functionality.

title: Detection and Classification of Pests and Diseases of Cassava Plants Using Deep Learning
authors: N. K. H. M. Nanayakkara, D. M. L. M. Dissanayake
publish: 2025 International Conference on Advances in Technology and Computing (ICATC)
abstract: Cassava, a crucial crop especially in tropical regions, faces significant productivity losses due to pests and diseases. This research presents a deep learning approach for classifying cassava pests and diseases using Convolutional Neural Networks (CNNs) with ResNet-18 architecture. A dataset of 6,300 images across nine disease classes was compiled from Sri Lankan cassava fields and supplemented with Kaggle data. The dataset was annotated and augmented to enhance model robustness. ResNet-18 was used for model development, trained with 70% of the data, validated on 20%, and tested on 10%. The model achieved a training accuracy of 96.21%, validation accuracy of 87.06%, and test accuracy of 88.25%, demonstrating good generalization and reliability. Additionally, a web interface was developed for disease detection, providing an efficient tool for cassava disease management and potential yield improvements. This research differs from prior studies by addressing a broader scope of pests and diseases rather than focusing solely on leaf-related diseases. By integrating multiple pest and disease categories, the study provides a more comprehensive solution for cassava health management. The successful integration of the model into a web interface enables efficient diagnosis and post-harvest quality assessment, benefiting farmers and stakeholders by reducing reliance on expert intervention, improving yields, and contributing to food security.

title: Spark Vision AI Agent: Real-Time Child Digital Eye-Strain Detection via Rule-Based NLP & CNNs
authors: Judah Osama Sleibi, Amani Yousef Owda, Majdi Owda
publish: 2025 2nd Beyond Technology Summit on Informatics International Conference (BTS-I2C)
abstract: This research investigated the ability to diagnose digital eye strain in children through a diagnostic AI agent by applying a Uniform-Cost Search algorithm (UCS) and a deep learning Convolutional Neural Network (CNN) model. The research analyzed sixteen dry eye symptoms, highlighting dryness as the most critical symptom related to the digital eye strain that results in redness, headaches, and blurred vision. The employment of the uniform cost search algorithm resulted in noticeable efficiency and significantly reduced the search space to just 0.38% instead of its full size, and achieved diagnostic thresholds rapidly within milliseconds. Furthermore, the algorithm highlighted the relationship between varying threshold levels and symptom combinations required for accurate diagnoses. The CNN model training results show a remarkable and stable performance, with fast convergence and no over-fitting. The improved CNN model significantly achieved a validation accuracy of 98.89% with an F1 of 0.9882 under approximately fifteen minutes due to enhancements in the training process, such as temporal smoothing, multi-scale prediction, early stopping, and anti-over-fitting measures. On the other hand, the original model achieved an accuracy of 96.19% under twelve minutes. The improvements contribute significantly to this performance boost, albeit at an increased computational cost. Overall, this research provides a promising approach for AI-assisted diagnosis of digital eye strain in children.

title: Dental Biometrics for Identification using Image Properties and Learning based Schemes
authors: Azmath Unnisa, Nirmala S Guthpa, Sanjeev Lingareddy
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent and Innovative Technologies in Computing, Electrical and Electronics (IITCEE)
abstract: Dental biometrics offers a trustworthy and durable method for identifying individuals in both forensic and clinical contexts. This study proposes a computer vision-based framework for automated dental identification using panoramic dental radiographs. A custom-trained YOLOv8 model identifies each tooth and extracts the region of interest (ROI) for consistent feature computation. The framework combines deep features from ResNet-50 with handcrafted features such as HOG, LBP, and GLCM to represent the structural and semantic characteristics of these images well. The DENTEX-2023 dataset is used to evaluate the framework using verification measures from the real-world context. The extracted features were evaluated with supervised classifiers (e.g. SVM, Random Forest, and k-NN) under the various fusion schemes (and either the raw deep features, the handcrafted features, or the fused features). The proposed hybrid framework achieves Rank-1 accuracy of 94.6%, Rank-5 accuracy of 98.8%, and a True Acceptance Rate (TAR) of 95.6% with a False Acceptance Rate (FAR) of 0.01. The system has an area under the curve (AUC) of 0.972 and an Equal Error Rate (EER) of 4.8%, showing stable verification and identification results. The experimental results have shown that the framework yields reproducible and discriminative results, suggesting that dental biometrics can be a reliable approach for forensic identification and digital health systems using secure channels.

title: Enhanced Credit Default Prediction Performance by Combining Genetic Algorithm Feature Selection and Heterogeneous Ensemble
authors: Much Aziz Muslim, Dwika Ananda Agustina Pertiwi, Kamilah Ahmad, Shafie Mohamed Zabri, Jumanto Unjung, Iswanto Iswanto, Shahrul Nizam Salahudin
publish: 2025 2nd Beyond Technology Summit on Informatics International Conference (BTS-I2C)
abstract: In recent years, artificial intelligence-based credit evaluation models have been developed to assist financial institutions in identifying borrowers who may potentially default on their loans. Existing research has shown that heterogeneous ensemble methods, which integrate multiple algorithms to produce a new model, outperform traditional credit scoring techniques. This study seeks to enhance credit default prediction performance by utilizing a heterogeneous ensemble stacking model that combines multiple classifiers with an XGBoost meta-learner and is optimized using genetic algorithm-based feature selection. Three credit datasets Australia, Germany, and PPDai were employed to facilitate the analysis. The results indicate that the German credit dataset achieves the best performance, with an accuracy of 0.9408 and the lowest error rate of 0.0553. The proposed model also consistently outperforms other comparison models. Beyond performance improvements, this research provides a more stable, scalable, and interpretable credit scoring framework, offering practical value for financial institutions seeking enhanced risk management, reduced default probability, and greater transparency in automated credit decision-making. The comparative findings demonstrate that the method proposed in this study is both effective and impactful for modern financial applications.

title: Implementing a Transformer Fleet Management Strategy Combining Reliability and Sustainability Dimensions: A German Utility Case Study
authors: Alexei Babizki, Philipp Birgmeier, Lando Peters, Thomas Brock
publish: 2025 25th Conference of the Electric Power Supply Industry (CEPSI)
abstract: Power transformers are vital assets for energy utility companies and industrial operators, necessitating effective management to ensure high availability, extended lifespan, and cost-effective operations.However, existing diagnostic methods often only provide a single value, such as a "Health Index", lacking specificity for stakeholders responsible for fleet management from different perspectives, such as operational costs (OPEX) and investment expenditures (CAPEX). Moreover, conventional methodologies take the operating age of transformers exclusively into account and place significant emphasis on this factor for e.g. replacement decisions, which can often lead to distorted results.This paper presents a comprehensive approach for transformer condition assessment, utilizing a two-dimensional methodology enabling holistic optimization of the decision-making process in asset management. The two-dimensional methodology considers the information from various diagnostic analyses and handles them in an objective way. Furthermore, it covers a possible solution for dealing with "data uncertainty", which is essential during the decision process in asset management.Results from a real project involving a power transformer fleet in a German power utility are presented to illustrate the practical implications of the two-dimensional approach. Applying the two-dimensional condition assessment methodology, critical units within a larger transformer fleet were identified during an initial evaluation. To reduce data uncertainty, targeted measurements were subsequently conducted. Finally, during an internal inspection and further diagnosis, the reason for the existing failure mode could be identified and repaired.The paper emphasizes that asset management decisions derived from condition assessment and data uncertainty analyses are crucial for reducing failure risk, consequently increasing the overall reliability of the transformer fleet. By addressing data uncertainty, asset managers can make informed decisions regarding replacement, repair, and proactive lifetime extension measures.

title: Modeling the Impact of U.S.-China Flight Connectivity on Pandemic Dynamics and China's Economy
authors: Yuhong Mu, Mengyu Jiang, Shanshan Zhang, Lichao Yang
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Data Mining (ICDM)
abstract: The international aviation network has facilitated global population movement. This has not only significantly contributed to the cross-border spread of infectious diseases, but has also had substantial economic consequences worldwide during the pandemic. Thus, understanding the dynamic relationship between mobility, epidemic spread, and economic trends is crucial for informing more balanced and adaptive public health and economic policies. Existing models have a tendency to prioritize infection trends, neglecting to account for the nonlinear interplay between population mobility, epidemic spread, and economic fluctuations. We propose a hybrid model architecture that combines the graph sample and aggregate (GraphSAGE) algorithm for modeling spatial dependencies driven by population flow and Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM) for capturing temporal patterns of infections and economic indicators. The simulation results demonstrate the model's capacity to predict infection dynamics and economic trends accurately. These findings underscore the value of data-driven strategies in enabling timely public health responses and in supporting the formulation of coordinated cross-border mobility policies during global health emergencies.

title: Evaluation of CNN Models and Benchmark to the Hardware Accelerator
authors: Poornashree S J, Prashant V Joshi, Sudharshan K M, Tony M George
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent and Innovative Technologies in Computing, Electrical and Electronics (IITCEE)
abstract: Convolutional Neural Networks (CNNs) play a critical role in applications such as image recognition, natural language processing, and autonomous systems. This paper benchmarks five widely used CNN architectures-MobileNet, ResNet, VGG, Inception, and EfficientNet-on an edge-computing platform to evaluate their suitability for real-time deployment. All models are trained using TensorFlow, converted to TensorFlow Lite (TFLite), and executed on the ARM Cortex-A 72 processor of the Raspberry Pi under identical experimental conditions. Performance metrics including inference time, accuracy, model size, and power consumption are systematically analyzed. Benchmark results show significant variations across architectures, with ResNet50 and VGG16 exhibiting high computational cost and latency, whereas MobileNetV2 and EfficientNet-B2 demonstrate superior efficiency. Among all models, EfficientNet- B2 achieves the best overall trade-off, delivering the highest accuracy (93.2%), low inference latency (155 ms), a compact model size (29.1 MB), and the lowest incremental power consumption (0.041 W). These findings highlight EfficientNet- B2 as the most suitable architecture for resource-constrained edge devices requiring real-time inference.

title: Recommendation Fashion Products Using Collaborative Filtering and Variational Autoencoders (CF-VAE)
authors: Aura Widiya Paramitha, Z. K. A. Baizal
publish: 2025 2nd Beyond Technology Summit on Informatics International Conference (BTS-I2C)
abstract: The growth of the digital fashion industry requires accurate and personalized recommender systems for users. Previous studies in the area of fashion recommender system able to understand user preferences. However, limited user interactions with items, as well as the presence of new users or items without historical data, still remain a problem. The prior works that utilize content-based and visual-based approach, only use type of information and are less effective in providing relevant recommendations when data is limited. To address this problem, this study proposes hybrid Collaborative Filtering Variational Autoencoders (CF-VAE) approaches as a solution that combines strength CF in capturing user interaction patterns and the ability of VAE to form latent representations of user preferences to generate more accurate and personalized fashion product recommendations. The CF-VAE model is implemented employing an encoder and decoder architecture combined with Matrix Factorization (MF) as a baseline model. The experiment was conducted using a fashion rental dataset with 11,032 interactions of 5,438 users and 2,685 fashion items. The evaluation results show that CF-VAE outperforms both baseline MF and pure Multinomial-VAE (Mult-VAE). CF-VAE achieves 223.4% improvement in Precision@10, CF embeddings to Mult-VAE contribute an additional 52% improvement, demonstrating the effectiveness of the hybrid architecture. Therefore, as a hybrid solution, this study is proven to be capable and effective in handling sparse data with a matrix density of 99.92% and producing more relevant recommendations for users.

title: Mesh-Grid Field Deployment in Rural Madagascar
authors: Lucas Richard, Aina Sariaka, Nicolas Saincy, Nolwenn Le Saux
publish: 2025 International Conference on Electrical and Computer Engineering Researches (ICECER)
abstract: The world is lagging behind to achieve the Sustainable Development Goal 7 of the United Nations, i.e. ensuring access to affordable, reliable, sustainable and modern energy for all by 2030. More than 675 million people are still lacking access to electricity, mainly in Sub-Saharan Africa despite the availability of abundant solar resources. Current traditional electrification methods, whether they are based on national grid extension or the distribution of Solar Home System, are struggling to cope with the pace of population growth in Sub- Saharan African countries. Thus, there is room for innovative rural electrification models. Nanoé, a French-Malagasy social company, proposes a Lateral Electrification model based on the progressive building of decentralized and decarbonized electrical infrastructures in a bottom-up manner, with the help of local entrepreneurship. This paper presents this rural electrification model with its associated technological innovations as well as the field deployment of 4 DC micro-grids with decentralized production and storage, named mesh-grids.

title: AI-Enabled Framework for Health Tracking through IoT and Wearable Sensors
authors: Kavita Kanwar, Sonu Sharma, Kamini Pareek
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Artificial Intelligence for Innovations in Healthcare Industries (ICAIIHI)
abstract: As wearable devices become a part of day-to-day healthcare, real-time monitoring of health is becoming more and more of a necessity that is growing exponentially. Small and efficient system is proposed in this study that handles health data directly on the internet of things edge devices rather than relying on cloud servers. A Hybrid CNN-LSTM model was constructed on publicly available data which had the heart rate, breathing rate and motion activity. The data was filtered with Discrete Wavelet Transform and reduced with 8-bit integer quantization (INT8) to reduce power consumption and faster processing. The model scored F1-Macro Score of 0.96 and a very low latency of 4.5 milliseconds which indicates that the model is applicable in continuous and energy efficient health monitoring.

title: Enhanced Multi-Modal Deep Learning Framework for Esophageal Cancer Detection and Progression Analysis Using VAREPOP-APOLLO Dataset
authors: Ashish Mohod, K.P. Yadav
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Artificial Intelligence for Innovations in Healthcare Industries (ICAIIHI)
abstract: Early and correct diagnosis of esophageal cancer is one of the biggest issues since it has a rather heterogeneous presentation and it is difficult to understand the interaction of anatomical, structural, and metabolic characteristics. The paper presents an Enhanced Multi-Modal Deep Learning Framework combining Computed Tomography (CT), Magnetic Resonance Imaging (MRI) and Positron Emission Tomography (PET) data of the VAREPOP-APOLLO Esophageal Cancer dataset (The Cancer Imaging Archive, thecae). The suggested system uses a CT-based anatomical encoding (3D-CNN), MRI feature extraction (Vision Transformer or ViT), and PET-based metabolic representation (Fully Connected Network). Instead, such modality-specific features are learned and mixed adaptively via a Cross-Attention Fusion Mechanism, and Bi-LSTM time modeling is then used to model disease progression across sequential scans. Besides, Monte Carlo Dropout is applied to the uncertainty estimation which offers reliability-conscious and interpretable predictions. The performance of the experiment with 37 cases of patients is excellent, with Accuracy = 0.989, AUC = 0.991, and Dice = 0.983, being better than the current multimodal fusion and single-modality methods. The originality of the framework in dynamically weighting the contribution of modality, modeling longitudinal behavior, and providing explainable AI to be validated by a clinical is emphasized by comparative analysis with the previous methods. The results substantiate that the proposed system does not only increase the precision of diagnoses but also provides the ability to monitor the progression at their early stages establishing a robust and interpretable basis of AI-driven precision oncology and computer-assisted diagnosis in patients with esophageal cancer.

title: AI-Optimized Serverless Computing for Scalable Customer Sentiment Analysis in Open Banking APIs
authors: Rajesh Vayyala
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Data Mining (ICDM)
abstract: This article discusses the intersection of AI optimization and serverless computing for improving customer sentiment analysis in Open Banking APIs. Through the use of a real-world dataset, our research shows how a serverless infrastructure is able to scale dynamically to meet varying workloads so that sentiment analysis is both responsive and efficient. We blend advanced natural language processing and machine learning algorithms to examine, categorize, and interpret customer feedback, thus enabling financial institutions to gain a deeper understanding of user experiences and market trends. The approach not only facilitates real-time analysis but also underscores the possibility of agile, cost-effective solutions that guide strategic decision-making in the rapidly evolving landscape of open banking.

title: Small Models, Big Impact: Tool-Augmented AI Agents for Wireless Network Planning
authors: Yongqiang Zhang, Mustafa A. Kishk, Mohamed-Slim Alouini
abstract: Large Language Models (LLMs) such as ChatGPT promise revolutionary capabilities for Sixth-Generation (6G) wireless networks but their massive computational requirements and tendency to generate technically incorrect information create deployment barriers. In this work, we introduce MAINTAINED: autonomous artificial intelligence agent for wireless network deployment. Instead of encoding domain knowledge within model parameters, our approach orchestrates specialized computational tools for geographic analysis, signal propagation modeling, and network optimization. In a real-world case study, MAINTAINED outperforms state-of-the-art LLMs including ChatGPT-4o, Claude Sonnet 4, and DeepSeek-R1 by up to 100-fold in verified performance metrics while requiring less computational resources. This paradigm shift, moving from relying on parametric knowledge towards externalizing domain knowledge into verifiable computational tools, eliminates hallucination in technical specifications and enables edge-deployable Artificial Intelligence (AI) for wireless communications.

title: EHMAFO-Based Sensorless Control of PMSM Using Dual-LESO for Position Error and Disturbance Compensation
authors: Zhiqi Li, Chengrui Li, Yi Wang, Gaolin Wang, Dianguo Xu
abstract: The sensorless control of permanent magnet synchronous motor (PMSM) is constrained by the position errors of non-ideal factors (such as the observer structure and the motor parameter mismatch) and the sensitivity to load disturbances, which make it face challenges in achieving high-precision position estimation and robustness performance. To address these issues, this article proposes a novel sensorless control scheme based on enhanced hybrid model active flux observer (EHMAFO) aided by dual-linear extended state observer (LESO). First, the EHMAFO is designed to achieve stable operation in a wide speed range, and the mechanisms of position estimation errors are analyzed from multiple perspectives. Then, this article proposes a dual-LESO compensation structure.
