One of the second-order (2nd)-LESO is designed for estimation and compensation of the position error, significantly improving the estimation accuracy. Another 2nd-LESO is used to observe and suppress the speed fluctuations caused by external load disturbances, enhancing the dynamic response and disturbance rejection capability of the system. In addition, an online resistance identification method is introduced to improve robustness against parameter variations. Finally, the effectiveness of the proposed scheme is validated by experimental results.

title: A Modular Multiactive-Bridge Converter Combining Multiwinding Transformers and High-Frequency Link With Fewer Elements and Power Loss
authors: Yiming Zhang, Peiyao Rao, Hongbiao Xie, Tao Zhang, Xiaoying Chen, Yizhan Zhuang, Siqi Li, Sizhao Lu
abstract: To reduce the number of components and power loss in modular multiactive bridge (MMAB) converters used for vehicle-grid-interaction systems, this article proposes an MMAB converter that combines multiwinding transformers (MWT) and a high-frequency link (HFL), which is called MMAB-MH. The MMAB-MH converter connects multiple H-bridges of power ports to a single HFL through different MWTs, which maintains a good scalability while having fewer transformers than the conventional MMAB. This means lower core losses, copper losses, and cost. The topology of the MMAB-MH converter and its characteristics of the port expansion are described. The mathematical model is developed to characterize the power transfer among different ports. An online closed-loop control scheme for the proposed MMAB-MH converter is designed. A prototype of the four-port MMAB-MH converter is built and compared with the conventional MMAB converter to validate the effectiveness and advantages of the proposed converters. Experimental results reveal higher efficiency is achieved with fewer components.

title: ModernTCN-Attention: A Temporal Dependency-Aware Model for Mortality Prediction in Acute Kidney Injury Patients
authors: Chunchun Li, Wei Wang, Qi Liu, Chuanjian Wang
publish: Proceedings of the 2025 4th International Conference on Health Big Data and Intelligent Healthcare

title: Direct hybridization of integrated circuits cooled to different cryogenic temperatures: device design
authors: Xavier de la Broïse, Jean-Luc Sauvageot, Erwan Botin, Jean Charbonnier, Meriem Guergour, Edouard Deschaseaux
abstract: We present the design of a hybridization and integration of three integrated circuits, each cooled to different cryogenic temperatures. The integration is facilitated by the use of superconducting, and thermally super-insulating multilayer deposits at the interfaces. These circuits are populated with through-silicon vias (TSVs) and hybridized by bumps, enabling up to 14,000 isolated electrical connections between the lower circuit and the upper circuit. This solution will allow the place ment of an electronic readout circuit immediately below a detection or a qubit cryogenic circuit, despite the circuits being cooled to different cryogenic temperatures.

title: Polarization-Insensitive Michelson interferometer modulator based on thin-film lithium niobate_supp1-3667531.docx
authors: Jinsong Xia
abstract: Integrated lithium niobate electro-optic modulators are emerging for applications in next-generation optical fiber communication networks. In this work, we demonstrate a polarization-insensitive Michelson interferometer modulator based on a thin-film lithium niobate on insulator platform. By leveraging a mode-conversion-based architecture, the device achieves polarization-insensitive operation while maintaining a low voltage-length product and optical insertion loss. Experimental results show a polarization-dependent loss of 0.36 dB, insertion losses below 0.9 dB, and a half-wave voltage of 11.2 V, corresponding to a voltage-length product of 1.12 V·cm, with an electro-optic bandwidth of ∼21.8 GHz. These performance metrics establish the proposed modulator as a promising candidate for next-generation optical communication systems utilizing standard single-mode fibers, ensuring robust performance in polarization-diverse environments.

title: Integral Sliding Mode Fault-tolerant Tracking Control for Second-order Multi-agent Systems Mixed Event-triggered and Channel-fading
authors: Donghui Xu, Kang Wang, Chunsong Han, Guangtao Ran
abstract: This paper addresses the integrated challenge of consensus tracking for second-order nonlinear multi-agent systems (MAS) simultaneously subject to stochastic communication channel fading, actuator faults (both partial loss-of-effectiveness and bias types), and constrained network resources. To tackle this, a synergistic control scheme is proposed that co-designs a fading-resilient event-triggered communication mechanism with a chatter-free adaptive continuous integral sliding mode control (SMC) protocol. The event-triggering condition explicitly incorporates the statistical characteristics of channel fading, enabling efficient bandwidth usage under unreliable links. The proposed continuous SMC, equipped with an adaptive law, eliminates chattering entirely and operates without requiring prior knowledge of fault or disturbance bounds. It is rigorously proven that the tracking errors converge to a bounded region, and Zeno behavior is excluded, ensuring practical implement ability. Numerical simulations validate the effectiveness and superior performance of the proposed method compared to conventional discontinuous SMC and periodic sampling schemes.

title: Fundamental Frequency Modulation with Dead-Time Compensation for Suppressing Surge Voltage in Modular Multilevel Resonant DC-DC Converters
authors: Jing Sheng, Xiaosheng Zhang, Xinchun Feng, Xin Xiang, Heya Yang, Wuhua Li, Xiangning He
publish: IEEE Journal of Emerging and Selected Topics in Power Electronics
abstract: Modular multilevel resonant dc-dc converters (MMRDCs) are becoming promising candidates in medium-voltage dc (MVDC) power distribution systems, owing to their outstanding features, including high scalability and control flexibility. The fundamental-frequency quasi-square-wave (QSW) modulation is a widely used technique for MMRDC. However, in practical drive implementation, the dead time in submodule (SM) devices could introduce a delay in the arm output voltage relative to the ideal modulation signal, leading to a mismatch between the upper arm and lower arm output voltages. This mismatch can cause surge voltages and current distortion in the modular multilevel converter (MMC) arms, jeopardizing the safe and stable operation of the converter. This paper presents a detailed analysis of the influence of dead time on modulation under various operating conditions and proposes a dead-time compensation method to achieve ideal QSW arm voltages. The proposed modulation reduces undesired voltage and current stress and mitigates disturbances caused by high dv/dt. Finally, validation is provided through both simulation and experimental results from a 10 kV MMRDC prototype.

title: An Instrumental Activities of Daily Living Decline Detection Analysis Pipeline Using Temporal Changes in In-Home Sensor Data
authors: Hiroki Morita, Satoshi Nakagawa, Yoshinori Fujiwara, Misato Nihei
abstract: This study proposes a practical analysis pipeline for detecting early decline in instrumental activities of daily living (IADL) from in-home sensor data, aiming to capture behavioral signs that are less affected by individual differences in lifestyle and living environments. Early detection of IADL decline is critical for maintaining independence in older adults, yet conventional assessments based on self-reports or home visits provide only intermittent and subjective information. We instead quantify temporal changes in behavior using data from passive infrared motion sensors installed in the home. Using data from 22 single-living older adults, we extracted 34 input features: temporal differences in 33 daily behavioral indicators between two 28-day observation windows, and one static clinical attribute representing walking ability. A logistic regression model was evaluated with subject-wise leave-one-subject-out cross-validation. The proposed method achieved an area under the receiver operating characteristic curve of 0.858 with a 95% confidence interval of 0.714 to 0.974, indicating promising performance for early screening. Exploratory feature reduction suggested that a subset of 14 features related to four behavioral groups (outing, nighttime toilet use, nighttime awakening, and post-waking toilet use) preserved the performance of the full Delta model, implying the possibility of optimizing sensor placement and reducing implementation costs. These findings support the feasibility of an objective, data-driven approach to early IADL decline detection from unobtrusive in-home monitoring and may contribute to preventive interventions that help older adults maintain independent living.

title: Test-Time Few-Shot Object Detection via Dynamic Prototype Fusion
authors: Yanlai Wu, Yuan Li, Hongfeng Wei, Weikai Li, Ying Tang
abstract: Test-time few-shot object detection (FSOD) represents an innovative approach for identifying novel categories using a limited number of support examples, obviating the need for model fine-tuning. Despite advancements, existing FSOD methods, including our prior work, continue to grapple with challenges posed by domain/category shift and limited data availability. Building upon our previous research on test-time FSOD, this article proposes a novel dynamic prototype fusion network (PFN) to overcome these limitations. To mitigate the impact of the distribution shift, a dynamic prototype refinement method is introduced that updates prototypes from supporting images in an adaptive manner. Further, limited samples are mitigated through exhaustive exploitation of information within support images. Specifically, we design a dual-level multiscale information integration approach that effectively fuses information across different network layers and image scales, enhancing the model’s discriminating capabilities. Additionally, a mask-based preprocessing technique harnesses segmentation labels on support samples, effectively suppressing the adverse impact of background noise on model accuracy. Notably, to align with the constraints of test-time scenarios, model parameters remain fixed during the configuration step, with only prototypes being updated each time users input novel supporting samples. As a result, our method achieves superior performance over existing state-of-the-art FSOD methods on multiple benchmarks, demonstrating remarkable potential in the realm of FSOD. The code is available at <uri xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">https://github.com/CatfishW/TIDEV2</uri>

title: Mu-Mixer: A Hierarchical MLP-based Framework for Cover Song Identification
authors: Jungwoo Heo, Hyun-Seo Shin, Chan-Yeong Lim, Kyo-Won Koo, Seung-Bin Kim, Jisoo Son, Ha-Jin Yu
abstract: Cover Song Identification (CSI) is a fundamental task in music information retrieval, essential for copyright protection and content organization. While Convolutional Neural Networks (CNNs) and Transformers have significantly advanced CSI, they suffer from limited receptive fields and quadratic computational complexity, respectively. In this paper, we propose Mu-Mixer, a novel all-MLP architecture tailored for CSI that achieves global receptive fields with linear complexity. In contrast to image-based MLP-Mixers, which indiscriminately mix information across the temporal axis, Mu-Mixer incorporates a Multi-scale Temporal Mixing Block (MTMB). This strategy hierarchically segments the time dimension T into multiple scales to preserve local rhythmic motifs while capturing long-range structural dependencies. We evaluate Mu-Mixer on the AI-Hub Music Similarity dataset, where it achieves state-of-the-art performance. Our results demonstrate that Mu-Mixer provides a superior balance between identification accuracy and computational efficiency, proving that self-attention is not a prerequisite for modeling long-range acoustic dependencies in musical signals.

title: Hybrid Deep Learning and Transformer-Based Approaches for Sentiment and Emotion Analysis in the Sri Lankan Cake Industry
authors: Udan Diwyanjalee, SMM Lakmali
publish: 2025 International Conference on Advances in Technology and Computing (ICATC)
abstract: Small and Medium-sized Enterprises (SMEs) in Sri Lanka are increasingly turning to social media to gain insights into consumer preferences; however, they frequently lack sophisticated analytical tools to derive actionable insights. This research introduces a hybrid deep learning framework aimed at sentiment and emotion analysis specifically for the Sri Lankan cake sector. Customer reviews and social media comments were gathered and processed through various methods including text cleaning, emoji translation, stopword elimination, tokenization, and sequence padding. Several deep learning models— Convolutional Neural Networks (CNN), Bidirectional Long Short-Term Memory (BiLSTM), and Gated Recurrent Units (GRU) were assessed, along with hybrid models that combined CNN and BiLSTM with GPT-2 embeddings. The CNN+GPT-2 model achieved the highest accuracy in sentiment classification at 96.14%, while BiLSTM performed best in emotion classification with an accuracy of 91.57%. In terms of trend forecasting, Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM) outshone both SARIMA and Prophet, yielding the lowest error metrics and the most reliable sentiment predictions. These results underscore the potential of hybrid deep learning and transformer-based models to effectively capture complex consumer sentiments and provide SMEs with practical, AI-driven insights. This study is among the first to implement such hybrid NLP models within a localized Sri Lankan context, presenting a scalable approach for business intelligence and informed decision-making.

title: Clinical Assessment of Lycopodium 200 for Hyperlipidemia Management in Homoeopathy
authors: Ginu D Mohan, Venkatesan Hariram, Dr.K.L.Babu, Dr.K.M. Tullanithi, Dr.M.Sridevi
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Artificial Intelligence for Innovations in Healthcare Industries (ICAIIHI)
abstract: Hyperlipidaemia is a medical disorder characterised by elevated amounts of lipids, including cholesterol and triglycerides, in the bloodstream. This disorder increases the chance of significant cardiac complications. Contemporary pharmaceuticals such as statins and fibrates are often given; nevertheless, they frequently induce adverse effects, incur significant costs for prolonged usage, and many patients have difficulties with consistent administration. Due to these issues, there is a need for secure, affordable, and efficient solutions. Homeopathy presents one such alternative. Lycopodium clavatum with 200C potency is often used in homoeopathy to enhance liver function, facilitate digestion, and rectify metabolic imbalances, potentially affecting blood lipids. The research included a dataset of 210 individuals diagnosed with hyperlipidaemia. The fasting lipid profile total cholesterol (TC), low-density lipoprotein (LDL), triglycerides (TG), and high-density lipoprotein (HDL) was assessed before to therapy and after 12 weeks of administering Lycopodium 200. Analysis revealed a 12% decrease in total cholesterol (TC), a 15% reduction in low-density lipoprotein (LDL), a 10% decline in triglycerides (TG), and a 6% rise in high-density lipoprotein (HDL), with statistically significant findings (p < 0.05). Lycopodium 200 may serve as a supportive alternative for the management of hyperlipidaemia.

title: NextGen GridOps Framework Accelerates Utilities' Smart Grid Transformation
authors: Ivan Xiaoning Liang, Elle Wong, Xinting Guo
publish: 2025 25th Conference of the Electric Power Supply Industry (CEPSI)
abstract: Smart grid transformation promises enhanced grid reliability and efficiency, yet utilities face significant hurdles in realizing the full potential of smart grid transformation. Fragmented implementations due to a lack of unified strategy and standardized approaches, complex technology stacks struggling with increasing automation, and misaligned IT and business strategies hinder the transformation progress. Enterprise Architecture (EA) offers a powerful solution to these challenges.This paper explores how EA, guided by frameworks like TOGAF®, empowers utilities to overcome these obstacles. Through a holistic, business-driven approach, EA facilitates the creation of a shared vision, establishes architectural principles and standards for system integration and interoperability, and guides the development of a modern, adaptable technology stack capable of supporting advanced automation.Specifically, this paper provides a case study of a utility that has successfully leveraged on DNV’s NextGen GridOps Framework, a TOGAF® based approach to modernize electrical grids, to accelerate its smart grid journey. By implementing a robust EA framework, the utility achieved standardized seamless IT/OT integration, improved grid visibility, and optimized operations. This demonstrates how EA, through practical application of the NextGen GridOps Framework, enables utilities to strategically navigate smart grid complexities and unlock its transformative power, delivering tangible improvements in efficiency, reliability, and cost-effectiveness.

title: Symmetrical components theory for calculation of fault current using simple drawings
authors: Wai Meng Lee, Sheng Hong Lim, Ian Fu
publish: 2025 25th Conference of the Electric Power Supply Industry (CEPSI)
abstract: This paper proposes a simplified approach for the computation of fault current during unbalanced conditions which include single phase to ground fault, phase to phase fault and double phase to ground fault. Complex mathematical equations based on phasor methods are used in classical technique for calculating unbalanced fault current. Though this method offers accurate results, its application in real-time fault analysis is often mathematically cumbersome and time-consuming. To overcome this limitation, this paper proposes an intuitive approach utilizing simple drawing of symmetrical component to analyse the behaviour of electrical power system during an unbalanced fault. The proposed method covers all three scenarios of unbalanced fault in detail and explains all calculation processes step by step without performing exhaustive mathematical calculations. Furthermore, this method extends to account for the impact of transformers, particularly delta/star configurations, which introduce phase shifts and trapping of zero-sequence currents. Through the simple drawing of symmetrical components in place of phasor computation, this technique offers power engineers a simple, yet efficient technique for the analysis of fault currents under unbalanced conditions.

title: Artificial Intelligence-Based Clustering and Routing Framework for Smart Healthcare Applications
authors: Sulalah Qais Mirkar, Shinde Shilpa
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Internet of Things: Smart Innovation and Usages (IoT-SIU)
abstract: The smart hospital needs to keep an eye on patient health vitals parameters by 24 * 7. Those parameters include a patient’s oxygen levels, heart rate, blood pressure, and blood sugar levels. The research paper proposes an architecture framework that is AI-driven for routing data packets along with clustering WSN nodes. It improves wireless sensor network life by adding Q-learning-based adaptive routing with fuzzy C-means clustering methods. The proposed methodology works with the reinforcement learning AI technique to conserve the energy of a node, transfer critical urgent vital data to the base station, and elect cluster heads from each cluster based on the high remaining energy of a nodes and its proximity to the base station and other nodes in that cluster. Data validation is taken place by t-test and ANOVA methods so as to refine its contingency. The static threshold value of $\mathbf{P}$ is less than 0.12 relative to LEACH protocols, and the proposed methods outperformed the existing protocols that are mentioned. The performance can be captured by data delivery ratio and reduced excess routing overhead. The finding displays that the AI technique is effective and expandable and also able to handle smart healthcare dynamics in real-world scenarios.

title: Federated Hypergraph Learning with Local Differential Privacy: Toward Privacy-Aware Hypergraph Structure Completion
authors: Linfeng Luo, Zhiqi Guo, Fengxiao Tang, Zihao Qiu, Ming Zhao
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Data Mining (ICDM)
abstract: The rapid growth of graph-structured data necessitates partitioning and distributed storage across decentralized systems, driving the emergence of federated graph learning to collaboratively train Graph Neural Networks (GNNs) without compromising privacy. However, current methods exhibit limited performance when handling hypergraphs, which inherently represent complex high-order relationships beyond pairwise connections. Partitioning hypergraph structures across federated subsystems amplifies structural complexity, hindering high-order information mining and compromising local information integrity. To bridge the gap between hypergraph learning and federated systems, we develop FedHGL, a first-of-its-kind framework for federated hypergraph learning on disjoint and privacy-constrained hypergraph partitions. Beyond collaboratively training a comprehensive hypergraph neural network across multiple clients, FedHGL introduces a pre-propagation hyperedge completion mechanism to preserve high-order structural integrity within each client. This procedure leverages the federated central server to perform cross-client hypergraph convolution without exposing internal topological information, effectively mitigating the high-order information loss induced by subgraph partitioning. Furthermore, by incorporating two kinds of local differential privacy (LDP) mechanisms, we provide formal privacy guarantees for this process, ensuring that sensitive node features remain protected against inference attacks from potentially malicious servers or clients. Experimental results on seven real-world datasets confirm the effectiveness of our approach and demonstrate its performance advantages over traditional federated graph learning methods.

title: Explanation Space: A New Perspective into Time Series Interpretability
authors: Shahbaz Rezaei, Xin Liu
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Data Mining (ICDM)
abstract: Human understandable explanation of deep learning models is essential for various critical and sensitive applications. Unlike image or tabular data where the importance of each input feature (for the classifier's decision) can be directly projected into the input, time series distinguishable features (e.g. dominant frequency) are often hard to manifest in time domain for a user to easily understand. Additionally, most explanation methods require a baseline value as an indication of the absence of any feature. However, the notion of lack of feature, which is often defined as black pixels for vision tasks or zero/mean values for tabular data, is not well-defined in time series. Despite the adoption of explainable AI methods (XAI) from tabular and vision domain into time series domain, these differences limit the application of these XAI methods in practice. In this paper, we propose a simple yet effective method that allows a model originally trained on the time domain to be interpreted in other explanation spaces using existing methods. We suggest five explanation spaces, each of which can potentially alleviate these issues in certain types of time series. Our method can be easily integrated into existing platforms without any changes to trained models or XAI methods. The source code is available at https://github.com/shrezaei/TS-X-spaces.

title: Cost-Effective High-Cardinality Data Stream Processing: A Comparative Analysis of Probabilistic Data Structures for Enterprise-Scale Applications
authors: Ishay Shor, Akshay Mittal
publish: 2026 IEEE 16th Annual Computing and Communication Workshop and Conference (CCWC)
abstract: High-cardinality data stream processing presents significant computational and economic challenges for enterprises managing billions of daily events. Traditional exact counting exhibits linear memory growth and quadratic time complexity, making it prohibitively expensive at scale. We present a comprehensive comparative analysis and economic evaluation of four algorithmic approaches—naive exact counting, heap-based top-k selection, Space-Saving, and Count-Min Sketch—under realistic production workloads processing 10 billion daily events. Our empirical results demonstrate <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$10.9 \times$</tex> performance improvements and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{9 6 \%}$</tex> memory reduction compared to exact methods, translating to $3.26 million annual cost savings. We provide productionready implementations, statistical validation (95 % confidence intervals, Bonferroni correction), and practical deployment guidelines, enabling informed technology adoption decisions for highcardinality streaming workloads.

title: Development of a Python-Based Visual Chatbot for Advancing Human-Computer Interaction in Research Applications
authors: Rohan Gupta, Krishnam Gupta, Chirag Pandit, Prabhjot Singh, Prabhmannat Singh
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent and Innovative Technologies in Computing, Electrical and Electronics (IITCEE)
abstract: A visual chatbot is a progressive kind of communication software that has the ability to process, apprehend and provide responses to any prompt as asked in different formats like images, Videos, and textual data. As a default chatbot is dependent on text input in order to provide a solution to a problem, the need for a chatbot that can take input in another format like visually in the form of images is created with the help of NLP and Python programming along with its different libraries. It also makes use of OpenAI in order to answer universal questions and in the form of results, it gives a solution to every problem that is asked in front of the model in any form. It can be quite effective in terms of business deals, shopping, academic fields, industrialization, etc.

title: An Integrated Solution for Agricultural Decision Support Systems
authors: Mallegowda M, Prasanna Kumar K R, Shivalingesh J Patil, Skanda S Kumar, Vinayak Vittal Divate, Vishnu Patel M S
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent and Innovative Technologies in Computing, Electrical and Electronics (IITCEE)
abstract: This paper introduces an Innovative Integrated Solution for Agricultural Decision Support Systems designed to address evolving challenges in agriculture. The solution integrates predictive modeling, precision farming techniques, and market integration to optimize agricultural practices. Utilizing advanced machine learning algorithms, the system forecasts crop yields by analyzing climatic elements such as temperature, humidity, <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{p H}$</tex> levels, and rainfall. Complementing this is the Fertilizer Prediction service, which customizes nutrient recommendations to minimize waste and promote eco-friendly farming. The Price Prediction module aids in strategic planning by assessing risk-to- reward ratios based on market trends. Additionally, the system incorporates modern image processing to detect and manage leaf diseases promptly. An intuitive E-cart interface bridges the gap between farmers and consumers, promoting transparency in transactions. This comprehensive approach modernizes farming, benefiting both seasoned farmers and those new to technical nuances.

title: Simulation of Vulnerability-Driven Trust Evaluation in Microservice Service Meshes
authors: S Prasad Ashwini, N Pari Sheba
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent and Innovative Technologies in Computing, Electrical and Electronics (IITCEE)
abstract: The basis of today's discussion of distributed systems in security-critical areas such as <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$5 \mathrm{G} / 6 \mathrm{G}$</tex> networks, finance, and healthcare comprises cloud-native microservice architectures and MSAs. While enabling secure communication, service meshes lack the runtime trust assessment capabilities required to dynamically determine the trustworthiness of individual microservices. This is a requirement and necessity with the widespread utilisation of third-party libraries and the ensuing supply-chain attacks. A simulation platform for vulnerability-driven trust measurement in MSAs has been created and implemented in this work, accommodating controlled vulnerability injections such as malicious or outdated dependencies. Vulnerability measurements, exploit indicators, and behavioural anomalies are processed by a runtime trust assessment module to produce dynamic trust scores. Service continuity, access control, and adaptive routing decisions are guided by these scores. Baseline static-security meshes are compared with a trust-aware variant in experiments under a range of attack scenarios, including supply-chain exploitation and lateral movement. Security resilience, latency, throughput, and trust-score convergence are some of the metrics of evaluation. The results show that trust-aware meshes can rapidly detect and invalidate compromised services, reducing attack dwell time and propagation while imposing an acceptable performance overhead. By introducing trust-adaptive behaviour into service coordination, our approach enhances runtime security for MSAs and provides a reproducible platform for subsequent work.

title: Reimagining E-Governance Document Security with Blockchain and Dynamic Consensus
authors: Priyanka Mishra, Ganesan R
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Metaverse, and Cybersecurity (ICAMAC)
abstract: Digitally signed PDFs, used in contracts and invoices to ensure content integrity, are vulnerable to “shadow attacks,” which exploit the PDF specification to embed malicious, standard-compliant shadow documents. The first real-world instance, CVE-2022-25641, exposed weaknesses in existing defenses. This paper proposes a blockchain-based framework with keyless signature infrastructure to validate PDF documents securely in an e-government ecosystem, protecting against shadow attacks while preserving signature confidentiality. Performance evaluation shows the system reduces storage costs by 30% compared to traditional methods, offering a secure and collaborative environment. or Math in Paper Title or Abstract.

title: Research on Power Stability Control Technology of Smart Grid under Security Reinforcement Learning
authors: Yingda Chen, Ruisheng Li, Haofan Li, Wei Chen, Jiawei Huang
publish: 2025 25th Conference of the Electric Power Supply Industry (CEPSI)
abstract: This study focuses on power stability control technology in smart grids under safe reinforcement learning, aiming to enhance the grid’s adaptability to complex operating environments and improve the precision and stability of power regulation. With the increasing uncertainty of renewable energy integration and load variations, traditional stability control technologies face limitations in response speed and adaptability. Based on an analysis of power fluctuation characteristics and the shortcomings of conventional stability control methods, this paper introduces a safe reinforcement learning algorithm. This algorithm continuously learns and optimizes through interactions between the agent and the grid environment to achieve precise power control. Unlike traditional methods that rely on mathematical modeling or rule-based control, reinforcement learning autonomously learns optimal strategies, enhancing the flexibility and adaptability of the stability control system. To ensure grid security, this study designs corresponding safety mechanisms, including the construction of safety constraints, risk assessment models, and protective barriers to prevent erroneous strategies during learning from compromising grid stability. The proposed method effectively suppresses power fluctuations under various operating conditions. Compared with traditional PID control, it reduces frequency deviation by approximately 30% and shortens frequency recovery time by 25%. Compared with MPC control, it decreases the number of voltage limit violations by 67% and improves the load recovery rate by over 4%. These results demonstrate that the proposed safe reinforcement learning approach outperforms traditional methods in terms of dynamic response speed, steady-state performance, and safety robustness, significantly enhancing grid stability under complex disturbances. The findings provide an innovative solution for smart grid stability control and offer technical support for intelligent applications such as renewable energy integration and grid dispatch optimization.

title: Ensuring A Future Ready And Future Oriented Workforce Through Specialized Essential Courseware For Knowledge Management Amongst Technical Staff
authors: Kok Tee Lim, Bryan Zhen Yuan Lee, Wiwin Renandya
publish: 2025 25th Conference of the Electric Power Supply Industry (CEPSI)
abstract: As the energy sector evolves with innovative technologies, traditional equipment and methodologies risk becoming obsolete. To bridge this gap, utilities must ensure that operations and maintenance (O&M) staff are equipped with up-to-date knowledge and skills. This paper presents a systematic, in-house training framework designed to upskill essential technical personnel using company resources, reducing reliance on external parties for troubleshooting and defect rectification. By accelerating the diagnosis process and specifying parts without external support, the utility improves asset uptime and operational efficiency. The training program spans multiple technical domains and incorporates both theoretical knowledge and hands-on field experience. Expertise gained on-site is continuously integrated into course materials, capturing the institutional knowledge of experienced staff nearing retirement. Regular participant feedback is used to refine and update the content, ensuring the material remains relevant and effective. This structured approach has demonstrated measurable success in knowledge retention, operational readiness, and workforce continuity. It also ensures a smoother transition for new staff taking over critical roles, preserving the legacy and practical know-how of departing personnel. The paper highlights how this blended learning model—combining site-based experience with structured education—effectively equips technical teams with future-ready competencies to manage and maintain evolving energy infrastructure.

title: ReFIRE - A Hybrid CBIR Framework Integrating Visual Features, AI Embeddings, and Adaptive Relevance
authors: Rahul Mehta, Harish Patidar
publish: 2025 World Conference on Cutting-Edge Science and Technology (WCCEST)
abstract: This paper presents a hybrid image retrieval framework designed to overcome long-standing weaknesses in conventional CBIR systems. Existing approaches, both commercial and academic, often rely on fixed feature weights and provide limited flexibility when user needs change. The proposed system combines handcrafted visual features-such as color, texture, and shape-with semantic embeddings from transformer networks and contrastive learning models, producing a multi-layered representation for each image. A feedback loop allows users to mark retrieved images as relevant or irrelevant, and the ranking algorithm updates dynamically in response. By recalibrating feature weights in real-time, the system enhances retrieval accuracy while maintaining alignment with evolving user preferences. Experiments on both broad datasets and specialized domains, including medical X-rays and product images, showed rapid gains after only a few iterations. On average, the framework achieved a Mean Average Precision (MAP) of 89.2% and a Precision@10 of 84.7%, while maintaining near real-time query speed. These findings suggest that an interactive hybrid approach offers a practical way to narrow the semantic gap, making CBIR systems more accurate, scalable, and user-centric.

title: xTime: Extreme Event Prediction with Hierarchical Knowledge Distillation and Expert Fusion
authors: Quan Li, Wenchao Yu, Suhang Wang, Minhua Lin, Lingwei Chen, Wei Cheng, Haifeng Chen
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Data Mining (ICDM)
abstract: Extreme events frequently occur in real-world time series and often carry significant practical implications. In domains such as climate and healthcare, these events, such as floods, heatwaves, or acute medical episodes, can lead to serious consequences. Accurate forecasting of such events is therefore of substantial importance. Most existing time series forecasting models are optimized for overall performance within the prediction window, but often struggle to accurately predict extreme events, such as high temperatures or heart rate spikes. The main challenges are data imbalance and the neglect of valuable information contained in intermediate events that precede extreme events. In this paper, we propose xTime, a novel framework for extreme event forecasting in time series. xTime leverages knowledge distillation to transfer information from models trained on lower-rarity events, thereby improving prediction performance on rarer ones. In addition, we introduce a MoE mechanism that dynamically selects and fuses outputs from expert models across different rarity levels, which further improves the forecasting performance for extreme events. Experiments on multiple datasets show that xTime achieves consistent improvements, with forecasting accuracy on extreme events improving from 3% to 78%.

title: IDentra: Bridging Identity and Infrastructure
authors: J. Karthiyayini, K Diya Devaiah, Mohisha Gupta, Raksha, Varun Kumar
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent and Innovative Technologies in Computing, Electrical and Electronics (IITCEE)
abstract: The Secure Identity Management System is an advanced digital platform designed to streamline identity verification and document management through cutting-edge security measures. Utilizing biometric authentication including fingerprint and facial recognition—the system eliminates the need for traditional passwords, ensuring a secure, user-friendly login experience. Core features include digital identity cards, automatically issued upon registration and verifiable through digital signatures, and a robust document management system secured by RSA encryption. Real-time status dashboards provide visibility into document validity and activity, while smart notifications automate alerts for expirations and security events. Built with a modern technology stack including React, Node.js, and MongoDB, the system offers web and mobile accessibility. Its development is structured into four phases spanning 16 weeks. Future upgrades envision blockchain integration, AI-powered verification, and broader interoperability with third-party identity services, marking it as a scalable, future-ready solution for secure identity management. Additionally, rigorous compliance with data protection regulations such as GDPR ensures user privacy and legal adherence across jurisdictions.

title: Measuring the Readiness of Artificial Intelligence Implementation in Enhancing Customer Service at Electrical Company Using UTAUT 2 and TRI
authors: Randhy Kusriansyah, Nur Aini Rakhmawati
publish: 2025 2nd Beyond Technology Summit on Informatics International Conference (BTS-I2C)
abstract: Artificial Intelligence (AI) is transforming how companies deliver services, especially in industries such as electricity where efficiency and reliability are crucial. This study investigates the readiness for AI implementation in customer service at electrical Company. The research integrates the Unified Theory of Acceptance and Use of Technology 2 (UTAUT2) and the Technology Readiness Index (TRI) frameworks to assess customer perceptions and psychological readiness. UTAUT2 evaluates performance expectancy, effort expectancy, social influence, and facilitating conditions, while TRI measures optimism, innovativeness, discomfort, and insecurity. A quantitative approach will be used with 115 respondents representing company customers. The study aims to identify dominant factors influencing readiness and propose strategic recommendations to improve AI adoption in customer facing operations. Based on the questionnaire results analyzed using Structural Equation Modeling–Partial Least Squares (SEM-PLS), the findings indicate that the integrative model of UTAUT2 and TRI successfully explains the level of customer intention and readiness toward AI chatbot adoption. The relationship between BI and Readiness to Implement AI (RI) is very strong (β = 0.62; p < 0.001), confirming that behavioral intention is the primary factor determining AI implementation readiness. Overall, the customer readiness level reached an average score of 3.72, categorized as "Ready", indicating that customers show a high level of readiness to adopt AI-based chatbots in the company’s electricity services.

title: Energy-Efficient Indoor Air Quality Control Via Cloud-Based Reinforcement Learning and Modular IoT Sensors
authors: Alex Kagozi, Lina Chato
publish: 2026 IEEE 16th Annual Computing and Communication Workshop and Conference (CCWC)
abstract: Indoor air quality (IAQ) plays an important role in human health, comfort, and cognitive performance. This work presents an edge-cloud control framework that integrates reinforcement learning (RL) with low-cost air quality sensors for real-time ventilation control. A mass-balance simulation of multiple pollutant measures (AQI, TVOC, and eCO2) was used to train two RL agents, Deep Q-Network (DQN) and Proximal Policy Optimization (PPO) via a two-phase curriculum that first learns pollutant regulation and later incorporates energy efficiency. In simulation, both agents achieved stable AQI control. DQN provided the best energy-performance trade-off <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(142\ \text{Wh})$</tex>, reducing energy use by approximately 39 % compared to the rule-based baseline (234 Wh) while maintaining acceptable pollutant levels. PPO delivered the strongest pollutant tracking but at the highest energy cost <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(375\ \text{Wh})$</tex>. The rule-based controller achieved low overall pollutant values but at a higher cost compared to DQN. All controllers were then deployed in the cloud and connected to a Raspberry Pi5 via MQTT. The Pi collected real-time sensor data and controlled a 775DC motor fan during alcohol-induced pollutant spikes. Although pollutant dynamics were influenced by uncontrollable outdoor conditions, both RL agents showed robust responsiveness, increasing air abstraction during spikes and reducing it afterward to save energy.

title: Identify Dyscalculia, Dysgraphia Learning Disabilities in Deaf and Mute Primary Students and Help to Improve Learning Abilities
authors: Gavithra Perera, Hansanie Neththasinghe, Dilshan Rasanjana, Yasith Thalakotunna, Jenny Krishara, Karthiga Rajendran
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent and Innovative Technologies in Computing, Electrical and Electronics (IITCEE)
abstract: Learning disabilities, particularly Dyscalculia and Dysgraphia, significantly hinder students' academic, social, and future occupational outcomes. Deaf and mute primary students face amplified challenges due to limited availability of specialized educational resources and tools that cater to their unique communication needs. This research presents an innovative, intelligent learning environment designed specifically to identify and mitigate Dyscalculia and Dysgraphia among deaf and mute primary students. Leveraging advanced artificial intelligence, computer vision, and machine learning (ML) technologies, the developed system incorporates Sinhala Sign Language (SLSL) and interactive, adaptive learning methodologies. The identification process employs Convolutional Neural Networks (CNNs) to analyze handwritten numerical inputs and sign language gestures, categorizing students based on the severity of their conditions. Subsequently, personalized, engaging instructional activities facilitate gradual skill development and continuous improvement. Robust data privacy measures ensure ethical standards, while automated tracking provides actionable insights for educators and parents. Initial findings indicate significant improvements in student performance and engagement, demonstrating the system's efficacy in delivering inclusive, culturally relevant, and accessible educational interventions tailored for deaf and mute students.

title: A Fog-Enabled Ensemble Intrusion Detection Framework with SMOTE-Based Feature Engineering for Securing IoT in Smart Cities
authors: Mani Pendey, Sanjeev Kumar Prasad
publish: 2025 World Conference on Cutting-Edge Science and Technology (WCCEST)
abstract: As the Internet of Things (IoT)-driven smart city ecosystem is rapidly expanding, ensuring the security of heterogeneous and resource-constrained IoT devices has become ever more important. This paper proposes a robust Fog-Enabled Intrusion Detection Framework that couples SMOTE-enriched features balancing with a stacked ensemble classification model, which is comprised of Random Forest, XGBoost, and LightGBM. The framework solves major issues like class imbalance, high-dimensionality, and latency-prone centralized detection. Network traffic is preprocessed by the system employing normalization and encoding, which are followed by SMOTE to balance minority attack samples synthetically. Mutual information-based feature selection is used to select relevant features in order to improve the performance of the classifier. The ensemble model is implemented at the fog layer to facilitate real-time anomaly detection near the data source. Classification on benchmark data sets — UNSW-NB15 and CICIDS2017 — exhibits high accuracy of 95.1% and 80.6%, respectively, coupled with excellent per-class metrics. In comparison to baseline classifiers such as SVM and ANN, the presented framework yields significant improvements in precision and recall. Real-world feasibility was verified through fog node simulation, providing low inference latency and negligible resource consumption. This architecture provides the foundation for scalable, decentralized, and adaptive security technologies for smart cities.

title: Computer Vision-Based Leaf Growth Monitoring System of Aeroponic-Grown Potato Plant
authors: Estiyanti Ekawati, Zayna Mosa Kalila, Eko Mursito Budi, Sri Nanan B. Widiyanto, Novi Tri Astutiningsih
publish: 2025 2nd Beyond Technology Summit on Informatics International Conference (BTS-I2C)
abstract: Leaf area is a key indicator of plant health and development. However, manual measurement is time-consuming and labor-intensive, especially when monitoring aeroponic-grown potato plants with multiple leaves over extended periods. This study applied a computer vision-based system to automate leaf growth monitoring using the YOLO (You Only Look Once) v8 framework. A dataset was collected from a controlled aeroponic system: 25 images of young leaves (4,869 individual leaf segments) and 35 of mature leaves (12,368 segments). Based on evaluation of various YOLOv8 model configurations, the best model achieved a mask mAP@50 of 0.396 and 0.250 for young leaves and mature leaves, respectively. The challenge to track the mature canopy was due to severe leaf occlusion and self-similarity in dense foliage. Despite the challenge, this study demonstrates proof of concept for tracking early leaf growth and highlights the significant computer vision challenges posed by dense, mature canopies in aeroponic systems.

title: Machine Learning and CRISPR-Cas13 systems for targeted RNA editing in Autism Spectrum Disorders
authors: Saanvi Verma, Vinay Maurya, Vinay Rishiwal, Mano Yadav, Sunita Yadav
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Internet of Things: Smart Innovation and Usages (IoT-SIU)
abstract: Autism Spectrum Disorder (ASD) is a complicated neuro-developmental disease that impairs a person’s ability to recognize and participate in social interactions and communication properly. Restricted, repetitive behaviours and interests also define it. A combination of genetic abnormalities and developmental circumstances causes ASD. Despite significant advances in understanding the disorder, a comprehensive and widely accepted model of ASD pathogenesis has yet to be established, and effective treatments addressing the full spectrum of symptoms remain unavailable. Recent advancements in CRISPRCas13 systems, which uniquely target and edit single-stranded RNA (ssRNA), offer a promising, reversible, and non-genomedisruptive approach for treating transcript-level aberrations in ASD. This paper proposed a novel framework based on Machine Learning and CRISPR-Cas13 for Ribonucleic acid (RNA) editing systems to REPAIR (RNA Editing for Programmable A to I Replacement) and RESCUE (C to U conversion), to correct abnormal RNA transcripts in ASD-associated genes such as FMR1, MECP2, CHD8, and SHANK3. By modulating splicing, correcting base errors or silencing transcripts without altering DNA, Machine learning and CRISPR-Cas13 systems provide a safer alternative to traditional genome editing techniques such as CRISPR-Cas9, TALENs, etc. Machine Learning and CRISPR-Cas13-mediated RNA editing open new therapeutic avenues in personalized and precision medicine for ASD, especially in cases where permanent DNA modifications carry higher risks. Key parameters for evaluating the efficacy of Machine Learning and CRISPR-Cas13-based RNA editing in ASD research include editing efficiency over time, off-target editing frequency and changes in RNA and protein expression levels.

title: Enhancing Bone Fracture Detection in Medical Imaging: Implementing Transfer Learning and Adversarial Training
authors: Mohd Hanafi Ahmad Hijazi, Nurin Afiqah Abdul Hamid, Khadijah
publish: 2025 International Conference on Advances in Machine Intelligence, and Cybersecurity Technologies (AMICT)
abstract: The demand for efficient and robust medical image analysis poses significant challenges in accurately detecting bone fractures within specific anatomical regions. Deep learning (DL) models are often vulnerable to adversarial attacks and exhibit limited generalization across diverse datasets, raising concerns about their reliability in clinical settings. This paper aims to compare the performance of U-Net and ResNet50 architectures for bone fracture detection in X-ray images under varying data conditions. Using transfer learning and adversarial training with the Fast Gradient Sign Method (FGSM), three experiments were conducted: training on clean, pre-processed datasets; training on adversarial examples; and training on a combination of clean and adversarial datasets. Two pre-processing techniques, Gaussian Filter and Canny Edge Detection were also applied on the datasets. The performance was evaluated using accuracy, precision, recall, and F1-score. Results show that U-Net outperformed ResNet50 across all experiments and achieved an accuracy of 80%, and F1-score of 80% with adversarial training on combined Gaussian Filter datasets, demonstrating great robustness and generalization capability. On the other hand, ResNet50 showed limited adaptability to adversarial perturbations, especially when processing edge-based features. This paper highlights the effectiveness of adversarial training in enhancing model robustness and highlights U-Net's potential as a reliable tool for bone fracture detection.

title: A Low-Cost, AI-Assisted Smart Farming Solution for Automated Irrigation and Disease Monitoring
authors: Debanjan Bera, Partha Biswas, Bristi Chakraborty, Soumadip Chandra, Sandip Chakraborty
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Internet of Things: Smart Innovation and Usages (IoT-SIU)
abstract: The paper describes a low-cost smart farming system with IoT applicability that strives to tackle some of agriculture’s greatest challenges: scarcity of water, shortage of labor, and management of plant diseases. This solution adopts Arduino Uno with sensors for soil moisture, temperature, humidity, and light to monitor environmental conditions for irrigation, which is automated on the basis of real-time data. The system, which functions using solar energy with an added rainwater harvesting mechanism, is therefore kept sustainable and is suitable for deployment in inaccessible or off-grid locations. A deep learning model (MobileNetV2, transfer learning) was trained on the tomato subset of the Kaggle New Plant Diseases Dataset (10 tomato disease classes plus healthy) using the dataset’s train/validation split with standard augmentations, achieving $88.64 \%$ accuracy (macroprecision 0.89; macro-recall 0.89). The system provides realtime environmental updates and control via LCD display and mobile phone functionality to monitor field conditions and receive updates virtually for the farmers to act accordingly. The result shows an improvement of up to $40 \%$ in waterefficient savings and very reliable identification and early disease diagnosis. The integrated use of automation, AI, and renewable energy thus presents a very scalable and efficient framework for precision agriculture, particularly suited to small- and resource-poor farms.

title: ResNet-50-Based Fatigue Detection Using Facial Micro-Expressions and Blinking Analysis
authors: Uma Devi T, L.N.Jayanthi, V. Amirthalingam, J.Jagan Babu, M. Muthulekshmi, Sharathkumar B S
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Artificial Intelligence for Innovations in Healthcare Industries (ICAIIHI)
abstract: Detecting fatigue is crucial for improving safety in high-risk sectors, including transportation, healthcare, and manufacturing. This research presents a deep learning (DL) model built on ResNet-50 to identify weariness using facial micro-expressions and blinking patterns. The system evaluates facial expressions, such as eyebrow movement, mouth twitching, and eye closure duration, while monitoring blink frequency and duration to enhance detection precision. The model is trained on a dataset of 5,000 labeled images of fatigue and non-fatigue, with various facial expressions and lighting conditions to improve generalization. According to the results of the experiments, the suggested method significantly outperforms the conventional machine-learning models, reaching an accuracy of 97.5%. The real-time deployment demonstrates an average inference time of 9.8 milliseconds per frame, making it appropriate for real-world applications. This research highlights the efficacy of DL-based non-intrusive fatigue monitoring, providing a dependable solution for accident prevention and occupational safety.

title: Practical Approach on Protection Coordination of Medium Voltage Network with Embedded Generation
authors: Sajani Thotagamuwage, Prabhath Premathilake, Harshani Kokila, Heshani Manjula Fernando
publish: 2025 25th Conference of the Electric Power Supply Industry (CEPSI)
abstract: The growing penetration of renewable energy sources has led to a significant increase in embedded generators (EGs) within medium voltage (MV) networks, exerting considerable pressure on network capacity and the protection systems. Protection coordination of MV feeders with high concentrations of embedded generation has become a challenging task, requiring the handling of variable and bi-directional power flow conditions. The main objective of this study is to identify and clear faulty sections under various fault conditions without indiscriminate tripping of protective devices. Practical scenarios were analyzed using field data. The paper addresses the application of adaptive protection schemes and proper coordination of protection devices in MV distribution feeders containing mini hydro and solar PV generation. Different fault scenarios and possible protection failures highlight the importance of designing flexible, reliable protection systems to accommodate the evolving characteristics of modern distribution networks. This approach facilitates flexible and reliable methods for the protection coordination in the system while keeping a healthy MV distribution network.

title: Decision letter for "Descriptor: Digital Dataset of Animal Tissues for Macroscopic Pathology in Cancer: Image and Ultrasound Data (DATP-IU)"
authors: N/A

title: FPGA – Based Adaptive FIR Filter Using LMS Algorithm
authors: Heba H. Ali, Isam S. Hameed, Salah H. Alkurwy, Raghad A. Kareem
publish: 2025 2nd Beyond Technology Summit on Informatics International Conference (BTS-I2C)
abstract: This This paper describes the design and the testing of an adaptive FIR filter built on an FPGA using the Least Mean Squares (LMS) algorithm. The study compares different types of multipliers, such as the traditional array multiplier (conventional type), the Vedic multiplier, and the Binary Coded Decimal (BCD) multiplier, within a 3-tap FIR filter design. The multiplier unit has been the target for better architecture performance as it has a principal role in the finite impulse response (FIR) filter. Having been designed using Verilog, all conducted multiplier were tested to decide the best among them for the adaptive FIR filter. Testing the design was done in Quartus II and ModelSim software. Results illustrated that the BCD multiplier type was the most qualified through using only 12 registers and 151 logic elements compared to Array and Vedic multipliers. Obtained simulated results offers a maximum speed of 1282.05 MHz. Also, the performance of the design reflects an apparent error reducing of the tested signal with proper values compared to theoretically obtained results. Generally, the proposed FPGA based adaptive FIR system supports its effectiveness and reliability along with capability of high-speed operation.

title: Edge-Deployed Deep Learning for Real-Time Positive and Negative Affirmation Detection
authors: Jessica Sarah Mathew, Gobi Ramasamy, Syam Mohan
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent and Innovative Technologies in Computing, Electrical and Electronics (IITCEE)
abstract: Tiny Machine Learning (TinyML) enables the deployment of efficient machine learning models on resourceconstrained devices, supporting real-time applications directly at the edge. In this work, we present an audio classification model developed using Edge Impulse to distinguish between positive and negative affirmations. The model was trained and validated on affirmation datasets, achieving an accuracy of 91.4 % with a validation loss of 0.21. The confusion matrix shows <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{8 8. 5 \%}$</tex> accuracy for negative affirmations and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{9 4 \%}$</tex> accuracy for positive affirmations, with corresponding F1scores of 0.91 and 0.92, indicating robust performance across both classes. This study demonstrates the feasibility of deploying affirmation classification models on low-power devices, paving the way for practical applications in mental health monitoring and support. Potential use cases include integration into therapeutic tools for psychologists, enabling real-time detection of self-talk patterns that may support interventions such as cognitive-behavioural therapy and mood tracking. The results suggest that TinyML can extend psychological support beyond clinical settings into everyday life.

title: Building an Open-Source Data Lakehouse: A Hands-on Exploration
authors: Md Abdullah Al Asad
publish: 2026 IEEE 16th Annual Computing and Communication Workshop and Conference (CCWC)
abstract: This paper explores the concept of data lakehouses, a powerful approach for managing and analyzing vast amounts of data. While traditional data lakes offer flexibility but lack structure, and data warehouses provide structure but struggle with diverse data formats, data lakehouses bridge this gap. This paper delves into the architecture of data lakehouses, highlighting their ability to ingest, store, and analyze data from various sources, regardless of format. We present a hands-on approach to constructing an open-source data lakehouse using readily available tools like Trino, Apache Iceberg, and MinIO. Through this practical example, we illustrate the process of data ingestion, storage, and exploration within the data lakehouse. This paper serves as an educational resource for data practitioners and researchers, empowering them to leverage the cost-effectiveness and flexibility of open-source tools to build their own data lakehouses. By unlocking the potential of diverse data, organizations can gain valuable insights and make informed decisions across various domains.

title: Enhancing Scholarship Allocation Fairness Using GRU and Synthetic Minority Oversampling Technique
authors: Andi Syarifah Yasmine, Zahir Zainuddin, Abd Salam At Taqwa
publish: 2025 2nd Beyond Technology Summit on Informatics International Conference (BTS-I2C)
abstract: Fair scholarship distribution plays a crucial role in advancing educational equity; however, manual selection processes often suffer from subjectivity, inconsistency, and potential bias. To address these limitations, this study proposes a predictive framework that integrates the Gated Recurrent Unit (GRU) neural network with the Synthetic Minority Oversampling Technique (SMOTE) for classifying scholarship eligibility among high school students. The dataset consists of 367 student records obtained from SMAN 2 Makassar, encompassing academic and socio-economic attributes. Data preprocessing involved cleaning, categorical encoding, normalization, and stratified data splitting to ensure balanced representation. The baseline GRU model trained on imbalanced data achieved a validation accuracy of 91.38% and a test accuracy of 79.45%, revealing overfitting and bias toward majority classes. After the application of SMOTE to balance the minority class, the retrained model achieved improved performance with 96.55% validation accuracy and 83.56% test accuracy, accompanied by a significant reduction in test loss. These findings demonstrate that SMOTE effectively enhances the GRU model’s generalization capability and predictive fairness. Overall, the proposed GRU–SMOTE framework offers a robust, data-driven, and efficient approach for fair scholarship eligibility prediction. It can serve as a reliable decision-support system in educational data mining, promoting objectivity and transparency in scholarship selection processes.

title: A Multi-Modal Entropy-Weighted Fusion Framework for Melanoma Detection
authors: Samved Vyas, Reet Nayan, Sparsh Khanal, Prem Kumar Ramesh
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent and Innovative Technologies in Computing, Electrical and Electronics (IITCEE)
abstract: Malignant form of skin melanoma is the most lethal form of skin disease. Therefore, timely detection is extremely crucial for a patient's survival. Traditional methods to detect malignant skin melanoma are often time-consuming and resource intensive. This paper proposes an entropy-weighted attention-based fusion framework, combined with a robust ensemble of classifiers, that classify skin lesions as benign or malignant. We take advantage of multiple pre-trained models, namely Vision Transformer (ViT), ResNet-50, and MobileNetV3, for potent feature extraction from dermoscopic images. The features extracted using the mentioned deep learning models are then normalized, projected, and concatenated with weights. The concatenated matrix of features is then used to train the ensemble. Experimental results show an overall accuracy of 91.2%. This study highlights the potential of blending feature extraction and attention-based fusion with ensemble classification for effective melanoma detection.

title: Implementation Consideration for Customer-side DERMS
authors: Ivan Christian, Elle Wong, Xinting Guo
publish: 2025 25th Conference of the Electric Power Supply Industry (CEPSI)
abstract: In response to the changing landscape of distribution network from passive grid to active grid due to DER penetration, distribution utility companies envision to stay relevant by gradually evolving its main role from network operator to system operator. Whilst DERMS are generally perceived as the right tool to harness the untapped potential of DER, it comes with many different shapes and forms in the market, which often confuse utility companies in the DERMS implementation. In this paper, we demystify DERMS terminology and qualitatively compare what can and cannot be done by each solution, share our perspective on customer side DERMS solution in terms of capability, value proposition, and integration with ADMS.

title: Performance Comparison of Machine Learning Algorithms for the Prediction of Polycystic Ovary Syndrome Disease
authors: Priyanka Kumari, Komal Singh Gill
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Artificial Intelligence for Innovations in Healthcare Industries (ICAIIHI)
abstract: Women of reproductive age suffer from hormonal imbalance that leads to multiple health issues generally associated with follicle abnormality often known as Polycystic ovarian Syndrome (PCOS). It is one of the leading causes of infertility and is often associated with long-term health problems such as Type 2 diabetes, cardiovascular disease, and mental health disorders. Approximately 5 to 10 percent of women of reproductive age suffer from this disease. The problem of PCOS has shown a significant rise in the last decade. Early diagnosis of PCOS can reduce the long-term impact and stabilize hormone balance. Researchers are presenting various machine learning models to address solutions to this issue. This paper presents significant contributions in this field and compares the models to evaluate their performance in the efficient prediction of PCOS disease. The ML models, viz. Random Forest, LightGBM, and Stacking have been evaluated. The results indicate that Stacking outperforms all other ML models with 94 % accuracy. In the future, issues such as data balancing, multimodal study, and external validation need further improvements for a real-time model for PCOS detection.

title: A Trustworthy Hybrid ML-GenAI Architecture for Autonomous, Behavior-Aware Resource Allocation in Internet2 Traffic
authors: Shideh Yavary Mehr, Abhishek Phadke
publish: 2026 IEEE 16th Annual Computing and Communication Workshop and Conference (CCWC)
abstract: Internet2 (I2), a high-performance research and education network, faces increasing pressure to allocate bandwidth and compute resources adaptively as traffic volumes and application behaviors evolve. Traditional static resource allocation strategies cannot meet the real-time demands of large-scale scientific workflows, distributed edge computing, and data-intensive academic environments. To address this challenge, this paper introduces a trustworthy hybrid ML-GenAI architecture that autonomously predicts, explains, and optimizes I2 traffic resource allocation with high precision. Our method integrates the two complementary machine learning models: K-Nearest Neighbors (KNN) for fine-grained local pattern detection and Random Forest (RF) for robust global trend prediction—**to capture both micro-level traffic fluctuations and macro-level demand patterns. These ML signals are further augmented using a Generative AI (Gen-AI) reasoning module that performs semantic flow characterization, anomaly explanation, and uncertainty justification. The Gen-AI component provides natural-language rationales for model decisions, enabling transparent decision-making and enhanced operator trust. Trustworthiness is ensured through three mechanisms: (1) Model cross-validation and ensemble agreement between KNN and RF to reduce bias and improve reliability; (2) Gen-AI-based explanation synthesis that generates interpretable justifications for allocation decisions; and (3) Uncertainty-aware resource allocation, where Gen-AI flags high-risk predictions and triggers safe-fallback allocation policies. Experimental results on a multi-terabyte Internet 2 flow dataset (2021-2025) show that the proposed hybrid system reduces misallocation by 32%, improves anomaly detection by 21%, and enables auditable, behavior-aware decisions that outperform traditional ML-only baselines. This work represents one of the first trustworthy hybrid ML-GenAI approaches for next-generation autonomous network resource management.

title: Real-Time Underwater Plastic Detection Using Deep Learning: A Comparative Study of YOLOv8 and YOLOv10
authors: Sohini Chatterjee, Vani Kandasamy
publish: 2026 IEEE 16th Annual Computing and Communication Workshop and Conference (CCWC)
abstract: Underwater plastic pollution poses significant risks to marine ecosystems and human health by threatening biodiver-sity and contaminating food sources. Precise detection is essential for implementing timely corrective actions to conserve marine life and protect the environment. This study explores the application of YOLO-based deep learning models, particularly YOLOv8 and YOLOv10, to effectively detect and classify submerged plastic debris. By leveraging these models, the project can significantly enhance existing pollution control strategies through scalable real-time monitoring. With YOLOv8 achieving a higher mean average precision (mAP) of 0.856 over YOLOv10's 0.843, the research underscores the importance of accurate detection for effective conservation efforts. The findings highlight the potential for these models to provide automated tracking systems that support ongoing marine pollution management. Looking ahead, the project aims to develop a prototype capable of handling real-time data, facilitating improvements in pollution response and prevention, ultimately safeguarding our oceans and their delicate ecosystems.

title: Advanced Transformer-Enhanced Framework for Real-Time IoT Malware Detection
authors: Dabeeruddin Syed, Davut Özhan, Muhammad Aftab, Keerthikant Raghunathan, Ameema Zainab, Mounika Vuppala
publish: 2025 International Conference on Electrical and Computer Engineering Researches (ICECER)
abstract: The rapid growth of Internet of Things (IoT) devices has introduced new security concerns, offering more possibilities to cyber attackers to exploit their vulnerabilities, limited resources, and less secure frameworks. Classical machine learning and deep learning approaches provide adequate threat detection accuracy, but also require more computational power than is available in IoT environments with limited resources. In this paper, we evaluate the performance of lightweight transformer models – MiniLM, TinyBERT, MobileBERT, and ALBERT –for malware detection in IoT traffic. Each model is trained and tested on a benchmark dataset, and their performance is compared with the models proposed in the previous literature in terms of accuracy and training time. Experimental results demonstrate that while all lightweight models achieve competitive accuracy, ALBERT outperforms the rest, achieving 99.48%accuracy performance, making it suitable for real-world IoT deployments.

title: Power-Optimized ALU Framework Based on Pseudo Dynamic Buffer Domino Logic
authors: Chetana Reddy, Surekha R Sakhare, Yasmeen M S, Ruchitha B L, Sanjana Benkal, Vikas Vardhan S P
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent and Innovative Technologies in Computing, Electrical and Electronics (IITCEE)
abstract: With the rapid scaling of CMOS technology, balancing high-speed performance with low-power consumption has emerged as one of the central challenges in modern VLSI design. Among the core digital components, the Arithmetic and Logic Unit (ALU) plays a crucial role in determining the efficiency and performance of the overall system. While conventional domino logic circuits are favoured for their speed and compact design, they often suffer from high switching activity and considerable leakage power, making them less suitable for low power applications. To overcome these limitations, this paper presents a semi-custom design of a 64-bit ALU based on Pseudo Dynamic Buffer (PDB) Domino Logic, implemented using Cadence Genus with 90nm CMOS technology. The ALU is designed to perform 16 operations—eight arithmetic and eight logical—and its performance is compared against a general ALU using conventional logic. The introduction of PDB logic significantly reduces unnecessary signal transitions, which in turn minimizes both dynamic and static power dissipation. The proposed design demonstrates superior energy efficiency with a 27.44% reduction in switching power and a remarkable 79.45% reduction in leakage power, demonstrating the superior energy efficiency of the proposed design. These results confirm that integrating PDB-based domino logic into ALU architecture provides an effective pathway toward achieving low-power digital processors for next-generation VLSI systems.

title: Using Typical Interaction Patterns, Predictive AI Can Recognize Subtle Pre-Disease Behavior
authors: V K Mishra, Sreeja Avujugari, Thanmai Bhasyakarla, Likitha Sai Laxmi Bhuma, Nikitha Boini, Apoorva Boddugari
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Artificial Intelligence for Innovations in Healthcare Industries (ICAIIHI)
abstract: By lowering the risk of neurocognitive and chronic illnesses and facilitating informed decisions prior to traditional clinical diagnosis, this technology sets a new benchmark in preventive healthcare. One of the biggest obstacles to preventive healthcare is the early identification of pre-disease behaviours. Conventional diagnostic techniques frequently rely on overt symptoms, which manifest later in the illness. In this paper, a unique predictive AI framework is presented that analyses common interaction patterns in both digital and physical domains to detect modest pre-disease behavioural signals. Lifestyle/contextual data, typing and smartphone usage patterns, sleep metrics, physical activity, and voice prosody are just a few of the multi-modal data streams that the system integrates.By using attention-based deep neural networks with multi-scale feature fusion to minimize overfitting and dynamically prioritize informative signals, the model enables real-time monitoring of pre-disease states. Unlike previous disease-specific approaches, this framework identifies micro-deviations across multiple conditions, providing actionable insights for early intervention. Experimental validation on both synthetic and real-world datasets shows up to 99% predictive accuracy, outperforming single-modality models. An explainable AI layer enables clinicians to interpret feature contributions and personalize preventive measures.

title: Photovoltaic Performance of $\text{FeCl}_{3}, \text{CuCl} 2$, and $\text{BiCl}_{3}$-Based Sensors for Energy Conversion Application
authors: Bharath Venkatagiri, Sujatha Basa Reddy, Ninaada M S
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent and Innovative Technologies in Computing, Electrical and Electronics (IITCEE)
abstract: In this work, low-cost, solution-processed photovoltaic sensors based on iron(III) chloride <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\left(\text{FeCl}_{3}\right)$</tex>, copper(II) chloride (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{C u C l}_{\mathbf{2}}$</tex>), and bismuth(III) chloride (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{B i C l}_{\mathbf{3}}$</tex>) composites deposited onto graphene nanoplatelet (GNP)-coated substrates are fabricated and their performance evaluated. The metal chlorides were combined in binary and ternary forms in different molar ratios to create six different sensor configurations. Using a four-point probe method, the conductive base layer, which was made of GNP films, was confirmed to have uniform sheet resistance. Through UVvisible absorption studies, the bandgap energies of the individual chlorides were ascertained. <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{FeCl}_{3}$</tex> showed a lower bandgap (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\sim 2.86 \text{eV}$</tex>) than <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{CuCl}_{2}$</tex> and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{BiCl}_{3}(\sim 3.2-3.25 \text{eV})$</tex>, suggesting enhanced absorption in the visible region. A highprecision multimeter was used to record output voltages while photovoltaic performance was evaluated under ambient solar irradiation. Out of all the configurations, the ternary composite sensor (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathrm{S} 2: \text{FeCl}_{3}-\text{CuCl}_{2}-\text{BiCl}_{3}$</tex> in a <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$2: 2: 1$</tex> molar ratio) showed the highest photovoltaic (163.13 mV), indicating strong synergistic effects in charge generation and transport. The potential of the suggested sensors for passive, self-powered light detection in UV-visible ranges was demonstrated by their efficient operation in the absence of external bias. This research lays out a scalable, eco-friendly path for creating flexible photovoltaic sensors that can be used for early-stage IoT energy modules and real-time light monitoring.

title: Federated Hybrid CNN-ViT with Explainable AI for Skin Disease Diagnosis
authors: Mr.G.Prabhakar, Sollu Shivani, Vanam.Meghana, Shaik Shaheena, Siddela Tejasri, Tukkayyagari Malini
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Artificial Intelligence for Innovations in Healthcare Industries (ICAIIHI)
abstract: Skin disease ID with AI faces many tough parts. This list has things like unbalanced data, hard-to-get tech, gaps in how to see the AI's work, & use issues in actual clinics. This work brings up a new Mix Hybrid CNN-VIT model that also has Multi-Mode Clear AI (XAI). This mixed setup joins CNN's skill in close feature pulls with VT's wide area learning. This means better right hits & strong use. A shared learning setup lets many health places train the model. It does not need to share raw info. So, it keeps stuff private but makes the data set more mixed. Also, fake data made by GANs cuts bias in skin tones & disease steps not much seen.
