To make the AI work easier to get, the system uses multi-mode XAI. It gives both focus maps & words that match how skin docs think. Tuned for use at the edge, the model is ready for real-time use in clinics with few tools. Tests show it does better than old CNNs. It's righter & easier to see its work. This makes this way a big, fair & doc-friendly AI setup for skin checks.

title: A Machine Learning Framework for DNA Sequence–Based Disease Classification: Integrating Computational Genomics with Predictive Modeling in Healthcare
authors: Busani Sravani, Aragidda Srivalli, Boi Jahnavi, Choppa Meghana, Bandam Rajasri, Baddam Pranathi Reddy
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Artificial Intelligence for Innovations in Healthcare Industries (ICAIIHI)
abstract: DNA sequence analysis has the potential to greatly help in the early diagnosis of inherited diseases. Most of the current approaches have limited ability to be used for other genetic diseases because they are essentially concentrated on cancer. The drawbacks of current approaches include narrow disease coverage, poor accuracy, and very small datasets. We propose a DNA sequence-based classifier that might be able to detect a range of inherited diseases other than cancer to address these issues. Our method integrates advanced machine learning models with preprocessing techniques, feature selection, and k-mer representation. The precision of the system is more than 90% due to ensemble learning, while it is 75–80% with the prior methods. This improvement reduces the amount of erroneous classifications and gives scientists and physicians a more reliable tool with which to study genetic diseases.

title: Comparative Evaluation of Machine Learning Models for Classifying Sleep Disorders
authors: Akula Thejaswini, Praveen Chouksey, Mrutyunjaya. S Yalawar
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Artificial Intelligence for Innovations in Healthcare Industries (ICAIIHI)
abstract: Accurately identifying sleep disorders is crucial to enhancing human quality of life because of the substantial detrimental effects that sleep disturbances have on health. It significantly affects both mental and physical health. Effective treatment of many illnesses depends on early diagnosis and prediction. In order to tackle this escalating issue, machine learning methods including feature extraction and classification algorithms, dimensionality reduction, and clustering have all gained popularity. These methods are essential for increasing the precision and effectiveness of sleep stage classification. Numerous studies have been conducted recently to categorize sleep disorders. This study examines, evaluates, and contrasts the most recent machine learning methods for categorizing sleep disorders based on methodology and evaluation criteria such as area under call (AUC), recall, accuracy precautions, type of datasets used, etc.

title: SecureCloudOps: Zero-Trust Identity Federation and Runtime Monitoring in Azure Kubernetes Service (AKS)
authors: Radhakrishnan Krishna Kripa
publish: 2025 World Conference on Cutting-Edge Science and Technology (WCCEST)
abstract: In this paper, we present SecureCloudOps, a unified Zero-Trust framework that fuses federated identity management with real-time runtime security monitoring for Azure Kubernetes Service (AKS). SecureCloudOps establishes robust identity federation across Azure Active Directory (AAD) and third-party OpenID Connect (OIDC) providers, and dynamically enforces access policies using Open Policy Agent (OPA) and Gatekeeper. To address the limitations of static security models, our framework integrates eBPF-based runtime instrumentation for continuous behavior monitoring of containerized workloads. This identity-to-runtime coupling enables precise anomaly detection, policy refinement, and automated remediation in real time. Our experimental deployment across production-grade AKS clusters demonstrates that SecureCloudOps effectively mitigates complex threats such as lateral movement, privilege escalation, and data exfiltration. Compared to baseline tools like Falco, Tracee, and Azure Defender, our solution achieves higher detection accuracy (96.8% TPR), lower false positive rates (2.3%), and reduced operational latency—without compromising resource efficiency. SecureCloudOps offers a closed-loop Zero-Trust enforcement model that redefines workload security in multi-tenant, cloudnative environments.

title: Model for InDels and Substitutions in Nanopore DNA Storage Based on Measured Data
authors: Peter Farkaš, Katarína Farkašová, Tomáš Janvars
publish: 2025 International Conference on Electrical and Computer Engineering Researches (ICECER)
abstract: In this paper a simple model for basic impairments which occur in nanopore DNA storage system is presented. It is based on results from real measurements. It models deletions, substitutions and insertions. Other types of damages are not taken into account in order to keep it simple. It can be used for example for preliminary simulation experiments connected with search for coding schemes suitable for nanopore based DNA storage systems or for teaching. Results of comparison of this model with another more complicated model with memory are also presented in this paper.

title: Platformer Game Level Generation Based on Player Behavior Using Reinforcement Learning and TOAD-GAN Integration
authors: Mohammad Ovi Sanjaya, Arif Djunaidy
publish: 2025 2nd Beyond Technology Summit on Informatics International Conference (BTS-I2C)
abstract: This paper proposes an adaptive level-generation framework that integrates Proximal Policy Optimization (PPO) with the Token-based One-shot Arbitrary Dimension GAN (TOAD-GAN) to achieve dynamic difficulty adjustment in platformer games. The PPO agent maps player-behavior metrics to difficulty parameters that condition TOAD-GAN through a post-generation imputation module. The approach avoids retraining the generator by modifying enemy density, power-up distribution, and time limits directly on the tokenized output. Experiments show that PPO trained with a difficulty-margin simulator converges substantially better than a random simulator baseline (average reward −136.7 vs. −2260.8). Structural fidelity is preserved, with TPKL-Divergence increasing only modestly (0.47 → 0.57 after difficulty imputation). A rule-based playability repair module further improves solvability from 65% to 80% across 100 generated levels. Results demonstrate that RL-driven conditioning can effectively guide token-based generators toward producing adaptive and playable content.

title: Performance Analysis of Fuzzy Logic Controlled Battery Inverters under Bidirectional Operation
authors: AA. Maharaja Adhisthanaya Indrawan, Suwito, Rudy Dikairono, Akbar Roisul Adimi
publish: 2025 2nd Beyond Technology Summit on Informatics International Conference (BTS-I2C)
abstract: Battery inverters enable bidirectional energy exchange between storage systems and the grid, ensuring efficient utilization of renewable energy. However, nonlinearities, load fluctuations, and parameter uncertainties challenge conventional PID-based controllers. This paper presents a Fuzzy Logic Controller (FLC) designed to enhance inverter performance in both charging and discharging modes. Unlike traditional methods, the proposed controller adaptively manages nonlinear dynamics without requiring precise system models, resulting in improved transient response and current stability. Simulation results in MATLAB/Simulink demonstrate that FLC achieves up to 90% faster rise time and smoother current regulation than PID, proving its robustness and suitability for smart grid applications.

title: SPOT: Spatio-Temporal Pattern Mining and Optimization for Load Consolidation in Freight Transportation Networks
authors: Sikai Cheng, Amira Hijazi, Jeren Konak, Alan Erera, Pascal Van Hentenryck
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Data Mining (ICDM)
abstract: Freight consolidation has significant potential to reduce transportation costs and mitigate congestion and pollution. An effective load consolidation plan relies on carefully chosen consolidation points to ensure alignment with existing transportation management processes, such as driver scheduling, personnel planning, and terminal operations. This complexity represents a significant challenge when searching for optimal consolidation strategies. Traditional optimization-based methods provide exact solutions, but their computational complexity makes them impractical for large-scale instances and they fail to leverage historical data. Machine learning-based approaches address these issues but often ignore operational constraints, leading to infeasible consolidation plans. This work proposes SPOT, an end-to-end approach that integrates the benefits of machine learning (ML) and optimization for load consolidation. The ML component plays a key role in the planning phase by identifying the consolidation points through spatio-temporal clustering and constrained frequent itemset mining, while the optimization selects the most costeffective feasible consolidation routes for a given operational day. Extensive experiments conducted on industrial load data demonstrate that SPOT significantly reduces travel distance and transportation costs (by about 50%) on large terminals) compared to the existing industry-standard load planning strategy and a neighborhood-based heuristic. Moreover, the ML component provides valuable tactical-level insights by identifying frequently recurring consolidation opportunities that guide proactive planning. In addition, SPOT is computationally efficient and can be easily scaled to accommodate large transportation networks.

title: Efficient Keratoconus Classification Using Quantized Grayscale Tomography and a Lightweight CNN
authors: Abdlrahman T. Alqazaz, Mohammed A. M. Abdullah, Bashar A. Saeed
publish: 2025 2nd Beyond Technology Summit on Informatics International Conference (BTS-I2C)
abstract: Keratoconus is a degenerative condition of the cornea that may cause considerable visual deterioration. Diagnosing keratoconus remains challenging for ophthalmologists, primarily because it is a time-intensive process and involves complex clinical evaluations. This study introduces a compact convolutional neural network (CNN) architecture specifically developed to analyze both RGB and grayscale tomography images for the purpose of keratoconus detection. To the best of our knowledge, this is the first study to utilize low-depth grayscale images for the detection of keratoconus. The proposed CNN model was applied to classify color-coded maps derived from the Pentacam device. Each map was tested under five different formats—RGB, 8-bit grayscale (256 levels), 4-bit grayscale (16 levels), 3-bit grayscale (8 levels), and 2-bit grayscale (4 levels)—to enable a comprehensive comparison. The model was evaluated and compared to assess the effectiveness of grayscale quantization on keratoconus detection using color-coded maps. Results indicated that the RGB input yielded the lowest performance. Among the grayscale formats, the 8-level grayscale achieved the highest performance when using corneal thickness (CT) or elevation anterior (EA) maps. For sagittal anterior (SAG) maps, both colored images and the 8-level grayscale format yielded the highest classification accuracy. Additional experiments with traditional machine learning (ML) approaches demonstrated variable performance, with certain configurations yielding strong results and others performing less effectively.

title: Impact of Lung Data Augmentation Through Instance Replication on KNN Classification Performance
authors: Vinoth. R, T. Vijayan, V. Ganesan, B. Kalaiselvi
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent and Innovative Technologies in Computing, Electrical and Electronics (IITCEE)
abstract: Resampling is a commonly employed technique to enhance classifier performance in the presence of imbalanced datasets. However, its efficiency diminishes when applied to small-sized datasets, presenting challenges in evaluation. Resampling, being adaptable to any base learner, offers a versatile solution for improving classification accuracy. This study investigates the impact of resampling on KNN classifiers across various split percentages using a lung cancer dataset with varying levels of imbalance. The focus is on evaluating the performance of the K-Nearest Neighbors (KNN) classifier under different replication levels. Performance metrics such as accuracy, precision, recall, F1-score, and ROC score are calculated for each replication percentage. The results demonstrate that resampling consistently enhances the classifier's performance as replication levels increase. Detailed interpretations of these metrics are discussed, emphasizing the efficacy of resampling in improving predictive accuracy for lung cancer classification. This study highlights the potential of resampling as a reliable tool for addressing data imbalance and optimizing classifier outcomes.

title: Cross-Device Motion Interaction via Apple’s Native System Frameworks
authors: Ezequiel França Dos Santos
publish: 2025 International Conference on Electrical and Computer Engineering Researches (ICECER)
abstract: We introduce an open-source, fully offline pipeline that transforms a consumer-grade iPhone into a motion controller with real-time tactile feedback, using only native Apple frameworks. Designed for rapid prototyping and applied mobile HCI scenarios, the system integrates CoreMotion for inertial sensing, MultipeerConnectivity for peer-to-peer data transmission at 10 Hz, and CoreHaptics for immediate tactile confirmation. A built-in logger captures end-to-end latency without requiring clock synchronization, yielding a mean delay of 70.4 ms and 95th percentile below 74 ms on typical 5 GHz Wi-Fi (−55 dBm RSSI). We validated the pipeline through a real-time demonstrator game, KeepCalm, deployed during a public event with 21 participants. Results showed stable connections, zero packet loss, and negligible power impact (24 mW on iPhone 13 mini). With fewer than 500 lines of Swift code and no reliance on cloud infrastructure, this system provides a compact, reproducible foundation for embodied interaction research, casual games, and offline educational tools. All source code, latency logs, and provisioning scripts are openly released under an MIT license.

title: Exploring SHAP: A Deep Dive into Feature Attribution for Explainable AI
authors: Shiv Preet, Gunjan Chhabra, Anupama Mishra
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Internet of Things: Smart Innovation and Usages (IoT-SIU)
abstract: As artificial intelligence is becoming important aspect of all our daily life decision making, there is a dire need to improve the interpretability and explanation and transparency for the decisions made by the $\mathbf{A I}$ in critical domains like health care, finance and so on. This research paper discusses the efficacy of SHAP or shapely additive explanations which offers a cohesive and better approach for improving abilities of the automated decision-making process by taking into account weighted contribution of each parameter participating in the decision making. This research highlights benefits of redefining specific predictions using Shapely values. It highlights a novel application of Shapely values by integrating SHAP in automated decision pipelines without compromising the outcomes. Results in this research paper demonstrate that SHAP helps in better and reliable AI and make it more robust for the future AI models. With the help of SHAP and other such XAIs (explainable AI), AI can take better automated decisions and help in improving real time and critical systems, for example, healthcare, diagnostics, finances, stocks and so on.

title: When LLM Meets Simplicial Complex: A Novel Graph Prompt Learning on Ethereum Transaction Networks
authors: Yuxin Liu, Stephen Chan, Jeffrey Chu, Yuanyuan Zhang, Yuzhou Chen
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Data Mining (ICDM)
abstract: Fraudulent activity on blockchain networks poses significant risks to the integrity and trust of decentralized finance ecosystems. The timely and accurate detection of fraud nodes such as phishing addresses within large-scale Ethereum transaction networks remains a major challenge due to their dynamic, sparse, and evolving structures. While methods like graph deep learning (e.g., graph neural networks) have been extensively explored, they are not inherently designed to capture higherorder interactions and textual information embedded within graph data. Motivated by the urgent need for advanced and robust fraud detection techniques, we introduce a novel graph prompting method named Large Language Model-Simplicial Complex (LLM-SC) based graph prompting framework that leverages LLM-based multi-agent collaboration system, LLMbased financial news prompt function, and simplicial neural networks to capture both the structural and contextual dimensions of blockchain activity. The empirical studies demonstrate the effectiveness of our approach, and these results provide a new tool for blockchain analytics platforms and regulatory authorities, enabling earlier and more accurate identification of fraudulent behavior and ultimately supporting safer and more resilient digital financial systems. The code is available at https://github.com/y13564/LLM-SC.

title: A Review on Detection of non-invasive Oral Cancer based on Deep Learning Techniques, Challenges and Future Directions
authors: Pankaj Shantilal Shinde, Saroj Kumar Pandey, Manoj Kumar Ojha
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Artificial Intelligence for Innovations in Healthcare Industries (ICAIIHI)
abstract: Early detection of oral cancer remains a significant clinical challenge due to the invasiveness, subjectivity, and frequent failure to identify lesions in their early stages of development of traditional diagnostic methods. Recent advancements in non-invasive imaging and deep learning technologies have opened new possibilities for accurate, rapid, and patient-centered diagnosis. This paper comprehensively analyzes deep learning (DL) techniques used for the detection and classification of oral cancer and potential malignancies, including convolutional neural networks (CNNs), recurrent neural networks (RNNs), autoencoders, extreme learning machines (ELMs), Transformer models, and generative adversarial networks (GANs). We summarize the major contributions of these techniques across various imaging modalities, including histopathology, hyperspectral imaging, autofluorescence, spectroscopy, and photography. Furthermore, this paper highlights some unresolved key challenges, such as limited labeled datasets, diverse imaging conditions, difficulties in deep feature selection, data imbalance, and the need for reliable artificial intelligence systems. Finally, we envision future directions, including multimodal data fusion, advanced hybrid models, personalized medicine, improved dataset management, and real-time AI-assisted diagnosis. In summary, this review highlights the transformative potential of artificial intelligence in achieving accurate, non-invasive, and early oral cancer detection, while outlining the key steps required for its clinical translation.

title: Detection Strategies and Analytical Insights Into Autism Spectrum Disorder
authors: Pallikonda Priyanka, G. Thrishika, G.Revathi, K.Sathwika Raj, K. Bhavya, K. Mounika
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Artificial Intelligence for Innovations in Healthcare Industries (ICAIIHI)
abstract: Autism Spectrum Disorder (ASD) represents a neurodevelopmental condition that influences an individual's communication, behavior, and social interaction. Early and precise identification of ASD is crucial for effective treatment and long-term improvement. This research investigates various computational and analytical methods designed to enhance the detection and understanding of ASD. The paper discusses multiple diagnostic approaches, data-driven algorithms, and AI-based frameworks that improve accuracy in detecting behavioral patterns associated with autism. Through comprehensive analysis, the study emphasizes the integration of technology in clinical assessment and highlights the potential of intelligent systems for early intervention. The outcome of this work aims to support clinicians, caregivers, and researchers in creating a more reliable and efficient ASD evaluation process.

title: RIAC: A Hybrid Framework for Real-Time Arabic Captions Generation Using ViT and GPT
authors: Hamza M. Alabdallah, Suleiman Y. Yerima
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Metaverse, and Cybersecurity (ICAMAC)
abstract: In the rapidly advancing realm of artificial intelligence, the Real-Time Image Arabic Captioning (RIAC) system presented in this paper is a pioneering framework designed to bridge the semantic gap between visual content and linguistic annotation in Arabic. By integrating the state-of-the-art Vision Transformer (ViT) and GPT-2 models, RIAC transcends conventional challenges in image captioning, offering real-time generation of contextually relevant and linguistically accurate Arabic captions. This hybrid model capitalizes on the strengths of advanced computer vision and natural language processing technologies, further augmented by real-time English to Arabic translation to accommodate the intricacies of the Arabic language. The RIAC system is rigorously evaluated on widely recognized metrics, yielding a CIDEr score of 0.91. RIAC showcases its capability to produce captions that are not only coherent and grammatically precise but also naturally resonant with the Arabic-speaking population. This work marks a significant leap towards creating a more inclusive and accessible digital ecosystem, paving the way for future innovations in multilingual image captioning technologies.

title: Decarbonization Trailblazer: Engineering and Natural Carbon Management at Stesen Janakuasa Tuanku Muhriz (SJTM)
authors: Shamsul Amri Sulaiman, Muhammad Asyraf Ridzuan, Kamalia Rasyidah Sufyan
publish: 2025 25th Conference of the Electric Power Supply Industry (CEPSI)
abstract: Jimah East Power (JEP), sometimes referred to as Stesen Janakuasa Tuanku Muhriz (SJTM), is establishing a new benchmark in sustainable energy production by pioneering decarbonization initiatives through a synthesis of cutting-edge engineering and natural carbon management solutions. This paper examines five (5) pivotal projects: Project REACH, Project DRAGON, Project EFB-Pellet Co-firing, Project GREENLIFE, and Project Mangrove Replantation, which collectively establish JEP as a decarbonization hub in the power generation sector.

title: MuRAL-CPD: Active Learning for Multiresolution Change Point Detection
authors: Stefano Bertolasi, Diego Carrera, Diego Stucchi, Pasqualina Fragneto, Luigi Amedeo Bianchi
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Data Mining (ICDM)
abstract: Change Point Detection (CPD) is a critical task in time series analysis, aiming to identify moments when the underlying data-generating process shifts. Traditional CPD methods often rely on unsupervised techniques, which lack adaptability to task-specific definitions of change and cannot benefit from user knowledge. To address these limitations, we propose MuRAL-CPD, a novel semi-supervised method that integrates active learning into a multiresolution CPD algorithm. MuRALCPD leverages a wavelet-based multiresolution decomposition to detect changes across multiple temporal scales and incorporates user feedback to iteratively optimize key hyperparameters. This interaction enables the model to align its notion of change with that of the user, improving both accuracy and interpretability. Our experimental results on several real-world datasets show the effectiveness of MuRAL-CPD against state-of-the-art methods, particularly in scenarios where minimal supervision is available.

title: Incorporating Interactive User Feedback Through Precision Matrix Adjustments for High-Dimensional Anomaly Detection
authors: Taylor Dinkins, Weng-Keen Wong, Haifeng Chen, Yanchi Liu, Kai Ishikawa
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Data Mining (ICDM)
abstract: Anomaly detection involves finding unusual data instances of interest. This task has often been compared to looking for a needle in a haystack, especially in high dimensions. One strategy for improving anomaly detection is to leverage expert feedback in a human-in-the-loop process to label anomalies and nominal points. For high-dimensional data, the majority of existing anomaly detection approaches that incorporate expert feedback do not scale and often result in algorithms that are too slow to operate in an interactive setting with a human expert. We introduce a new anomaly detection algorithm intended for high dimensional data that can efficiently incorporate expert feedback on each round of querying. Our approach uses ideas from metric learning to perform an efficient incremental update when the user labels a data instance. We demonstrate through extensive experiments that our work provides the best tradeoff between performance and running time among existing approaches.

title: E-Rickshaw Battery Guardian: An Integrated Multi-Sensor Approach for Theft Prevention and Advanced Battery Health Monitoring
authors: Ajay Yadav, Dev, Mohammad Umar Abdullah, Shreyas Reddy, Mahesh Kumawat, Rahul Mukherjee
publish: 2025 World Conference on Cutting-Edge Science and Technology (WCCEST)
abstract: E-rickshaws play a pivotal role in urban mobility in India; however, challenges such as battery theft and deteriorating battery performance threaten their reliability and sustainability. This paper proposes a novel “Battery Guardian,” an integrated system that combines weight-based theft detection, real-time battery health monitoring, ultrasonic proximity sensing, and a dedicated microcontroller to verify battery connectivity. Additionally, a small backup battery ensures that alerts remain functional even if the main battery is disconnected. Experimental results from laboratory and realworld test shows the system's robustness, while advanced data analytics and IoT connectivity promise significant enhancements in predictive maintenance and security. This work lays the foundation for the next generation antitheft and battery management solutions in the rapidly evolving electric mobility sector.

title: Healthcare IoT Mitigating Cyber Threat for Detecting Anomaly Using Generative Adversarial Networks
authors: Hitesh Kalra, Jyotirmaya Sahoo, Kannagi Anbazhagan, Shaktijeet Mohapatra, A. Veeramuthu, Manpreet Singh
publish: 2025 International Conference on AI-Driven STEM Education and Learning Technologies (AISTEMEDU)
abstract: The pursuit of Internet of Things (IoT) in healthcare also transformed patient monitoring and medical data management allowing patients to track their vital signs and diagnose automatically. Nonetheless, this connectivity puts medical devices and patient data in high cyber risks such as unauthorized access, tampering of data, and attacks based on anomalies. The common methods used in anomaly detection are based on centralized machine learning models which are highly likely to compromise the data privacy, limited scalability, and low accuracy in dealing with heterogeneous IoT data. To overcome these difficulties, this study suggests the Healthcare GAN Framework (HGF), a hybrid between Generative Adversarial Networks (GANs) and Federated Learning to perform powerful anomaly detection of medical IoT data. GANs are used in HGF to create synthetic normal data patterns to boost the identification of abnormal behaviors. Simultaneously, Federated Learning supports decentralized learning on many healthcare nodes with patient privacy and scalability guaranteed. The framework that is suggested is applied to control the IoT-enabled medical equipment, identify abnormal trends in physiological measurements, and forestall possible cyberattacks in real-time. As it is shown in the results of the experiments, HGF is more accurate in detecting anomalies, falses are minimized, and sensitive medical information is safely handled in contrast to traditional centralized models. This is a good method of enhancing cybersecurity in healthcare IoT infrastructures. The suggested approach increases the detection accuracy by 97.2, maintains data privacy by 93.4, increases the scalability efficiency by 90.4, and increases the model convergence speed by 94.5.

title: Fine-Grained Zero-Shot Composed Image Retrieval with Complementary Visual-Semantic Integration
authors: Yongcong Ye, Kai Zhang, Yanghai Zhang, Enhong Chen, Longfei Li, Jun Zhou
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Data Mining (ICDM)
abstract: Zero-shot composed image retrieval (ZS-CIR) is a rapidly growing area with significant practical applications, allowing users to retrieve a target image by providing a reference image and a relative caption describing the desired modifications. Existing ZS-CIR methods often struggle to capture fine-grained changes and integrate visual and semantic information effectively. They primarily rely on either transforming the multimodal query into a single text using image-to-text models or employing large language models for target image description generation, approaches that often fail to capture complementary visual information and complete semantic context. To address these limitations, we propose a novel Fine-Grained Zero-Shot Composed Image Retrieval method with Complementary VisualSemantic Integration (CVSI). Specifically, CVSI leverages three key components: (1) Visual Information Extraction, which not only extracts global image features but also uses a pre-trained mapping network to convert the image into a pseudo token, combining it with the modification text and the objects most likely to be added. (2) Semantic Information Extraction, which involves using a pre-trained captioning model to generate multiple captions for the reference image, followed by leveraging an LLM to generate the modified captions and the objects most likely to be added. (3) Complementary Information Retrieval, which integrates information extracted from both the query and database images to retrieve the target image, enabling the system to efficiently handle retrieval queries in a variety of situations. Extensive experiments on three public datasets (e.g., CIRR, CIRCO, and FashionIQ) demonstrate that CVSI significantly outperforms existing state-of-the-art methods. Our code is available at https://github.com/yyc6631/CVSI.

title: Axial Ratio Enhancement of a Reconfigurable Dual Circularly Polarized Transmitarray Antenna Using Hybrid Coding Scheme
authors: Jun Yang, Chen Yang Meng, Yin Li, Jun Wei Wu, Hui Dong Li, Si Ran Wang, Jun Yan Dai, Qiang Cheng
publish: IEEE Transactions on Antennas and Propagation
abstract: This communication presents a novel hybrid coding strategy to enhance the axial ratio (AR) performance of reconfigurable dualcircularly polarized transmitarray antennas (RTAs). The proposed approach integrates PIN-diode–based 1-bit dynamic phase control with Pancharatnam–Berry (PB) phase modulation introduced by element rotation, thereby enabling independent and decoupled control of the lefthand circularly polarized (LHCP) and right-hand circularly polarized (RHCP) components. A particle swarm optimization (PSO) algorithm is employed to optimize the rotation matrix, achieving accurate beam steering and significant cross-polarization suppression, which together lead to a notable improvement in AR performance. To validate the proposed concept, an 18 × 18 1-bit reconfigurable linear-to-dual-circularly polarized transmitarray is designed and fabricated. Simulation results indicate that, compared with the case without hybrid coding, the proposed hybrid coding scheme enhances the 3-dB AR bandwidth by 25.5% and 24% for RHCP and LHCP, respectively, and yields an average AR reduction of at least 2.08 dB within the 0°–60° scanning range in both the E- and H-planes. Measured results show good agreement with simulations, confirming the effectiveness of the proposed design for satellite communication applications.

title: A New Control Strategy of a Boost PFC for Enhanced GaN-based DC-DC Converters
authors: Fabio Cacciotto, Salvatore Torrisi, Santi Agatino Rizzo
publish: IEEE Open Journal of Power Electronics
abstract: This paper presents an AC-line-synchronized hiccup-mode control strategy for Power Factor Correction (PFC) stages in two-stage AC/DC converters supplying GaN-based Quasi-Resonant Flyback (QRF) converters. The proposed method exploits the regulatory allowance below the IEC 61000-3-2 power threshold to intermittently disable the PFC, while selectively re-enabling it only within the high-efficiency portion of the AC mains cycle. By combining a voltage-hysteresis-based DC-bus management with AC synchronization, the proposed strategy significantly reduces switching losses at light and medium loads while preserving robust transient performance during sudden load steps. Unlike conventional PFC techniques, the PFC is physically powered down at the controller-supply level, avoiding residual losses and cold-start conditions, and ensuring immediate restart without inrush or surge currents. Design criteria for the DC-bus voltage limits are analytically derived to guarantee stable operation of the downstream QRF converter under worst-case load and line conditions, without requiring bulk-capacitor oversizing or increased overcurrent protection limits. The effectiveness of the proposed control strategy is validated through detailed simulations and experimental results on a 100 W GaN-based prototype, demonstrating improved efficiency at light-to-medium loads and superior transient robustness compared with conventional PFC management techniques.

title: Differential Predictive Control of Residential Building HVACs for Enhancing Renewable Local Consumption and Supporting Fast Voltage Control
authors: Patrick Salter, Celina Wilkerson, Qiuhua Huang, Paulo Cesar Tabares-Velasco, Dongbo Zhao, Dmitry Ishchenko
publish: IEEE Open Access Journal of Power and Energy
abstract: High penetration of distributed energy resources (DER) in distribution systems, such as rooftop solar PVs, has caused voltage fluctuations which are much faster than typical voltage control devices can react to, leading to increased operation cost and reduced equipment life. Residential buildings consume about 35% of the electricity in the U.S. and are colocated with rooftop solar PV. Thus, they present an opportunity to mitigate these fluctuations locally, while benefiting both the grid and building owners. Previous works on DER-aware localized building energy management mostly focus on commercial buildings and analyzing impacts either on buildings or the grid. To fill the gaps, this paper proposes a distributed, differential predictive control scheme for residential HVAC systems for maximizing renewable local consumption while maintaining occupant comfort. In addition, a detailed controller-building-grid co-simulation platform is developed and utilized for analyzing the potential impacts of the proposed control scheme on both buildings and distribution systems. Our studies show that the proposed method can provide benefits to both the buildings’ owners and the distribution system by reducing electricity bills by 5%, voltage violations and fast fluctuations by 48%, and the number of tap changes in voltage regulators by 19%.

title: Data-Driven Safety-Aware Reinforcement Learning for Transient Scheduling of Turbofan Engines
authors: Weiqun Fan, Jiaxian Sun, Chunyan Hu, Xingen Lu, Keqiang Miao, Qingwen Zeng
abstract: Safe transient operation is essential for turbofan engines, yet conventional min–max schedules are often conservative and require extensive manual tuning. This paper proposes a data-driven, safety-aware reinforcement learning framework for acceleration and deceleration scheduling. A multi-condition long short-term memory (LSTM) surrogate is trained using ground-test measurements augmented with trajectories generated by a calibrated high-fidelity component-level engine model. Similarity-consistent corrected variables are adopted to represent spool-speed dynamics across the flight envelope, and a physics-informed surrogate is included as a baseline. On the surrogate environment, a safety-aware Soft Actor–Critic (SAC) agent is trained offline to generate spool-speed reference trajectories while enforcing limits on surge margin, turbine inlet temperature, and spool overspeed. The learned scheduler is deployed at the feedforward scheduling layer and provides constraint-aware speed commands to a conventional inner-loop controller and protection logic, preserving a certifiable feedback structure. Proximal Policy Optimization (PPO) and Twin Delayed DDPG (TD3) are implemented as scalar-action baselines, whereas the SAC scheduler uses a progress-based two-dimensional action to jointly adjust the advancement along a nominal speed path and the fuel-flow profile. Simulations on the component-level model over multiple maneuvers and representative (H, Ma) points, with parameter perturbations and sensor noise, showthat the proposed scheduler reduces the mean rise and settling times of the high-pressure spool by about 31% versus a min–max schedule and achieves a further 3–4% speedup over PPO-, TD3-, and PINN-based baselines while satisfying all safety constraints.

title: Towards High-Fidelity and Animatable Mesh Reconstruction From Single-View In-the-Wild Animal Images_supp2-3667527.mp4
authors: Xiaoguang Han
abstract: Animals are integral to our society, contributing to the richness and variety of life and providing companionship for many people. Estimating 3D shapes and poses from animal imagery is vital for studying animal behavior and creating lifelike representations for educational and virtual reality applications. Existing approaches for single-view animal reconstruction either struggle in generating 3D models well aligned with the input image or lack fine details and consistent topology. To address these issues, we propose HiAnimal, a novel framework that aims to generate high-fidelity and animatable 3D animals with strong scaling ability to novel data. HiAnimal tackles the challenge by integrating the merits of pixel-aligned implicit prior and the parametric model. Particularly, we predict the per-pixel occupancy-uv field based on an intermediate normal map representation inferred from the input image. This improves the model's ability to capture fine details and generalize across different instances. We further incorporate the parametric shape prior by aligning the SMAL model with the estimated reconstruction, ensuring consistent mesh topology and more regularized results with cleaner geometry. We achieve this by introducing a novel method that estimates a pixel-aligned UV correspondence field for precise alignment. Extensive experiments have verified our superior performance over the state-of-the-art approaches.

title: Modeling and Compensation of Robot Kinematic Errors Based on Error Separation Techniques
authors: Tao Ling, Xu Zhang, Dawei Tu, Xianpeng Zhang
abstract: Accurate positioning of a robot end effector is essential for precision operations. Conventional calibration methods typically identify kinematic parameters from end-effector spatial errors, but they overlook parameter coupling and thus offer limited accuracy improvements. To address this limitation, this article proposes a kinematic comprehensive error model based on error separation technology. The method separates the pose error caused by bidirectional angular positioning deviations of joint rotation axes from the spatial error of the end effector. Subsequently, it identifies rigid-flexible coupling kinematic parameters under the influence of link self-gravity. To enhance parameter identification, a pose error measurement method is developed using a specially designed experimental tool, and the iterative one-by-one forward floating search (IOOFFS) algorithm is proposed as the pose selection strategy. Based on the identified parameters, joint angle compensation is used to realize kinematic error compensation. Experimental validation on the TB6-R10 robot demonstrates that the proposed method can significantly enhance the position accuracy of the robot end effector. Specifically, the maximum absolute positioning error is reduced from 2.995 to 0.223 mm, and the average positioning error is reduced from 1.738 to 0.094 mm, corresponding to an overall reduction of 94.61%.

title: Scalable Building HVAC Control Through Laxity-Based Reinforcement Learning
authors: Ruohong Liu, Yuxin Pan, Yize Chen
abstract: Demand flexibility is playing a vital role in maintaining grid power balance, reducing peak demand, and saving energy bills. Given their highly shiftable characteristics and significant contribution to a building’s energy consumption, Heating, Ventilation, and Air Conditioning (HVAC) systems can provide valuable demand flexibility to the power systems by adjusting their power consumption in response to electricity price and power system needs. To exploit such flexibility in terms of both operation time and demand level, modeling and efficient aggregation techniques of HVAC load flexibility are investigated in this paper. To tackle the curse of dimensionality issue associated with an increasing number of HVAC devices, the notion of laxity for HVAC is proposed in this work to quantify the urgency level of each building’s operation request. Considering the inherent complexity and uncertainty presented in HVAC systems, we design a novel deep reinforcement learning (RL)-based controller to schedule the aggregate dispatched power based on the efficient representation of aggregated laxity information as well as the electricity price. For each HVAC load, only laxity information is revealed instead of full states, and integrated with proposed RL agent using least-laxity-first (LLF) rule for power allocation. To validate the superior efficiency and performance provided by our laxity-based RL agent, we evaluate the temperature control and the energy cost savings of a large number of HVACs in both single-zone and multi-zone scenarios under varying climate and electricity market conditions. Our data-driven approach also significantly saves the modeling and data burden compared to model-based controllers. Empirically, numerical results also indicate that the proposed approach outperforms standard RL with full HVAC states. Compared to the widely used deep RL baseline, it achieves up to 42% reduction in total energy cost while maintaining comparable average temperature deviation.

title: MERGE: A Bimodal Audio-Lyrics Dataset For Static Music Emotion Recognition
authors: Pedro Lima Louro, Hugo Redinho, Tiago Filipe Rodrigues Ribeiro, Ricardo Santos, Ricardo Malheiro, Renato Panda, Rui Pedro Paiva
abstract: The Music Emotion Recognition (MER) field has seen steady developments in recent years, with contributions from feature engineering, machine learning, and deep learning. The landscape has also shifted from audio-centric systems to bimodal ensembles that combine audio and lyrics. However, a lack of public, sizable and quality-controlled bimodal databases has hampered the development and improvement of bimodal audio-lyrics systems. This article proposes three new audio, lyrics, and bimodal MER research datasets, collectively referred to as MERGE (for Music Emotion Recognition next GEneration, the research project that funded its creation), which were created using a semi-automatic approach. To the best of our knowledge, this is the largest bimodal quality-controlled static MER dataset. Furthermore, the dataset is annotated both with discrete emotion categories (according to Russell's four emotion quadrants) and continuous arousal-valence values. To comprehensively assess the proposed dataset and establish a baseline for benchmarking, we conducted several experiments for each modality, using feature engineering, machine learning, and deep learning methodologies. Additionally, we propose and validate fixed train-validation-test splits. The obtained results confirm the viability of the proposed datasets, achieving the best overall result of 81.74% F1-score for bimodal classification.

title: Evaluating 3D Flash Memory retention reliability under Temperature Elevation
authors: Yuqian Pan, ZhaoXu Wang, Xuepeng Zhou, Rui Zhang, Zhenglin Liu, Runze Yu
abstract: This investigation identifies a potential threat that compromises the data retention integrity of 3D NAND flash memory under accelerated thermal-elevation conditions. To examine the influence of thermal-elevation conditions, controlled preconditioning heating phases with varying ramping rates were applied to actual flash memory devices prior to data retention. Experimental results indicate that rapid temperature elevation can significantly impact the retention reliability of 3D NAND flash memory devices. For equivalent retention periods, the RBERs of the tested flash chips increased by approximately 4.8 times as the duration of thermal elevation was shortened. Furthermore, flash chips that underwent P/E cycling were more sensitive to reduced ramping durations than devices in the early life stage. Based on these findings, the mathematical model for RBER in 3D NAND flash memory was refined to account for elevated temperature effects. The optimized model demonstrates strong predictive performance, as indicated by a coefficient of determination value of 0.95.

title: Dynamic Query Management and Internal Consistency Representation based Transformer for Online Vectorized HD Map Construction
authors: Wenjing Bai, Yunzhou Zhang, Qi Guo, Wei Liu, Shangwei Du, Jun Hu, Shuai Cheng, Zuotao Ning
abstract: The online vectorized map construction technique employs a neural network model to forecast the vectorized representation of a specific region around automobiles, using data obtained from sensors mounted on automobiles. Due to advances in end-to-end object detection with transformers framework, the research on query-based online mapping has attracted substantial attention. However, the fixed number of queries and the random initialization of query embeddings constrain the model's performance. Moreover, the transformer architecture for object detection is based on the assumption that queries are identically distributed and independent, a premise that is not entirely applicable to map point queries which possess established subordinate relationships with map element instances. To address these issues, we initially incorporate a simplified transformer layer that utilizes semantic priors in bird's-eye view features for query initialization. The queries are then sent to a transformer-based map decoder for optimization and combined with a dynamic query management mechanism to eliminate low-confidence queries, hence maintaining computational efficiency. Furthermore, to guarantee that point queries within each instance preserve a consistent representation and avoid feature confusion among map element instances, we proposed an instance internal consistency map decoder. We conduct extensive experiments on commonly used map construction datasets to evaluate the proposed method. The experimental results demonstrate that our proposed method achieves state-of-the-art performance on the nuScenes and Argoverse 2 datasets.

title: Broadband Vibration Energy Harvesting via Coordinated Dual-Bistable Oscillators for Self-Powered Subway Bogie Monitoring
authors: Dilong Tu, Yuan Zhang, Yong Qin, Xiaoqin Chen, Mengzhou Liu, Haixia Jia, Linlin Kou, Yang Guan, Jing Liu
publish: IEEE Transactions on Instrumentation and Measurement
abstract: To address the limitations of battery dependency, high maintenance costs, and narrow operational frequency bands in wireless sensor nodes (WSNs) for subway train bogie monitoring, this paper proposes a self-powered WSN based on a dual-bistable vibration energy harvesting (D-BVEH) system. The D-BVEH employs a dual-array design with tunable magnet-spring nonlinear dynamics, enabling broadband energy harvesting across 6–51 Hz and dual output peaks at 35 Hz and 51 Hz, achieving a 48% wider bandwidth compared to single bistable harvesters. Key parameters, including magnet dimensions and spring stiffness, were optimized through finite element analysis and parametric simulations to ensure balanced dual potential wells and low energy barriers, enhancing adaptability to complex vibration conditions. Experimental results demonstrate a harvested power of 14.1 mW, with reliable acquisition of 3-axial acceleration and temperature data in the bench test using field-collected operational vibration data that simulates real subway vibration conditions. The system eliminates external power dependencies and offers a cost-effective, adaptive solution for bogie health monitoring, significantly advancing intelligent maintenance in rail transportation systems.

title: A New Hybrid OMA/NOMA Scheme for Dynamic Resource Allocation and Pilot Contamination Reduction in Distributed Massive MIMO
authors: Diogo Acatauassu, Aline A. Ohashi, Gilvan S. Borges, André L. P. Fernandes, Daniel B. da Costa, Maria V. Marquezini, André M. Cavalcante, João C. W. A. Costa
abstract: Pilot contamination reduction and efficient resource allocation are key points in the development of distributed massive multiple-input multiple-output (MIMO) networks, also known as cell-free massive MIMO networks. In this context, this study proposes a simple adaptive hybrid orthogonal multiple access (OMA)/non-orthogonal multiple access (NOMA) strategy to be used in those innovative networks, capable of achieving efficient resource allocation and pilot contamination mitigation. The proposed strategy exploits the advantages of both the OMA and NOMA schemes by adaptively switching between them for uplink channel estimation and downlink transmission, that is, the switching process is gradual and relies on the number of users in the network as well as the number of available pilot sequences. The decision policy is designed in a simple manner to obtain a low-complexity but powerful method that is attractive in terms of practical aspects. This simultaneous hybrid approach reduces pilot contamination between users, leads to flexible and more efficient resource allocation, improves spectral efficiency (SE), increases the network's achievable sum rates, and offers a good compromise between the number of served users and achievable rates. The results are obtained using a flexible model for imperfect successive interference cancellation (ISIC) and indicate that the proposed strategy can achieve better performance than entirely OMA and NOMA-based systems.

title: Leveraging GPT-4o's Multimodal Capabilities for Enhanced Video Anomaly Detection in Complex Scenarios
authors: Arsovski Sasa, Tan Ye Zi, Choo Yen Lee
publish: 2025 International Conference on Advances in Machine Intelligence, and Cybersecurity Technologies (AMICT)
abstract: Video anomaly detection refers to the identification of unexpected events in video streams that deviate from typical patterns. In our research we use OpenAI's GPT-4o and GPT-4o mini models' multimodal capabilities to explore a unique method for video anomaly detection. Methodology presented in this paper employs prompt-based labelling that relies on structured prompts to direct the GPT-4o, and GPT-4o-mini multimodal models’ analysis, enabling effective video labelling and behavior classification for video anomaly detection tasks. We compare our method with the state-of-the-art techniques for detecting anomalies in videos, including the divide-and-conquer strategies, rule-based reasoning, two-stream frameworks, and context feature improvement. The performance of the method proposed in our research was evaluated on a specially selected subset of the Shanghai Tech and UCSD datasets. Additionally, we provide insight into the advantages and disadvantages of each technique for video anomaly detection and compare results with suggested prompt-based labelling methods used with GPT-4o and GPT 4o-mini multimodal models. Results evaluation show that our method achieves results that are similar or better compared to traditional models. However, there is potential for further refinement, such as real-time detection, optimized response structuring, and model fine-tuning. Our method provides the groundwork for incorporating multimodal AI models into practical anomaly detection tasks and opens the possibility for further deep evaluations on real-life datasets to confirm the system's reliability and scalability across a range of applications.

title: Design and Analysis of SIMD Functional Units for RISC-V P-Extension Instructions
authors: Nancy Gupta, Gopal Raut, David Selvakumar, Pranose J Edavoor, Prasoon, Nissy Niharika
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent and Innovative Technologies in Computing, Electrical and Electronics (IITCEE)
abstract: The RISC-V (RV) instruction set architecture (ISA) has gained widespread adoption due to its modularity, open source, and extensibility. The P-extension introduces SIMD (Single Instruction, Multiple Data) operations, which aim at efficient execution of neural networks, machine learning, computer vision, signal processing, and edge computing workloads. This paper presents the design, analysis, and verification of independent p-SIMD units with a hierarchical decoder for RV32 and RV64 P-extensions in Verilog HDL that support a comprehensive subset of 323 instructions. The front- end interface for the P-SIMD units facilitates integration with the existing RV32/64I core's pipeline at the execution stage. Functional simulation and UVM based verification have been performed in Vivado and QuestaSim to validate the accuracy and robustness. These P-SIMD units serve as the foundational component for high-performance SIMD / vector-capable RISC-V cores targeting compute-intensive embedded systems viz. edge computing, IoT etc.

title: Decentralized Health Monitoring: A Blockchain Approach to Patient Data Security
authors: Kiran P, Manohar P, Harshitha S, Priyadarshini S, Yamini C
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent and Innovative Technologies in Computing, Electrical and Electronics (IITCEE)
abstract: Blockchain technology is increasingly recognized as a transformative approach in healthcare, especially for managing, storing, and sharing medical data. While current methods mainly focus on secure handling of Electronic Health Records (EHRs), the framework proposed here adds two key features: a doctor verification system that ensures only authorized practitioners can access sensitive records, and a patient-centered reminder module to support timely management of appointments and medications. The architecture consists of three main components—secure record management, doctor registration and verification, and intelligent reminders. Additionally, the framework combines blockchain with the InterPlanetary File System (IPFS) and Internet of Things (IoT) modules, improving both security and accessibility. This paper presents the architecture, methodology, and implementation, and discusses the potential benefits and challenges, highlighting the ability of decentralized healthcare systems to provide tamper-resistant, transparent, and patient-focused solutions.

title: Kernel-based Clustering for hybrid CNN-based Real-Time Face Emotion Annotation
authors: Nur Nadiah Asyikin Samruzlan, Raseeda Hamzah, Muhammad Asyraf Suhaile, Khyrina Airin Fariza Abu Samah, Wan Nur Azhani W. Samsudin
publish: 2025 International Conference on Advances in Machine Intelligence, and Cybersecurity Technologies (AMICT)
abstract: This research introduces the real-time representation of facial emotion annotation system to help children with Autism Spectrum Disorder (ASD) in the therapy process. The system utilizes Kernel-based similarity to clustering the faces into three emotions which are angry, sad and happy. YOLOv8 is used in detecting faces along with three AI Convolutional Neural Network (CNN) pre-trained models comparison comprising InceptionV3, MobileNetV2 and Xception in the categorization of emotions. The test accuracy of InceptionV3 is 63.76 %, MobileNetV2 61% and Xception 56.56 %. The models were good when it came to specific emotions such as happiness but not when the specific emotion was subtle such as fear and disgust. Experiments show that the used of hybrid variant which are the combination of all three CNN architecture, performed best with test accuracy of about 80.1%. The hybrid method exploits InceptionV3 as the feature extractor with robust capability, MobileNetV2 as the lightweight processing and Xception as the detailed analyzer. The advantage of this combination is that both accuracy and stability were enhanced and were efficient in real time applications.

title: Accelerating Drug Repurposing with AI: A Case Study of Existing Drugs
authors: Ashok Kumar Saini, Devesh Bandil, Ruchi Dave, Krati Dubey, Prashant Vats
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Artificial Intelligence for Innovations in Healthcare Industries (ICAIIHI)
abstract: Artificial intelligence (AI) is a revolutionary driver of drug discovery and repurposing, providing new avenues to speed up pharmaceutical innovation and precision medicine. Conventional drug development is time-consuming, expensive, and usually limited by high attrition rates. Current advancements illustrate the way AI-based approaches— spanning machine learning, deep learning, natural language processing, and generative models—facilitate swift discovery of drug candidates for various diseases like COVID-19, Alzheimer's, cancer, multiple sclerosis, and pulmonary hypertension. AI aids in hypothesis generation, structure-based screening, biomarker stratification, and supply chain optimization, and even implements clinical data for real-world validation. Research emphasizes its contribution to the discovery of synergistic drug combinations, adaptive trial design augmentation, and verification of action mechanisms using bio-simulation and large language models. However, despite all these advances, challenges still abound with regard to data quality, model interpretability, ethical adoption, and regulatory buy-in. Nevertheless, AI-assisted repurposing is a cost-efficient avenue to find new uses for approved drugs, thereby shortening development timelines and increasing therapeutic possibilities. Together, these findings highlight AI's potential to transform drug discovery pipelines and shorten the bench-to-bedside gap. Future directions highlight interdisciplinary teaming, explainable AI models, and the incorporation of clinician feedback to optimize translational potential.

title: Short-term voltage stability analysis of hybrid renewable energy systems with composite load
authors: Ming Yang Hng, Yitian Dai, Robin Preece
publish: 2025 25th Conference of the Electric Power Supply Industry (CEPSI)
abstract: The increasing integration of Hybrid Renewable Energy Systems (HRES) into modern power grids presents new challenges for short-term voltage stability (STVS), particularly in scenarios involving Fault-Induced Delayed Voltage Recovery (FIDVR). This study investigates the STVS characteristics of an HRES microgrid under FIDVR conditions, focusing on the STVS impact due to variation in HRES generation compositions and topology between inverter-based resources (IBRs) and synchronous generators using the Transient Voltage Stability Index (TVSI) as the key analytical metric. The results indicated that generation configurations dominated by Inverter-Based Resources (IBRs) exhibit the best short-term voltage stability (STVS) overall, whereas standalone synchronous generators perform the worst in comparison. This research contributes valuable insights for microgrid operators and policymakers by elucidating the complex interactions between generation composition, topology, load dynamics, and grid strength in HRES environments. The findings will help to enable optimizing dispatch of different type of generation resources to ensure reliable grid operations.

title: Development of Asset Health Management System to Embrace Digital Asset Management
authors: Lo Kwong Shing, Lam Cheuk Yin, Leung Man Yee
publish: 2025 25th Conference of the Electric Power Supply Industry (CEPSI)
abstract: The enterprises in the power and energy sector are undergoing digital transformation to include asset information in a digital platform with agile approaches, which helps strengthen operational efficiency, optimise resources and empower data-driven decision making based on conditions of power assets.This paper shares the development and insight of an overarching Asset Health Management System (AHMS), which has been adopted by CLP Power Hong Kong Limited (CLP Power) for integrating operation data and condition indicators (e.g. loading, operational time, observed defects, dissolved gas analysis results, partial discharge, furfuraldehyde content in oil, etc.) collected from various online condition monitoring devices and offline site tests for purpose of asset health assessment. Besides, the AHMS serves as a centralised platform that eases the management of all types of power assets in CLP Power, including switchgears, transformers, cables and overhead lines at different voltage levels. It provides advanced analytics to give insight into asset performance and lifespan.The paper will discuss the design algorithm and key features of AHMS, which support flexible maintenance regimes that address the performance of power assets in a dynamic manner. By leveraging AHMS, CLP Power can implement diversified and effective intervention strategies with optimal efficiency, enabling data-driven decision-making for the delivery of high-performance power assets.

title: A Deep Learning Approach to Efficient Corn Pest Detection: Evaluating YOLO Models for Sustainable Agriculture
authors: Sumedha Mishra, Atul Dubey, Vani Kandasamy
publish: 2026 IEEE 16th Annual Computing and Communication Workshop and Conference (CCWC)
abstract: Crop pests continue to be one of the biggest challenges in agriculture, destroying crops and reducing yields around the world. Traditional pest control methods, such as chemical pesticides and coated seeds, can reduce damage but also harm the environment, contaminate soil, and pose health risks to humans and animals. This research aims to address these issues by developing a deep learning model and a web application that can detect corn pests accurately and efficiently. The model was created by performing transfer learning on the YOLOv8 and YOLOv9 object detection models, trained on 11 pest categories from the IP102 dataset. After testing different learning rates and epochs, the YOLOv9 model achieved the best validation results, with an mAP50 of 75.2%. When focusing on the nine most common pest categories, the model reached an mAP50 of 85.6%, showing that it can successfully detect the majority of corn pests. In addition, another model was trained with augmented images of the three lower-performing categories, and the ensemble of both models achieved a test mAP50 of 75.7%. These findings demonstrate that deep learning based pest detection can serve as a safer and more targeted alternative to traditional pest control methods, reducing pesticide use while maintaining healthy crop production.

title: A Comparative Analysis of Heterogenous and Homogenous GraphSAGE Models for Movie Recommendations System
authors: Yusry Anandita Yulianti, Fitriyani
publish: 2025 2nd Beyond Technology Summit on Informatics International Conference (BTS-I2C)
abstract: The film industry currently utilizes various platforms that provide access to thousands of films. However, this situation also makes it difficult for people to find the movies they want. Therefore, an adaptive recommendation system is needed for users. Several studies have been implemented, but they are still limited to the use of traditional methods such as Collaborative Filtering (CF) and Content-Based Filtering (CBF), which still face difficulties in discovering complex interactions between multiple data entities. GraphSAGE can learn the process of aggregation and neighbor sampling to produce consistent embedding values for recommendation systems. Based on this process, this study proposes a comparison of Heterogeneous and Homogeneous Graph Sample and Aggregate (GraphSAGE) methods in finding the most effective representation for movie recommendation systems. The novelty of this experiment lies in the process of integrating complex (multi-entity) relationships between users, movies, and genres for model learning, the process of setting standard hyperparameters to ensure model fairness, and the correlation analysis between metrics for model stability and consistency. This experiment uses the MovieLens 2024 Belief Dataset with sampling and data splitting to prevent leakage. Heterogeneous (GraphSAGE) results provide the best value compared to Homogeneous GraphSAGE, namely the Precision metric of 83.09%, the Recall metric of 97.63%, the F1-Score metric of 89.73%, the Mean Average Precision (MAP) metric of 96.72%, and the Normalized Discounted Cumulative Gain (NDCG) metric of 99.72%. The findings of this study provide evidence that the process of combining multi-entity data and standard hyperparameter settings can significantly improve model performance for accurate recommendation systems.

title: A Deterministic Model of Dengue Fever with two Delays and a Control through the Distribution of Impregnated Mosquito Nets
authors: Mohamed Coulibaly, Gérard Kanga, N’Da N’Guessan Anastase Mian
publish: 2025 International Conference on Electrical and Computer Engineering Researches (ICECER)
abstract: Our aim in this paper is to present a more realistic dynamics of dengue-fever in humans and mosquitoes through a deterministic mathematical model with two delays. One time delay before humans become infectious and another time delay before mosquitoes become infectious. We prove some mathematical properties of this model. Secondly, we improve this model by introducing public authority control reflecting the implementation of barrier measures with the distribution of impregnated mosquito nets to the general public. We establish certain mathematical stability properties this new model, for controlling the number of daily infectious bites. We prove that with a certain calibration of the control introduced, the coverage of the disease can be greatly reduced. Numerical simulations support our results.

title: Performance Evaluation of LightGCN for Amazon Product Recommendation
authors: Nuh Priyonggo, Fitriyani, Danang Triantoro Murdiansyah
publish: 2025 2nd Beyond Technology Summit on Informatics International Conference (BTS-I2C)
abstract: The rapid growth of user–product interactions on large-scale e-commerce platforms such as Amazon has created challenges in generating relevant product recommendations, particularly due to data sparsity and the abundance of items. Traditional collaborative filtering methods often struggle to capture complex higher-order relationships within such sparse data. To address these limitations, this study applies the Light Graph Convolutional Network (LightGCN) model, which represents user-item interactions as a bipartite graph and propagates embeddings through neighborhood connections without requiring nonlinear transformations. This study uses the Amazon Reviews dataset across three product categories, including Industrial_and_Scientific, Musical_Instruments, and Video_Games. The model’s performance was evaluated using Recall@K and NDCG@K metrics and compared to a traditional Item-Based Collaborative Filtering (IBCF) baseline. Experimentally, LightGCN outperforms the traditional Collaborative Filtering model by an average of 116% in Recall@20 and 101% in NDCG@20. Although LightGCN still depends on historical interactions, its embedding propagation enables better utilization of existing data, effectively mitigating the impact of sparsity. The results also show that LightGCN generates more personalized but less diverse recommendations than IBCF, revealing a trade-off between accuracy and diversity. These findings underscore that LightGCN delivers a highly efficient and effective solution for powering modern recommendation systems, particularly in large-scale e-commerce environments.

title: Performance assessment of RLS and NLMS algorithms for the traffic noise cancellation
authors: Chiheb Amira, Khelladi Hassina
publish: 2025 International Conference on Electrical and Computer Engineering Researches (ICECER)
abstract: In this investigation, two adaptive algorithms based on two different approaches in noise cancellation are considered. The study focuses on the normalized least mean squares algorithm (NLMS), a widely used variant of the least mean square technique (LMS). The NLMS algorithm exploits the stochastic gradient method in the adaptation process. A second algorithm is handled in this paper built on the least squares estimation (LSE), known as the recursive least squares algorithm (RLS). Both algorithms are examined for active noise cancellation (ANC) by analyzing their noise suppression ability and the convergence swiftness using real recordings of different types of noise. From the simulation experiments, the RLS algorithm provides superior noise suppression; whereas the NLMS converges more quickly. While most of the researches have been in the direction of mainly reducing noise in speech or biomedical signals by considering the signal to noise ratio (SNR) parameter, the present research is devoted to completely eliminating traffic noise signals through the application of adaptive filtering techniques for an optimal acoustic comfort.

title: Behavioral Biometrics for Zero Trust Authentication: A CNN-LSTM Framework for Continuous Authentication *
authors: Damodhara Reddy Palavali, Dinesh Kumar Elumalai, Suneetha Pothireddy
publish: 2026 IEEE 16th Annual Computing and Communication Workshop and Conference (CCWC)
abstract: Modern authentication systems largely depend on fixed credentials, and these credentials can easily be leaked and used against the insiders. Behavioral biometrics, on the other hand make it possible to authenticate long-term and uninterrupted authentication based on unique interaction patterns that the electronic devices have. This work designs a hybrid CNN-LSTM model for continuous authentication of enterprise work-stations for a Zero-Trust Architecture (ZTA), using keystroke dynamics, mouse dynamics, and device context, where these latter three form an Authentication Confidence Score (ACS) which determines risk-adaptive access control. The findings are based on a synthetic dataset developed from 500 subjects participating in 60,000 sessions and present 96.8 % accuracy, 1.7 % False Acceptance Rate (FAR), <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{1. 5 \%}$</tex> False Rejection Rate (FRR), an Area Under the Curve (AUC) of 0.985, and an inference time of 240 milliseconds-latency comparable to real-time, enterprise efficacy. Extensive evaluation, including ablation studies finding that more than 50 % error rate reductions occur when multi-modal fusion is employed over unimodal solutions; adversarial evaluation to show how attack-like situations find resiliency in performance; privacy-preserving evaluation through federated learning and edge-based inference finding compliance with GDPR and NIST SP 800-207 requirements; and generalizability testing finding multi-modal behavioral biometrics to be a successful user-invisible security enhancement for a zero-trust enterprise architecture.

title: Novel Quantum Cryptography Protocol for Secure Satellite Communication
authors: Neha N, Kiran K N, Mahima M Hebbar, K U Dikshitha
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent and Innovative Technologies in Computing, Electrical and Electronics (IITCEE)
abstract: Quantum communication leverages the principles of quantum mechanics, such as superposition and entanglement, for inherently secure data transmission that can detect any eavesdropping attempt. This research addresses the limitations of classical encryption by proposing a hybrid Quantum Key Distribution (QKD) protocol that integrates the Bennett & Brassard 1984 Protocol(BB84) and Scarani-Acín-Ribordy-Gisin 2004 Protocol(SARG04) protocols. The primary objective is to enhance resilience against advanced threats such as Photon Number Splitting (PNS) and collective attacks, which are significant vulnerabilities in standard QKD implementations. The quantum channel is modeled using Qiskit Aer's noise framework, incorporating fiber attenuation (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$0.1-0.25 ~\text{dB} / \text{km}$</tex>), depolarization (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$1-5 \%$</tex>), and detector dark counts (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$0.01-0.03$</tex> probability), varied across 10 rounds. A simulation spanning 10 rounds evaluated key metrics, including Quantum Bit Error Rate (QBER), photon loss, and final key entropy. The results demonstrate the protocol's robustness, achieving a 0 % QBER in 50 % of the trials, representing a significant improvement in secure key generation. The average QBER across all rounds was 12.5 %, which is well below the 11 % theoretical threshold for secure communication. The highest QBER recorded was <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{5 0 \%}$</tex> in Round 02, where high depolarization and an eavesdropping attempt were simulated. The work's findings indicate that the hybrid BB84-SARG04 protocol significantly enhances the security and reliability of quantum communication.

title: GlucoGaze: A Risk Prediction Tool for microRNAs as Novel Biomarkers for Type 2 Diabetes Mellitus and Linked Diseases
authors: Saket Pathak
publish: 2026 IEEE 16th Annual Computing and Communication Workshop and Conference (CCWC)
abstract: Type 2 diabetes mellitus (T2DM) is a metabolic condition characterized by insulin resistance and high blood glucose levels, affecting roughly 462 million people worldwide. MicroRNAs (miRNAs) regulate gene expression post-transcriptionally, and recent studies suggest a link between novel miRNAs and T2DM risk, progression, and related diseases. This research presents GlucoGaze, a risk prediction tool integrating fasting glucose levels from inexpensive medical devices with demographic factors to assess diabetes risk and predict brain hemorrhage, associated with miRNA patterns identified through blood-based analysis. An extensive literature review identified promising miRNA candidates, followed by data collection from GEO and ArrayExpress, including expression profiles and glucose levels, and preprocessing for data integrity. miR-144 was isolated as the top regulatory candidate via differential expression analysis and IRS1-miRNA correlation regression modeling. Statistical and machine learning (ML) techniques were applied for feature engineering and selection, resulting in predictive models leveraging the PIMA diabetes dataset and a Kaggle stroke dataset of 5110 observations with 12 attributes. The LSTM model achieved the strongest results for diabetes risk prediction (accuracy = 0.972, F1 = 0.984), while logistic regression performed best for IRS1 prediction (accuracy = 0.913, F1 = 0.896). Rigorous cross-validation and model evaluation ensured the reliability and effectiveness of the models. The results reveal a strong correlation between miR-144 and T2DM, alongside the development of an app for predicting T2DM and hemorrhage risk. These findings suggest that GlucoGaze holds promise for expediting diagnosis and personalized management of diabetes and related diseases when further refined and validated in clinical settings.

title: Food Recipe Recommender System Based on Available Ingredients Using Case-Based Reasoning
authors: Azra Feby Awfiyah, Z. K. A. Baizal
publish: 2025 2nd Beyond Technology Summit on Informatics International Conference (BTS-I2C)
abstract: Nowadays, the public has a growing concern with regard to healthy living, leading to an even greater demand for a system that could assist users in selecting a recipe according to the ingredients they have at home. The problem is becoming even more pressing, given that Indonesia produces 14.73 million tons of food waste each year, the highest level among countries in Southeast Asia, due to the limitations related to the optimization of food ingredient consumption. Even if various studies have developed a recipe recommender system, the developed systems are still faced with the limitations related to a lack of adaptability to new circumstances and an inability to learn continuously even if they have past experiences related to their usage. Considering this limitation, this paper will propose a Case-Based Reasoning-based recipe recommender system that will learn actively and continually by implementing a learning cycle, Retain. In this research, the system will make use of TF-IDF-based material representation and will utilize the strategy related to the search by employing a Cosine Similarity approach, guaranteeing the system has the opportunity to learn by adding any new case automatically to the dynamic Knowledge Based. For this evaluation, it uses a prequential evaluation method that mimics online learning with temporal data splitting first 10% of the data used for establishing the Knowledge Base with the rest treated sequentially. For every case entered into the system, testing serves to generate recommendations before storing the case for knowledge updates periodically. The simulation test has been conducted to compare the dynamic CBR model with a Retain-based strategy against a similar model lacking a learning strategy, resulting in a 61-70% improvement, respectively, regarding all assessment parameters, including, NDCG@10, Recall@10, Precision@10, F1-Score@10, MRR. The simulation results presented an unforeseen opportunity to a number of applications related to food, affirming that the approach related to Retain could significatively influence the results, increasing their quality, as well as paving the grounds to future developments utilizing a more advanced model related to semantic arguments, related to better accuracy rates by the system developed.

title: Grid-scale Hong Kong Battery Energy Storage System (HKBESS) to Smooth out the Demand Peak in a Green Manner
authors: Kaushal Vaishnav, Henry Chi Pun Chan, Anthony Kun Hym Wong
publish: 2025 25th Conference of the Electric Power Supply Industry (CEPSI)
abstract: To meet the decarbonisation targets set under the Hong Kong Government’s Climate Action Plan 2050 and CLP’s Climate Vision 2050, CLP Power has planned various decarbonisation initiatives for power generation and transmission. This paper highlights the development of sustainable power using grid-scale battery storage in Hong Kong to reduce peak system demand, facilitate the integration of clean energy sources, and enhance system flexibility and load regulation capability as coal-fired generation is gradually phased out.
