In this work, we apply the DCT-based JPEG and the Wavelet-based JPEG-2000 compression algorithms, with eleven levels of varying compression, to synthetic datasets generated by the CARLA Driving Simulator. Using relatively simple depth estimation algorithms, which rely on DFF (Depth from Focus) and DFR (Depth from Reference), this study quantitatively evaluates the effects of lossy compression on their performance. We find that these algorithms are insensitive to low levels of lossy compression in comparison to the losslesscompressed baselines. Additionally, the JPEG-2000 compression algorithm outperforms the more widely used JPEG algorithm for equal compression ratios, indicating that datasets for use in DFF and DFR applications can be compressed using the JPEG-2000 algorithm and achieve order-of-magnitude reductions in dataset size while maintaining comparable accuracy.

title: ProHD: Projection-Based Hausdorff Distance Approximation
authors: Jiuzhou Fu, Luanzheng Guo, Nathan R. Tallent, Dongfang Zhao
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Data Mining (ICDM)
abstract: The Hausdorff distance (HD) is a robust measure of set dissimilarity, but computing it exactly on large, high-dimensional datasets is prohibitively expensive. We propose ProHD, a projection-guided approximation algorithm that dramatically accelerates HD computation while maintaining high accuracy. ProHD identifies a small subset of candidate “extreme” points by projecting the data onto a few informative directions (such as the centroid axis and top principal components) and computing the HD on this subset. This approach guarantees an underestimate of the true HD with a bounded additive error and typically achieves results within a few percent of the exact value. In extensive experiments on image, physics, and synthetic datasets (up to two million points in D = 256), ProHD runs 10-100× faster than exact algorithms while attaining 5-20× lower error than random sampling-based approximations. Our method enables practical HD calculations in scenarios like large vector databases and streaming data, where quick and reliable set distance estimation is needed.

title: Explanations Go Linear: Post-Hoc Explainability for Tabular Data with Interpretable Meta-Encoding
authors: Simone Piaggesi, Riccardo Guidotti, Fosca Giannotti, Dino Pedreschi
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Data Mining (ICDM)
abstract: Post-hoc explainability is essential for understanding black-box machine learning models. Surrogate-based techniques are widely used for local and global model-agnostic explanations but have significant limitations. Local surrogates capture non-linearities but are computationally expensive and sensitive to parameters, while global surrogates are more efficient but struggle with complex local behaviors. In this paper, we present ILLUME, a flexible and interpretable framework grounded in representation learning, that can be integrated with various surrogate models to provide explanations for any black-box classifier. Specifically, our approach combines a globally trained surrogate with instance-specific linear transformations learned with a meta-encoder to generate both local and global explanations. Through extensive empirical evaluations, we demonstrate the effectiveness of ILLUME in producing feature attributions and decision rules that are not only accurate but also robust and computationally efficient, thus providing a unified explanation framework that effectively addresses the limitations of traditional surrogate methods.

title: Effective Series Decomposition and Components Learning for Time Series Generation
authors: Zixuan Ma, Chenfeng Huang
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Data Mining (ICDM)
abstract: Time series generation focuses on modeling the underlying data distribution and resampling to produce authentic time series data. Key components, such as trend and seasonality, drive temporal fluctuations, yet many existing approaches fail to employ interpretative decomposition methods, limiting their ability to synthesize meaningful trend and seasonal patterns. To address this gap, we introduce Seasonal-Trend Diffusion (STDiffusion), a novel framework for multivariate time series generation that integrates diffusion probabilistic models with advanced learnable series decomposition techniques, enhancing the interpretability of the generation process. Our approach separates the trend and seasonal learning into distinct blocks: a Multi-Layer Perceptron (MLP) structure captures the trend, while adaptive wavelet distillation facilitates effective multi-resolution learning of seasonal components. This decomposition improves the interpretability of the model on multiple scales. In addition, we designed a comprehensive correction mechanism aimed at ensuring that the generated components exhibit a high degree of internal consistency and preserve meaningful interrelationships with one another. Our empirical studies on eight real-world datasets demonstrate that STDiffusion achieves state-of-the-art performance in time series generation tasks. Furthermore, we extend the model's application to multi-window long-sequence time series generation, which delivered reliable results and highlighted its robustness and versatility. The source code of our model is officially released as STDiffusion on Github https://github.com/mobkageyama/STDiffusion.

title: AI-Powered Medical Imaging: Enhancing Diagnostic Accuracy in Radiology
authors: Hasan Mahmud Sozib, Mahdia Amina, Tanvir Mahmud, Mohammad Delowar Hossain Gazi, Fahim Shahrear, Tasmim Jamal Joti, Anjuman Awal
publish: 2025 International Conference on Advances in Machine Intelligence, and Cybersecurity Technologies (AMICT)
abstract: Medical imaging plays a pivotal role in disease diagnosis, particularly in identifying thoracic conditions such as COVID-19 and pneumonia using chest X-rays. However, the complexity and variability in medical images often limit the effectiveness of traditional convolutional approaches, which struggle with capturing long-range spatial dependencies. To address these limitations, we propose a hybrid deep learning architecture that integrates the strengths of Vision Transformers (ViT) for global spatial feature extraction with recurrent neural network variants—RNN, GRU, and LSTM— for temporal sequence modeling. In our framework, the ViT-Base-Patch16-224 model, pretrained on ImageNet-21k, is employed to extract high-dimensional embeddings from 16×16 image patches. These embeddings are then passed through RNN-based architectures to model sequential dependencies among the patches, enabling a richer and more context-aware representation. The proposed models were evaluated on a balanced dataset of 6939 posteroanterior chest X-ray images, comprising three classes: COVID-19, Pneumonia, and Normal. Extensive experiments revealed that the ViT-LSTM model achieved the highest classification accuracy of 96.33%, outperforming both ViT-GRU (95.51%) and ViT-RNN (93%). These results highlight the effectiveness of combining global attention mechanisms with sequential learning for robust and interpretable medical image classification. Our findings suggest that ViT-LSTM is a promising approach for enhancing diagnostic precision in radiology and could support clinical decision-making in real-world healthcare settings.

title: Envisioning Multiple Contexts of a Historical Landmark Using Augmented Reality: The Chimney Tower
authors: Dinna N. Mohd Nizam, Zhi J. Wong, Zaidatol H. Abdullah Sani, Muhammad A. Adnan, Dinar M. Kusumo
publish: 2025 International Conference on Advances in Machine Intelligence, and Cybersecurity Technologies (AMICT)
abstract: Augmented reality (AR) is a technology that can be used to help close the gap of misinformation and information dissemination to visitors in historical centers. It also attracts visitors and helps them envision old historical artefacts or buildings in almost complete detail. Recent historical research has uncovered additional possible purposes for the Chimney Tower landmark, situated in the Federal Territory of Labuan, Malaysia. However, envisioning the possible atmosphere of the tower is limited for visitors, as the current booklets are static and limited. Therefore, this study aims to design and develop a bespoke augmented reality mobile application to help visitors visualize the possible purposes of the locally known landmark through three-dimensional representations. Experts from the museum and visitors collaborated on the design, and the AR application was developed using Unity. In addition, thirty other visitors participated in assessing the prototype application using the System Usability Score (SUS) tool and conducted brief interviews. The SUS scored 81.33, indicating a highly positive perception among the participants. The short interview evaluations provided positive feedback, and visitors valued the innovative approach of using AR to reimagine the landmark in multiple contexts. Future opportunities and refining the application for a diverse audience were also suggested.

title: Application Study on Ammonia Production by Urea Direct-injection Pyrolysis in SCR Denitration Technology of Gas Steam Combined Cycle Unit
authors: Chen Dong, Xiaobing Wang, Zhuang Yuan, Shuhong Li, Xiaotao Xu, Lei Shi
publish: 2025 25th Conference of the Electric Power Supply Industry (CEPSI)
abstract: The SCR denitration system of a 9F Gas Steam Combined Cycle Unit is taking as an example to study the application of Urea pyrolysis technology. Numerical simulation of urea pyrolysis process under the condition of gas turbine outlet flue gas is studied. The results show that the main products of urea pyrolysis are ammonia and isocyanic acid under the condition of gas turbine outlet flue gas. The urea pyrolysis efficiency increases with the distance from the injection point. When the distance from the injection point is 34.2 meters, the urea pyrolysis efficiency reaches 99%. When the injection points of urea solution are arranged with 12 or 29, the root mean square deviation of pyrolysis product distribution on the catalyst inlet section was less than 5%. In practical engineering application, the metering dilution system with dilution water can keep the atomized particle parameters of each injector in the optimum working condition under different loads. The injector with heat insulation structure and cooling air can avoid water evaporation of urea solution, which can lead to the clogging of the injector.

title: A Comprehensive Review Of Hippotherapy And Exercise Interventions For Motor Rehabilitation In Children With Cerebral Palsy
authors: Rajarajan S, Rega Shree B, Kaavyasree Dhasarath, Kokila G, Vedanayagi R, Janaki.R Janaki.R
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Artificial Intelligence for Innovations in Healthcare Industries (ICAIIHI)
abstract: Cerebral palsy (CP) is a lifelong neurodevelopmental disorder characterized by impairments in movement, posture, and balance. This review synthesizes contemporary evidence on two prominent rehabilitative approaches: hippotherapy and structured exercise interventions. Drawing upon two recent systematic reviews and meta-analyses, we analyze clinical outcomes across gross motor function, balance, gait, muscle strength, and spasticity. We examine the neurophysiological mechanisms underpinning hippotherapy’s unique biomechanical stimulus and compare its efficacy against conventional and alternative exercise modalities such as treadmill training, whole-body vibration, robotic-assisted gait, and virtual reality. The synthesis reveals that both hippotherapy and targeted exercise programs significantly improve gross motor function and postural balance, with hippotherapy demonstrating superior effects on trunk control, sitting function, and hip adductor spasticity reduction. While exercise interventions broadly enhance balance indices, they show inconsistent benefits for gait speed and muscle strength. Methodological heterogeneity remains a challenge, yet standardized protocols and certification frameworks are emerging. We propose an integrated model of care where hippotherapy serves as a potent sensory-motor catalyst, complementing structured exercise regimens to maximize functional gains. Future research must prioritize long-term follow-up, direct comparative trials, and standardization of outcome measures to optimize clinical translation.

title: A Modular, Passive Scanning Framework for Efficient Web Vulnerability Enumeration and Comprehensive Security Analysis
authors: Sainath Reddy Akkala, Kora Sai Ruchith, Jasmine Sabeena, Y A Siva Prasad, Chukka Santhaiah
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent and Innovative Technologies in Computing, Electrical and Electronics (IITCEE)
abstract: Organizations around the world continue to face escalating and increasingly complex cyber threats, posing serious risks to digital systems and business continuity. Global cybercrime costs are projected to reach $10.5 trillion annually by 2025, while the average cost of a single data breach has risen to $4.88 million. These statistics highlight the pressing need for advanced security mechanisms that can detect vulnerabilities efficiently and at scale, without disrupting day-to-day operations. In response to this challenge, this study introduces a modular and passive vulnerability scanning framework designed to automatically identify and analyse 47 categories of web application security exposures. Unlike conventional active scanners that rely on intrusive probing, the proposed framework adopts a non-disruptive approach by analysing server responses and client-side components to detect sensitive configuration files, exposed backups, outdated JavaScript libraries, and other critical weaknesses. The underlying scanning engine employs a structured detection logic powered by parallel network operations and precise pattern matching to enhance both accuracy and performance. To validate the system's effectiveness, the framework was tested across 1,000 unique web environments encompassing a wide variety of real-world configurations and deployment scales. The evaluation demonstrated consistent results in identifying both legacy vulnerabilities and previously unreported threat vectors, confirming the framework's operational reliability and scalability. Overall, this solution provides a safe, proactive, and adaptable layer of defense that can significantly enhance the security posture of modern digital infrastructures.

title: Factors influencing the development of a hydrogen economy
authors: Tiran Fernando, Scott Trevean, Thomas Koller
publish: 2025 25th Conference of the Electric Power Supply Industry (CEPSI)
abstract: This paper examines the critical role of hydrogen as an energy carrier in achieving global decarbonization targets, analysing the key factors influencing the development of a sustainable hydrogen economy. It highlights the necessity of long-term offtake agreements for de-risking early projects and underscores the indispensable nature of proactive government support through strategic policy frameworks and financial derisking mechanisms. The analysis further emphasizes the crucial role of fostering international collaboration and knowledge sharing in establishing harmonized standards, facilitating technology transfer, and enabling the development of global supply chains. By synthesizing current research and policy approaches, this paper concludes that a concerted global effort, encompassing robust government intervention and strong international partnerships, is essential to unlock the full potential of hydrogen and accelerate the transition towards a sustainable energy future. Finally, it identifies key areas for future research, including detailed lifecycle emissions analysis, sector-specific techno-economic assessments, public perception studies, evolving international trade dynamics, and the longterm effectiveness of implemented policies.

title: Therapeutic and Diagnostic Applications of Lasers and Emerging Technologies
authors: Prasanna Phutane, Anil Pethe
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Artificial Intelligence for Innovations in Healthcare Industries (ICAIIHI)
abstract: Laser technology has already found its way into a broad range of medical specialties from dermatology to cosmetic surgery. Lasers are amazing tools with a wide range of applications in variety of fields, as we can hardly say any field to have not been applying laser in one form or other. There are numerous and expanding uses for lasers in medicine. The application of lasers to diagnose and cure medical conditions is referred as Laser medicine. It is used in many technologies and instruments. A laser can be described as a device that emits light of a particular wavelength produced due to stimulation of atoms or molecules and then amplifies the radiation, and produces a narrow-focused beam of light. Although it is applied in almost every field form electronic to precision cooking, this review discusses its applications only in the healthcare domain. Its application in ophthalmology, dermatology, surgery, cancer therapy, dental procedures, infection treatments and in diagnostics is discussed with attention to the medical conditions in which laser therapy is applied.

title: Engineering and Neurophysiological Evaluation of Transcranial Direct Current Stimulation versus Sham Stimulation on Coma Recovery and ICU Mobility in Severe Traumatic Brain Injury: A Pilot Study
authors: Yukta Ghugal, Sharath Hullumani, Irshad Qureshi
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Artificial Intelligence for Innovations in Healthcare Industries (ICAIIHI)
abstract: Background: Severe traumatic brain injury [TBI] mostly causes prolonged coma, impaired consciousness, and functional recovery impairment. Conventional rehabilitation alone has limited effects in the intensive care unit [ICU] phase. Transcranial direct current stimulation [tDCS], a non- invasive and safe brain stimulation method, could boast neuroplasticity and enhance recovery results. To compare the effects of active versus sham tDCS, combined with standard ICU physiotherapy, on coma recovery and mobility in severe TBI patients. This single-center, parallel-group, assessor- blinded randomized controlled pilot trial enrolled 20 adults [18–65 years] with severe TBI [GCS ≤ 8, coma > 1 week]. Participants were randomized [1:1] to receive either active anodal tDCS [2 mA, 20 min, 10 sessions over 2 weeks] or sham stimulation, both with standardized ICU physiotherapy. Primary outcomes were change in Coma Recovery Scale–Revised [CRS-R] and ICU Mobility Scale [IMS] from baseline to day 14. Statistical analyses followed intention-to-treat principles. Both groups showed reference significant within-group improvements in CRS-R and IMS over 2 weeks. Active tDCS produced a greater mean CRS-R improvement than sham [+2.81 vs +0.80; between-group difference +2.01, 95% CI 1.48–2.54, p < 0.001]. IMS improvement was larger in the active group [+1.28 vs +0.88] but did not reach statistical significance [p = 0.113]. No serious adverse events were reported, minor and erythema/tingling was observed. The combination of active tDCS and physiotherapy significantly improved coma recovery in Severe TBI patients compare to sham treatment, although the improvement in mobility was positive, but not statically significant. The combination of AI and ML made it possible to precisely assess the efficacy of tDCS, anticipate treatment responses, and interpret data objectively. The larger studies are made possible by this pilot study, which demonstrates the safety and potential of AI enhanced tDCS in ICU neurorehabilitation.

title: Federated and Explainable Ethical Digital Twin Framework for Smart Hospitals: Towards Responsible and Trustworthy AI in Healthcare
authors: Rocky Kumar, Gaurav Kumar, Hemant K Upadhyay, Sudhir Kumar, Tahseen Ahmad Bhat, Manish Kumar
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Artificial Intelligence for Innovations in Healthcare Industries (ICAIIHI)
abstract: Artificial Intelligence (AI) and Digital Twin (DT) technologies are transforming healthcare delivery and management that enable real-time monitoring and predictive diagnosis, as well as personalized treatment. However, the sudden proliferation of AI-driven systems in hospitals raises important ethical challenges involving issues of data privacy, fairness, explainability, and accountability. Many of the existing AI solutions work as non-transparent black-box models and hinder clinician trust in the applicability in sensitive medical contexts. To overcome these challenges, this study introduces a Federated and Explainable Ethical Digital Twin Framework for smart hospitals. The framework combines IoT-enabled data acquisition, Federated Learning (FL) to enable decentralized and privacy-preserving model training, Explainable AI (XAI) to support the interpretability of diagnostic outputs, and blockchain-based audit trails to provide transparency, security, and accountability. The proposed layered architecture is based on implementation of ethical principles of governance - in accordance with standards of International Electrotechnical Commission (IEC) P7000, as well as in accordance with standards of World Health Organization (WHO) - at all stages of data processing and decision making. A case study into the monitoring of ICU patients demonstrates the potential of the system to produce interpretable alerts on the monitoring of patient vitals and important events such as cardiac arrest, with superior predictive accuracy (92%), high clinician interpretability (4.7/5) and conformance to privacy standards that traditional AI models do not. These results demonstrate that responsible AI that is ethically aligned is compatible with high-performance outcomes in health care environments. The framework helps to build the basis of trustworthy, transparent and sustainable digital twin ecosystems, which are fueling a new paradigm of responsible innovation in predictive and preventive medicine.

title: Survey on Mobile Call Setup Using Machine Learning
authors: Chetan D S, Kavya N P, Vishwachetan D, Soumya C S
publish: 2025 World Conference on Cutting-Edge Science and Technology (WCCEST)
abstract: Machine Learning (ML) offers promising tools to improve mobile call setup by predicting network behavior, managing resources dynamically, and minimizing delays. This survey discusses ML applications for call setup, including existing mechanisms, ML methods, real world Placing, and challenges in future research.

title: Evaluating the Effectiveness of the Tale–Tweaking Tool (TTT) in Enhancing Students' Narrative Writing
authors: Noraziah Mohd Amin, Anwar Farhan Mohamad Marzaini, Muhammad Haziq Abd Rashid, Nur Fatin Shahmina Mohd Fauzey, Mohd Firdaus Abdullah, Fasyin Diyana Mohamad Fadzil
publish: 2025 IEEE 14th International Conference on Engineering Education (ICEED)
abstract: The Tale-Tweaking Tool (TTT) is a smartphone app created to improve storytelling skills among first-semester diploma students at Universiti Teknologi MARA (UiTM). Many students used to expository writing struggle with writing a concise 100-word narrative, a key requirement in UiTM's new English curriculum (LCC111). To help with this, TTT offers lectures, related multiple-choice questions, and step-by-step exercises to build narrative writing skills. After using the app, students completed an online survey, which showed that TTT improved their understanding of sentence construction, story sequencing, concise writing, and narrative elements like plot, character, and setting. Overall, TTT is a valuable teaching tool that supports narrative writing and could play a key role in enhancing creative writing instruction more widely.

title: Integrating Carbon Footprint Awareness into an E-Barter Application for Sustainable Consumption in Malaysia
authors: Choo Yen Lee, Gigi Coh Rui Min, Pong Hon Keat, Arsovski Sasa
publish: 2025 International Conference on Advances in Machine Intelligence, and Cybersecurity Technologies (AMICT)
abstract: Human activities are increasingly disrupting natural ecosystems, with climate change driven primarily by greenhouse gas (GHG) emissions posing a critical global challenge. Promoting awareness of individual carbon footprints is essential for fostering sustainable behaviour and informed decision-making. This study investigates the integration of carbon footprint tracking into an E-Barter System to enhance environmental awareness and encourage eco-conscious consumption. A survey of 40 Malaysian respondents was conducted to assess user awareness, usage patterns, expectations, and satisfaction with two existing e-arter platforms. Results indicate limited understanding of carbon footprints, underscoring the need for accessible, user-friendly digital tools that promote sustainable trading practices. The proposed system incorporates carbon tracking, gamification, and educational features to guide users toward reducing emissions and adopting responsible consumption habits. Findings demonstrate the potential of e-barter platforms to combine digital exchange with environmental education, fostering a culture of sustainability within communities.

title: Quantum-Inspired Algorithms for Large-Scale Data Analytics: A Tensor Network Approach
authors: Jasmin Jarsania, Chhelabhai Chavda
publish: 2026 IEEE 16th Annual Computing and Communication Workshop and Conference (CCWC)
abstract: While full-scale quantum computers remain years away from practical deployment, quantum-inspired algorithms executing on classical hardware have emerged as a promising approach for solving computationally intractable problems in large-scale data analytics. This paper presents a comprehensive framework for applying quantum-inspired tensor network algorithms to complex data problems including high-dimensional clustering, feature selection, and recommendation system matrix factorization. We introduce the Quantum-Inspired Algorithm Library (QIAL), a scalable implementation that seamlessly integrates with Apache Spark and other big data frameworks. Through extensive experimentation on datasets ranging from 1 million to <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{1 0 0}$</tex> million records, we demonstrate that our approach achieves 2.6x speedup compared to classical algorithms while maintaining superior accuracy (average improvement of 2.1% across five benchmark datasets) and reduced memory footprint (up to <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{5 2 \%}$</tex> reduction). Our tensor network-based clustering algorithm shows particular promise for NP-hard combinatorial optimization problems that are currently intractable for conventional big data systems. The proposed framework opens new avenues for practical quantum-inspired computing in enterprise-scale data analytics applications.

title: Multi-Modal Financial Data Visualization: A Systematic Review of Integrated Personal Finance Management Interfaces
authors: Anitha N, Vijay D, Vishal Hanamant Pujar, Vishnu P S
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent and Innovative Technologies in Computing, Electrical and Electronics (IITCEE)
abstract: Personal finance management has progressed from fundamental budgeting applications to sophisticated, multi-modal platforms that consolidate various financial data into a singular interface. The paper provides a systematic literature review of assessing the design, algorithms, and user experience of integrated personal finance dashboards. The review underscores three significant deficiencies: the prevalence of fragmented solutions over fully integrated systems, insufficient empirical research on cognitive load in intricate financial visualizations, and a lack of extensive longitudinal analysis of user behavior and engagement. The paper looks at several articles and industry reports to learn more about important algorithmic methods that make it possible to combine data from different fields, such as rule-based categorization, ensemble machine learning, natural language processing, and graph analytics, as well as predictive analytics. The results show that unified dashboards make things more open, usable, automated, and collaborative. However, they increase complexity, protect less privacy, and reduce trust. We propose a nine-domain integration framework which consists of finance-related domains: banking, budgeting, investing, loans, insurance, taxes, stocks, crypto, collaborative finance and advisory. Furthermore, We provide design recommendations and a research roadmap for future personal finance tools.

title: AI Powered Business Growth Assistant to Unlock Hidden Opportunities & Boost Client Engagement Using Gen AI
authors: Ankitha K, Esha Kunder, Shetty Khyathi Rajesh, Vaishnavi N, Moolya Jyothii Rama, Yakkali Anil Kumar
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent and Innovative Technologies in Computing, Electrical and Electronics (IITCEE)
abstract: This paper suggests an AI system for companies to discover opportunities embedded in customer communication, including emails and meeting videos. For matching a company catalog of services with customer requirements, the system utilizes tools such as Open AI's Whisper for audio transcription, Llama for summarizing, and semantic similarity methods. Since we are under confidentiality, we have simulated datasets similar to the real-world interactions. The transcription accuracy test, tool choice, and literature review have all been done to date. Outcomes are the aim of the system's intuitive dashboard, secure storage, approval processes, and recommendation of the relevant services to the client based on the company service catalog. This article describes our approach, process, and vision for such a system could improve business results and speed up decision making.

title: Intelligent Network Forensics with Transformer Models and ECDSA-Based Evidence Integrity
authors: B Abisha, V Sheeja Kumari
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent and Innovative Technologies in Computing, Electrical and Electronics (IITCEE)
abstract: The accelerated emergence of cybercrime demands intelligent network forensic models that have the capabilities of identifying threats on-the-fly and organizing evidence. His classic forensic models can easily experience scalability, automation, data integrity. The study presents a system of AI-assisted forensic using RoBERTa and GPT in favor of the Elliptic Curve Digital Signature Algorithm (ECDSA) to provide both anomaly detection and an additional feature of evidences, which cannot be tampered with. RoBERTa is capable of enticing the context information of the traffic on the network in order to create an outstanding score of anomaly, and GPT is able to produce easy-to-understand forensic reports which can be fed through an investigator chain attacker procedure to the MITRE ATT&CK warning framework to decrease the working to do load. The ECDSA is a process of proving all the artifacts in an immutable fashion that offers forensic reliability and their admissibility as a legal authority source of data. Both validation and the UNSW-NB15 test outcomes were better than minimum-level methods, with the accuracy and precision of 94.2 and 93.8 and a recall of 95.1 and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$F 1$</tex>-score of 94.4, respectively, this showed superiority of the results to those of baseline methods. Anomaly detection realized with the help of AI and automated reporting and cryptographic controls turn this framework into one step toward scalable and easily intelligible, legally relevant network forensic systems.

title: Preserving the Evidence: Artifact-Aware Preprocessing for Robust Deepfake Detection
authors: Andrew Alfonso Lie, Rendy Susanto, Gabriel Mackenzie, Abram Setyo Prabowo
publish: 2025 2nd Beyond Technology Summit on Informatics International Conference (BTS-I2C)
abstract: The current problem in the world of deepfakes faces the challenge of a 700% increase in fraud cases in the fintech sector (2023) with projected losses reaching $40 billion by 2027. Although intensive research focuses on model architecture, the preprocessing stage, which is fundamental and crucial in the preservation of manipulation artifacts, is still under-explored. This study presents a systematic comparative analysis of preprocessing techniques for deepfake detection using EfficientNetV2-S as the backbone on the Celeb-DF v2 dataset (5,639 videos, 1:1 real-to-fake ratio). We evaluate two scenarios: (1) baseline preprocessing with BlazeFace face detection, photometric normalization, and light augmentation; (2) adaptive multi-scale enhancement that integrates gamma correction, JPEG compression simulation, adaptive sharpening-blur (CPU-side), with Symlet-4 level-3 wavelet decomposition and spatial attention fusion (GPU-side). The experimental results show significant improvements: at the frame level, AUROC increased from 99.07% to 99.62% with a 59.2% reduction in false positives (240→98 cases); at the video level, the proposed method achieved perfect precision of 100% on the Celeb-DF v2 test set (zero false positives out of 83 real videos). This result is benchmark-specific and does not imply universal perfect precision, AUROC of 99.96%, and Equal Error Rate of 0.60%. Error analysis identified that detection failures mainly occurred under extreme lighting conditions and high-quality deepfakes with adversarial post-processing. This research advances the field through: (1) a systematic evaluation framework that rigorously quantifies the impact of preprocessing, (2) an adaptive multi-scale pipeline that preserves and amplifies forensic artifacts without introducing additional backbone parameters, and (3) empirical evidence that preprocessing significantly elevates deepfake detection performance. The study is limited by its evaluation on a single dataset and its use of empirically determined fusion weights, which motivate future work on cross-dataset validation, temporal modeling, and learnable fusion mechanisms.

title: Summarize-Pro: A Transformer-Based Framework for Multimodal and Multilingual Content Summarization
authors: Madhuri J, Akash Mishra, Amit Rathod, Diksha, Harsh Sagar Gupta
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent and Innovative Technologies in Computing, Electrical and Electronics (IITCEE)
abstract: The rapid expansion of digital content has created a strong need for efficient summarization systems. Summarize-Pro is a multimodal and multilingual platform that supports multiple input formats, including text, documents (PDF, DOCX, TXT), web URLs, and YouTube videos—transcribed using OpenAI Whisper. The framework leverages transformer-based models optimized for different input types: BART-Large-CNN for documents, Pegasus-XSUM for web content, Long-T5-TGlobal for long transcripts, and mBART-Large-50 for multilingual summarization. It also integrates auxiliary modules such as KeyBERT for keyword extraction, BERTopic for topic modeling, RoBERTa-SQuAD2 for interactive question answering, and gTTS for generating audio summaries. Users can further customize summary style and prompts. Natural Language Processing (NLP) techniques are combined with multimodal and multilingual capabilities, Summarize-Pro produces concise, contextually rich, and accessible summaries that enhance comprehension and user engagement across diverse digital content sources. Evaluations are done using ROUGE metrics which demonstrate high coherence making Summarize-Pro a scalable, user-centric tool for global content understanding.

title: Cumulant Generating Function Classifier for Non-Gaussian Data
authors: Kaan Icer, Kaushallya Adhikari, Steven Kay
publish: 2025 International Conference on Electrical and Computer Engineering Researches (ICECER)
abstract: The cumulant generating function classifier (CGFC) is a non-parametric classifier that can handle noisy data effectively, even under training data-deficient conditions. This work investigates the effectiveness of the CGFC for non-Gaussian data, although the CGFC mollifies the empirical training data using a Gaussian kernel. Experimental results show that the CGFC achieves near-optimal performance under white Gaussian noise (WGN) and Laplacian noise. Further analysis of the performance optimality shows that the Kullback-Leibler divergence (KLD) between the true and the mollified empirical probability density function (PDF) for these distributions remains sufficiently low. This indicates that the tilted PDF used in CGFC represents the underlying distribution well for the classification task. Additionally, a linear relationship between the variance of the mollifying kernel and the variance of the noise was identified.

title: Hybrid Edge AI and Augmented Reality System for Offline Herb Identification in Low-Connectivity Supply Chains
authors: William Aparecido Celestino Lopes, Samira Nascimento Antunes, Sergio Miele Ruggero, Marcelo Tsuguio Okano
publish: 2026 IEEE 16th Annual Computing and Communication Workshop and Conference (CCWC)
abstract: This paper presents a comparative case study of two computer-vision artifacts designed for accurate herb identification in low-connectivity supply-chain environments. The first system employs a YOLOv8 deep-learning model optimized for Edge AI deployment, achieving <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">${\geqslant} \mathbf{0. 7 0}$</tex> precision and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$<35 ~\text{ms}$</tex> inference latency on a mobile chipset. The second artifact, ARomaticLens, is an offline augmented-reality (AR) mobile application capable of recognizing 18 pre-registered species with 100 % accuracy using marker-based vision. Both solutions were evaluated in CEAGESP, the largest food distribution center in South America, under real-world lighting and connectivity constraints. Results reveal complementary functionalities: YOLOv8 enables automated, real-time inspection, while ARomaticLens enhances user interaction and training. Together, they demonstrate the feasibility of a conceptual hybrid AI+AR approach, in which automated edge-based detection and offline augmented reality can complement each other for decision support and training in agri-food logistics. The study contributes to ongoing research in edge computing, computer vision, and wireless communication for intelligent systems.

title: Decision letter for "Descriptor: Digital Dataset of Animal Tissues for Macroscopic Pathology in Cancer: Image and Ultrasound Data (DATP-IU)"
authors: N/A

title: IoT-Enabled MQTT Control System for BLDC Motor Speed Regulation Using Fuzzy-PID
authors: Henry Widodo, Afif Widaryanto, Sigit Arianto, Fajar Adi Marianto
publish: 2025 2nd Beyond Technology Summit on Informatics International Conference (BTS-I2C)
abstract: This paper presents the design and implementation of an intelligent control system for Brushless DC (BLDC) motors by integrating a Fuzzy-PID controller with an Internet of Things (IoT) monitoring and data management framework. The control system combines the stability of a traditional PID controller with the adaptive gain-tuning capability of Fuzzy Logic to improve robustness against nonlinearities and external disturbances in motor dynamics. The IoT architecture employs the MQTT protocol and the Node-Red platform to provide real-time data acquisition, visualization, and remote monitoring of motor performance. Experimental results demonstrate that the proposed Fuzzy-PID controller achieves improved adaptability and disturbance rejection compared to conventional PID control, while maintaining competitive transient performance. Furthermore, the MQTT-based IoT system achieves a low communication latency of approximately 0.04217 seconds, ensuring real-time responsiveness. System performance is evaluated using integral error indices (IAE, ISE, ITAE, and ITSE), confirming the potential of the Fuzzy-PID approach for efficient and intelligent BLDC motor control in IoT-based applications.

title: RFE-SMOTEENN-RF: An Anomaly Detection Model Used in IoT Networks
authors: Satyendra Singh Rawat, Vikas Thada, Amit Kumar Mishra, Anand Jawdekar, Vivek Katiyar, Anupama Mishra
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Internet of Things: Smart Innovation and Usages (IoT-SIU)
abstract: The increasing popularity of Internet of Thinks devices in real-life services has made them an attractive target for cyberattacks, requiring the use of reliable and effective intrusion detection systems (IDS). IoT network research is currently faced with three main challenges: high-class imbalance, classification complexity, and computational overhead. Our suggested RFE-SMOTEENN-RF approach addresses these issues by combining a Random Forest classifier, SMOTE-ENN, and Recursive Feature Elimination (RFE). SMOTE-ENN helps balance the training data and increases the detection of rare attack types by generating synthetic samples for those less common classes. RFE progressively removes the least significant features from the dataset until just the needed ones are left. Random Forest was chosen because it can handle multiclass problems, recognize feature importance, and be effective in high-dimensional situations while offering an appropriate balance between accuracy and computational cost. On the IoTNet24 dataset, the results show that our method performed exceptionally well, achieving 99.99% accuracy, 99.95% precision, 100% recall, and a $99.97 \% \mathrm{~F} 1$-score. It is performed well on other datasets; RT-IoT2022 obtained 98.21% accuracy, 87.46% precision, $87.50 \%$ recall, and an $87.11 \%$ F1-score, while CSE-CICIDS2018 scored above $98.50 \%$ on all parameters.

title: Next-Generation Stress Recognition through Intelligent Machine Learning on Physiological Data
authors: Karuna Yadav, Virendra Kumar Swarnkar
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Artificial Intelligence for Innovations in Healthcare Industries (ICAIIHI)
abstract: Stress is a major physiological and psychological state that affects human beings negatively in terms of health, cognitive functions and general well-being. As the requirement of the dependable and automatic nature of stress monitoring systems is on the rise, the incorporation of physiological data with more efficient machine learning algorithms is becoming an inspiring alternative. This study suggests a model that can be implemented to recognize stress in the next generation, based on physiological information, including heart rate variability, electrodermal activity, and respiration. The major goal will be to create a smart machine learning model that will be able to detect the stress states and facilitate proactive interventions. The methodology encompasses selections of preprocessing and feature extraction procedures of raw physiological data and then utilising pattern-recognition algorithms such as Support Vector Machines, Random Forests and neural networks like Convolutional Neural Network (CNN) and Long Short-Time Memory (LSTM). Rigorous hyperparameter optimization is performed using Particle Swarm Optimization (PSO) and improves the accuracy of the prediction system. To assess the measure of accuracy, precision, recall and F1-score, all the models are trained and validated on a benchmark physiological dataset. An experimental study conducted shows that a CNN-LSTM model optimized with PSO is more effective in identifying the pattern than the classical machine learning classifiers and presents higher accuracy and stability under different conditions. Not only the proposed framework has demonstrated extreme reliability in stress level detection but also has presented the effective generalization when tested on unseen physiological data. The work would be of importance to advance the field of intelligent stress monitoring systems as it involves physiological sensing in combination with cutting edge machine learning. The results have potential to be applied in the areas of healthcare, work place efficiency, individual wellbeing, and human computer interaction. Future research will be conducted to real-time implementation of the proposed system onto wearable devices to deliver continuous and unobtrusive stress monitoring and adaptive stress reduction interventions.

title: A Low-Latency Burst-Mode FIFO with Look-Ahead Prefetching for AI Accelerators
authors: Anushka Ganguly, Arindam Chakraborty
publish: 2025 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence for Computing, Astronomy and Renewable Energy (AICARE)
abstract: Efficient memory access is one of the most important aspects for AI accelerators that operate through frequent weight streaming. The conventional FIFO architectures experience high latency due to single-word transactions, resulting in stall cycles and inefficient data movement. Although existing burst-mode FIFOs have been observed to have improved throughput, the difficulties related to idle cycles in between still need to be addressed. In this work, we propose a low-latency FIFO with a look-ahead prefetching mechanism to ensure continuous weight availability and reduced stall cycles for faster computational speed in AI applications.

title: Explainable AI-as-a-Service (XAIaaS) for Fraud Detection in Cloud-Based Financial Transaction Systems
authors: Mukul Mangla, Tejaskumar Pujari, Geol Gladson Battu, Keshav Kaushik, Renu Kumawat, Mukesh Soni
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Internet of Things: Smart Innovation and Usages (IoT-SIU)
abstract: This study presents an Explainable AI-as-a-Service (XAIaaS) architecture for detecting fraud in cloud-based financial transaction systems. The framework combines machine learning and SHAP-based explanation to provide accurate identification and interpretability. Low latency is essential for real-time deployment with a cloud-native microservice architecture for scalability. Comprehensive evaluations show improved precision, recall, and user trust in a realistic scenario with minimal computing expense. Comparing the integrated XAI methodology to baseline models and other explanation approaches demonstrates its effectiveness. Drift detection and cost-performance optimization are included into the framework, making it suitable for high-regulated volume activities. This work advances trustworthy, transparent AI for financial cybersecurity.

title: Development of a Scaled Autonomous Vehicle with LiDAR, IMU, and Vision-Based Deep Learning Control
authors: Marcos A. J. Coelho, João M. Neto, Matheus D. de Costa, Gabriela R. Roque, Roderval Marcelino
publish: 2025 International Conference on Electrical and Computer Engineering Researches (ICECER)
abstract: Autonomous vehicles have the potential to improve traffic safety, reduce accidents, and enhance urban mobility. This work presents the development of a 1:10 scale autonomous vehicle prototype integrating Light Detection and Ranging (LiDAR), an Inertial Measurement Unit (IMU), and a monocular camera, with perception and control implemented using deep learning models in the Keras framework. A dataset of 11,560 images, synchronized with LiDAR and IMU readings, was collected on a marked indoor track to train and evaluate three models: two linear neural networks and one Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM) recurrent network. The LSTM achieved the lowest validation loss (0.05885 %), outperforming linear models (0.1872 %), at the cost of a 5.8× longer training time. Experimental results highlight the trade-off between model complexity, accuracy, and computational cost. Implementation challenges included integration of the Intel RealSense D435 camera with Raspberry Pi hardware and the need for an optimized power supply to ensure system stability. The proposed platform provides a cost-effective and reproducible testbed for autonomous navigation research, enabling future extensions to full-scale vehicle applications.

title: Evolution of YOLO Models and a Comparative Evaluation for Pedestrian Detection
authors: Ma Althea Retig, Fakeha S. Ansari, Maitha Almarri, Suleiman Y. Yerima
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Metaverse, and Cybersecurity (ICAMAC)
abstract: With an increasing reliance on personal vehicles globally, traffic volumes have risen, resulting in more trafficrelated injuries and fatalities. Robust, real-time detection of pedestrians and road objects is essential for collision avoidance and safety-critical systems. The evaluation of contemporary deep learning-based models designed to enable machine vision based object detection is currently a topic of interest to researchers in various domains. Therefore, this study explores the application of YOLO (You Only Look Once) models for real-time obstacle detection to enhance road safety. We review various YOLO model variants, including YOLOv4-tiny, YOLOv5, YOLOv8, YOLOv9, YOLOv10, and YOLOvll. We implemented the models and conducted experiments to evaluate their performance using metrics such as mean Average Precision (mAP), and bounding box loss. The results indicate that YOLOv11 outperforms other models with a mAP of 0.68 with constricted resources, demonstrating its robustness and precision for pedestrian detection in traffic environments. This study underscores the importance of dataset diversity and model generalizability in developing effective real-time traffic safety applications.

title: Intent-Aware Clickstream Analytics with Explainable AI: A Scalable Data Engineering Framework
authors: Vivek Venkatesan, Balaji Sundareshan, Deepak Barla, Chakkaravarthy Arunachalam
publish: 2026 IEEE 16th Annual Computing and Communication Workshop and Conference (CCWC)
abstract: Modern clickstream analytics demands low-latency and interpretable data processing pipelines capable of identifying not only what occurred during a session but also why. This paper presents a scalable data engineering framework that integrates behavioral telemetry, rule-based heuristics, and explainable artificial intelligence (XAI) to infer user intent and generate interpretable behavioral narratives. The proposed architecture ingests simulated or real-world clickstream data through modular and low-code pipelines that combine event normalization, behavioral feature extraction, and lightweight classification with large language model (LLM) driven explanation layers. The result is an end-to-end workflow that transforms raw telemetry into context-aware insights while maintaining transparency and reproducibility across open-source and serverless environments. Empirical evaluations on <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{1 0, 0 0 0}$</tex> simulated sessions show that the hybrid rule and the LLM approach improve interpretability scores by 12 % and reduce false positives by 18 % compared to baselines based on rules. The pipeline achieves an average inference latency below 600 ms per session. Qualitative feedback from pilot users highlights improved reasoning about behavioral patterns and greater confidence in AI-assisted interpretation. By unifying scalable data engineering and explainable AI, this work advances the design of intent-aware clickstream analytics systems that are modular, cost-efficient, and adaptable to both research and operational contexts. The framework bridges the gap between data infrastructure engineering and human-understandable behavioral intelligence.

title: MINDtrack: A Conversational Interface with an AI-Powered Adaptive Human State Recognition System
authors: Meghana Sai P, Naveensri D, Reeba Rose L
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent and Innovative Technologies in Computing, Electrical and Electronics (IITCEE)
abstract: The fast advancement in digital communication has augmented the requirement of the availability of the emotionally intelligent computational framework to be capable of perceiving the diversity of expression that individuals demonstrate in their emotional readings and reactions. The limitation of traditional conversational agent is that they are dependent on a restricted number of channels of input that are either text or speech based and as such this constrained ability to investigate the multidimensional nature of affective experience. Driven by such disadvantages, we introduce MindTrack: A Multimodal Framework of Human State Recognition with Conversational Integration with artificial intelligence (AI). Emotion Inference Architecture: A mixture of computer vision, acoustic modeling and natural language understanding to provide correct emotion inference. Swin Transformer can help to perceive finer details of facial gestures, Wav2Vec 2.0 can gain an idea of paralanguage based on the human voice, and, DeBERTa Transformer enables sentiment analysis to be performed in language streams. A Gated Multimodal Unit (GMU) is an emotion synthesizer that adapts the strength of each modality depending on the confidence of the situation. Upon the detection of the affective states, an ideal DialoGPT conversational generator is used to come up with empathetic, personalized, and context-sensitive dialogue responses. Moreover, the interpretability and trust are enhanced by having a real-time visualization interface that is based on interaction. That opens the possibilities of numerous potential applications such as psychosocial support, online learning, employment and customer interactions. A multimodal intelligence and adaptive dialogue synergy provides a groundbreaking foundation of the emotional responsiveness in digital communication.

title: Recent Development And Advancements In Asset Management Systems Enable Power Utilities In Addressing Challenges
authors: Kang Seng Seow, Sungin Cho
publish: 2025 25th Conference of the Electric Power Supply Industry (CEPSI)
abstract: Key challenges and risks in power utilities nowadays include ever-evolving industry regulations and emerging requirements related to climate adaptation measures, aging assets and infrastructure, and overall sustainability. In addition, decarbonization initiatives and critical infrastructures across various other sectors have significantly increased the value of power generation and electrical networks due to the growing dependency on reliable and resilient power infrastructure. As a result, robust asset management system for power utility assets is becoming increasingly critical in addressing these challenges. The primary outcomes of effective asset management system are the realization of value and the achievement of organizational objectives, which involve providing assurance that asset management decisions are being made based on appropriate decision criteria for resource allocation, enabling organizations to adapt more rapidly and effectively to internal and external changes, and promoting long-term planning, considering future impacts, risks, uncertainties and opportunities. This paper investigates the development and advancements in key activities of Asset Management System, particularly those related to Strategic Asset Management Plan development, decision-making and data asset management. These development and advancements, especially those outlined under the recently published ISO 55000 series of standards, enable power utilities to enhance planning processes and make more balanced asset management decisions when addressing potential risks and opportunities.

title: CT Scan-Based COVID-19 Detection Using Feature Extraction and Stack-Based Ensemble Techniques
authors: Yekkanti Lakshmi, Sourav Mondal, Debnarayan Khatua, Pathan Nayeem Khan, Harendra Kumar, Gorla Siva Rama Krishna
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Artificial Intelligence for Innovations in Healthcare Industries (ICAIIHI)
abstract: During the COVID-19 pandemic, rapid and precise diagnostic tools are urgently required to enable early diagnosis and mitigate the load on healthcare systems. The current work investigates whether Artificial Neural Networks (ANN) and other machine learning classifiers can identify COVID-19 from CT scan images. The method involves preprocessing CT images and extracting deep features using pretrained convolutional architectures like ResNet50 and InceptionV3. These characteristics are subsequently categorized using Support Vector Machine (SVM) and then tested with boosting algorithms, including XGBoost, Light- GBM, CatBoost, Gradient Boosting, and AdaBoost. To improve the performance, a stack-based ensemble method is employed with ANN as the meta-learner. The experimental outcomes show that the ensemble method performs better than stand alone models, with increased accuracy and fewer false positives. These results demonstrate the power of leveraging ensemble techniques and deep learning for effective and accurate COVID-19 detection.

title: Wi-Proctor: A Privacy-Preserving Examination Proctoring Environment Using Device-Free WiFi Sensing
authors: Saksham Midha, Chris Rodrigues, Vitthal S. Gutte
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent and Innovative Technologies in Computing, Electrical and Electronics (IITCEE)
abstract: The surge in online and hybrid education has intensified scrutiny of academic integrity, as traditional exam supervision methods are not only ineffective in curbing miscon- duct but also raise significant privacy concerns. Current AI-based proctoring solutions using webcams and facial recognition are increasingly criticized for being invasive, potentially discrimina- tory, and psychologically taxing on students. This paper proposes Wi-Proctor, a privacy-preserving exam proctoring solution that leverages WiFi Channel State Information (CSI) for device-free sensing. Rather than monitoring students directly, Wi-Proctor observes the examination environment to detect unauthorized activities without storing any personally identifiable information. The system employs a novel spatio-temporal deep learning architecture combining Convolutional Neural Networks (CNNs), Recurrent Neural Networks (RNNs), and attention mechanisms to analyze sanitized CSI data streams. Our experimental evaluation demonstrates that Wi-Proctor achieves 92% accuracy in detecting suspicious activities with a false positive rate below 5%, while maintaining real-time performance with detection latency under 2 seconds. To facilitate further research, we introduce the Proctoring Activity Dataset (PAD-CSI), the first publicly available benchmark for WiFi-based examination proctoring.

title: NPK and Water Quality Monitoring System with Crop Suggestion
authors: M.S. Saranya, M. Jaswanth, M. Lokesh, M. Venkata Sai Kumar Reddy
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent and Innovative Technologies in Computing, Electrical and Electronics (IITCEE)
abstract: Tamil Nadu, being a coastal state, faces uncertain conditions in agriculture, which leads to lower production. Even though there are more people and land, the output isn't increasing as it should. In the past, farmers used to share knowledge and experience with each other, but now this is not enough because of changing weather conditions. Different factors and elements in agriculture provide important information that helps understand farm problems. The growth of the IT industry has brought some important developments in agricultural science, giving farmers better information to help them grow crops more effectively. Using modern technology wisely in farming is important today. Machine learning helps create clear models from data and provides predictions. Issues like soil nutrients, water quality, predicting which crops will grow well, when to plant, how much water and fertilizer to use, and how to protect crops can all be addressed. Because of changing weather, there is a need for better methods to help farmers grow crops and manage their farms more efficiently. This can help future farmers improve their farming. A recommendation system can be used to suggest which crops to grow based on local conditions and needs. By using data mining, we can find useful information from agricultural data. A crop dataset has been studied, and crops are recommended based on how productive they are and which season they fit best.

title: Multimodal Deep Learning Framework for Early Detection of Depression Using EEG and Speech Biomarkers
authors: Premnath K., S. Pandiaraj
publish: 2025 World Conference on Cutting-Edge Science and Technology (WCCEST)
abstract: Depression is among the most prevalent mental health issues in the world, being underdiagnosed in the majority of cases because of the subjectiveness of assessment procedures and the inability to take clinical action. This paper presents the concept of a multimodal deep learning model that will combine both the electroencephalogram (EEG) and speech biomarkers to allow the prompt and precise identification of depressive conditions. EEG modality is recorded to capture the patterns of neural activity, and speech aspects, such as prosodic, spectral and linguistic cues are used to capture the existence of minor emotional and cognitive changes. Each of the modalities uses a hybrid architecture (Convolutional Neural Networks (CNNs) spatial feature extraction and Bidirectional Long Short-Term Memory (BiLSTM) networks temporal sequence modelling). The model includes a cross-attention fusion mechanism that is able to align and integrate modality specific embeddings, increasing the ability to serve interdependent neurophysiological and behavioural clues. The proposed multimodal framework in cross-validation experiments reached an AUROC of 0.931 +-0.012, accuracy of 89.6% +- 1.8, and F1-score of 0.927 +- 0.017, which showed its strength and balance across folds and resistance to noise and unimodal dropout. Moreover, SHAP-based explainability also offers interpretable results on the most significant EEG channels and speech characteristics, which facilitates clinical interpretation and trust. The potential of the framework to be scaled up and provide privacy, as well as its potential to be used in wearable neuro-monitors and telehealth, makes it one of the promising solutions to be deployed in the wearable neuro-monitoring and telehealth settings, allowing the proactive mental health screening and individualized intervention plans.

title: Towards Trustworthy Vital Sign Forecasting: Leveraging Uncertainty for Prediction Intervals
authors: Li Rong Wang, Thomas C. Henderson, Yew Soon Ong, Yih Yng Ng, Xiuyi Fan
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Data Mining (ICDM)
abstract: Vital signs, such as heart rate and blood pressure, are critical indicators of patient health and are widely used in clinical monitoring and decision-making. While deep learning models have shown promise in forecasting these signals, their deployment in healthcare remains limited in part because clinicians must be able to trust and interpret model outputs. Without reliable uncertainty quantification - particularly calibrated prediction intervals (PIs) - it is unclear whether a forecasted abnormality constitutes a meaningful warning or merely reflects model noise, hindering clinical decision-making. To address this, we present two methods for deriving PIs from the Reconstruction Uncertainty Estimate (RUE), an uncertainty measure well-suited to vital-sign forecasting due to its sensitivity to data shifts and support for label-free calibration. Our parametric approach assumes that prediction errors and uncertainty estimates follow a Gaussian copula distribution, enabling closed-form PI computation. Our non-parametric approach, based on k-nearest neighbours (KNN), empirically estimates the conditional error distribution using similar validation instances. We evaluate these methods on two large public datasets with minute- and hour-level sampling, representing high- and low-frequency health signals. Experiments demonstrate that the Gaussian copula method consistently outperforms conformal prediction baselines on low-frequency data, while the KNN approach performs best on high-frequency data. These results underscore the clinical promise of RUE-derived PIs for delivering interpretable, uncertainty-aware vital sign forecasts.

title: Fast Sampling for Privacy-Preserving Lazy Multiplicative Weight Update
authors: Xiaoyu Li, Zhao Song, Jiale Zhao
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Data Mining (ICDM)
abstract: The multiplicative weight update method is a well-known algorithm commonly used for decision making based on recommendations from n experts. Despite being widely used, the classical MWU algorithm suffers from a linear runtime complexity, which limits its efficiency on large problems. Additionally, since expert data may be sensitive, it is important to develop privacy-preserving methods. In this paper, we propose the first data structure that approximates the multiplicative weight update in sublinear time (o(n)) while providing differential privacy guarantees, significantly improving efficiency for large-scale problems. Our data structure both enhances computational efficiency and ensures privacy protection, with theoretical guarantees on both utility and privacy, enabling fast and private decision making.

title: Large Language Model Deployment on Resource-Constrained Edge Devices: A Practitioner's Survey
authors: Rahul Maliakkal, Yashasvi Makin, Plawan Rath, Rohit Jain, Ankur Sadhoo
publish: 2026 IEEE 16th Annual Computing and Communication Workshop and Conference (CCWC)
abstract: Large Language Models (LLMs) have transformed many areas of artificial intelligence (AI), but their substantial resource requirements limit their deployment on mobile and edge devices. Although LLMs have demonstrated impressive capabilities in various applications, the next challenge is deploying these models on resource-limited edge devices. Recent innovations in software optimizations and specialized hardware have made it feasible to deploy LLMs on edge devices. Edge deployment offers benefits including local data processing, cost savings, reduced latency, and personalized responses. This paper reviews the latest techniques that make the deployment of LLMs on edge devices feasible and provides actionable, constraint-driven guidelines for practical edge deployment.

title: High Rank Matrix Completion with Adaptive Neighbor Graphs
authors: Shiqin Wang, Kun Xie, Jiazheng Tian, Jigang Wen, Gaogang Xie
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Data Mining (ICDM)
abstract: Matrix completion is a method for imputing missing data, typically based on the assumption of a low-rank structure. However, in practice, various factors can obscure this structure, leading to a perceived high-rank nature. Recently, a few studies begin to address high-rank data completion, their methods rely on specific hypothesis distributions that are often impractical to validate in real-world scenarios. Therefore, in this paper, we tackle high-rank data completion without any hypothesis distribution. Firstly, we theoretically demonstrate that the Radial Basis Function (RBF) kernel feature mapping has the capability to transform general high-rank data into low-rank data in a higher-dimensional feature space. Secondly, combining with the adaptive row and column neighbor information, a novel graph-based high-rank matrix completion algorithm is developed. Thirdly, experiments on four real datasets across three domains demonstrate that the proposed method reduces reconstruction error by up to 63% compared to the second-best method at the same missing rate. Furthermore, to achieve similar accuracy, it requires up to 40% fewer samples. Our code is released at https://github.com/shiqinyeah/Graph-HRMC.git.

title: Statistical approach to the green energy transition in electricity production
authors: Rubén González González, Jose Guillermo Rosas Mayoral, Xiomar Gómez Barrios
publish: 2025 International Conference on Electrical and Computer Engineering Researches (ICECER)
abstract: A key goal of the European Union is to reach climate neutrality by 2050; to achieve this, decarbonizing the electricity sector will be essential. This work aims to carry out a statistical approximation of what should be the evolution of the electricity sector in Spain in terms of renewable energy production and the performance of an energy storage system so that it is possible to decouple the production of electricity from the use of renewable sources. A statistical study has been conducted to assess the need for increasing renewable energy production to replace electricity generated by CO<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</inf>-emitting technologies. The study also explores the relationship between this increased production and the roundtrip efficiency of energy storage systems that enable the effective use of electricity derived from renewables. Data from Red Eléctrica Española for the period between 2019 and 2024 were used, classifying them by months and generating a typical day for each month of the year. The main result of the study is that by doubling renewable energy production and having a seasonal energy storage system with a general roundtrip efficiency (RTE) of 50%, replacing the electrical energy generated from fossil resources with energy from renewable sources would be statistically possible.

title: Indonesian Named Entity Recognition (NER) Using IndoBERT, BiLSTM, and CRF Models
authors: Ginanjar Abdurrahman, Taufiq Timur Warisaji, Agung Nilogiri, Ari Eko Wardoyo, Budi Satria Bakti, Reni Umilasari, Yusril Izzi Arlisa Amiri, Nanda Kurnia Wardati, Zainul Arifin
publish: 2025 2nd Beyond Technology Summit on Informatics International Conference (BTS-I2C)
abstract: Natural Language Processing (NLP) plays a crucial role in the development of text-based artificial intelligence systems, particularly in Named Entity Recognition (NER) tasks. The main challenges of Indonesian NER lie in morphological complexity, semantic ambiguity, and the limited availability of labeled corpus and linguistic resources. This study proposes a hybrid architecture of IndoBERT–BiLSTM–CRF to improve the accuracy and consistency of entity label prediction. IndoBERT generates contextual embeddings, BiLSTM strengthens bidirectional dependencies between tokens, and CRF optimizes label transitions globally. The dataset consists of 262,477 tokens with 15 entity types, divided into 80% training, 10% validation, and 10% test data. Training was conducted for 20 epochs using the AdamW optimizer and the negative log-likelihood loss function CRF, with evaluation based on Precision, Recall, and F1-Score. The training results showed a significant decrease in loss from 19.59 to –1.05 and a sharp increase in F1-score from 0.487 to 0.503 in the first six epochs before reaching a plateau until the end of training. The final evaluation using unseen data showed an average F1-score of 0.50 (micro/weighted), consistent with the training and validation results, indicating that the model did not experience overfitting. High-frequency entities such as Event achieved F1-score=0.96, while minor entities such as Quantity and Region were not detected. These findings demonstrate that the multilevel approach based on IndoBERT–BiLSTM–CRF is capable of producing more stable, adaptive, and contextual Indonesian NER performance compared to a single transformer-based model and contributes to strengthening the Indonesian NLP research ecosystem.

title: Intelligent Talent Analytics: A Machine Learning-Based Framework for Predicting Employee Attrition and Workforce Productivity in Hybrid Work Environments
authors: Amit Verma, Nutan Karnik, Priya Bennett
publish: 2025 World Conference on Cutting-Edge Science and Technology (WCCEST)
abstract: With the rise of hybrid work environments, there's a whole new layer of complexity wrapped around managing the workforce, both in terms of predicting employee churn and language indicators of productivity. To do so, this paper introduces a framework to the ITA based on machine learning, and more precisely, the Random Forest algorithm. The model was trained on a curated HR data set that includes demographic, behavioral, and hybrid-specific variables, including the frequency of remote work and collaboration activity. Experimental results indicate that Random Forest performed best, 88% in accuracy, 0.86 in F1-score and 0.91 in AUC, especially comparing with the common logistic regression. Analysis of feature importance revealed that role, perception in overtime hours and remote engagement were the most important features to predict attrition. Productivity was forecasted on normalized KPIs, including the time-to-delivery of projects, attendance, and manager scores. The framework facilitates integration of ethical AI with the help of these interpretability and fairness-aware modeling approaches, especially SMOTE-based balancing. A process plan for its implementation is developed, thus enabling practical usage inside HR systems. The present study enriches the HR analytics literature in that it applies predictive modeling to the context of hybrid work and presents scalable evidence for strategic workforce planning.

title: Optimization of Multi-Layer LEACH in Wireless Sensor Networks using Genetic Algorithm
authors: Sam Lie Lie, Florence Sia, Salmah Fattah
publish: 2025 International Conference on Advances in Machine Intelligence, and Cybersecurity Technologies (AMICT)
abstract: Wireless Sensor Networks (WSNs) face critical energy constraints that limit network lifetime and data reliability. The Low-Energy Adaptive Clustering Hierarchy (LEACH) protocol and its multi-layer variant (ML-LEACH) improve scalability through clustering and hierarchical routing, but both suffer from probabilistic Cluster Head (CH) selection that neglects residual energy and node distribution. This paper addresses the problem by integrating a Genetic Algorithm (GA) into ML-LEACH to optimize CH selection using a fitness function based on residual energy, intra-layer density, and inter-layer communication cost. Simulations conducted in MATLAB with 100 nodes over a 100 m × 100 m area show that GA-ML-LEACH reduces energy consumption, extends network lifetime, and achieves higher cumulative throughput by sustaining data delivery for longer rounds. These results demonstrate that layer-aware GA optimization enhances ML-LEACH performance and provide a foundation for future hybrid evolutionary approaches in WSN optimization.

title: Real-Time Email Security Through Proxy-Integrated DistilBERT DLP Framework
authors: Likitha R, Manohar P, Alkama Eqbal, Ankit Sharma, Yash Maurya
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent and Innovative Technologies in Computing, Electrical and Electronics (IITCEE)
abstract: Data Loss Prevention (DLP) is a crucial security strategy that protects sensitive information from unauthorized access or leakage. Traditional DLP systems, which use static rules and keyword matching, are often ineffective against modern, complex threats and can produce many false positives [1]. To overcome these challenges, this project proposes an advanced Machine Learning (ML)-based DLP solution. Our approach leverages a fine-tuned DistilBERT model, a lightweight yet powerful version of BERT, for its speed, accuracy, and scalability in classifying emails. The proposed architecture integrates the AI-DLP Engine into a proxy server, enabling real-time analysis of email content, metadata, and behavioral patterns to classify data flows. Evaluation results demonstrate the system's ability to identify highly sensitive information with very high confidence, making it a dynamic and proactive approach to enterprise data security [2].

title: Comparative Analysis of Deep Learning-Based Denoising Techniques for MRI Images
authors: Rekha Awasthi, Goldi Soni, RPS Chauhan
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Artificial Intelligence for Innovations in Healthcare Industries (ICAIIHI)
abstract: MRI, a critical diagnostic tool, is tainted by noise that compromises image resolution and resolution. Standard algorithms, such as Gaussian filtering and wavelet-based techniques, are relatively easy to use, but they smoothen the image and erase crucial details. Recent deep-learning has vastly outperformed older methods to reduce MRI noise while maintaining valuable anatomical information and fine details. The paper compares different deep-learning models for denoising MRI scans and keeping anatomical integrity. The findings demonstrate that deep-learning models outperform more conventional approaches but require considering the tradeoffs between network complexity and denoising performance. These findings point towards deep-learning as a promising tool to improve MRI denoising in clinical applications to drive diagnostic improvements.

title: Speech Reconstruction from Silent Lip Movements Using Deep Learning
authors: M. V. Subba Rao, Thammineni Naga Amulya, Yaramala Aparna, Ravula Pranavi, Sudhadi Madhumitha, Soudarla Sai Priya
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Artificial Intelligence for Innovations in Healthcare Industries (ICAIIHI)
abstract: To reconstruct speech from videos of silent lip movements, a deep learning method is suggested. While a Bidirectional LSTM (Bi-LSTM) models sequential dependencies in lip dynamics, a 3D Convolutional Neural Network (3D-CNN) is used to capture spatiotemporal visual features. A neural vocoder is used to translate the extracted features into speech after mapping them to mel-spectrograms. Comparing the approach to traditional architectures, experiments on the GRID Corpus show that it produces speech that is more natural and understandable. The project advances the creation of reliable silent speech interfaces for use in secure communication, accessibility, and human–computer interaction applications.

title: What is Real Anymore? A Solution to Detect Hyper Realistic AI-Generated Imagery
authors: Andrew McDonald, Ahmad AL. Doulat, Mohammad Khan, Chelsie Dubay, Jeff Roach
publish: 2026 IEEE 16th Annual Computing and Communication Workshop and Conference (CCWC)
abstract: Building on the WIRA image dataset, this work introduces the Symbiote Particle, an enhanced dual stream CNN model architecture designed to detect AI-generated patterns when used to train on distinct image categories. Additionally, a methodology is proposed to find a single hyperparameter configuration that helps the Symbiote Particle generalize well not only on one WIRA image category, but multiple image categories. Across all 62 WIRA image categories, the modelstrategy configurations collectively achieved a mean accuracy of 65%, mean precision of 68%, mean recall of 77%, and mean F1 score of 66%. These configurations collectively achieved a mean accuracy of 73%, mean precision of 73%, mean recall of 88%, and a mean <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$F 1$</tex> of 77% on more than half of the 62 WIRA image categories. Finally, these configurations collectively achieved perfect performance with 100% accuracy, precision, recall, and F1 in 3 WIRA image categories. These results establish the Symbiote Particle as an effective solution to detect hyper realistic AI-generated content.
