This study emphasizes the transformative potential of AI-driven automation in industrial technologies and predictive maintenance by closing the gap between raw sensor data and actionable insights. It is the first of a five-part series that lays the framework for future research into real-time monitoring, adaptive learning models, and industrial AI deployment tactics.

title: Performance Analysis of Double Material Cylindrical Surrounding Gate Mosfet
authors: Nupur Mittal, Piyush Charan, Imran Ullah Khan, Zohaib Hasan Khan
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent and Innovative Technologies in Computing, Electrical and Electronics (IITCEE)
abstract: The investigation explored how self-heating and temperature sensitivity impact the linearity of a double-material cylindrical surrounding gate MOSFET. Results indicate that self-heating can reduce the device's linearity by decreasing transconductance and increasing output conductance. Temperature sensitivity affects the device's mobility and threshold voltage. Hence, it is essential to count on these factors during the planning and assessment of double-material cylindrical surrounding gate MOSFETs (DMCSG MOSFET) to guarantee peak performance. The study utilized a TCAD 3-D device simulator to obtain these results. The DMCSG MOSFET is an innovative transistor design that demonstrates improved performance characteristics compared to standard MOSFETs. This device utilizes a double material cylindrical gate surrounding the channel to enhance control and improve electrostatic integrity, resulting in better transistor performance. With its cylindrical gate structure enclosing the channel, the DMCSG MOSFET achieves superior gate control while minimizing short channel effects. By incorporating two different materials in the gate, it enhances electrostatic integrity, reducing gate leakage and improving device reliability. This transistor design also presents several advantages over traditional planar MOSFETs. The cylindrical gate geometry enables better electrostatic control, resulting in reduced power consumption and improved switching speed. Moreover, the DMCSG MOSFET demonstrates outstanding scalability, rendering it fitting for cutting-edge integrated circuit designs. Within this abstract, we provide a synopsis of the DMCSG MOSFET's pivotal characteristics, encompassing its dualmaterial surrounding gate configuration and enhanced electrostatic management. We emphasize its benefits over traditional MOSFETs, such as diminished power consumption, heightened switching speed, and augmented scalability. The DMCSG MOSFET shows great potential for use in high-performance electronic devices and integrated circuits, paving the way for advancements in the field of semiconductor technology.

title: Real Time LPG Leakage Detection and Refill Scheduling Using IOT
authors: B. Ragavi, K. Sanjay, P. Sandhiyadevi, P Sivakumar, M.R. Mano Jernila
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent and Innovative Technologies in Computing, Electrical and Electronics (IITCEE)
abstract: Liquefied Petroleum Gas (LPG) leakage is of great concern as it causes both fire hazards and health problems. This work reports a real time LPG detection and refill scheduling using IOT on the RP2040 microcontroller, MQ-2 gas sensor, and Wi-Fi module for real-time monitoring to increase the home security. The system also continuously monitor and sense the LPG leakage with the MQ-2 sensor and sends data to the ThingSpeak cloud platform through Wi-Fi. It provides the real-time alerts in the form of mobile notifications and SMS. If there is a leakage in LPG, an automatic emergency response system will response with the help of buzzer operation and also shutdown control, is initiated to avoid accidents. The system also keeps track of LPG levels and automatically orders a refill when the cylinder is low. The RP2040 microcontroller, is cost effective with high-performance were as it provide an efficient data processing and smooth connectivity. Experimental findings validate the high sensitivity of the system and also provides the stable cloud-based notification, positioning for smart home security and automation.

title: Strategy for the Dispatch of VPP in Industrial Zones Based on a Quantitative Assessment Model of Adjustment Costs
authors: Qizhao Zhang, Wanying Zhao, Lei Liu, Wenjun Liu, Yong Liang, Chang Lu, Qu Yi, Wenxuan Zhang, Dawei Li
publish: 2025 25th Conference of the Electric Power Supply Industry (CEPSI)
abstract: Virtual Power Plant (VPP), as a key technology integrating Distributed Energy Resources (DER), Energy Storage Systems (ESS), and Controllable Loads (CL), has become an important approach to enhance power grid flexibility. Aiming at the load characteristics of high energy consumption and high regulation potential in industrial areas, this paper constructs a VPP dispatching strategy based on quantitative regulation cost, aiming to optimize resource allocation and reduce operation costs. Firstly, analyze the energy distribution and load characteristics in industrial areas, establish wind-power and photovoltaic output models and dynamic aggregation methods for adjustable resources, evaluate the response capabilities of residential, commercial, and industrial loads through pattern-matching technology, and find that industrial loads have significant advantages in the range of regulation power. Secondly, construct an objective function including operation costs, penalty costs, and demand - response benefits, combined with energy storage charging and discharging constraints and power grid power balance conditions, to form a multi-objective optimal dispatching model. Simulation results taking a certain industrial area as an example show that this strategy can reduce the load peak-valley difference from 110MW to 90MW, significantly smoothing load fluctuations; the VPP with energy storage has higher total profits compared to the scenario with only wind power, and has prominent cost, verifying the effectiveness of the quantitative regulation - cost model in improving economy. The research provides a scalable dispatching strategy and technical path for the practical application of VPP in industrial areas, helping to achieve coordinated optimization of "source - load - storage" and efficient consumption of new energy.

title: AI-Driven Immersive Emulation for Tabletop Scenarios
authors: Trenton Watkins, Ben Davis, Raveendra Babu Ponnuru, Mohammed Azab
publish: 2026 IEEE 16th Annual Computing and Communication Workshop and Conference (CCWC)
abstract: Effective cybersecurity training requires preparing personnel at every organizational level to operate under pressure, interpret incomplete information, and make rapid, high-stakes decisions. However, current training practices struggle to capture the dynamic, adaptive, and adversarial nature of real-world incidents. These methods lack realism, rely on rigid scripts, and fail to model evolving attacker behavior or the cascading consequences of defensive decisions. As a result, organizations continue to show substantial readiness gaps. CISA's recent assessments indicate that most victims remain unprepared across both technical controls and decision-making processes, from junior analysts to executive leadership. Meanwhile, large-scale exercises such as Cyber Storm demonstrate the value of adaptive, inject-driven training but demand resources and coordination not feasible for routine use. This gap motivates the need for a training system that combines the accessibility of tabletop exercises with the realism and adaptivity of full-scale cyber simulations. In this work, we introduce an AI-driven immersive emulation platform that transforms traditional tabletop scenarios into dynamic, decision-responsive training experiences. The system integrates multiple specialized AI agents (Including adversary emulation, scenario orchestration, content generation, and performance analysis) coordinated through a modular Model Context Protocol (MCP) architecture. These components enable real-time scenario evolution, realistic attack behaviors aligned with the MITRE ATT&CK framework, and automated generation of supporting artifacts such as logs, emails, and incident injects. The platform also delivers structured, role-specific feedback through a performance intelligence engine that evaluates both quantitative metrics and qualitative decision-making. By blending adaptive AI agents with scalable microservice architecture, the system offers a practical, repeatable, and highly realistic alternative to traditional tabletop training. We expect this approach to measurably improve situational awareness, decision-making speed, ethical and legal compliance, communication effectiveness, and overall readiness during cybersecurity incidents.

title: Age Prediction in Low-Resolution Images: A Comparative Study of Deep Learning and Super-Resolution Models
authors: Jonathan Adrian, Gede Putra Kusuma
publish: 2025 2nd Beyond Technology Summit on Informatics International Conference (BTS-I2C)
abstract: This study aims to enhance age prediction accuracy from low-resolution (LR) facial images by evaluating and comparing the effectiveness of deep learning models and super-resolution techniques. The research addresses the challenge of accurate age estimation in real-world scenarios where LR images are prevalent, which often degrade the performance of traditional models. Three super-resolution models, SRCNN, RCAN, and EDSR, were employed to reconstruct high-resolution (HR) images from LR inputs, while three age prediction models, CORAL CNN, GRA_Net, and Attention VGG-16, were tested on HR images. The best-performing age prediction model, CORAL CNN, was then trained on the super-resolved (SR) images to evaluate the improvement in age estimation. Experiments were conducted using the UTKFace dataset, with performance evaluated using Mean Absolute Error (MAE), Mean Squared Error (MSE), R<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup> score, and Structural Similarity Index (SSIM). Results demonstrated that CORAL CNN achieved the best age prediction performance on HR images, with an MAE of 6.2319, an MSE of 73.8212, and an R<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup> score of 0.8181. Among the super-resolution models, RCAN outperformed others, achieving an SSIM of 0.9129 and an MSE of 0.0009. When combined with CORAL CNN, RCAN further improved age prediction accuracy on SR images, achieving an MAE of 6.2617, an MSE of 75.3740, and an R<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup> score of 0.8063, outperforming traditional bicubic interpolation. These findings demonstrate the potential of combining super-resolution techniques with deep learning models to address resolution limitations in age prediction tasks.

title: 110 kV Grid Impedance Measurement System – Initial Commissioning and Derived Requirements
authors: Kevin Alexander Winterfeld, Johannes Schräder, Patrick Möbius, Marc Florian Meyer, Florian Grumm, Philipp Wagner, Maximilian Schifferdecker, Detlef Schulz
publish: 2025 International Conference on Electrical and Computer Engineering Researches (ICECER)
abstract: Grid impedances are an increasingly important characteristic to analyze, describe, and understand the behavior of public power supply grids within the context of the energy transition, specifically an increasing number of grid connected inverters and the progressive replacement of synchronous generators by grid-forming inverters. The demand for analyzing tools increases as the share of renewables rises, short-circuit power decreases and grid structures become increasingly inverter dominated. This contribution presents a broadband grid impedance measurement system for high voltage grids and its initial commissioning in Hamburg’s public distribution grid. Experiences from all development phases are summarized to a requirement set and key aspects are discussed. Specifically, the choice and handling of the insulation medium, the design and arrangement of the power electronic components as well as applicable standards are further highlighted.

title: Clean Code Against Dirty Cash: A Survey on Anti-Money Laundering Techniques
authors: Riya Kansal, Janvi Sharma, Tarandeep Kaur Bhatia, Shaurya Gupta
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Internet of Things: Smart Innovation and Usages (IoT-SIU)
abstract: Money laundering has become a significant challenge for financial institutions and regulatory authorities worldwide due to criminals who continue to exploit financial systems to hide illicit funds. Current anti-money laundering (AML) procedures still do not adequately combat current practices of money laundering with the help of advanced artificial intelligence (AI) and machine learning (ML) techniques. In addition, new methods of detecting anomalies in AML have benefited from recent developments in deep learning based on the use of transformer architectures, recurrent neural networks, and graph neural networks (GNN). The use of Generative Adversarial Networks (GAN) has improved the ability to train AML models through the use of synthetic transaction data created using the GANs. A major concern of AI-based AML systems is the “Black Box” nature of how these models make decisions, therefore raising questions of transparency and regulatory compliance. Furthermore, to mitigate this issue, researchers are looking to explainable methods like LIME (Local Interpretable Model Agnostic Explanations) and SHAP (Shapley Additive Explanations) as a means of improving the interpretability of models and ensuring compliance with financial regulations. In this review, we integrated the contributions of recent literature and findings from a critical survey of modern AI-driven AML systems to provide a comparative perspective on the pros and cons of AI-based AML systems in detecting cross-border financial anomalies. This review also provides a valuable resource for researchers, policymakers and financial analysts who wish to apply modern ML techniques to improve existing AML methods.

title: Machine Learning Integration with Big Five Personality Traits for Career Suitability Prediction
authors: Minakshi Roy, Kalpana Sharma
publish: 2025 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence for Computing, Astronomy and Renewable Energy (AICARE)
abstract: This research work is carried out to have a clear understanding on the personalities of the individual. Big five model is applied to assess the human’s personality traits, namely conscientiousness, agreeableness, extraversion, openness, and neuroticism. Choosing a right career based on own qualitative characteristics is the best thing for a person which may lead to good collaboration with working industry and co-workers. These perceptions may help the individuals to grow more in the more suitable jobs matched with their behavioral parameters which were identified by the five factor model. Each factor of this model has unique role which may play a significant role in the career prediction. The organizing ability of the person is detailed by the parameter conscientiousness. The job profiles which have direct connection with social involvement can be chosen by the people with high degree of extroversion. If an individual enjoys his work, then his skillful attitude may multiply the outcome for his institute. Another factor openness to new experience aids the promotion of individual’s innovation and exploration. On the other side neuroticism can guide us for stress management and taking self-care at work. Machine learning algorithms also play a vital role by proposing a model that combine students interest with their dream jobs. The main aim of our work is to find the interrelation between personality based Big five model and ML based career model to predict the best career for a learner.

title: IoT-Driven Fintech Solutions for Solar Energy Access: A Systemic Enabler Analysis Using ISM and MICMAC
authors: Kuldeep Singh, Shalini R
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent and Innovative Technologies in Computing, Electrical and Electronics (IITCEE)
abstract: The integration of FinTech innovations and Internet of Things (IoT) technologies is emerging as a transformative pathway for enhancing access to decentralized solar energy systems, especially in underserved regions. This study identifies and models fifteen key enablers that drive the adoption of IoT-enabled FinTech solutions in solar panel deployment. Enablers were extracted through a rigorous strategic review of literature and elicitation from experts, supplemented with trend validation using Google Trends keyword analytics. VOS viewer was employed to construct a scatter plot visualization of co-occurrence data, revealing core thematic clusters among the enablers. To structure the interrelationships among these enablers, Interpretive Structural Modeling (ISM) was applied, followed by MICMAC analysis to classify them based on driving and dependence power. The resulting framework highlights foundational and strategic enablers crucial for policy formulation, technological innovation, and financial inclusion in solar energy access. The MICMAC and ISM analysis indicates that Government Support and Financing Mechanisms serve as fundamental drivers significantly impacting the entire ecosystem, facilitating the implementation of essential technologies including cloud infrastructure, blockchain, IoT and innovative business models. Dependent factors at a higher level, including platform awareness, digital literacy, risk conditions, and advanced operational enablers such as predictive maintenance and dynamic pricing, are effective only when supported by foundational policy, financial, and technological drivers, thereby improving access to solar energy. The proposed model serves as a foundational roadmap for future empirical research and real-world application in sustainable energy-finance ecosystems.

title: Design and Analysis of a Low-Power Reconfigurable ALU for Edge Computing Applications
authors: Devendiran K, Latha R
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent and Innovative Technologies in Computing, Electrical and Electronics (IITCEE)
abstract: In this paper, the design and analysis of a low-power Reconfigurable Arithmetic Logic Unit (RALU) suitable for edge computing applications is presented. Unlike conventional fixed-function ALUs, the proposed RALU architecture supports runtime reconfiguration to perform arithmetic, logical, and comparison operations using a single, unified hardware block. Implemented using Verilog and synthesized on a general-purpose SPARTAN-6 FPGA platform, the design incorporates clock gating, module reuse, and selective activation to optimize power and area. Simulation results confirm correct functionality and single-cycle execution for all supported operations. Comparative analysis with a traditional ALU demonstrates a 28% reduction in dynamic power consumption and a 22% decrease in logic utilization, with negligible timing overhead. The proposed design enhances operational flexibility, making it well-suited for energy-constrained, real-time embedded systems. This work reinforces the value of reconfigurable computing in achieving power-aware, adaptive digital processing for next-generation edge devices.

title: Towards Propagation-Aware Representation Learning for Supervised Social Media Graph Analytics
authors: Wei Jiang, Tong Chen, Wei Yuan, Xiangyu Zhao, Quoc Viet Hung Nguyen, Hongzhi Yin
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Data Mining (ICDM)
abstract: Social media platforms generate vast, complex graph-structured data, facilitating diverse tasks such as rumor detection, bot identification, and influence modeling. Real-world applications like public opinion monitoring and stock trading – which have a strong attachment to social media - demand models that are performant across diverse tasks and datasets. However, most existing solutions are purely data-driven, exhibiting vulnerability to the inherent noise within social media data. Moreover, the reliance on task-specific model design challenges efficient reuse of the same model architecture on different tasks, incurring repetitive engineering efforts. To address these challenges in social media graph analytics, we propose a general representation learning framework that integrates a dual-encoder structure with a kinetic-guided propagation module. In addition to jointly modeling structural and contextual information with two encoders, our framework innovatively captures the information propagation dynamics within social media graphs by integrating principled kinetic knowledge. By deriving a propagationaware encoder and corresponding optimization objective from a Markov chain-based transmission model, the representation learning pipeline receives a boost in its robustness to noisy data and versatility in diverse tasks. Extensive experiments verify that our approach achieves state-of-the-art performance with a unified architecture on a variety of social media graph mining tasks spanning graph classification, node classification, and link prediction. Besides, our solution exhibits strong zero-shot and few-shot transferability across datasets, demonstrating practicality when handling data-scarce tasks. The code is available at https://github.com/WeiJiang01/RPRL.

title: Recent Progress and Prospects of Hydrogen Economy: Review and Bibliometric Analysis
authors: John Olorunfemi Abe, Olawale Muhammed Popoola, Abimbola Patricia Popoola
publish: 2025 International Conference on Electrical and Computer Engineering Researches (ICECER)
abstract: Hydrogen is increasingly needed for sustainable energy. This study explored the Web of Science Core Collection database on hydrogen economy research and publishing growth from 2015 to 2024. The descriptive analysis of the bibliometric study's primary data showed 35,103 authors published 9,983 articles in 1,821 journals. The study found global hydrogen economy research interest as publications rose from 565 to 1910. The hydrogen economy spans many fields, with Chemistry leading research. The most cited papers were Zou and Zhang (2015), Staffell et al. (2019), and Abe et al. (2019), with 5028, 2591, and 2185 citations. Wang Y. published the most, 144 articles and 4652 citations. Zhang and Li followed with 141 and 137 publications and 9501 and 7092 citations. The top journals include International Journal of Hydrogen Energy (1,082 publications and 45,444 citations), Energies (261 publications and 4,593 citations), and Fuel (165 articles and 4,758 citations). With 2,183 and 1,379 articles and 85,004 and 46,649 citations, China and Spain led the research in the hydrogen economy. Tsinghua University demonstrates the strongest collaboration with other affiliations. The main funding agencies were the NSFC, the Spanish Government, and the EU. The hydrogen economy research highlights "hydrogen," "performance," "energy," "hydrogen production," and "storage" as the dominant themes. At the same time, "electrolysis," "electrocatalysts," "kinetics," "mechanism," and "model" reflect emerging areas of interest, implying unexplored topics and revealing possible areas for future research.

title: A Hybrid Deep Feature and Machine Learning Approach for Multi-Class Lung Cancer Classification from CT
authors: Mobarak Abumohsen, Enrique Costa-Montenegro, Silvia García-Méndez, Amani Yousef Owda, Majdi Owda
publish: 2025 2nd Beyond Technology Summit on Informatics International Conference (BTS-I2C)
abstract: Lung cancer (LC) remains one of the leading causes of cancer deaths worldwide, and this supports the urgency of achieving precise and timely diagnosis. In this paper, a multi-phase automated LC classification system is proposed based on a hybrid approach integrating classical machine learning (ML), deep feature extraction, and feature selection techniques. A proper dataset, Merge_CT, was obtained by combining two public CT (computed tomography) scan datasets and reclassified into three clinically relevant classes, i.e., benign, malignant, and normal. The pipeline was evaluated at three phases: 1) raw image-based ML, 2) deep feature extraction by using pre-trained CNNs (VGG16, DenseNet121, MobileNetV3), and 3) feature selection using RFE, ANOVA, MI, and GA. MobileNetV3-SVM achieved the best accuracy (99.80%), and feature selection increased efficiency, highlighting its strong discriminative ability for LC classification. The result presents evidence for an interpretable, scalable approach to assisting LC diagnosis in the clinical environment.

title: Smart Hearing Multilingual Device with Gesture Detection
authors: Adya Amar, Anshika Singh, M Sai Nandita, Stuti Anuva Nayak, Nagesh K N
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent and Innovative Technologies in Computing, Electrical and Electronics (IITCEE)
abstract: A significant issue that arises is the communication barriers suffered by individuals with speech and hearing impairments. The smart system includes sign language to voice and text to sign language conversion system which enables bidirectional for differently abled people. The proposed project includes the Raspberry Pi5 which acts as the processing unit connected to a camera for detecting hand gestures and speech input. The text will be detected and will be converted into the sign language. It utilizes the google translator API for multilingual support. It even converts the sign into speech with the help of Pygame. This allows to bridge the communication gap in the society.

title: Integrating Climate Change Effects on Energy Systems Infrastructure Planning Models
authors: Vijay Naga Mohan Pothabathula, Harshada Dattatray Borse, Alexandre Oudalov, Sebastian Porras Aparicio
publish: 2025 25th Conference of the Electric Power Supply Industry (CEPSI)
abstract: This work presents a novel approach to integrating current and projected climate change impacts into long-term energy system infrastructure planning. Climate change is altering weather patterns, increasing temperature variability and precipitation, and intensifying extreme weather events. These changes pose challenges to maintaining energy supply-demand balance and highlight the need for resilient infrastructure. Traditional capacity expansion models often overlook climate-driven variability in renewable energy generation and demand fluctuations caused by extreme weather. To address this gap, the proposed method embeds climate parameters—such as air temperature, wind speed, and solar irradiance—into long-term planning models. These parameters are derived from global climate projections (ICCP RCPs, CORDEX) and processed into representative hourly profiles across network zones.The model links climate scenarios to electricity demand and asset performance, enabling planners to assess climate impacts on both supply and demand. A bottom-up approach is used to model temperature-sensitive sectors, capturing how cooling demand responds to temperature changes. Renewable energy outputs are adjusted based on climate forecasts, improving accuracy in generation, storage, and transmission planning. Sensitivity analyses reveal that climate-induced variability significantly affects renewable generation profiles and alters capacity requirements for renewables, storage, and grid reinforcement. The findings emphasize the importance of robust electrification strategies and efficiency improvements, particularly in the cooling sector. The study concludes by advocating for a flexible and resilient grid capable of adapting to weather variability and high-impact, low-frequency events. Using a zonal model of India’s energy system, the research provides a framework for climate-informed investment planning. It offers actionable insights for stakeholders to support cost-effective, resilient infrastructure development and encourages policy measures that integrate climate considerations into energy system planning.

title: SafeStreets: Deep Learning-Based Real-Time Traffic Violation Detection System
authors: Chayadevi M L, Samarth Uday, Sourav Mantesh Shet
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent and Innovative Technologies in Computing, Electrical and Electronics (IITCEE)
abstract: The list of traffic violations on two-wheeler that has contributed to the increase of accidents on city roads, covers the non-use of a helmet, triple riding, covered or fragmented license plates, and illegal parking on the opposite side of the road. The main causes of traffic congestion and reduced transport capacity stem from these violations. The traditional manual methods of surveillance and enforcement face operational challenges because personnel numbers remain low and human observation suffers from unpredictable timing and mistakes which result in inefficient monitoring of entire areas. The system uses artificial intelligence and computer vision technologies to monitor traffic in order to solve its problems. The system processes live video streams from roadside cameras which operate at strategic locations to detect helmet usage and count riders and verify license plate visibility and identify parking violations through deep learning algorithms. Deep learning methods which include convolutional neural networks prove to be the most suitable solution for object detection and image recognition tasks when performing high-accuracy classification and fast processing of moving traffic scenes. The same situation is with modular design, allowing for the diverse deployment of different kinds of urban settings and the ability to blend with the current traffic management system for the easy generation of evidence and the reporting of violations. The experimental results demonstrate that detection accuracy and operational efficiency and enforcement reliability have improved when compared to traditional surveillance methods. The system offers a complete flexible solution which solves road safety problems and ensures regulatory compliance and smart city transportation system development.

title: Multi-Day Scheduling for Electric Vehicle Routing: A Novel Model and Comparison Of Metaheuristics
authors: Dominik Köster, Florian A. Porkert, Klaus Volbert
publish: 2025 2nd Beyond Technology Summit on Informatics International Conference (BTS-I2C)
abstract: The increasing use of electric vehicles (EVs) requires efficient route planning solutions that take into account the limited range of EVs and the associated charging times, as well as the different types of charging stations. In this work, we model and solve an electric vehicle routing problem (EVRP) designed for a cross-platform navigation system for individual transport. The aim is to provide users with an efficient route for their daily appointments and to reduce possible inconveniences caused by charging their EV. Based on these assumptions, we propose a multi-day model in the form of a mixed integer programming (MIP) problem that takes into account the vehicle’s battery capacity and the time windows of user’s appointments.The model is solved using various established metaheuristics, including tabu search (TS), adaptive large neighborhood search (ALNS), and ant colony optimization (ACO). Furthermore, the performance of the individual approaches is analyzed using generated ensembles to estimate their behavior in reality and is compared with the exact results of the Google OR-Tools solver.

title: Alerting System for Unusual Activity Detection
authors: Krishna A. N, Udai K C
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent and Innovative Technologies in Computing, Electrical and Electronics (IITCEE)
abstract: This paper proposes an automated, scalable, and failure-tolerant alert mechanism for detecting suspicious behavior, e.g., the detection of weapons, fire, or a person hiding their identity. Real-time computer vision processing detects potential security threats and automatically triggers call alerts for instant response. Containerization technologies provide cross-platform support and self-recovery mechanisms to ensure seamless deployment and high availability. Automatically rejoining fail- tolerant container nodes upon restoration enables persistent monitoring with limited human involvement. Furthermore, technology enables quick recovery from security breaches and minimizes the damage that may be caused by the breach. The process was developed in high-security settings for improved situation awareness, speeding up the threat reduction, and organizational security resistance to external and internal attacks. The alert system is automated and utilizes a fault-tolerant architecture with replicated container nodes designed for total non-failure at the node level <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(100 \%)$</tex>, providing uninterrupted operation and high availability. Coupled with this fault-tolerant infrastructure, the real-time computer vision model provides highly accurate object detection with a recognition rate of 92 % in detecting suspicious activities like weapons, fire, and hidden identities. This synergy of the fully fault-tolerant node system and an extremely accurate model of detection further supports a valid and efficient approach to increased security and quick reaction to threats.

title: Hybrid Random Forest and Neural Network Approach for Thyroid Risk Classification
authors: Nitin Rakesh, P. Sathyanathan, S. Rajes Kannan, L Mohana Sundari, N. Mohankumar, Berlin S
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Artificial Intelligence for Innovations in Healthcare Industries (ICAIIHI)
abstract: Thyroid disorders affect a significant portion of the global population, necessitating accurate and timely risk evaluation for effective care. This work introduces a hybrid approach that combines Random Forest (RF) and Neural Network (NN) models to enhance the categorisation efficiency of thyroid risk categories using the Thyroid Risk Prediction Dataset from Kaggle. The hybrid algorithm employs RF expertise in handling large amounts of information and feature importance, together the NN ability to discern intricate dynamic relationships. Following to preparation and choosing characteristics, the algorithm was training and evaluated on a collection of over 10,000 samples containing attributes. The hybrid technique achieved a classifying accuracy of 99.81%, outperforming the effectiveness of individual RF (95.21%) and NN (96.41%) models. The hybrid approach obtained an accuracy of 96.9%, a recall of 97.3%, and an F1-score of 97.1%, demonstrating an equitable efficiency for every metrics. The findings indicate suggest combining of RF and NN systems creates a robust platform for thyroid risk evaluation, improving detection accuracy. The suggested method can assist hospital professionals in the prompt detection and treatment, ultimately improving wellness benefits.

title: The Creation of Digital Deception: An Examination of the Development of Deep fakes
authors: Dev Baloni, Prem Prakash Agrawal, Hemant Chauhan, Ayush Kumar Mishra, Chandradeep Bhatt, Yogendra Narayan Prajapati
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Internet of Things: Smart Innovation and Usages (IoT-SIU)
abstract: Deepfake refers to the creation or manipulation of audio, video, or other media content using advanced artificial intelligence (AI) techniques, particularly deep learning algorithms. The term is derived from the combination of “deep learning” and “fake.” Deepfake technology enables the synthesis of hyper-realistic content, often involving the substitution of one person’s likeness or voice with another, leading to the creation of deceptive or fabricated multimedia. Typically, deepfake creation involves training a deep neural network on a large dataset of images or videos of a target individual. The model learns to understand and replicate the target’s facial expressions, gestures, and voice patterns. Once trained, the model can generate new content that convincingly imitates the target’s appearance and behavior. Deepfake have been utilizes for different aims, ranging from entertainment and art to more concerning applications like misinformation, identity theft, and malicious propaganda. The rise of deepfake technology has raised ethical, legal, and security concerns. The potential for misuse includes the spread of fake news, the manipulation of political events, and the erosion of trust in digital media. Efforts to combat deep fakes involve developing detection tools, implementing legislation, and raising awareness about the existence and implications of this technology. As deepfake technology continues to advance, there is an ongoing need for interdisciplinary collaboration to address its societal impacts and establish safeguards against its misuse.

title: Task Dependency Graph Generation: Designing Graphs for Tasks in a Distributed Environment
authors: Kalyani Borkar, Parth Chaudhari, Paridh Gautam, Rashmi Sharma, Gunjan Chhabra
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Internet of Things: Smart Innovation and Usages (IoT-SIU)
abstract: One of the most fundamental interdependence and effective task in a distributed computing scenario is the efficient execution of activities. Task Dependency Graph (TDG) is a major technique in coordinating scheduling and execution of assigned activities. This study explores the generation of TDGs through combining machine learning (ML) and implementations in Python in order to increase efficiency and flexibility. This paper demonstrates the review of different approaches including machine learning based ones along with different algorithms and heuristics. Additionally, the impact of mentioned techniques also analyses on the scalability, resource allocation, and execution time of tasks and system as well. The structural integrity and feasibility of execution are measured using graph efficiency metrics such as path length, degree centrality, and execution depth. Results from our simulations show that combining graph analysis and supervised learning provides substantially higher accuracy for task dependency predictions and yields better task execution ordering under resource constraints. Using a Python sandboxing environment, the method allows visualization and topological confirmation and performance evaluation while providing immediate feedback to the users. By showcasing how TDG-based scheduling can be adapted and scaled, this work provides a foundation for future improvements using either dynamic graph updates or reinforcement learning

title: AI-Driven ESG Scoring for Sustainable Investment Decisions
authors: Mahafuj Hassan, AFM Rafid Hassan Akand, Md Azharul Islam, Ali Hassan, Hammed Esa, Md Abdur Rob, Anupom Debnath, Arif Ahmed Sizan, Rakibul Hasan
publish: 2025 International Conference on Advances in Machine Intelligence, and Cybersecurity Technologies (AMICT)
abstract: Environmental, Social, and Governance (ESG) investing is increasingly recognized as a cornerstone of sustainable finance, yet traditional ESG scoring frameworks face persistent challenges including inconsistent methodologies, limited transparency, and data quality issues. This study aims to develop and evaluate an AI-driven ESG scoring framework capable of delivering more transparent, reproducible, and accurate assessments for sustainable investment decision-making. Using a synthetic dataset of 1,000 global companies across nine industries and seven regions spanning 2015–2025 (11,000 observations), we integrated 16 original ESG and financial variables with 29 engineered features capturing environmental efficiency, resource usage, and financial performance ratios. Two supervised machine learning models— Random Forest (200 trees, max depth 15, Gini impurity) and Gaussian Naive Bayes—were trained and validated using stratified 80–20 splits with cross-validation. The Random Forest model achieved superior performance (Accuracy 86.50%, Precision 83.49%, Recall 91.00%, F1-score 0.8708) compared to Naive Bayes (Accuracy 51.00%, Recall 8.00%), with ESG Efficiency and Carbon Intensity emerging as dominant predictors. These results demonstrate Random Forest’s ability to capture complex ESG–financial interactions, providing a reliable and interpretable tool for high-ESG firm identification. The findings support the integration of AI-enhanced ESG scoring into portfolio management workflows, offering scalable, data-driven, and transparent alternatives to traditional ratings. Future research should explore explainable AI techniques and multimodal data integration to further enhance transparency, robustness, and global applicability.

title: Assessing the Feasibility of Renewable Energy Break-Even in India Using ARIMA and Polynomial Regression Models
authors: Piya Roy, Dipanwita Chakrabarty, Debashis Chatterjee
publish: 2025 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence for Computing, Astronomy and Renewable Energy (AICARE)
abstract: India is now undergoing a strategic shift towards renewable energy source to reduce the dependency on conventional energy source like fossil fuel. This study presents a comprehensive forecasting approach to examine the feasibility of achieving break-even when the renewable energy supply will meet the rising energy demand of India. Both ARIMA and polynomial regression models have been used for the forecasting by analyzing national load demand and renewable energy supply data. The study has used secondary data that have been taken from GRID India. The intersection of national load demand and renewable energy supply gives a potential breakeven time. The result demonstrate that a 3rd order polynomial gives the best balance between accuracy and generalizability, whereas the ARIMA $(0,1,14)$ model exhibits high predictive performance with low error rates. This combined methodology delivers a reliable, data driven finding that will help researchers and policy makers immensely to reduce the usage of non-renewable energy significantly.

title: Key Press Velocities and Depth for User Authentication
authors: Majd Z. Tahat, Ayman Gharaibeh, William B. Glisson, William Francis, Luke McMillan, Lucas Feazel, Baker Al Smadi, John David Spurgeon
publish: 2026 IEEE 16th Annual Computing and Communication Workshop and Conference (CCWC)
abstract: Keystroke dynamics is all about finding the differences in how people type on a keyboard. To find these differences, it is necessary to collect data from a user as they type. This research investigates the potential of using additional analog data to improve keystroke dynamics-based user authentication, specifically the velocities of pressing and releasing a key on a keyboard and key press depth, captured with a hall effect keyboard. The study collects data from four subjects, where each subject performed 250 password entries of a fixed password on a hall Effect keyboard. From this data, traditional keystroke metrics such as Hold time (H), Down-Down time (DD) (time between key presses), and Up-Down time (UD) (time between key release and next key press), along with the new analog metrics: Press Velocity Down (VD), Release Velocity Up (VU), and key press Depth (DP), were extracted. Using these collected keystroke metrics, each metric was evaluated with various Machine Learning (ML) models, specifically Random Forest (RF), Support Vector Machine (SVM), and Extreme Gradient Boosting (XGBoost). The results showed that the lowest VD or VU score in accuracy was 84.5%, with its highest accuracy being in the RF model, where VU scored 91% and VD scored 92%. DP scored poorly, with its highest accuracy score being 41.5% and precision up to 51% (RF). Thus, the results indicate that VD and VU can be used as accurate data points for keystroke dynamics, while DP cannot.

title: Development of an AI-Driven System for Early Bowel Cancer Detection using Capsule Endoscopy Imaging
authors: Amanda Julia Isa, Muhammad Ary Murti, Bambang Hidayat
publish: 2025 2nd Beyond Technology Summit on Informatics International Conference (BTS-I2C)
abstract: The advancement of Wireless Capsule Endoscopy (WCE) has significantly enhanced the diagnosis of gastrointestinal disorders, particularly intestinal bleeding and bowel cancer, by providing a non-invasive imaging solution. Unlike conventional endoscopic procedures, which require the insertion of a tube equipped with a camera, WCE involves an ingestible capsule that captures and transmits images wirelessly from within the digestive tract. However, WCE generates a vast number of images, making manual review time-consuming and challenging for physicians to identify the most relevant frames for cancer detection. To overcome this limitation, this study introduces an AI-based detection approach that integrates watershed feature extraction, expert medical input, and k-Nearest Neighbor (k- NN) classification. The proposed system achieves an accuracy of up to 91.66% in detecting potential cases of small bowel cancer, demonstrating its potential for practical application in clinical diagnostics.

title: Regression-based Finish Time Prediction of 100km Trail Running using Structured Athlete Performance
authors: Mohd Shamrie Sainin
publish: 2025 International Conference on Advances in Machine Intelligence, and Cybersecurity Technologies (AMICT)
abstract: Finish times prediction in trail running offers valuable support to athletes, coaches, and event organizers in planning and performance assessment. This study presents a benchmarking analysis using a newly compiled exploratory dataset comprising 100 trail runners. The benchmark dataset consists of 18 structured features, including demographic attributes (age and gender), performance indices (ITRA and UTMB scores), race history (number of 50km and 100km events), and recent performance times. Time-based features were normalized, and missing values were replaced using sentinel values and binary indicators. Furthermore, the study introduces the integration of recent race finish times alongside elevation gain to enhance model accuracy. Thirteen regression-based models were evaluated to explore the predictive power of these features. Among them, Support Vector Regression (SVR) demonstrated the better results compared to other regression models on the actual race dataset, with an R<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup> score close to zero (-0.018), 256.25 minutes on Mean Absolute Error (MAE), and 294.84 minutes on Root Mean Squared Error (RMSE). These results provide promising indicators in race time prediction using limited data and contribute to future research in endurance sports analytics.

title: Lightweight Security and Authentication Framework for Edge-Enabled Internet of Medical Things systems
authors: N. Srinivas Rao, Bondugula Mani Samskruthi, Dharavath Mounika, Chimmani Shreya, Adem Sumanjali, Baddi Jasmitha
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Artificial Intelligence for Innovations in Healthcare Industries (ICAIIHI)
abstract: The Internet of Medical Things (IoMT) refers to the networks of the connected devices in the healthcare industry, where data are electronic health records (EHR). These data are vulnerable to unauthorized access, security breaches, and hacking. This paper introduces a Secure Reversible Data Hiding (SRDH) method integrated with lightweight encryption, as well as tamper detection by the checksum, as a solution to these issues. Firstly, Patient data is encrypted using the RC4 algorithm, then, data is hidden in the diagnostic image & apos;s Region of Non- Interest by the reversible watermarking method. The checksum at the receiver side is a value used to check the integrity of the content, and the original image can be obtained by the embedding algorithm if there is no modification. Performance on medical datasets show that the proposed method has a large payload, almost invisible distortion, and efficient change detection, which is a great advantage for real-time smart healthcare scenarios.

title: Digital Transformation of Power Network Work Management with Modern Work Platform and AI Image Detection Solution
authors: Wai Kit Eric So, Lai San Terence Lam, U Man Kyle Lei, Chi Man Chao, Kin Tong Yuen
publish: 2025 25th Conference of the Electric Power Supply Industry (CEPSI)
abstract: In the past, paper documents were the primary method of collecting data on maintenance activities. However, manual input of the collected information into the system resulted in a high error rate and made systematic analysis difficult. The entire process suffers from poor usability, is time-consuming, and requires complicated data storage. Users find it hard to do the planning on critical and risky equipment and concentrate their resources on risky equipment.Companhia de Electricidade de Macau (CEM) is actively pursuing digital transformation. In the past few years, the Work Management System within our company has been used to handle project work and maintenance activities, we developed user-friendly Mobile Application for frontline staff, designed to streamline workflows and manage onsite human and equipment safety under the reengineered guided workflow which based on the standard operation procedure. Besides collecting maintenance data, site attendance, site image and incident reports are also included. Eventually, regular corrective, preventive, and risk-based maintenance could be planned strategically to improve the power network reliability. In the future, we could base on the dataset collected, to support Reliability-Centered Maintenance (RCM) methodology, predictive maintenance, and online monitoring. This could help us in determining the optimal maintenance strategy and effectively reduce the failure rate, and the whole process is available and visible to the management.Consequently, after operating data with image collection, we are searching for a general Intelligence to recognize and identify inappropriate content in images, The goal is to provide a service to users and to locate the potential risk of specific equipment within customer substations. The service returns a confidence score for the image dataset it identifies so that you can make informed decisions about how users want to use the results.This paper will introduce the challenges and obstacles encountered during our digital transformation and share the valuable insights we have gained along the way, our Proof of Concept of Image Detection to the adoption of artificial intelligence (AI) and system integration, standardization of digitization equipment, and design with the goal of optimizing risk management and reinforcing the decision-making process on maintenance aspect.

title: Hybrid CNN-GRU Model for Seizure Detection Using EEG Biomedical Signal Data
authors: Zayniddin Bozorov, Yokubbaeva Umida Abduvakhob Kizi, Haider Alabdeli, D Kalidoss, Kalvin Antony J, Raed Waheed Kadhim
publish: 2025 International Conference on AI-Driven STEM Education and Learning Technologies (AISTEMEDU)
abstract: In the field of biomedicine, using electroencephalogram (EEG) statistics to locate epileptic seizures has grown to be an important area of research. The essential motive for that is that seizures are unpredictable and might have a significant effect on a person's health. By offering EEGxNet-CGRU, a unique hybrid deep learning structure that combines Convolutional Neural Networks (CNNs) and Gated Recurrent Units (GRUs) to enhance the automated prognosis of seizures using electroencephalogram (EEG) data. The convolutional neural network (CNN) component is used to extract robust spatial and frequency-domain functions from raw multichannel EEG recordings. The GRU component, however, is used to locate long-range temporal correlations that are part of sequential mental activity patterns. The version is proven to have an excessive degree of sensitivity and specificity in distinguishing between seizure states and states that do not contain seizures. To teach and examine the usage of a preferred EEG dataset. The consequences of an evaluation study showed that EEGxNet-CGRU is more effective at classifying information and uses significantly less computing energy than standalone CNNs, GRUs, and standard system mastery classifiers. Because of this, the approach demonstrated presents a framework that can grow and function with real-time data, making it possible to create innovative and non-invasive seizure tracking structures.

title: Parts of Speech Tagging in Maithili using BiLSTM
authors: Nidhi, Prabhat Kumar Singh, Harish Patidar
publish: 2025 World Conference on Cutting-Edge Science and Technology (WCCEST)
abstract: Parts-of-Speech (POS) tagging incorporates systematically identifying and tagging every word in a text with its respective grammatical category, like noun, verb, adjective, etc., and it is a key part of understanding any language. But when it comes to less-resourced languages like Maithili, this task becomes especially tricky due to limited data and tools. To tackle the challenge, our research introduces a POS tagger powered by neural networks. This study employs a Bidirectional Long Short-Term Memory (BiLSTM) architecture embedded with a Softmax output layer to enable contextualized word classification. The model was trained and evaluated on a meticulously annotated text corpus comprising <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{1 0, 0 0 0}$</tex> Maithili sentences, ensuring linguistic precision and reliability across syntactic categories. A robust model that achieved an impressive 90.03% accuracy. This POS tagger not only handles common grammatical structures really well, but also opens the door for more advanced language processing tools in Maithili-from better search engines to smarter translation apps and deeper linguistic research.

title: Privacy-Preserving Automated Rosacea Detection Based on Medically Inspired Region of Interest Selection
authors: Chengyu Yang, Rishik Reddy Yesgari, Chengjun Liu
publish: 2025 International Conference on Electrical and Computer Engineering Researches (ICECER)
abstract: Rosacea is a common but underdiagnosed inflammatory skin condition that primarily affects the central face and presents with subtle redness, pustules, and visible blood vessels. Automated detection remains challenging due to the diffuse nature of symptoms, the scarcity of labeled datasets, and privacy concerns associated with using identifiable facial images. A novel privacy-preserving automated rosacea detection method inspired by clinical priors and trained entirely on synthetic data is presented in this paper. Specifically, the proposed method, which leverages the observation that rosacea manifests predominantly through central facial erythema, first constructs a fixed redness-informed mask by selecting regions with consistently high red channel intensity across facial images. The mask thus is able to focus on diagnostically relevant areas such as the cheeks, nose, and forehead and exclude identity-revealing features. Second, the ResNet-18 deep learning method, which is trained on the masked synthetic images, achieves superior performance over the full-face baselines with notable gains in terms of accuracy, recall and F1 score when evaluated using the real-world test data. The experimental results demonstrate that the synthetic data and clinical priors can jointly enable accurate and ethical dermatological AI systems, especially for privacy sensitive applications in telemedicine and large-scale screening.

title: AI-Powered Continuous Patient Monitoring: Advancing Healthcare Data Analytics
authors: Ghousia Jabeen, M.Rajyalaxmi, Kafila
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Artificial Intelligence for Innovations in Healthcare Industries (ICAIIHI)
abstract: Artificial Intelligence (AI) has revolutionized round-the-clock monitoring of patients since it will enable physiological data to be analyzed in real time using wearable gadgets and smart sensors. This paper suggests a Continuous Patient Monitoring System (CPMS) framework based on AI and integrating the Internet of Medical Things (IoMT), machine learning models, and predictive analytics to improve clinical decision-making. The proposed model uses Support Vector Machine (SVM) and Random Forest (RF) algorithms to detect abnormalities in patients at an early stage and predict their risk. The experimental outcomes on the basis of secondary healthcare datasets show that the SVM model was found to have an accuracy of 94, which surpassed other models in terms of precision and recall. The system guarantees active intercessions, minimizes hospital re-admissions and augments patient safety. The critical discussion is made on comparative evaluation, model optimization and integration issues. Results indicate the possibilities of blockchain to guarantee security of data and 5G-IoMT to provide scalable real-time solutions to healthcare.

title: Modernizing Philology Education with Neural Machine Translation of Ancient Languages
authors: Nidal A. Al-Dmour, Yoqutxon Raxmonqulova, Khaled Shaalan, Arasuraja Ganesan, Priyanka Priyadarshini
publish: 2025 International Conference on AI-Driven STEM Education and Learning Technologies (AISTEMEDU)
abstract: The introduction of neural machine translation (NMT) into the teaching of philology is an opportunity that can radically change the process of decoding and teaching ancient texts in a more accurate and accessible way. This paper discusses a new strategy that will use high-level methods of translation to facilitate language knowledge and the conservation of heritage. The current approaches are mostly inadequate to retain the semantic richness and contextual depth of ancient languages, mainly because of low linguistic data and obsolete systems of translations. The consequences of these concerns are disjointed interpretations and hinder learning. To overcome these issues, it creates a proposal of a Transformer-Based Neural Machine Translation (T-NMT) architecture that is aimed at translating and maintaining the semantic structure of ancient texts. Through attention mechanisms and contextual embeddings, T-NMT is able to guarantee further linguistic and meaning alignment of language pairs. The suggested approach can be used in the curriculum of philology to enable comparative study of languages, digital preservation, and to make students interested in historically significant content. It is also used to aid endangered or extinct languages through automation of translation and reduces experts reliance. We have shown that T-NMT drastically increases the level of translation coherence, preserves semantic status, and increases the pedagogic quality of ancient textual information. This practice demonstrates great prospects in the development of AI-based philological education and cultural conservation.

title: Novel Bio-Inspired Computational Technique for Real-Time Control of Automotive Suspensions
authors: Pratyashi Satapathy, Beemkumar Nagappan, Saksham Sood, R Siva, Shashikant Patil, Basavaraj Devakki
publish: 2025 International Conference on AI-Driven STEM Education and Learning Technologies (AISTEMEDU)
abstract: The automotive suspension real-time control is essential to the improvement of the ride comfort, stability, and general driving safety under the dynamic road conditions. Bio-inspired computational methods provide one of the opportunities to reach intelligent and adaptive performance of suspensions by replicating optimization mechanisms in nature. Nevertheless, current control techniques tend to fail in rapid adaptation, low resistance to random road perturbations, and optimal compromise between comfort and handling of the vehicle. The result of these challenges is poor consistency in passenger experience and efficiency particularly in highly dynamic driving conditions. In order to address these drawbacks, the current paper suggests a Novel Ant Colony to Optimization-Inspired Adaptive Suspension Control (ACO-ASC) framework. The model builds on the collective decision-making and pheromone-trail updating process in the ant colonies to adaptively and in real time optimize the suspension parameters (damping coefficients and spring stiffness) based on the changing terrain to ensure rapid convergence and adaptability in response to changing terrain conditions. The given approach is implemented to improve the ride comfort and stability of the vehicle of electric and urban cars that work on irregular and unstable roads. The framework has the ability to dynamically tune the suspension characteristics to provide smooth vibration isolation, and the tire-road contact to be safe and energy efficient. The results indicate that the ACO-ASC technology is much better at enhancing real time adaptability by 98.7, ride comfort by 91.5, handling stability by 93.2, and energy usage by 95.4 than the traditional suspension control systems.

authors: Alen Sabu, Zhantong Qiu, Harish Patil, Changxi Liu, Wim Heirman, Jason Lowe-Power, Trevor E. Carlson

title: MelodyGLM: Multi-task Pre-training for Structured Symbolic Melody Generation_supp1-3667433.pdf
authors: Kejun Zhang
abstract: Pre-trained language models in natural language processing (NLP) have substantially advanced music understanding and generation. However, traditional pre-training methods for symbolic melody generation are limited in their ability to capture multi-scale, multi-dimensional musical structures, primarily due to fundamental differences between textual and musical domains. In addition, the scarcity of large-scale symbolic melody datasets constrains further progress. In this paper, we introduce MelodyGLM, a novel multi-task pre-training framework tailored for structured symbolic melody generation. MelodyGLM enhances autoregressive blank infilling pre-training through melodic <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$n$</tex-math></inline-formula>-gram and long-span sampling strategies, which target local and global structural modeling, respectively. Specifically, we define three types of melodic <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$n$</tex-math></inline-formula>-grams (pitch-based, rhythm-based, and pitch–rhythm hybrid) to model multi-dimensional melodic structures and their intra- and inter-dimensional dependencies. Furthermore, we construct MelodyNet, one of the largest symbolic melody datasets for pre-training, which comprises 440,102 melody pieces extracted from nearly 1.6 million MIDI files. Both subjective and objective evaluations demonstrate that MelodyGLM outperforms previous masked pre-training methods and other symbolic music generation models in modeling melodic structure. In particular, subjective evaluations show that MelodyGLM achieves average gains of 0.75 and 0.69 points in melody continuation and inpainting tasks, respectively, across consistency, rhythmicity, structure, and overall quality.

title: Enhancing TIPS-Pentacene Organic Thin Film Transistor Performance Through High-k PVP Dielectrics and Direct-Written Silver Nanoparticle Electrodes on PET Substrate Towards Flexible Electronics
authors: Nur Syahadah Yusof, Mohamed Fauzi Packeer Mohamed, Mohamad Khairi Ishak, Muhammad Ammirrul Atiqi Mohd Zainuri, Siti Fatimah Abd Rahman
abstract: As the global demand for printed electronics and wearable devices accelerates, significant efforts have focused on advancing fabrication methods for organic thin film transistors (OTFTs). Inkjet printing, a widely explored approach, offers low-cost, low-temperature processing, and compatibility with flexible substrates. Nevertheless, nozzle clogging and stringent ink and substrate requirements continue to hinder material versatility and large-scale commercialization of OTFTs. Meanwhile, Oxide-based dielectrics, particularly Silicon Dioxide (SiO<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sub>), have been extensively used owing to their favorable electrical insulating properties. However, their inherently low gate capacitance density results in high operating voltages, restricting OTFTs from realizing their full potential in practical applications. This work introduces a direct, solution-processable method for fabricating low-voltage OTFTs. Direct-write printing was employed to pattern the source, drain, and gate electrodes, while spin-coating was utilized for depositing both a TIPS-pentacene semiconducting layer and a polyvinylpyrrolidone (PVP) high-k dielectric. The fabricated device featured a 120 μm channel length, demonstrating a saturation mobility of 0.686 cm<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup>/Vs, threshold voltage of –3.5 V, an on/off current ratio of 10<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">6</sup>, and a subthreshold swing of 105.9 mV/decade. Notably, the entire process was conducted below 150 °C, and the devices operated at voltages under –5 V. These results highlight direct-write printing with high-k dielectrics as a promising approach for realizing flexible, low-voltage OTFTs, offering a scalable solution for emerging printed electronic systems.

title: CAWT: Correlated Attention Based Wavelet Transformer for UAV Trajectory Prediction in High-Maneuverability Environments
authors: Huan Zhao, Yuxin Xue, Zhaojun Gu
abstract: Flight trajectory prediction (FTP) is crucial for the autonomous development of unmanned aerial vehicles (UAVs). However, in complex, high-maneuverability motion scenarios, accurately modeling UAV motion patterns with low-dimensional features is challenging. This paper proposes a Correlated Attention-Based Wavelet Transformer (CAWT) model for high-precision FTP of the rotary wing UAV in high maneuverability environments. Firstly, based on the cross-domain feature decomposition characteristics of the wavelet transform(WT), a learnable WT module with adjustable low-pass filter coefficients is proposed to expand the model’s receptive field for features. Subsequently, a cross-correlated attention module is developed to address the physical correlation characteristics between sensor data, enabling feature grouping and capturing associated features. Finally, a kinematic consistency (KC) loss is designed, and the weights of the KC and mean squared error losses are optimized using dynamic entropy weighting to create a combined loss that guides model training. The proposed method is validated on three publicly available datasets and three self-collected datasets. The analysis of predicted trajectories and evaluation indicators shows that the CAWT has significantly better prediction accuracy than the baseline models. Additionally, the ablation experiment confirms the synergistic effect and necessity of each module of CAWT in high-precision FTP tasks.

title: Optimization of Thermal Flow Dynamics in Tobacco Leaf Curing via IoT-Integrated Deep Neural Network-Assisted CFD_supp1-3666820.docx
authors: Yukai Huang
abstract: In bulk tobacco curing barns, thermo-aerodynamic interactions critically determine product quality through coupled heat-mass transfer processes. To address persistent discrepancies between computational fluid dynamics (CFD) simulations and experimental results caused by insufficient sensor validation, we develop an Internet of Things-enabled CFD-deep neural network-integrated platform (IoT-CFD-DNN IP) integrated platform based on data-physics fusion and edge-cloud adaptive learning. This framework synthesizes IoT sensor data with high-fidelity CFD simulations to overcome spatial sensing limitations, constructs dual-source inputs for DNN modeling, and autonomously optimizes curing conditions by learning airflow-heat-leaf couplings through a two-stage adaptive strategy: offline pre-training establishes thermo-aerodynamic mappings while online fine-tuning dynamically updates weights via real-time sensor data. The cybernetic loop is completed by executing control commands on barn controllers via IoT. CFD simulations were conducted for key tobacco curing stages: yellowing (38°C), color-fixing (48°C), and stem-drying (62°C). A multi-parameter monitoring platform, coupled with DNN, validated and refined these simulations. The results demonstrated that optimization using the IoT-CFD-DNN IP significantly improved simulation accuracy, with temperature and wind speed field fitting accuracy increasing by 86.7% and 55.9% during the yellowing phase, 65.3% and 55.4% during the color-fixing phase, and 75.3% and 61.6% during the stem-drying phase. Beyond accuracy gains, the platform enhances tobacco quality consistency and reduces energy consumption versus conventional methods. This study establishes a cyber-agronomic convergence framework, integrating multi-sensor cyber-physical systems with adaptive DNNs to enable self-optimizing curing through closed-loop fan/temperature control. The paradigm advances precision agriculture by autonomously generating high-fidelity digital twins, providing an intelligent foundation for smart curing operations.

title: Realization of DDoS Resilience through XDP-Based Communication Control with Individual Identification Tokens in UDP Data_supp2-3665591.pdf
authors: Takashi Honda

title: Design and Optimization of Highly Miniaturized Liquid Crystal Phase Shifters Based on Defected Ground Structures
authors: Robin Neuder, Marc Späth, Martin Schüßler, Alejandro Jiménez-Sáez
abstract: This paper presents a fast and compact design method for planar liquid crystal (LC) phase shifters, using circuit models based on filter design principles. By applying equivalent circuit optimization to phase shifters utilizing defected ground structure inverted microstrip lines (DGS-IMSL), an efficient optimization framework is developed. This framework enables a fast and comprehensive phase shifter optimization regarding compactness, bandwidth, and impedance matching, all while enabling millisecond-range response times. To validate the optimization framework, two phase shifters based on 23rd-order filters are designed and experimentally verified at center frequencies around 28 GHz and 60 GHz. The 28 GHz prototype achieves a high compactness exceeding <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$1250^{\circ }/\lambda _{0}$</tex-math></inline-formula> and a figure of merit (FoM) above <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$65^{\circ }$</tex-math></inline-formula>/dB with an LC layer thickness of 4.6 <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\mu$</tex-math></inline-formula>m. The 60 GHz prototype features sub-25 ms response times enabled by a thin 2 <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\mu$</tex-math></inline-formula>m LC layer, compactness over <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$650^{\circ }/\lambda _{0}$</tex-math></inline-formula>, and an FoM greater than <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$65^{\circ }$</tex-math></inline-formula>/dB. Altogether, the proposed design framework enables the realization of fast, application-specific LC phase shifters with highly optimized performance.

title: Multi-View Wireless Sensing via Conditional Generative Learning: Framework and Model Design
authors: Ziqing Xing, Zhaoyang Zhang, Zirui Chen, Hongning Ruan, Zhaohui Yang, Zhiyong Feng
abstract: In this paper, we incorporate physical knowledge into learning-based high-precision target sensing using the multi-view channel state information (CSI) between multiple base stations (BSs) and user equipment (UEs). Such kind of multi-view sensing problem can be naturally cast into a conditional generation framework. To this end, we design a bipartite neural network architecture, the first part of which uses an elaborately designed encoder to fuse the latent target features embedded in the multi-view CSI, and then the second uses them as conditioning inputs of a powerful generative model to guide the target’s reconstruction. Specifically, the encoder is designed to capture the physical correlation between the CSI and the target, and also be adaptive to the numbers and positions of BS-UE pairs. Therein the view-specific nature of CSI is assimilated by introducing a spatial positional embedding scheme, which exploits the structure of electromagnetic(EM)-wave propagation channels. Finally, a conditional diffusion model with a weighted loss is employed to generate the target’s point cloud from the fused features. Extensive numerical results demonstrate that the proposed generative multi-view (Gen-MV) sensing framework exhibits excellent flexibility and significant performance improvement on the reconstruction quality of target’s shape and EM properties.

title: Analysis and Characterization of a Planar Fiber-Coil Sensor for High-Frequency Immersion Ultrasonic Sensing
authors: Babak Moeinimaleki, Mohammed Alshammari, Zebadiah Miles, Ayush Chinmay, Xiaoli Wang, Musaddeque Syed, Yiming Deng, Zhen Qiu, Sunil Kishore Chakrapani, Ming Han
abstract: In this work, we characterize a fiber-coil sensor for high-frequency ultrasonic detection in immersion. The sensor incorporates a low-finesse Fabry–Perot interferometer, formed by two weak chirped fiber Bragg gratings inscribed 40 cm apart in a single-mode fiber, which is tightly coiled to inner and outer radii of 3 and 5 mm in a planar ring configuration. We report a comprehensive angular and spatial characterization of the sensor response under immersion, including oblique incidence across a wide ultrasonic frequency range from 2.25 MHz to 10 MHz. Angular sensitivity is assessed by recording temporal signals at various axial rotation angles, while complementary numerical modeling reveals the origin of pulse distortion under oblique incidence. Furthermore, a line scan across a focused beam with an effective width of approximately 1.22 mm demonstrates the segment-based sensing mechanism. Unlike resonant piezoelectric transducers, the fiber-coil sensor exhibits broadband detection across the studied frequency range, highlighting its potential in nondestructive testing, biomedical imaging, and acoustic sensing. In addition, the frequency response of single silica fibers and arrays of coupled fibers are investigated using the finite element method, clarifying resonance behavior.

title: Explainable AI-Enabled Privacy-Preserving Query Processing on Blockchain Ledgers With Statistical Metadata
authors: Joy Dutta, Deepak Puthal
abstract: Blockchain has gained increasing attention for managing eHealth data, yet most existing ledger designs face fundamental challenges in simultaneously enforcing strict privacy protections, enabling low-latency data queries, and supporting transparent AI-driven decision-making. This paper proposes a novel blockchain ledger architecture integrating Explainable Artificial Intelligence (XAI) through SHapley Additive exPlanations (SHAP) and statistical metadata for privacy-preserving and efficient query processing in eHealth applications. Our method introduces dynamic, expert-informed sensitivity classification and interpretable SHAP values directly embedded in block headers. The integration significantly reduces query data requirements by approximately 99.78%, maintaining patient data privacy through role-based access control and metadata-driven querying. Empirical validation in an IoMT-enabled healthcare scenario confirms substantial query efficiency, preserving privacy and transparency improvements, justifying the computational overhead associated with metadata creation. The results highlight the practical utility and robustness of our proposed blockchain architecture for rapid clinical decision-making.

title: Characterization of heat dissipation in quantum cascade lasers: experiment, analytics, and applications
authors: I. I. Vrubel, E. D. Cherotchenko, A. A. Nikitin, V. V. Podoprigora, D. A. Mikhailov, D. V. Chistyakov V. Yu. Mylnikov, S. N. Losev, E. A. Kognovitskaya, A. V. Lutetskiy, S. O. Slipchenko, A. G. Gladyshev, K. A. Podgaetskiy, A. V. Babichev, D. S. Papylev, A. Yu. Andreev, M. A. Ladugin, A. A. Marmalyuk, I. I. Novikov, V. I. Kuchinskii, L. Ya. Karachinsky, N. A. Pikhtin, A. Yu. Egorov, N. G. Deryagin, V. V. Dudelev, G. S. Sokolovskii
publish: IEEE Transactions on Components, Packaging and Manufacturing Technology
abstract: Heat removal from an active region of a quantum cascade laser (QCL) is a key challenge for achieving continuous-wave lasing and ensuring reliable operation. A key issue in addressing this challenge is the efficiency of all stages of post-growth processing of heterostructures and QCL chip assembly. This paper presents a model of heat propagation across a QCL chip, demonstrating the nonuniformity of this process, characterized by an effective diffusivity constant, which measures the efficiency of heat removal from the QCL’s active region. It is demonstrated that the diffusivity constant can be experimentally determined by measuring the nonlinear chirp of the Fabry-Perot modes’ frequency. Experimental studies of a series of QCL samples fabricated from the same heterostructure but with differences in the post-growth process demonstrated that the proposed model and experimental chirp measurement technique enable the identification of the most optimal approaches to post-growth processing for efficient heat removal from the active region.

title: FBMAN: A Lightweight Network Framework for Continuous Lumbar Motion Angle Prediction Tailored to SEMG Characteristics
authors: Dianfan Zhang, Yameng Dong, Shuhong Cheng, Fei Liu, Hongbo Wang, Qiang Luo
publish: IEEE Transactions on Instrumentation and Measurement
abstract: The measurement of biological signals for assistive rehabilitation devices such as wearable exoskeletons, particularly angle measurement, has garnered increasing research interest. Since surface electromyography (sEMG) precedes movement onset, it enables early angle prediction with lower latency than direct measurement. However, existing angle prediction models are often computationally intensive and inadequately tailored to the unique characteristics of sEMG signals. To address this, we propose a novel Fourier basis mapping and adaptive normalization (FBMAN) framework for lumbar sEMG-based angle prediction. Specifically, sEMG and motion signals were acquired using Delsys equipment to establish a dataset, followed by signal processing via dual-tree complex wavelet transform (DTCWT) combined with the Nelder-Mead algorithm. An entropy-weighted evaluation scheme was developed to construct a comprehensive denoising assessment system. Within the prediction module, slice-level adaptive normalization (SAN) and Fourier basis mapping (FBM) were innovatively introduced to accommodate sEMG’s non-stationarity and time-frequency properties.
