Remarkably, when integrated with lightweight architectures such as multilayer perceptrons (MLPs), the FBMAN-MLP framework demonstrated superior performance in both cross-subject and cross-day experiments. Experimental analysis of sliding window sizes revealed that a 200 ms window optimally balanced real-time capability and predictive accuracy, achieving a root mean square error (RMSE) of 0.054 deg and a prediction latency of 14.078±0.421 ms. By leveraging sEMG’s time-frequency and non-stationary features without additional sensors, our method outperforms existing approaches, validating FBMAN’s potential for assistive rehabilitation applications.

title: Toward the Internet of Agentic AI: Protocols, Architecture, and Challenges
authors: Yuzheng Ren, Jiahui Yang, Haijun Zhang, Fei Richard Yu, Yifu Xiao, Xueyan Cao, Chunlei Sun, Ying He
abstract: With the rapid development of artificial intelligence (AI), the Internet of things (IoT), and 6G, autonomous agents are increasingly deployed in a wide range of applications. However, existing network architectures, centered on human control and fixed protocols, lack the flexibility to support dynamic, intent-driven collaboration among heterogeneous agents. To address this, this article introduces the Internet of agentic AI (IoA), a new paradigm that positions agents as primary entities for communication and coordination. We present the architecture, core protocols, and interaction models that enable IoA functionality. Furthermore, we propose an intelligence-based reinforcement learning (IRL) framework that replaces scalar rewards with multi-dimensional random variables, supporting adaptive, multi-objective agent coordination and enabling more expressive policy learning. Simulation results validate the effectiveness of the proposed approach. The article also presents representative use cases and outlines future research directions.

title: Classification of Eczema Stages Using Deep Feature Extraction and Ensemble Machine Learning:A Sri Lankan Context
authors: T. Kayalvizhy, S. Sweshthika, A. Ramanan, V. Vinoharan, S. Pirabakaran
publish: 2025 International Conference on Advances in Technology and Computing (ICATC)
abstract: Eczema, a common chronic inflammatory skin disorder, presents challenges for timely and accurate diagnosis, particularly in distinguishing its stages: acute, subacute, and chronic. Traditional diagnostic methods such as visual inspection and clinical scoring are often subjective, time-consuming, and prone to variability. This study proposes a framework that integrates advanced deep feature extraction with machine learning techniques for the automatic classification of eczema stages. We employed ResNet-50, a pretrained convolutional neural network (CNN), to extract deep, high-level image features from clinical skin images. These 2048-dimensional feature vectors were then classified using a bagging ensemble of linear Support Vector Machine (SVM) models to improve robustness and accuracy. Our dataset consists of 300 high-resolution images collected in collaboration with dermatologists, representing the three eczema stages equally. Images underwent comprehensive preprocessing, including cropping and data augmentation, to enhance model generalisation given the limited dataset size. The proposed model achieved classification accuracy of 93.34% on the test set, with precision, recall, and F1-scores exceeding 0.90 across all classes. The achievement demonstrates the system’s capability to reliably differentiate eczema stages, surpassing traditional clinical methods in objectivity and consistency. This combination of deep learning–based feature extraction and classical machine learning classification offers computational efficiency and practical applicability, especially in resource-limited settings. Ultimately, this approach contributes towards improving early diagnosis, guiding treatment decisions, and enhancing patient outcomes in dermatological care.

title: Vaani AI Desktop Assistant
authors: Ananya Verma, Bhavpreet Kaur, Diya Agnihotri, Disha Garg, Harvinder Singh, Silky Sachar
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Internet of Things: Smart Innovation and Usages (IoT-SIU)
abstract: This paper discusses the design and deployment of Vaani, a voice-based AI desktop assistant developed in Python for the most part and deployed using AWS services including Amazon SageMaker, Amazon S3, and an EC2 Linux instance. Vaani integrates speech recognition, data processing, and text-to-speech functionality through API integrations for seamless functionality. Some of the challenges faced by developers included compatibility of scripts with default settings within SageMaker. Some of these issues were able to be solved through innovative problem solving and the flexibility provided by AWS’s services. This desktop assistant is a testament to how cloud-based AI systems can revolutionize desktop automation by offering scalable, cost-effective, and user-friendly solutions. The paper discusses in detail all features and architectures of Vaani that will guide future innovations in AI-driven automation. The system utilizes two sets of data: a real-time log of user interactions and AI replies stored as CSV in the cloud, and a pre-defined command data set of voice command to action mappings. Two-stage utilization of data enables adaptive learning and deterministic task running for robustness and reproduccibility. Vaani therefore shows the possibility of cloud-based AI to provide desktop automation that is scalable, economical, and easy to use, and yet sheds light to inform future innovations in AI-based assistants.

title: Effective Two Factor Role-based Access Control for Cloud Computing using Twofish encrypted JAR File
authors: Maha Saddal, Gohar Rahman, Mehmood Ahmed, Adeel Ahmed, Salmah Binti Fattah
publish: 2025 International Conference on Advances in Machine Intelligence, and Cybersecurity Technologies (AMICT)
abstract: Despite the rapid advancements in the security of web services on cloud computing, there are still some challenges of data integrity, confidentiality, and access control while sharing data on cloud servers. Typically, existing solutions employ cryptographic techniques by revealing data decryption keys to authorized users only. However, these arrangements produce heavy computational demands, sensitive to user data, for key dispersion and data management. In this paper, a new, effective two-factor role-based access control for web-based cloud computing using Twofish-encrypted JAR files is proposed. The suggested framework highlights the implementation of secret keys using JAR file applications rather than a security device. Simulation results show that encryption times for plaintexts of varying lengths ranged between 5.36 and 5.42 seconds, while decryption remained consistent at approximately 5.00 seconds. JAR file creation time scaled proportionally with file size, confirming the system’s efficiency and scalability. These findings demonstrate the framework's effectiveness for secure, lightweight, and privacy-preserving cloud access control.

title: P4 Shield: Secure Execution of Users' P4 Programs with In-VM Information Inside Clouds
authors: Masaki Iwai, Kenichi Kourai
publish: 2026 IEEE 16th Annual Computing and Communication Workshop and Conference (CCWC)
abstract: Recently emerging programmable network switches can control packet forwarding by software, e.g., using the P4 language. For virtual machines (VMs), virtual P4 switches have been developed to execute P4 programs inside virtual switches. If users could load their own P4 programs into virtual P4 switches, it would enable custom packet forwarding using information inside their VMs. In clouds, however, users cannot fully trust virtual P4 switches, nor can virtual P4 switches trust users' P4 programs. To address these issues, this paper proposes P4 Shield for enabling the secure execution of each user's P4 programs outside clouds' virtual switches by using P4 VMs prepared per user. P4 Shield protects P4 programs from clouds by running P4 VMs as confidential VMs, whose states are protected by processors. It also protects clouds' virtual switches from users' P4 programs by confining them in uBPF sandboxes created in P4 VMs. To allow P4 programs to use information inside users' VMs, P4 Shield provides P4 external functions for accessing information in the shared memory between a P4 VM and a user's VM. We have implemented P4 Shield using Open vSwitch and confirmed its effectiveness.

title: Experimental Comparison of Memory-Based Collaborative Filtering Methods in Recommendation Systems
authors: Wiwik Suharso, Triyanna Widiyaningtyas, Ilham Ari Elbaith Zaeni, Muhammad Fatkhur Rizal, Salahudin Robo, Lukman Hakim
publish: 2025 2nd Beyond Technology Summit on Informatics International Conference (BTS-I2C)
abstract: The Memory-Based Collaborative Filtering (MBCF) method uses implicit queries derived from user preferences. The method allows users to quickly and accurately obtain personalized recommendations. For this purpose, MBCF develops user-based and item-based techniques. Both techniques use previous rating data to measure the similarity between active users/items and target users/items. However, users exhibit different behavioral patterns when rating an item. Some users give high ratings for various items, while others give low ratings for the same items. These differences reflect the user's rating behavior. We compared three similarity measures: Cosine, Adjusted Cosine (ACosine), and Pearson. The measures were used in mean-centered prediction methods under user-based and item-based, respectively. In practice, normalization of mean-user ratings and mean-item ratings is applied to the ACosine and Pearson calculation formulas, respectively. All experiments used the original MovieLens 100K dataset. The experimental results show that Cosine, Pearson, and ACosine under item-based obtained the lowest error, respectively. The evaluation metrics show RMSE values of 0.988, 1.002, and 1.005, respectively. Meanwhile, the MAE values are 0.773, 0.785, and 0.785, respectively. Item-based techniques outperform user-based techniques in three similarity measures.

title: Competency Matrix Design for an Engineering Bridging Course to Calculus 1 Using P-Graph Methodology
authors: John Raymond B. Barajas, Pee Jay N. Gealone, Oliver M. Padua, Nico O. Aspra, Arpon T. Lucero, Marben S. Ramos, Marites E. Olesco
publish: 2025 IEEE 14th International Conference on Engineering Education (ICEED)
abstract: In the Philippines, the influx of K-12 graduates from non-STEM academic tracks highlighted the need for targeted bridging programs to support students transitioning into engineering degree programs. However, existing interventions are often constrained by the absence of a structured framework to systematically address foundational competency gaps. Without such a basis, bridging programs risked misalignment with the prerequisites of higher-level courses such as Calculus 1. To address this issue, this study developed a novel Pre-Calculus competency matrix that identified the most efficient pathway for students to achieve readiness for Engineering Calculus 1. The framework utilized the Solution Structure Generation (SSG) algorithm from the P-graph methodology, originally intended for chemical process synthesis. In this adaptation, Pre-Calculus competencies were treated as input resources, intermediate skills, and final learning outcomes. The algorithm generated an exhaustive map of prerequisite relationships among competencies, from which redundant or non-essential paths were identified and removed. The result was an optimized sequence of essential skills that led to the targeted proficiency in Calculus 1. This competency matrix was then used to design a modular bridging course that is expected to be both instructionally coherent and timeefficient. The proposed approach offered a novel paradigm for competency mapping by integrating systems engineering principles with curriculum design. Overall, the study contributed to educational research by demonstrating a replicable methodology for developing outcome-based bridging programs in engineering education, particularly in contexts characterized by diverse student academic backgrounds and varying levels of preparedness.

title: AI-Driven Photoluminescence Modeling and Experimental Study of Luminescent Silicon Quantum Dot Ink for Quantum Communication Applications
authors: Siddhartha Roy, Susmita Biswas, Nitu Saha, Ahona Ghosh, Rangan Sarkar, Raisa Santra
publish: 2025 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence for Computing, Astronomy and Renewable Energy (AICARE)
abstract: Luminescent materials based on silicon are of good quality and at the same time, they can be used for integrated photonic systems meant for quantum communication. The present paper deals with the study of silicon luminescence from quantum dot ink as a quantum emitter. Moreover, it provides a model for the inked photoluminescence by employing machine learning. Four different regression models were used for the evaluation - MLP, Random Forest, Decision Tree, and Linear Regression. The optimised model, Random Forest got the highest accuracy based on MAE, MSE, and $\mathbf{R}^{\mathbf{2}}$ scores. This combination of quantum photonics and AI modeling gives an idea of the emission dynamics and also facilitates the invention of fast and efficient nextgeneration quantum communication technologies.

title: WASD - Water Saving Device: A Low-Cost IoT-Based Device for Domestic Greywater Reuse
authors: Samuele Rebecchi, Samuele Marcellino, Luca Magoni
publish: 2025 International Conference on Electrical and Computer Engineering Researches (ICECER)
abstract: In response to escalating global drinking water scarcity we propose an innovative, automatic system for reusing clean sink water to flush toilets. Existing solutions for water recycle in the houses involve purifiers and complex treatments, leading to high costs and constant maintenance. WASD, utilizing sensors and a solenoid valve, rapidly detects and separates clean water, directing it to toilet tank while sending non-reusable water to the drain. This cost-effective and user-friendly approach aims to establish sustainable water practices in domestic settings, contributing to solve the shortage of drinking water.

title: An $n$-Card Threshold Protocol Is Impossible
authors: Shizuru Iino, Yang Li, Kazuo Sakiyama, Daiki Miyahara
publish: 2026 IEEE 16th Annual Computing and Communication Workshop and Conference (CCWC)
abstract: Card-based cryptography uses a physical deck of playing cards to achieve secure computations. The first card-based protocol, called the five-card trick, allows Alice and Bob to decide whether to have a date without revealing their actual minds, i.e., it performs a secure computation of the logical two-input AND function. It uses a two-color deck of red and black cards, and the Boolean values are encoded based on the arrangement order of two distinct cards. The five-card trick was later optimized into a four-card AND protocol, achieving the minimal number of cards, because each input bit is encoded using two cards. Afterward, an alternative encoding, where each bit is represented by a single card, enabled a three-card AND protocol with a minor modification to the five-card trick. However, the construction of a two-card AND protocol remains an open problem. In this study, we provide a negative result for the problem: a two-card AND protocol is impossible. We extend this result to a more general class of functions, and consequently, derive the main impossibility theorem: an <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$n$</tex>-card <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$n$</tex>-input threshold protocol is impossible. Our result implies that the three-card AND protocol uses the minimal number of cards. A unique aspect of our proof is its simplicity; we show that it suffices to consider the initial and its subsequent states, unlike previous impossibility proofs that enumerate all possible states.

title: Enhanced Dengue Disease Prediction Using Machine Learning
authors: Ch. Harshini, Y. Lahari, R. Nihari, Sk. Jareena, S. Yashwitha, V. Swapna
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Artificial Intelligence for Innovations in Healthcare Industries (ICAIIHI)
abstract: Dengue fever keeps showing up as a big problem in public health. Catching the risks early on can stop bigger issues from happening later. These days doctors mostly rely on lab tests to find the dengue virus. They check things like platelet counts. That approach works fine enough. But it is not always easy for everyone to get access to it. We built a system that predicts dengue risks with machine learning. It runs on the XGBoost algorithm. We made a web app with Django to handle everything. The system pulls in basic health details from users. It takes symptoms. It takes blood pressure readings. It takes blood sugar levels too. There is a slider for users to show how severe their symptoms seem. Then it gives out predictions fast through a simple interface. The interface includes diagrams. It works in multiple languages as well.We added one more feature to help make everything easier to understand. It draws on explainable AI with SHAP values. Those values point out the main factors behind each prediction. So the model ends up feeling more open and reliable. That sets it apart from those old black-box ways that hide all the details. To keep users informed and learning, we included daily care advice. The app has a part with simple facts about the disease too. Keep in mind this tool is not meant to replace real lab tests. Still it serves as a good first step for raising awareness and issuing alerts. That could push people to get proper treatment earlier. In the end it might stop things from getting worse right away. Our work focuses on blending machine learning precision with clear model explanations. We emphasized human-centered design to push the field forward. All of this creates a strong tool for real-life education on disease risks.

title: Comparative Analysis of Deep Learning Based Denoising of Biomedical Signal for Atrial Fibrillation Applications
authors: Nadeem Pasha, K M Ravikumar
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Internet of Things: Smart Innovation and Usages (IoT-SIU)
abstract: Electrocardiogram (ECG) signals play a vital role in detecting atrial fibrillation (AF). However, the presence of noise often reduces diagnostic accuracy and may lead to misinterpretation. In order to address the challenges, modern signal processing methods, including machine learning and deep learning, are increasingly applied for denoising ECG recordings. The overall performance differs with respect to the type of denoising techniques adopted. A detailed comparative analysis of various denoising techniques is carried out using MIT-BIH Atrial Fibrillation Database (AFDB). In the AFDB records, 1,188 five-second segments were extracted using R-peak–centered windows, and 305 high-quality segments were retained after multi-stage quality filtering for training and evaluation. The popular deep learning denoising models are tested under Gaussian noise and realistic electromyographic (EM) noise. The model performance is determined using Mean Squared Error (MSE), Signal-to-Noise Ratio (SNR), and Pearson Correlation Coefficient. The Denoising Autoencoder (DAE) delivered the best results among other denoising methods, in terms of low mean squared error of 0.09 and high signal-to-noise ratio 10.6 dB under EM noise, high correlation coefficient of 0.987254, The DAE model outperforms other models with respect to effectiveness, waveform preservation, and noise generalization.

title: Predictive Business Strategy Formulation Using Machine Learning: An Ensemble Learning Approach for Operational Forecasting and Decision Optimization in Indian Enterprises
authors: Amit Verma, Suby Baby, Pritish Chandra Vaish
publish: 2025 World Conference on Cutting-Edge Science and Technology (WCCEST)
abstract: In the high-stakes world of today's volatile, data driven business decision environment, predictive strategy is imperative for competitive advantage and operational agility. Most of the conventional strategic planning frameworks do not have the necessary analytics to predict the changing market dynamics especially in an emerging market like India. In this paper we propose a machine learning based ensemble framework with which data centric strategic decision support can be provided to Indian factories. Leveraging a dataset of more than 15,000 operational records across retail, manufacturing, and logistics, we utilized three models-Random Forest, XGBoost, and Stacked Ensemble-to predict critical business metrics. The Stacked Ensemble model outperformed XGBoost (accuracy: 93.4%) and Random Forest (91.2%) for the highest prediction performance with an accuracy of 94.6%, RMSE of 0.251, and an R2 of 0.94. The feature importance analysis also highlighted inventory turnover rate, lead time, regional demand density were top 3 predictors influencing strategy meanings. The model delivered a 12-18% improvement in resource allocation efficiency and a 20% reduction in forecast error for companies tested. These findings confirm the revolution possible from ensemble learning in the space of real-time business strategy formulation for Indian organizations by making them far nimbler and data driven, both in operations and strategic direction.

title: Optimizing CNNR-NNT Model for Effective Product Recommendation in E-Commerce
authors: Simran Kalra, Ayaan Faiz, Diksha Aggarwal, M.Vigenesh, P.N.Ramesh, Sara Elais
publish: 2025 International Conference on AI-Driven STEM Education and Learning Technologies (AISTEMEDU)
abstract: The high pace of e-commerce platforms has increased pressure on the need to have a precise and personalized product recommendation systems. The best recommendation models are to optimize to provide better user experience and more sales. Current approaches have frequent weaknesses in their ability to work with high dimensional user-item data, inadequate interaction sparseness and inadequate prediction performance, resulting in suboptimal recommendations. To resolve these issues, Hybrid CNNR-NNT Framework (HCNNF) is suggested, which consists of Convolutional Neural Network-based Recommendation (CNNR) that provides sufficient features extraction and Neural Network Trees (NNT) that enables accurate product recommendation. The HCNNF is effective in capturing complex user-item interaction, eliminating the dimensionality problem, and enhancing the prediction accuracy. The suggested approach is implemented into e-commerce data sets to provide personalized and real-time recommendations, which can be better customer satisfaction and customer engagement. The experimental results show that the optimized CNNR-NNT model is more advantageous than the currently existing methods and has a greater precision in recommendation, less time to compute, and greater capability to handle dynamic user preferences. The CNNR-NNT results are Precision 0.83, Recall 0.78, F1-Score 0.80, and NDCG 0.82, which is better than the existing approaches, and guarantees better personalized recommendations of the product.

title: Systematic Literature Review of Explainable LLM-Powered ChatOps for CI/CD Pipeline Diagnostics and Developer Support
authors: A.M.A.D. Abeysinghe, W.M.J.I. Wijayanayake
publish: 2025 International Conference on Advances in Technology and Computing (ICATC)
abstract: This systematic literature review examines how explainable Large Language Models (LLMs) transform chat-ops applications for CI/CD pipeline diagnostics and developer support. Analyzing contemporary research from 2021–2025, this review synthesizes findings from studies on conversational AI agents, LLM-driven DevOps automation, and explainable AI techniques in software development. Key methodological approaches include Domain-Specific Languages (DSLs) for chatbot development, multi-agent orchestration frameworks, retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) systems, and two-staged LLM frameworks for detecting and remediating failures. Key findings reveal significant advancements in automating root cause analysis, orchestrating pipelines intelligently, and creating natural language interfaces for complex DevOps operations. Integrating explainable AI principles with conversational interfaces improves developer productivity, reduces mean time to resolution (MTTR), and enhances collaboration between development and operations teams. However, persistent interpretability, security governance, integration complexity, and maintaining human oversight in automated decision-making processes. This review provides a comprehensive roadmap for researchers and practitioners seeking to leverage explainable LLM-powered chat-ops solutions for enhanced CI/CD pipeline management and developer support.

title: Emotion Detection from Kannada Text Using Deep Learning Models
authors: Shantesh R Hosmath, S Manohar, Saman L Nayak, Sanaath S Bhat, Chandravva Hebbi
publish: 2025 International Conference on Electrical and Computer Engineering Researches (ICECER)
abstract: Emotion Detection has become an essential feature of Natural Language Processing (NLP) due to the demand for emotional Artificial Intelligence systems. Although a good deal of studies has been done on emotion classification in English and English-Kannada code-mixed text, few studies have been exclusively done for the classification of sentiment in the Kannada language. This work addresses this gap by presenting a robust approach to emotion detection in Kannada text, targeting seven categories: joy, anger, fear, sad, surprise, disgust, and neutral. A dataset of 7,000 Kannada sentences was collected and validated to form a reliable foundation for experimentation. First, an LSTM was used, and it obtained an accuracy of 60.75%. To boost the contextual knowledge, the ‘bert-base-multilingual-cased’ model was used. This approach ultimately yielded a testing accuracy of 82% – much higher than in the previous case. The result was slightly upgraded with the use of a combination of BERT and LSTM at 83.71%.

title: Enhancing Phishing Detection Through Machine Learning
authors: Bahman A. Sassani Sarrafpour, Zhen Xue
publish: 2025 International Conference on Advances in Machine Intelligence, and Cybersecurity Technologies (AMICT)
abstract: Phishing attacks are among the most serious cyber threats, especially in social engineering. They affect individuals and businesses daily, leading to significant financial and data losses. Traditional blacklist-based defenses are ineffective, as blacklist updates often lag behind the emergence of new phishing sites. Machine learning has become a powerful tool across many fields. In cybersecurity, it enables the development of models to detect threats more accurately and reliably than traditional methods. This research applies three popular machine learning models—Random Forest (RF), Decision Tree (DT), and Extreme Gradient Boosting (XG-Boost)—to build phishing detection classifiers. Five feature selection methods are compared and integrated with these models to enhance detection performance and identify the most effective feature subset. The goal is to develop an effective machine learning model with feature selection to better combat phishing and protect users online. The models will be evaluated using a confusion matrix, accuracy, precision, recall, ROC AUC, and Gini score to determine the most effective feature selection method. A larger dataset will be used to retrain the chosen model, thereby improving its generalization. This thesis contributes to phishing detection research and cybersecurity defense

title: Data-Driven Culturomic Analysis of Historical Themes in Fiction Using Decade-Level Topic Modeling
authors: Michael Andrés Freeman, Ahmad Al Doulat, Mohammad Shoeb Khan
publish: 2026 IEEE 16th Annual Computing and Communication Workshop and Conference (CCWC)
abstract: This paper presents a large-scale culturomic analysis of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{3 0, 2 5 6}$</tex> English-language fiction books published between 1820 and 1929, with the objective of examining whether historical and cultural events leave detectable thematic traces within fictional narratives. Using a decade-level modeling framework, the corpus was processed through a pipeline integrating text normalization, Latent Dirichlet Allocation (LDA), and semantic clustering. The LDA models produced 589 highprobability topic terms, which were subsequently aggregated into five overarching meta-themes using TF-IDF and Non-Negative Matrix Factorization. The results reveal both explicit and implicit cultural signals, including a distinct Napoleonic topic cluster in the 1820s and prominent maritime terminology in the 1840s, corresponding to major geopolitical and exploratory events of those decades. The temporal evolution of the identified meta-themes-Family and Domestic Life, Religion and High Formality, Military and Exploration, National Identity and Institutions, and Youth and Dialogue-further aligns with documented historical and literary trends of the nineteenth and early twentieth centuries. These findings demonstrate that fiction, despite its imaginative nature, encodes measurable cultural patterns and can serve as a viable foundation for culturomic research. The study also highlights the feasibility of using open-source tools to perform large-scale historical text analyses, offering a scalable framework for future work.

title: Late Feature Fusion of Lightweight Deep Learning Architectures for Skin Lesion Classification
authors: Abhinav Neelam, Abhishek Verma
publish: 2026 IEEE 16th Annual Computing and Communication Workshop and Conference (CCWC)
abstract: Classifying skin lesions is an important and active area of research because of the importance of enabling early detection, which can greatly improve the chances of cure. There are many challenges in skin lesion images including scales, angles, rotations, lighting variations, background noise, etc. In addition, the focus on enabling detection using lightweight architectures is not explored thoroughly. We propose a late feature fusion using three separate lightweight architectures: MobileNet-v3 Large, FasterNet-T1, and shViT-s4. In addition, to reduce image variation and improve generalization of all models, on-the-fly augmentation is employed to generate realistic skin lesion samples. The ISIC 2019 dataset is used to train and evaluate the proposed models to classify eight different categories of skin lesion. For late feature fusion, the level two classifiers tested after fusion are XGBoost, LightGBM, Logistic Regression (LR) and Support Vector Machine (SVM). Various evaluation metrics are computed to adequately assess the performance of the proposed models. Fusion using Logistic Regression and SVM improve upon the lightweight architectures. The Logistic Regression classifier achieves an F1-Score of 89.63 % compared to the best individual F1-Score of 88.97 % by FasterNet-T1 while increasing inference time by only 0.11 seconds. In addition, the confusion matrix and the t-SNE plots are visualized to explore and understand various weaknesses of the models that classify the ISIC 2019 dataset.

title: Deepfake Image Detection System
authors: Swathi Darla, Yashaswini Nag M N, Ayush Kapoor, Harsh Ranjan, Suraj Kumar
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent and Innovative Technologies in Computing, Electrical and Electronics (IITCEE)
abstract: Rapid advances in deep learning and generative adversarial networks (GANs) have greatly increased the realism of deepfake technologies, raising serious concerns about the integrity of digital media. While visual manipulation has received substantial attention, recent progress in voice cloning and audio synthesis poses equally significant threats by undermining biometric security and enabling identity fraud. This paper presents a comprehensive study of unimodal deepfake detection systems, evaluating two independent pipelines: a convolutional neural network (CNN)-based image detector and audio detectors using Mel-Frequency Cepstral Coefficients (MFCCs) with Support Vector Machines (SVMs) and CNNs applied to spectrogram representations. By excluding multimodal fusion and recurrent architectures, the study isolates unimodal performance and enables a focused comparison across visual and auditory domains. Experimental results show that CNN-based image models achieve over 90 % accuracy on the Celeb-DF v2 dataset with Grad-CAMbased interpretability, while MFCC+SVM and spectrogrambased CNNs reach approximately 97 % and 98 % accuracy on the Fake-or-Real and DEEP-VOICE datasets, respectively. Although unimodal detectors perform well in controlled environments, challenges remain in cross-dataset generalization, noise robustness, and adversarial resilience, and this work establishes a lightweight baseline for future domain-specific deepfake forensic systems.

title: Secure Federated Deep Learning for Breast Cancer Detection: Enhancing Privacy with Obfuscation and Multi-Modal Intelligence
authors: Shruthi B Gowda, Usha K
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent and Innovative Technologies in Computing, Electrical and Electronics (IITCEE)
abstract: Among women's health disorders, breast cancer ranks as one of the most prevalent and life-threatening diseases. However, privacy regulations and distributed clinical data restrict the use of centralized deep learning systems. A Secure Federated Deep Convolutional Neural Network (FDCNN) is developed in this research to facilitate breast cancer detection among different medical centers without compromising patient confidentiality. The proposed system integrates Differential Privacy (DP), Homomorphic Encryption (HE), and featurespace obfuscation to ensure secure parameter transmission and resistance against inference attacks. The architecture leverages multi-modal medical imaging-including mammography, histopathology, and ultrasound-to improve diagnostic generalization. Experimental evaluations on benchmark datasets under simulated federated conditions demonstrate a classification accuracy of 93.7% and an AUC of 94.9%, closely approximating centralized learning performance while reducing privacy leakage risk by 70%. The results validate that the proposed privacy-enhanced FDCNN achieves high diagnostic efficacy with strong data confidentiality, establishing its potential for deployment in HIPAA- and GDPR-compliant healthcare environments.

title: Deep Learning for Disease Detection in Medical Imaging: From Classification to Segmentation and Localization
authors: Anurag Shrivastava, Hassan M. Al-Jawahry, Sorabh Lakhanpal, Neeraj Varshney, Neha Mathur, Ashish Parmar
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Artificial Intelligence for Innovations in Healthcare Industries (ICAIIHI)
abstract: The field of medical image has been enhanced significantly through the development of deep learning (DL) to change disease detection and diagnosis in numerous health care applications. DL models are more accurate and efficient due to the convolutional neural networks (CNNs) and the transfer learning technique to identify abnormalities in medical imagery such as CT scans, MRIs, and X-rays. The literature reviewed gives the various DL models that have been used in disease classification, segmentation, and localization with proven effectiveness in identifying complex diseases like lung disorders, retinal diseases, and cancers. Despite these achievements, there remain certain issues like data inadequacy, interpretation of the models, and the need to generalize the models to other populations and other types of imaging. To address these limitations, hybrid models, data augmentation and uncertainty estimation methods are being considered. It is also pointed out in comparative studies that in some diagnostic tasks, the DL systems can be equal or even superior to health care professionals. Explainable AI combined with federated learning is expected to enhance greater clinical acceptability and ethical use. In this review, the most recent work is combined with the identified emerging trends and the future development of using deep learning in imaging in the medical context, all oriented at providing better decision support in the clinical setting and patient outcomes.

title: Trusted Collective Learning for Conflictive Multi-View Decision-Making
authors: Nengjun Zhu, Zhiyu Zhang, Chenmeijin Liang, Jian Cao, Siji Zhu, Xiao Wei
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Data Mining (ICDM)
abstract: When processing multi-view data, conflicts may occur since different views have unique insights. Existing studies always consider conflict as a bad factor and thus adopt a negative operation, i.e., eliminating or minimizing conflicts. However, in a multi-view decision-making scenario, conflict can highlight view differences and reveal the reliability of individual and collective decisions. To this end, we propose a novel trusted collective learning method (TrustCL) that can actively handle conflicts between individuals and obtain a collective opinion and its reliability by considering conflicts. Specifically, TrustCL first learns view-specific evidence supporting individual opinions. To deal with conflicting opinions across views, TrustCL assembles a reliability-oriented collective learning phase to determine which view owns a higher priority. It further combines priority and view-specific evidence to conclude the final collective opinion and its reliability. Experiments on a real-world multidisciplinary consultation dataset demonstrate the superiority of our method and exhibit some interesting findings regarding conflictive multi-view decision-making. Our code is available at https://github.com/ifbettrer/TrustCL

title: Reinforcement Learning for Personalized Treatment Plans in Chronic Disease Management
authors: Shashank Singh, Abhishek Yadav, Nixalkumar Patel, Keshav Kaushik, Snehlata Wankhade, Mukesh Soni
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Artificial Intelligence for Innovations in Healthcare Industries (ICAIIHI)
abstract: In this research study we explore the use of Reinforcement Learning (RL) in developing personally tailored treatments for the management of chronic diseases. By utilizing patient specific data such as health status, treatment history and genetic information, the RL model continuously revises treatment plans to achieve the highest successful treatment rates. Model training, the effectiveness of treatment, the level of patient compliance with treatment plans and the cost-effectiveness of treatment plans were simulated by the model. Results showed that RL models have better outcomes than traditional treatment plans and that utilization of RL will provide patients with better overall health outcomes, higher levels of compliance with treatment plans and improved forecasts of future health improvements. Additionally, the RL model was demonstrated to be cost-effective, producing equivalent or better results at lower costs. The results demonstrate the potential of RL to revolutionize the field of chronic disease management; a dynamic, personalized and efficient approach that can ultimately enhance patient care.

title: SciARLearn: Augmented Reality Approach for Science Education
authors: Muhammad Hazwan bin Hussin, Nor Ashikin Mohamad Kamal, Norizan Mat Diah, Nur Azima Alya binti Narawi
publish: 2025 IEEE 14th International Conference on Engineering Education (ICEED)
abstract: Traditional methods of education have proven to be beneficial for students. However, several research studies have been done regarding the issue that exposed the flaws of the traditional method. One prevalent challenge and problem is the difficulty of properly engaging students using the textbook, especially because of a pack of information being added and the static images of the content may be unable to capture student attention and their understanding. Therefore, this problem has sparked the idea for the development of SciARLearn: Augmented Reality Approach for Science Education. This paper focuses on enhancing interactive learning by integrating the augmented reality approach in education. This can be implemented by using the marker-based augmented reality method, which integrates the scanning process of augmented reality with the predefined markers. Cards with different images are used, acting as the target marker for deploying the 3D virtual content. Therefore, teachers who teach Science subjects will be able to utilize an interactive way of teaching as an alternative to inefficient traditional teaching methods by using AR.

title: CodeShield: A Hybrid AI System for Robust Code Similarity Detection and Interpretable Code Quality Analysis
authors: Abhinav Singh, Dumpa Revanth Venkata Sai, Hanoch Yedidya Karkada, S. Geetha
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent and Innovative Technologies in Computing, Electrical and Electronics (IITCEE)
abstract: In the rapidly growing digital era, code plagiarism has evolved, with subtle structural and semantic changes which are applied to evade traditional text-matching detectors. There is a growing need for tools that not only identify such changes, but also provide constructive feedback on code quality in the educational sector. CodeShield is an AI-powered system that addresses these challenges by combining text-based, abstract syntax tree (AST), and semantic similarity techniques for robust, multi-layered code similarity detection. It integrates static analysis to provide standards-based code quality feedback. Leveraging large-scale datasets like CodeSearchNet-Python, CodeShield delivers comprehensive similarity scoring and actionable insights for arbitrary Python code, enabling flexible and extensible use in both academic and professional settings.

title: Syntactic analysis and modification of smart contract code using an adapted version aimed at optimizing gas consumption in for loops
authors: Zilga Heritiana Randriamiarison, Hajarisena Razafimahatratra, Nicolas Raft Razafindrakoto, Yassine Rhazali
publish: 2025 International Conference on Electrical and Computer Engineering Researches (ICECER)
abstract: A smart contract is a program deployed on a blockchain network. It is secure, transparent, and immutable after deployment. Executing a smart contract requires gas to function. Gas is consumed during both deployment and transaction on the network. Optimizing gas consumption is one of the most difficult challenges to master. Many approaches have been proposed; however, it remains a significant a challenge. The purpose of this article is to explain gas consumption in the blockchain network and to present an approach for optimizing gas usage in a For() loop. This work was carried out in several stages, including: parsing solidity code according to its version, implementing a Control Flow Graph to identify loops to be optimized, and finally applying a smart contract code optimization algorithm. Our approach is robust, scalable, and easily adaptable. Experiments show that any syntactic or version change to a smart contract impacts gas consumption. The approach is practical and has distinctive characteristics. Its strength lies in its ability to optimize smart contracts across all solidity version from 0.5.x to 0.8.x.

title: Chat Analyzer: A Data-Driven Approach to Understanding Communication Patterns
authors: Supriya Suman, Nimisha Verma, Garvit Verma, Puja Bhandari, Manoj Diwakar, Neeraj Kumar Pandey
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Internet of Things: Smart Innovation and Usages (IoT-SIU)
abstract: With messaging applications like WhatsApp, Telegram, Facebook Messenger and likewise increasing at an exponential rate, there is a rapidly developing need to study and make insights out of chat data. Chats in online spaces form an integral aspect of today’s communication that yield immense text data, which can be analyzed to assess linguistic patterns, social activities, and even mental preferences. This work presents a Chat Analyzer, a tool that analyzes chat logs from various messaging platforms to reveal informative patterns, sentiment trends, and activity distributions. Natural Language Processing (NLP), data visualization, and statistical techniques are employed through the tool to offer an interactive interface for analyzing user chat behavior. Techniques like tokenization, topic modeling, and sentiment analysis are employed to reveal finer insights into the conversation dynamics. The article presents architecture, methodology, and empirical findings from actual chat datasets. Additionally, we present potential applications within psychology research, communications research, and digital forensic examination. We further signal the ethical implication of chat analysis regarding user privacy as well as data security. Lastly, the paper signals the limitations of existing chat analytics tools and suggests improvements toward greater accuracy, scalability, and ease of use.

title: Introduction of Regional Power Grid Battery Storage Project
authors: Yu-Hsuan Tai, Ting-Yu Liu, Ming-Jhe Yeh, Hsin-Chang Lee, Hsu-Liang Tsai, Ming-Hung Huang
publish: 2025 25th Conference of the Electric Power Supply Industry (CEPSI)
abstract: On September 2022, government approved the "Regional Power Grid Battery Storage" as part of the domestic forward-looking infrastructure demonstration project. This project involves selecting secondary substations (S/S) in regions with high renewable energy penetration to deploy the "Energy Storage System (ESS)", "Energy Management System (EMS)" and "Diesel Generator (DG)". These installations aim to regulate power system quality during normal operations and provide emergency backup power in case of upstream transmission system failures due to major disasters.When a blackout occurs, Taiwan Power Company (Taipower, TPC) can monitor and remotely operate the system via its Feeder Dispatch and Control Center (FDCC). Using a segmented power supply strategy and phased loading approach, the system can integrate with photovoltaic (PV) generation along the feeder to maintain partial independent power supply for local townships. This ensures that critical users and essential residential electricity needs within the region are met.Taipower has completed the deployment of "Regional Power Grid Battery Storage" sites in eight secondary substations. The operation modes can be categorized into three main types:I. Grid-connected mode: When the upstream transmission system is operating normally, the power conversion system (PCS) operates in "grid following mode", functioning as a current source to regulate feeder power. This mode supports such as system Automatic Frequency Control (AFC), Voltage-Active Power (or Reactive Power) Control, Smoothing of renewable energy.II. Islanding mode: While transmission system black out, the PCS switches to "Grid Forming Mode", playing the role of a voltage source. In this mode, EMS could load and connect each section of the feeder one by one, restart the disconnected PV, and maintain the balance of the regional power grid with the PV power output. Furthermore, the DG might start operation based on the remaining battery power.III. Restoration procedure: Once the upstream transmission system is restored, FDCC ensures that EMS has received the synchronization signals from the micro Phasor Measurement Units (PMU), FDCC then switch off feeder circuit breaker (FCB), transitioning the system from "Islanding Mode" back to "Grid-Connected Mode".

title: Fast Percolation Centrality Approximation with Importance Sampling
authors: Antonio Cruciani, Leonardo Pellegrina
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Data Mining (ICDM)
abstract: In this work we present PERCIS, an algorithm based on Importance Sampling to approximate the percolation centrality of all the nodes of a graph. Percolation centrality is a generalization of betweenness centrality to attributed graphs, and is a useful measure to quantify the importance of the vertices in a contagious process or to diffuse information. However, it is impractical to compute it exactly on modern-sized networks. First, we highlight key limitations of state-of-the-art samplingbased approximation methods for the percolation centrality, showing that in most cases they cannot achieve accurate solutions efficiently. Then, we propose and analyze a novel sampling algorithm based on Importance Sampling, proving tight sample size bounds to achieve high-quality approximations. Our extensive experimental evaluation shows that PercIS computes high-quality estimates and scales to large real-world networks, while significantly outperforming, in terms of sample sizes, accuracy and running times, the state-of-the-art.

title: Optimizing Supply Chain Management through Resilience and Capabilities Integration
authors: Shobhit Goyal, Febin Prakash, A. Thangamani, Ramesh Saini, Senthamilselvan K, Madhur Grover
publish: 2025 International Conference on AI-Driven STEM Education and Learning Technologies (AISTEMEDU)
abstract: The supply chain management is crucial in the delivery of efficiency, competitiveness, and sustainability within the international markets. It has become critical to integrate the resilience and capacity to respond to uncertainties and disruption. Nonetheless, conventional methods usually lack the flexibility, are not adequate to evaluate risks, and do not have the flexibility to achieve the tradeoff between resilience and performance. In order to eliminate such challenges, the present study presents a Resilient Supply Chain Framework (RSCF) that combines a Multi-Objective Genetic Algorithm with System Dynamics Modeling, allowing to optimize dynamic resilience. The framework aids decision-makers to simulate cases of disruption, trade-offs, and the adaptive strategy. As per the method applied, there is better resilience, fewer vulnerabilities, and operational continuity at an approximate cost. Results indicate that RSCF provides an effective solution to the efficient, robust and adaptable balance of modern supply chains ensuring sustainability of competitiveness. RSCF realized a 3.2-day reduced recovery time, a 95 percent supplier reliability increase, a 7.8 percent reduction in operations costs, and a 6 percent satisfying rates.

title: EN-CBAM: A Novel Deep Learning Approach For Accurate Respiratory Disease Detection
authors: Ganapriya V, Tejaswini N, Parvathi C, Nisarga Hospet, Rakshita G Hiremath
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent and Innovative Technologies in Computing, Electrical and Electronics (IITCEE)
abstract: Rrespiratory diseases are common throughout the world. Diagnostic instruments are important for the proper detection and treatment of respiratory diseases. A timely diagnosis of respiratory disease is essential. This paper investigates the performance of the deep learning model based on the EfficientNet-B0 baseline and Convolutional Block Attention Module (CBAM), to detect diseases such as pneumonia, COVID-19, and tuberculosis with high accuracy. The goal is to achieve an accuracy of over 90 % on a dataset of chest X-ray images, which would significantly improve upon the state of-the-art results in this field. For comparison, existing models such as VGG, Res-Net, and Dense-Net have reported accuracies ranging from 80 % to 88 % on similar datasets. Meanwhile, more complex models like Capsule Networks and Attention-based models have achieved accuracies up to 89 % and 90 %, respectively. By pushing the boundaries of accuracy in respiratory disease diagnosis, this model has the potential to make a meaningful impact on healthcare outcomes and encourage healthcare experts in providing timely and effective treatment.

title: Anomaly Detection for Industrial Equipment Using Patchcore on Audio and Vibration Spectrograms
authors: Hinako Shimizu, Yano Shouhei
publish: 2026 IEEE 16th Annual Computing and Communication Workshop and Conference (CCWC)
abstract: In manufacturing industries, early detection of equipment anomalies is essential for quality assurance and cost reduction. Conventional inspection often relies on human sensory evaluation, which is labor-intensive and error-prone. This study investigates an unsupervised anomaly detection approach based on PatchCore, applied to spectrogram images generated from both audio and vibration signals. A custom sensor device was developed to capture vibrations in the range of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{0. 1}-\mathbf{1 0 0 0 ~ H z}$</tex> and acoustic signals in the range of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{1 0 0}-\mathbf{2 4, 0 0 0}$</tex> Hz. The collected time-series data are converted into spectrograms and analyzed with a pre-trained CNN backbone followed by coreset sampling. In experiments, a fan system was used as a testbed where imbalance was artificially introduced by attaching rubber bands to the blades under different airflow levels. Evaluation metrics such as AUC and precision-recall confirmed that the proposed method achieves over 95 % detection accuracy when combining audio and vibration data, while single-modality results varied depending on the anomaly strength. These results demonstrate that PatchCore-based spectrogram analysis is effective for industrial anomaly detection and preventive maintenance applications.

title: YOLO-Driven Brain Tumor Detection and Segmentation in MRI
authors: Premnath K, S. Pandiaraj
publish: 2025 World Conference on Cutting-Edge Science and Technology (WCCEST)
abstract: In order to make a proper diagnosis of brain tumor at the early stages of the disease, it is necessary to have precise information concerning the location and size of tumors as well as the information about the type of tumor and the disease occurrence. Human interpretation of MRI is still not feasible given that it takes too much processing time and is characterized by random human error that renders the process unfavorable in medical diagnostics. The study introduces a deep learning-based system of brain tumor detection to obtain greater detection rates and performance. The article gives a comparison of the YOLO based segmentation versus CNN classification in the detection of brain tumors and their location within MRI images. Researchers evaluated their system on many cases of brain tumor images with various tumor size and shape and contrast of intensity. The model performance is also improved by the normalization skills and data augmentation processes. YOLO v8 real time object detection has a higher accuracy in tumor feature extraction since it reduces the false positives needed to provide medical diagnostic accuracy. Deep learning-based detection models have been found to be beneficial to medical image and clinical decision and have demonstrated strong gains when applied in tumor detection experiments hence reflecting a bright future application in the field. By means of these technological developments, medical practitioners are able to attain greater accuracy in the diagnosis area besides enhanced curative techniques to attain improved health care outcomes since they can be used to enhance therapeutic strategies.

title: Bhutan’s First CubeSat Mission for Space Environment Observation: On-Orbit Magnetometry, Thermal Profiling, and Radiation Dosimetry
authors: Lhendup Thinley Dorji, Chencho Dorji, Srijana Gajmer, Pooja Lepcha, Karma Wangchuk
publish: 2025 International Conference on Electrical and Computer Engineering Researches (ICECER)
abstract: The paper presents Bhutan’s first Cube- Sat mission dedicated to space environment observation through on-orbit magnetometry, thermal profiling, and radiation dosimetry. The mission aims to develop a compact, reliable sensor system capable of simultaneously measuring in-situ gamma radiation and Earth’s magnetic field with high accuracy and resolution. Collected data are stored in onboard flash memory and periodically downlinked to the ground station. The significance of the paper is to establish a valuable repository for geophysical and space environment research within Bhutan. The original contribution lies in the design of the CubeSat. Future research directions could include better communication mechanism of the model.

title: Challenges from Migration of Carbon Free Fuel - Hydrogen in Macau
authors: Lu Chang, Ngan Choi Pan
publish: 2025 25th Conference of the Electric Power Supply Industry (CEPSI)
abstract: The natural gas consumption in Macau, as a better substitute for liquid fossil fuels, is currently undergoing a significant increase. Besides the demand for power generation, Macau is using natural gas in various fields, including vehicles, housing, and industries. The substantial consumption of natural gas in northern mainland China results in significant supply shortages, which, in turn, affect the sustainability and reliability of power generation in Macau. To meet environmentally friendly goals and improve power generation performance, one promising solution for mitigating natural gas consumption and decreasing CO<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</inf> emissions in gas-fired power plants is the blending of natural gas with hydrogen. This paper primarily examines the current frameworks of hydrogen blending technology development in both the power generation field and city pipeline transportation. Additionally, it will explore the considerations of hydrogen integration on existing infrastructure, such as the percentage of hydrogen in natural gas, pressure, and the construction standards of pipelines. Considering Macau's unique characteristics and urban planning, this study analyzes hydrogen blending technology and investigates two different categories of standards: European Standards and Chinese National Standards. Moreover, it points out the possibilities, limitations, potential challenges, and benefits of applying hydrogen injection technology to Macau's natural gas pipelines, offering recommendations for policy and technical adaptations necessary for a successful transition in Macau's pipelines.

title: Reconfiguration of Busbar Protection System During Renewal
authors: Lorenz Bäni, Ya Ping Ji, Sow Meng Lian, Wei Wen Lim, Kar Eng Tan, Der Chyuan Chung
publish: 2025 25th Conference of the Electric Power Supply Industry (CEPSI)
abstract: Numerical Busbar Protection (BBP) systems are critical to ensuring the reliability, security and stability of modern electrical power systems. The first numerical BBP system in Singapore was commissioned with the nation’s first 400kV switchgear in 1998 and the system have been widely implemented in the transmission network since. As the early batch of numerical BBP systems have reached the end of their service life, Singapore Power has embarked on a program to renew these aging systems in 2023.This paper discusses the risks and challenges associated with BBP system renewal works and focus on the key mitigation strategy that has been implemented to ensure a smooth and efficient upgrade to a new, more advanced BBP system – progressive reconfiguration of old BBP systems to optimize the availability of busbar protection throughout the course of renewal works.

title: Design and Implementation of a Multi-Parameter Health Monitoring SoC using RISC-V and TinyML for Real-Time Edge-Based Diagnostics
authors: Sahana B, Manju Devi, Neha S, Soubhagya M
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent and Innovative Technologies in Computing, Electrical and Electronics (IITCEE)
abstract: The increasing demand for continuous cardiac monitoring has created the need for portable, low-power, and intelligent health-tracking systems capable of operating reliably in edge environments. Traditional cloud-dependent solutions suffer from latency, bandwidth limitations, and privacy concerns, making them unsuitable for real-time diagnostics. This work presents a compact multi-parameter health monitoring System-on-Chip (SoC) implemented on an ESP32-C3 RISC-V microcontroller with integrated TinyML for on-device analytics. The system acquires ECG signals using the AD8232 module and measures heart rate and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{SpO}_{2}$</tex> using the MAX30100 optical sensor, while HRV metrics are computed directly from ECG-derived RR intervals. A lightweight TinyML model deployed on the microcontroller enables local classification of cardiac conditions without cloud support, achieving low latency and reduced energy consumption. Experimental evaluation demonstrates stable ECG waveform detection, accurate heart rate, and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{SpO}_{2}$</tex> estimation, and reliable HRV computation, supported by real-time visualization through serial and dashboard interfaces. The results validate the feasibility of a low-cost, scalable, and edge-based IoMT system capable of early detection of cardiac anomalies such as arrhythmia and hypoxemia, highlighting its potential for wearable and remote healthcare applications.

title: Task Offloading in Edge-Cloud Computing: Optimal Algorithm and Complexity for the Backlogged Case
authors: Ye Xia
publish: 2026 IEEE 16th Annual Computing and Communication Workshop and Conference (CCWC)
abstract: We study a task offloading problem in edge-cloud computing environments with task backlogs. Specifically, we consider a scenario in which an event at the edge triggers a computationally intensive program that can be partitioned into a collection of independent tasks. The objective is to assign each task to either the edge or one of the cloud servers in order to minimize the overall completion time of all tasks. A distinguishing feature of this problem is that, at the time of task assignment, both the edge and cloud servers may already have backlogs of previously assigned tasks. We propose an algorithm that builds on a dynamic programming solution for knapsack-like problems and establish a theoretical framework proving its optimality. Additionally, we conduct a detailed complexity analysis and show that the algorithm's complexity is independent of the backlog sizes. This is a significant result, as backlogs can often be much larger than the total size of the newly arrived tasks.

title: A Dynamic Systems Approach to the Impact of Lifestyle and Sociodemographic Factors on Diabetes Incidence
authors: Grizelda Audria Wijaya, Irma Palupi
publish: 2025 2nd Beyond Technology Summit on Informatics International Conference (BTS-I2C)
abstract: This paper proposes a dynamic systems model based on Ordinary Differential Equations (ODEs) to analyze the impact of lifestyle and sociodemographic factors on diabetes incidence. The study addresses the need for interpretable and predictive models that capture the complex interactions among factors such as physical activity, diet, age, and gender. Publicly available datasets are used in parameter estimation using Random Forest Regression (RFR), identifying key transition rates among three population compartments: Susceptible, Prediabetic, and Diabetic. The ODE simulation, solved using the fourth-order Runge–Kutta (RK4) method, is evaluated with three simulation scenarios. The baseline model achieved (R<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup> = 0.80), the fixed 20% disturbance achieved the best alignment (R<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup> = 0.93), and the random 0–20% disturbance showed slightly lower agreement (R<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup> = 0.91) due to fluctuating disturbance intensity. These results demonstrate that consistent behavioral improvement produces more stable and sustainable outcomes than irregular disturbance, offering interpretable and data-driven insights for preventive health strategies.

title: Ensemble-Based Big-Data Analytics for Evaluating New Hampshire's Congressional Districting Fairness
authors: Sara Asgari, Farzane Asgari, Quinn Basewitz, Ethan Bergmann, Jackson Brogsol, Martina Kampel, Becca Keating, Dylan Torrance, Sadegh Asgari, Diana Davis
publish: 2026 IEEE 16th Annual Computing and Communication Workshop and Conference (CCWC)
abstract: Redistricting fairness has become a central concern in U.S. elections, with ensemble-based methods emerging as a leading tool. However, most existing studies focus on highdistrict and two-party voting settings. This paper conducts an ensemble-based, geospatial big data analysis to evaluate the political geography and fairness of congressional districting in New Hampshire, an analytically difficult two-district swing state with a large Independent-voting population, and highlights the unique challenges and solutions for similar scenarios. Our end-to-end pipeline, implemented in Python using custom code built on GeoPandas, GerryChain, NetworkX, MAUP, and NumPy, allows automated data preparation, graph modeling, MCMCbased ensemble generation, vote simulations, spatial visualizations, and ensemble-based diagnostics at scale. We construct a unified multi-election, precinct-level dataset, by preparing and reconciling election and geographic records, and perform a longitudinal analysis of vote-share distributions to characterize the state's evolving partisan landscape. We then develop graphbased models of precinct adjacency and apply the ReCom algorithm to generate thousands of valid plans under various constraints. We, finally, conduct various outlier analyses and assess outcome sensitivity to Independent voter behavior. Our results show that while conventional fairness measures provide inconsistent or inconclusive signals in this two-district setting, spatial diagnostics reveal signs of potential incumbent-favoring redistricting. Simulation experiments further demonstrate the pivotal role of Independent voters in this swing state. We also find that when imposing county-split constraints in ensemble generation, the enacted plan falls toward the high-split end of the distribution. Overall, our integrated methodology offers practical guidance for evaluating redistricting and political geography in New Hampshire and can be readily applied to other challenging and low-district settings, providing a scalable template for datadriven redistricting analysis.

title: Lightweight cryptosystem for mMTC and IoT application
authors: Peter Farkaš, Jakub Otruba, Tomáš Janvars, Katarína Farkašová, Frederik Pavelka, Lukáš Patrnčiak
publish: 2025 International Conference on Electrical and Computer Engineering Researches (ICECER)
abstract: It is expected that in 6G networks mMTC will be a substantial part of the overall traffic. On the other hand the security in networks of devices with constrained resources and computational capabilities is an actual challenging problem. Therefore there is a vivid research in the area of lightweight cryptographic systems suitable for IoT, WSN and similar use cases. Stream cypher systems form one promising branch of such research. Such systems are based on pseudorandom number generators (PRNGs) which could be synchronized in transmitters and receivers. Recently a True RNG (TRNG) was proposed which is using asynchronous ring oscillators and therefore it cannot be directly used in stream cypher systems. It did pass NIST SP 800-22 test suite. In this paper a lightweight cryptosystem together with connected protocol is proposed which is using PRNGs inspired by the mentioned TRNG and physical layer security (PLS) for key generation.

title: IoT PCAP Traffic Data Attack Analysis Using Ensemble Machine Learning
authors: Raymond Frederico, Fedrick Tasripin, Wesley Benedict, Christopher Limawan
publish: 2025 2nd Beyond Technology Summit on Informatics International Conference (BTS-I2C)
abstract: The proliferation of Internet of Things (IoT) devices has created a vast landscape of emerging security threats and cyberattacks against IoT-based networks. Guaranteeing the safe behavior of IoT systems depends critically on the identification of such threats. In this work, we present an IoT threat detection system utilizing machine learning techniques applied to PCAP (Packet Capture) traffic data. The system recognizes prospective security issues and categorizes network traffic using a mix of multiple machine learning techniques. Experimental evaluation on the Edge-IIoTset2023 dataset demonstrates that among individual classifiers, Random Forest achieved the highest accuracy of 94.64%, while ensemble methods outperformed single models. In particular, the Voting Ensemble combining top performing models reached an accuracy of 95.73%, representing an improvement of over 1% compared to the best performing standalone model. These findings highlight that ensemble learning provides a more robust and scalable means of IoT network protection. Future work will explore advanced balancing techniques and real-time deployment scenarios to further improve detection capabilities.

title: IoT-Driven Hydrological Risk Assessment and Alert Mechanism
authors: Abhishek US, Ayush B A, Mallikarjuna, Preetham V A, Vijayashri V Belgaonkar, Poongothai C
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent and Innovative Technologies in Computing, Electrical and Electronics (IITCEE)
abstract: Flooding is a common and destructive natural disaster, with increasing frequency and severity due to climate change. Traditional flood management methods often rely on outdated technology, leading to delayed responses and compromised safety. To address these challenges, the Internet of Things (IoT) has introduced innovative solutions, with Internet of Things (IoT) technology playing a key role. IOT is a specialized cellular communication technology designed for lowpower, wide-area applications, making it ideal for connecting sensors across remote areas. Its extended range and low power consumption enable the deployment of long-term water level sensors, even in areas with unreliable connectivity. An IOTbased flood detection and alert system offer real-time monitoring by continuously collecting and analyzing data from strategically placed sensors. When water levels exceed predefined thresholds, alerts are sent to communities and authorities through SMS, email, or app notifications, allowing timely evacuations and coordinated emergency responses. This system also collects historical data to improve the understanding of flood risk and inform future infrastructure planning, helping mitigate flood impacts. Using IOT, the system transforms flood management from reactive to proactive, enhancing disaster preparedness and resilience.

title: Performance Degradation of an Optimal LQ Controller based on a Reduced-Order Observer Designed via the Sylvester Algebraic Equation
authors: Verica Radisavljevic-Gajic, Faris Omar Mohmoud Safieh
publish: 2025 International Conference on Electrical and Computer Engineering Researches (ICECER)
abstract: This paper considers the problem of an optimal linear-quadratic (LQ) control based on a reduced-order observer (ROO) designed via the Sylvester equation. Since observers estimate state variables with certain errors, the optimal performance gets degraded in the case when observer estimates are used for feedback instead of the system states. In this work, the analytical expression is derived for the optimal LQ criterion loss for steady state optimization. The paper derives a formula based on the least square method for the selection of initial conditions of ROO that can drastically reduce the optimal performance loss. Since in some applications of ROO with LQ optimal controllers the performance loss can be very high it is mandatory that the corresponding applications be reexamined in the spirit of the results reported in this paper.

title: Exploring Cardinalities in Online Social Media with Roles and Contexts for Building Foundational Models of Trust
authors: Fahad Alsulami, Siddhartha Bhattacharyya
publish: 2026 IEEE 16th Annual Computing and Communication Workshop and Conference (CCWC)
abstract: Cognitive trust and its foundational concepts are essential for understanding how trust evolves across interpersonal and digital environments. These concepts have been extensively investigated for decades across psychology, marketing, and computer science. In contrast, digital trust is a more recent phenomenon, building upon three cognitive principles: the relationship between a trustor and a trustee, the risks and uncertainties inherent in interaction, and prior experiences together with contextual factors that shape perceived integrity and reliability. Various computational models of trust have been proposed for digital environments, including human-computer interactions, networked systems, social media applications, and IoT devices. However, within social media, limited attention has been devoted to categorizing trust relationships into cardinalities. This step is crucial for understanding the structural evolution of trust. Therefore, this research examines how human-to-human trust relationships manifest across these cardinalities, including one-to-one, one-to-many, many-to-one, and many-to-many. We empirically analyze these four cardinalities, map them to contexts and roles on social media platforms, and incorporate them into a generalizable framework that supports the future development of computational trust models. The quantitative computation of trust, however, lies beyond the scope of this paper.

title: Ensuring Privacy and Data Anonymization in Smart Homes for Secure Data Sharing
authors: Yugandhar Suthari, Karthik Babu Manam
publish: 2025 International Conference on AI-Driven STEM Education and Learning Technologies (AISTEMEDU)
abstract: The blistering development of Internet of Things (IoT) devices and smart home technologies has increased the urgency to protect the privacy and safety of data transferred in these ecosystems. Smart houses generate large volumes of incredibly sensitive data, such as personal behavioral trends, health data and home security data, which are especially vulnerable to privacy breaches. The paper explores the approaches to maintaining privacy and anonymization of data in smart home scenarios, focusing on the advanced cryptographic protocols, data masking protocols, and anonymization algorithms. In such a manner, smart home systems can support the secure data exchange between devices, services, and final users, making it less likely that unauthorized access may occur. Different privacy-saving strategies are discussed, and their effectiveness in ensuring the anonymity of users who do not affect the performance of smart home services are critically evaluated. The paper suggests a holistic model of secure data sharing that combines data anonymization, encryption, and decentralized governance processes, which is a solid approach to privacy concerns reduction in an ever-connected smart home environment.

title: Low-Light Image Enhancement via SwinIR-Based Denoising and Attention U-Net with Laplacian Pyramid Decomposition
authors: Chandu Vaidya, Gunjan Mohod, Anushka Hedaoo, Karishma Meshram, Swayam Shahu, Sourabh Singh Chauhan
publish: 2025 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence for Computing, Astronomy and Renewable Energy (AICARE)
abstract: In the enhancement of low-light images, noise, low contrast, color distortion, and loss of details have remained great issues that have not been overcome by computer vision. The traditional products like gamma correction, CLAHE, and histogram equalization are used to improve the brightness, but they are incapable of finding the balance between boosting and reducing noises. Recent Retinex, GAN and encoder decoder processes show improvements but are incapable of preserving fine details of natural color restoration. The current paper suggests a hybrid model that comprises SwinIR-denoising, UNet reconstruction using attention-focused skip connections, and guided filtering with Laplacian decomposition. It is stated that the model trained in the LOL dataset achieved PSNR of 20.21 dB, SSIM of 0.774 and MSE of 0.0134 in a 58 -minute training period and thus it has good brightness correction abilities, detail preservation and realistic texture reproduction in low-light images.

title: Empowering Smallholder Tea Farmers in Sri Lanka with AI-Driven Precision Agriculture
authors: K.M.L.J. Kumara, B.M.G.S.T.S.K. Wickramasinghe
publish: 2025 International Conference on Advances in Technology and Computing (ICATC)
abstract: Sri Lanka’s tea industry relies heavily on smallholder farmers, who produce over two-thirds of the nation’s tea. However, these farmers face persistent challenges such as soil nutrient degradation, crop diseases, labor shortages, and fluctuating market conditions. This study investigates the potential of AI-driven precision agriculture to address these issues by developing a low-cost, farmer-friendly digital platform integrating artificial intelligence, IoT sensors, and mobile technology. Adopting a convergent mixed-methods approach, the study combined quantitative data from 207 smallholder tea farmers across 11 districts with qualitative insights from interviews, focus groups, and field demonstrations. Key findings reveal strong digital readiness among farmers, with 97.6% owning smartphones and 84% demonstrating app usage comfort. Despite this, affordability remains a significant barrier, with 87.4% unwilling to spend more than Rs. 5,000 on technology. High interest was recorded in AI-powered disease detection (25.8%) and soil monitoring (21.7%), validating the platform’s design priorities. Connectivity constraints in rural areas underscore the need for offline-capable solutions. Qualitative insights emphasized trust-building, peer validation, and localized training as critical to adoption. The research concludes that a mobile-first, offline-enabled precision agriculture platform, with phased feature rollouts and affordable pricing models, can substantially enhance productivity, sustainability, and decision-making for smallholder tea farmers. These findings contribute to the discourse on digital inclusion and rural agricultural innovation in South Asia.

title: Life Cycle Assessment for Substations and Activities for Reduction of Greenhouse Gas Emissions
authors: Tatsuya Watanabe, Shinki Noguchi
publish: 2025 25th Conference of the Electric Power Supply Industry (CEPSI)
abstract: With the increasing global awareness of decarbonization and being environment-friendliness, sulfur hexafluoride (SF<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">6</inf>) alternative solutions, Life Cycle Assessment (LCA), etc. have been highlighted in the substation sector at the recent international conferences. The LCA is a method of assessing the environmental impact of substation equipment over its life cycle.
