To counteract these attacks or threats several security mechanisms have evolved with different approaches. These approaches focus on enhancing the FL-IoT environment by preserving data confidentiality or security by checking the authentication of users using IoT devices. With the advancement in the development of edge devices, maintaining the synchronization between FL and IoT is critical for achieving desirable intelligence in next generation networks.

title: Quantifying Domain Discrepancy in Skeleton-Based Industrial Action Recognition Using Maximum Mean Discrepancy
authors: Zhafira Hajar, Chao-Lung Yang, Wiwik Anggraeni
publish: 2025 2nd Beyond Technology Summit on Informatics International Conference (BTS-I2C)
abstract: Cross-domain human action recognition remains a difficult challenge when the camera viewpoint and recording environment differ between datasets. This study examines skeleton based features as a bridge between a source dataset of Electronic Device Assembly (EDA) activities and a target dataset of factory CCTV footage. The goal is to quantify how much the upper body pose distributions differ across domains, rather than to train an action recognition model. Upper body keypoints are extracted with a single YOLOv8m-Pose detector, normalized by shoulder midpoint and span, and summarized in short temporal windows. A synthetic viewpoint augmentation rotates the normalized upper body skeletons through a range of pitch and yaw angles to emulate the top view of the factory cameras. The domain discrepancy between source and target window features is then measured with the linear Maximum Mean Discrepancy statistic. Results show that a pitch of about sixty degrees and zero yaw gives the smallest MMD value with respect to the factory dataset, which indicates the closest match to the top-down CCTV geometry. These findings suggest that MMD on upper body skeleton windows can act as a simple diagnostic metric for domain similarity before later transfer learning on action labels.

title: Design of a Linear Quadratic Gaussian Control System for the Stability of an Octocopter
authors: Aditya Andreans, Fadilah Akbar, Lutfi Hakim, Ahmad Hanif Asyhar
publish: 2025 2nd Beyond Technology Summit on Informatics International Conference (BTS-I2C)
abstract: One type of UAV, the octocopter, possesses a nonlinear and inherently unstable dynamic system, thereby requiring a well-designed control system to achieve stable flight. This study aims to implement a Linear Quadratic Gaussian (LQG) control design to stabilize the octocopter during hovering conditions. The LQG method combines the Kalman Filter, which estimates unmeasurable state variables, and the Linear Quadratic Regulator (LQR), which controls the process using the estimated states. The LQG controller design in this research is carried out using MATLAB. The simulation results show that the maximum overshoot value at all three angles is less than 0.1 rad and requires less than 0.2 seconds to reach stability.

title: 5G NR Coverage Planning for Vehicle-to-Infrastructure Services Using N40 Band: A Case Study on Ahmad Yani-Tunjungan Road, Surabaya
authors: Kevin Aditya Pratama, Tri Agus Djoko Kuntjoro, Arrizky Ayu Faradila Purnama, Muhammad Zulfiqri Firdiansyah
publish: 2025 2nd Beyond Technology Summit on Informatics International Conference (BTS-I2C)
abstract: This study presents a simulation-based design of a 5G New Radio (NR) network operating at the 2.3 GHz (n40) band to support Vehicle-to-Infrastructure (V2I) services along the Ahmad Yani-Tunjungan corridor in Surabaya. Coverage and capacity analyses were performed using ATOLL, evaluating SS-RSRP, SS-SINR, and downlink throughput as key performance indicators. The results show that the network achieves an average SS-RSRP of-82.43 dBm and an average SS-SINR of 18.49 dB, with most of the route maintaining strong and stable signal quality. Link-budget and propagation analysis yield a maximum coverage distance of 933 m, while data-rate computation indicates a downlink capacity of 159.95 Mbps. The simulated Peak RLC Throughput averages 219.27 Mbps, confirming sufficient capacity to support V2I traffic under peak vehicle density conditions of approximately 9,675 vehicles/hour. With the deployed 10-site configuration, the network provides uniform coverage and reliable performance across the corridor. This case study offers a validated 5G NR network plan for Surabaya and proposes a methodology for evaluating frequency-band trade-offs in urban V2I environments.

title: A Comparative Study of MongoDB on MongoDB Atlas, AWS EC2, Local Docker Containers, and Local Self Machine
authors: Mallegowda M, Kamaljeeth Anand, C S Darshan, Abhishek D P, Yogesh K N, Anita Kanavalli
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent and Innovative Technologies in Computing, Electrical and Electronics (IITCEE)
abstract: MongoDB is a popular NoSQL database known for scalability and high throughput, but its performance depends heavily on the underlying infrastructure. This study presents a comparative benchmarking of MongoDB across four deployment environments: MongoDB Atlas, AWS EC2, Docker containers on local VMs, and native local installations. Using YCSB and mongo-perf, we evaluate read-heavy, write-heavy, and mixed workloads, measuring throughput, latency, disk I/O, resource utilization, scalability, and cost-efficiency. Results show that Atlas, while easy to use, underperforms compared to EC2 and local setups, which achieve higher throughput and flexibility under complex workloads. These findings provide guidance for database architects and system designers in selecting deployment strategies optimized for performance, scalability, and cost.

title: Self-Supervised Learning: The Future of Unlabeled Data in Machine Learning
authors: T. Raghu, Himanshu Sharma, Shubham Kshirsagar, Mrunalini Buradkar, Rajkumar Bhagat, R. Anusuya
publish: 2025 World Conference on Cutting-Edge Science and Technology (WCCEST)
abstract: Heavy use of labeled data has become a huge bottleneck in scaling up machine learning systems. In this paper, I am presenting a self supervised learning (SSL) model that is capable of learning informative, representations on unlabelled data by using a hand-crafted pretext task. The general approach is as follows [insert introducing a few words about the approach: e.g., contrastive learning or masked prediction], pretraining a deep encoder on unlabeled data, and then fine-tuning by downstream task with minimal supervision. We benchmark the method on [insert dataset/domain], showing that it can reach competitive performance compared to full supervised models using a small part of labeled training data. Quite strong and generalizable properties of the learned features are supported by results of comparative experiments, as well as ablation studies. This would strengthen SSL as a feasible option in face of labels scarcity, and future directions of transferring self-supervised to a wider area and deployment scenarios.

title: Toward Seamless IoT Interoperability for Energy and Mobility Integration: A Systemic Perspective
authors: Hanguan Wen, Guodong Huang, Yanjun Liu, Wei Chen, Guohang Xu, Bo Lei
publish: 2025 25th Conference of the Electric Power Supply Industry (CEPSI)
abstract: The convergence of energy and mobility systems, accelerated by electric vehicle adoption and the rise of smart grids, presents significant challenges and opportunities for system interoperability. This paper investigates how Internet of Things (IoT) technologies can enable seamless integration across these domains by addressing technical, semantic, and regulatory barriers. We identify key challenges such as legacy infrastructure, protocol fragmentation, data heterogeneity, and institutional misalignment. To address these, we review enabling solutions including IoT middleware platforms, ontology-driven data models, standardized communication protocols, and privacy-preserving frameworks. A layered architecture is presented to illustrate how modular and scalable integration can be achieved in real-world systems. Additionally, we highlight emerging research opportunities in adaptive architectures, decentralized data governance, semantic automation, and secure edge intelligence. By synthesizing multidisciplinary insights, this work provides a foundation for developing resilient and interoperable energy-mobility ecosystems, supporting the broader goals of sustainability, decarbonization, and digital transformation.

title: Numerical Analysis of a Carbon Fiber Reinforced Polymer (CFRP) Prosthetic Knee-South African Context
authors: Unathi Bosika, Mathapelo Sekwai, Thabo Mathonsi
publish: 2025 International Conference on Electrical and Computer Engineering Researches (ICECER)
abstract: As the need for prosthetic limbs grows, underprivileged populations want affordable, long-lasting, and functional prosthetic options. The goal of this project is to design and simulate a high-performing, prosthetic knee that will increase above-knee amputees' mobility with balance between weight and load bearing. For durability, comfort, and adaptability in a variety of terrains, the design used biomechanical principles, optimal mechanical materials, and structures. In accordance with many safety and quality regulations, the knee is made to endure cyclic stress, offer steady gait support, and require the least amount of maintenance. The knee is designed using four bar linkage system theory and the material used was carbon fiber reinforced composites. The simulation results showed the limit force of approximately 5 000 N. This means that the knee would be suited for individuals who weigh less than 203 kg. Therefore, this meant the prosthetic knee would be suited for lower loads that would avoid failure due to shearing, which is common in composites. The design aims to provide prosthetic balance between load bearing and weight for the minimum individual mass and determine if CFRP can be suitable for developing prosthetic knees. Further studies are proposed to determine sustainable methods of production for CFRP.

title: Brain Tumor MRI Classification Using Weighted Fractional Fourier Transform and K-Nearest Neighbor
authors: Dwi Ratna Sulistyaningrum, Mahmud Yunus, Anugrah Arief Yahya Lubis, Budi Setiyono
publish: 2025 2nd Beyond Technology Summit on Informatics International Conference (BTS-I2C)
abstract: Technological advances can accelerate human work in various fields, including the medical field, such as addressing disorders of the human brain. Digital image processing can be applied to problems such as identifying brain tumors, helping medical technicians diagnose them. In this study, the weighted fractional Fourier transform (WFRFT) and K-Nearest Neighbor (KNN) were implemented for the classification of Magnetic Resonance Imaging (MRI) images of brain tumors. There are four types of MRI images used, namely glioma, meningioma, pituitary, and images without brain tumors. The image classification process consists of 2 processes: training and testing. During training, the training images are extracted using WFRFT features and then selected using Principal Component Analysis (PCA) to obtain lower-dimensional image representations. The selected features are then classified using KNN to obtain a KNN classification model. After obtaining the KNN classification model, the next step in the testing process is to evaluate the test images using the model trained during training. The best results in this study identified brain tumors with an average accuracy of 98.125% using WFRFT feature extraction.

title: Drowsiness Detection in Drivers Based on Eye Aspect Ratio Analysis
authors: Ahmad Wali Satria Bahari Johan, Vessa Rizky Oktavia, Ardian Yusuf Wicaksono
publish: 2025 2nd Beyond Technology Summit on Informatics International Conference (BTS-I2C)
abstract: Driver fatigue is a leading cause of road accidents, particularly during long-distance driving, and necessitates reliable, real-time detection methods to prevent safety compromises. This study presents a drowsiness detection system designed to provide early warnings by analyzing eye-blinking patterns through real-time facial recognition and eye tracking using an in-vehicle camera. The system leverages MediaPipe to extract facial landmarks and compute the Eye Aspect Ratio (EAR), a geometric measure that quantifies eye openness. By employing a threshold-based decision mechanism, the system triggers audible alerts when EAR values indicate signs of drowsiness, such as prolonged or frequent eye closures. Evaluated on a dataset of 667 driver images, the system achieved an accuracy of 85% in drowsiness detection, with an optimal EAR threshold of 0.289 identified for maximizing performance. The approach demonstrates robustness against common real-world challenges, including variations in lighting conditions and camera positioning, offering a non-intrusive, adaptive, and highly reliable solution. By focusing on eye-based biometric features derived from facial landmarks, this system contributes an original and practical advancement in driver monitoring technology, with significant potential to reduce fatigue-related accidents and enhance overall road safety.

title: Graph Neural Networks for Smarter Plant Disease Diagnosis and Classification
authors: K. Ananthajothi, K. Lokesh
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent and Innovative Technologies in Computing, Electrical and Electronics (IITCEE)
abstract: Agriculture is the backbone of any country like India, where this large population rests highly on farming. The data-driven technologies, the deep learning techniques, and Graph Neural Networks (GNNs) have been established as very powerful tools for improvement in agricultural productivity. This paper presents an advanced GNN-based model for plant disease detection and classification. The proposed method uses a dataset of plant images containing leaves to detect and predict apple scab, cercospora leaf spot, late blight, and powdery mildew. Instead of the traditional CNNs and wavelet transformations, GNNs are applied in this study in order to model the spatial relationships between diseased and healthy regions in the plant leaves. These images are then represented as graphs with nodes as representations of the leaf's various parts and edges for their structural associations. GNN-based model learning features from graph-structured data improves disease classification accuracy without degradation in noise and lowquality inputs. Results show that the integration of GNN with plant disease prediction provides a scalable, efficient, and intelligent approach for real-time disease detection. This method will help farmers and agricultural experts diagnose diseases early on, which may lead to timely interventions, reduced crop losses, and improved food security.

title: Forecasting and Modeling of Complex Retail Sales: Evaluating SARIMAX, Prophet, and LSTM models
authors: Brandon A. J. Nieto, Mayra Tualombo, Saba Infante, Aracelis Hernández
publish: 2025 International Conference on Electrical and Computer Engineering Researches (ICECER)
abstract: Time series models are the foundation for studying the behavior of a stochastic process over a period of time. Sales forecasting is a valuable tool for supply chain management, with a direct impact on total costs. Corporación Favorita provides a dataset spanning from 2013 to 2017 that includes daily sales data from over 50 stores. We propose the use of seasonal autoregressive integrated moving average with exogenous regressors (SARIMAX), Facebook Prophet, and long short-term memory network (LSTM) models to evaluate the Corporación Favorita sales forecasting. We aim to assess whether the addition of external factors, such as oil prices and holidays, helps to achieve better results without compromising running time and performance. The results show that Prophet outperformed SARIMAX and LSTM, achieving the highest Relative MAE of 90.21%, where values closer to 100% indicate better predictive accuracy. These findings highlight Prophet's potential for retail demand forecasting, especially when external regressors are incorporated.

title: Analysis of Iron Losses in a Parallelepipedic Ferromagnetic Core under Longitudinal Variable Induction at Industrial Frequencies
authors: Velontsoa Jeannot, Rafanotsimiva Liva Falisoa, Raharinaivo Remi Saray, Rakoto Dominique Gerard, Kall Briant
publish: 2025 International Conference on Electrical and Computer Engineering Researches (ICECER)
abstract: This article is dedicated to analyze the dimensions interventions of a parallelepipedic ferromagnetic domain subjected to a variable longitudinal induction at industrial frequencies on the iron loss of domain. For this purpose, an analytical expression of the active power consumed by the domain is established and identified with that of Steinmetz empirical power. The results of simulation with various thicknesses, combined with experimental results, allowed to conclude that a system with a cast solid core consumes a too great power that a laminated core with variable current for a same voltage. In iron loss term, the domain is equivalent to a conductor, having equivalent length and section, supplied with the induced voltage generated by its surface offered to the magnetic flux.

title: Web-Native Architecture for Real-Time ROV Video Streaming and Analytics Next.js, WebRTC, and Python FastAPI
authors: Mutiara Sabrina R, Erwin Adriono, Yudi Eko Windarto
publish: 2025 2nd Beyond Technology Summit on Informatics International Conference (BTS-I2C)
abstract: Real-time video streaming with sub-second delay is essential for precise teleoperation of remotely operated vehicles (ROVs), where even small latency can degrade maneuverability and operational safety. This work presents a web-native architecture for low-latency ROV monitoring that integrates RTSP acquisition, WebRTC-based delivery, and real-time analytics using Next.js and Python FastAPI. The system bridges RTSP camera outputs to browser playback through a MediaMTX gateway while decoupling the media and control planes to support scalable object detection using YOLOv8. Field evaluation on a Raspberry Pi 4B at 720p shows a clear trade-off between codec efficiency and network resilience. H.264 hardware encoding incurs minimal computational load, while VP8 offers greater robustness under packet loss. Under optimal settings (VP8, 30 Mbps), the system achieves an average glass-to-glass latency of 165 ms and a 320 ms time-to-first-frame, meeting real-time teleoperation requirements. A bitrate sweep (10–40 Mbps) identifies 30 Mbps as the threshold for zero-drop operation. Stress testing further shows graceful degradation under jitter, delay, and packet loss. These results provide a validated browser-native reference architecture along with a quantified analysis of latency, codec behavior, and end-to-end performance for real-time ROV streaming.

title: SASF-ML: Scalable Adaptive Security Framework for Machine Learning
authors: J. Sridhar, M. Giri, E.S. Harshitha, M. Karthik Raja, N. Govardhan, T. Dhana Sree
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent and Innovative Technologies in Computing, Electrical and Electronics (IITCEE)
abstract: Generated huge volume of data through wearable devicesin many crucial applications, hackers used advanced mechanisms to force variety kinds of threats, many of the applications are preferred to ML techniques to identify these kinds of threats but still attackers force threats on ML techniques phases like attacks on training data samples, attacks on features, and attacks on decision boundaries. In this paper we proposed SASF-ML (Scalable Adaptive Security Framework for Machine Learning) to provide privacy and protect data from entire life cycle of ML technique. The method combines DP (differential privacy), HE (hemimorphic encoding), and identification of threats by calculating entropy. APFC (Adaptive Policy and Feedback Controller) mechanism is used to tune the method continuously to identify and protect data from various types of threats. The SASF-ML approach performance is evaluated by using four types of data samples under different constraints. The outcome of SASF-ML approach is calculated by executing with various types of datasets, improves 21 percent robustness, shows 97.1 percent of accuracy, shows only 7.2 percent of overhead, and shows better results than other similar kind of approaches.

title: Data-Driven Super-Resolution Reconstruction of Quasi-Geostrophic Turbulence Via Enhanced Diffusion Model and Fourier Neural Operator
authors: Yanan Guo, Junqiang Song, Xiaoqun Cao, Hongze Leng
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Data Mining (ICDM)
abstract: High-fidelity simulation and reconstruction of physical fields are essential in both scientific research and engineering, yet classical solvers can be prohibitively expensive at resolutions needed to capture fine-scale structures. We propose FNODiffSR, a data-driven super-resolution framework that couples a residual-guided diffusion model with an Adaptive Weighted Fourier Neural Operator (AWFNO). AWFNO models longrange spectral dependencies while selectively emphasizing highfrequency components, and the diffusion module employs a conditional probability-flow ODE instead of stochastic sampling to deterministically bridge low- and high-fidelity representations. Final reconstructions are obtained by integrating this ODE with an adaptive time-stepping solver. Experiments on quasigeostrophic turbulence across varied upsampling and sparsesampling regimes show that FNODiffSR consistently surpasses interpolation and learning-based baselines in reconstruction fidelity, structural similarity, and physical consistency (as assessed by a dimensionless equation-residual), while offering predictable runtime and scalability. These qualities make FNODiffSR a strong candidate for high-quality scientific data recovery and downstream analysis.

title: SmartMed: An IoT-Enabled Pill Dispensing and Monitoring System for Medication Adherence of AFib Patients
authors: Ronaldo C. Maaño, Abegail M. Acuña, Roselyn A. Maaño, Maria Corazon D. Valdeavilla
publish: 2025 International Conference on Electrical and Computer Engineering Researches (ICECER)
abstract: Atrial fibrillation (AFib), a form of cardiac arrhythmia, demands consistent medication adherence to prevent severe complications such as stroke and thromboembolic events. This study introduces an Internet of Things (IoT)-based smart pill dispensing system designed for medication management and caregiver support in patients with AFib, particularly in resource-constrained settings. Using an experienced prototyping-based iterative design approach, SmartMed integrates automated medication scheduling, real-time dose verification, and a mobile application that enables both routine adherence monitoring and remote emergency dispensing by caregivers. The system features an ESP32 microcontroller, ultrasonic sensors for pill retrieval detection, and cloud-based synchronization through Firebase, ensuring seamless communication between the device and mobile platforms. Testing demonstrated 100% adherence rates, significantly outperforming traditional manual medication management (average 78.3%). System Usability Scale (SUS) evaluations yielded an overall score of 86.5/100, reflecting excellent usability and strong user confidence in safety and reliability. Feedback from patients, caregivers, and healthcare professionals highlighted the system's user-friendly interface, effective real-time alerts, and safety mechanisms that prevent accidental double-dosing. SmartMed presents a clinically relevant solution that bridges critical gaps in medication adherence, especially in underserved and rural communities. Future work will focus on integrating wearable health monitoring. This innovation showcases the potential of IoT-enabled healthcare tools to enhance patient outcomes and revolutionize chronic disease management in the digital health era.

title: TransUNet: Hybrid CNN-Transformer Model for Automated Breast Cancer Segmentation in Ultrasound Images
authors: Geerthana Vimalambikaipakan
publish: 2025 International Conference on Advances in Technology and Computing (ICATC)
abstract: Breast cancer is a leading cause of mortality among women worldwide, and early detection is critical for effective treatment. Ultrasound imaging is widely used for diagnosis, but manual interpretation can be slow and error-prone. This study proposes a hybrid TransUNet model combining CNN and transformer encoders to accurately segment breast cancer in ultrasound images. Using the public BUSI dataset with 780 images and annotated masks, extensive data augmentation was applied to improve robustness. The model was trained with a combined Dice and binary cross-entropy loss function for 50 epochs. It achieved a Dice score of 0.6836 and an IoU of 0.5411 on test data, demonstrating promising segmentation accuracy. Future work will investigate comparative evaluation with UNet++, Swin-UNet, and Attention-UNet, and also advanced augmentations, attention mechanisms, and explainability for clinical utility improvement.

title: A Proposed AI-Powered Framework to Mitigate Fraud in Online Loan Application System
authors: Foster Yeboah, George Yeboah, Kofi Bampoh, Owusu Nyarko-Boateng, Jones Yeboah
publish: 2025 International Conference on Electrical and Computer Engineering Researches (ICECER)
abstract: The rapid evolution of digital lending platforms has transformed how individuals access financial services, with e-loan application systems becoming increasingly prevalent across both government and private sectors. These platforms allow users to submit personal information and supporting documents entirely online, enhancing convenience and financial accessibility. However, as adoption expands, financial institutions face growing concerns related to fraud, applicant misrepresentation, and the challenges of evaluating individuals with limited or non-traditional credit histories. To address these issues, this study introduces a structured framework for credit risk evaluation and fraud mitigation within digital loan processing systems. The proposed approach integrates advanced data-driven techniques, centering on a robust classification mechanism that leverages historical application data to enhance decision-making accuracy. A series of decision tree classifiers, ADTree, BFTree, J48, SimpleCart, Random Forest and REPTree were compared using accuracy, sensitivity, and specificity as evaluation metrics. Among them, Random Forest emerged as the most effective, offering reliable classification performance and strong generalization on unseen data. It was incorporated as the core decision-making engine within the AI-powered E-Loan framework. The system uses Random Forest to analyze applicant data, score credit risk, and intelligently route loan request automatically approving low-risk applications while flagging high-risk cases for manual review. This integration ensures improved fraud detection, efficient application handling, and objective credit assessments. By adopting this methodology, financial institutions can streamline operations and ensure more secure, scalable, and trustworthy digital lending services.

title: Text to SQL AI Agentic Workflow Implementations: A Systematic Literature Review
authors: Eranda De Silva, Dinesh Asanka
publish: 2025 International Conference on Advances in Technology and Computing (ICATC)
abstract: This study comprehensively examines recent advancements and persistent challenges in the development of Text-to-SQL systems, specifically focusing on the integration of agentic workflows with a RAG or Graph-Retrieval Augmented Generation (Graph-RAG) approach. The core task of translating natural language queries into Structured Query Language (SQL) statements aims to democratize data access. However, existing methods, including standalone Large Language Models (LLMs), frequently exhibit limitations related to the ambiguity of natural language, the complexity of real-world database schemas, and the generation of factually incorrect or syntactically invalid SQL. This review synthesizes the literature to demonstrate how multi-agentic frameworks with RAG and Graph-RAG architectures address these issues. The decomposition of complex queries into sub-tasks including intent classification, schema linking, and error corrections posited to enhance the system's flexibility, generalization capabilities, and overall reliability. Concurrently, the RAG and Graph-RAG database context approach offers a promising method for grounding LLMs with high-fidelity, structured, and contextual information. By leveraging vector databases or knowledge graphs to represent database schemas and domain-specific knowledge, these RAG systems augment the LLM’s prompt, thereby mitigating hallucinations and improving the accuracy of the generated SQL. TigerVector illustrates the potential of hybrid retrieval methods that combine semantic and relational search within a single graph database, enabling multi-hop reasoning crucial for handling complex enterprise and financial datasets. Despite these advancements, several challenges remain, notably the automation of creating effective, LLM-friendly database representations and achieving fully autonomous continual learning. Future research should focus on fortifying security protocols to facilitate the robust and scalable Text-to-SQL AI Agentic Graph-RAG workflows.

title: Automated Diabetic cum Retinopathy Detection with Using Transfer Learning
authors: Shivani Gusain, Aditya Gusain, Subrat Kothiyal, Ankit Tomar, R K Tripathi
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Internet of Things: Smart Innovation and Usages (IoT-SIU)
abstract: Diabetic Retinopathy-(DR) is a very serious eye diseases which mainly causeD by diabetes and main cause of preventable blindness.DR required early detection to prevent permanent vision loss. Manual examination by doctor is slow, subjective and inconsistent result. This research proposes an optimized VGG19 based deep learning model by using transfer learning and the Adam optimizer, trained and refined on the APTOS-2019 dataset. The model classifies images into one of five DR stages: No DR (0), Mild DR (1), Moderate DR (2), Severe DR (3), and Proliferative DR (4). Here some Preprocessing steps are follow, including CLAHE enhancement, data augmentation, and class balancing, improve accuracy and generalization. Using Precision, Accuracy, F1-score and Recall and confusion matrix as evalution critera, the model achieved robust classification outcomes of 97.41% Accuracy, outperforming standard CNNs and demonstrating reliability and efficiency for real-world clinical DR screening.

title: Smart Neurological Disorder Management Using IoT and Edge Computing
authors: R.Bindhiya, S Murugan, P. Perumal, Nadimpilli.Aneela, Sumesh Chandran, Sailesh L
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Artificial Intelligence for Innovations in Healthcare Industries (ICAIIHI)
abstract: Neurological diseases, such as Parkinson’s disease, Alzheimer’s disease, epilepsy, and multiple sclerosis, provide considerable problems to global healthcare owing to their chronic characteristics and the need for ongoing monitoring. Traditional methods dependent on scheduled clinical appointments often neglect to monitor real-time symptom variations, preventing timely intervention and individualised therapy. This work presents an advanced neurological disease management system that integrates Internet of Things (IoT) devices with edge computing to provide continuous, real-time monitoring and analysis of patient health. Wearable sensors and home-based devices gather physiological and behavioural data, which are analysed locally at the edge to identify abnormalities, provide predictive insights, and produce prompt warnings for healthcare professionals. This reduces latency, decreases network reliance, and improves data privacy in comparison to centralised cloud-based solutions. The technology further enables personalized healthcare recommendations and proactive actions, enhancing patient results and quality of life. Experimental simulations illustrate the viability of real-time processing, precise anomaly detection, and effective data management. The proposed method provides a scalable, intelligent, and patient-focused framework, enabling advanced neurological healthcare that is adaptive, economical, and technologically sound.

title: Tactical Visualization in Basketball Using Smartphone Video and Homographic Projection
authors: Yoshiyuki Kido, Saya Yamada, Yuki Horiike, Yuto Kotora
publish: 2025 2nd Beyond Technology Summit on Informatics International Conference (BTS-I2C)
abstract: This paper proposes a low-cost method for tactical visualization in basketball using video recorded from a single smartphone. While professional teams rely on multi-camera high-speed systems to capture precise player movements, such equipment is impractical for amateur clubs and school teams. To provide an accessible alternative, we develop a lightweight analysis pipeline consisting of player detection, team identification, and geometric transformation. Players are detected using YOLOv5, and their on-court positions are approximated by the center of the bottom edge of their bounding boxes. Team affiliation is determined by a custom bib detection model trained on five dataset variations, with the highest accuracy achieved when using images of players actually wearing bibs. Detected player coordinates are then projected onto a bird’s-eye view court using a perspective transformation implemented with OpenCV.Experimental results show that the system can generate top-down visualization videos from smartphone footage, demonstrating the feasibility of this approach for low-cost basketball analysis.

title: Vortex Shield - Honeypot-Based Intrusion Detection System Using Machine Learning
authors: Manohar P, Bharath V, Mourya S Halli, Dhanraj, Hemanth Kumar R
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent and Innovative Technologies in Computing, Electrical and Electronics (IITCEE)
abstract: The increasing sophistication of modern cyber threats has revealed the shortcomings of traditional Intrusion Detection Systems (IDS), which often fail to recognize novel or stealthy attacks such as zero-day exploits. Honeypots have emerged as an effective defensive mechanism, offering a proactive way to observe and analyze attacker behavior within a controlled environment. This work proposes a honeypotdriven IDS architecture that combines behavioral deception with machine learning to enhance detection accuracy. The system integrates the Cowrie honeypot with a real-time data processing framework built on Apache Kafka and the ELK stack (Elasticsearch, Logstash, and Kibana). Authorized users are directed to the secure internal network, while unauthorized access attempts are transparently redirected to the honeypot. All interaction logs are continuously collected and streamed for further analysis. These data are then processed using the XGBoost algorithm, which classifies and distinguishes malicious behavior from legitimate network activity. Experimental evaluations demonstrate that the model effectively identifies brute-force attempts and other suspicious activities while providing timely alerts to administrators. The results highlight the practical advantage of integrating honeypots with intelligent machine learning models like XGBoost to build IDS solutions that are more adaptive, precise, and resilient against evolving cyberattacks.

title: Evaluation of YOLOv12 Single-Shot and Gemini 2.5 Zero-Shot Approaches for Greenhouse Capsicum Detection and Segmentation
authors: Ayan Paul, Rajendra Machavaram
publish: 2025 International Conference on Electrical and Computer Engineering Researches (ICECER)
abstract: Accurate detection and segmentation of greenhouse-grown capsicum are critical for advancing robotic harvesting and yield estimation. This study compares two contrasting paradigms: the single-shot You Only Look Once version twelve (YOLOv12), a task-specific detector, and Gemini 2.5, a zero-shot vision–language model enabling prompt-based recognition without retraining. A dataset of 300 annotated greenhouse images was used for evaluation across detection and segmentation tasks. YOLOv12 demonstrated superior performance, achieving a detection mAP@0.5 of 0.843 and a box-level segmentation mAP@0.5 of 0.508, with validation accuracies of 85% for detection and 69% for segmentation. On a smaller test set, YOLOv12 attained an F1-score of 0.77, supported by its lightweight design (18.1 MB) and fast inference (9.1 ms per image on a T4-class GPU). In contrast, Gemini 2.5 achieved a maximum detection accuracy of 78% with the "capsicum" prompt (other prompts: 72–62%) and a segmentation accuracy of 64%, yielding an F1-score of 0.69. However, its large-scale architecture (128B MoE core + 12B verifier) incurred significantly higher computational costs and inference latency (hundreds of milliseconds per image). Overall, YOLOv12 offers practical deployment efficiency, while Gemini 2.5 provides adaptability, underscoring the trade-offs between custom-trained and zero-shot approaches for agricultural automation.

title: Balancing Data and Boosting Performance through SMOTE and Hyperparameter Optimization for HEIs Marketing Priority Classification
authors: Amalia Nur Alifah, Paramaditya Arismawati, Tegar Arifin Prasetyo
publish: 2025 2nd Beyond Technology Summit on Informatics International Conference (BTS-I2C)
abstract: In the HEIs competitive infrastructure, efficient and focused marketing tactics are cornerstones to gain prospect students. Several of the existing studies on universities marketing have focused on and promoted overall promotional perspectives, with no defensible databases pre-validated techniques to classify high schools as a target market for students’ recruitment. Moreover, imbalanced data distribution and the absence of comparing machine learning models reveal a gap in literature. Filling this gap, the goal of the current paper is to categorize the potential high schools in terms of multiple factors such as quality of school, student population, distance from university and parents’ financial ability. It accomplishes this by using Random Forest and XGBoost classifiers and investigates how the use of SMOTE and hyperparameter tuning affect the outcome. Performance is analyzed under four scenarios of absence/presence of SMOTE and tuning (without both, only without SMOTE with tuning; with SMOTE but no tuning, and with both) using accuracy, precision, recall F1-score, log loss and confusion matrix analysis. The results show that both algorithms are strong even at baseline level (F1-scores of 0.92 – 0.93), and the XGBoost is more accurate than Random Forest, while Random Forest has better probability calibration (lower log-loss). The combination of SMOTE and hyperparameter optimization is the most effective method, where Random Forest results in the lowest log-loss (0.2244) demonstrating utmost probability estimation. These results provide empirical guidance for universities to adopt an appropriate classification method, thereby improving effectiveness and efficiency with which high-potential schools can be targeted in student recruitment efforts.

title: Anomaly Detection Based on Edge Computing Using Transfer Learning in Industrial IoT
authors: Ganesh D, Sunila Choudhary, Alok Kumar Mishra, Sudhanshu Dev, R.M. Gomathi, Rakhi Chakraborty
publish: 2025 International Conference on AI-Driven STEM Education and Learning Technologies (AISTEMEDU)
abstract: The industrial Internet of Things (IIoT) setting produces volumes of heterogeneous data that are interconnected, and anomaly detection is an essential part of the reliability, security, and efficiency of the system. The use of edge computing allows the analysis to be done nearer to data sources, which minimizes latency and reliance on cloud-based centralized data warehouses. The current anomaly detection solutions of IIoT usually use the traditional machine learning or shallow deep learning models, which cannot cope with the presence of high-dimensional data, low generalization, and large communication, resulting in poor accuracy and slow reaction. To solve these issues, this paper develops the Edge- IIoT Anomaly Framework (EIAF) that incorporates transfer learning with ResNet50 and an autoencoder that is deployed on the edge devices. ResNet50 will be utilized to extract highly detailed, transferable IIoT data stream feature representations, and an autoencoder will be utilized to recreate patterns successfully to detect anomaly indicators of outliers. This synergy improves the accuracy of the detection and reduces the use of resources. The proposed framework is especially appropriate in real-time monitoring of the industrial environment, where it is possible to detect faults before they occur and conduct proactive maintenance and prevent cyber-physical threats. Experimental results show that EIAF outperforms the current methods in accuracy on detecting the anomalies (by 97.2 percent), decreasing false positive rate (by 2 percent), and having a lower latency (178 ms), and a 94.5 percent reduction in resources consumption, which proves its suitability to serve as a solid and scalable solution to intelligent IIoT systems.

title: AI-Based Predictive Maintenance for Sustainable Gas Turbines
authors: Khaled Alruwaili, Enio Pedone, Gleyzer Martins
publish: 2025 International Conference on AI-Driven STEM Education and Learning Technologies (AISTEMEDU)
abstract: Gas turbines still represent a major technology in the modern power production industry, due to their large power density and flexibility of operation. However, corrosion, compressor fouling and combustion instabilities are major problems that undermine the long-term performance of gas turbines. These difficulties foster the ineffectiveness of efficiency, the increased rate of maintenance, and the more emissions, thus negatively influencing economic sustainability and environmental sustainability. Traditional control systems are generally reactive and they act only after the situation of a failure has occurred and therefore are not able to predict and avert degradation on performance. The current review explores the recent explosion of Artificial Intelligence (AI) and Machine Learning (ML) applications in transforming the gas turbine control and maintenance paradigm into a predictive paradigm instead of a reactive paradigm. Using AI-ML systems, it is possible to analyze past and current sensor data using both data-driven algorithms and physics-based models that will aid in predicting malfunctioning before it occurs and adjust operational parameters to be at optimal levels before a fault develops. The use of a human-in-the-loop approach also guarantees that AI outputs can be interpreted and be in line with the expertise of an engineer. Therefore, intelligent analytics combined with domain knowledge is a promising direction to a safer, cleaner, and more reliable gas turbine with improved performance over the lifecycle and reduced environmental impact.

title: Design and Implementation of a Role Based Blockchain Cloud Framework for Healthcare Data Transmission
authors: Sandeep Seetaram Naik, Mamatha Balachandra, Manjula C Belavagi, Pradeep B S
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent and Innovative Technologies in Computing, Electrical and Electronics (IITCEE)
abstract: Healthcare data transmission demands robust security and privacy measures due to the sensitive nature of patient information and the involvement of multiple stakeholders across distributed environments. This paper presents the design and implementation of a role based blockchain cloud framework for secure and efficient healthcare data transmission. The proposed framework integrates blockchain technology with cloud computing, leveraging blockchain's decentralized trust model, immutability, and smart contract capabilities to enforce Role Based Access Control (RBAC) policies across all data transactions. By assigning fine grained permissions to different user roles (such as patients, doctors, and administrators) and embedding these rules into smart contracts, the system guarantees that only authorized entities can access or modify medical data. Concurrently, the cloud infrastructure provides scalable data storage and high throughput processing for large volumes of medical records, while the blockchain ledger maintains an immutable audit trail of all access and data exchange events. A prototype of the framework was implemented on a permissioned Blockchain network and evaluated to assess its performance and security. Experimental results demonstrate that the framework achieves secure data transmission with minimal latency overhead and negligible impact on throughput, while preserving data confidentiality and integrity under different workload conditions. Thus, the role based Blockchain cloud approach offers a scalable, transparent, and auditable solution for trustworthy healthcare data sharing and management.

title: Optimized PV Converter Fed HAPF for Improved Power Quality
authors: Purnachander Rao Perala, Prakash Kamisetti, M. Surya Kalavathi, K. Ranjith Kumar
publish: 2026 IEEE 16th Annual Computing and Communication Workshop and Conference (CCWC)
abstract: This paper proposes an advanced control approach for improving power quality in electrical distribution systems using a photovoltaic-fed hybrid active power filter (HAPF-PV). The method effectively suppresses harmonic distortion, facilitates reactive power compensation, and enhances power factor. The HAPF is energized by a photovoltaic system integrated with a high-gain I-Luo DC-DC converter, which significantly improves voltage boosting performance and ensures efficient power transfer even under fluctuating irradiance levels. To maximize energy extraction from the PV system, a Class Topper Optimization (CTO)-based maximum power point tracking (MPPT) algorithm is employed. A Decoupled Neural Network (DNN)-based controller is adopted to generate the reference compensation current by dynamically adapting to changes in nonlinear and unbalanced load conditions. The generated reference signal is processed through a hysteresis current controller to generate appropriate gate switching signals for the interfaced power converter. This enables precise harmonic mitigation under dynamic operating scenarios. The proposed technique ensures sustainable integration of renewable energy while simultaneously enhancing system stability and power quality. The efficacy of the control scheme is validated through MATLAB simulations conducted under nonlinear loads. The results prove notable improvements in energy conversion efficiency, harmonic suppression, and overall system performance.

title: Automated Prediction System for Dry Eye Disease Using Machine Learning Techniques
authors: Mehulkumar Kantaria, Apeksha Koul, Bhagirath Bhatt, Dipaben M Knataria, Ankur Changela, Yogesh Kumar
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent and Innovative Technologies in Computing, Electrical and Electronics (IITCEE)
abstract: Lifestyle exposure to more screens and environmental factors contribute to the growing issue of Dry Eye Disease (DED) which emphasizes the requirement for practical pre-diagnostic tools. The current study proposes a machine learning technique to predict DED based on behavioural patterns, environmental factors, and physiological measures, including screen time, sleep habits, stress, and overall health. Several supervised learning models were trained, and the model that showed the best results was CatBoost, which achieved an accuracy of 94 %, precision of 0.93, recall of 0.92, and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$F 1$</tex>-score of 0.92. XGBoost model also achieved a high accuracy of 93%, along with similar precision and recall values. An analysis of the features' importance helped generate important risk factors that can be actively utilized in clinical practice. The proposed technique enables non-invasive, scalable, and early diagnosis of DED, which can be applied to integrating telemedicine, mobile health applications, and workforce health trackers.

title: Digital Simulation based Demonstration of Induction Machine
authors: Harinatha Reddy Chennam, Bramhananda Reddy Teegala, Pradep Kumar Yadav Allagadda, Y.V Siva Reddy, Ravi Sankara Reddy Netapally, Sathish Krishna Anumala
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Internet of Things: Smart Innovation and Usages (IoT-SIU)
abstract: The higher education is undergoing a shift, particularly in technical fields where visual mastery is very much keen.. Virtual Reality breaks this deadlock by with a fully immersive, 360-degree environment. By examining the mechanics of an induction motor through this lens, this paper shows that being able to “see” and “touch” a digital component doesn’t just make a lesson more interesting-it fundamentally rewires how students absorb and remember complex engineering data.

title: AI-Powered Interfaces to Healthcare: A Patient-Centric Study of OPD Scheduling
authors: Dipanwita Chakrabarty, Satakshi Chatterjee, Arunangshu Giri, Dibyendu Rath, Shamba Chatterjee
publish: 2025 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence for Computing, Astronomy and Renewable Energy (AICARE)
abstract: The present study has investigated patient satisfaction while interacting with AI equipped outpatient department or OPD and also evaluated the perceived usefulness of AI in the context of OPD scheduling. Based on the Unified Theory of Acceptance and Use of Technology 2 (UTAUT2) model, the study identified four constructs, Information sharing, time-based efficiency, credibility and perceived compatibility. These constructs influence AI usefulness, which in turn induce patient satisfaction. A survey was conducted involving 337 Indian patients using a structured questionnaire as survey instrument. The responses of the survey participants were analyzed using SEM. The result has shown strong influence of all four factors over perceived usefulness, which again significantly influence patient satisfaction. This shows that an effective use of AI can improve service quality of the OPD and can boost patients’ trust. Also, AI can align the OPD service acknowledging patient preferences which can increase their satisfaction. The study contributes theoretically by integrating crucial AI useful applications in digital healthcare interfaces. It also gives a guideline to the healthcare service providers to design a user-friendly system that can satisfy and engage patients.

title: A Hybrid Multi-Model Deep Learning and Ensemble Framework for Automated Lung Cancer Detection Using CT Imaging
authors: Jyoti Gautam, Shivam Dhamejani, Krishna Ku. Mishra, Alwyn Ekka
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Artificial Intelligence for Innovations in Healthcare Industries (ICAIIHI)
abstract: Lung cancer remains one of the most challenging public health issues, with more than two million new cases and close to two million fatalities reported annually. Early diagnosis is strongly correlated with patient survival, and low-dose computed tomography (LDCT) is currently the most effective large-scale screening technique. However, manual interpretation of CT scans is time-consuming, inconsistent, and heavily dependent on radiologist expertise. This study proposes an integrated hybrid approach that merges deep learning feature extractors with classical machine learning classifiers for automated lung cancer identification. The framework incorporates a custom Convolutional Neural Network (CNN) along with transfer-learning models such as ResNet50, DenseNet121, VGG16, InceptionV3, and EfficientNetB7. Extracted features are processed using ensemble classifiers including Support Vector Machine (SVM), XG-Boost, Random Forest, and LightGBM. Extensive preprocessing steps—covering segmentation, contrast enhancement, normalization, and augmented data generation—ensure high-quality and uniform inputs. Experiments conducted on a dataset of 15,000 CT images indicate that the ResNet50-SVM combination performs best, achieving 99.6% accuracy, 99.8% sensitivity, and 99.4% specificity, while also demonstrating roughly 40% lower computational overhead than conventional end-to-end deep neural networks. Validation with external datasets confirms strong generalization, achieving an accuracy of 98.2%.

title: A CNN–LSTM Hybrid Framework for Multimodal Stock Market Movement Prediction Using Nifty 50 Data
authors: Prathyusha P., Charithra C. M., Vinish P., Prakash Pinto, Mohamed Ismail Mohamed Riyath
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent and Innovative Technologies in Computing, Electrical and Electronics (IITCEE)
abstract: Stock market prediction remains an enduring challenge due to the complex interplay of quantitative indicators and qualitative market sentiment. Traditional econometric techniques are useful for identifying linear patterns, yet they often fall short when markets behave unpredictably or when sentiment driven changes dominate. This study involves a Convolutional Neural Network Long-Short Term Memory (CNN–LSTM) hybrid framework designed to integrate financial news sentiments with historical Nifty 50 index data for short-term stock forecasting. CNN layers are used to pick up meaningful patterns from news-based sentiment inputs, whereas the LSTM technique helps track how price movements evolve over time and trend continuity in numerical price sequences. A fusion layer integrates both features to predict bullish and bearish market movements. Empirical analysis on five years data from Nifty 50 demonstrate that the hybrid CNNLSTM significantly outperforms traditional statistical methods and single modality deep learning models. This model achieves an accuracy of 83.5% and directional accuracy of 85.4%, surpassing transformer based fusion architectures under similar data constraints. Statistical significance testing confirms the reliability of the improvement <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(\mathrm{p}<0.001)$</tex>. The results show that combining text and numerical data gives the model a clearer picture of market behavior and improves its predictive ability, which aids investors. traders and financial analysts make decisions in emerging economies like India.

title: Improvement of Vehicle License Plate Detection and Recognition with the Addition of the CLAHE Method
authors: Eliza Zulkarnain, Indrabayu
publish: 2025 2nd Beyond Technology Summit on Informatics International Conference (BTS-I2C)
abstract: Automatic License Plate Recognition (ALPR) detection and recognition play an important role in modern traffic monitoring systems, especially in the implementation of Electronic Traffic Law Enforcement (ETLE). The main challenge of this system is the low image quality owing to uneven lighting and vehicle movement. This study proposes to improve the performance of the YOLOv11 model by adding a preprocessing stage using Contrast Limited Adaptive Histogram Equalization (CLAHE) and Unsharp Masking to improve contrast and image sharpness. The dataset was taken from ETLE CCTV footage on Jalan A.P. Pettarani, Makassar, under low-light conditions. The test results showed that the combination of CLAHE and Unsharp Masking gave the best performance with a Precision of 0.946, Recall of 0.977, mAP50 of 0.978, and mAP50–95 of 0.833. The evaluation of character recognition using EasyOCR also showed the lowest Character Error Rate (CER) value of 6.35%. Thus, this combined method has been shown to be effective in improving the detection and recognition accuracy of license plates and has the potential to be implemented in ETLE systems under real traffic conditions.

title: Retinal Fundus Image Supported Examination of Macular Scar using Handcrafted Features
authors: Ramya Mohan, Mohamed Abouhawwash, David Taniar, Rajinikanth Venkatesan
publish: 2025 International Conference on Advances in Technology and Computing (ICATC)
abstract: Eye is a vital organ in human physiology and its illness will cause various vision issues. Early diagnosis and management of eye health is essential to treat the vision issues. The clinical approaches include the personal check and image based eye examination. Retinal Fundus Image (RFI) is a common imaging modality for analyzing the eye illness and this work considered the examination of Macular Scar (MS) for the study. This study proposed a machine-learning tool to examine the healthy/MS RFI-database and to achieve better efficiency, it implemented Spider-Wasp algorithm based features optimization. The different phases in the ML-tool includes; image collection and initial adjustment, features extraction using local binary pattern and discrete wavelet transform, features reduction with considered scheme, serial features fusion to generate fused-features vector, and classification and 3-fold cross validation to confirm the performance. This research implemented a binary-classification and the experimental outcome presents an accuracy of >92% during the healthy/MS classification.

title: AI-Based Early Detection of Neurological Disorders Using MRI and Deep Learning
authors: Ayush Thakur, Pragya Rajput, Tanmay Kumar Jaiswal, Keshav Chandra, Krishna Razdan
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Artificial Intelligence for Innovations in Healthcare Industries (ICAIIHI)
abstract: The early identification of neurological conditions like dementia or Alzheimer’s disease is extremely difficult since treatment choices are limited because obvious symptoms frequently don’t show up until major changes in the brain have already taken place. In order to identify what the human eye might overlook, we are investigating how Artificial Intelligence—more particularly, deep learning—can assist us in analyzing Magnetic Resonance Imaging (MRI) scans. Building and training an intelligent model that could recognize the faint, nearly imperceptible warning indications of neurological disorders in their early stages was our aim. We trained our algorithm to discriminate between brains that were healthy and those that were exhibiting the early symptoms of a problem by feeding it a huge library of scans. The results are really positive: our AI learned to identify particular, objective biomarkers and patterns, and it showed excellent accuracy, correctly identifying possible problems well in advance of a traditional diagnosis. This study highlights AI’s potential as a good clinical partner, breaking the door open. to preemptive intervention, more personalized care and ultimately. improved patient outcomes.

title: CLP ONE - Customer Smart Energy Management Transformation in Hong Kong
authors: Tracey Tsz Yan Chan, Ian Wai Pan Yung, Keith Cheuk Hang Yuen, Carmen Ka Man Wong, Kobe Lai Ling Leung, Rachel Sui Kei Ngan
publish: 2025 25th Conference of the Electric Power Supply Industry (CEPSI)
abstract: This paper aims to showcase CLP ONE, a successful end-to-end Customer Energy Management Ecosystem, an innovative end to end journey design approach that combines electricity monitoring, cloud-based analytics, and end-to-end customer engagement through a mobile application. The app’s save-to-loyalty incentive model will drive higher user engagement and energy savings, proving its scalability for smart grid adoption.Introduced in 2024, CLP ONE signifies a major shift in energy management, transitioning from a premise-based system to a person-based approach. This innovative platform allows customers to manage all their electricity service accounts, regardless of whether they are apartments, village houses, or even the fastest growing electric vehicles (EV), all within a single mobile app profile, removing the need for accessing individual premise accounts.The mobile app ensures a seamless customer experience throughout the entire energy management cycle. Users can apply for new electricity services, monitor their energy consumption, view bill amounts, pay bills electronically, and even facilitate transitions to new residences all within the same mobile app. With CLP ONE, customers benefit from a more efficient and cohesive approach to managing their energy needs.Based on the mobile app interface, the cloud analytics will then process the vast array of smart meter data collected every two hours, offering insights into customers' energy behaviours in relation to their neighbourhoods, enabling them to optimize consumption. The mobile application serves as an interactive platform that displays energy usage trends and offers personalized product recommendations based on individual energy consumption patterns, which could help customers identify energy-efficient appliances tailored to their specific needs, further promoting informed energy-saving decisions.Moreover, the energy savings realized through this intelligent management system can be converted into loyalty points, incentivizing users to adopt more environmentally sustainable practices. These points can be redeemed for shopping discounts on the recommended energy-efficient appliances identified by customers' energy consumption patterns mentioned earlier, establishing a tangible rewards system that not only encourages energy conservation but also enhances customer satisfaction. Furthermore, these loyalty points could possibly contribute to any intangible green index rating concepts, which may potentially support additional initiatives such as personal or corporate green loans, in line with global efforts toward achieving a carbon-neutral future. Additionally, energy-saving tips and tutorials are also gamified to allow customers earn more loyalty points and eventually reinforce customers’ saving behaviour.This holistic approach not only fosters sustainable energy usage but also enriches the consumer experience, turning energy management into a rewarding endeavour. In the following sections, we will delve into the architecture of the customer energy management ecosystem, illustrating its components from foundational infrastructure to smart grid and cloud analytics, culminating in customer-facing applications. We will highlight how this ecosystem empowers customers to make informed energy management decisions and explore real business examples that incentivize greener habits to demonstrate how customer smart energy management is transformed to a new level.

title: Hybrid Sequential Pattern Mining and Machine Learning for Context-Aware Event Prediction in IoT
authors: Pooja Goyal, Bradley Helmholz, Trinh Tran, Farhan Rahman Arnob, Renee Bryce
publish: 2026 IEEE 16th Annual Computing and Communication Workshop and Conference (CCWC)
abstract: Context-aware event prediction is a critical challenge in Internet of Things (IoT) systems, where accurate forecasting enables adaptive and personalized applications. Existing approaches often rely on either sequential pattern mining or machine learning models in isolation, limiting their ability to capture complex temporal dependencies. In this work, we introduce a hybrid framework that integrates sequential pattern mining-via POERMH (Partially-Ordered Episode Rule Mining with Head support) and PrefixSpan-with discriminative learning models to enhance context-event prediction on mobile IoT devices. Our key contribution lies in leveraging mined sequential structures as feature representations for classifiers, combined with optimized time-interval segmentation to preserve temporal dynamics. Empirical evaluation across real-world IoT datasets demonstrates that this integration significantly outperforms standalone baselines, with Support Vector Machines achieving an F1-score of 67.08 %, representing a substantial improvement over traditional sequence-only methods. These findings highlight the importance of coupling symbolic pattern discovery with statistical learning for robust context modeling, offering a scalable approach for next-generation IoT applications.

title: AI Driven Sickle Cell Disease Detection
authors: Manoj Kumar Singh, V Varsha, Dibyanshu Sahoo, Tannu Agrawal
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Artificial Intelligence for Innovations in Healthcare Industries (ICAIIHI)
abstract: It is a blood condition known as sickle cell disease (SCD) which modify shape of red blood cell. Early detection is important for prevention. Due to absence of proper medical facilities in many areas, these tests are not accessible. Deep learning is useful in medical image processing. This work proposes an AI system involving Convolutional Neural Networks (CNNs) for detection of sickle-shaped cells from blood smear images. The system uses preprocessing methods to achieve high accuracy in classification from test datasets. Furthermore, such AI-powered approaches can significantly help in advanced diagnostic technologies and under-resourced regions, enabling large-scale screening programs. By automated image recognition, the system has less dependency in manual assessments, enhances decision-making, and lays the foundation for scalable, portable, and real-time diagnostic solutions.

title: Regression-Based Modeling of Antisense Oligonucleotide Efficacy Using Sequence, Structural, and Off-Target Features
authors: Ava George, Yu Bai, Jialin Tang
publish: 2026 IEEE 16th Annual Computing and Communication Workshop and Conference (CCWC)
abstract: Antisense oligonucleotides (ASOs) are a promising class of nucleic acid-based therapeutics that regulate gene expression by binding target mRNAs, with applications in genetic and rare diseases. However, designing effective ASOs remains difficult due to the vast combinatorial space of sequences, secondary structures, and chemical modifications. Recent work has leveraged deep learning and graph neural networks to address these challenges. Building on this foundation, the present project explores a complementary pipeline using classical machine learning and statistical methods for ASO design and evaluation. The workflow integrates multiple computational stages: retrieval of target mRNA sequences from NCBI, interaction prediction using the miRanda algorithm, and structural analysis via ViennaRNA. Off-target interactions were systematically assessed, and custom Python scripts were developed to merge outputs into a unified dataset. Feature engineering incorporated both numeric and categorical predictors, such as cell line and density, enabling model testing of inhibitory efficiency. Features of sequence, structure and offtarget interactions trained multiple regressors including Linear Regression, Ridge, Lasso, Random Forest, and Gradient Boosting. Models were evaluated using nested cross-validation with groupaware splits to prevent leakage. Random Forest achieved the highest predictive performance to predict inhibition outcomes (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathrm{R}^{2} \approx 0.627, \text{MAE} \approx 9.47$</tex>). These results highlight both the feasibility and the challenges of applying interpretable machine learning techniques to ASO design, particularly in the presence of substantial missing data. Future directions include exploring a normalized hybridization energy gradient with relative energy per nucleotide. This work demonstrates the potential for combining bioinformatics tools, structural modeling, and machine learning to advance the rational design of therapeutic ASOs.

title: Rekyl: A Blockchain-Based Smart Recycling Management Platform
authors: Benitha Christinal. J, M. Niranjani, Sivaranjani. S, R. Chennappan, Satheesh. K, Poongothai P
publish: 2025 World Conference on Cutting-Edge Science and Technology (WCCEST)
abstract: Rekyl is a web platform that makes recycling more rewarding and transparent by using blockchain technology. When users recycle things like food waste, plastics, or e-waste, they earn digital “Recyclo” tokens, which can be traded in for discounts or eco-friendly products. The platform partners with trusted recycling companies to ensure proper handling of materials and uses analytics to show users the positive effect their recycling has on the environment, such as reducing carbon footprints. All token transactions are secured and openly recorded on the blockchain, so users can trust that their efforts are making a real, measurable difference. The paper outlines the system design, features, and global scalability of Rekyl.

title: Bridging Theory and Practice in AI-Driven Assessments for UAE K-12 Education
authors: Hanan Ghreir, Mahmoud Hamash, Taoufik Boulhrir, Megat Aman Zahiri Megat, Peter Tiernan, Enda Donlon
publish: 2025 IEEE 14th International Conference on Engineering Education (ICEED)
abstract: This study investigates the integration of artificial intelligence (AI) in educational assessments across private K–12 schools in Abu Dhabi, United Arab Emirates (UAE), focusing on teachers' perceptions, adoption patterns, and systemic challenges. Aligned with the UAE's National AI Strategy 2031, which positions AI as a catalyst for educational innovation, the research employs a quantitative survey of 610 teachers to evaluate the alignment between theoretical expectations and practical realities. Findings reveal moderate AI adoption in assessments (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$M=3.70$</tex> for student evaluation), with tools like ChatGPT predominantly used for content creation and student engagement. While teachers express strong optimism for AI's future role in personalising learning (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$M=3.89$</tex>) and enhancing feedback (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$M=3.94$</tex>), adoption remains constrained by ethical concerns (e.g., algorithmic bias), infrastructural disparities, and insufficient institutional support. The study highlights a critical paradox: enthusiasm for AI's transformative potential coexists with hesitation to deploy it in high-stakes tasks, underscoring the irreplaceable role of human judgment in pedagogy. Recommendations emphasise the need for ethical frameworks, teacher-centred professional development, and equitable resource allocation to bridge the gap between AI's promise and practice. By addressing these challenges, the UAE can advance its vision of a dynamic, inclusive educational landscape powered by responsible AI integration.

title: Towards Generalizable Time Series Forecasting Via IB-Regularized Transformer-Diffusion
authors: Dagyeong Na, Jinho Kang, Junseok Kwon
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Data Mining (ICDM)
abstract: Multivariate Time Series Forecasting (MTSF) remains challenging due to the need to capture complex temporal dependencies and ensure robustness against distribution shift. While Transformer-based models excel at capturing long-range patterns, and diffusion models offer generative flexibility, directly applying diffusion often leads to excessive uncertainty. To address these limitations, we propose a unified forecasting framework (IB-TransDiff) that combines global modeling based on Transformers and local refinement based on diffusion, guided by the Information Bottleneck (IB) principle. To improve generalization, we introduce a novel regularization strategy that penalizes the second moment of the conditional embedding to reduce its mutual information with the input. Experiments on multiple benchmarks demonstrate that our method consistently outperforms existing approaches in MSE and MAE. In addition, our method reduces uncertainty, achieving notable improvements in QICE, CRPS, and PICP metrics. These results highlight the value of integrating information-theoretic constraints into conditional diffusion models for robust and accurate time series forecasting.

title: E-Commerce Product Review Analysis Based on Emotion Detection with IndoBERT and Qwen 2.5 Text Generation
authors: Muhammad Rafan Pradipta, Warih Maharani
publish: 2025 2nd Beyond Technology Summit on Informatics International Conference (BTS-I2C)
abstract: The rapid growth of e-commerce has generated a wide variety of textual review data, making current polarity-based sentiment analysis (positive & negative) often inadequate to capture the semantic nuances needed for actionable product evaluation. This study addresses this gap by developing a rigorously validated and integrated two-stage framework for comprehensive product review analysis. Its key innovation is the integration of high-granularity emotion detection, which acts as a structured reasoning framework for state-of-the-art Large Language Models (LLMs). To obtain an optimal emotion detection model, experiments were conducted using 5249 data points from the Indonesian PRDECT-ID review dataset, comparing data balancing techniques (Class Weights vs. Oversampling) with the multilingual BERT baseline. The experimental results show that IndoBERT + Oversampling achieves the best performance (Accuracy 0.70, F1 Score 0.67), representing a significant improvement over the BERT baseline (Accuracy 0.62). The emotion detection results of the best-configured model were then fed into Qwen 2.5-14b, which was chosen because it outperformed comparable models in its class such as GPT-4o mini and Gemma2-27b on MMLU and GSM8k. To assess the output generated by Qwen2.5-14b, a qualitative evaluation by 27 professional respondents validated the reports generated by Qwen 2.5, with 85.2% rating the output as "Clear" and "Complete." While the F1 Score indicates areas for future improvement in distinguishing semantically ambiguous emotions, this robust user validation confirms that the proposed end-to-end system is an effective and practical solution for generating actionable, emotion-driven product evaluations in the e-commerce sector.

title: An Assessment Framework for IT/OT Convergence Maturity in Industrial Environments
authors: William Aparecido Celestino Lopes, Sergio Miele Ruggero, Marcelo Tsuguio Okano
publish: 2026 IEEE 16th Annual Computing and Communication Workshop and Conference (CCWC)
abstract: The growing convergence between Information Technology (IT) and Operational Technology (OT) has reshaped industrial environments by enabling digital integration, advanced analytics, and cyber-physical connectivity. However, this integration also increases the exposure of industrial systems to cyber risks, interoperability challenges, and governance complexity. This study proposes and applies a six-dimensional maturity assessment framework to evaluate the current state of IT/OT convergence in Brazilian industrial organizations. A structured survey using Likert-scale metrics was administered to professionals across multiple industrial sectors, capturing perceptions of system integration, interoperability, cybersecurity practices, governance structures, data management, and enabling technologies. Results reveal intermediate maturity levels in system integration and interoperability, while cybersecurity, data governance, and coordinated IT/OT management remain underdeveloped. The findings highlight persistent barriers to digital transformation and reinforce the need for targeted governance, cybersecurity strategies, and data-driven practices in industrial operations. The study contributes a preliminary diagnostic tool that can be replicated and expanded in future evaluations of digital maturity within Industry 4.0 environments.

title: AI-Driven Melanoma Detection Using Convolutional Neural Network
authors: M. Purushotham Reddy, Sunkari Pavani, Priya Gill, Pabba Saketh
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Artificial Intelligence for Innovations in Healthcare Industries (ICAIIHI)
abstract: This research paper presents an enhanced ensemble approach for early classifying melanoma skin cancer using the dermatoscopic pictures. This approach developed the enhanced model by combining four convolutional neural network models Custom baseline CNN, ResNet50, DenseNet121, and MobileNetV2 under a meta- ensemble framework which will distinguish between the benign and malignant lesions. This models were trained separately on publicly available dataset known as Melanoma Skin Cancer Dataset of 10000 Images which has 500 benign and 500 malignant images for testing and 5000 benign and 4605 malignant images, which underwent to rigorous preprocessing steps with including image resizing,normalization and augmentation to enhance learning efficiency and generalization. Each model was fine-tuned and also evaluated separately, and evaluated the prediction probabilities and were aggregated using a soft voting mechanism which followed by a meta-ensemble model to achieve final classification. This methodology uses the immense strengths of the diverse CNN architectures, which offers the improved stability and accuracy in the skin lesion classification. This approach demonstrates the ensemble learning in advancing image-based melanoma diagnosis and also fostering AI-focused healthcare systems.

title: A Comparative Study Of Diabetes Risk Prediction Model Using Machine Learning
authors: Toshniwali Bhargav, Julee Prajapat, Shivangi Tiwari, Zeya Hashmi
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Artificial Intelligence for Innovations in Healthcare Industries (ICAIIHI)
abstract: Millions of people worldwide suffer from diabetes, which is quickly becoming one of the most prevalent health problems. With the availability of health data and technological advancements, people can better manage their health and prevent problems like kidney failure, heart disease, and nerve damage by detecting diabetes early. In this study, we examine how Machine Learning (ML) models can help identify the risk of diabetes before it becomes severe. Using the popular PIMA Indian Diabetes dataset, we compare three popular ML algorithms—Logistic Regression, Support Vector Machine (SVM), and Random Forest—to see which one produces the most accurate predictions. Diabetes, which affects millions of people worldwide and frequently goes undiagnosed until major complications start to show, is quickly emerging as one of the most prevalent health issues. Early diabetes detection can have a significant impact on a person's ability to manage their health and prevent problems like kidney failure, heart disease, and nerve damage.We used standard metrics to assess the models' performance after cleaning the data and choosing the most significant features. Because it can learn patterns from multiple decision trees, the Random Forest classifier outperformed all the other models, providing balanced results and high accuracy. The study demonstrates how machine learning (ML) can help medical practitioners by providing prompt, data- driven insights into a patient's risk level.Such predictive tools can eventually be incorporated into screening programs, hospitals, or mobile heal apps with additional development, assisting individuals in taking preventative measures and enhancing general health outcomes.

title: Live Emotion Based Music Recommender System
authors: Pooja M, Kavyashree H L, Pratham M, Meghana G
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent and Innovative Technologies in Computing, Electrical and Electronics (IITCEE)
