abstract: The Live Emotion Based Music Recommendation improves the user experience by evaluating facial expressions and recommending songs that correspond with the identified mood. This study use deep learning approaches to identify emotions in real time and incorporates a convolutional neural network (CNN) for face emotion classification. Video frames are captured, face characteristics are processed, and identified emotions are mapped to carefully selected Spotify playlists by the algorithm. A web interface built using Flask shows real-time emotion recognition and dynamically modifies song suggestions.

title: Analysis of Electric Vehicle Battery Charging and Discharging Cycles at Different C-Rates and Temperatures
authors: Anirudh A, Savitha P B, Madhu S, Soumyashree N
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent and Innovative Technologies in Computing, Electrical and Electronics (IITCEE)
abstract: Globally, electric automobiles (EVs) are the newest and most innovative technology in the transportation industry. Their decreasing consumption of fuel and emissions of greenhouse gases are making them increasingly significant. Super capacitors, fuel cells, and batteries are examples of storage mechanisms for energy that are used to store power, which is used as energy for electric vehicles (EVs) and powers their electric motor. Lead-acid and nickel metal hydride batteries were originally utilized in automobiles. Li-ion batteries (LIBs) have risen to the top of the market for usage in electric vehicles (EVs). State of charge (SOC) estimate is an important parameter in battery management system (BMS) and has contributed to the advancement of electric vehicle technology. The added benefit is that it gives users access to precise SOC measurements through real-time battery monitoring. This lowers the danger of overvoltage of cells by extracting correct SOC measurements. Many techniques for estimating state of charge (SOC) rely on the open circuit voltage (OCV) gradient. This paper examines the impact of a voltage reduction in high-power lithium-ion batteries at various SOC, at various temperatures and C-rates. OCV during charge and discharge cycles at 0.3 and 2 C-rate for every 10% variation in SOC at ambient temperature and at 40 °C has been recorded. The outcome demonstrates that the voltage reduction is greater at higher C rates than it is at lower C rates, and this is also true at higher temperatures.

title: Binary Flaw Detection: A Security Analysis Paper
authors: Fahad Amin
publish: 2025 International Conference on Advances in Machine Intelligence, and Cybersecurity Technologies (AMICT)
abstract: We propose a novel, scalable framework for overlapping community detection designed for vulnerability localization in binary code graphs. The framework is an algorithmic pipeline ("Candidate + Refine") that (i) generates compact overlapping community seeds, (ii) refines m embership using an objective that balances intra-community density and overlap sparsity, and (iii) reconciles boundary communities in a distributed setting. We evaluate the approach on standard benchmarks (DBLP, LiveJournal, YouTube) and LFR synthetic graphs, and report consistent improvements in extended NMI (+6–12%) and end-to-end runtime (6 hours on YouTube vs. 10–48 hours for baseline methods). We also provide ROC / precision‚ Äìrecall analyses, runtime scalability plots, and visual examples of detected communities to aid interpretability. Code and artifact details are provided in the supplementary material.

title: A Miniaturized 3-D Integrated Low-Pass Filter Using a Novel Flexibly Configurable Three-Port Capacitor Based on TSV Technology
authors: Xiangkun Yin, Xiang Li, Fengjuan Wang, Tao Zhang, Qijun Lu, Zhangming Zhu
publish: IEEE Transactions on Components, Packaging and Manufacturing Technology
abstract: A miniaturized three-port capacitor structure based on through-silicon via (TSV) technology is proposed for high-density 3-D integrated radio frequency (RF) circuits. By integrating three interdigital capacitors into a single structure with a common electrode, the proposed capacitor reduces the number of interconnection ports by 50%, significantly improving area efficiency and signal integrity. The structure is validated through full-wave simulation and equivalent circuit modeling, showing good capacitive performance and high self-resonant frequency. A compact low-pass filter (LPF) is demonstrated using the proposed capacitor, achieving a cutoff frequency of 3.99 GHz, insertion loss of 0.18 dB, and return loss better than 25 dB within an area of 0.32 × 0.31 mm².

title: RAP-CNN: A Deep Learning Framework for Mangrove Aboveground Biomass Estimation Using Multi-Source Remote Sensing Data
authors: Jiasen Zhuang, Peixia Huang, Guohao Liu, Shulei Wu, Penghua Qiu, Huandong Chen
publish: IEEE Journal of Selected Topics in Applied Earth Observations and Remote Sensing
abstract: As a typical blue carbon ecosystem, mangroves play a vital role in the global carbon cycle and coastal ecological security. Accurately estimating their aboveground biomass (AGB) is crucial for carbon stock accounting and ecosystem monitoring. However, mangrove AGB estimation faces two core challenges: complex, inaccessible habitats limiting high-quality traditional sampling, and inherent limitations of single-source remote sensing data with inadequate multi-source feature fusion—restricting deep learning applications and relevant research. This study adopted UAV-LiDAR for high-precision sampling and proposed an improved CNN, Residual Attention Pyramid CNN (RAP CNN), with distinct advantages in tackling these challenges: the SE module enhances biomass-sensitive features, suppresses redundancy, and resolves inadequate multi-source fusion; the SPP module captures multi-scale spatial context to improve estimation stability in heterogeneous areas; residual connections mitigate gradient vanishing and strengthen deep feature ex traction. Integrating Sentinel-1/2 multi-source data, it upgrades from simple feature stacking to intelligent synergy of multisource information. Innovatively combining UAV-LiDAR-derived high-precision samples (tree height and DBH via individual tree segmentation) with species classification results and allo metric equations, we constructed species-level biomass samples, overcoming traditional sampling bottlenecks. Results show RAP CNN achieves R2 = 0.7645, RMSE = 8.3903 Mg/ha on the training set and R2 = 0.7576, RMSE = 13.0467 Mg/ha on the validation set, outperforming traditional methods like RF and XGBoost. These findings verify RAP-CNN's effectiveness and UAV-LiDAR's key role, providing valuable technical support for carbon stock assessment and ecological protection.

title: DR4SV: A Digital Twin-Enhanced Resource Allocation Mechanism for Satellite-Terrestrial Integrated Vehicular Networks_supp1-3665833.pdf
authors: Kai Peng
abstract: The Mobile Edge Computing (MEC)-Empowered Internet of Vehicles (IoVs), a transformative paradigm characterized by real-time decision-making and distributed processing via data offloading to the network edge, has garnered substantial research interest. Nonetheless, emerging applications such as autonomous driving pose dual challenges of computational bottle necks and inadequate wide-area coverage in resource scheduling. To this end, we propose a Digital Twin (DT)-enhanced, satellite assisted resource allocation framework that synergistically coordinates MEC and satellite networks while leveraging DTs for network virtualization and collaborative training, thereby fully exploiting the low-latency capabilities of edge computing, the high-capacity processing of satellite computing, and the cost efficient simulation advantages of DT technology. During the training phase, we develop DR4SV, a Deep Reinforcement Learning (DRL)-based online optimization method that constructs specialized intelligent agents tailored for satellite-collaborative IoV scenarios. By incorporating depthwise over-parameterized convolutional layers, DR4SV enables multi-channel synergistic processing across heterogeneous communication channels while establishing robust policy alignment between physical agents and their DT counterparts. This architecture facilitates real-time state mapping and multi-dimensional parallel simulation within the digital space, enhancing resource allocation efficiency while dynamically balancing latency and energy consumption with accelerated convergence. Extensive experiments are conducted based on real-world datasets, encompassing hyperparameter con figurations and rigorous performance evaluations. The numerical results demonstrate that DR4SV achieves reductions in both time and energy consumption, with optimization rates reaching up to 6.8% and 19.1%, respectively. Meanwhile, the correlation between DTs and physical entities reaches up to 97%, thereby validating the effectiveness and feasibility of DT technology in satellite-terrestrial integrated vehicular networks.

title: Performance-Optimized Heterogeneously Integrated 0.15 μ m GaN Circulator and 45 nm RF SOI Voltage-Boosted Clock Generation IC
authors: Nishant Patil, Armagan Dascurcu, Nusrat Jahan, Harish Krishnaswamy
publish: IEEE Transactions on Microwave Theory and Techniques
abstract: Advanced CMOS technology nodes face limitations in linearity due to their reduced breakdown voltages, whereas III–V semiconductors offer superior device performance but have limited BEOL and integration capabilities, making complex circuit design challenging. The precise and efficient integration of III–V MMICs with CMOS ICs enables leveraging the most suitable device for each function, thereby achieving enhanced dynamic range. However, this approach demands precise integration of the ICs with carefully controlled interconnects. This article presents the demonstration of a heterogeneously integrated GaN LPTV switched-transmission-line circulator combined with a CMOS SOI clock generation IC, harnessing the high integration density of CMOS alongside the superior switching characteristics of GaN. Measurement results demonstrate a dc transmission loss of 2.0dB and port-to-port isolation >17dB across a 3-dB bandwidth from dc to 3.35GHz. The circulator achieved IP1dB of 12.1dBm and IIP3 of 25.6dBm, attributed to voltage-boosted swings and precise, robust phase alignment from the CMOS SOI IC. Furthermore, the circulator and clock chip operate using only one single-phase external clock at 2.6GHz, simplifying system-level integration significantly compared to other solutions that require complex multiphase clock sources.

title: Design of a Compact Reconfigurable Microstrip Bandpass Filter With Switchable Triple- and Quadruple-Passbands
authors: Ching-Wen Tang, Yi-Zhan Lee
abstract: This paper presents a compact planar microstrip bandpass filter with reconfigurable triple- and quadruple- passband responses for dynamic spectrum access in cognitive and software-defined radio systems. Unlike conventional multiband filters that employ fixed resonant paths or complicated switching networks, the proposed topology enables selective switching between two distinct multiband operating modes using a simple and compact circuit configuration. A half-wavelength stepped open stub is strategically incorporated to generate multiple transmission zeros near the passband edges, thereby enhancing stopband suppression and passband selectivity. Reconfigurability is achieved by integrating only two PIN diodes into the filter structure, resulting in low switching complexity and minimal circuit overhead. The proposed filter is fabricated on a Rogers RO4003C substrate and occupies a compact area of 0.33λg × 0.28λg. In the triple-passband mode, measured center frequencies of 2.53, 3.37, and 4.37 GHz are obtained, whereas in the quadruple-passband mode, center frequencies of 2.57, 3.10, 3.89, and 4.45 GHz are achieved. Measured results are in good agreement with full-wave electromagnetic simulations, confirming the effectiveness and practical feasibility of the proposed reconfigurable multiband filter for modern wireless front-end applications.

title: Predictive Monitoring of Industrial Processes and Quality Indices via Joint Training of Soft Sensor and Time-Series Forecasting
authors: Yonghang Li, Xiaochen Hao, Xunian Yang, Xingzhi Zheng, Libin Wei
abstract: Predictive monitoring of industrial processes and quality indices, particularly free calcium oxide (f-CaO) in cement production, is of critical importance. However, it is significantly constrained by the asynchronous sampling between high-frequency process data and delayed laboratory quality measurements. To address this challenge, this article proposes a joint modeling framework that collaboratively optimizes a multiscale attention Kolmogorov–Arnold network (MSAKAN) soft sensor and a MambaMoE time-series forecasting model. The MSAKAN component combines multiscale attention with a Kolmogorov–Arnold network to enhance nonlinear mapping capabilities, while the MambaMoE component employs a mixture-of-experts mechanism for multitask forecasting of future process variables. A cascaded adversarial-inspired joint training strategy further bridges the temporal disparity between process and quality data by enabling mutual adaptation between the two models. Experimental results on a real-world cement plant dataset demonstrate the effectiveness of the proposed framework. The MSAKAN model achieves a pretraining coefficient of determination (<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$R^{2}$</tex-math></inline-formula>) of 0.8724, and the jointly trained system reduces the mean absolute error of predictive f-CaO monitoring to 0.1573. These results confirm the synergy of the proposed approach and show substantial performance gains over state-of-the-art baselines.

title: Grid-Reg: Detector-Free Gridized Feature Learning and Matching for Large-Scale SAR–Optical Image Registration
authors: Xiaochen Wei, Weiwei Guo, Zenghui Zhang, Wenxian Yu
publish: IEEE Transactions on Circuits and Systems for Video Technology
abstract: It is highly challenging to register large-scale, heterogeneous SAR and optical images, particularly across platforms, due to significant geometric, radiometric, and temporal differences, which most existing methods struggle to address. To overcome these challenges, we propose Grid-Reg, a grid-based multimodal registration framework comprising a domain-robust descriptor extraction network, Hybrid Siamese Correlation Metric Learning Network (HSCMLNet), and a grid-based solver (Grid-Solver) for transformation parameter estimation. In heterogeneous imagery with large modality gaps and geometric differences, obtaining accurate correspondences is inherently difficult. To robustly measure similarity between gridded patches, HSCMLNet integrates a hybrid Siamese module with a correlation metric learning module (CMLModule) based on equiangular unit basis vectors (EUBVs), together with a manifold consistency loss to promote modality-invariant, discriminative feature learning. The Grid-Solver estimates transformation parameters by minimizing a global grid matching loss through a dual-loop search strategy to reliably find patch correspondences across entire images. Furthermore, we curate a challenging benchmark dataset for SAR-to-optical registration using UAV MiniSAR data and Google Earth optical imagery. Extensive experiments demonstrate that our proposed approach achieves superior performance over state-of-the-art methods.

title: QuadraFormer: Unified Query and Resource Forecasting for Database Workloads
authors: Songwei Han, Jiangtao Cui, Luming Sun, Yingfan Liu, Zhangqian Mu, Ming Li
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Data Mining (ICDM)
abstract: Modern database systems rely on workload forecasting to guide performance tuning, resource allocation, and query optimization. However, evolving query patterns and resource demands make accurate workload forecasting increasingly challenging. Most existing methods separately model query and resource behaviors using simple statistics or single-scale forecasting models, thereby ignoring their inherent correlations and leading to inaccurate modeling of real-world workload behaviors. In practice, real-world workloads show that query and resource behaviors are often interdependent and exhibit temporal dynamics. However, most forecasting methods overlook the inherent correlations between query and resource, as well as the temporal patterns across multi-scale resolutions in real workloads. To address these issues, we propose QuadraFormer, an end-to-end forecasting framework that jointly predicts query and resource usage. First, we introduce a unified workload representation that integrates query and resource information into a structured input, enabling joint modeling of workload across variates. Second, we develop an adaptive multi-scale routing mechanism that selects appropriate temporal resolutions to capture periodic patterns and multi-scale variability. Third, we design a novel Quadra-attention mechanism that models both intra- and inter-patch temporal dependencies as well as intra- and inter-dimensional dependencies. Extensive experiments on real-world workloads show that QuadraFormer consistently outperforms competitive baselines, achieving the highest F1 score of 98.38% and accuracy of 86.17%, with F1 improvements of up to 10.28% over the best-performing baseline, while reducing training time by up to 2.16×.

title: Selective Cloud Offloading for Accurate and Efficient Object Detection
authors: Davood Dehghani, Yueting Chen, Xiaohui Yu, Nick Koudas
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Data Mining (ICDM)
abstract: High-accuracy object detection on resource-constrained devices is becoming increasingly important for applications in autonomous systems, smart surveillance, and mobile computing. However, deploying high-performance object detection models on these devices is impractical due to computational limitations, and transmitting and processing all data on a much more powerful remote server running significantly more complex and accurate models, known as full cloud offloading, incurs high latency and is constrained by network bandwidth. In this paper, we propose a selective cloud offloading framework that provides users with control over the tradeoff between prediction accuracy and processing cost. Our approach employs a lightweight object detection model on the edge to make initial predictions, leveraging conformal prediction to quantify uncertainty. Only high-uncertainty regions are offloaded to the cloud, where more powerful models refine predictions, improving overall detection accuracy. To further optimize efficiency, multiple uncertain regions are combined into a single image before offloading, reducing transmission and processing costs. We present the architecture of our system and evaluate its performance on real datasets, demonstrating that it achieves cloud-level accuracy while significantly reducing offloading overhead.

title: A Stacking-Based Ensemble Framework for Anomaly Detection in High-Dimensional Data
authors: Mayank Mehra, Senthil Vadivu M, Jeevanand ES
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent and Innovative Technologies in Computing, Electrical and Electronics (IITCEE)
abstract: Research suggests that return and refund fraud poses a significant challenge to the fashion e-commerce, leading to financial losses and operational inefficiency. Traditional anomaly detection methods struggle with class imbalance and inconsistency across a single model approach. In this work, we propose a novel Hybrid Stacking Ensemble Framework which integrates Isolation Forest, One-Class Support Vector Machine (OCSVM), and Autoencoder with a Logistic Regression meta-classifier. To address the severe imbalance in the dataset, SMOTE was used to generate synthetic samples for minority class. Experiment demonstrated that while baseline imbalanced dataset achieved an accuracy of 76% and F1-score of 0.76, the resulted dataset with SMOTE achieved approximately 0.92 F1-score. This paper contributes a hybrid ensemble and data augmentation pipeline that can serve as a foundation for suture anomaly detection research.

title: Deep Learning-Based Gait Analysis from Wearable Sensors for Early Parkinson's Disease Detection
authors: Kumar P, Muthu Kumar S, Senthil Pandi S, Manjunath T C
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent and Innovative Technologies in Computing, Electrical and Electronics (IITCEE)
abstract: Parkinson's Disease (PD) is a progressive neurodegenerative disorder that impacts a person's motor control, causing symptoms like tremors, muscle stiffness, and problems with balance. Early detection is vital for starting treatment and slowing the disease's progression. However, traditional clinical exams often miss the subtle initial signs, leading to delayed diagnosis. Recent advances in wearable technology provide a non-invasive way to monitor gait patterns, which often contain early clues of PD-related motor decline. This study presents a deep learning framework that uses inertial measurement units (IMUs) and pressure insoles to analyze spatiotemporal gait dynamics. The framework uses Convolutional Neural Networks (CNNs) to capture the spatial features of gait and Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM) networks to learn and represent their temporal dynamics. To improve its robustness and ability to generalize, our pipeline includes data augmentation, dimensionality reduction, and attention mechanisms. It also features an Explainable AI (XAI) component, using Grad-CAM and SHAP to highlight key gait features that are most relevant to PD onset. We validated the framework on public benchmark datasets and custom gait recordings. Our results show high classification accuracy that surpasses traditional statistical and machine learning methods. The proposed approach connects wearable sensing, advanced AI, and clinical decision support, providing a scalable, accurate, and interpretable solution for the early detection of PD+.

title: Comparative Analysis of Machine Learning and Deep Learning Algorithms in Distinguishing AI-Generated and Human-Written Essays
authors: Adrian Prakoso H, Kemas M Lhaksmana
publish: 2025 2nd Beyond Technology Summit on Informatics International Conference (BTS-I2C)
abstract: This study presents a comparative analysis of machine learning (ML) and deep learning (DL) approaches for distinguishing essays generated by artificial intelligence (AI) from those written by humans. A linear-kernel Support Vector Machine (SVM) and a Random Forest (RF) classifier using Term Frequency–Inverse Document Frequency (TF-IDF) features were compared against a fine-tuned DistilBERT model. The experiments were conducted on a balanced dataset of 7,766 samples and evaluated using multiple runs to ensure statistical robustness. Results show that SVM achieved the highest accuracy (91.06%), while DistilBERT provided a competitive F1-score with better precision–recall balance, reflecting its stronger contextual understanding. In contrast, RF offered high recall but tended to overclassify AI-generated texts. These findings highlight the complementary strengths of classical lexical-based models and transformer-based architectures, demonstrating that both remain effective for AI-generated text detection in academic integrity and content authenticity applications.

title: ATERS: An Advanced Text Emotion Recognition System for Non-Binary Sentiment Analysis Based on Fine-Tuned DistilBERT
authors: Hamza M. Alabdallah, Suleiman Y. Yerima
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Metaverse, and Cybersecurity (ICAMAC)
abstract: In an era where digital communication is pervasive, the ability to automatically interpret the emotional expressions of text presents a unique challenge and opportunity for advancements in natural language processing. This research introduces the Advanced Text Emotion Recognition System (ATERS), a system made by utilizing the streamlined and efficient architecture of DistilBERT, a distilled version of the BERT model. ATERS is designed to accurately classify text into distinct emotional states, addressing the complex spectrum of human emotions beyond simple sentiment polarity. The system was trained and evaluated on a dataset comprising tweets annotated with six emotional categories: joy, sadness, anger, fear, love, and surprise. Through a combination of feature extraction and fine-tuning techniques, ATERS demonstrated significant accuracy of 93.75% in emotion classification, outperforming traditional sentiment analysis models. In this paper, we detail the methodologies employed in developing ATERS, its evaluation metrics, and insights gained from its performance. This study highlights the potential of transformer-based models in emotion recognition tasks and contributes to the broader understanding of sentiment analysis, paving the way for future applications that require advanced emotional insight.

title: Lightweight YOLOv8 Model for Detecting Downy Mildew on Golden Alisha Melon Leaves
authors: Hendra Supendar, Jufriadif Na’am, Mochamad Wahyudi, Sumanto
publish: 2025 2nd Beyond Technology Summit on Informatics International Conference (BTS-I2C)
abstract: Early detection of downy mildew, a major disease affecting melon crops, is crucial to minimize yield losses and support sustainable precision agriculture. This study aims to develop an automatic detection model for downy mildew on Golden Alisha melon leaves using a computer vision approach based on the YOLOv8 architecture. A custom dataset was collected from real agricultural fields in Serang, Indonesia, consisting of a relatively small number of healthy and infected leaves that were manually annotated by experts. To mitigate the limited dataset size and enhance model generalization, the data were preprocessed through resizing, normalization, and extensive augmentation to generate diverse image variations that better reflect real-field conditions. The YOLOv8 model was trained with tuned hyperparameters to balance accuracy and computational efficiency. Experimental results show that the proposed model achieved outstanding performance, with a Precision of 0.983, Recall of 0.93, mAP@50 of 0.995, and mAP@50–95 of 0.9, indicating high detection accuracy and robustness under varying lighting conditions. These results demonstrate that the YOLOv8- based model can accurately identify and localize infected areas in real time, providing a reliable tool for early disease diagnosis and effective crop management in the field, while also highlighting the need for future work involving larger and more diverse datasets.

title: Scalable Automated Cloud-RAN Dimensioning for 6G Networks
authors: Mbarka Soualhia, Chunyan Fu
publish: 2026 IEEE 16th Annual Computing and Communication Workshop and Conference (CCWC)
abstract: Automated Cloud-Radio Access Network (Cloud-RAN) dimensioning is critical for achieving cost-effective and scalable 6G deployments. Traditional planning methods rely on manual analysis and static assumptions, making them unsuitable for dynamic, large-scale environments. This paper presents an automated Cloud-RAN dimensioning framework that jointly optimizes site selection, gNB allocation, and area coverage under demand and resource constraints. The problem is formulated as a Mixed-Integer Nonlinear Programming (MINLP) model that minimizes total deployment cost and fronthaul distance while capturing nonlinear infrastructure-performance interactions. To address scalability, the framework integrates multiple metaheuristic solvers - Simulated Annealing (SA), N on-Dominated Sorting Genetic Algorithm II (NSGA-II), and Particle Swarm Optimization (PSO) - within a unified execution environment. Comparative evaluation includes baseline methods, such as Greedy heuristics and Mixed-Integer Quadratically Constrained Programming (MIQCP), highlighting trade-offs between solution quality and computational effort. Results show that SA is effective for small-scale networks, while NSGA-II and PSO provide superior convergence and cost efficiency for medium- and large-scale scenarios. The study offers practical guidance for selecting appropriate optimization strategies for diverse 6G Cloud-RAN planning problems.

title: Design and Verification of a Glitch-Filtered Clock Gating Controller Using System Verilog
authors: Shriniketana Kalale, Subodh Kumar Panda, Manjunath G Asuti, Sudarshan D
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent and Innovative Technologies in Computing, Electrical and Electronics (IITCEE)
abstract: Dynamic Power reduction is a critical design aspect in modern FPGA based digital systems and SoC's. Clock gating is a widely implemented technique in reducing dynamic power consumption by disabling the clock toggles during inactive period of the system. However, conventional clock gating techniques do not offer a mechanism to filter the glitches which are present in the input signals. The proposed work offers a solution to overcome the glitches and optimizes the dynamic power consumption. The design is synthesized in the Cadence environment using 90 nm technology. The design is verified using a System Verilog based test-bench with randomized input pulses to validate the filtered signal and gated clock behaviour. The optimized area and dynamic power achieved are <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$128.556 \mu \mathrm{m}^{2}$</tex> and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$2.68 \mu \mathrm{W}$</tex> respectively. This methodology can be effectively utilized in FPGA and SoC based designs to enhance energy efficiency and improve overall system reliability, making it highly suitable for complex digital applications.

title: Early Diabetes Detection And Tracking System Using Ensemble Learning
authors: K. Seshagiri Rao, Pavithra Akula, Sushmitha Daram, Divija Bezawada, Swetha Boppisetti, Meghana Chadurupalli
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Artificial Intelligence for Innovations in Healthcare Industries (ICAIIHI)
abstract: Diabetes is a disease caused by high sugar levels in the blood when insulin is not produced or used properly. Early prediction is important to reduce the chances of serious health problems. Machine learning helps in predicting diabetes by learning patterns from past medical data. In this project, health features like glucose, BMI, age, insulin, and pregnancies are used for training. The data is normalized using Standard Scaler so that all values are in the same range. Since the dataset is imbalanced, SMOTE is applied to balance positive and negative cases. Three models are trained: Random Forest, Gradient Boosting, and XGBoost. These models are combined using a Voting Classifier, which improves accuracy through ensemble learning. The performance is measured using accuracy, confusion matrix, and classification report. Finally, a Streamlit app with Gemini AI is developed to provide predictions, advice, and doctor recommendations in a simple way.

title: Soft Computing Techniques for Predictive Student Performance Analysis
authors: Rajesh D, S. M. Srilanga Meenatshi, Arijit Das, Ashutosh Singh, V. Purushothama Raju, Sushil Shukla
publish: 2025 World Conference on Cutting-Edge Science and Technology (WCCEST)
abstract: The global educational institutions are increasingly embracing the data-driven approach to enhance learning among students and the performance of the institutions. In that regard, the current study provides an empirical study of soft computing methods of predicting student performance with the use of Kaggle educational data. The paper looks at hybrid systems combining fuzzy logic, neural networks and genetic algorithms. It is important to note that the FCM-PSO-ANFIS model showed the best predictive accuracy of 92.40%, whereas its MSE was 0.16667 and RMSE 0.40826. Results indicate that behavioral and academic factors are the most influential, which lead to 8 and 5% accuracy decreases in their absence. It is a hybrid framework that enables early intervention and personalized learning using interpretable, robust analytics.

title: A Smart System Protection Scheme (SPS) for a Resilient Sarawak State Grid, Malaysia
authors: Jacqueline Anak Ban, Lo Thuan Kai
publish: 2025 25th Conference of the Electric Power Supply Industry (CEPSI)
abstract: The Sarawak State Grid in northern Borneo, Malaysia, faces challenges in maintaining grid stability due to its geography and the configuration of its power system. The grid relies primarily on hydropower, supplemented by gas turbine and coal-fired plants, with most major power plants located in the northern part of the state. Electricity is transmitted through a 275 kV transmission backbone, but the significant distance between generation sites and load centers, coupled with the complexities of long-distance transmission, leads to operational difficulties. These include risks like frequency instability, voltage instability, thermal overloads, and potential system failures. These issues are common in global power systems.To mitigate these risks and ensure grid stability, the System Protection Scheme (SPS) was introduced in 2009. SPS monitors real-time grid conditions and automatically takes corrective actions during disturbances to maintain system integrity and prevent blackouts. Over time, the SPS has evolved in response to operational experience and the increasing complexity of the grid. A major challenge in 2020 was network latency, worsened by the growing number of contingencies in the system. To address this, key improvements were made to the SPS infrastructure, including shifting computational tasks from the Monitoring Control Workstation (MCW) at the control center to the Data Acquisition Workstation (DAW) at substations. This decentralized approach reduced the burden on the MCW and improved response times during disturbances. Additionally, the Data Acquisition Concentrator (DAC) was upgraded to handle tasks like load-shedding and feeder selection, further enhancing real-time responsiveness.The design of the SPS has also evolved to align with the growing demands of the Sarawak State Grid. Initially focused on event-based responses, the SPS now employs a proactive, continuous monitoring approach. This shift enables it to manage grid risks more effectively and maintain higher operational standards.The effectiveness of the SPS has been demonstrated through actual system events, where it successfully prevented blackouts and reduced recovery times. These events highlight the SPS's critical role in ensuring grid resilience and reliability, particularly during extreme conditions. As a result, the SPS has become an essential element of the mitigation strategy, enhancing the stability and resilience of the Sarawak State Grid.This paper traces the evolution of the SPS, its impact on grid stability, and its key role in safeguarding the transmission network from instability. It also presents case studies of real-world events to evaluate the SPS's performance and share insights from operational experiences.

title: Feasibility Study of Nearshore offshore Wind Power Development in Macau Under Dual Carbon Targets
authors: Chon Man Leong, Deng Ziyang
publish: 2025 25th Conference of the Electric Power Supply Industry (CEPSI)
abstract: This study explores the feasibility of establishing a demonstration offshore wind farm in Macau's nearshore waters against the backdrop of its "dual carbon" targets and energy transition, considering its limited geographical and natural resource conditions. Currently, Macau's energy structure heavily relies on external electricity imports and fossil fuels, making renewable energy development a crucial pathway for low-carbon transformation.The study first analyzes Macau's current energy situation and transition challenges, then evaluates potential offshore wind farm sites based on marine geography, wind resource characteristics and environmental constraints. Due to the lack of direct offshore wind monitoring data in Macau, this research adopts a multi-source data fusion approach, combining long-term onshore wind observations from Macau's Geophysical and Meteorological Bureau with offshore wind energy studies from adjacent regions.For wind farm design, comprehensive consideration is given to water depth, navigation channel restrictions and typhoon impacts in Macau's waters to propose suitable turbine selection and layout solutions. Power generation is estimated by using power curve, also estimate the reducible direct carbon emissions. Results show that certain nearshore areas in Macau have potential for developing small-to-medium scale offshore wind power, which could provide supplementary clean energy.This research provides technical references for Macau to explore demonstration offshore wind projects, offering valuable theoretical support and practical guidance for achieving optimized energy structure and sustainable development in Macau.

title: Efficient Transfer Learning of DINO-Based Transformers via MLP and Progressive Unfreezing
authors: Jae-Yong Seong, So-Yoon Kim, Bo-Min Kim, Hyun-Suk Lee, Seong-Geon Bae
publish: 2025 International Conference on Electrical and Computer Engineering Researches (ICECER)
abstract: This study proposes an efficient framework to improve the classification performance of Distillation with NO label(DINO)-based Vision Transformer models in a small labeled dataset environment. The proposed method utilizes powerful multi-crop-based data augmentation techniques and various visual transformations such as Auto Augment and Gaussian Blur to maximize the diversity of receptive fields and local features in the input image. In addition, by introducing a gradual unfreezing strategy that keeps the backbone network frozen during the initial 1 to 3 epochs of learning and trains only the Multi-Layer Perceptron(MLP)-based classification head, the problem of overfitting during fine-tuning was alleviated while maintaining a stable pre-trained generalized visual representation. The lightweight MLP head structure, AdamW optimizer, and Cosine Annealing learning rate scheduling were also applied to simultaneously improve the learning stability and convergence speed of the model. In extensive experiments centered on the STL-10 dataset, the proposed framework showed significant performance improvement in Top-1 accuracy, K-Nearest Neighbors(KNN) accuracy, and Linear Probe results compared to existing DINO models, and demonstrated more than 50% faster inference. Although the number of parameters in the MLP head increased, the actual execution efficiency was found to be rather improved thanks to parallel processing optimization and learning schedule adjustment. This study presents meaningful progress in further increasing the transfer learning efficiency and versatility of the self-supervised learning-based DINO-Transformer model, thereby significantly expanding the practical possibilities for field applications.Future research aims to improve more generalized performance and strengthen practical application possibilities by optimizing learning rates, diversifying augmentation policies, and automating backbone unfreezing.

title: Multi-modal Based Railway Accident Classification and Track Fault Prediction for optimized healthcare
authors: Saradha S, Jeevika G, Keran Loretta D, Monisa R J, Pooranidevi S
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Artificial Intelligence for Innovations in Healthcare Industries (ICAIIHI)
abstract: Railway transport is an important aspect of mobility that is susceptible to accidents such as derailments and machine malfunctions. Concentionl rule-based inspections are not scalable and generalized. In response to this gap, we suggest a multi-modal deep learning model, with a combination of structured incident data such as train speed logs,sensor measurements and unstructured track images, in order to obtain end-to-end accident severity and track fault classification. The system uses CNNs to analyze images and LSTM temporal-spatial models to analyze incident reports [1] and InceptionV3 backbone to improve the detection of visual defects [2]. SMOTE based augmentation [3] is used to reduce data imbalance and PCA is used to reduce the complexity of the feature space without discriminatory strength [4]. Enhancing ensemble techniques is done to optimize final predictions (XGBoost, LightGBM, CatBoost, Random Forest) [5]. This system aims to support railway safety management by accuractely predicting accident severity and rescue resource allocation. Experiments show the possibility of an 83% track fault detection rate and a 91% severity classification rate which is unique in that the system incorporates both structured and unstructured data. The framework offers a resource-efficient, real-time predictive solution to the railway safety system, and Vision Transformers and attention modules can potentially improve it [6].

title: BLB-HGNN: Bag of Little Bootstraps for Training Heterogeneous GNNs
authors: Aditya T. Vadlamani, Sama Salarian, Saket Gurukar, Srinivasan Parthasarathy
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Data Mining (ICDM)
abstract: Graphs can model complex relational data, which makes them invaluable in numerous machine-learning applications. While the graph's structure can be efficiently represented and stored, the associated feature memory is substantial. The feature memory can be terabytes or petabytes for web-scale graphs, especially at companies like Pinterest and Google. The extra feature memory can require external storage devices with slower I/O times for data loading, making training even slower. To address the storage issue with feature memory, we aim to reduce the number of training nodes needed through sampling, reducing the storage requirement. However, the problem is that simply training a model with fewer nodes will result in worse performance. To solve that problem, we propose BLB-HGNN, a training algorithm based on the Bag of Little Bootstraps. BLB-HGNN independently trains several replicas of the architecture on different subsamples of the data. For each training epoch, our blb-sampler creates a bootstrap resampling of the data for the replica to train on. The trained replicas are merged using parameter averaging and then fine-tuned for inference. We conduct experiments with the OGB_MAG and MAG240M datasets to demonstrate the effectiveness of BLB-HGNN over simple training. We also conduct experiments on the impact of different sampling methods and model merging techniques. With almost no additional runtime cost, BLB-HGNN consistently provides a performance boost of up to 5% compared to standard training with the same training budget. Applying a non-uniform sampling method, such as Personalized PageRank or Spread Sampling, further improves performance. Furthermore, BLB-HGNN can achieve performance close to full dataset training with less than 50% of the training data on specific models. To our knowledge, this is the first work addressing the storage problem and uses Bag of Little Bootstraps for HGNN training.

title: A Universal Metric of Dataset Similarity for Cross-Silo Federated Learning
authors: Ahmed Elhussein, Gamze Gürsoy
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Data Mining (ICDM)
abstract: Federated Learning (FL) enables collaborative model training across institutions without sharing raw data, making it valuable for privacy-sensitive domains like healthcare. However, FL performance deteriorates significantly when client datasets are non-IID. While dataset similarity metrics could guide collaboration decisions, existing approaches have critical limitations: unbounded costs that lack interpretability across domains, requirements for direct data access that violate FL's privacy constraints, and poor sample efficiency. We propose a novel metric that extracts model representations after a single federated training round to predict whether collaboration will improve performance. Our approach formulates similarity assessment as an optimal transport problem with a hybrid cost function that captures both feature-level differences and label distribution divergence between clients. We ensure privacy through a careful composition of Secure Multiparty Computation (SMC) and Differential Privacy (DP) mechanisms. Our theoretical analysis establishes a formal connection between the proposed metric and weight divergence in federated training, explaining why early-round activations can predict long-term collaboration outcomes. Empirically, the metric remains tightly correlated with weight divergence throughout training, reinforcing the validity of our single-round probe. Extensive experiments on synthetic benchmarks and real-world medical imaging tasks demonstrate that our metric reliably identifies beneficial collaborations providing practitioners with an actionable tool for participant selection in cross-silo FL.

title: On the Necessity of Multi-Domain Explanation: An Uncertainty Principle Approach for Deep Time Series Models
authors: Shahbaz Rezaei, Avishai Halev, Xin Liu
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Data Mining (ICDM)
abstract: A prevailing approach to explain time series models is to generate attribution in time domain input. A recent development in time series XAI is the concept of explanation spaces, where any model trained in the time domain can be interpreted with any existing XAI method in alternative domains, such as frequency or time-frequency domain. The prevailing approach is to present XAI attributions either in the time domain or in the domain where the attribution is most sparse. In this paper, we demonstrate that in certain cases, XAI methods can generate attributions that highlight fundamentally different features in the time and frequency domains that are not direct counterparts of one another. This observation suggests that both domains' attributions should be presented to achieve a more comprehensive interpretation. Thus it shows the necessity of multi-domain explanation. To quantify when such cases arise, we introduce the uncertainty principle (UP), originally developed in quantum mechanics and later studied in harmonic analysis and signal processing, to the XAI literature. This principle establishes a lower bound on how much a signal can be simultaneously localized in both the time and frequency domains. By leveraging this concept, we assess whether attributions in the time and frequency domains violate this bound, indicating that they emphasize distinct features. In other words, UP provides a sufficient condition that the time and frequency domain explanations do not match and, hence, should be both presented to the end user. We validate the effectiveness of this approach across various deep learning models, XAI methods, and a wide range of classification and forecasting datasets. The frequent occurrence of UP violations across various datasets and XAI methods highlights the limitations of existing approaches that focus solely on time-domain explanations. This underscores the need for multi-domain explanations as a new paradigm. The source code is available at https://github.com/shrezaei/TS-X-spaces

title: Efficient Sequential Recommendation for Long Term User Interest Via Personalization
authors: Qiang Zhang, Hanchao Yu, Ivan Ji, Chen Yuan, Yi Zhang, Chihuang Liu, Xiaolong Wang, Christopher E. Lambert, Ren Chen, Chen Kovacs, Xinzhu Bei, Renqin Cai, Rui Li, Lizhu Zhang, Xiangjun Fan, Qunshu Zhang, Benyu Zhang
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Data Mining (ICDM)
abstract: Recent years have witnessed success of sequential modeling, generative recommender, and large language model for recommendation. Though the scaling law has been validated for sequential models, it showed inefficiency in computational capacity when considering real-world applications like recommendation, due to the non-linear(quadratic) increasing nature of the transformer model. To improve the efficiency of the sequential model, we introduced a novel approach to sequential recommendation that leverages personalization techniques to enhance efficiency and performance. Our method compresses long user interaction histories into learnable tokens, which are then combined with recent interactions to generate recommendations. This approach significantly reduces computational costs while maintaining high recommendation accuracy. Our method could be applied to existing transformer based recommendation models, e.g., HSTU and HLLM. Extensive experiments on multiple sequential models demonstrate its versatility and effectiveness. Source code is available at https://github.com/facebookresearch/PerSRec.

title: A Hybrid Deep Learning Approach for Phishing Detection Using TabNet and Transformer-Based Text Analysis
authors: K. Ananthajothi, K. Lokesh
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent and Innovative Technologies in Computing, Electrical and Electronics (IITCEE)
abstract: Phishing remains an important threat to cybersecurity, where human trust and technological weaknesses are leveraged to steal sensitive details like credentials, money-related information, and personal data. State-of-the-art detection models such as TabNet work efficiently on well-structured tabular data but struggle with advanced phishing techniques involving both URL manipulation and misleading text content. This project suggests a hybrid deep learning architecture with TabNet for structured features—URL features, SSL certificate expiration, and domain age—combined with a Transformer-based model for processing textual data like email bodies and webpage HTML. Through the combination of insights between structured and unstructured data, the model achieves more complex patterns than single-type data-based systems. Using benchmark datasets—like the well-known Phishing Websites Dataset and a list of phishing emails dataset—the hybrid model achieved 96.7% accuracy with high precision, recall, and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{F 1}$</tex>-score. The multi-head attention mechanism enables the detection of refined obfuscation patterns, and SHAP-based feature importance analysis delivers interpretability. Real-time evaluation guaranteed efficient detection with more than 96 % confidence on unseen phishing instances altogether. In general, this hybrid approach gives a robust, scalable, and interpretable phishing detector solution superior to conventional TabNet-alone frameworks and enhances cybersecurity defense even further.

title: From Detection to Action: Integrating Machine Learning and Large Language Models for Child Online Safety
authors: Sherif E. Abdelhamid, Mohamed S. Elhussiny
publish: 2026 IEEE 16th Annual Computing and Communication Workshop and Conference (CCWC)
abstract: Online grooming and exploitation have become increasingly pervasive threats targeting minors through social media, gaming platforms, and online communication channels. The subtle, adaptive, and manipulative nature of such predatory language presents a significant challenge for both parents and automated systems in terms of early detection. This research presents a comprehensive hybrid framework that integrates machine learning (ML), natural language processing (NLP), and large language models (LLMs) to detect, explain, and mitigate online predatory behavior. A hybrid dataset of chat messages was constructed using both human and AI-generated content (GPT-4o and Claude 3.5 Sonnet) to ensure linguistic diversity and enhance model generalization. Multiple supervised classifiers, including Support Vector Machine (SVM), Logistic Regression, Random Forest, K-Nearest Neighbors (KNN), and an Ensemble Voting Classifier, were trained using TF-IDF and Word2Vec features. The Ensemble model achieved the highest performance with 96.9 % accuracy using TF-IDF and improved to 97.5 % when augmented with part-of-speech (POS) tag frequencies. Beyond classification, this research incorporates Gemini 2.0 Flash, a large language model designed to enhance interpretability and provide real-time support. Gemini analyzes unsafe messages, explains why they are considered threats, highlights alarming keywords, and provides parents with clear, actionable safety recommendations. In an evaluation of 200 unsafe messages, Gemini's explanations and suggested actions aligned with human expert judgments by over 99% and conformed to best-practice frameworks established by the Internet Crimes Against Children (ICAC) Task Force, the U.S. Department of Homeland Security's Know2Protect initiative, and INTERPOL's Crimes Against Children program. To operationalize these capabilities, we developed a multi-tier web platform named SafeGuard. The platform consolidates the ML models, Gemini reasoning engine, and real-time monitoring tools into an interactive environment featuring dashboards, alert panels, message scanners, and guidance modules. SafeGuard transforms automated predictions into interpretable and trustworthy digital safety intelligence, enabling parents and guardians to understand, verify, and respond to threats in real time. The findings demonstrate that combining lexical, syntactic, and semantic analysis with expert-level LLM reasoning provides a robust and ethical approach to combating online grooming and exploitation. The system represents a significant advancement in explainable and human-centered AI for child online protection.

title: SOFTAT: Towards Socially-Aware, Fair, and Trustworthy Assistive Technology
authors: Youna Jung, Yoosun Chung, Chanhee Choi, Sunme Lee, Songyon Shin
publish: 2026 IEEE 16th Annual Computing and Communication Workshop and Conference (CCWC)
abstract: Assistive Technology (AT) plays a vital role in enhancing the independence, participation, and social inclusion of individuals with disabilities. Among AT solutions, Augmentative and Alternative Communication (AAC) systems are particularly significant, as they directly mediate users' communication, social identity, and self-expression. Despite advances in hardware and software, existing AAC technologies often focus on technical functionality while neglecting user context, emotional needs, intersectional diversity, and security concerns. In addition, AAC users have historically received limited attention in marketing and consumer research. To address this gap, this paper examines key limitations in current AAC technologies and systems across three dimensions: social awareness, design, and security. To address these limitations, we propose the SOFTAT (Socially-aware, Fair, and Trustworthy Assistive Technology) framework, which emphasizes user-centric design, inclusive and adaptive communication support, and robust security and privacy mechanisms. By integrating social, design, and security perspectives, SOFTAT offers a roadmap for developing AAC systems that empower users, foster authentic communication, and enhance autonomy and dignity.

title: DP-Prox: A Robust and Differentially Private Framework for Federated Instruction Tuning of Small LLMs on 8 GB Edge Devices
authors: Vinay Venkatesh, Yichen Liu
publish: 2026 IEEE 16th Annual Computing and Communication Workshop and Conference (CCWC)
abstract: This paper introduces DP-Prox, an end-to-end framework for robust and differentially private federated instruction tuning of small Large Language Models (LLMs) on 8 GB edge devices. DP-Prox is instantiated using the 3.8 billionparameter Phi-3-mini model with 4-bit Quantized Low-Rank Adaptation (QLoRA), and combines (i) FedProx-style proximal regularization on low-rank adapters to stabilize optimization under highly Non-IID smart-home data, and (ii) Differentially Private SGD (DP-SGD) applied directly to adapter gradients to provide user-level <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(\epsilon, \delta)$</tex>-differential privacy against an honest-but-curious server. In a realistic IoT instruction-following benchmark with Dirichlet-skewed clients (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\alpha=0.3$</tex>), DP-Prox improves F1-score from 0.80 (Edge-FIT with FedAvg) to 0.82 while reducing convergence rounds from <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$T=50$</tex> to <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$T \approx 40$</tex>. Under a strict privacy budget <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\left(\epsilon=5, \delta=10^{-5}\right)$</tex>, DP-Prox maintains an F1-score of 0.77 with only <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$+15 \%-20 \%$</tex> local overhead, and preserves the communication efficiency of QLoRA-based federated training. Qualitative analyses further show that DP-Prox yields more consistent instruction-following behavior across heterogeneous homes, especially on multi-device routines and privacy-sensitive queries. Together, these results position DP-Prox as a practical and regulation-ready blueprint for deploying small, edge-resident LLMs in smart-home IoT environments.

title: Explainable AI in Oral Carcinoma Detection: Legal Challenges and Policy Pathways in India
authors: Keya De Mukhopadhyay, Tanurup Das, Abhisikta Basu
publish: 2025 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence for Computing, Astronomy and Renewable Energy (AICARE)
abstract: Oral carcinoma is a disproportionate burden on India’s public health system, causing almost one-third of the world’s oral cancer incidents, mainly due to tobacco usage, inequity in the socio-economic structure, and late presentation. Artificial Intelligence (AI) provides new avenues for early identification, mainly in resource-scarce environments, but the “black-box” behavior of most AI models undermines their clinical credibility. Explainable AI (XAI), which focuses on transparency, understanding, and responsibility, becomes a necessity in healthcare and not merely a luxury. The current paper investigates the potential role of AI and XAI in the identification of oral carcinoma in the Indian setting. It looks at epidemiological patterns, gaps in detection, and new AI programs, prior to the critical discussion of the legal and ethical issues concerning the protection of data, liability, intellectual property right, and the vacuum in regulations. comparative points are derived from the U.S. FDA’s Good Machine Learning Practices and the EU AI Act. Based in Beauchamp and Childress’ principles in bioethics, the current paper contends that India needs a convergent legal–policy structure in order for XAI in oncology to be safe, inclusive, and equitable.

title: Innovative Deep Learning Approaches for Robust Medical Image Denoising: A Study of Contemporary Techniques and Future Prospects
authors: Bhavana Godavarthi, S. Arunmozhi
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Artificial Intelligence for Innovations in Healthcare Industries (ICAIIHI)
abstract: The combination of twenty years of computational and internet development and data storage progress with wireless technology has revolutionized medical imaging together with diagnostic procedures. Medicine professionals use modern innovations to diagnose diseases better and maintain active patient health conditions. Medical imaging technologies produce visually important data that sophisticated vision systems cannot recognize by themselves. Image deterioration occurs during acquisition and transmission stages because of noise contamination which reduces their quality. Medical imaging techniques at an advanced level help understand disease development processes due to their ability to deliver essential information about pathogenesis. The required quality of diagnostic images can be obtained through image processing techniques. Medical imaging systems have expanded diagnostic application spaces because they supply detailed pictures of patient anatomy and physiological functions. The acquisition of high-quality images requires a Signal-to-Noise Ratio (SNR) that reaches peak levels to achieve through multiple scans of the same subject. The diagnostic field benefits from various modern imaging methods including MRI and PET in addition to CT along with X-ray equipment and including Fluoroscopy and Ultrasound devices and using Endoscopy and Elastography and Tactile Imaging to offer diagnostic and treatment services to patients. Medical diagnostic screenings that involve feature extraction and object recognition processes face challenges because of imaging noise disturbances. The incorrect analysis or ineffective treatment of patients can occur through miscommunication caused by noise when appropriate measures are not taken to process the information. The main purpose of image denoising techniques focuses on eliminating noise elements while maintaining image features of important significance.

title: Performance Analysis of Integral Sliding Mode Controller (Ismc) for Output Power Regulation in a Dynamic Wireless Charging System Applicable to EV
authors: Varun Patil, Sahana Kalligudd, Rajeev R Pathak
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent and Innovative Technologies in Computing, Electrical and Electronics (IITCEE)
abstract: Dynamic wireless charging is a developing method to extend the driving range of electric vehicles (EVs) without interrupting their operation. A major challenge in such systems lies in maintaining stable output power at the secondary side, where fluctuations arise due to varying coupling conditions and load disturbances. This study evaluates the performance of three control strategies: proportional-integral-derivative control (PID), sliding mode control (SMC) and integral sliding mode control (ISMC) in regulating the output power of a buck converter in the receiver stage of a dynamic wireless charging system. A reference power is considered, and simulation results are obtained under dynamic operating conditions. The results show that while the PID controller provides basic regulation, it suffers from slower transient response and limited robustness. The SMC approach improves disturbance rejection but shows minor deviations from the reference. In contrast, the ISMC controller achieves superior performance, with output power consistently tracking the reference value and exhibiting strong robustness against disturbances. These findings highlight ISMC as a promising approach for reliable and efficient dynamic wireless EV charging.

title: Predictive Model for Starlink Maritime Performance Using Multi-Horizon RandomForest
authors: Md Muntasir Hossain, Xingya Liu, Helen H. Lou, Ruhai Wang, Kazi Fazlee Rabbi
publish: 2026 IEEE 16th Annual Computing and Communication Workshop and Conference (CCWC)
abstract: Low Earth orbit (LEO) satellite systems have become a crucial enabler of broadband access for maritime industries, where traditional networks are unavailable. However, the high mobility of LEO constellations and constantly changing weather conditions result in unpredictable link fluctuations, limiting the ability of maritime platforms to plan bandwidth usage proactively. To the best of our knowledge, no prior work has developed a short-term predictive model for maritime LEO connectivity using real experimental field measurements. This paper proposes a data-driven forecasting model that predicts future downlink throughput using multi-horizon RandomForest regression. The model is trained using real experimental coastal measurement data incorporating recent throughput history, network-layer indicators, and environmental variables. The proposed approach reduces mean absolute error by approximately 31 % compared to a persistence baseline for 15-minute horizons. It maintains a measurable improvement at 30 minutes, despite increased stochasticity. These findings confirm that proactive bandwidth awareness is feasible on maritime platforms and can effectively support operational decisions such as adaptive streaming, routing, and resource scheduling. The performance gap between forecasting horizons also highlights the need for expanded offshore datasets to improve prediction robustness under harsher maritime environments.

title: Forecast Residential Power Usage via Machine Learning and Climatic Feature Incorporations
authors: Tanushree B Indresh, Parvathi C
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent and Innovative Technologies in Computing, Electrical and Electronics (IITCEE)
abstract: Accurate forecasting of residential electricity demand requires models that capture both usage patterns and external environmental influences. This study develops a machine learning–based framework that integrates historical household energy consumption with weather variables to improve prediction accuracy. The UCI Household Electric Power Consumption dataset was augmented with external temperature, humidity, and related climatic parameters to incorporate environmental context. After comprehensive preprocessing and feature engineering, five models—Linear Regression, Random Forest, XGBoost, LSTM, and GRU—were evaluated using MAE, RMSE, and R<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup>. Results show that weather-integrated features significantly enhanced forecasting performance, with XGBoost achieving the best accuracy (R<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup> = 0.63). This work demonstrates that incorporating weather conditions into consumption modelling provides more reliable forecasts and offers a scalable approach for smart energy management in residential settings.

title: Investigating Least-Cost Scenarios of SSEG Wheeling: A case study of Namakwa District Municipality
authors: Palma Maluleke, Obafemi O. Olatunji, Paul A. Adedeji, Nkosinathi Madushele
publish: 2025 International Conference on Electrical and Computer Engineering Researches (ICECER)
abstract: The increased national support for Small-Scale Embedded Generation (SSEG) and the dispersed locations with abundant renewable energy sources across the country have necessitated power wheeling from point of generation to final use. However, ensuring energy affordability requires that wheeling be carried out at the least possible cost to ensure system sustainability. The Namakwa District Municipality (NDM), being the largest district in the country by land area, is replete with abundant renewable energy resources across different municipalities. As the district explores wheeling option to meet its growing energy demand, it is crucial that this is carried out at the least possible cost. Hence, this study aims to investigate the least-cost SSEG wheeling scenarios. The Power Flow MW-mile method was employed to analyze energy supply and demand by the off-taker at the point of delivery. This method was simulated using the Digsilent Model to assess the cost implications of charging for reactive energy or energy losses on the overall wheeling costs. The method also incorporates financial transaction factors, such as the average electricity selling price by NDM and lifecycle cost of SSEG, to determine transmission wheeling costs. Three scenarios were investigated to assess wheeling costs: Business as usual (no wheeling), Wheeling at 1.3 MW of 4 MW, and Wheeling at 3 MW of 4 MW. Findings from this study indicate that scenario 3 is the most preferred, offering significant cost savings and revenue generation compared to the other scenarios. Based on Scenario 3, the estimated annual cost of wheeling 3 MW of power is approximately R491 952.09, which is substantially lower than the R2 638 950.00 spent on bulk electricity purchases.

title: Predictive Modeling of Drug Efficacy using Machine Learning Algorithms
authors: Panduranga Ravi Teja, Rishav Dubey, Animesh Kumar Sharma
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Artificial Intelligence for Innovations in Healthcare Industries (ICAIIHI)
abstract: Integrating artificial intelligence (AI) and machine learning (ML) technologies into the pharmaceutical industry transforms the processes involved in drug discovery, development, and deployment predictive modelling. The predictive models based on ML support the determination of drug efficacy and the prediction of drug toxicity and stability in numerous diseases, including oncology, neurodegenerative diseases, infectious diseases, chronic diseases and more. Formulation optimization, personalized dosing regimens, and numerous other facets of the drug development process are greatly facilitated. ML technology applied in optimizing the design of clinical trials, identifying patient-specific response patterns, and predicting high adverse event risk patients is well documented and assists in cost and time reduction in the drug development process. AI techniques also transform quality control, supply chain, and health delivery predictive analytics systems. The delivery of precision medicine and the achievement of optimal outcomes in patients are the direct results of AI. Predictive modelling in drug adherence, therapeutic drug monitoring, and nanocarrier drug delivery systems is a testimony to the versatility of the discipline at both preclinical and clinical stages. In spite of hurdles relating to integration of data, interpretability, and ethics, current trends like large language models, hybrid algorithms, and topological descriptors offer promising directions to overcome these constraints. Together, these studies highlight the innovative power of ML-driven predictive modeling in speeding up innovation in pharma chemistry and healthcare along with safety, efficacy, and patient-centric outcomes.

title: A Survey on AI Powered Speech Enhancement Solutions to Laryngeal Disorders
authors: Abhigna Patel CH, Siva Priyanka S, Triveni P, Varsha B, Bhanu Chandra Marepally
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent and Innovative Technologies in Computing, Electrical and Electronics (IITCEE)
abstract: Laryngeal disorders can have a major impact on speech quality, leading to communication difficulties and a reduced quality of life. For effective treatment and management, it's crucial to detect these speech disorders early and accurately. Recent technological advances in deep learning and artificial intelligence have catalysed innovations in automated voice pathol- ogy detection systems. This comprehensive review synthesizes findings from recent literature, including the in-depth exploration of speech disorder detection strategies in laryngeal diseases and the application of deep transfer learning for multi-class classification of voice disorders. Furthermore, the integration of assistive technologies such as IoT-based self-assistive devices for individuals with sensory impairments illustrates the broadening impact of intelligent systems in healthcare. We systematically examine the fundamentals of laryngeal pathology, review feature extraction and machine learning methodologies, and critically assess state-of-the-art deep learning models for voice disorder classification. Challenges in data availability, model generalizabil- ity, and real-world clinical translation are discussed. Finally, we outline emerging opportunities at the intersection of medical informatics, deep learning, and assistive technology for developing robust, scalable, and patient-centric solutions for speech disorder detection and rehabilitation in laryngeal disease contexts.

title: Optimization of Green Electricity Injection into Smart Grids Using a Tensor-Based Approach: A Case Study of the City of Antsiranana (Madagascar)
authors: Juan Pedro Randriamaromila, Jaomiary Antonio, Jean Nirinarison Razafinjaka
publish: 2025 International Conference on Electrical and Computer Engineering Researches (ICECER)
abstract: This research explores the optimization of green electricity injection into smart grids using a tensor-based modeling approach, with a case study focusing on the city of Antsiranana, Madagascar. The study evaluates the current integration level of renewable energies, especially solar PV, and proposes the use of Natural Gradient Descent (NGD) combined with tensorial grid modeling to reduce losses and improve operational efficiency. Results show significant reductions in energy loss, enhanced grid stability, and better load demand response. This paper provides a comprehensive methodological framework for decentralized smart grid deployment in resource-rich, infrastructure-poor regions

title: ElectraGuard:A Blockchain-Based Opinion Polling System for Tamper-Proof Surveys
authors: Kamalika Bhowal, Srijit Mondal, Abir Chattopadhyay, Kousik Dasgupta
publish: 2025 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence for Computing, Astronomy and Renewable Energy (AICARE)
abstract: In our Society public opinion surveys are necessary for understanding societal viewpoints, but the conventional polling platforms are at a high risk of forgery, data breaches and manipulation. To ensure the reliability and integrity of polling outcomes, there is a growing need for secure and transparent mechanisms [1]. This paper presents ElectraGuard, a blockchain-based online polling platform designed to deliver trustworthy, tamper-resistant, and user-friendly opinion polling. Built on the Ethereum blockchain using smart contracts, ElectraGuard ensures decentralised execution, voter anonymity, and one-response-per-participant integrity [2]. The system leverages cryptographic hashing and distributed ledger technology to record each submission immutably, preventing result falsification or post-hoc modification. Supporting multiple categories of institutional and organizational polls, the platform features a web-based interface that enables secure participation and real-time result visualization. Testing shows that ElectraGuard greatly improves the security, ability to check results, and trustworthiness of online polling, providing a strong base for clear and checkable digital surveys.

title: Simulation of GPS Spoofing-Based Man-in-the-Middle Attack on a Remote-Controlled Ambulance Car
authors: Kiran P, Latha P, Venumadhava H, Aiyappa T B, Utkarsh Kher
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent and Innovative Technologies in Computing, Electrical and Electronics (IITCEE)
abstract: This project presents a simulation of a GPS spoofing-based Man-in-the-Middle (MITM) attack on a remotecontrolled vehicle, modeled as an ambulance. The objective is to demonstrate how false GPS signals can mislead navigationdependent systems. Using an ESP32 for Wi-Fi and GPS manipulation, and an Arduino Uno for motor control, the system injects spoofed coordinates to divert the vehicle from its intended path. This highlights vulnerabilities in GPS-based systems, particularly in emergency response, and emphasizes the need for secure navigation mechanisms.

title: Personalized Seizure Onset Prediction in Epilepsy Using Decision Tree Classifiers
authors: P. Perumal, M. Muthulekshmi, M. P. Revathi, Sindhuri Suseela Mantena, Shaini S, Vaijayanthimala M
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Artificial Intelligence for Innovations in Healthcare Industries (ICAIIHI)
abstract: The prediction of seizure onset in epilepsy is crucial for enhancing patient safety and facilitating proactive management. This research presents a personalized seizure prediction model employing Decision Tree classifiers, based on 7,000 samples from the Kaggle Epilepsy Onset Prediction dataset. The objective is to differentiate between seizure and non-seizure states using supervised learning in a binary classification framework. The collection comprises labelled samples that represent various brain activity conditions. Data preparation and balancing procedures were implemented for optimal training. Customized models were developed for people utilizing Decision Tree classifiers, selected for their simplicity, interpretability, and appropriateness for real-time, resource-constrained applications. The experimental assessment utilizing 10-fold cross-validation produced favorable outcomes, with the model attaining an accuracy of 99.92%, sensitivity of 99.96%, and specificity of 99.73%. This data highlights the model's capacity to accurately identify seizure start while sustaining a minimal false alarm rate. The research illustrates that Decision Tree classifiers may function as efficient and comprehensible instruments for personalized seizure prediction, even when utilized with moderately big datasets. This method demonstrates significant promise for incorporation into wearable health monitoring devices for real-time seizure control.

title: Edge-AI Framework for Air Quality Index (AQI) Forecasting with Quantized Deep Learning Models
authors: Anandi Dutta, Kazi Sifatul Islam, Damian Valles
publish: 2026 IEEE 16th Annual Computing and Communication Workshop and Conference (CCWC)
abstract: This study investigates the complex relationship between weather conditions and air quality, focusing on forecasting the Air Quality Index (AQI). A five-year dataset was compiled from Weather Underground and the U.S. Environmental Protection Agency's (EPA) Air Quality Index Daily Values Report, integrating historical weather variables (temperature, humidity, wind speed, precipitation) with daily AQI values for key pollutants like PM2.5 and Ozone. Exploratory data analysis, including scatter plot analysis, K-Means clustering, and association rule mining, revealed significant insights. Scatter plots indicated a positive relationship between temperature/dew point and PM2.5/Ozone levels, while humidity showed a negative trend with Ozone. KMeans clustering identified distinct clusters corresponding to various temperature and humidity conditions for both PM2.5 and Ozone. Association rule mining uncovered non-obvious relationships, such as high pressure and lack of precipitation correlating with elevated PM2.5, and specific meteorological conditions influencing Ozone levels. For AQI prediction, a comprehensive methodology was developed, starting with traditional statistical models (ARIMA, SARIMA, Prophet) and progressing to machine learning (Random Forest, LightGBM) and deep learning models (LSTM, LSTM-GRU, Transformer). The LSTM-GRU model offers the best balance of accuracy for both trends and fluctuations. To optimize deployment on edge devices, the best-performing model was subjected to quantization, converting it from FP32 to int8 TensorFlow Lite format. This significantly improved inference time and reduced energy consumption on both Raspberry Pi 5 and Jetson AGX Orin. The study demonstrates the effectiveness of integrating diverse datasets and advanced modeling techniques for accurate air quality forecasting, highlighting the advantages of edge computing for real-time, low-latency applications in environmental monitoring.

title: AMF-TR: An Adaptive Multi-Modal Fusion Transformer with Redundancy for Robust Object Detection in ADAS
authors: Mridul Jain, Rohini V, Meenakshi Malviya Rajoriya
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent and Innovative Technologies in Computing, Electrical and Electronics (IITCEE)
