abstract: Reliable object detection is vital for Advanced Driver Assistance Systems (ADAS) and autonomous vehicles, yet existing approaches struggle in adverse weather, poor lighting, and with small or occluded objects. We propose AMF-TR: Adaptive Multi-modal Fusion Transformer with Redundancy, an end-to-end framework that integrates RGB cameras, LiDAR, radar, and optional thermal or polarization sensors through modality-specific encoders and a Transformer-based fusion core. An adaptive querying mechanism enhances small-object detection, while a safety-centric loss inspired by Responsibility-Sensitive Safety (RSS) ensures safer predictions. Eliminating hand-crafted components such as anchors and NMS, AMFTR introduces a redundancy module for robustness against sensor failures. Experimental results on the nuScenes dataset demonstrate state-of-the-art performance with <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{7 1. 2 \%} \mathbf{m A P}$</tex> and 73.8% NDS, highlighting improved accuracy and resilience for autonomous driving perception.

title: Linguistic Steganography in Text with Artificial Intelligence
authors: Bárbara Emma Sanchez Rinza, Mariano AldairAquino Regalado
publish: 2025 International Conference on Electrical and Computer Engineering Researches (ICECER)
abstract: In this work, by means of linguistic steganography (which focuses on hiding information within texts, taking advantage of the characteristics and rules of the language to hide the secret information), it is intended to hide a text, which will be embedded within another text, called readable text, which the program must generate, using natural language. The text to be hidden can be placed in every 3 words of the text generated by the program, but the program must generate a readable text so as not to generate suspicions that hidden information is being sent.This project also uses decision trees, a supervised learning algorithm that can be used for both classification and regression tasks.In summary, we propose to combine the technique of digital steganography in text in order to generate a coherent text that can hide private messages within others, classifying words and generating plain text and a decision tree to create a system that can hide them according to defined grammatical rules of each language.

title: The Impact of Artificial Intelligence in Identifying Stroke Risk Factors
authors: S.Thangamayan, Anurag Shrivastava
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Artificial Intelligence for Innovations in Healthcare Industries (ICAIIHI)
abstract: The application of artificial intelligence (AI) in the field of adolescent mental health care has demonstrated an encouraging prospect of early diagnosis, forecasting risks, and individualized therapy. According to recent research, machine learning, deep learning, and multimodal AI strategies have been used to predict psychiatric disorders, including depression, anxiety, and internalizing disorders. The AI-based solutions will depend on a wide range of data sources, such as electronic health records, wearable devices, social media activity, voice or facial recognition to determine mental health status objectively. These methods improve predictive validity, allow timely interventions and assist clinicians in decision making and overcome the constraints of conventional assessments. Nevertheless, there are still problems related to the small size or cultural bias of the dataset, privacy issues, and the lack of standard evaluations framework. Scoping reviews note the significance of the multimodal AI systems where behavioral, physiological, and digital biomarkers are merged to enhance robustness and generalizability. Moreover, AI applications are spread to workflow optimization of the healthcare sector, tracking treatment progress, and enhancing interaction with the patient. All in all, AI-inspired mental health technologies provide scalable, accurate, and proactive solutions to the well-being of adolescents, yet further studies are needed to make it ethical, equitable, and effective.

title: Timestamp-Based Sentiment Analysis of Public Perception toward Persatuan Sepakbola Seluruh Indonesia (PSSI) on Social Media X during 2023–2025 Using a Transformer Model
authors: Nadhif Faris Andrifa Spaer, Fitriyani Fitriyani
publish: 2025 2nd Beyond Technology Summit on Informatics International Conference (BTS-I2C)
abstract: The Football Association of Indonesia (PSSI) has become a significant topic of public discourse, particularly concerning major issues between 2023 and 2025. However, public opinion towards PSSI on the social media platform X tends to be unstructured and challenging to analyze, requiring an approach capable of understanding the context of informal text. To overcome this, this study implements a timestamp-based sentiment analysis framework using the IndoBERT model to understand not only the sentiment of opinions but also how they change over time. The analysis classifies public opinion into positive, negative, and neutral categories. The model reached an accuracy of 81.38%, showing it can reliably identify public sentiment. Through the timestamp-based approach, this research uncovers how people’s opinions about PSSI shift in line with important moments such as match results, policy changes, and organizational issues. This method offers a clearer view of how public perception develops over time and highlights the strength of the IndoBERT model in capturing sentiment patterns within Indonesia’s football community.

title: 5G Network Planning for Sustainable Smart City Development: A Case Study of Ibu Kota Nusantara
authors: Alifka Zaky Maulana, M. Muhsin, Arrizky Ayu Faradila Purnama, Mirza Nuria Husna, Nur Aufa, Muhammad Najwa Hani Abdillah
publish: 2025 2nd Beyond Technology Summit on Informatics International Conference (BTS-I2C)
abstract: The relocation of Indonesia government center to Ibu Kota Nusantara (IKN) aims to accelerate Indonesia’s digital transformation through the establishment of advanced communication infrastructure. This study focuses on planning coverage design of a 5G New Radio network within the 3.5 GHz (n78) band to evaluate network performance based on key performance indicators (KPIs), including SS-RSRP, SS-SINR, and throughput. The network planning utilizes a gNodeB transmit power of 49 dBm, an antenna height of 29 m, and the 3GPP Urban Macro (UMa) propagation model, resulting in an inter-site distance of approximately 568 m and total of 113 gNodeB’s deployed for full area coverage. The network simulation was performed using Atoll software to analyze coverage, signal quality, and data capacity in the IKN area. The results show that 89.4% of the coverage area achieved SS-RSRP values above −90 dBm, 78.2% achieved SS-SINR above 10 dB with a peak of 22 dB, and 84.7% achieved downlink throughput above 100 Mbps, with an average Throughput was approximately at around 265 Mbps and a maximum value close to 500 Mbps.

title: A Systematic Review of Methodological Approaches in Researching Extended Realities in Education for Individuals with Visual Impairments
authors: Mahmoud Hamash, Hanan Ghreir, Peter Tiernan
publish: 2025 IEEE 14th International Conference on Engineering Education (ICEED)
abstract: Education for individuals with visual impairments traditionally relies on specialised materials and teaching methods. However, the emergence of Extended Reality (XR) offers a transformative opportunity to enhance learning through immersive and interactive experiences. This systematic review examines the methodological approaches employed in research on the use of XR in education for individuals with visual impairments, identifying limitations and gaps in methodological practices. Following the Preferred Reporting Items for Systematic Reviews (PRISMA) guidelines, data were collected from studies published between 2013 and 2023 across reputable databases such as ScienceDirect, ERIC, JSTOR, Taylor & Francis Online, and Scopus. A total of seventy-one research papers, with a predominant focus on higher education and Virtual Reality (VR), were analysed. The review critically evaluates the research designs, data collection methods, and analytical frameworks used in these studies, highlighting trends, challenges, and areas for improvement. By offering insights into methodological strengths and weaknesses, this review provides recommendations for future research, supporting scholars in advancing the field with more rigorous and comprehensive methodologies.

title: What Religion is ChatGPT? Analyzing Inter-faith Bias In The World’s Most Popular AI
authors: James Mountstephens
publish: 2025 International Conference on Advances in Machine Intelligence, and Cybersecurity Technologies (AMICT)
abstract: It is challenging to develop an AI tool that will satisfy all users when personal, political, and religious preferences vary widely. As more people use AI to find knowledge, ethical issues, which include bias in responses, arise. By their nature, LLMs depend on training data selection and a post-training alignment process which both have the potential to introduce bias. ChatGPT is still the most popular AI used worldwide and OpenAI founder Sam Altman has publicly expressed the desire for neutrality in ChatGPT responses, but is this possible in both principle and practice? Bias is a complex issue and can be both explicit and implicit. Recent research has uncovered explicit religious bias in earlier versions of ChatGPT in response to neutral, closed questions. The novel contribution of the research presented here is to update and extend this recent work with i) the addition of open questions, both positive and negative, ii) the identification and analysis of the criteria used by ChatGPT to evaluate religions, and iii) testing of newer GPT models for the existence of explicit religious bias. Three experiments were conducted. The first experiment required ChatGPT to explicitly grade six major religions and bias was inferred from consistently higher grades for any one religion. The second two experiments required ChatGPT to name the single religion it would either chose or reject, and were evaluated according to frequency of positive and negative mentions for religions in open responses. The findings confirm that the latest versions of ChatGPT still have a clear religious bias in favor of Eastern religions and Buddhism in particular. The significance of this bias will depend on one’s own belief system.

title: Machine Learning-Based Stroke Prediction Using Clinical Feature Engineering and Cost-Sensitive Learning
authors: Vaibhav Chandrakar, Yogesh Kumar Soni, T. Sahil Rao, Divya Soni
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Artificial Intelligence for Innovations in Healthcare Industries (ICAIIHI)
abstract: Stroke prediction using machine learning faces severe class imbalance (19.52:1) and asymmetric misclassification costs in highly imbalance datasets. We developed eight WHO/CDC/ADA-validated features and systematically evaluated 18 model configurations comprising 12 baseline models (3 algorithms: Logistic Regression, Random Forest, XGBoost × 4 sampling methods: Original, SMOTE, ADASYN, SMOTEENN) and 6 cost-sensitive variants with explicit 100:1 false negative penalties. The optimal configuration (Logistic Regression + SMOTEENN + Cost-Sensitive) achieves 90% recall, detecting 45 of 50 strokes with only 5 false negatives and $1,043 total cost—representing 76.6% cost reduction versus baseline ($4,900). Average improvements across all configurations demonstrate +34.3 percentage points recall gain and $1,559 cost savings (38.9% reduction). SHAP explainability analysis reveals three engineered features rank within top 10 predictors (age_group #2, bmi_category #5, smoker_flag #10), validating clinical relevance of domain-guided feature construction. This interpretable, cost-effective framework demonstrates practical viability for healthcare deployment

title: Advanced Data Processing Framework for Consumer Wearable Heart Rate Monitoring and Clinical Risk Assessment
authors: Jaynil D. Panjwani
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Internet of Things: Smart Innovation and Usages (IoT-SIU)
abstract: In todays wearable devices creates tons of cardio data, they give a lot of insightful analysis if properly managed. However after having this much data we still don’t have clinical interpretation of this data. In this paper I have created new algorithm and statistical validation method from Samsung health data export feature in your Samsung wearable. This framework have intelligent pdf based quality check and have preprocessed 24,873 heart rate from a huge data after removing unwanted ones. this data are from Samsung Fit 3 watch and system achieves 97.2% data completeness after running my framework.Advanced circadian modeling reveals robust daily patterns (R<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup> = 0.847, p < 0.001) with nocturnal minimum (68.2 ± 8.4 bpm at 04: 00) and afternoon peak (107.8 ± 14.2 bpm at 16: 00). There was inc. in tachycardia of 31.8% by clinical risk stratification stating specific lifestyle of user. We also worked on framework scalability by making it opensource and improving efficiency. overall this IoT wearable device can provide long term health monitoring and our framework can give a deep insides on health data in this framework.

title: An IoT-Enabled E-Guide System for Integrated Healthcare Center Navigation and Service Optimization in Oman
authors: Yasir Abdelgadir Mohamed, Akbar Khanan, Mohamed Bashir
publish: 2025 International Conference on Electrical and Computer Engineering Researches (ICECER)
abstract: In the era of smart healthcare transformation, the need for accessible, intelligent, and patient-centered service delivery has become increasingly an essential requirement. This paper proposed a design and implementation of an IoT-enabled E-Guide system that can facilitate real-time navigation and service optimization across healthcare centers in the Sultanate of Oman where the research has been conducted. The proposed system intends to integrate Internet of Things (IoT) devices, location-based services (LBS), and cloud-based communication technologies so as to assist patients and visitors in locating nearby healthcare facilities, accessing service availability, and receiving optimized navigation guidance within medical complexes according to their needs. The system architecture employs sensor-based data collection, GPS-enabled routing, and a web-based interface to deliver recommendations and live updates in the context. Key modules include user authentication, healthcare facility mapping, queue monitoring, emergency access routing, and bilingual (Arabic–English) support to enhance inclusivity. The backend infrastructure utilizes MQTT and RESTful APIs to ensure an efficient, scalable, and secure communication between mobile clients and cloud services. In a 6-week pilot (n=38), user surveys showed a 23% increase in satisfaction and a 31% improvement in service accessibility versus baseline. Mean update response time dropped from 2.4 s to 1.1 s under operational load, and task completion reached 94% (SUS 87.6). Future work aims to incorporate AI-driven personalization and predictive analytics for further enhancement.

title: Mitigating the Information Cocoon Effect in Cognitively Aligned Recommendations: A Human-Centered Approach
authors: Mary Nusrat, Hasibur Rashid Mahi, Sarfuddin Bhuiyan, Gahangir Hossain
publish: 2026 IEEE 16th Annual Computing and Communication Workshop and Conference (CCWC)
abstract: This paper introduces a Gray-Box Recommender System (RS) framework that integrates Large Language Models (LLMs) with Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) to deliver transparent, context-aware, and cognitively aligned recommendations. While deep learning-based RSs achieve high accuracy, their opaque “black-box” nature undermines user trust and system interpretability. Conversely, transparent “white-box” models often suffer from lower predictive performance. The proposed framework navigates the trade-off between performance and explainability by architecting a partially transparent model that exposes reasoned decision-making processes without compromising algorithm security or user privacy. By augmenting a hybrid collaborative-content-based filtering with an RAG-enhanced LLM explanation module, the framework provides contextual, cognitively-aligned recommendations that mitigate critical challenges including the information cocoon effect, coldstart problems, and recommendation hallucination. We present the formal architecture, a detailed algorithm pipeline, and evaluation methodologies. Theoretical analysis and a proposed validation plan suggest this human-centered, gray-box approach significantly advances personalization, interpretability, and trust in next-generation explainable RS (XRS).

title: Heatwave Susceptibility Analysis Using Autoencoder-Based Feature Extraction, Hierarchical Clustering, and SHAP Interpretability
authors: Skanda P R, Suhas Raj H R, Shridhar Bhat, Vipul S, Jyothi Shetty, Vinod A R
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent and Innovative Technologies in Computing, Electrical and Electronics (IITCEE)
abstract: Heatwaves resulting from global temperature rise create serious threats to human wellness, particularly in areas without sufficient heatwave adaptation strategy implementation. This research introduces heatwave evaluation methods which combine environmental, socio-economic and health system data. Heatwave Susceptibility Score (HSS) is developed, which combines parameters like temperature, air quality, population density, and health infrastructure. The method utilizes Autoencoder-based feature extraction to reduce data dimensionality, followed by Hierarchical Clustering for regional categorization, and SHAP (SHapley Additive exPlanations) analysis for model interpretability. The results divided the States of India into four heatwave susceptibility categories: Very High, High, Moderate, and Low. SHAP analysis revealed that temperature variation, air pollution, and population density are the main factors that determine heatwave risk. This framework functions as a tool for policymakers to locate heatwave-prone regions in order to set priority areas for intervention strategies against heatwaves.

title: Sentiment Insights and Usability Evaluation for KADUMALIN Translation System
authors: Suraya Alias, Mohd Shamrie Sainin, Retno Kusumaningrum
publish: 2025 International Conference on Advances in Machine Intelligence, and Cybersecurity Technologies (AMICT)
abstract: Machine Translation (MT) has evolved and become necessary with the rise of generative AI. However, the effort to preserve a low-resource language such as Kadazandusun in Sabah becomes a challenge due to the informal Malay Sabahan slang, improper spellings, and sentiment used by younger generations, hence affecting the translation’s accuracy. This paper presents a user-centered evaluation of Kadumalin, an NLP-based machine translation system with spell checker features. We conducted a post-use survey analysis using the System Usability Scale (SUS) with 250 participants where Kadumalin achieved an average SUS score of 66.2, which indicates towards acceptable benchmark level of usability standard. Next, user feedback responses were analyzed to understand sentiment insights by comparing the lexical, machine learning, and hybrid deep learning sentiment analysis models. Our proposed hybrid BERT+XGBoost model with improved weight optimization achieved an FI (macro) score of 0.941608, encouraging results even on a small, imbalanced dataset. Positive feedback supported our effort to preserve the Kadazandusun language and its easy access. Negative feedback pointed out occasional mistranslations caused by limited corpus data, while neutral comments suggested improvements to the user interface design. These findings provide actionable insights for improving MT usability by combining SUS with sentiment analysis task in evaluating NLP-based solutions.

title: Hybrid Machine Learning Algorithms-Based IoT Architecture for Real-Time Smart Home: Activities Classification and Anomaly Detection
authors: Alemayehu Belay Kassa, Hailu Belay, Kevin Kornegay
publish: 2026 IEEE 16th Annual Computing and Communication Workshop and Conference (CCWC)
abstract: Integrating IoT devices into smart homes opens an opportunity for automation, while privacy and security aspects are still a source of concern. We introduced a hybrid machine learning approach for anomaly detection and activity classification in smart homes. The framework shows good performance over a dataset of 1,048,575 data points with 31 device attributes for real-time application. We experimented using seven machine learning algorithms such as Logistic Regression, SVM, Gradient Boosting, Random Forest, Naïve Bayes, KNN and XGBoost on their accuracy and efficiency. Although SVM, KNN and Gradient Boosting reached an accuracy greater than 99%, their long execution time and the need for real-time evaluation were challenges. Naïve Bayes achieved low accuracy (57%) and Logistic Regression 96%. Finally, Random Forest (99.98%) and XGBoost (99.90%) were selected for addition since both are very good in practice. The architecture of the system is based on a realtime simulation engine, semantic logging, rule-based decisionmaking and a Streamlit dashboard hosted on SQL Server. It has seven activity types: anomaly, eat, leisure, other, personal, sleep, and work with remarkable precision and high accuracy. It also supports adaptive monitoring and alerts to gain users trust and system explainability. This framework becomes a scalable and practical solution for smart home management.

title: An Enhancement Technique for Fundus Images to Improve Accuracy in Computer Vision for Early Glaucoma Detection
authors: Agieza Muhammad FurqoistMadjid, Zilvanhisna Emka Fitri, Ahmad Nasikun, Hanung Adi Nugroho
publish: 2025 2nd Beyond Technology Summit on Informatics International Conference (BTS-I2C)
abstract: Glaucoma is a progressive degenerative disease of the optic nerve caused by blockage in the eye's fluid drainage system (aqueous humor), which can lead to permanent blindness. Patients with glaucoma will encounter challenges in executing daily activities independently, endure social isolation, and face emotional difficulties. Early detection of glaucoma symptoms is very helpful for medical professionals in determining the appropriate time for vision rehabilitation and psychological support, thus reducing the negative impact of glaucoma on their daily lives. One of the non-invasive early detection techniques is fundus imaging that enables the recognition of the optic disc (OD) and optic disc cup (OD-cup) regions. This study compares the accuracy of MobileNetV2 in optimizing the classification process between the PAPILA and ACRIMA datasets using image quality improvement techniques. The results show that the RGB inter-channel addition technique is highly optimal for the PAPILA dataset, with an accuracy of 77.78%, and the conversion to grayscale color space technique can improve the system's accuracy when classifying the ACRIMA dataset to 95.74%.

title: Trustworthiness in Image Pre-Processing Techniques for Stroke Classification Using DenseNet and LIME
authors: Christopher Ijok, Daphne Teck Ching Lai, Rosyzie Anna Binti Hj Mohd Apong
publish: 2025 International Conference on Advances in Machine Intelligence, and Cybersecurity Technologies (AMICT)
abstract: Image classification has seen significant advances in accuracy enabling its application across diverse fields. This study focuses on the healthcare industry. It evaluates the impact of various preprocessing technique combinations on the classification of Acute Ischemic Stroke (AIS) from CT scans using convolutional neural networks (CNNs). CNN’s performance is assessed using metrics such as the F1 score. Additionally model interpretability is addressed through Local Interpretable Model-Agnostic Explanations (LIME) which provides insight into the reliability of classification outcomes. The findings highlight that the most effective preprocessing combinations were: (1) erosion without Gaussian blur (2) dilation with Gaussian blur and (3) erosion with Gaussian blur. However LIME analysis reveals that models may rely on non-brain regions for decision-making indicating that accuracy metrics alone may not reflect true model validity. These results emphasize the importance of interpretability in medical imaging models. Future work will involve expanding the dataset testing alternative CNN architectures and exploring more advanced preprocessing pipelines.

title: CAST-GNN: Continual Adaptive Learning for Custom Spatio-Temporal Knowledge Graphs via Graph Neural Networks
authors: Gözde Ayşe Tataroğlu Özbulak, Yash Raj Shrestha, Jean-Paul Calbimonte
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Data Mining (ICDM)
abstract: Real-time video streams present unique challenges for continual learning systems, demanding models that can incrementally update representations, preserve past knowledge, and reason over complex semantic relationships without sacrificing efficiency. In this paper, we introduce CAST-GNN, the first unified Graph Neural Network architecture expressly designed for continual adaptation on streaming Spatio-Temporal Knowledge Graphs (STKGs) derived from open-source video benchmarks. CAST-GNN integrates dynamic temporal embedding layers, adaptive self-attention, episodic graph pattern memory, and a novel hybrid selective replay buffer with Fisher-based regularization and knowledge distillation to mitigate catastrophic forgetting. Through comprehensive experiments on four diverse STKG benchmarks (UCF-101, HMDB-51, Kinetics-400, and SomethingSomething), our model achieves 96-97% accuracy, between 0.13-0.31 % forgetting, by consistently outperforming re-implemented continual-learning baselines under identical conditions. Ablation studies confirm the critical synergy between temporal embeddings and adaptive attention. We further demonstrate XAI-driven interpretability by aligning global distributional shifts with local node-level attributions. CAST-GNN not only advances robust semantic reasoning and knowledge retention but also provides a scalable, explainable framework applicable to a wide array of real-world streaming scenarios.

title: Network Analysis of Social Media Networks for Influencer Identification
authors: Kumar P, D. Praveena, S Karkuzhal, Senthil Pandi S
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent and Innovative Technologies in Computing, Electrical and Electronics (IITCEE)
abstract: The explosive growth of social media platforms has transformed the way individuals, organizations, and communities communicate, collaborate, and influence one another. Within this digital ecosystem, influencers users with disproportionate reach, credibility, and impact play a critical role in shaping opinions, disseminating information, and driving online and offline behaviors. Identifying such influencers accurately is vital for applications in digital marketing, public health awareness, political campaigning, disaster communication, and misinformation control. This paper explores the use of network analysis methods for influencer identification in social media networks by examining structural, relational, and temporal properties of user interactions. Unlike conventional popularity metrics such as follower counts or likes, network analysis techniques leverage graph-theoretic measures, community detection, and centrality indices to uncover nodes that hold true strategic importance within social structures. The novelty of this paper lies in combining structural graph analysis with machine learning-based activity patterns to create an end-to-end influencer identification framework. A modular system is proposed, encompassing network construction, feature extraction, hybrid influence scoring, supervised model training, and visualization dashboards. Experimental validation is performed using benchmark datasets from Twitter, Reddit, and Instagram, focusing on domains such as political discourse, product promotions, and health campaigns. Results are presented through comparative evaluations against baseline methods, highlighting improvements in accuracy, interpretability, and computational efficiency. The paper underscores the importance of combining network topology, temporal dynamics, and semantic analysis to achieve a holistic view of influence. Furthermore, the paper addresses ethical considerations related to privacy, bias, and responsible usage of influencer identification systems. By integrating multidisciplinary perspectives from computer science, data science, sociology, and marketing, this paper demonstrates how advanced network analysis techniques can redefine influencer detection strategies in the digital era.

title: VOX: real-time AI Translator
authors: Adrija R Pramod, Al Amthan, M Liya, K Shreya John Bosco, A Jayakrishnan
publish: 2025 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence for Computing, Astronomy and Renewable Energy (AICARE)
abstract: This paper focuses on developing a real-time AI translator that enables seamless multilingual communication. The system accomplishes this objective by successfully integrating a series of advanced technologies such as Automatic Speech Recognition (ASR), Neural Machine Translation (NMT), Machine Translation (MT), and Text-to-Speech (TTS). The process begins with the system initially processing spoken words as written text through ASR technology. Subsequently, the translation is conducted through both the NMT and MT processes, which complement each other to ensure the accuracy of the translation while ensuring the contextual suitability of the translation throughout the process. Through the final stage of the process, TTS technology is applied to generate naturally flowing speech within the target language, allowing users to communicate easily and smoothly with each other despite using different languages. The translator has been intentionally designed with the real-world user in mind. It is appropriate for application in various contexts such as travel, business activities, study, and intercultural relationships. Through these advanced AI methods, the system aims to convincingly bridge communication gaps that currently exist and easily facilitate communication at the global level.

title: Random Forest Regression Approach for Balancing Vegetable Production and Price Forecasting in Sri Lanka
authors: A.M.D.D. Divithotawela, Pathum Udukumburage
publish: 2025 International Conference on Advances in Technology and Computing (ICATC)
abstract: Sri Lanka’s agriculture sector, a pillar of the national economy, is extremely vulnerable to price volatility in essential crops such as potatoes, particularly in major production and trading hubs like Welimada, Bandarawela, and Nuwara Eliya. This research project addresses this critical issue by developing a supervised machine learning framework that leverages Random Forest regression to forecast wholesale potato prices, integrating market-specific prices, climatic indicators, rainfall, and inflation to model complex non-linear interactions. The methodology involved comprehensive data preprocessing, meticulous hyperparameter tuning, and rigorous five-fold cross-validation, ensuring resilient generalization despite a limited historical data window. Model evaluation yielded strong results: R<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup> values were 0.470 for Welimada, 0.701 for Bandarawela, and 0.807 for Nuwara Eliya, with corresponding RMSE values of 35.87, 25.24, and 21.30, respectively. The entire workflow, from data ingestion, model training, and result analysis to application deployment, was implemented using Python’s data science stack and presented via a Streamlit web application for real-time access. Unique to this project, the application not only forecasts prices but also provides market analytics tailored to stakeholders, including revenue estimation and oversupply risk detection per region, empowering informed decisions for farmers, traders, and policymakers. By offering transparent, actionable, and context-aware price insights, the system alleviates uncertainty, enhances market participation, and ultimately supports greater stability and sustainability in Sri Lankan agriculture. This solution, designed to be extensible, paves the way for broader crop integration, real-time analytics, and further reinforcement of food system resilience in the future.

title: Real-Time Anomaly Detection Across Million-Vehicle Fleets Using Quantum-Inspired Classical Algorithms*
authors: Dinesh Kumar Elumalai, Damodhara Reddy Palavali
publish: 2026 IEEE 16th Annual Computing and Communication Workshop and Conference (CCWC)
abstract: The connected vehicles create massive telematics data streams for predictive maintenance and safety monitoring. The difficulty of finding rare failure patterns in millions of vehicles generating petabytes of data per day complicates the task of conventional machine learning. This work provides a quantum-inspired classical algorithm that utilizes the principles of quantum computing-superposition, entanglement, and amplitude amplification minimize the computational cost without implementing the quantum hardware. We use tensor networks for high-dimensional sensor data compression, Grover-inspired hierarchical search reducing complexity from <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathrm{O}(N)$</tex> to <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathrm{O}(\sqrt{N})$</tex>, and quantum amplitude amplification for weak signal detection. On synthetic automotive telematics datasets (100K-5M vehicles, <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$200+$</tex> sensors each), our speedup can work on classical baselines and is 15-85 times larger, with 96.3% detection accuracy with a <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\sim 0.42 \%$</tex> false-positive rate. Early warnings are offered by this system, which was developed 30 to 60 days before thresholdbased approaches, so that 82% of failures are detected with the 30+ day lead time as compared to <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$43 \%$</tex> for LSTM networks. This work introduces real applications of quantum computing in automotive fleet management, and it also touches on large IoT pattern recognition challenges.

title: Gambit: A Neurological and Neurodevelopmental Disorder Assistant Mobile Application with Symptom Detect and Neurologist Search Features
authors: Mahfuzulhoq Chowdhury, Sumaiya Zaman, Jihadul Islam, Taha Ibne Abdullah, Junain Uddin, Md. Ashraful Islam Semon
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent and Innovative Technologies in Computing, Electrical and Electronics (IITCEE)
abstract: For both children and adults, neurological and neurodevelopment abnormalities can result in a number of health risks, such as behavioral issues, physical, emotional, and cognitive impairments. No automated ICT-based system with features for ADHD risk, autism, Alzheimer's, epilepsy, Parkinson's, motor disorder, dyslexia, dyspraxia, dyscalculia, brain tumor disorder detection, symptom checking, treatment center search, neurologist search, medical test time booking, and query assistance was offered by the existing works on neurological and neurodevelopment disorders. In order to surmount the existing limitations, this paper presents a mobile application for neurological and neurodevelopment disorders assistant that includes features for detecting a variety of disorders, including ADHD, autism, dyslexia disorders, brain tumors, Alzheimer's, epilepsy, Parkinson's, motor disorders, and medical test time booking, treatment center search, neurologist search, and query assistance. According to the findings of the app's beneficialness and diligence evaluation, over 60 % of users submitted superb comments for the proposed mobile application.

title: Message from the Program Co-Chairs: ICDM 2025
authors: N/A
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Data Mining (ICDM)
abstract: Welcome to the 25th IEEE International Conference on Data Mining! Since its first edition in 2001, the ICDM conference series has become a premier forum for data mining researchers, users, practitioners, and developers to discuss and exchange original research and directions. True to its name, with submissions from 50 different countries, ICDM is a highly international forum. The 25 editions of the conference have taken it to twelve countries across the world. This year, the 25th edition, takes ICDM to Washington, DC, the capital of the United States of America.

title: Demand for Long-Term-Oriented Human Capital as a Strategic Response to Innovation Aspiration Gaps: An Analysis of Online Job Postings by Firms
authors: Shijun Huang, Jieyu Hu
abstract: Against the backdrop of the innovation aspiration gap (IAG), how firms strategically adjust their human capital structure to address challenges remains a critical issue that has not been fully explored in academia. Integrating behavioral theory and human capital theory, this study explores how firms respond to the innovation aspiration gap by recruiting long-term oriented (LTO) talent. Utilizing web crawling technology, this study collected job posting data from recruitment platforms for listed companies in China's high-tech industry from 2014 to 2024, and employed the Qwen-2.5 large language model to perform semantic analysis on job descriptions to identify positions requiring LTO talent. The findings reveal that IAG positively influences corporate demand for LTO talent. Drawing on the process perspective of performance feedback, this study examines the contextual factors influencing this relationship. In the performance evaluation stage, IAG persistence negatively moderates this positive relationship. In the attribution stage, AI technology adoption exerts a positive moderating effect. In the search and change stage, executive risk preference similarly exerts a positive moderating effect. These results were validated through a series of endogeneity and robustness checks. Further analysis indicates that recruiting LTO talent effectively narrows the firm's innovation aspiration gap. Methodologically, this study extends the measurement paradigm for complex managerial traits by utilizing large language models. Theoretically, it bridges the logical disconnect between performance feedback and strategic execution from a human capital perspective, and deepens research on the boundary conditions of the innovation aspiration gap based on a process perspective.

title: Towards Learning Shift-Invariant Representations for Healthcare Series Classification
authors: Yuanbo Liu, Xiucheng Li, Xinyang Chen, Hongwei Liu, Zhijun Li
publish: IEEE Transactions on Knowledge and Data Engineering
abstract: Accurate classification of healthcare time series is critical for clinical decision-making. However, existing models often struggle under real-world data shifts and lack interpretability- two key requirements for reliable medical deployment. To address these challenges, we propose SHINE, a novel endto-end framework that learns disentangled and shift-invariant representations by modeling the generative process of multivariate healthcare signals. Specifically, SHINE first introduces a genuine data representation learning that disentangles healthcare signals into trend, seasonality, and noise components, reflecting distinct temporal dynamics of healthcare series. Then, we inject several inductive biases into each component to encourage latent representations to be invariant to data shifts and aligned with their corresponding semantic units. Extensive experiments on six healthcare benchmarks spanning ECG, EEG, and continuous glucose monitoring (CGM) domains-under a variety of simulated real-world shift scenarios-demonstrate that SHINE consistently outperforms state-of-the-art baselines, providing robust performance and clinically meaningful interpretations grounded in the estimated components.

title: Circular Patch Antenna Array with Independently Controllable Reconfiguration of Polarization, Frequency and Radiation Pattern
authors: Chang Jiang You, Qin Yu Li, Jia Jun Sheng, Chao Qiang Xia, Xue Qing Qi, Yi Long Liu, Ying Fan Shi
publish: IEEE Transactions on Antennas and Propagation
abstract: A 1×2 antenna array with three-parameter independently controllable reconfiguration of polarization, frequency and radiation pattern is proposed. One C-shaped slot and two PIN diodes are integrated into the circular patch to achieve polarization reconfiguration. Four sector-shaped parasitic patches and four varactor diodes are employed to obtain frequency reconfiguration. To achieve the reconfiguration of radiation pattern, the phase of each unit in 1×2 array is configured through the feeding network to switch between 0° and 180°. The polarization of proposed antenna array can be toggled between linear polarization, left-hand circular polarization, and right-hand circular polarization. The radiation pattern can be switched between 0° single-beam and ±35° dual-beam within a frequency tuning range of 24.1%. The proposed antenna array features independently controllable reconfiguration across three key parameters, supports comprehensive polarization states, and requires fewer switches. It is well-suited for wireless communication systems operating in complex and dynamic environments.

title: Deep Learning and Fresnel Zone Theory for Enhanced Channel Prediction Using Adaptive Image Features
authors: Jiaxuan Li, Xiaoying Zhang, Wei Li, Xiaoran Liu, Jibo Wei, Haitao Zhao
publish: IEEE Antennas and Wireless Propagation Letters
abstract: The advancement of communication technologies has imposed increasingly stringent requirements on the precision and generalization capabilities of channel models. Traditional statistical and deterministic models frequently encounter challenges in achieving an optimal balance between these two aspects. To address this challenge, this letter introduces a channel prediction model based on deep learning and Fresnel zone propagation theory to achieve accurate prediction of path loss (PL) and delay spread (DS). To improve the extraction of environmental information from environmental images for channel prediction, an enhanced image environmental feature representation method is proposed. This method adaptively adjusts the dimensions of the input image based on the distance between the transmitter (Tx) and receiver (Rx). Furthermore, an image importance distribution map (IIDM) is defined to delineate the variations in significance across the different regions of the environmental image. Subsequently, a model architecture capable of processing images of variable sizes is developed utilizing spatial pyramid pooling (SPP) techniques. Finally, using channel measurement data obtained at 5.9 GHz in Changsha, the performance of the proposed model is validated employing a leave-one-out cross-validation method. Compared to existing models, the proposed prediction model achieves a reduction in the average root mean square error (RMSE) for PL and DS prediction by approximately 12.78% and 8.2%, respectively.

title: A Wideband Lowpass Frequency Selective Surface With Sharp Roll-Off and Extensive Deep Stopband
authors: Songsong Qian, Yilong Wang, Huangyan Li, Wen Wu
publish: IEEE Antennas and Wireless Propagation Letters
abstract: This paper presents a wideband lowpass frequency selective surface (FSS) with wide stopband of deep rejection and sharp roll-off for high selectivity. The proposed structure employs a triple-layer structural configuration separated by two supporting honeycomb layers, which facilitates the strategic distribution of three transmission poles and four transmission zeros, enabling high frequency selectivity and wide stopband performance. An equivalent circuit model is developed to elucidate the operational mechanism, showing excellent agreement with full-wave simulations. The FSS exhibits a low insertion loss of less than 1.67 dB within the lowpass band up to 12.26 GHz, while maintaining a transmission coefficient below -10 dB from 12.45 GHz to 21.11 GHz. A remarkably narrow transition band of only 160 MHz between -3 dB and -10 dB underscores its steep roll-off characteristic. A prototype is fabricated and measured, demonstrating good agreement with simulations and stable performance under both TE and TM polarizations for incident angles up to 45°.

title: Greedy Low-Rank Gradient Compression for Distributed Learning with Convergence Guarantees_supp1-3667669.pdf
authors: Kun Yuan
abstract: Distributed optimization is pivotal for large-scale signal processing and machine learning, yet communication overhead remains a major bottleneck. Low-rank gradient compression, in which the transmitted gradients are approximated by low-rank matrices to reduce communication, offers a promising remedy. Existing methods typically adopt either randomized or greedy compression strategies: randomized approaches project gradients onto randomly chosen subspaces, introducing high variance and degrading empirical performance; greedy methods select the most informative subspaces, achieving strong empirical results but lacking convergence guarantees. To address this gap, we propose GreedyLore—the first <underline xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">Greedy</u> <underline xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">L</u>ow-<underline xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">R</u>ank gradient compression algorithm for distributed learning with rigorous convergence guarantees. GreedyLore incorporates error feedback to correct the bias introduced by greedy compression and introduces a semi-lazy subspace update that ensures the compression operator remains contractive throughout all iterations. With these techniques, we prove that GreedyLore achieves a convergence rate of <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\mathcal{O} (\sigma / \sqrt{NT }+ 1 / T ) $</tex-math></inline-formula> under standard optimizers such as MSGD and Adam—marking the first linear speedup convergence rate for low-rank gradient compression. Extensive experiments are conducted to validate our theoretical findings.

title: Ethical and Privacy Issues for AI in 6G Empowering Robotics
authors: Eirini Kanaki, Ali Hessami, Paulo Gonçalves, Periklis Chatzimisios
abstract: The convergence of Artificial Intelligence (AI), robotics, and 6G technologies is redefining autonomous systems across domains such as healthcare, manufacturing, and public safety. This paper explores the ethical and privacy challenges arising from this integration, particularly focusing on robotic systems that perform tasks using AI-based systems and communicate within environments enabled by 6G wireless technology, e.g., AI-driven robotic systems in 6G environments. We analyze key regulatory frameworks, including the EU AI Act, GDPR, and IEEE 7000 series, and present a structured ethical assessment of healthcare robotics. Emphasis is placed on safeguarding human rights, dignity, and autonomy, while addressing risks related to data privacy, bias, accountability, and transparency. The paper advocates for responsible innovation, proposing comprehensive frameworks that ensure trustworthy and ethically aligned robotic systems in future 6G-connected ecosystems.

title: A Reconfigurable Time-Domain In-Memory Computing Macro Using FeFET-Based CAM With Multilevel Delay Calibration in 28-nm CMOS
authors: Jeries Mattar, Mor M. Dahan, Stefan Dünkel, Halid Mulaosmanovic, Gunda Beernink, Sven Beyer, Eilam Yalon, Nicolás Wainstein
publish: IEEE Transactions on Circuits and Systems I: Regular Papers
abstract: Time-domain nonvolatile in-memory computing (TD-nvIMC) offers a promising pathway to reduce data movement and improve energy efficiency by encoding computation in delay rather than voltage or current. This work presents a fully integrated and reconfigurable TD-nvIMC macro, fabricated in 28 nm CMOS, that combines a ferroelectric FET (FeFET)-based content-addressable memory array, a cascaded delay element chain, and a time-to-digital converter. The architecture supports binary multiply-and-accumulate (MAC) operations using XOR- and AND-based matching, as well as in-memory Boolean logic and arithmetic functions. Sub-nanosecond MAC resolution is achieved through experimentally demonstrated 550 ps delay steps, representing a <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$2000\times $</tex-math> </inline-formula> improvement over prior FeFET TD-nvIMC work, enabled by multilevel-state calibration with <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\leq 100$</tex-math> </inline-formula> ps resolution. Write-disturb resilience is ensured via isolated triple-well bulks. The proposed macro achieves a measured throughput of 222.2 MOPS/cell and energy efficiency of 1887TOPS/W at 0.85 V, establishing a viable path toward scalable, energy-efficient TD-nvIMC accelerators.

title: Multiagent Adaptive Critic Control With Expert Knowledge for Wastewater Treatment Plants
authors: Ding Wang, Xin Li, Jin Ren, Junfei Qiao
abstract: In this article, a multiagent adaptive critic control algorithm is developed for the multivariable control problem of wastewater treatment plants. The wastewater treatment plant is regarded as a type of decentralized interconnected system in this algorithm. To reduce the design difficulty of control strategies, multiple agents are created, and each agent is only responsible for optimizing the control strategy of a single subsystem. In addition, based on a well-designed Q-function, the agent considers the impact on other subsystems while optimizing its own control strategy. Therefore, the control algorithm exhibits good performance in both the implementation difficulty and the control accuracy. To ensure the initial performance of the control algorithm, the prior control strategy with expert knowledge is integrated into the control algorithm. Considering the disturbance factors in wastewater treatment plants, the direct control strategy is modified into an incremental control strategy, which improves the anti-interference performance of the control algorithm. The stability of the proposed algorithm is proved by constructing Lyapunov functions. The superiority of the control algorithm is verified through quantitative comparison results with other control algorithms.

title: Achieving Secure and Efficient Clustering for Multiple Source Time Series Data in Industrial Internet of Things
authors: Xucheng Qiao, Songnian Zhang, Hui Zhu, Yandong Zheng, Fengwei Wang
abstract: In the advancing field of information technology, the Industrial Internet of Things (IIoT) plays a pivotal role across various domains. To fully leverage the massive data generated by IIoT, the Balanced Iterative Reducing and Clustering using Hierarchies (BIRCH) algorithm is well-suited for clustering large-scale time series data due to its incremental and order-sensitive design. Although aggregating multisource data on cloud servers becomes a practical and effective approach, it raises privacy concerns. Existing privacy-preserving BIRCH schemes designed to mitigate these concerns, however, rely on homomorphic encryption for atomic operations and reveal the insertion path of sample points, resulting in significant limitations in performance and security. To address these issues, we propose SBIRCH, a novel secure BIRCH scheme. Specifically, we first introduce oblivious hierarchical updates to preserve insertion paths. Then, we present a shuffling-based obfuscation technique to protect intermediate clustering results during node splitting. In addition, we optimize division operations on encrypted data, delivering high precision and significantly improving efficiency. Security analysis confirms that our scheme effectively protects multisource data, intermediate clustering results, and clustering relationships. While maintaining consistent security, experimental evaluations demonstrate that our scheme achieves substantial improvements in computational efficiency and markedly reduces communication overheads compared to existing schemes.

title: Continuous-Time Transformer Based Channel Prediction with Non-Uniform Pilot Pattern
authors: Yiliang Sang, Ke Ma, Lebin Yao, Pengyu Wang, Zhaocheng Wang, Zhu Han, Sheng Chen
abstract: Deep learning based channel prediction has garnered significant attention to mitigate channel aging in high-mobility multiple-input multiple-output (MIMO) systems. However, existing channel prediction methods extract the temporal correlations from the channel sequences estimated at uniform pilots, which require dense pilot configuration to mitigate Doppler aliasing in high-mobility scenarios and incur substantial estimation overhead. To tackle this problem, we propose a channel prediction method based on continuous-time transformer with the non-uniform pilot pattern, thereby enabling accurate prediction across arbitrary time scales with only a small number of pilots. Specifically, we first design the non-uniform pilot pattern based on Chebyshev polynomial roots and then prove its optimality under Doppler-dominated channel variations with relatively stable user velocity, wherein a subset of pilots are densely configured to provide a finer resolution of Doppler phase estimation. To adapt to the non-uniform pattern, a continuous-time transformer is further proposed, which integrates the superior feature extraction capability of transformer with the continuous-time modeling strength of neural ordinary differential equation (ODE) for flexibly processing the estimated channel sequences with non-uniform time scales. More concretely, the attention mechanism is extended to the continuous-time domain by incorporating neural ODE, while a high-frequency temporal encoding is designed to fit rapidly time-varying channels. Besides, an element-wise prediction mechanism is proposed to efficiently capture temporal correlations and prevent overfitting. Simulation results demonstrate that our proposed method can realize accurate continuous-time channel prediction in high-mobility scenarios, and significantly outperforms existing channel prediction methods.

title: Cooperative ISAC Systems with Extended Targets: Performance Analysis and Beamforming Design
authors: Yixiao Gu, Yifan Zhou, Han Hu, Jie Xu, Dan Zeng
abstract: This paper investigates a cooperative integrated sensing and communication (ISAC) system, where multiple base stations (BSs) employ coordinated transmit beamforming to communicate with their respective users and jointly sense a set of extended targets (ETs). Different from prior cooperative ISAC works considering point targets (PTs), the visible scatterers on the same extended target (ET) and the radar cross section (RCS) of the same scatterers observed by multiple BSs are considered to be different. Given the model, we first derive the Cramér-Rao bound (CRB) for the BSs to cooperatively estimate the ET’s parameters, thus quantifying the cooperative sensing gains. Next, based on the derived CRB, we formulate a joint node selection and coordinated transmit beamforming design problem with the object of minimizing the average trace of sensing CRB, while satisfying the minimum communication rate constraint, the maximum transmit power constraint, and the node selection constraints. To solve this non-convex optimization problem, we first utilize the block coordinate descent (BCD) method to decompose it into node selection sub-problem and beamforming design sub-problem. Next, the continuous relaxation and linear programming (LP) approach are employed to handle the node selection sub-problem, and a decentralized augmented Lagrangian manifold optimization algorithm is developed to solve the beamforming sub-problem with reduced computation complexity. Numerical simulations demonstrate that the proposed design outperforms benchmark designs with larger CRB-rate region. Moreover, our results show the impacts of the ET’s state and the number of network nodes on network performance to enable valuable ISAC beamforming design insights.

title: Sum Secrecy Rate Maximization in Movable Antenna-Assisted NOMA Secure Transmission Systems
authors: Yulei Lou, Yulong Zou, Hong Wang, Jia Zhu, Keqian Zhang, Wentong Lin, Longshen Chen
abstract: In this paper, we investigate the downlink non-orthogonal multiple access (NOMA) secure transmission systems, where a base station (BS) uses superposition coding to transmit information to users in the presence of multiple colluding eavesdroppers. To improve the information security, we propose a movable antenna (MA) and active beamforming (AB) assisted NOMA (MAABaN) secure transmission scheme, in which the BS is equipped with multiple MAs to enhance the physical layer security (PLS). To this end, we formulate a sum secrecy rate maximization optimization problem by jointly optimizing the AB vectors and the MA positions. To solve the complex non-convex problem, we employ the alternating optimization method to transform the original problem into two subproblems. Regarding the subproblem of MA positions, we analyze the coupling relationship between the position variables, and optimize one set of position variables while keeping others fixed. Then, we propose a low-complexity iterative algorithm for the MA positions based on multi-start gradient ascent (MSGA), and construct a joint optimization framework of MA and AB. Numerical results demonstrate that the proposed MAABaN scheme can effectively enhance PLS performance, and the proposed MSGA-based algorithm can also be applied to MA-assisted NOMA secure transmission systems.

title: Automated Software Test Case Generation Using Directional Partially Weighted Ensemble Large Language Models with Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG)
authors: Wantana Sisomboon, Jakkrit Kaewyotha, Wararat Songpan
abstract: Ensuring software quality relies on testing practices that can reliably confirm whether delivered systems meet user requirements. While Acceptance Test-Driven Development (ATDD) encourages early collaboration between stakeholders and developers, creating comprehensive acceptance test cases remains a human task that heavily depends on domain expertise. Recent progress in Large Language Models (LLMs) has made automated test case generation feasible. However, approaches based on a single model often suffer from performance instability, uneven test coverage, and a limited ability to generalize across heterogeneous requirements. This study proposes an automated test case generation framework that combines directional partially weighted ensemble large language models (LLMs) with Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG). The framework assigns model influence dynamically according to directional performance trends and degrees of partial correctness and consolidates candidate outputs through a weighted majority-voting mechanism and cross-validation conducted over multiple folds. Five state-of-the-art LLMs, Claude, ChatGPT, Gemini, Qwen, and Mistral, were evaluated using 16 criteria derived from four widely adopted black box testing techniques: Boundary Value Analysis, Equivalence Partitioning, Use Case testing, and State Transition testing. Under the evaluated experimental conditions, the proposed framework achieved 100% test coverage, indicating complete consistency with the defined gold-standard acceptance criteria. Cross-validation results further demonstrate improved robustness, stronger generalization to previously unseen requirements, and linear scalability, supporting practical deployment in real-world software development environments, and complexity analysis to confirm linear throughout growth (O(n)), which supports efficient deployment under increasing workloads.

title: Codebook Transfer With Vision-to-Language Translation for Vector Quantization
authors: Baoquan Zhang, Guotao Liang, Tianran Chen, Yunming Ye, Zhiyuan Wen, Xiaochen Qi, Yao He
publish: IEEE Transactions on Pattern Analysis and Machine Intelligence
abstract: Vector quantization (VQ) is a fundamental research problem in image synthesis, which aims to represent an image with a discrete token sequence. Existing studies effectively address this problem by learning a discrete codebook from scratch and in a code-independent manner to quantize continuous representations into discrete tokens. However, learning a codebook from scratch and in a code-independent manner is highly challenging, which may be a key reason causing codebook collapse, i.e., some code vectors can rarely be optimized without regard to the relationship between codes and good codebook priors such that die off finally. In this paper, inspired by pretrained language models, we find that these language models have actually pretrained a superior codebook via a large number of text corpus, but such information is rarely exploited in VQ. To this end, we propose a novel codebook transfer framework with vision-to-language translation, called VQCT-VLT, which aims to transfer a well-trained codebook from pretrained language models to VQ for robust codebook learning. Specifically, we first introduce a pretrained codebook from language models and part-of-speech knowledge as priors. Then, we construct a vision-related codebook with these priors for achieving codebook transfer. Finally, a novel codebook transfer network is designed to exploit abundant semantic relationships between codes contained in pretrained codebooks for robust codebook learning. Although the above version can achieve superior image synthesis performance, we find the learned codebook difficult to align the text semantics. To this end, we introduce image captions as auxiliary supervisory information and then design a vision-to-language translation module to further achieve vision-language-aligned codebook learning. Experimental results on various tasks show that our VQCT-VLT method achieves superior performance over previous state-of-the-art VQ methods.

title: Operating Multiple GAI Models in AI Training Clusters: Trends, Challenges, and Opportunities
authors: Meng Yuan, Qiang Wu, Xiangbin Wang, Songjing Tao, Ran Wang
abstract: As AI-Generated Content (AIGC) transforms digital experiences, the demand for training and deploying multiple Generative AI (GAI) models at scale is rapidly growing. This demand is pushing AI training clusters to transition from dedicating resources to one foundation model toward concurrently hosting heterogeneous models, including both general- purpose and domain-specific variants, within the same infrastructure. This shift from single-model to multi-model operations reshapes AI system architecture and introduces new challenges in resource scheduling and orchestration. Clusters need to manage heterogeneous and dynamic demands across compute, memory, and networking, making efficient scheduling a challenge. In this article, we highlight the emerging paradigm of operating multiple GAI models in large-scale clusters. We analyze key trends driving this change, identify core challenges in scheduling and resource management, and explore promising solutions. Specifically, we propose topology-aware scheduling and AI-driven optimization techniques to improve resource utilization and training efficiency in GAI workloads. Finally, we outline future research directions, including secure multi-tenant scheduling, model-aware orchestration, and sustainability-focused cluster management.

title: Hyperspectral Sparse Unmixing via Joint Robust Spatial Priors and Weighted Total Variation Regularization
authors: Fan Li, Xiyu Chen, Shaoquan Zhang, Jiaqiang Zhou, Huasheng Zhu, Yuyang Liu, Hongyu Zhang, Chengzhi Deng, Shengqian Wang
publish: IEEE Journal of Selected Topics in Applied Earth Observations and Remote Sensing
abstract: Sparse unmixing, which introduces a large spectral library to transform the problem of mixed-pixel decomposition into the search for an optimal subset of endmembers that best represents the spectral characteristics of an image, has become a prominent research focus. Building upon this, the integration of spatial information from the image is an effective approach to improve unmixing accuracy. Although numerous existing methods have explored spatial information from various perspectives, they often become ineffective in scenarios characterized by abundant outliers and complex noise, leading to loss of detail in abundance maps. To address these limitations, this paper proposes an unmixing method integrating robust spatial priors and weighted total variation sparse regularization (RSPWTV). The objective is to fully exploit the spatial information from multiple angles, aiming to enhance unmixing accuracy while increasing robustness against noise and outliers, and effectively preserving edge structures. Specifically, building upon the robust sparse unmixing (RSU) framework, the proposed method introduces two key innovations. First, a weighted total variation (WTV) regularization model incorporating an adaptive weighting mechanism is constructed. By combining spectral angular similarity and spatial proximity information within local neighborhoods, discriminative adaptive weighting factors are generated to dynamically adjust the smoothing intensity across different regions. This strategy strengthens smoothing in homogeneous areas to effectively suppress noise while reducing smoothing in edge regions to preserve geometric structures and detail. Second, a superpixel-guided pre-processing strategy is employed. This involves performing coarse unmixing to obtain initial abundance estimates, which are then used to construct a filter-guided weighted sparse prior. This prior facilitates image smoothing, thereby mitigating the interference of noise in the unmixing process and significantly improving the overall stability and accuracy of the results. The proposed model is efficiently solved using the alternating direction method of multipliers (ADMM). Experimental validation on both simulated data and real hyperspectral images demonstrates that the proposed RSPWTV method outperforms existing unmixing methods in terms of outlier resistance, noise robustness and edge preservation capability, exhibiting superior unmixing performance.

title: IEEE Membership
authors: N/A

title: A Graph Convolutional Network with Pretrained Features and Iterative Polar Coordinate Attention for Cross-Subject EEG-based Neuropsychiatric Diagnosis
authors: Yin Liu, Jiaojiao Deng, Runyi Xu, Yi Zhou
publish: IEEE Journal of Biomedical and Health Informatics
abstract: Electroencephalography (EEG)-based diagnosis of neuropsychiatric disorders offers a non-invasive and cost-effective solution for early detection. However, robust cross-subject generalization remains a major challenge due to substantial inter-individual variability in EEG signals. To address this, we propose PreIPCA-GCN, a novel graph convolutional network that integrates pretrained temporal features and Iterative Polar Coordinate Attention (IPCA)-based brain connectivity modeling. Specifically, we utilize a modified version of LaBraM, a large-scale pretrained EEG model, to extract subject-invariant node representations. Functional brain connectivity is then characterized using Pearson correlation and cosine similarity in polar space, capturing both connectivity strength (radius) and phase synchronization (angle). To fuse these complementary cues, we introduce a dual-path IPCA mechanism, refining the adjacency matrix across iterations. PreIPCA-GCN is evaluated on six public EEG datasets covering five neuropsychiatric disorders (e.g., attention-deficit/hyperactivity disorder, Alzheimer's disease, schizophrenia), consistently demonstrating strong cross-subject accuracies under both hold-out (86.77%-95.69%) and leave-one-subject-out cross-validation (88.81%-97.49%). Comprehensive comparative results show that PreIPCA-GCN outperforms several state-of-the-art methods. Ablation studies further confirm the effectiveness of both the pretrained node features and IPCA-based fused adjacency matrix in improving cross-subject generalization. These findings suggest PreIPCA-GCN as a robust and generalizable framework for cross-subject EEG-based neuropsychiatric diagnosis, offering strong potential for future clinical applications.

title: Comparative Analysis of CNN and LSTM Models for Muscle Activation Detection Using EMG signals
authors: Venkitesh Sasikumar, Jishnu Vijayan, Hema P Menon
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent and Innovative Technologies in Computing, Electrical and Electronics (IITCEE)
abstract: Electromyography (EMG) is a vital technique for assessing muscle activity, enabling the distinction between resting and active muscle states. This study explores the effectiveness of Convolutional Neural Networks (CNNs), a sophisticated machine learning architecture, and Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM) networks, a deep learning approach, particularly in scenarios with limited data availability. Real-time EMG signals were collected using surface electrodes placed on the biceps of subjects. These signals were captured and processed through an Advancer Muscle Sensor V3 connected to an Arduino board. To ensure signal clarity, a low-pass filter was applied to eliminate noise and artifacts. Key features such as Zero Crossings (ZC), Slope Sign Changes (SSC), Mean Frequency (MNF), and Median Frequency (MDF) were extracted for further analysis. To prevent overfitting and ensure model generalization, different training strategies were employed during model development. The proposed CNN model achieved an accuracy of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$90.42 \backslash \%$</tex> and LSTM model achieved an accuracy of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$90.64 \backslash \%$</tex>.

title: Correlation Analysis of LMS Data Using Built-In AI Tools to Identify Engagement Patterns
authors: Maria Lapina, Marina Tokmakova, Alexey Dmitrienko, Marina Gordova, Gaydaa AlZohbi, B. Deepanraj
publish: 2025 International Conference on AI-Driven STEM Education and Learning Technologies (AISTEMEDU)
abstract: The article reflects the findings of a research conducted to determine behavioral patterns of student engagement depending on the correlation analysis of the digital footprint in the learning management system (LMS) Moodle. Based on one of the courses, Introduction to Information Security, the authors examined the data of 148 students watching video lectures, discussing on a forum, and their academic performance. With the help of integrated analytical tools, some of the main indicators of effective learning, including; thoughtful viewing, early participation in discussion and social engagement, were revealed. The methods of statistical analysis such as Pearson correlation and the t-test have shown significant associations between these markers and the end of course score. Depending on the obtained results, recommendations are formulated in practice to streamline the learning process, such as the use of interactive work with lectures and active involvement in discussions in such forums. The research proves the possibility of utilizing educational analytics and data mining to enhance the quality of education and facilitate the process of making pedagogical choices.

title: On the Optimality of Evaluating Big-Five Personality Trait Detection using Exact Match Metric
authors: Angad Singh, Priti Maheshwary, Nitin Kumar Mishra, Neerja Dubey
publish: 2025 World Conference on Cutting-Edge Science and Technology (WCCEST)
abstract: Personality traits are fascinating in understanding human behavior. The Big-Five model is the obvious choice of most researchers to represent personality traits due to its wide acceptance. Numerous machine and deep learning models use multimedia data, especially textual data, to predict personality traits in Big-Five models. Also, feature selection is a crucial aspect of these models. Accuracy is the most prevalent metric used to evaluate these models' performance, and it takes each trait separately. However, all personality traits must be considered jointly to judge personality holistically. The Exact Match (EM) metric is used for the same purpose in multi-label classification. Therefore, this article evaluates the performance based on exact match metric and different feature selection methods using SVM classifier for all the personality traits in Big-Five model.

title: An Anti-Spoofing Architecture with WavResSa for Deepfake Detection
authors: Po Cheng Chan, Jia Ching Wang
publish: 2025 2nd Beyond Technology Summit on Informatics International Conference (BTS-I2C)
abstract: This paper presents an WavResSA, a novel anti-spoofing architecture for Deepfake Detection. We propose WavResSA, which applies a pre-trained WavLM [1] base model as a front-end feature extractor to extract speech embeddings, followed by a ResNet backbone enhanced with a self-attention mechanism, enabling the network to capture long-range temporal–spectral dependencies of spoofed audio. WavResSA achieved achieved an EER of 7.04% on the ASVspoof 5 dataset, which includes both logical access (LA) and Deepfake (DF) conditions with diverse channel effects and codec distortions, show the robustness of the proposed framework.. These results validate the effectiveness of our architecture design and training strategies in improving the robustness of deepfake and spoofing detection systems.

title: Lightweight 3D U-Net for Accurate Hippocampus segmentation in brain MRI for Alzheimer's detection
authors: Anusha Preetham, Akchara TKB, Nagarathna C R, Pallavi Kulkarni, Pooja Nayak
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent and Innovative Technologies in Computing, Electrical and Electronics (IITCEE)
abstract: The importance of hippocampal segmentation in MRI is essential in the study of disorders like Alzheimer, epilepsy and schizophrenia and still difficult because of anatomical variation. We present a deep learning model with U-Net, super-resolution (SR) and Monte Carlo (MC) dropout to enhance reliable and uncertainty-sensitive segmentation. With the Medical Segmentation Decathlon dataset, SR was able to preserve fine details but MC dropout was able to give consistent predictions and uncertainty maps. The proposed model demonstrated Dice = 0.92 and IoU = 0.84 with lower over- (0.12), and under-segmentation (0.17) rates, and thus, a robust, reproducible, and clinically relevant performance across scalable neuroimaging applications.

title: Hybrid Deep Learning Framework for Detecting Gray Leaf Spot Disease in Maize
authors: Ajatray Swagat Bhuyan
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent and Innovative Technologies in Computing, Electrical and Electronics (IITCEE)
abstract: The disorder that is denoted by the name Gray-Leaf-Spot (GLS) is the disease in the plantations which is caused due to fungi Cercospora-zeae-maydis, it mostly causes a type of gray lesions on the leaves, preventing photosynthesis and leading to less yields. The deterrence strategies mostly involve planation of resistant corn varieties, practicing of crop rotation to minimize fungal spore presence, and maintenance of clean fields. The application of fungicides at appropriate time, mostly during the periods of high humidity and mild temperatures also helps in controlling the disorder and safeguard the crop. This research allows for the deep-learning model built to differentiate between the two main types of the image data, the corn leaves affected by the disease and the healthy-plants. The visual photos undergo de-noising to improve quality. The cleaned data is then used to train VGG16 algorithm based model, and it achieves high accuracy. In summary, this research aims to develop a highly accurate image classifier for categorizing between the disorder and healthy plant, and it achieves an accuracy rate of 99.90%.

title: Dual Refrigeration System Using Peltier Plate
authors: Nilam N. Ghuge, Prathamesh S. Kulkarni, Pratiksha P. Mane, Omkar T. Gadekar
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent and Innovative Technologies in Computing, Electrical and Electronics (IITCEE)
abstract: This prototype introduces a compact box that can heat and cool at the same time, using Peltier plates. The box is built with two floors: the top part is a heating section, and the bottom part is a cooling section. We use four Peltier plates-two for each section. The main idea is to connect the plates differently for heating and cooling. For heating, we connect the Peltier plate in reverse connection, and for cooling, we connect the Peltier plate one way. We also added heat sinks to make the use of energy more efficient. Our device can control the temperature in both sections at the same time, and it is portable. The system provides heating and cooling in separate chambers simultaneously and it is suitable for small portable applications such as food delivery and medical storage. Experimental results show that the cooling chamber reaches near 2.5-3°C while the heating chamber maintains temperatures above 60°C.

title: Convolutional Neural Networks for Satellite Imagery Interpretation in Automated Cloudburst Hazard Detection
authors: Flamin David, Harish M, Blessed Sam B
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent and Innovative Technologies in Computing, Electrical and Electronics (IITCEE)
abstract: Cloudburst events are sudden, highly intense rainfall occurrences that often cause flash flooding, landslides, and massive destruction to human settlements and infrastructure. Accurate prediction of such events is critical for risk mitigation and emergency preparedness. This paper presents an image-based prediction system that employs deep learning techniques to analyze satellite and meteorological imagery for identifying regions likely to experience cloudbursts. The study utilizes AlexNet for efficient visual feature extraction, with VGG16 and ResNet50 employed for comparative assessment. VGG16's extended depth and small convolutional filters enable precise feature representation, while ResNet50's residual connections enhance model generalization and mitigate gradient degradation. Preprocessing steps including normalization, scaling, and augmentation ensure robustness to cloud structure variability. Performance evaluation using accuracy, precision, recall, and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$F 1$</tex> score demonstrates that leveraging multiple convolutional network architectures yields higher reliability and reduced false detection rates.
