The proposed solution offers an automated, scalable system for meteorological services and disaster response agencies to anticipate hazardous cloudbursts before they occur. This research contributes to early warning systems by focusing on image based deep learning techniques, enhancing protection of socio-economic stability in at risk regions.

title: Heterogeneous Graph Anomaly Detection with Graph Wavelet Transformer
authors: Xiaoou Zhang, Chuan Zhou, Yang Liu, Shuai Zhang, Peng Zhang, Yujia Zhu, Qingyun Liu
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Data Mining (ICDM)
abstract: Graph Anomaly Detection (GAD) identifies deviant patterns including anomalous nodes, edges, and subgraphs in graph data, with significant applications in social networks, cybersecurity, and financial risk control. While spectral methods have proven effective for homogeneous graph anomaly detection, their application to heterogeneous graphs remains challenging due to structural complexity and semantic richness. Existing heterogeneous graph anomaly detection methods either rely on manually designed meta-paths or decompose the graph into homogeneous subgraphs, leading to limited flexibility or loss of structural integrity. To address these limitations, we propose the Graph Wavelet Transformer (GWT), a novel spectral-based approach that integrates global graph properties and spectral analysis without requiring meta-path information. GWT employs a three-stage process: heterogeneous-to-homogeneous graph conversion, global dependency modeling via graph transformers, and spectral-aware feature enhancement focusing on frequency band components. Extensive experiments on multiple benchmarks demonstrate that GWT significantly outperforms ten baseline methods, providing a new paradigm for heterogeneous graph anomaly detection that preserves structural completeness while achieving computational efficiency.

title: The Demon is in Ambiguity: Revisiting Situation Recognition with Single Positive Multi-Label Learning
authors: Yiming Lin, Yuchen Niu, Shang Wang, Kaizhu Huang, Qiufeng Wang, Xiao-Bo Jin
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Data Mining (ICDM)
abstract: Context recognition (SR) is a fundamental task in computer vision that aims to extract structured semantic summaries from images by identifying key events and their associated entities. Specifically, given an input image, the model must first classify the main visual events (verb classification), then identify the participating entities and their semantic roles (semantic role labeling), and finally localize these entities in the image (semantic role localization). Existing methods treat verb classification as a single-label problem, but we show through a comprehensive analysis that this formulation fails to address the inherent ambiguity in visual event recognition, as multiple verb categories may reasonably describe the same image. This paper makes three key contributions: First, we reveal through empirical analysis that verb classification is inherently a multilabel problem due to the ubiquitous semantic overlap between verb categories. Second, given the impracticality of fully annotating large-scale datasets with multiple labels, we propose to reformulate verb classification as a single positive multi-label learning (SPMLL) problem - a novel perspective in SR research. Third, we design a comprehensive multi-label evaluation benchmark for SR that is carefully designed to fairly evaluate model performance in a multi-label setting. To address the challenges of SPMLL, we futher develop the Graph Enhanced Verb Multilayer Perceptron (GE-VerbMLP), which combines graph neural networks to capture label correlations and adversarial training to optimize decision boundaries. Extensive experiments on realworld datasets show that our approach achieves competitive improvement on the more meaningful multi-label Average Precision (MAP) metric while remaining competitive on traditional top-1 and top-5 accuracy metrics. To our knowledge, our research is the first work that the formulate, solving, and evaluating of verb classification in the SPMLL fashion, which provides theoretical insights and practical tools for advancing situation recognition research. The code and dataset will be available in https://github.com/JaMesLiMers/Multi_Label_ImSitu.git.

title: Test-Time Graph Rebirth for GNN Generalization Under Distribution Shifts
authors: Xin Zheng, Bo Li, Yu Zheng, Qin Zhang, Haishuai Wang, Yuxuan Liang, Alan Wee-Chung Liew, Shirui Pan
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Data Mining (ICDM)
abstract: Recent advancements in test-time adaptation (TTA) offer promising solutions for mitigating performance degradation caused by distribution shifts. However, they may fall short in practical deployment of GNNs during test time, due to the significant reliance on impractical retraining or modifications to online GNN models. To address such challenges, in this work, we propose a novel method, i.e., Test-Time Graph REBirth, dubbed TT-GREB, to effectively generalize the well-trained GNN models to the test-time graphs under distribution shifts by directly manipulating the test graph data. Concretely, we develop an overall framework designed by two principles for obtaining newly reborn test graphs, corresponding to two sub-modules: (1) prototype extractor for re-extracting the environment-invariant features of the test-time graph; and (2) environment refiner for re-fining the environment-varying features to explore the potential shifts. Furthermore, we propose a dual test-time graph contrastive learning objective with an effective iterative optimization strategy to obtain optimal prototype components and environmental components of the test graph. Extensive experiments on real-world graphs under diverse test-time distribution shifts verify the effectiveness of our proposed method, showcasing its superior ability to manipulate test-time graphs for better GNN generalization ability.

title: Channel Estimation in MIMO-OFDM: A Review
authors: Kuldip Kumar, Khaing Su Latt Thar, Thiha Naing
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Internet of Things: Smart Innovation and Usages (IoT-SIU)
abstract: The fast evolution of wireless systems requires high-rate transmission of data with enhanced reliability. MIMO together with OFDM has surfaced as a crucial technology to offer improved spectral efficiency and counteract multipath fading. But, to counter ISI and facilitate good signal reconstruction at the receiver, channel estimation accurately is essential. This paper provides an extensive review of several channel estimation methods in MIMO-OFDM systems. The advantages and shortcomings of each are stated, as well as their suitability in other channel environments. In addition, recent developments and hybrid methods that integrate more than one estimation method are considered. This review identifies the estimation accuracy, computational complexity, and spectral efficiency trade-offs and discusses the implications of future research into improving channel estimation for next-generation wireless networks.

title: Explainable AI for Medical Image Analysis: Enhancing Diagnostic Transparency and Clinical Trust
authors: Shaheena K V, Anila R Nambiar, Haider Alabdeli, Navdeep Singh, Ravi Kalra, Praveen Mittal
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Artificial Intelligence for Innovations in Healthcare Industries (ICAIIHI)
abstract: Explainable Artificial Intelligence (XAI) is indeed causing an uproar in the sphere of medical image analysis. It is simply a matter of developing trust, creating transparency, and promoting clinical utilization of AI systems. This systematic review gathers the knowledge of 25 latest studies to provide us with a clear image of the current state of XAI, the issues in it, and of the possible future directions. The results demonstrate that there is a growing reliance on deep learning models that are enhanced with interpretability techniques, such as saliency maps, self-explainable architectures and ensemble techniques. The papers emphasize the need to consider clinical relevance, usability and compliance with regulatory standards, particularly in such crucial fields as breast cancer diagnosis, brain tumor classification and analysis of Alzheimer disease. Despite all this progress, there remain some large issues to address, such as the lack of standardized platforms of evaluation, the difficulties of connecting technical descriptions to clinical reasoning, and the necessity to uphold equitability and confidentiality in federated learning settings. Besides that, new solutions such as compression that protects privacy and multi-modal explanations are leading to more effective and reliable solutions. In this review, interdisciplinary teamwork, emphasis on human-centered design and standardization of benchmarks are highlighted as the necessary conditions to promote responsible and effective XAI in medical image analysis.

title: A Dynamic Time Warping Based Cyber-Physical Intrusion Detection System for Smart Grid and Smart Metering Infrastructure
authors: Harsh Kumar, Sanjeev Kumar
publish: 2026 IEEE 16th Annual Computing and Communication Workshop and Conference (CCWC)
abstract: Advanced Metering Infrastructure (AMI) enables fine-grained monitoring in modern smart grids but also exposes new cyber-physical attack surfaces. Existing anomaly detectors are largely amplitude-centric and remain insensitive to temporal distortions, allowing adversaries to manipulate smart meter traces via time-warping, replay, or shape-preserving scaling while preserving plausible statistics. This paper presents DTW-IDS, a Dynamic Time Warping (DTW) based intrusion detection framework that explicitly models nonlinear temporal inconsistencies in AMI data streams. DTW-IDS constructs multi-level statistical baselines, aligns incoming segments using constrained DTW, and applies cross-meter consensus to distinguish legitimate behavioral variation from coordinated manipulation. Experiments on 60 days of simulated AMI data show that DTW-IDS detects timewarped theft, replay substitutions, and magnitude-scaled attacks with over 95 % true-positive rate and under 3 % false-positive rate, outperforming thresholding and Euclidean similarity while remaining suitable for real-time deployment.

title: AI-Driven Wildfire Reasoning Utilizing Multimodal Retrieval-Augmented Generation
authors: Ashen Sandeep, Sunera Sandaruwan, Sithum Jayarathna, Venura Samarappuli
publish: 2025 International Conference on Advances in Technology and Computing (ICATC)
abstract: Wildfires pose a major natural threat, causing sub-stantial loss of life and economic damage worldwide. Each year, vast areas are destroyed, and experts warn that climate change will likely intensify both the frequency and severity of these events in the future. To mitigate these hazards, a Visual Question Answering (VQA) system has been developed for the domain of wildfires, powered by multimodal RAG pipelines and large content models. The proposed solution processes multimodal input and provides logical, context-aligned responses to user prompts through a conversational user interface (CUI). Here, a multimodal Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) framework is designed for wildfire detection, which integrates dedicated IMAGE RAG and CSV RAG components. The IMAGE RAG component processes diverse visual data, including UAV imagery, drone captures, and mobile phone images, to identify fire signatures, smoke patterns, and burn severity assessments. Simultaneously, the CSV RAG component handles structured environmental data encompassing meteorological readings, historical wildfire records, and sensor outputs to provide contextual understanding of fire conditions and risk factors. The system achieved a performance of 74.13 % accuracy on an evaluation dataset of wildfire-related question-answer pairs, which demonstrates its effectiveness in real-world information synthesis tasks. This multi-modal RAG framework addresses critical gaps in current wildfire monitoring systems by providing an intelligent, query-driven interface that can synthesize environmental data for improved decision-making in wildfire management and prevention strategies.

title: Solar-Safe: An Edge AI - Powered Fault Detection in PhotoVoltaic Modules Using Deep Learning Models
authors: Jishnu Teja Dandamudi, Yogendra Chhetri, Prajeesh C B, Abhishek S
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent and Innovative Technologies in Computing, Electrical and Electronics (IITCEE)
abstract: The global push to decrease air pollutants has accelerated the adoption of renewable energies, with photovoltaic (PV) energy systems playing a pivotal role. PV powerhouses consist of numerous PV modules that require regular inspection, but traditional methods are often time-consuming and pose safety risks. In this work, we develop several machine learning (ML) and deep learning (DL) models-such as Convolutional Neural Networks (CNN), Involutional Neural Networks (INN) based approaches-to investigate PV powerhouses, focusing on the detection of common defects such as hotspots and hot substrings. The models will be trained and evaluated using datasets containing thermal images of PV modules captured from both aerial and terrestrial platforms, with performance assessed using statistical metrics like F1 score, accuracy, and precision. Subsequently, both models were deployed and executed on a Google Coral Edge TPU board to enable fast and efficient on-device inference for real-time PV module inspection, and the results will be compared with existing methods to demonstrate the effectiveness of the proposed approach. Literature Review, Code and Results: Github

title: Performance Analysis of Random Forest and Decision Tree for Automated Assessment of Concept-Map Quality
authors: Didik Dwi Prasetya, Roudhotulloh Nazakhan, Wahyu Sakti Gunawan Irianto, Utomo Pujianto, Zulfiqar Naufal Maulana, Ahmad Kholish Fauzan Shobiry
publish: 2025 2nd Beyond Technology Summit on Informatics International Conference (BTS-I2C)
abstract: Manual assessment of concept maps often requires considerable time and is prone to subjectivity and inconsistency across evaluators. To address this challenge, this study investigates the use of machine learning to automate the evaluation of concept-map proposition quality. The Decision Tree and Random Forest were involved in predicting proposition quality scores. A total of 691 propositions collected from student-generated concept maps on Relational Database topics were preprocessed through text cleaning and transformed into numerical features using TF-IDF. Both models were trained using an 80:20 data split, and hyperparameters were optimized using GridSearchCV. Model performance was evaluated using accuracy, precision, recall, F1-score, confusion matrix, and Cohen’s Kappa as a reliability measure. The Random Forest model achieved the best performance with an accuracy of 81.2% and a Cohen’s Kappa of 0.872, outperforming Decision Tree, which achieved an accuracy of 79.8% and a Kappa of 0.852. These results demonstrate that Random Forest offers higher reliability and consistency in automatic proposition assessment, indicating its potential for supporting scalable and objective evaluation of student concept maps in educational settings.

title: Security Risks and Privacy Challenges of Large Language Models in Enterprise Environments
authors: Soliana Hailemichael, Maria Chano, Eman El-Sheikh, Thaier Hayajneh
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Metaverse, and Cybersecurity (ICAMAC)
abstract: Large Language Models (LLMs), such as ChatGPT and Gemini, are increasingly adopted across sectors including education, healthcare, and corporate environments. While they offer operational efficiencies, their integration raises significant data security and compliance concerns. This study evaluates whether LLMs retain and disclose sensitive organizational data when exposed to proprietary inputs such as HR records, financial documents, and code samples. Using controlled experiments, we assessed the susceptibility of these models to prompt injection and role-based manipulation attacks. Findings indicate that both models exhibit varying degrees of data leakage and compliance violations, particularly in the context of GDPR and intellectual property protections. Based on these results, we propose a practical framework to guide the responsible use of LLMs in enterprise environments. The framework includes mitigation strategies for minimizing data exposure, ensuring regulatory compliance, and reducing the risks associated with AI integration in the workplace.

title: Enhancing the Performance of Open-Source LLMs for E-Commerce CRM Automation Using Multi-Agent Systems
authors: S. Ajintha, M. Carmel Wijegunasekara
publish: 2025 International Conference on Advances in Technology and Computing (ICATC)
abstract: The rapid growth of e-commerce has heightened the need for efficient Customer Relationship Management (CRM) automation. Proprietary Large Language Models (LLMs) like GPT-4 excel in CRM tasks but are costly for small and medium-sized enterprises (SMEs). Open-source alternatives, such as LLaMA-3.1, offer affordability but are limited in terms of accuracy and contextual understanding. This paper presents a multi-agent framework to optimize open-source LLMs for three key CRM tasks: Named Entity Disambiguation (NED), Policy Violation Identification (PVI), and Knowledge-based Question Answering (KQA). Evaluated on the CRMArena benchmark with 390 queries, the framework using LLaMA-3.1-70B achieved accuracies of 92.31% (NED), 59.23% (PVI), and 85.38% (KQA), outperforming prior open-source benchmarks (<55%). The approach demonstrates cost-effective CRM automation comparable to proprietary models, with inference costs as low as $0.04 per task. Future enhancements include fine-tuning and real-world deployment to further improve robustness.

title: Extended Chacha20 Stream Cipher With Modified The Round Quarter Function for IoT System
authors: Muladi Muladi, Soraya N. Mustika, Achamd Hamdan, Abd Kadir Mahamad, Anik Nur Handayani, Satrio Dwi Sanjaya
publish: 2025 2nd Beyond Technology Summit on Informatics International Conference (BTS-I2C)
abstract: Salsa20 and Chacha20 are two stream ciphers developed using bit permutation processes on keystream matrices. Both are also known as Latin dance stream ciphers based on the nature of the bit rotation in words to the right or left. Chacha20 is a stream cipher that has been widely implemented to improve data security from cryptanalysis attacks. Many studies have demonstrated the strength of this cipher, but improvements are needed to anticipate vulnerabilities from different types of attacks. This study further develops both stream ciphers by modifying the Quarter Round (QR) function. The modifications made include changing the rotation constant by using prime numbers (13, 11, 7, 5), tested through simulations using Matlab and statistical tests using the NIST Statistical Test Suite (NSTS). The results showed that the proposed rotation constants of (13, 11, 7, 5) have 25% faster encryption time and 44% decryption time compared to (16, 12, 8, 7) in Chacha20/20 with almost equal symbol diffusion characteristics.

title: A Framework for Predicting Diabetic Retinopathy Using Deep Learning and Image Augmentation
authors: Vibhor Sharma, Deepak Srivastava, Suman Pant, Manoj Kumar, David Asirvatham
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Internet of Things: Smart Innovation and Usages (IoT-SIU)
abstract: Diabetic retinopathy is the main avoidable reason of people’s blindness. The person who have suffering from diabeties for a long period may face this issue. This study presents a deep learning-based method for auto identifying of DR using retinal images of high resoultion. To improve performance and generalization, this system uses transfer learning along with existing convolutional neural networks (CNNs) such as ResNet50, VGG16, InceptionV3, and MobileNetV2, as well as employing sufficient data augmentation. After undergoing preprocessing procedures like scaling, normalization, and contrast enhancement, the EyePACS dataset was utilized for training and assessment. To evaluate robustness, stratified 5-fold cross-validation was used. ResNet50 was having sensitivity of 88.0%, a 91.2% specificity, and the good accuracy (89.3%). Confusion matrices and ROC analysis verified the model’s efficacy at every DR severity level. Additionally, comparisons of inference times demonstrated that MobileNetV2 is a portable, effective substitute for real-time applications. These results imply that the suggested architecture is both computationally feasible and accurate enough to be used in clinical and mobile-based screening systems.

title: Predictive Analytics for Power Usage of Conveyor Belt Systems using Generative AI and IIOT Data
authors: Naeem Ayoub, Mohsin Raza, Arne Bilberg
publish: 2025 International Conference on Electrical and Computer Engineering Researches (ICECER)
abstract: Energy efficiency plays a crucial role in the field of industrial automation, particularly in relation to conveyor belt systems that operate continuously in various sectors, including mining, logistics, and manufacturing. Smart Industrial Components (SIC) are required to process, manage, and produce a substantial amount of different types of IIOT (Industrial Internet of Things) data. This historical data is the main source for analysing the current and future conditions of the components. In this paper, a predictive analytics algorithm is proposed to analyse the trend of real-time and future power consumption. The proposed algorithm seamlessly combines generative AI with IIoT data to enhance predictive modelling capabilities. This study highlights key challenges, methodologies, and future directions in leveraging AI-driven analytics for industrial energy management, as well as investigates the application of predictive data analytics for conveyor belt systems focusing on two key aspects: (1) historical data analysis to monitor the current condition of the conveyor belt system and (2) the use of generative AI for future prediction essential for analysing future trends of power consumption running at the specific condition.

title: Brightstream App: Revolutionizing Meralco’s Streetlight Management
authors: Yvonne Jazzey D. Melo, Jaybert H. Gamayon
publish: 2025 25th Conference of the Electric Power Supply Industry (CEPSI)
abstract: Brightstream App is a product of the 5D methodology (Discover, Define, Develop, Design and Deliver), the approach used by One Meralco to process improvement projects. It offers a structured yet flexible approach to problem-solving rooted in design thinking. The approach is from identifying a problem to validating and implementing a solution.Brightstream App is applicable for both mobile and desktop use. It was developed in 2024 using Microsoft Power Platform primarily for Meralco Flat Streetlight database and mapping. The software used is part of the company’s standard software suite and can only be accessed within the organization. Without the Brightstream app, it takes an average of 8 to 15 months to disconnect a flat streetlight account. But during the implementation of using the Brightstream app, it can execute and disconnect a flat streetlight account in 1-month average time. Aside from operational efficiencies the management of unpaid bills also improved. For the period of 6 months implementation, it was able to reduce Collection Rate of Arrears (CRA) by Php9M or 66.67%, Ending outstanding (EOS) reduced by Php 2.1M or 17.7% and the flat streetlight unpaid bills from September to October 2024 with 2 bills and up reduced by Php 2.6M.Brightstream App is the first digital flat streetlight tool for Meralco through maximizing the use of Power Apps for mobile and desktops views, Power BI for inventory map visualization, Excel and SharePoint List for database. The other benefits of Meralco from Brightstream app are the following: (1) eliminates data redundancies from paper-based or manual, (2) improved decision making, (3) used for back-up and recovery, (4) ensures data integration, (5) for easy access and data sharing within organization, (6) data asset management for flat streetlights and (7) improved end user productivity. It made use of the innovative low-code application development platform – Microsoft Power Apps to create this innovative application that automate and improve flat streetlight processes. It tailored to Meralco business requirements of improving efficiency, productivity and collaboration without requiring a tech-heavy approach. The focus of this paper is to present the processes and the results of Brightstream App for Meralco’s Flat Streetlight Management and its progression from 2024 to present. All the data used in this project were gathered exclusively from the project owners.

title: Construction and Application of a Customer Service Satisfaction Model in the Electric Vehicle Charging Infrastructure Industry: An Empirical Analysis of Five Southern Provinces in China
authors: Junmin Zhang, Yingda Chen, Shujie Bi
publish: 2025 25th Conference of the Electric Power Supply Industry (CEPSI)
abstract: This study examines the factors influencing customer satisfaction in China's electric vehicle (EV) charging infrastructure sector, set against the backdrop of the increasing prevalence of electric vehicles and the growing attention to the construction and service quality of charging facilities. The primary objective of the research is to identify and analyze the key elements that affect customer satisfaction within this industry, aiming to provide an empirical basis for enhancing the customer experience. The study employs statistical methods such as multiple regression analysis and variance analysis to systematically assess the impact of infrastructure diversity, technological innovation, service quality, and pricing on customer satisfaction. The findings indicate that infrastructure diversity, technological innovation, and service quality have a significant positive impact on customer satisfaction, with improvements in these areas potentially increasing satisfaction levels by approximately 30%, 25%, and 20%, respectively. In contrast, pricing exhibits a notable negative effect, where a 1% increase in price correlates with a roughly 2% decrease in customer satisfaction. These results highlight the necessity to prioritize diversity and technological innovation in the construction of charging infrastructure, while pricing strategies should be adjusted cautiously to avoid adverse effects on customer satisfaction. This research provides a comprehensive analysis of the customer satisfaction framework in the electric vehicle charging infrastructure sector, addressing a gap in the existing literature and offering practical recommendations for manufacturers and policymakers to enhance customer satisfaction and promote strategic industry development. By optimizing infrastructure construction and service strategies, it is possible to significantly improve overall customer satisfaction and thereby contribute to the healthy development of electric vehicle charging infrastructure in China.

title: Mathematical Reasoning in Large Language Models for Malayalam
authors: Aadidev S Raj, Vikram Vincent, Nisha Vargheese, Sridevi R, Ashok Immanuel
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent and Innovative Technologies in Computing, Electrical and Electronics (IITCEE)
abstract: Mathematical reasoning is a fundamental skill in early childhood education, but existing large language models (LLMs) exhibit inconsistent performance when applied to low-resource languages such as Malayalam. The results illustrate the serious hurdles for multilingual mathematical reasoning. The model frequently produced inappropriate results, or could not interpret Malayalam numerals. More advanced logic was at times misunderstood, and at times repaired. The model often incompletely reasoned; it put together the problem in a correct manner, but output numerically inaccurate results. This points to a misunderstanding of the pet dependability of symbolic computation when separated from understanding. Despite developing rather advanced models very quickly, there were only a few marginal increases in capability, and there remained resistance to many types of problems. Broadly, the exploratory nature of this study highlights the biggest challenge of inclusion of languages in AI for education. Even though LLMs appear to demonstrate promise to mediate learning in high-resource languages, as the examples in this report show, without considerable adaptation, their dependability still in local languages is extremely weak.

title: Domain-Specific Fine-Tuning of Multimodal LLMs for Active Perception
authors: Shruti Kumbhar, Aarya Joshi, Rucha Ranade, Kalyani J. Kulkarni, Amin Nazir Nagiwale
publish: 2025 World Conference on Cutting-Edge Science and Technology (WCCEST)
abstract: Multimodal Large Language Models (MM-LLMs) are revolutionizing artificial intelligence by their ability to process diverse sensory inputs, including vision, audio, and text. This paper investigates the fine-tuning of MM-LLMs specifically for critical military surveillance. We explore the integration of various modalities to achieve a comprehensive understanding of military environments, evaluating models such as BLIP and unsloth/Llama-3.2-11B-Vision-Instruct for their performance in this domain. The paper highlights the fine-tuning methodolo-gies employed to optimize these models for military-specific tasks, demonstrating their enhanced capabilities in interpreting complex visual cues and understanding contextual information relevant to defense. The advantages of these fine-tuned MM-LLMs over traditional LLMs in mimicking human-like perception and interaction within military contexts are showcased, with a focus on domain-specific use cases such as identifying military personnel, their ranks based on visual and potentially textual cues derived from their uniforms and surroundings. Challenges encountered during fine-tuning and potential future research directions within the scope of purely military applications are also discussed.

title: High-Accuracy Potato Leaf Disease Classification Using Transfer Learning with ResNet50
authors: Ayush Kumar, Pragati Baghel, Abhishek Bajpai, Naveen Tiwari, Shalinee Sahu, Shriyanshi
publish: 2025 International Conference on Advances in Machine Intelligence, and Cybersecurity Technologies (AMICT)
abstract: This study introduces a deep learning-based method for detecting and classifying potatoes disease in an automatic manner using a hybrid model that combines neural networks (CNN) with the ResNet50 architecture. The main objective is to optimize the early diagnosis of crop diseases in potato crops for early and late blight, so as not to reduce the yield from the potential loss of yield. The data set consists of publicly available images as well as local samples taken in Kannauj to offer some degree of diversity and real-world applicability. To improve generalization, the images were pre-processed using respective techniques including normalization, resizing, and data augmentation. The ResNet50 model was pre-trained on the ImageNet dataset and fine-tuned using transfer learning for the particular classification task. The model was assessed using key metrics for the proposed model that included precision, precision, recall, F1-score, and confusion matrix. The proposed model achieved high classification accuracy, thus establishing the potential of the model for implementation in real-time environments through established mobile or IoT-based presented platforms to help farmers monitor and manage disease effectively.

title: Tunable Mm-Wave Generation by SOA based Four Wave Mixing
authors: Naveenkumar M., Shivaleela E. S.
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent and Innovative Technologies in Computing, Electrical and Electronics (IITCEE)
abstract: As bandwidth demands continue to grow, shift from lower frequency spectrum to higher frequency spectrum particularly in the millimeter-wave range is needed. Generation of high-frequency signals in a cost-effective and efficient manner remains a challenge, due to the limited frequency response of electronic devices. Large amount of data at very high speeds, with very low latency can be carried by millimeter wave spectrum, which makes it an ideal carrier choice for accommodating the increase in data demands of various types of users such as online gaming systems, IoT based applications, mobile users, connected homes, video-based online applications, selfdriving vehicles, and other cloud-connected devices. Millimeter wave generation can be achieved by different techniques, in particular optical methods offer many advantages. In this work, we have generated tunable mm-waves, 23.44 G Hz to 224.69 G Hz, experimentally, by using semiconductor optical amplifier (SOA) based four wave mixing (FWM).

title: AI-Driven Security Analysis of Encrypted Communications
authors: Lakshmi Narasimham Rallabandi, Fasee Ullah, Shashi Bhushan
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Internet of Things: Smart Innovation and Usages (IoT-SIU)
abstract: This research looks into some of the challenges of cyber-security pertaining to encrypted information. Although there are cybercriminals unable to access information due to encrypting data, other forms of cyber-security threats still exist. Quantumly, the threats arise from metadata, e.g., the size of packets, the timing of packets, and the flow of packets. The research focuses on metadata of online encrypted traffic and trains algorithms for frequent anomaly detection, with the personally identifiable information being removed. Using the CICIDS2017 data set, our group built a system to categorize data as either normal or encrypted anomalous traffic. The classifiers being analyzed in this situation are the Decision Tree classifier, the Linear Support Vector Classifier, and the Stochastic Gradient Descent classifier. The LinearSVC model was the best one, with a score of 0.6069 and a 0.7552 accuracy against the test sample. These results highly suggest the effectiveness of automated and machine learning-based systems in identifying the misuse of a network from the pattern of activities in the flow of packets traversing the network without having to do a deep packet inspection.

title: Cognitive AI for Leadership and Organizational Development
authors: Geetha Manoharan, Jyoti Kulshreshtha, B. Gangadhar, Aashish Dhiman, C. Visvesvaran, Avnish Sharma
publish: 2025 World Conference on Cutting-Edge Science and Technology (WCCEST)
abstract: Cognitive Artificial Intelligence (AI) is quickly changing leadership and organisational development in the contemporary business environment. In this paper, the authors examine how cognitive AI and machine learning methods can be used to analyse data on employees and reveal essential patterns regarding attrition, job satisfaction, and performance. The project uses Exploratory Data Analysis (EDA) and feature visualisation and a Random Forest classification model to forecast employee attrition and determine the most influential variables. Through the Python libraries, Pandas, Seaborn, Matplotlib, and Scikit-learn, among others, a thorough analysis was performed. The findings denote that there are substantial correlations amid employee contentment, salary and turnover. The research paper identifies the strength of evidence-based decision making in promoting organisational performance and leadership practices. The results join the accelerating overlap between AI and human resource management and provide practical information for strategic interventions.

title: Sentiment Analysis for Brand Management Using Machine Learning Techniques
authors: Radhakrishnan P, Nidhi Sharma, Raksha Thakur, Tejas Yaduvanshi, Sujay Mugaloremutt Jayadev, Gayrat Bekbergenov
publish: 2025 World Conference on Cutting-Edge Science and Technology (WCCEST)
abstract: In this digital age, brands are being evaluated and defined more by what people say about them on social media networks, particular twitter. As a sub-field of Natural Language Processing (NLP), sentiment analysis provides an effective way of brand assessing consumer attitudes. In this paper, the author will discuss the application of machine learning (Logistic Regression, Random Forest, and Support Vector Machine (SVM)) to Twitter data in identifying and interpreting sentiments about a brand. The experiment includes the fine preprocessing of tweet texts, extracting features based on TF-IDF, training and evaluating models. Focusing on the comparative analysis, the results show that SVM classifier performs better than others with an accuracy of 71.09 which is slightly higher than the Logistic Regression (70.31) and Random Forest (69.58). The results are backed with classification reports, confusion matrices, and plots. The study aims at highlighting the practical use of machine learning in brand management, which can help monitor brand feedback in real-time and make strategic decisions based on consumer reactions.

title: A Practitioner’s Blueprint: An AI-Powered Prisma Framework for Multi-Cloud IAM Security
authors: Yuvaraj Madheswaran
publish: 2025 International Conference on Electrical and Computer Engineering Researches (ICECER)
abstract: Enterprises adopting multi-cloud environments face significant challenges in managing identity and access across diverse IAM models. Existing tools provide visibility into excessive permissions but often lack explainability, cross-cloud normalization, and operational safety, limiting their effectiveness in practice. This paper presents a practitioner’s blueprint for an AI-Powered Prisma Framework that unifies identity governance across AWS, Azure, and GCP. The framework introduces a Unified Permission Intermediate Representation (UP-IR) to normalize entitlements, an Explainable Least-Privilege (X-LP) engine to deliver transparent and auditable policy recommendations, and an Identity Attack-Path Simulator (I-APS) to evaluate lateral-movement risks before enforcement. A Compliance-Aware Remediation Planner (CARP) integrates technical remediations with regulatory controls, while a Closed-Loop Safe Deployment (CLSD) process ensures safe rollouts through simulation, canary testing, and rollback mechanisms. Together, these components bridge the gap between research concepts and operational needs, enabling organizations to achieve explainable, auditable, and continuously validated least-privilege access in complex multi-cloud environments.

title: An Eigenvector-Based Computational Method for Power and Efficiency Analysis of Bias-Flip Circuits
authors: Son Nguyen, Rajeevan Amirtharajah
publish: 2025 International Conference on Electrical and Computer Engineering Researches (ICECER)
abstract: Piezoelectric transducers are extensively utilized in vibration energy harvesting to convert mechanical vibrations into electrical energy to power low-power sensors and wearable devices. To increase the extracted output power from piezoelectric harvesters, numerous bias-flip circuits have been developed to efficiently rectify AC harvester output. In this paper, a mathematical analysis using eigenvectors and eigenvalues is proposed to accurately calculate the bias flip efficiency, output power, and power extraction improvement of several widely used bias-flip configurations. The proposed calculation method is validated through comparison with experimental results obtained from testing a fabricated 180 nm SOI CMOS bias-flip integrated circuit. The theoretical and experimental findings provide a comprehensive performance comparison of prevalent bias-flip circuit designs, offering insights for optimizing future vibration energy harvesting systems.

title: Cloud-enabled NLP for Automated Medical Record Summarization and Decision Support
authors: S. M. Srilanga Meenatshi, Shaini G.S, Sindhuri Suseela Mantena, M. Muthulekshmi, M. P. Revathi, Monisha R
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Artificial Intelligence for Innovations in Healthcare Industries (ICAIIHI)
abstract: The rapid expansion of electronic health records (EHRs) has posed difficulties retrieving essential patient information for clinical decision-making. This research presents a cloud-based Natural Language Processing (NLP) system for automating medical record summarization and decision assistance. The system utilizes sophisticated NLP methodologies, such as Named Entity Recognition (NER) and Bidirectional Encoder Representations from Transformers (BERT), to derive essential clinical insights from unstructured medical material. An experimental assessment was performed on a dataset of 50,000 anonymized patient records. The proposed method achieved 93.2% accuracy in extracting essential medical entities and a 91.5% F1-score in summarization quality relative to expert-generated summaries. The decision assistance module enhanced diagnostic prediction accuracy by 14.7% and decreased manual review time by an average of 37%. Cloud integration improved processing speed, with an average response time of 1.8 seconds per document, demonstrating scalability for real-time applications. The findings demonstrate that cloud-based NLP may substantially improve medical record summarization, optimize clinical processes, and improve decision-making precision. The research highlights the possibility of combining NLP with cloud computing to provide prompt and accurate patient care for healthcare workers.

title: Aspect-Based Sentiment Analysis on Public Service Reviews using a Hybrid Rule-Based and Bidirectional Encoder Representations from Transformers (BERT) Approach
authors: Entin Martiana Kusumaningtyas, Aliridho Barakbah, Arna Fariza
publish: 2025 2nd Beyond Technology Summit on Informatics International Conference (BTS-I2C)
abstract: Public service providers increasingly rely on customer feedback to evaluate and improve the quality of their services. However, the unstructured nature and high volume of textual reviews pose significant challenges for manual analysis. Aspect-Based Sentiment Analysis (ABSA) offers a fine-grained approach to understanding public opinion by identifying specific aspects of service and determining the sentiment associated with each. In this study, we propose a hybrid methodology that combines a rule-based aspect extraction framework with a BERT-based sentiment classification model to analyze public service reviews. The dataset consists of 1,100 reviews related to water utility services collected from social media platforms and official mobile applications. The rule-based module utilizes domain-specific dictionaries and regular expressions to extract key service aspects, such as dirty water, leakage, water usage, meter issues, no water supply, new installation, billing, and appreciation. For sentiment classification, we fine-tune a pre-trained BERT model to classify each aspect-related sentence into positive, negative, or neutral categories. Experimental evaluations demonstrate that the rule-based aspect extraction achieved an average accuracy of 0.78 across all aspects and an F1-score of 0.77, while the BERT model reached 0.98 accuracy in sentiment classification. These results highlight the potential of combining interpretable rule-based methods with powerful contextual language models for accurate and scalable sentiment analysis in the evaluation of public water services.

title: Deep Learning–Enhanced Soft-Tissue Deformation Models for High-Fidelity Virtual Surgery
authors: Deepak Upadhyay, Nookala Venu, Sonia Rani, Umesh Chandra Sharma, Lokesh Kumar, Deepesh Tamrakar
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Artificial Intelligence for Innovations in Healthcare Industries (ICAIIHI)
abstract: The paper has investigated how deep learning may be used to generate fast soft-tissue deformation models for real-time use during virtual surgical training while maintaining realistic accuracy. The traditional FEM-based deformation models of soft tissues have both low speed and poor performance in terms of time required to train, which makes them unsuitable for real-time use by surgeons who require immediate feedback. In this research, a GNN (Graph Neural Network) had been trained using synthetic tissue simulation data, and then evaluated in order to predict displacement in the soft tissue deformation model. The results from the evaluation showed scatter plots along the diagonal line, the distribution of the errors were minimal, and the latency was always below 20 ms even at high mesh cases. Additionally, different types of graphs (heatmap, radar, and stream plots), illustrated that the model was robust and consistent regardless of the direction of the forces applied and changes in the stiffness of the tissue. Although this research is not complete, it illustrates how incorporating ideas of physics into data-driven machine learning methods may produce better tools for patient-specific surgical planning and medical training.

title: TIMED: Adversarial and Autoregressive Refinement of Diffusion-Based Time Series Generation
authors: MohammadReza EskandariNasab, Shah Muhammad Hamdi, Soukaina Filali Boubrahimi
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Data Mining (ICDM)
abstract: Generating high-quality synthetic time series is a fundamental yet challenging task across domains such as forecasting and anomaly detection, where real data can be scarce, noisy, or costly to collect. Unlike static data generation, synthesizing time series requires modeling both the marginal distribution of observations and the conditional temporal dependencies that govern sequential dynamics. We propose TIMED, a unified generative framework that integrates a denoising diffusion probabilistic model (DDPM) to capture global structure via a forward-reverse diffusion process, a supervisor network trained with teacher forcing to learn autoregressive dependencies through next-step prediction, and a Wasserstein critic that provides adversarial feedback to ensure temporal smoothness and fidelity. To further align the real and synthetic distributions in feature space, TIMED incorporates a Maximum Mean Discrepancy (MMD) loss, promoting both diversity and sample quality. All components are built using masked attention architectures optimized for sequence modeling and are trained jointly to effectively capture both unconditional and conditional aspects of time series data. Experimental results across diverse multivariate time series benchmarks demonstrate that TIMED generates more realistic and temporally coherent sequences than state-of-the-art generative models.

title: Ethical Decision-Making Algorithms in Autonomous Vehicles
authors: Manraj Singh, Karanvir Singh, Jarrett Signoretti, Penelope Mankey, Tauheed Khan Mohd
publish: 2026 IEEE 16th Annual Computing and Communication Workshop and Conference (CCWC)
abstract: AV (Autonomous Vehicle) technology has seen rapid advances in recent years, but fully autonomous solutions have not yet been thought viable due to safety concerns. Most of these concerns revolve around the ability of autonomous systems to make the correct ethical decision in difficult and complex driving situations. This study focuses on testing AV decision-making algorithms, specifically on their ability to make safe and sound decisions in the face of difficult and complex driving situations that may arise through obstacles such as accidents or pedestrians. Safe decisions will be defined as following all traffic laws, while ethical decisions will be defined as minimizing harm that may be unavoidable. Using Grand Theft Auto <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{V}$</tex> as an autonomous vehicle simulation platform, an algorithm will be put to the test to ensure that it can navigate real-world situations both ethically and safely. The study found the natural consequences of what happens when ethical decision-making is flawed and fails

title: AutomataSim: A Visual Educational Platform for Simulating and Understanding Finite Automata
authors: Emmett Kjolseth, Ziang Chen, Parteek Kumar
publish: 2026 IEEE 16th Annual Computing and Communication Workshop and Conference (CCWC)
abstract: This paper introduces AutomataSim, a Python-based desktop application developed to simulate deterministic finite automata (DFA) through an interactive graphical interface. The system features a domain-specific language (DSL) for defining automata, step-by-step simulation, and visual state transitions using Graphviz. Built on a layered architecture, AutomataSim includes secure user account management and persistent storage using SQLite. The application is designed as an educational tool to enhance understanding of automata theory. AutomataSim is intended to support comprehension by bridging abstract formalism with intuitive, interactive visualization.

title: Vehicle Intelligent Tracking and Alert System Using Artificial Intelligence
authors: Monalisha Biswas, Tanay Halder, Partha Ghosh, Surya Shekhar Santra, Avijit Kumar Chaudhari, Vandana Yadav
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent and Innovative Technologies in Computing, Electrical and Electronics (IITCEE)
abstract: Artificial Intelligence is transforming automobile industry by enhancing safety through Control Systems and Automation which will increase the efficiency in manufacturing through automation and predictive maintenance. With continu- ous development of the automobile industry, almost every family has a car, so the car safety issues is gradually highlighted. The automobile has been a part of people's life and its demands are more diversified. The main focus of problem should be the interior safety of automobiles is we need to solve urgently. It is an important means for people to know the safety problems inside the car and take precautions against them. Vehicle want to be reliable and strong sensor which will be compatible with the overall environment and deliver the alert-based messages to the user to avoid unforeseen risks. The goal of our research and development is to create a self-sufficient, reliable, automated vehicle diagnose system that can reduce the risk of accident signif- icantly. Our Vehicle Intelligent Tracking and Alert System using Artificial Intelligence continuously observes vehicle health and driver responsiveness as well as negligence and will send a alert message to user register phone number. Basically, microcontroller which stores the data logs containing vehicle parameters data of the automobile periodically, but also actively monitors for any sudden vehicle future accident cause detection. Our research ensures the accident prevention strategies. The purpose of the research is to identify the negligence aspects which may leads to high risk of unforeseen scenarios this approach the risk of sudden accident, parts failure will reduce and lead to betterment of mankind.

title: Tracking Online Hate During the COVID-19 Pandemic in the Netherlands: A Hybrid Machine Learning and Keyword-Based Analysis of Dutch Social Media
authors: Mahboobeh Zangiabady, Alberto Garcia-Robledo, Wouter Lotgerink, Vincenzo Savarese
publish: 2025 International Conference on Electrical and Computer Engineering Researches (ICECER)
abstract: Hate speech on social media platforms like X has emerged as a growing concern, particularly during times of social stress such as the COVID-19 pandemic. While the Netherlands has seen a rise in online hostility, especially on Dutch-language platforms, studies focusing on hate speech targets and dynamics remain limited. This paper presents a large-scale analysis of Dutch social media during 2020—the first pandemic year—using a hybrid approach that combines sentence-based heuristics, supervised machine learning, and keyword-driven target identification. Our best-performing classifier, an SVM trained on a Dutch hate speech corpus with Bag-of-Words features, achieved an F1-score of 72.6% and identified 158,677 hate speech posts from a corpus of nearly four million tweets. Of these, 52,959 were further classified into specific target groups. The results show that political beliefs were the dominant target (56.2%), followed by religion, nationality, and ethnicity, with sexual orientation and gender accounting for much smaller shares. Compared to prior works our study has a more granular target labeling while achieving a good classification performance. These findings highlight the prevalence and patterns of hate speech in the Dutch Twittersphere and offer a replicable method for low-resource language settings.

title: Utilizing Drone for High-Voltage Transmission and Distribution Line Inspection and Regulatory Compliance Challenges
authors: Kim Thai Chai
publish: 2025 25th Conference of the Electric Power Supply Industry (CEPSI)
abstract: The adoption of drone technology in the power sector is transforming traditional inspection practices by improving operational efficiency, safety, and data accuracy. This paper investigates the deployment of multi-rotor drones equipped with high-resolution visible light and thermal imaging sensors for inspecting hotspots in high-voltage transmission and distribution overhead lines at Sarawak Energy Berhad (SEB).Traditionally, such inspections require manual climbing and the use of handheld devices, posing logistical challenges. In contrast, drone-based inspections enable remote data collection from multiple angles, reducing inspection time, enhancing defect visibility, and minimizing operational disruptions.The paper analyzes SEB’s drone inspection program, offering a comparative assessment against conventional methods in terms of time efficiency, manpower, and coverage. It also addresses key challenges in implementation, including regulatory compliance with national aviation guidelines, permit requirements, and operational limitations. Future integration with AI-driven anomaly detection as a strategic pathway to enhance asset monitoring and predictive maintenance capabilities.

title: Student Budget and Time Management System for PGs/Hostels/Societies
authors: Reema Rallan, Sarneet Kaur
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Internet of Things: Smart Innovation and Usages (IoT-SIU)
abstract: Students who stay in shared accommodation such as dorms, apartments or student societies have a tendency of not balancing their finances and their schedule thus causing a lot of stress and less efficiency. This study describes the development of a web-based system to provide convenient applications to track monthly bills and structure the daily processes. This system will be designed with the help of the Flask framework of the Python language, enabling students to monitor, compare, and visualize their spending and time management patterns, where the storage of data will be supported by SQLite/PostgreSQL. The backend is based on the Pandas and Scikit-Learn libraries to provide simple predictive analytics and useful information about the future expenses and time consumption. The frontend, which is created independently, integrates well using the RESTful APIs. We are aimed at assisting students in their better financial literacy and time management, which will eventually result in a more well-organized and less stressful academic setting. The system has been designed in order to be lightweight, scaled as well as adaptable to upgrades in the future such as mobile access and also advanced recommendation capabilities.

title: A Taxonomy of AI-Driven and IoT-Based Solutions for Smart Traffic Management
authors: K. Y. Ahmed, C. S. Said, N. A. Madzlan
publish: 2025 International Conference on Advances in Machine Intelligence, and Cybersecurity Technologies (AMICT)
abstract: Urban traffic congestion remains a major challenge or rapidly growing cities. This study emphasizes the significance of the Internet of Things (IoT) in facilitating realtime monitoring, control, and decision-making for urban traffic management. We systematically examined 150 studies (2014–2024) and, following the application of established inclusion and exclusion criteria, selected 105 for comprehensive analysis. We created a detailed taxonomy of IoT-based traffic management solutions, sorting them by application area, processing method, communication protocol, and data collection method. Five areas of solutions were found: Smart Traffic Signals, Vehicle-to-Vehicle (V2V) Communication, IoT Security, Smart Traffic Monitoring, and Intelligent Forecasting. We used a cross-validation method with an 80/20 split to check the reliability of the analysis among reviewers using Cohen’s kappa coefficient, and we found that the taxonomy was stable. The results show that flow optimization, safety, and predictive ability all have measurable benefits, but scalability, integration, cost, and security are still problems. We suggest standardization, AI-enabled architecture, and real-world pilots, and we give researchers, planners, and policymakers useful advice.

title: Classification and Detection of ASD using ML Models in Adults
authors: Neeru, Prashant Upadhyaya, Gaganjot Kaur
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent and Innovative Technologies in Computing, Electrical and Electronics (IITCEE)
abstract: Autism spectrum disorder, also termed a neuro developmental condition, originates during the early stages of brain development. A significant number of adults remain undiagnosed for years, relying on adaptive coping strategies to hide their core autistic traits. This makes the condition more challenging to detect for others, as well as for clinicians, resulting in misdiagnosis or underdiagnosis. Hence, traditional screening methods may not capture the more subtle symptoms of ASD in adults. Consequently, the use of AI-based diagnostic tools and machine learning models can analyze complex and high-dimensional data to detect hidden characteristics that are difficult for humans to detect. In this paper, the comparison and evaluation of four types of ML models are conducted for the detection of autism in adults. The models are logistic regression (LR), then K-Nearest Neighbor (K-NN), the third one is Support Vector Machine (SVM), and finally Naive Bayes (NB). The SVM linear kernel model demonstrated the overall best performance by achieving 98% accuracy on the adult dataset.

title: A Fabrication-Motivated Model for Improving Simulations of On-Wafer Transmission Lines
authors: Florian Bergmann, Nicholas R. Jungwirth, Nicholas Derimow, Bryan T. Bosworth, Meagan C. Papac, Eric J. Marksz, Tomasz Karpisz, Jerome Cheron, Vincenzo A. St. George, Moitreya Adhikary, Jooeun Lee, Angela Stelson, Ben F. Jamroz, Christian J. Long, Nathan D. Orloff
publish: IEEE Microwave and Wireless Technology Letters
abstract: Electromagnetic simulations are a ubiquitous tool for microwave engineers. We benchmark simulations by their agreement with experimental results. Transmission lines are an excellent test case because they have well-established measurement methodologies and are straightforward to simulate. However, when disagreements between simulation and experiment arise, finding the root cause can be quite difficult due to the number of design parameters. Here, we investigated a fabrication-motivated model that reduces this complexity by leveraging correlations between transmission line design parameters. We validated our model by comparing its prediction of cross-sectional geometry to scanning electron microscopy (SEM) measurements. Our procedure provides a simple path for microwave engineers to improve simulations on a broad range of on-wafer devices.

title: Lightweight WebAssembly-Based Intrusion Detection for Zero Trust Edge Networks
authors: Jonathan Weber, Ilir Murturi, Xhevahir Bajrami, Reza Farahani, Praveen Kumar Donta, Schahram Dustdar
abstract: IoT devices deployed across computing continuum infrastructures present significant security challenges due to resource constraints and decentralization. Traditional centralized intrusion detection systems struggle in such environments because of limited connectivity, high latency, and single points of failure. To address these challenges, this article extends a learning-driven Zero Trust framework tailored to resource-constrained edge environments, with an approach for evaluating lightweight intrusion detection models in Zero Trust edge environments. Our extended approach enables systematic evaluation of lightweight machine learning models for localized intrusion detection, comprising three layers: (i) compilation, (ii) execution, and (iii) measurement. The proposed approach is implemented using Rust and WebAssembly to ensure portable, efficient, and isolated execution across heterogeneous devices. Using this framework, seven representative intrusion detection models (i.e., Decision Tree (DT), Random Forest (RF), k-Nearest Neighbor (KNN), Logistic Regression (LR), Artificial Neural Network (ANN), and Convolutional Neural Network (CNN) variants) were implemented and evaluated on the UNSW-NB15 dataset. Results show that RF achieved the best trade-off between detection accuracy and efficiency, while simpler models (DT and LR) offered near-instant inference with minimal resource usage, making them ideal for highly constrained devices. In contrast, more complex models such as deep neural networks and KNN introduced significant overhead for only modest accuracy gains. These findings underscore the need to balance accuracy and resource efficiency for effective Zero Trust edge security.

title: Multi-Task Physics Informed Neural Network based Steering Control Strategy for Steer-by-Wire Systems
authors: Feng Wang, Xinhui Zeng, Chenghao Fang, Haobin Jiang, Yingfeng Cai
abstract: The Steer-by-Wire (SBW) system is recognized as a crucial technology for facilitating autonomous driving. However, system nonlinearities, parametric uncertainties, and external disturbances present significant challenges to steering control. This paper proposes a control strategy integrating the multi-task physics-informed neural network (MTPINN) with model predictive control (MPC), using the rack position as the tracking target. Firstly, the SBW model including the motor, synchronous belt, ball screw, and rack is established, with the rack force being treated as an extended state of the system to construct the extended disturbance observer (EDO). Then, by embedding both the extended disturbance observation equation and the prediction equation into the neural network, the MTPINN model that represents the transient behavior of the SBW system is constructed. This model is integrated with the MPC architecture to improve the performance of rack position tracking control. Finally, the bench test results indicate that the maximum observation error RMSE and the maximum tracking error RMSE are 0.348kN and 0.784mm, respectively, validating the effectiveness of the MTPINN-MPC steering control strategy.

title: Hybrid Digitized-counterdiabatic Quantum-classical Benders Decomposition for EV Charging Navigation in Coupled Transportation and Distribution Network
authors: Yanyu Zhang, Yanhao Li, Hengji Li, Feixiang Jiao, Xibeng Zhang, Yi Zhou, Abhisek Ukil
abstract: With the increasing integration of Transportation Network (TN) and Distribution Network (DN), the growing prevalence of electric vehicles (EVs) has posed significant challenges to both TN and DN operations, such as congestion at charging stations (CS) and overload in DN. To address these challenges, this paper proposes a bi-layer coupled model that integrates EV route navigation and CS power scheduling. The upper layer of the model optimizes the lowest-cost routes for multiple EVs to reach CS, while the lower layer optimizes the energy-efficient scheduling of CS to meet EV charging demands. For solving the model in the form of Mixed-Integer Linear Programming (MILP), quantum computing has demonstrated significant potential. Therefore, a hybrid quantum-classical algorithm combining the digitized-counterdiabatic Quantum Approximate Optimization Algorithm (DC-QAOA) with Benders Decomposition (DC-Q-BD) is developed. This algorithm leverages the parallelism of quantum computing and the rapid convergence of digitized-counterdiabatic evolution. It also employs collaborative processing between a classical CPU and a quantum processing unit (QPU) to handle continuous and discrete variables, respectively. Results from practical case studies demonstrate that the proposed algorithm significantly enhances solution efficiency and quality. It provides cost-effective charging routes for EVs and notably reduces the adverse impacts of EV charging on CS. This work provides a quantum-based approach for addressing the EV charging navigation problem in coupled TN and DN.

title: ES-SNN: e-Flash Memory-based Stochastic Spiking Neural Networks for Edge Inference
authors: Hyeyeon Jeon, Jungnam Kim, Yoon Kim, Minsuk Koo
abstract: In this work, we propose an e-Flash memory-based Stochastic Spiking Neural Network (ES-SNN), which integrates stochastic spiking neurons with e-Flash memory-based multi-level synapses for probabilistic computation. We design an energy-efficient, capacitor-less stochastic output neuron using a 28 nm CMOS process. The e-Flash-based non-volatile synapses achieve 3-bit weight storage per cell, enabling compact and energy-efficient inference. System level evaluation results demonstrate that the proposed ES-SNN achieves an energy efficiency of 608 Tera Operations per Second per Watt (TOPS/W) for a single-layer fully connected SNN, highlighting its potential for low-power neuromorphic computing applications.

title: Intelligent Servo Motor Diagnosis via Control-Sensing Consistency Contrast in Industrial Environments: Toward Noninvasive Monitoring
authors: Jinze Jiang, Yaguo Lei, Ke Feng, Bin Yang, Zidong Wang
abstract: Accurate diagnosis of incipient interturn short-circuit faults and effective prevention of rapid deterioration are regarded as critical for ensuring the reliability of servo motor drive systems, particularly in high-end equipment applications where operational precision is paramount. Mainstream diagnosis methods are typically based on multisource signal fusion to improve the accuracy of incipient fault detection. However, the noninvasive monitoring requirement in industrial settings is often overlooked, thereby increasing implementation complexity. To address this practical challenge, an intelligent diagnosis method, termed control-sensing consistency contrast learning (CS-CCL), is proposed. First, angular position pulses obtained from the motor controller are utilized, together with phase currents, to enable noninvasive diagnosis. Second, weak fault-induced features are extracted from the above monitoring signals based on temporal consistency, and their correlations are captured through control-sensing consistency to improve the efficiency of feature fusion. Finally, a model pretraining and fine-tuning strategy based on self-supervised contrastive learning is developed to accommodate various diagnostic tasks. The proposed CS-CCL was evaluated across variouscoupled shorted-turn ratios and rotating speeds, yielding accuracies of 94.57%, 99.27%, and 99.39%, respectively. This performance validates its capability for accurate incipient ISCF diagnosis under noninvasive monitoring, outperforming other methods via the effective leverage of temporal and control-sensing consistency.

title: Practical Assessment of Soil Heavy Metal Levels through Novel Mathematical Technique
authors: Judgi.T, Raman Verma, Sidhant Das, Viranshu Kumar, Prabhu, Priyadarshi Das
publish: 2025 International Conference on AI-Driven STEM Education and Learning Technologies (AISTEMEDU)
abstract: Detecting heavy metal pollution in the soil is important to the environment and sustainable farming. Proper measurement of the presence of heavy metals can be used to identify the causes of pollution and reduce the potential threats to the ecology. The classical soil heavy metal analysis techniques usually conduct the analysis based on direct concentration, which may be time-laborious, computationally demanding, and suffer error issues because the data patterns and inter-element correlations are complex. This paper suggests a Soil Assessment Framework (SAF) based on Principal Component Analysis (PCA) that is combined with K-Means Clustering in order to overcome these shortcomings. PCA optimizes the reduction of data dimensions without loss of variance, which points out the most critical aspects that cause heavy metal variability. K-Means clustering then categorizes the soil samples according to the similarity of heavy metal profiles and allows distinguishing the patterns of contamination and hotspots more accurately. The suggested SAF technique enables the effective and precise analysis of the presence of heavy metals in the soil, and hence a scalable tool of analysis of the environment and sound decision-making. The usage of SAF in testing datasets proved that the application could be effectively used to distinguish between a polluted and a clean zone. The results show that SAF does not only increase the accuracy of the analytics but also reduces the complex soil datasets to actionable insights to enable focused remediation procedures and sustainable land management. The SAF-PCA model examined 4 soil samples, and the results were 85, 78, 90, and 80, which succeeding in differentiating the degree of contamination and establishing the major hotspots of the heavy metals. Keywords-Soil contamination, Heavy metals, Principal Component Analysis, K-Means Clustering, Environmental monitoring.

title: AI-Powered Biomarker Discovery for Disease Diagnosis and Prognosis
authors: Juginder Pal Singh, Sujata Negi Thakur, Pratik Angaitkar, Ashutosh Rastogi, Animesh Kumar Sharma
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Artificial Intelligence for Innovations in Healthcare Industries (ICAIIHI)
abstract: Artificial intelligence (AI) is now a revolutionary force behind biomarker discovery, disease diagnosis, and personalized medicine. The latest advances are powered by AI-enabled tools like machine learning, deep learning, and multi-omics integration to accelerate the discovery of predictive and prognostic biomarkers in different diseases including cancer, cardiovascular diseases, and neurodegenerative diseases. AI makes possible the examination of genomic, proteomic, and clinical large-scale data to facilitate the design of personalized therapy, prediction of diseases early on, and the optimization of therapeutic intervention. Analytical platforms such as deep neural networks, graph-based learning, and AI-driven sensors enhance the sensitivity and specificity of detection of biomarkers, while AI-driven predictive modeling makes prognosis evaluation and drug repurposing possible. Multidisciplinary approaches combining clinical mass spectrometry, bioinformatics, and big data analytics improve patient stratification and diagnostic accuracy even further. Especially, AI application in oncology, autoimmune diseases, and Alzheimer's disease showcases its potential in the future to revolutionize patient care with rapid, reliable, and personalized insights. Notwithstanding the challenges of model explainability and data heterogeneity, AI-driven biomarker identification is an important step towards precision medicine, laying a bridge between computational analysis and medical practice. Subsequent research would seek to integrate AI frameworks into routine diagnostics with the aim of facilitating real-time, scalable, and ethically responsible healthcare solutions.

title: A Human-Centered Approach to Interpretable Machine Learning in Clinical Decision Support Systems
authors: Vijay Nimbalkar, Latika Chawla, Myasar Mundher Adnan, Ashok Bhansali, Manish Gupta, Ravi Kalra
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Artificial Intelligence for Innovations in Healthcare Industries (ICAIIHI)
abstract: Interpretable machine learning (ML) paired with clinical decision support systems (CDSS) is revolutionizing healthcare in the context of enhancing transparency, personalization, and trust. This survey of 25 recent articles demonstrates that explainable artificial intelligence (XAI) is essential to enhance clinician adoption and patient outcomes in different clinical settings, such as oncology, emergency medicine, and mental health. Scholars note that to make AI human-centric, promote cooperation between clinicians and algorithms, and provide the transparency of data, researchers need to design AI in a more human-centric manner, which will help to align the use of ML tools with ethical, fair, and realistic principles. Although these have been made, there are still difficulties in the field, including the need to balance model accuracy and interpretability, deal with biases, and incorporate them into current clinical practice. New approaches are being designed, such as the use of electronic health records, engaging end-users in the development of designs, and the use of new approaches of explanations, such as data-centric and proximity-informed explanations.

title: Heartguard Ai: Ai Next Gen Adaptive Cardiac Risk Chatbot
authors: S Saranya, Mownisa V, Shrinithi C S, Surya P
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent and Innovative Technologies in Computing, Electrical and Electronics (IITCEE)
abstract: Cardio Vascular Diseases (CVDs) remain the leading cause of mortality across age groups. This paper presents HeartGuard AI, a next-generation adaptive cardiac risk chatbot integrating machine learning and deep learning to forecast and prevent heart-related diseases. The prevalence of serious cardiac events can be prevented greatly by early identification of hereditary and lifestyle-associated risk factors. The paper presents HeartGuard AI, a state-of-the-art adaptive cardiac risk chatbot, which is capable of forecasting and preventing heart- related diseases with the help of the machine learning algorithms, including K-Nearest Neighbor (KNN), logistic regression, and deep learning models. The system combines the medical records provided by the user and lifestyle data and family history to provide age-specific risk profiling and prevention measures. As opposed to the traditional fixed-point screening systems, HeartGuard AI develops together with the user in the various stages of life allowing continuous monitoring and customized recommendations. HeartGuard AI offers an interactive, accessible, and scalable solution to the development of cardiovascular health management by integrating adaptive questioning, real-time data analysis, and easy-to-use interfaces. The experimental evaluation achieved 93.2% accuracy for clinical risk classification and 95.8 % accuracy for ECG-based arrhythmia detection, demonstrating adaptability and robustness across datasets. The proposed architecture aligns directly with the objectives stated in the title by providing an adaptive AI-driven cardiac risk chatbot capable of real-time prevention and monitoring.

title: Disease Prediction Using Machine Learning
authors: Mayank Srivastava, Harshit Pandey, Pratibha Dixit
publish: 2025 World Conference on Cutting-Edge Science and Technology (WCCEST)
abstract: Chronic diseases such as heart disease and diabetes are increasingly prevalent, making early detection crucial for better management. The growing availability of healthcare data has facilitated the adoption of new machine learning based models for disease detection and prediction. This research develops a machine learning framework that utilizes algorithms like Random Forest, Support Vector Machine and Decision Tree for predicting heart disease and type-2 diabetes. By leveraging well-known datasets of Pima Indians Diabetes dataset and the Cleveland Heart Disease dataset, the proposed feature selection techniques enhances the accuracy to great extent. The results indicate that Random Forest algorithm achieves the highest predictive performance. This research aims to support healthcare professionals in making faster and more reliable diagnoses, which ultimately enhances patient care.

title: Introducing Energy Justice in Electrical Engineering Curricula: A Teaching Pedagogy Approach
authors: Salman Mohagheghi
publish: 2025 IEEE 14th International Conference on Engineering Education (ICEED)
abstract: Engineering designs frequently entail significant social and environmental implications. Hence, there is a growing need for a sociotechnical approach in engineering education—one that incorporates the examination of non-technical dimensions alongside technical content. Most common pedagogical strategies such as active learning, problem-based, and project-based exercises are designed to be closely aligned with the course's technical content. Alternatively, online discussions can offer a venue for open, unstructured exchange of ideas related to the non-technical aspects of engineering courses. This paper presents a case study from a graduate-level course on power and energy systems, in which students participated in online Canvas discussions related to energy justice and were asked to discuss its associated challenges, burdens, and opportunities. The discussion activity spanned a four-week period: students were given two weeks to submit an initial post addressing the prompt, followed by another two weeks to respond to their peers' contributions. The informal and asynchronous nature of the discussions encouraged participation. Notably, student contributions revealed thematic insights that had not appeared during in-class lectures. Evaluation of the activity suggests that increased instructor involvement and the application of a more detailed grading rubric could further enhance student engagement and learning outcomes.

title: An Enhanced Approach to Predict Heart Disease for Genx using Machine Learning Algorithms
authors: Pramod Kumar P, R Akshay, Srinivas Komakula, K. Sagar, Srinivas Madhira, N Vijay Kumar
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Artificial Intelligence for Innovations in Healthcare Industries (ICAIIHI)
abstract: Heart disease remains among the leading causes in which individuals die across the globe, especially the middle aged. It is not easy to predict it in the early days since there is a great number of various factors, which determine it, including the state of the clinical conditions, lifestyle behaviors, and processes, a lot of which are related to the working of the body. To aid this task this study proposes a new method of utilizing machine learning that is especially aimed at individuals belonging to the Gen X generation i.e., people between the age group of 40 to 60 years as they are the people that are more likely to experience an issue connected to the heart.The study relied on the UCI Heart Disease data and picked 216 records of individuals within this age category.The analysis of these records was done using 14 significant features of health care. To refine the data to facilitate the quality of the models made, the data were well prepared by addressing any irregular values, normalising the data on a normal scale and converting any non-numberical data to an appropriate form before the training of the models.
