There were four machine learning techniques that were evaluated, these include Logistic Regression, K-Nearest Neighbors (KNN), Decision Tree, and finally, Randome Forest.The study resulted that the KNN method performed best with the testing accuracy rating of 95 which is superior to the other methods.The research contributes to an existing gap in studies because this study targets a certain group of people of certain age as opposed to the general population. It also provides a comparison of the effectiveness of some of the commonly used machine learning methods to predict heart disease.

title: Machine Learning-Based COD Prediction Enhancement in Microalgae Cultivation with POME Using Imputation Techniques
authors: Irfan Mirda, Riyanarto Sarno, Atika Afriani, Kelly Rossa Sungkono, Abdullah Faqih Septiyanto, Taufiq Choirul Amri, Sang-Seok Lee
publish: 2025 2nd Beyond Technology Summit on Informatics International Conference (BTS-I2C)
abstract: Accurate COD prediction in microalgae-based Palm Oil Mill Effluent (POME) bioremediation systems is essential for effective process optimization and environmental protection. A critical challenge in real-time monitoring is the limitation of IoT sensors in directly measuring COD values, resulting in data availability restricted to periodic laboratory analysis which creates significant delays in process intervention. This study developed an IoT-based monitoring system integrated with imputation methods and machine learning algorithms to predict COD concentration during microalgae cultivation in POME media. To assess the impact of missing data handling on predictive performance, this research evaluated four machine learning algorithms on datasets prepared using four imputation strategies. Results demonstrated that MICE imputation combined with Random Forest achieved the best performance on per-minute data with R<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup> of 0.99, RMSE of 2.97, and MAE of 0.86, significantly outperforming hourly aggregation which achieved R<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup> of 0.95, RMSE of 32.66, and MAE of 12.22. Proper missing data handling at finer temporal granularity substantially enhanced predictive accuracy, enabling reliable real-time COD estimation without laboratory delays. The findings underscore the efficacy of integrating imputation methods with machine learning approaches to link real-time sensor data and laboratory-confirmed COD measurements in POME treatment facilities.

title: Thermal Models for Transformer Overload Management: Implementation Challenges and Solutions
authors: Alexander Alber, Luzia Pfeilschifter, Andreas Dreger, Andreas Kurz, Karsten Viereck
publish: 2025 25th Conference of the Electric Power Supply Industry (CEPSI)
abstract: Thermal models for transformers occupy a curious position in power engineering: extensively standardized yet persistently underutilized. This paper bridges the gap between theoretical elegance and operational pragmatism by documenting the implementation of the IEEE and IEC loading guides.We describe four functions: maximum load calculation for specified durations, maximum duration calculation for given loads, temperature forecasting based on ambient and load scenarios, and an Application Programming Interface (API) for control center integration. These functions enable operators to safely utilize transformer thermal capacity.Our implementation confronts several engineering challenges largely overlooked in academic literature: managing transitions between cooling stages, handling load asymmetry through parallel thermal models, tracking potential hot-spots across multiple windings, adapting to parameter uncertainty through self-learning algorithms, and balancing computational accuracy against real-time constraints. For each challenge, we present practical solutions validated through field implementation.The persistent disconnect between established thermal models and operational deployment suggests limitations not in the models themselves, but in how engineering knowledge transfers from theoretical formulation to practical implementation. By documenting these implementation experiences, we contribute to closing this gap and enabling more effective utilization of transformer assets.

title: Development of an Automatic Paddy Winnower Machine Powered by Solar Energy
authors: Hendriko Hendriko, Akhirul Akbarsyah, Mulyadi Mulyadi, Tianur, Nur Khamdi
publish: 2025 2nd Beyond Technology Summit on Informatics International Conference (BTS-I2C)
abstract: Rice is the leading agricultural commodity and staple food in Indonesia. The increasing demand for rice requires post-harvest processes that ensure high-quality grains, particularly cleanliness. Traditional methods for cleaning rice husks are time-consuming and labor-intensive. This research presents the development of a rice husk cleaning machine powered by photovoltaic (PV) solar energy, equipped with a proximity sensor for automated operation. Several tests have been conducted to examine the performance of the winnower machine and the solar system. The battery charging speed is 2.9 V/hour, and the voltage consumption of the machine is 0.35 V/hour. The power consumption is slightly higher compared to the charging rate. The charging process should be longer than the machine's operation time. The variable of a 10 mm gap of the exit hole and speed 3 of the fan was obtained as the fastest cleaning time, which is 20.27 minutes. Meanwhile, the highest cleaning effectiveness was obtained from the test using a 5 mm gap and a speed of 3, which is 96.4%. Optimum variable based on the FAO standard, 5 mm gap, and speed 3. Using these variables, the machine capacity is about 197kg/hour. The test also proved that the machine was operating automatically.

title: Computational simulation of diaphragmatic lymphatics’ intrinsic activity
authors: Alessandro Conte, Eleonora Solari, Cristiana Marcozzi, Giancarlo Storti Gajani, Daniela Negrini, Andrea Moriondo, Federica Boschetti
publish: 2025 International Conference on Electrical and Computer Engineering Researches (ICECER)
abstract: Spontaneous contractile activity has been observed in FITC–dextran–filled lymphatic vessels located along the peripheral margin of the pleural diaphragm in anesthetized adult Wistar rats. Based on their contractile behavior, individual lymphatic segments were grouped into four functional categories: active spontaneously contracting, stretch-responsive, passive, and non-contractile. Unlike central collectors, the most peripheral loops of the diaphragmatic lymphatic network, located in the muscle adjacent to the abdominal wall, depend on an intrinsic mechanism to drive lymph propulsion. Using ex vivo video microscopy, fluorescent particle motion was tracked along one such spontaneously contracting vessel, and a representative active segment was modeled in COMSOL Multiphysics. From the experimentally derived changes in vessel diameter, parameters such as transported volume and inlet–outlet flow rates were quantitatively estimated.

title: A target-based radial model in data envelopment analysis for measuring efficiency
authors: Chiang Kao
publish: 2025 International Conference on Electrical and Computer Engineering Researches (ICECER)
abstract: Data envelopment analysis is a technique that has been applied in the field of electrical engineering and related areas to measure the efficiency of production units such as power plants. A problem with the conventional radial model of this technique is the ambiguous efficiency caused by the slacks between the target and the projection point of the unit being assessed on the production frontier. This paper proposes a target-based model to take the slacks into account. When there is no slack, the target-based efficiency boils down to the conventional radial efficiency. A case of Taiwanese power plants shows that the conventional radial efficiency is largely affected by slacks, and the rankings based on which are unreliable. The target-based efficiency, in contrast, are unambiguous, and the resulted rankings are reliable, which concludes that the target-based efficiency is an appropriate measure of performance.

title: DiffSBOM: Capturing Semantic Changes in Software Upgrades for Supply Chain Transparency
authors: Runbin Yuan, Haoxiang Yang, Yuchen Zhang
publish: 2026 IEEE 16th Annual Computing and Communication Workshop and Conference (CCWC)
abstract: Software Bills of Materials (SBOMs) are critical for tracking dependencies in modern software systems, but they typically lack semantic detail about how components evolve across versions. This limits their usefulness for understanding upgrade impacts, assessing regressions, and supporting secure supply chain practices. We introduce DiffSBOM, a lightweight and extensible framework that enriches standard SBOMs with function-level semantic differences between versions. DiffSBOM leverages existing tools to extract and embed meaningful change information into SBOMs using a structured “upgrade” field. The framework operates without source code instrumentation or custom configuration, making it suitable for developers, auditors, and security teams. We evaluate DiffSBOM on 20 open-source projects written in C, Rust, Golang, and Python, demonstrating its scalability, usability, and minimal performance overhead. DiffSBOM produces compact, backward-compatible SBOMs that enhance transparency and reduce upgrade risks. As part of future work, we aim to integrate DiffSBOM's fine-grained upgrade insights with platforms such as GitHub Dependabot, enabling more informative update notifications and proactive vulnerability management.

title: Evaluating Simulated Annealing Against Ant Colony Optimization: A Case Study of Route Optimization
authors: Amalia Utamima, Dara Nasywa Akmal, Hasna Dhiya Nafitra, Moh Hasyim Baidlowi
publish: 2025 2nd Beyond Technology Summit on Informatics International Conference (BTS-I2C)
abstract: This paper explores the Capacitated Vehicle Routing Problem (CVRP) by applying Simulated Annealing (SA) and Ant Colony Optimization (ACO) algorithms to optimize delivery routes for ABC Company, a ready-to-drink beverages distributor in Indonesia. The study addresses ABC's need to improve distribution efficiency, moving away from the current suboptimal routing practices. Focusing on a delivery network of 142 stores and a fleet of five trucks, the research compares the performance of SA and ACO. The results reveal that SA reduces the total travel distance by approximately 19% compared to the company’s current route, outperforming ACO. These findings suggest significant potential for cost savings and enhanced operational efficiency in ABC's logistics processes.

title: Author response for "Descriptor: Digital Dataset of Animal Tissues for Macroscopic Pathology in Cancer: Image and Ultrasound Data (DATP-IU)"
authors: Fabrizia Auletta, Marton C. Mezei, Ilaria Benedetti, Giacomo Rossi, Alessandra Gavazza, Calogero M. Oddo

title: Cloud Architecture for Optimizing Digital Marketing Strategies in Small Businesses Using Sentiment Analysis
authors: Uman Ahmed Mohammed
publish: 2025 International Conference on AI-Driven STEM Education and Learning Technologies (AISTEMEDU)
abstract: Digital marketing has changed in an unmatched speed, thus offering even small and medium-sized enterprises (SMEs) with new opportunities to perfect their marketing strategies, enhance customer interaction, and trigger the overall growth. However, most SMEs are still at the mercy of inadequate infrastructure and deficiency of resources, and it is hard to take full advantage of these new digital tools. The paper presents a cloud-based architectural system that can streamline the digital marketing environment by incorporating the sentiment analysis. This is due to the fact that by utilizing the scalability and flexibility of cloud computing, SMEs will be able to access some of the most advanced marketing tools that would otherwise have been beyond their means. The architecture also integrates sentiment analysis, which is a machine-learning technology, which provides feedback received by the customer in various forms, such as, social media, online reviews, and blogs, in order to learn more about what customers prefer and what they do not. This kind of analysis can be used in the creation of personalized marketing campaigns, predicting market trends, and responsive reaction to the changing needs of customers. The article goes on to analyze the benefits of using cloud infrastructure including cost efficiency, real-time processing features, and large data storage. It also describes the obstacles that SMEs have faced in the implementation of these technologies. The effectiveness of the proposed cloud architecture is assessed with the help of a case study, therefore, proving its potential to promote the development of digital marketing strategies and promote business growth in a competitive environment of the market.

title: Realization of Maintenance-free OH lines with highly corrosion resistant specifications of coating and plating
authors: Yuma Hayashi, Kohei Ishikawa, Yuta Yamamoto, Yohei Tomonou
publish: 2025 25th Conference of the Electric Power Supply Industry (CEPSI)
abstract: Our company owns 30,000 OH transmission towers for transmission facilities mainly for 77kV and above, some of which were constructed over 100 years ago and are still in operation.Many of the OH transmission facilities were constructed during Japan's period of Rapid economic growth (1950s-1970s), and as they age, the costs of maintenance, such as replacing conductors and insulators and painting, are increasing.Japan is an island nation surrounded by the sea, and our company also has areas with severe corrosion by sea salt particles, including coastal areas. While it is necessary to carry out maintenance through painting and replacements, due to population decline and severe working conditions, there is a shortage of workers and high maintenance costs, making it difficult to carry out proper maintenance. Furthermore, as the number of facilities requiring maintenance increases in the future, it is socially required to continuously reduce maintenance costs to keep transmission charges low.As a strategy for O&M of OH transmission facilities from a long-term perspective, we have formulated a strategy to reduce O&M costs by adopting high-corrosion-resistant specifications for OH facilities to be updated, which will reduce long-term running costs despite the increase in initial costs, and to address issues such as worker shortages.We investigated the latest technology for high-corrosion-resistant specifications for all overhead transmission equipment, including towers, conductors, ground wires, insulators, fittings, accessories, and dampers, and evaluated their long-term reliability. We assumed the maintenance expansion period for various combinations of high-corrosion-resistant specifications and calculated the cost-effectiveness based on the difference in maintenance periods and frequencies compared to normal specifications.Regarding high-corrosion-resistant specifications, we verified the application of several methods. One example includes thick galvanizing, which increases the thickness of the usual hot-dip galvanizing used for steel products, including tower materials. We also examined hot-dip zinc-aluminum alloy plating, which enhances corrosion resistance against salt damage by mixing aluminum with the usual zinc plating. Additionally, we tested baked coating bolts that generate a strong coating film by spraying and heating the coating after plating.We identified specifications effective for areas with high degrees of corrosion due to sea salt particles and those effective for mountainous areas without the scattering of sea salt particles and calculated their annual costs.By introducing these specifications, we confirmed that we could reduce annual costs by up to 15% overall, regardless of whether the area is mountainous or coastal. Based on this strategy, we plan to standardize these specifications and fully introduce them into new and rebuilt towers and their equipment from FY2025, and we will continue to work on reducing maintenance costs in the future.This paper introduces the concepts of each highly corrosion resistant specification and service life, as well as the methods for calculating annual costs.

title: Comparative Analysis of Psychometric data for assessing mental health testing needs using Machine Learning Models
authors: Sahil R Jain, Aadit Rajendra Chaurasia, Mosam Biswas, Digamabar Puri, Varad Kotalwar, Gunjan Patil
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Artificial Intelligence for Innovations in Healthcare Industries (ICAIIHI)
abstract: The prevalence of psychological conditions such as depression, anxiety, and stress continues to increase globally, yet obtaining a timely diagnosis for many individuals remains a challenge due to limited and cumbersome screening resources. Although psychometric questionnaires are standard in mental health evaluations, determining who actually needs comprehensive testing is not straightforward. This work presents a comparative assessment of several supervised machine learning algorithms—Logistic Regression, Random Forest, support vector machine, XGBoost, and LightGBM—for predicting the necessity of further mental health evaluation based on psychometric survey responses. The analysis relies on data from established self-report tools like the PHQ-9 and GAD-7. Using systematic methodologies and robust model assessment protocols, this study aims to inform the development of smarter mental health triage systems, integrating machine learning with existing mental health workflows to optimize efficiency and maintain diagnostic reliability.

title: Intelligent Agricultural Advisory System: Chatbot Support, Disease Diagnosis, and Crop Recommendations
authors: Mahesh B. L., Rashmi Adyapady R., Kamath Abhishek, Akshatha, Sushmitha Shenoy
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent and Innovative Technologies in Computing, Electrical and Electronics (IITCEE)
abstract: The Agricultural Advisory System evolves the art of farming using real-time data related to soil health, market dynamics, and environmental conditions to create localized recommendations based on machine-learning inputs. This leads to increased agricultural productivity, efficiency, and sustainability. It provides reliable information at a regional level by relying on scientific techniques like neural networks and random forests for crop recommendation. Early agricultural disease identification is made possible by advanced image processing, which reduces crop losses, enables proactive disease management, and lessens the need for chemicals. Furthermore, the module offers personalized fertilizer recommendations by assessing soil health to create customized nutrient mixtures that promote sustainable soil management and increase long-term productivity. Additionally, the system provides round-the-clock assistance through an interactive chatbot. As a result, the support will help the farmer make decisions by providing them with real-time information, professional counsel, and updates on market trends.

title: Characterization of toroidal cores made from magnetic composites and epoxy resin
authors: Jesús Pascal González García, Mario Alberto Juarez Balderas, Javier Gustavo Cabal Velarde, Azdrubal Lobo Guerrero, María Fernanda Ramirez Ayala
publish: 2025 International Conference on Electrical and Computer Engineering Researches (ICECER)
abstract: This paper presents the fabrication and characterization of toroidal magnetic cores based on magnetite, magnesium ferrite, and DGEBA epoxy resin for high-frequency applications. The crystalline phases of the materials were confirmed through X-ray diffraction, while Fourier Transform Infrared Spectroscopy revealed interactions between the polymer matrix and magnetic fillers. The inductance and relative permeability of the manufactured cores were experimentally determined, showing that pure magnetite provides slightly higher initial permeability compared to the magnetite–magnesium ferrite composite. However, hysteresis loop measurements demonstrated that the doped cores exhibit reduced magnetic losses across a frequency range of 100 kHz to 500 kHz. These results indicate that the incorporation of magnesium ferrite contributes to improving stability and lowering hysteresis losses, despite a minor decrease in permeability. Therefore, the combination of epoxy resin with magnetite and magnesium ferrite enables the development of distributed air-gap composites with favorable magnetic behavior, which are suitable for efficient and compact magnetic components in modern high-frequency power converters.

title: 5G-based Cooling Load Prediction Information System of Air Conditioning for Decarbonisation in Shopping Mall (Paper ID 54)
authors: Anthony Lo, Kwok-On Wong, Simon Tsui, Paul Sat
publish: 2025 25th Conference of the Electric Power Supply Industry (CEPSI)
abstract: Buildings account for about 90% of Hong Kong’s total electricity consumption. To achieve decarbonisation, all commercial buildings in Hong Kong are required to reduce the electricity consumption by 30% to 40% on or before 2050. Hence, building owners are needed to set aggressive and proactive energy saving targets in promoting energy efficiency and conservation (EE&C), particularly for air conditioning which is the largest energy consumption in a building. Innovation and new technology are required.Cooling load prediction can provide critical information for both designing and improving the energy efficiency of an air conditioning system. It is influenced by the building’s geometry, weather conditions and the internal heat gains associated with the flow and activity level of occupants. The project team has invented an innovative 5G-based Cooling Load Prediction Information System of the central air conditioning for commercial building using Multiple Linear Regression and Autoregressive model. Raw data of hourly historical/current/forecast variables including occupant density derived from 5G webcams; and weather data such as dry bulb temperature, relative humidity, cloud coverage and sea water temperature obtained from Hong Kong Observatory website are used. This project is developed in the collaboration with a property management company and pilot installation in their managed flagship shopping mall. The projected energy saving is over 4% of waterside and 13% of airside.5G webcams count the occupant flow to predict the population density and hence the CO<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</inf> concentration for PAU control, which is innovative, more cost effective and reliable than using CO<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</inf> sensors. Since all data used are obtained from pre-installed equipment or public domain, the operation and maintenance costs of the system and the marginal cost of extension to other buildings are minimal. This 5G-based Cooling Load Prediction Information System has successfully supported the shopping mall to achieve energy saving and decarbonisation. It can also be applied in other commercial buildings where it works with control systems via BACnet control and therefore it could be easily replicated to various building services systems in commercial buildings.

title: Mitigating Perceptual Drift in ROS2 LiDAR-Based SLAM During Wall-Parallel Motion
authors: Rekha P, Smitha Gayathri D, Yasha Jyothi M Shirur, Maaz Hussain, Bindu S, Subodh Kumar Panda
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent and Innovative Technologies in Computing, Electrical and Electronics (IITCEE)
abstract: Autonomous mobile robots that rely on 2D LiDAR-based SLAM frequently exhibit instability when navigating long planar corridors or moving parallel to walls. This phenomenon, commonly observed in ROS2-based TurtleBot platforms, leads to erroneous localization updates, lateral drift, and random heading shifts, collectively termed perceptual drift or hallucination. This paper investigates the underlying causes of such errors in the AMCL and Cartographer frameworks, identifying key contributors including geometric degeneracy, scan-matching ambiguity, and improper covariance synchronization between odometry and LiDAR models. A reproducible simulation benchmark was developed in Gazebo using TurtleBot3 and Nav2 to systematically evaluate these behaviors. Experimental results demonstrate that fusing IMU and wheel-odometry data through robot_localization, combined with adaptive AMCL parameter tuning and LiDAR scan pre-filtering, significantly mitigates drift while maintaining computational efficiency. Quantitative metrics show reductions in localization RMSE by up to 37% and recovery-rate improvements exceeding 40% compared to default configurations. The findings provide actionable insights for enhancing SLAM robustness in constrained planar environments.

title: An Integrated Smart Appointment Management System for the Healthcare Infrastructure in Kuwait
authors: Nasser Mezher, Seyed Esmaeili, Marina Guindi
publish: 2025 International Conference on Electrical and Computer Engineering Researches (ICECER)
abstract: This paper addresses the inefficiencies in the appointment scheduling system within governmental medical clinics in Kuwait. Currently, the process is manual, time-consuming, and perceived as unfair, often requiring patients to physically visit registration offices early in the morning. To address these challenges, a smart healthcare appointment system is developed to provide a more efficient and transparent solution. The proposed system comprising three integrated components: a web application for doctors’ schedules, a desktop application for civil ID verification via barcode scanning, and a mobile application for patients to book appointments. The system ensures fair access to appointments, improves notification and validation through SMS and email, and integrates all components through a secure enterprise database with web services. Additional features include doctor attendance verification via fingerprint and automatic notifications to patients. The goal is to create a user-friendly, efficient, and transparent digital solution that improves healthcare accessibility and can be adopted widely across similar public health infrastructures.

title: Application of PDC Test on Distribution Cable Health Condition Monitoring in Singapore
authors: Alvin Koh Kai Kiat, Changyou Suo, Wen Kwang Chern, Jerome Choong Zhi Ming, Kevin Tan Wei Ming, Bobby Goh Jin Heng
publish: 2025 25th Conference of the Electric Power Supply Industry (CEPSI)
abstract: Cross-linked polyethylene (XLPE) cables are widely used in urban power systems, necessitating reliable insulation health diagnostics to avert failures in service. The polarization and depolarization current (PDC) method offer insights into insulation health and is valued for its convenience in field testing using portable equipment. Analysis of PDC test results yields polarization and conduction parameters such as dielectric loss tangent (tan δ), dielectric absorption ratio (DAR), aging factor, and insulation resistance (IR), which collectively assist in evaluating the XLPE insulation health state. However, the diagnostic efficacy of parameters like aging factor and tan δ derived from cable on-site tests has not been extensively studied and validated. This study employs the portable Megger S1-1568 to perform on-site PDC tests on 6.6 kV and 22 kV copper-conductor XLPE cables of various ages from SP Group, using a 5 kV test voltage and 70-second test durations. Key diagnostic parameters - including dielectric loss tangent (tan δ), insulation resistance (IR), and aging factor - are extracted. Results show that tan δ calculated via the Curie formula (at 0.1 Hz) aligns well with Very Low Frequency (VLF) tan δ measurements and, when combined with IR criteria, serves as a reliable insulation health indicator. In contrast, the aging factor shows weak correlation with cable condition, limiting its diagnostic value. These findings enhance the accuracy of XLPE cable insulation health diagnosis and offer practical insights for refining PDC-based assessment methodologies.

title: Automated Breast Cancer Detection Systems: A Systematic Review of Machine Learning and Deep Learning Techniques (2015–2025)
authors: Jyoti, Balwinder Kaur Dhaliwal
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Artificial Intelligence for Innovations in Healthcare Industries (ICAIIHI)
abstract: The challenge of breast cancer detection continues to be one of the more difficult tasks in medicine requiring highly accurate, automated diagnostic systems to better augment early breast cancer detection and improve patient outcomes. This systematic review examines 33 peer-reviewed studies published between 2015 and 2025 that employed machine learning and deep learning techniques on breast cancer detection. The studies discussed in this review provided detection accuracies between 65 – 99.31% with convolutional neural network (CNN) based methodologies consistently providing better detection accuracies than traditional machine learning approaches. The most frequently employed benchmark was the Mammographic Image Analysis Society (MIAS) dataset. The methodology confirmed with greatest accuracy was the VGG16 based CNN with the detected accuracy of 99.31%. Even with the progress, considerable methodological heterogeneity is still present with respect to dataset selection, preprocessing methodologies and validation protocols which limits reproducibility and comparison. The clinical transition of these AI systems is still limited due to real world limitations with workflow integration and limited interpretability of the various performance measures used for detection performance for the method. Important problems are inconsistent use of evaluative frameworks, limited validation of multi-institutional data and limited prospective clinical trials. Future directions emphasis the use of standardized benchmarks, the use of diverse population datasets, hybrid therapy models utilizing the performance of deep learning with the interpretive features of conventional techniques and AI techniques. The solution of these problems is critical to the growth of clinically deployable AI driven breast cancer detection systems with the possibility of making substantial changes in the detection accuracy and delivery of health care in the actual working environment.

title: Powering the Philippine Energy Transition Program Through Distribution Utilities’ Energy Sourcing
authors: Wesley M. Navarro, Aldrin Jay R. Cervas
publish: 2025 25th Conference of the Electric Power Supply Industry (CEPSI)
abstract: The Philippines has set its latest Philippine Energy Transition Program through the 2023-2050 Philippine Energy Plan (PEP) which has its focus on Renewable Energy (RE) sources. Targets of 35% RE share in the generation mix by 2030 and 50% RE share by 2040 were identified in the PEP by the Department of Energy (DOE). As the Philippines did not commit itself to net-zero emission goals since it also considered energy security and reliability of supply, the PEP also recognized the need for transition fuel such as natural gas and other emerging technologies like nuclear and hydrogen. To achieve the targets identified in the PEP, the government formulates policies and requirements that eventually affect the way distribution utilities source their energy requirement for its captive customers. The paper aims to identify key government energy programs and the challenges and opportunities these present to the distribution utilities as well as how the distribution utilities will navigate through the changes brought by these programs to ensure compliance and support to the Philippine Energy Transition Goals while upholding their mandate in providing reliable and least cost of available electricity to its consumers.

title: An Exploratory Gap Analysis of Faculty Expectations and Student Self-Assessed Competencies Across Six Core Engineering Domains: A Philippine Case Study
authors: Marites E. Olesco, John Raymond B. Barajas, Reynato A. Gamboa
publish: 2025 IEEE 14th International Conference on Engineering Education (ICEED)
abstract: Amid ongoing efforts to align engineering education with industry demands and graduate outcomes, understanding the disconnect between faculty expectations and student preparedness remains a critical area of inquiry. This study explored such an alignment by examining perceptual gaps between engineering faculty and first-year students across six competency domains: Technical and Creative Problem Solving, Collaboration and Group Dynamics, Ethics and Personal Responsibility, Communication Proficiency, Lifelong Learning and Self-Management, and Logical and Analytical Thinking. A cross-sectional survey was administered to <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{1 0 1}$</tex> faculty members from various higher education institutions in the Philippines and 434 freshmen from a state university in the country. Participants responded to a 45-item instrument using a five-point Likert scale, with faculty indicating the perceived level of need for each competency, and students rating their proficiency. Results revealed statistically significant differences across all six domains, with faculty consistently assigning higher importance to competencies than students' self-rating. Notably large effect sizes were observed in Logical and Analytical Thinking, Technical and Creative Problem Solving, and Communication Proficiency. Item-level findings identified the largest gaps in operational safety, ethical conduct, and interpersonal collaboration-skills deemed essential to engineering practice. These findings further underscore the urgent need to embed scaffolded experiential learning, ethical reasoning, and the development of soft skills within the early stages of engineering curricula. By illuminating specific areas where student self-assessed competencies fall short of faculty expectations, this study provides a data-driven basis for curricular reform. Future research should prioritize longitudinal program-level studies tracking student competency development throughout the academic trajectory.

title: Interpretable and Interactive Deep Survival Analysis with Time-dependent EXtreme Gradient Integration
authors: Xinyu Qin, Ruiheng Yu, Armin Khayati, Zixiao Qiu, Gengyi Zou, Yan Li, Lu Wang
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Data Mining (ICDM)
abstract: Accurate prediction of time-to-event outcomes, commonly known as survival analysis, is vital in high-stakes domains such as healthcare and business, where timely and trustworthy insights can have profound implications. Traditional survival analysis methods either provide predictions without clear explanations or offer interpretability without a mechanism to integrate expert insights. In many real-world scenarios, decision-makers require models that are both transparent and capable of interactively incorporating domain knowledge to refine predictions. The key challenge we address is how to simultaneously achieve accurate time-to-event forecasting, clear interpretability, and interactive integration of expert feedback. We propose the Interpretable and Interactive Deep Discrete-Time Survival Analysis framework, an approach that is both data-driven and knowledge-driven. Furthermore, it embeds expert knowledge into the model, dynamically aligns feature contributions with evolving risk patterns, and actively engages experts to guide the learning process interactively. This interactive strategy not only enhances predictive performance but also produces explanations that clearly reflect the critical factors identified by domain experts. Extensive evaluations on diverse clinical and business datasets demonstrate that our method captures feature importance and yields robust and reliable predictions that stand in contrast to conventional black-box models. These results indicate that bridging interpretability with interactive expert engagement can significantly improve decision support systems. By integrating accurate forecasting with human-aligned, interactive explanations, our framework offers a promising direction for developing more transparent and trusted models across a wide range of disciplines.

title: Rollout Algorithm for Task Assignment in Edge-Cloud Computing
authors: Ye Xia
publish: 2026 IEEE 16th Annual Computing and Communication Workshop and Conference (CCWC)
abstract: Edge-cloud computing enables latency-sensitive and computationally intensive applications, such as virtual and augmented reality, autonomous driving, and IoT. A central challenge in this setting is task offloading, where tasks generated at the edge can be executed locally or offloaded to the cloud to minimize overall completion time. This problem is NP-hard, and exact dynamic programming solutions, though optimal, become computationally difficult as the number of tasks or cloud servers increases. In this paper, we propose a heuristic algorithm based on the rollout method in the dynamic programming framework. The development of the algorithm leverages the connection between the task assignment problem and the knapsack problem. By decoupling the time and task dimensions and using a binary search over the objective value, the algorithm achieves substantial complexity reduction compared to existing exact methods. Complexity analysis demonstrates that it scales efficiently with the number of cloud servers.

title: LHGEL: Large Heterogeneous Graph Ensemble Learning using Batch View Aggregation
authors: Jiajun Shen, Yufei Jin, Yi He, Xingquan Zhu
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Data Mining (ICDM)
abstract: Learning from large heterogeneous graphs presents significant challenges due to the scale of networks, heterogeneity in node and edge types, variations in nodal features, and complex local neighborhood structures. This paper advocates for ensemble learning as a natural solution to this problem, whereby training multiple graph learners under distinct sampling conditions, the ensemble inherently captures different aspects of graph heterogeneity. Yet, the crux lies in combining these learners to meet global optimization objective while maintaining computational efficiency on large-scale graphs. In response, we propose LHGEL, an ensemble framework that addresses these challenges through batch sampling with three key components, namely batch view aggregation, residual attention, and diversity regularization. Specifically, batch view aggregation samples subgraphs and forms multiple graph views, while residual attention adaptively weights the contributions of these views to guide node embeddings toward informative subgraphs, thereby improving the accuracy of base learners. Diversity regularization encourages representational disparity across embedding matrices derived from different views, promoting model diversity and ensemble robustness. Our theoretical study demonstrates that residual attention mitigates gradient vanishing issues commonly faced in ensemble learning. Empirical results on five real heterogeneous networks validate that our LHGEL approach consistently outperforms its state-of-the-art competitors by substantial margin. Codes and datasets are available at https://github.com/Chrisshen12/LHGEL.

title: Financing Green Startups: A Blockchain-Powered Approach
authors: D Srinivas, Simon Giri, Kartikeya Raina, Hardev Singh, Kirti Udayai, Vasim Ahmad
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Internet of Things: Smart Innovation and Usages (IoT-SIU)
abstract: Green startups routinely encounter difficulty in obtaining financing owing to the high funding needs to launch their business, and the imprecise market acceptance and future returns of those businesses, rendering them unacceptable to traditional latter-day funding methods. The funding modality available today fails to provide the needed transparency, flexibility and accessibility to encourage ventures based on green projects. This paper develops a funding model based in blockchain for green startups, that employs tokenization, decentralised finance (DeFi) and smart contracts with automaticity, as an efficient way of funding to provide secure, traceable funding structures that make funds available related to the performance of the venture. We present a conceptual model showing the responsibilities of startups, funders and smart contracts within a de-centralised funding ecosystem. Various case studies such as Power Ledger and WePower are investigated in order to validate the practical relevance of the model. Our research indicates how blockchain mechanisms can heighten trust, enhance liquidity, and automate funding that is linked to impact. This paper contributes to future work on scalable platforms that are both regulation compliant and also provide a fit between Blockchain infrastructure and the unique requirements of sustainable innovation.

title: Machine Learning-Based Detection of Loan Repayment Risk in Rural Areas Using Socioeconomic Data
authors: A. Faiza, A. Asha Davina, Sherin. Cynthia
publish: 2025 World Conference on Cutting-Edge Science and Technology (WCCEST)
abstract: The precise estimation of rural loan repayment risk is a challenging task because of the complexity of socioeconomic characteristics and limited formal credit data. Conventional models are not effective in the efficient estimation of non-linear patterns and region-specific financial behaviors. To address this challenge, this work suggests a hybrid machine learning model integrating Support Vector Machine (SVM) with K-Means clustering to achieve strong risk determination. The objective is to improve prediction performance by using unsupervised clustering to identify latent borrower profiles, and then labeling them using SVM for repayment risk classification. The proposed system analyzes several socioeconomic variables such as the nature of income, landownership, occupation, and education, thus providing more advanced borrower segmentation and classification. The novelty lies in the use of unsupervised clustering with a supervised classifier to develop a hybrid model specifically for rural loan classification. The system is most likely to offer improved generalization, interpretability, and flexibility to the unique dynamics of rural credit markets.

title: Training Kit for Microcontroller Applications in Electrical Control at a University of Technology in Thailand
authors: Somkiat Maliphan, Methawee Kaewkhomson, Suriya Kaewarsa, Penkhae Wongsuriya, Prasit Chara, Patipong Jongrutham, Surapha Somsai, Phichet Tabumrung, Jeerapha Phelawan, Ura Klongklaew, Kritsada Boonmeewised, Nithiroth Pornsuwancharoen
publish: 2025 IEEE 14th International Conference on Engineering Education (ICEED)
abstract: This study developed a training set on applying microcontrollers in electrical control work for the Computer Programming in Electrical Control Work course under the Professional Certificate Program in Thailand. The aim was to enhance the efficiency and achievement of 20 third-year Electrical Engineering students at the Engineering and Technology Demonstration School. The training set included content on microcontroller applications, media production techniques, an academic achievement test, a practical assessment, and a user satisfaction survey. Data were analyzed using mean (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\bar{x}$</tex>), standard deviation (SD), and percentage (%). Results showed that the content quality was rated good (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\bar{x}=4.33$</tex>, <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{SD}=0.45$</tex>), and media production techniques were also at a good level (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\bar{x}=4.09$</tex>, <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{SD}=0.65$</tex>). The efficiency score was <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$80.60 / 80.10$</tex> (E1/E2), and user satisfaction was rated excellent (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\bar{x}=4.57$</tex>, SD <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$=0.50$</tex>). The experiment indicated that students became significantly more interested in writing C-language programs, aligning with the research hypotheses.

title: Performance Evaluation of Quantum Machine Learning Classifiers in Bioinformatics Applications
authors: Abdullah Faqih Septiyanto, Riyanarto Sarno, Fadlilatul Taufany, Siti Halimah Larekeng, Aziz Fajar, Kelly Rossa Sungkono, Sang Seok Lee
publish: 2025 2nd Beyond Technology Summit on Informatics International Conference (BTS-I2C)
abstract: Bioinformatics is a field that requires Artificial Intelligence (AI) capabilities to analyze and process large datasets. One solution currently reported to handle the problems of high complexity in AI models is Quantum Machine Learning (QML). QML is a way to process large datasets more quickly and accurately by using the rules of quantum physics. This research evaluates the ability of two QML methods, Quantum Support Vector Classifier (QSVC) and Variational Quantum Circuit (VQC), to predict off-target effects in CRISPR/Cas9. The dataset used has a very high data imbalance, making it a challenge for the model to handle the imbalance. To achieve the best model performance, our research uses hyperparameter optimization to determine the optimal parameters for the number of qubits and circuit depth with linear entanglement and ZZFeatureMap. Our evaluation performance uses AUROC and PRAUC as the primary references, followed by accuracy and F1-score. The comparison results indicated that QSVC achieved the best performance with six qubits and two repetitions, having AUROC 0.861, PRAUC 0.821, accuracy 0.785, and F1-score 0.781. In comparison, VQC had AUROC 0.713, PRAUC 0.602, accuracy 0.725, and an F1-score of 0.727. The results indicate that QSVC with appropriate parameters can provide significant results for imbalanced data in the case study of predicting off-target effects of CRISPR/Cas9. Future research is expected to process more datasets with QML enhancement to handle a larger number of datasets using more optimal resource utilization, addressing the analytical needs in the field of bioinformatics.

title: Evaluating Transformer Models for Sentiment Analysis of User Reviews in Personal Safety Apps
authors: Meera Elizabeth John, Syam Mohan E, Gobi Ramasamy
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent and Innovative Technologies in Computing, Electrical and Electronics (IITCEE)
abstract: The use of personal safety mobile applications, such as women's safety apps, SOS alert applications, and location sharing applications, is on the rise to decrease the growing rates of social issues, including harassment, assault, and other associated threats. Although reviews in the Play Store provide useful information about user experiences, the immense quantity of feedback makes it hard for developers and policy-makers to systematically detect patterns and actionable concerns. Sentiment analysis may provide more scalable results in the case of Play Store reviews, but there exists a limited amount of research that uses sentiment analysis in applications related to safety issues. This article uses Transformer-based architectures (BERT, RoBERTa, DistilBERT, and ALBERT) to classify 6,527 user reviews of 15 personal safety apps from the app store into positive, neutral, or negative sentiment. According to the evaluation of our experiments, BERT showed the highest performance with an accuracy of 0.8789, followed by DistilBERT(0.8720) and RoBERTa (0.8697) right behind it in performance. DistilBERT and ALBERT were quicker to provide inference but less stable in evaluating neutral sentiment. The results of our thematic analysis indicated that constructive feedback emphasized trustworthiness, usefulness, and simplicity of operation, while critical feedback mentioned technical glitches, app instability, and GPS problems. These findings show that Transformer-based sentiment analysis can provide more tractable information to developers and policy-makers than star ratings, potentially improving the trust and reliability of safety apps. his.

title: Explanation in Bio-inspired Computing: Towards Understanding of AI Systems
authors: Rolf Drechsler, Christina Plump, Bernhard J. Berger
publish: 2025 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence for Computing, Astronomy and Renewable Energy (AICARE)
abstract: Artificial intelligence methods and applications have recently seen a massive surge, partially caused by the success of neural networks in areas like image classification and LLMs for generating near-perfect natural-language texts. Unnoticed by the public, but highly important for many AI methods to function, bio-inspired optimisation techniques have also seen a rising usage. However, the more techniques are used, the more complex the explainability decreases. Even developers of Neural Networks can seldom state why the Neural Network’s results are what it is. The explainability of AI methods, as well as systems in general, is, however, essential for safety, security reasons, and to gain and maintain trust with system users. While research in explainability has therefore gained significant traction with prominent AI methods, such as neural networks, bio-inspired optimisation techniques have seen less research in this regard. The complexity of explainability with these algorithms lies in the use of populations and randomness. We present an approach to track individuals in bio-inspired optimisation techniques, aiming to improve our understanding of the quality of results from such optimisation algorithms. To that end, we introduce a data model, include this model in the standard implementations of these approaches, and provide a visualisation that allows for understanding the relational information of these individuals, yielding more insight into these optimisation techniques and providing a first step toward improved explainability.

title: Designing an AI-Powered Virtual Trainer to Enhance Presentation Skills of Sri Lankan Undergraduates Through Real-Time Multimodal Feedback
authors: Chathura Prageeth, Dileeka Boralugoda, Sammani Vishara, Avishka Perera, Samantha Rajapaksha, Samadhi Rathnayake
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent and Innovative Technologies in Computing, Electrical and Electronics (IITCEE)
abstract: Undergraduate students in Sri Lanka struggle with presentation skills due to performance anxiety, insufficient guidance, and lack of real-time feedback. SPEAKRAFT addresses this gap as an AI-powered virtual trainer that delivers intelligent, real-time coaching. It integrates ML models to evaluate facial expressions using OpenCV/DeepFace, vocal loudness, speech pacing, and filler words. The modular web platform employs FastAPI for AI orchestration, React/JavaScript for frontend interactivity, and Express JS/Mongo DB for backend functionality. Users engage in targeted practice sessions or full presentations, receiving granular feedback and visual analytics. The system also enables institutional applications for academic and professional communication development.

title: The Need Of Fleet Management – How To Manage Thousands Of Intelligent Devices Like RTUs And Cellular Modems In A Distribution Grid
authors: Peter Noglik, Theodoros Oikonomou, Willie Meagher, Tomasz Wojtowicz
publish: 2025 25th Conference of the Electric Power Supply Industry (CEPSI)
abstract: This paper discusses the necessity of a central fleet management system for managing thousands of intelligent measurement devices and Remote Terminal Units (RTUs) in the electric distribution grid. It outlines a lifecycle of engineering, installation, and maintenance based on a hypothetical project that simulates real-world situations. It describes the deployment of 20,000 monitoring and control systems for secondary substations within four years. The fleet management system supports each stage of the project, from device provisioning to network configuration, monitoring device status, and performing bulk firmware updates. A cost-benefit analysis is presented briefly, followed by conclusions and outlook.

title: Suggestion and Evaluation of Hollow Voxel-based 3D Tangible Display Device
authors: Kaoru Mitsuhashi
publish: 2025 International Conference on Electrical and Computer Engineering Researches (ICECER)
abstract: Commonly prevalent 3D displays largely comprise holographic and glasses-based types. However, these technologies present challenges such as an inability to perceive 3D imagery depending on the viewer's position relative to the device, as well as health concerns like eye strain and headaches from prolonged use. To solve these issues, our previous research proposed a solid voxel-based 3D tangible display. Nevertheless, this previous display had limitations, including an inability to deform objects and a restricted range of expressible shapes. In this research, we suggest a hollow voxel-based 3D tangible display device and evaluated its effectiveness. The Voxel display consists of individual units storing Voxel beads and a device, which lifts and fixes the Voxel beads in mid-air. By elevating and fixing the Voxel beads in the air, the aerial fixation device enables the placement of Voxel beads with vertical spacing, realizing a hollow structure. This allows for the hollow representation of 3D models using Voxel expressions.

title: Supervised Learning in Business Continuity Planning
authors: Nethravathi K, Binita Nanda, Shivani Sharma, Ajay Sidana, Prashant Patil, Neha Rajas
publish: 2025 World Conference on Cutting-Edge Science and Technology (WCCEST)
abstract: In the current business world, where everything is going digital, a cybersecurity threat is a big threat to the continuity of operations. To create efficient Business Continuity Plans (BCPs), it is necessary to classify and predict cyberattacks in a timely manner. The paper is an exploration of applying supervised machine learning models Random Forest, Gradient Boosting, and Logistic Regression to identify and categorize cyber threats on a real-world cybersecurity attack dataset with 39,999 instances and 25 features. The dataset was prepared using data preprocessing methods like treatment of null values, feature encoding, and train-test split. To the surprise of no one, the evaluation demonstrated that all the models worked relatively equally, with the Logistic Regression model yielding the best, albeit still poor accuracy of 33.86%. Despite the fact that the contemporary model performance leaves much to be desired, the study points to the crucial discoveries concerning the data imbalance and feature importance issues. These results indicate that, through better preprocessing of data, balancing of classes, and feature engineering, supervised learning models could play an important role in automated real-time cyber threat response mechanisms that would make business continuity planning more reliable.

title: EfficientNet and Transformer based Multi-modal Fusion for Alzheimer’s classification
authors: Madusha Sandaruwan, Muditha Tissera, Amodha Medagedara
publish: 2025 International Conference on Advances in Technology and Computing (ICATC)
abstract: Alzheimer's Disease (AD) presents a significant global health challenge, with projections suggesting it could affect over 139 million people by 2050, especially in low- and middle-income regions where early detection is challenging due to gradual progression often mistaken for normal aging. The Enhanced Multi-Modal Alzheimer's Disease Detection Pipeline addresses this issue by integrating T1-weighted MRI scans, clinical text narratives, and numerical metrics from the OASIS- 3 dataset, which includes longitudinal data from around 2,100 subjects across cognitively normal, mild cognitive impairment (MCI), and AD stages. The model architecture includes EfficientNet-B0 for MRI feature extraction, Bio_ClinicalBERT for text embeddings, and a multi-layer perceptron (MLP) for clinical numerics. These features are either concatenated or combined using cross-attention before binary classification, allowing for effective handling of longitudinal dynamics. Training with 5-fold Group K-Fold cross-validation yields an average AUC of 0.8952, accuracy of 0.8735, and F1 score of 0.7818. Ensemble methods enhance specificity but exhibit conservative recall. This pipeline improves AD detection by integrating varied data modalities, though it highlights the need for refinements such as adaptive weighting and external validation. Ultimately, it serves as a reproducible tool for early intervention, potentially slowing AD progression in aging populations.

title: Design and Simulation of Hybrid Error Correction Codes for Reliable Space Applications
authors: Soubhagya M, Manju Devi, Sahana B, Neha S
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent and Innovative Technologies in Computing, Electrical and Electronics (IITCEE)
abstract: This paper prese nts an innovative error detection and correction (EDAC) system specifically designed to address cosmic radiation-induced cell upsets of space-grade onchip memory in severe environmental conditions where cosmic radiation and multi-cell upsets are a frequent cause of corrupted data. Unlike traditional XOR-based two-dimensional codes, which can only perform single-bit or restricted multi-bit protection, the proposed scheme has a variable data width of 32 bit to 64-bit data width, resulting in much wider fault coverage. The presence of errors such as burst faults is adequately rectified using block-wise XOR redundancy and cyclic redundancy check (CRC) verification mechanism that reinforces the detection and correction mechanism by utilizing the diagonals, parity and generation of the check-bit. The decoding phase recovers the original 32-bit data by the 64-bit input that is encrypted; hence full recovery of data is achieved with high accuracy. To prove efficiency, the design can be implemented and synthesized in Xilinx Vivado, where power consumption, LUT utilization-flip-flop deployment and I/O efficiency will be discussed in details. Experimental results infer that the scheme provides the better robustness at low redundancy making it lightweight yet highly reliable solution to protect memory in aerospace systems. Other significant contributions are to scale to 32-bit inputs ensuring that the recovery still returns 32 bits, explicit support of burst errors through error correction, as well as the introduction of a hybrid CRC-XOR coding and signaling scheme that balances error resilience with minimal hardware overhead utilization.

title: Blood Group Detection using Fingerprint
authors: Ekta, Sarika Chaudhary, Poonam Sharma, Akansha Singh, Bhawna Kumari
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Internet of Things: Smart Innovation and Usages (IoT-SIU)
abstract: Medical diagnoses, transfusion compatibility, and emergency treatment all depend on blood group identification. This study offers a unique approach to forecast blood groups by means of deep learning utilizing fingerprint patterns. Fingerprint images were examined using a custom Convolutional Neural Network (CNN) and transfer learning models including VGG16, ResNet50,& LeNet. To increase model’s generalization and classification accuracy, the dataset was preprocessed and augmented. Experimental findings showed that ResNet50 beat other models, therefore stressing the possibility of dermatoglyphic characteristics in defining ABO and Rh blood groups. With relevance in biometric identification, healthcare, and forensic science, this work shows a hopeful, non-invasive substitute for conventional serological methods.

title: Hitless redundancy protection for line differential protection communcations with active multipath pseudowire over IP/MPLS
authors: Hansen Chan, Dominique Verhulst
publish: 2025 25th Conference of the Electric Power Supply Industry (CEPSI)
abstract: Line differential protection systems (87L) has stringent communication requirements, including low latency, symmetric delay, jitter mitigation, and high resilience. Building on the capabilities of IP/MPLS networks, which provide deterministic quality of service (QoS), flexible playout buffers for jitter control, and techniques to minimize delay asymmetry, this paper introduces an innovative solution: active multipath pseudowires. By replicating relay traffic over multiple active pseudowire paths over IP/MPLS networks, this technology significantly enhances network reliability with hitless recovery and multi-fault resilience. This paper also presents the results of an industry validation effort, demonstrating how modern IP/MPLS networks can meet the utmost reliability and availability standards required for line differential protection, thereby ensuring grid resiliency and availability.

title: IoT-Based Monitoring and Control System of Energy Consumption for Athenaeum Building in Caraga State University Cabadbaran City
authors: Rolando W. Agujar, Ellen Jane G. Gulben
publish: 2025 International Conference on Electrical and Computer Engineering Researches (ICECER)
abstract: This study suggests an Internet of Things-based energy monitoring and control system for the Athenaeum building at Caraga State University. The system employs a range of components, including Raspberry Pi, ESP32 microcontrollers, LED displays, and a solid state relay (SSR) are just a few used by the system to offer real-time statistics on energy usage and automate control of energy-consuming systems. In order to reduce energy consumption and encourage sustainable practices, the solid state relay functions is to switch ON/OFF that cuts off electrical systems’ power supply when energy usage reaches a certain level. The suggested solutions provides building administrators with an easy-to-use interface to track and manage energy use and modify settings if necessary. Building managers can make well-informed judgments to maximize energy usage and advance sustainability by using a real-time data on energy consumption and automation management of energy-consuming equipment. The implementation of this IoT-based solution aims to improve energy efficiency, reduce operating costs, and promote sustainable practices in university buildings. Validation tests confirmed the system’s high accuracy, with an error margin of 1.204%. Nonetheless, systems were employed in earlier research using cloud applications and web-based XBee technology along with additional relevant sensors and modules. This study highlights the potential of IoT-based energy monitoring and control to promote sustainability in university buildings, adding to the expanding body of research on the topic. Universities may make a substantial contribution to lessening the consequences of climate change by encouraging sustainable practices and lowering energy use.

title: Federated Learning over Hybrid Cloud for Privacy-Preserving Credit Scoring in Multi-Institution Financial Systems
authors: Geol Gladson Battu, K M Zabir, Mukul Mangla, Keshav Kaushik, Renu Kumawat, Mukesh Soni
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Internet of Things: Smart Innovation and Usages (IoT-SIU)
abstract: The authors of this study created a federated learning methodology in a hybrid cloud setting to make it easier for multiple financial institutions to do credit checks while protecting users’ privacy. Federated Averaging (FedAvg) and differential privacy and fairness-aware learning are added to the suggested system so that more than one person can train a model at the same time without sharing any private customer data. The hybrid cloud approach allows for fast computation, compliance with regulations, and low latency. There are a lot of tests that show the federated model works just as well as the central model. It doesn’t allow personal information to get out or make ethnic bias worse. It also shows that the system can handle dropped clients, talk quickly, and be fair to everyone. Based on these results, federated learning might be a safe, fair, and scalable way to check credit in real time and across multiple computers in a banking setting.

title: Hybrid CNN–Attention Framework for Intelligent Crop Disease Detection and Monitoring
authors: Lynsha Helena Pratheeba H.P, Neeharika Bhadradri, Deepthi T, Anjali
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent and Innovative Technologies in Computing, Electrical and Electronics (IITCEE)
abstract: Plant diseases pose a significant threat to sustain-able agriculture and farmer livelihoods by reducing crop yields and increasing input costs. Manual diagnosis is laborious, time-consuming, and often impractical for large farms. In this work, we propose a Hybrid CNN–Attention framework for robust, interpretable, and efficient detection of crop diseases from leaf imagery. The architecture blends convolutional feature extraction with a spatial attention module that highlights disease-specific regions while suppressing background noise. We evaluate the approach on the PlantVillage dataset and additional field-like subsets to assess generalization. The hybrid model achieves strong improvements over baseline CNN sand pretrained backbones in accuracy, precision, recall, and calibration metrics. We include ablation studies, per-class analyses, and deployment considerations for mobile, drone, and edge devices. Attention-based visualizations increase model interpretability and can support human-in-the-Ioop workflows. We also discuss limitations, dataset bias, and directions for multimodal fusion and federated learning for privacy-preserving on-device training.

title: Spatial Analysis of Pharmacies, Baby Shops, and Population Distribution in Skopje: A GIS Approach
authors: Andreja Naumoski
publish: 2025 International Conference on Electrical and Computer Engineering Researches (ICECER)
abstract: Access to pharmacies and baby and children’s stores is crucial for supporting maternal and child health in urban areas. This study employs Geographic Information Systems (GIS) to analyse the spatial relationship between population density and the locations of such stores in Skopje, North Macedonia. Using spatial interpolation and hotspot analysis, the research identifies regions with optimal coverage and those underserved by current facilities. Findings reveal that while most densely populated areas are well-served, several peripheral zones - notably in the north, east, and southeast - have limited access, presenting opportunities for new business ventures. The approach supports data-driven decision-making for business expansion and enhances community accessibility. The results can guide strategic planning to improve service distribution and ultimately benefit families throughout the city.

title: Intelligent Heart Disease Detection Using Deep Neural Networks
authors: Prasad kolhapure, Shravani Wadikar, Giridhar Hebbale
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Artificial Intelligence for Innovations in Healthcare Industries (ICAIIHI)
abstract: Many people around the world die because of heart problems, with coronary artery disease being a major contributor. The efficacy of interventions that seek to improve prognosis and reduce mortality opportunities rely on timely, precise diagnosis. Traditionally machine learning approaches have been demonstrated to be useful for heart disease prediction, however utilising deep learning trajectory, and Convolutional Neural Networks (CNN) in particular, has afforded additional capabilities to identifying complex patterns present in medical data. n this work, heart disease prediction is improved by applying an Arithmetic Optimization Algorithm alongside a Multilayer Perceptron Neural Network. The AOA helps identify the key features, and the approach is tested using the Cleveland dataset [3]. To maintain robustness and prevent overfitting the Novalid cross validation process has been applied. The hybrid MLPNN-AOA model obtained training accuracy of 99.4%, and 96.7% test accuracy, which improves predictive performance on diagnosing heart disease over traditional modelling methods. The findings present evidence of accuracies identifying heart disease at the incipient stages when associated approaches leveraging deep learning, optimization algorithms, and rigorous validation.

title: Analysis and Simulation of Some Target-Mediated Drug Disposition Mathematical Models
authors: Verica Radisavljevic-Gajic, Alya Alaleeli
publish: 2025 International Conference on Electrical and Computer Engineering Researches (ICECER)
abstract: Analytical studies and simulation verification were performed for some target-mediated drug deposition mathematical models, known also as TMDD models. It was previously observed for the single compartment and two-compartment TMDD mathematical models that in the short time interval (up to several days) the models performed satisfactory, but in the long run (ten and more days) the models show instability, indicating that the steady state values of the model state space variables cannot be reached. In this paper, we have conducted detailed analytical and simulation studies of the Quasi-Equilibrium QE-TMDD mathematical model and shown for the non-existing starting amount of the drug-receptor complex, it stays equal to zero at all times. Moreover, simulation studies on this model using MATLAB/Simulink show that the quasi-equilibrium assumption is violated. The same conclusion has been reached for the QSS-TMDD mathematical model. These observations rise concerns about usefulness and validity of the QE-TMDD and QSS-TMDD models from the mathematical and practical engineering points of view.

title: An Efficient Vision Transformer Architecture for Occlusion Detection
authors: Arnab Mukherjee, David O. Johnson
publish: 2026 IEEE 16th Annual Computing and Communication Workshop and Conference (CCWC)
abstract: In this work, we present an efficient way to detect occlusions using Vision Transformers (ViT). ViT's have significant advantages over traditional models like YOLOv8 and ResNet for detection tasks. They use powerful attention mechanisms that allow them to understand global context very well. While Convolutional Neural Networks (CNNs) are good at capturing details in a specific area, Vision Transformers are better at understanding the overall scene, which helps them tackle the complex issue of occlusion detection. However, ViT's have complex structures, with some models containing up to 85 million trainable parameters. Training these large models on small datasets can cause problems like overfitting. To solve this, we choose an efficient way to encode positions and find the right number of attention heads. In this work, we propose a lightweight Tiny ViT-based architecture that keeps strong representational power while maintaining a good determination-Q ratio. Our model produces useful attention maps that show its focus on important areas and proves to be better than many leading CNN approaches. When we tested it on the ETHZ-Occlusion-reID benchmark dataset, our method achieved an accuracy of over 98%, demonstrating its strong ability to generalize for real-world occlusion detection tasks.

title: Smart Robotic System for Crop Automated Harvesting Using YOLOv8 and Robotics
authors: Kumar P, Nalajam Geethanjali, Kirupavathy J, Senthil Pandi S
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent and Innovative Technologies in Computing, Electrical and Electronics (IITCEE)
abstract: Robotics and AI-powered crop harvesting are revolutionizing modern farming by enhancing efficiency, reducing manual intervention, and increasing crop yields. This study investigates the implementation of YOLOv8, a smart object detection model, for improved real-time fruit recognition and selective picking. The system combines deep learning, IoT connectivity, and robotic automation to identify and pick fruits efficiently. It utilizes a high-resolution camera, a robot arm that can adjust its grip, and cloud computing for data processing to work more effectively. YOLOv8's advanced architecture allows real-time fruit detection even in complex agricultural settings with obstacles and changing lighting conditions. This paper outlines the process of acquiring the dataset, labeling, training, and testing it to achieve a high detection rate. Additionally, IoT monitoring and voice control make it easy for farm workers to use. Smart farming analytics offer useful insights for better resource allocation and eco-friendly farming. The results show a remarkable improvement in harvesting efficiency, accuracy, and scalability. Future improvements include using groups of robots, AI-based quality checks, and blockchain tracing for supply chain transparency. The research shows how AI-driven automation revolutionizes agriculture, leading to a fully autonomous and smart harvesting system. The use of edge computing minimizes data processing latency, allowing fast decision-making. Additionally, intelligent irrigation systems enable water-conserving farming practices, supporting the autonomous harvesting process. The system can be adjusted to various crops and environmental conditions to work optimally in different farming applications.

title: Advancing Malaysia's Energy Transition through Responsible Variable Renewable Energy Integration
authors: Wee Zhao Liang, Nik Ahmad Nazirul Zamri, Norhasbi Abdul Wahab
publish: 2025 25th Conference of the Electric Power Supply Industry (CEPSI)
abstract: Malaysia's electricity supply industry, historically reliant on fossil fuels as main generating resource, is undergoing a significant transformation towards decarbonization, driven by national policies like the NETR 2023 and commitments to net-zero emissions. Leading this transition, Tenaga Nasional Berhad (TNB) has developed a comprehensive Energy Transition Plan, targeting a 35% emission intensity reduction by 2035 and achieving net-zero carbon emission by 2050 through increased renewable energy integration, coal phase-down, and innovative technologies such as battery energy storage system (BESS) and thermal plant co-firing. This study employs a mixed-methods approach, including load flow simulations, policy analysis, and case studies of integrating renewable energy sources, mainly large-scale solar and hydro projects, to evaluate grid readiness for higher renewable energy penetration into the Grid. Key highlights include the critical role of local grid infrastructure and regional grid interconnections, such as the ASEAN Power Grid (APG). Addressing key challenges like grid stability, infrastructure upgrades, and policy clarity is essential for achieving Malaysia's renewable energy targets and securing a sustainable energy future.

title: Integrating Data Centres Into Scenario Planning for a Resilient Power System Planning In Peninsular Malaysia
authors: Jusey Reccla Mojingkui, Muhammad Syafiq Bin Mazli
publish: 2025 25th Conference of the Electric Power Supply Industry (CEPSI)
abstract: The growing demand for cloud computing, artificial intelligence (AI), and big data analytics have driven an unprecedented surge in Data Centre (DC) development worldwide. On the supply side, advancements in AI chip processors and other enabling technologies have further accelerated DC growth. Given these trends, forecasting future electricity demand may seem straightforward, with DC expected to drive rapid electricity consumption growth. In Malaysia, which has rapidly emerged as a key DC hub due to its strategic location, competitive resource costs, and favourable business environment has experienced similar trajectory. Over the past two years, electricity demand from DC has shifted from being a negligible component compared to other bulk loads to a distinct and rapidly growing demand driver. As of Dec 2024, Malaysia’s DC electricity consumption has reached 434.5 MW [1] and the government has estimated that this demand will surge to 12.9 GW by 2030 [2]. However, experiences from established DC markets such as the United States, Ireland, and Singapore highlight challenges posed by this energy-intensive industry [3]. As power availability becomes constrained, restrictive policies have been introduced in these countries to regulate DC expansion. This raises a key challenge for electricity demand forecasting: should load projections reflect unrestricted DC growth, or should they account for regulatory limitations and supply constraints? To navigate these uncertainties, this paper proposes integrating DC into scenario-based power system planning. By treating DC as a distinct load category, the study examines variations in energy consumption driven by factors such as efficiency improvements, cooling technologies, AI workload shifts, and regulatory uncertainties. Three scenarios, which are Business-as-Usual (BAU), Progressive Realization DC Demand Growth, and Explosive DC Demand Growth are analysed to assess their implications for generation planning, grid stability, and infrastructure investment. Based on these scenarios, DC demand is projected to range between 10.1 GW and 15.9 GW by 2030, with increased uncertainty around the longer-term trajectory placing the demand to range between 16.7 GW and 25.0 GW by 2040. The findings highlight the need for adaptive scenario planning approaches that go beyond traditional forecasting, ensuring grid reliability, optimizing investment strategies, and supporting sustainability goals. As DC continues to evolve as a dominant electricity consumer, integrating them into power system planning through scenario analysis will be essential to build a resilient and efficient energy ecosystem.

title: Proposal of a Knowledge-driven Reasoning Method for Nursing Care Risks Using Ontology
authors: Takumi Furuichi, Tatsuji Munaka
publish: 2025 International Conference on Electrical and Computer Engineering Researches (ICECER)
abstract: To prevent accidents caused by inexperienced caregivers, it is crucial to develop systems capable of understanding and reasoning about caregiving risks. In this study, we propose a bottom-up ontology construction framework for care risk management, combining narrative processing with large language model (LLM)-based structured reasoning. We extract key events and entities from actual caregiving incident reports and use them to build and refine an ontology representing domain-specific risks iteratively. By leveraging the narrative robustness of LLMs in noisy domains and their capacity for knowledge-based inference, our framework enables proactive risk reasoning. We evaluate its effectiveness using a dataset of annotated incidents, demonstrating improved accuracy and coverage in risk detection.

title: Enhancing Power Quality in Renewable Energy through Hybrid Solutions
