authors: C M Shylendra Kumar, V Gopalakrishnan
publish: 2025 25th Conference of the Electric Power Supply Industry (CEPSI)
abstract: Global warming has influenced the earth due to the emission of burring gas, and other fossil fuels in order to produce energy. To combat this issue, power electronic converter (PEC)-interfaced renewable energy generators (REGs) are being increasingly integrated into the power grid. While this is a positive step towards sustainable energy, it has also brought about certain challenges. Some of the key issues are the impact on reactive power and harmonic emission which are crucial parameters affecting power system. The fluctuation in reactive power levels can lead to voltage instability and pose a threat to the overall stability of the grid. Therefore, it is of utmost importance to maintain and manage an adequate reserve of reactive power and also filter harmonics to ensure a stable and reliable power grid. In light of these challenges, this paper aims to address the major power quality problems associated with renewable energy sources and propose effective mitigation methods. The goal is to improve the power quality in renewables and ensure compliance with grid standards. By doing so, we can pave the way for a more sustainable and reliable energy future. Power quality challenges Emerging power quality challenge due to renewables is the issue of excess or shortage of reactive power, harmonics and voltage fluctuations. Hybrid PQ compensator (STATCOM/SVG +Passive Harmonic Filter). The STATCOM/SVG is a power electronics-based device that can smoothly inject or absorb reactive power in the power system. Its main feature that is very useful for renewable applications is its dynamic reactive support with very fast response time. However, when it comes to harmonic mitigation, it may not be as effective since it can only mitigate a few select lower order harmonics. This is why the use of a passive harmonic filter becomes necessary. The passive harmonic filter not only provides reactive power compensation but also helps in mitigating harmonics ranging from the 2nd to the 50th order using various filters such as Band pass (BP), High pass (HP), Double pass (D), and C-type filter. In any system, reliability and availability are crucial factors. The hybrid PQ compensator offers smooth and stepless reactive power compensation, addressing the challenges posed by power quality issues. Even though the solution is hybrid the response time is so engineered that it is 100% dynamic. From a energy efficiency standpoint, a hybrid system offers a 50% reduction in losses compared to a solution using only SVG/STATCOM system. This significant reduction in losses leads to improved energy efficiency, cost savings also optimizing the cooling requirements needed by power electronic based equipment (SVG). The paper will discuss how the optimized hybrid solution uses best of both active and passive technologies ensuring best power quality while integrating renewable generation into the grid.

title: Detect, Investigate, Judge and Determine: A Knowledge-Guided Framework for Few-Shot Fake News Detection
authors: Ye Liu, Jiajun Zhu, Xukai Liu, Haoyu Tang, Yanghai Zhang, Kai Zhang, Xiaofang Zhou, Enhong Chen
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Data Mining (ICDM)
abstract: Few-Shot Fake News Detection (FS-FND) aims to distinguish inaccurate news from real ones in extremely lowresource scenarios. This task has garnered increased attention due to the widespread dissemination and harmful impact of fake news on social media. Large Language Models (LLMs) have demonstrated competitive performance with the help of their rich prior knowledge and excellent in-context learning abilities. However, existing methods face significant limitations, such as the Understanding Ambiguity and Information Scarcity, which significantly undermine the potential of LLMs. To address these shortcomings, we propose a Dual-perspective Knowledge-guided Fake News Detection (DKFND) model, designed to enhance LLMs from both inside and outside perspectives. Specifically, DKFND first identifies the knowledge concepts of each news article through a Detection Module. Subsequently, DKFND creatively designs an Investigation Module to retrieve inside and outside valuable information concerning to the current news, followed by another Judge Module to evaluate the relevance and confidence of them. Finally, a Determination Module further derives two respective predictions and obtain the final result. Extensive experiments on two public datasets show the efficacy of our proposed method, particularly in low-resource settings.

title: Sub-nW Thyristor Based Wake-Up Timer for Low Duty Cycle IoT Sensing Applications
authors: Juan Luis Soler-Fernández, Angel Diéguez, J. Daniel Prades, Oscar Alonso
publish: 2025 International Conference on Electrical and Computer Engineering Researches (ICECER)
abstract: This work presents a sub-nanowatt Wake-Up Timer for energy-harvesting IoT applications, based on a thyristor-based ring oscillator and asynchronous digital logic. Fabricated in 180 nm CMOS, the circuit has a power consumption of (100 ± 55) pW at 1.2 V with a oscillation frequency of (0.40 ± 0.07) Hz. It includes a programmable prescaler and a 19-bit counter, enabling long sampling intervals with minimal area (compared to other solutions such as WakeUp Receivers). The design demonstrates stable operation across multiple samples and extended testing, offering a compact and energy-efficient solution for ultra-low duty cycle sensing nodes.

title: Real-Time Risk Alarm Detection System: A Case Study in Meralco Distribution System Addressing Power Reliability And Safety On Building Work Sites (BWS)
authors: Ryannel Sy
publish: 2025 25th Conference of the Electric Power Supply Industry (CEPSI)
abstract: Energized lines in Building Work Site (BWS) present significant safety risks, leading to injuries, fatalities, and line equipment damage. These hazards pose challenges to Meralco's mission of delivering reliable and uninterrupted power services.Therefore, drastic measures must be taken to prevent such incidents. This paper proposes the implementation of Real-time Risk Alarm (RRA) device, with its motion sensing and warning alarm system, would benefit BWS personnel in heightening their awareness should there be a breach in the allowable safe clearance from Meralco distribution line facilities thereby preventing possible outages and any potential accident.

title: Interpreting Silent Speech: Recognizing Hand Sign Language from Images using ViT
authors: Amna Asad, Maria Tariq, Sundas Munir, Khushbu Khalid Butt, Nadia Tabassum, Maha S. Albahar
publish: 2025 International Conference on AI-Driven STEM Education and Learning Technologies (AISTEMEDU)
abstract: Hand sign language is not only necessary to talk to and listen to people who are not able to hear but also to make them understand since it is a visual language. This paper will suggest a deep learning-based system with the ViT (Vision Transformer) model to identify the gestures of sign language and convert them to Speech to support deaf and mute people. The system records the hand movements using a camera which is then converted into text and the voice is produced using text-to-speech (TTS). Trained on 87, 030 images, it employs transfer learning to enhance the accuracy and training performance. It has been found that attention-based models significantly increase recognition accuracy and response time and allow people with hearing or speech deficiencies to communicate more easily.

title: IoT Enabled Early Warning System for Human Wildlife Conflict Mitigation Using AI
authors: Amit Agarwal, Abhishek Aggrawal, Arvind Goswami
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Internet of Things: Smart Innovation and Usages (IoT-SIU)
abstract: Human-Wildlife Conflict (HWC) has become a pressing worldwide issue, mainly in areas where natural habitats are invaded by human settlements. The conflicts have the tendency to lead to loss of human life, livestock, and agricultural crops, while, at the same time, jeopardizing the survival of threatened species. This paper describes the design and implementation of an IoT-integrated Early Warning System (EWS) with Short Message Service (SMS) communication to counter HWC in forest-edge and rural communities. The system utilizes low-cost sensors and camera modules connected to a Raspberry Pi 5 to perform real-time animal detection based on the YOLOv8 deep learning model. When detecting wildlife intrusions, the system sends automated SMS notifications through the Twilio API to allow for immediate response by farmers, forest management authorities, and local governments. The system was tested in the field in Uttarakhand, India, where it exhibited consistent performance under real-world conditions. Experimental results achieved over 90% detection confidence, with alerts sent in seconds of detection to assure minimal communication latency. Modular design allows scalability, versatility in diverse ecosystems, and operation in low-connectivity regions through solar-powered networks. Performance measures, including detection rate, response time, and user response, confirm the system effectiveness in conflict risk reduction and fostering coexistence. In addition to immediate mitigation, data obtained can be used for studies on wildlife behavior, conservation policy formulation, and land-use planning over extended periods. The solution proposed emphasizes the possibilities of linking IoT, edge computing, and mobile communications technologies to tackle sustainability issues and thus make a direct contribution towards UN Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs) on conserving biodiversity, building resilient communities, and innovation-led rural development.

title: A Spatio-Temporal Graph Neural Network for Fifteen Joint Finger Movement Prediction
authors: Robby Ardiansyah, Khairul Anam, Bayu Taruna Widjaja Putra, Rika Dwi Hidayatul Qoryah, Aviq Nurdiansyah Putra, Muchamad Arif Hana Sasono
publish: 2025 2nd Beyond Technology Summit on Informatics International Conference (BTS-I2C)
abstract: Finger movements play an important role in various daily activities, such as typing, playing musical instruments, and interacting with electronic devices, and have applications in medical rehabilitation and human-computer interfaces. Monitoring and analyzing finger movements can provide significant assistance for individuals with physical limitations. This research proposes a finger movement prediction system that integrates electromyography (EMG) signals and finger angle positions obtained from a camera. The model used is a Spatio-Temporal Graph Neural Network (STGNN), integrated with a Graph Convolutional Network (GCN) and Peephole LSTM. Performance evaluation is performed on three test scenarios: within session, cross session, and cross subject, using the coefficient of determination (R<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup>) metric. Results show the highest performance in the within session scenario with an average R<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup> of 89.30%, followed by cross session with 85.09%, and the lowest in cross subject with 66.11%. The MCP and PIP joints showed the highest prediction accuracy in most of the fingers, especially in the within session scenario, with the highest value of 92.84% in the ring finger MCP. In contrast, the lowest performance was recorded on the middle finger in the cross subject scenario with a score of 34.94%. Nonetheless, the DIP joints on the index and little fingers still performed well even between subjects. This finding confirms the effectiveness of the model in learning muscle activity patterns, while revealing challenges in generalization between individuals, especially in complex joints such as the CMC of the thumb. The findings emphasize the complexity of thumb movements and the importance of developing more generalist models for various testing conditions in the future.

title: AEGIS: AI-Driven Glucose Prediction and Personalized Insulin Dosing
authors: Yash Kishorbhai Pansheriya, Marjan Asadinia
publish: 2026 IEEE 16th Annual Computing and Communication Workshop and Conference (CCWC)
abstract: Managing Type 1 Diabetes (T1D) remains challenging because people must constantly estimate how food, insulin, and daily activities will affect their blood glucose. Even with modern pumps and continuous glucose monitoring (CGM) systems, dosing decisions are still largely reactive and rely on limited information. This paper presents an AI-driven, multimodal framework that supports safer and more personalized insulin dosing by integrating short-term glucose prediction, clinical risk assessment, and individualized bolus recommendations. Using real-world data streams-CGM glucose, bolus insulin, carbohydrate intake, and physical activity, we develop engineered physiological features and evaluate two predictive models: a multilayer perceptron (MLP) and an XGBoost regressor. Under a leave-one-patient-out evaluation, XGBoost achieves strong forecasting performance at 10- and 15-minute horizons. Predicted glucose values are then mapped into ADA glycemic risk categories, and a rule-based engine translates these predictions into clinically coherent insulin-dose suggestions. Together, this end-to-end system demonstrates how machine learning can evolve current diabetes management toward more anticipatory, personalized, and safer decision support.

title: Predictive Modeling of Customer Churn in Telecommunications
authors: Suhani Sharma, Kailash Chandra Bandhu
publish: 2025 World Conference on Cutting-Edge Science and Technology (WCCEST)
abstract: Customer churn in telecom makes a substantial hazard to the financial success and sustainability of telecom companies, with earning new customers being more expensive than saving current ones. This paper presents a data-oriented approach to predicting customer churn for telecommunication using advanced machine learning (ML) algorithms, including Logistic Regression (LR), Random Forest (RF), Decision Trees (DT)-Tree Model, Gradient Boosting (GBM), and Artificial Neural Networks. The study utilizes a real-world telecom dataset with various customer attributes, including service usage patterns, billing information, and customer demographics. Feature engineering techniques and data preprocessing strategies were employed to enhance model accuracy and robustness. The evaluation of Model performance was done using key metrics such as precision Metrics, accuracy, recall, ROC-AUC and F1-score, Among the evaluated models, Gradient Boosting and Random Forest proved to have better performance in identifying high-risk churn customers. The paper also explores the interpretability of models using SHAP values and presents actionable insights for churn mitigation strategies. This research paper helps in the field by highlighting the effectiveness of ensemble methods and interpretable AI techniques in customer churn prediction, supporting proactive decision-making for telecom providers.

title: ECHO: Effective Coreset-Driven Learning via Hierarchical Optimizations
authors: Alec F. Diallo, Weihe Li, Paul Patras
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Data Mining (ICDM)
abstract: Despite driving record performance, the increasing reliance of deep learning on ever-larger datasets has led to prohibitively high storage and management costs that threaten continued progress. While coreset selection offers a promising solution to this challenge, existing methods often rely on expensive iterative optimization procedures or fail to select samples that allow strong generalization across tasks. In this work, we introduce ECHO, a coreset construction and augmentation strategy that leverages the relational properties inherent to a dataset to find its most representative samples. Unlike prior methods, our approach constructs a structured graph that encodes intrinsic dataset patterns, based on which influential samples are identified and augmented to maximize generalization performance. Extensive experiments across five benchmark datasets and against eighteen different coreset selection baselines show that ECHO achieves up to 60% accuracy gains under extreme compression, while being orders of magnitude faster than state-of-the-art alternatives. These results establish a new benchmark for data-efficient learning, particularly under tight coreset budgets, and showcase the benefits of structured coreset selection for effective generalization.

title: Automatic Eye Disease Prediction using Eye-Tracking and Machine Learning
authors: Venkata Ramana S, Yella Devika, Ravuri Madhuri, Rathlavath Padma, Rati Yochana, Puli Sai Santhoshini
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Artificial Intelligence for Innovations in Healthcare Industries (ICAIIHI)
abstract: Catching eye diseases early on makes a huge difference. Things like diabetic retinopathy, glaucoma, cataract, and age-related macular degeneration can lead to serious vision loss if not spotted soon. Right now, most computer-aided diagnosis tools rely on retinal fundus images or OCT scans. Those methods demand a ton of processing power. They focus mainly on structural issues in the eye. This paper introduces a fresh approach instead. It draws from eye-tracking data stored in ASC files. These files record things like pupil changes and how people gaze around. The setup views those details as key functional signs for spotting diseases ahead of time. It starts by cleaning up the raw signals from the tracking. After that, it runs them through an Extreme Learning Machine, which folks call ELM, plus LSTM and Bi-LSTM setups. All of this handles classifying various eye conditions pretty efficiently. To gain more confidence from doctors and push for real use in clinics, the system includes ways to explain the AI decisions. Tools such as SHAP and attention layers help here. They highlight exactly which patterns in eye movements drive the final calls the most. Tests show this technique hits strong accuracy marks. It pulls that off while cutting down on computing needs a lot.

title: Accurate Setting-free Fault Locator for Power Transmission Networks Using Synchronized and Unsynchronized Data
authors: O.D.Naidu, Vedanta Pradhan, A V S S R Sai
publish: 2025 25th Conference of the Electric Power Supply Industry (CEPSI)
abstract: Accurate fault location on power transmission lines is critical for utilities to minimize downtime, reduce operational costs, and expedite system restoration. Traditional double-ended traveling wave-based fault locators have gained traction due to their high precision—often within two tower spans (~300 meters). However, these systems demand high-frequency sampling hardware (in the MHz range), intricate commissioning procedures, and detailed engineering configurations, which can be limiting. To overcome these challenges, we introduce a phasor-based fault location methodology that operates without the need for extensive settings. This approach utilizes both pre-fault and fault-time data and is compatible with synchronized or unsynchronized phasor measurements. When synchronization is absent, the method estimates the time alignment error using a derived design factor, which is either computed from synchronized data or configured during commissioning. The proposed technique has been validated through both laboratory experiments and field deployments, demonstrating reliable fault location accuracy. Notably, the solution is implemented on a protection relay platform using standard sampling rates (1 kHz), making it practical for widespread utility adoption.

title: RE Forecasting Implementation and Challenges for Distribution System Operator
authors: Chanon Phaepilin
publish: 2025 25th Conference of the Electric Power Supply Industry (CEPSI)
abstract: The rapid expansion of distributed solar energy presents new operational complexities for Distribution System Operators (DSOs), especially within Thailand’s single-buyer electricity market framework. This paper investigates the challenges and implementation of renewable energy (RE) forecasting by the Metropolitan Electricity Authority (MEA), with a particular focus on rooftop and small-scale solar installations. Key obstacles include non-uniform metering systems, varying system configurations, and limited access to direct generation data. The study examines forecasting techniques such as LSTM, LightGBM, and Neural Prophet, applying them across several solar project types. Findings indicate that LSTM offers superior accuracy for day-ahead forecasts, while LightGBM yields better performance for hour-ahead scenarios. Additionally, pre-trained models are explored as a practical solution for prosumer networks with limited site-specific data. The results underscore the need for standardized data collection and forecasting protocols to support grid reliability and future market transitions.

title: From Traditional Records to Digital Health Platforms: A New Era in Healthcare
authors: Aarti Chauhan, Tulika Kakkar, Ranjit Roy
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Artificial Intelligence for Innovations in Healthcare Industries (ICAIIHI)
abstract: Advancements in the healthcare sector are vital for economic growth, human capital development, equity, and resilience to health crises. Despite government schemes targeting marginalised sections of society, India’s healthcare system faces critical gaps—shortages of trained physicians, inadequate infrastructure at primary health centers, and poor implementation of policies. Many intended beneficiaries remain excluded due to digital inaccessibility and a lack of documentation. Digital healthcare, including Artificial Intelligence (AI) and machine learning (ML), offers transformative potential by improving diagnostics, enabling early disease detection, and reducing financial and psychological burdens. Tools such as mobile apps, wearable sensors, and telemedicine enhance access, efficiency, and personalization of healthcare services, especially for rural populations. AI-integrated mHealth applications can predict diseases, design customized treatment plans, and optimize resource allocation. However, challenges remain: digital illiteracy, resistance to technology adoption, connectivity issues, and cultural barriers hinder widespread implementation. This article explores the role of AI in transforming remote healthcare, evaluates government health schemes, and examines the challenges and opportunities in achieving sustainable digital healthcare transformation aligned with the Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs).

title: Experimental Analysis of the Impact of Dust Accumulation on Efficiency of Solar Panels
authors: Y.A. Munasinghe, H.K.I.S. Lakmal
publish: 2025 International Conference on Advances in Technology and Computing (ICATC)
abstract: This study offers a low-cost, functional dust monitoring scheme in solar PV systems using a wooden enclosure created by SolidWorks with readily available components. The study focuses on the impact of dust accumulation on solar panels and on how small volumes of dust significantly reduce solar panel efficiency. Hourly dust deposition and electrical output readings were recorded, and there was a clear correlation between dust thickness and solar panel efficiency. The low-cost, easy-to-design system enables intelligent, data-based cleaning schedules that can save solar PV owners’ water and labor while maintaining high efficiency levels. Under various conditions of dust, this pilot study forms the basis of improved monitoring solutions through which solar PV owners can ensure long-term sustainability and operational efficiency.
